title: A Framework for Assessing the Carbon Emission Reduction Value of Virtual Power Plants under the Unified Electricity Market
authors: Fangfei Lv, Jieping Han, Yuhan Liu, Jiaqi Li, Zeqi Ge, Xi Tang, Wanyu Zhao, Wendong Shao
publish: 2025 IEEE 9th Conference on Energy Internet and Energy System Integration (EI2)
abstract: With the advancement of electricity-carbon market coupling and the construction of a unified electricity market, virtual power plants (VPPs), as the main body of flexible resource aggregation, urgently need unified and quantitative standards for their carbon emission reduction performance evaluation. This paper first refines and streamlines the index system from the three dimensions of economy, environment and society, and clarifies the reviewable caliber. Secondly, the hesitation fuzzy language analytic hierarchy process (HFLTS-AHP) is introduced to construct a linkage framework of "index-weight-comprehensive score" to depict the weight extraction under expert hesitation and group preference. Finally, based on three types of VPPs in a certain region of China, three types of VPPs: power supply, load and hybrid are carried out. The results show that the four weights of winning electricity, plan completion rate, government policy compliance and carbon emission reduction are the highest. The hybrid VPP has the best comprehensive emission reduction effect, which is better than the power supply type and the load type. This study can provide a scientific basis for the evaluation of the carbon emission reduction effect of VPP, and at the same time provide theoretical support and practical reference for optimizing its operation strategy and improving comprehensive benefits

title: A Hierarchical Sensing IoT Framework for Fire Detection Integrating Low-Power Sensors with On-Demand AI-Driven Visual Verification
authors: K B Charan Teja, Turaga Krishna Kartheek, V Dhatrish, Vijaya Raghavan N, Saranya S N, Madhumala R B
publish: 2026 Sixth International Conference on Advances in Electrical, Computing, Communications and Sustainable Technologies (ICAECT)
abstract: Fire detectors play a vital role in terms of safeguarding the residential, industrial, and commercial settings. Nevertheless, traditional single sensor detectors tend to give false alarms because of non-hazardous factors like steam, dust or cooking smoke. On the one hand, the vision-based systems although more precise consume too much power when constantly in use. This paper suggests a multinode two-layer hierarchical sensing architecture that integrates the scalability of sensor-based systems with accuracy of on-demand object detection. The system connects a set of ESP32 nodes, which have temperature, flame and smoke sensors and are connected to a central Raspberry Pi via NRF24L01 modules. Upon detecting the abnormal readings, the Raspberry Pi triggers an ESP32-CAM to check the visual confirmation of an abnormal reading with the help of a trained flame detection model. The prototype had a 2-3 second detection latency, 97.8% communication reliability, 96% visual accuracy and 60% lower power consumption than continuous vision systems. The proposed solution provides a scalable and low power with high accuracy solution in smart IoT-based fire monitoring.

title: Coordinated Planning of EV Charging Stations and Distribution Networks for Urban–Rural Differentiated Scenarios
authors: Kai Yuan, Chongbo Sun, Zhi Gao, Ming Nie, Zhiwei Wan, Yudi Ding
publish: 2025 IEEE 9th Conference on Energy Internet and Energy System Integration (EI2)
abstract: The rapid growth of Electric Vehicles (EVs) has introduced new challenges to the secure and economical operation of distribution systems, due to the spatial–temporal heterogeneity of charging demand. Urban and rural areas exhibit distinct travel behaviors, load densities, and grid hosting capacities, making uniform planning approaches inadequate. To address this issue, this paper proposes a joint planning method for charging infrastructure and distribution reinforcements under urban–rural differentiated scenarios. Charging demand and network characteristics are modeled separately for cities and rural towns. A component library is developed, including DC fast and AC slow charging, PV–battery hybrids, transformer capacity expansion, reactive-power support, harmonic mitigation, phase balancing, and sensing/communication units, with unified integration rules to ensure efficient deployment. A two-stage stochastic programming framework is established: the upper level employs the Non-dominated Sorting Genetic Algorithm II (NSGA-II) to optimize facility and grid investments under budget constraints and trace cost–reliability Pareto fronts, while the lower level applies Second-Order Cone Programming (SOCP) to perform operational scheduling with coordinated charging and storage dispatch. Case studies on modified IEEE 12-bus urban and 8-bus rural feeders show that the proposed method achieves superior trade-offs between cost and Expected Energy Not Supplied (EENS), outperforming facility-only, grid-only, and conventional joint-planning baselines. For example, rural EENS can be reduced to about 100 kWh, while in the urban case fast-charging EENS decreases from approximately 1.8–2.0 MWh to about 1.6 MWh with comparable Capital Expenditure (CAPEX). The results demonstrate that the proposed approach provides a reusable and scalable planning paradigm, enabling the resilient integration of EVs and distribution networks across diverse application contexts.

title: A Deep Explainable Learning Framework for Transparent Decision-Making in Critical Domains
authors: Anitha Govindaram, G. Charlyn Pushpa Latha, K. Logu, C. Anuradha, Nelson Kennedy Babu C, Narenthirakumar Appavu
publish: 2026 Sixth International Conference on Advances in Electrical, Computing, Communications and Sustainable Technologies (ICAECT)
abstract: The pervasive adoption of Artificial Intelligence (AI) systems in pivotal sectors, including medicine, financial services, automated systems, and digital security, has heightened the demand for clarity and comprehensibility in algorithmic processes. Conventional complex learning paradigms, notwithstanding their impressive predictive capabilities, frequently operate as inaccessible “black boxes,” thereby impeding user confidence, responsibility, and adherence to established guidelines. In response to these pressing issues, this study presents TrustAI, an Advanced Understandable Learning Structure conceived to bolster clarity, comprehensibility, and dependability in AI-facilitated judgment. TrustAI synergizes understandable complex neural arrangements with explainable AI (XAI) components, facilitating both local and global model comprehensibility. The structure utilizes a two-phase approach: initially, an inherent understandability layer within model architecture ensures understandable feature attribution; subsequently, a post-analysis understandability mechanism employs gradient-centered and perturbation-centered methods to produce readily intelligible justifications. Broad assessments across crucial application areas illustrate that TrustAI attains comparable precision while furnishing reliable, domain-relevant elucidations of model forecasts. The findings underscore that incorporating understandability within the learning procedure can bridge the divide between AI efficacy and user assurance, encouraging ethical and clear utilization of AI technologies in critical judgment settings.

title: CAPSD: Confidence-Aware Promptable Segmentation Distillation for Small Dataset Transformer-Based Models
authors: Andreea-Maria Onaci, Mihai Negru
publish: 2025 IEEE 21st International Conference on Intelligent Computer Communication and Processing (ICCP)
abstract: In this work, we propose an innovative knowledge distillation framework tailored for promptable transformer-based segmentation models, focusing on Segment Anything Model 2 (SAM 2). Powerful models, such as transformer-based ones, which require large-scale datasets and extensive computation for good performance, are difficult to train, even with limited data, often yielding uncertain segmentation results across different use cases. We address this challenge by proposing a distillation strategy that allows a student model to inherit structured knowledge from a strong pre-trained teacher with the same architecture, even when trained on small datasets. The method combines hard supervision from ground-truth masks with soft guidance from the teacher’s logits, helping the student learn meaningful structures early in the training process. To address the challenge of noisy or overconfident predictions from the teacher, we introduce a confidence-aware agreement mask that modulates the soft loss and provides guidance for the student to focus on regions where the teacher is more confident. The work is extended with token-level distillation to enhance structural fidelity in student representations. Experiments demonstrate the effectiveness of our method in low-data regimes, with the student model achieving a notable reduction in the total loss (from 15.17 to 8.84) and up to $20 \%$ improvement in Intersection-over-Union (IoU) across various inference scenarios. Qualitative results further support these findings, showing visibly cleaner segmentation outputs with reduced noise, indicating improved generalization under data-scarce conditions.

title: Model Predictive Control-based Optimization of Proton Exchange Membrane Fuel Cell Output Characteristics
authors: Bo Fan, Yinke Dou, Pengyue Li, Bin Wang, Liwei Kou, Chunyan Ma
publish: 2025 IEEE 9th Conference on Energy Internet and Energy System Integration (EI2)
abstract: To address issues with the unstable output voltage of the proton exchange membrane fuel cell after startup, this paper proposes a control strategy based on model predictive control to improve the output voltage stability of PEMFC. Based on the establishment of a PEMFC electrochemical dynamic model, MPC is employed to real-time estimate and compensate for total disturbances suffered by the system, track the output voltage, and stabilize it at a fixed value. The effectiveness of the control technology is verified via simulation experiments, and comparative analyses are conducted with proportional-integral-derivative and Fuzzy-PID control methods. The results show that the proposed control strategy can effectively reduce the amplitude of PEMFC voltage oscillations, stabilize the PEMFC voltage, and achieve the smallest overshoot and shortest adjustment time of the closed-loop system under the same disturbances, thereby endowing the system with better robustness and anti-interference capability.

title: Mid-to-Long-Term Bilateral Contract Quantity Determination for Independent Energy Storage and New Energy Power Producers Considering Outage Events
authors: Ying Wang, Qian Wu, Siqi Yu, Sisi Wang, Junpu Zhang, Qian Wang
publish: 2025 IEEE 9th Conference on Energy Internet and Energy System Integration (EI2)
abstract: To address the hourly compliance challenge faced by new energy power producers (NEPPs) in mid- to long-term profile-following bilateral contracts, a contract-quantity determination method is proposed that explicitly incorporates the impact of planned maintenance and random outages of an independent energy storage (IES) on deliverability. Based on outage–derating–maintenance logs, high-resolution trajectories of power and energy availability are constructed, integrating severity classification, interaction effects between planned maintenance and random outages, and post-outage recovery ramps. A sample-average approximation (SAA) combined with a conditional value-at-risk (CVaR) reformulation is employed to linearize the availability-risk constraints, which are further integrated with chance constraints on NEPP output and IES operational limits, resulting in a mixed-integer program solvable by commercial optimizers. Numerical experiments demonstrate that, under outage conditions, the proposed approach effectively reduces compliance risk while enhancing the expected payoff of the NEPP–IES coalition.

title: Reliability of the post training-quantized vision models - a conformal prediction study
authors: Calin Timbus, Camelia Lemnaru
publish: 2025 IEEE 21st International Conference on Intelligent Computer Communication and Processing (ICCP)
abstract: We present a method to analyze the extrapolation capacity of convolutional neural networks, for classification tasks, in the context of model-in-production performance expectations. More precisely, we investigate the calibration capacities of the quantized int8 and float8 models after training, assessing the suitability of deployment to production, by taking into consideration conformal prediction metrics alongside the well-established classification metrics. We prove that conformal metrics, such as APS [1], RAPS [2] and ECRC [3] provide additional insights into the prediction capabilities of the models, and should, as such, be used in conjunction with classical machine learning metrics as an additional evaluation tool when determining the overall reliability of a model.

title: A Wildfire Identification Method for Transmission Corridors Based on Dual-Polarization Doppler Radar
authors: Shangzhi Li, Aihui Wen, Enze Zhou, Zhiwu Yang, Siyan Lin, You Zhou
publish: 2025 IEEE 9th Conference on Energy Internet and Energy System Integration (EI2)
abstract: Wildfires pose a serious threat to the safety of power transmission lines. Traditional satellite-based wildfire monitoring is prone to failure due to cloud occlusion, leading to missed alarms. To overcome the limitations of satellite-based wide-area wildfire monitoring, this paper first collects data from dual-polarization variables of Doppler weather radar echoes near typical wildfires, followed by an analysis and statistical characterization of these variables. Subsequently, based on the identified characteristics of wildfire echoes, a wildfire identification algorithm based on dual-polarization Doppler radar is proposed using fuzzy logic. The algorithm employs a multi-step screening process: initial identification via fuzzy logic, local feature verification of wildfire echoes, and spatiotemporal continuity check of the echoes as a whole. This process progressively filters wildfire echoes from point to local to global scales. It is demonstrated that the algorithm can eliminate over 90% of non-wildfire echoes when validated using typical wildfire echo data, laying a foundation for effective wildfire identification and further reducing the rate of missed wildfire alarms in power grids.

title: An Enhanced Whale Optimization Algorithm-Optimized Hybrid Model for Short-Term Photovoltaic Power Forecasting
authors: Haipeng Lian, Mengfei Li, Xuandong Dai, Jingfei Wang, Jieni Yang, Ying Zhang
publish: 2025 IEEE 9th Conference on Energy Internet and Energy System Integration (EI2)
abstract: Accurate short-term photovoltaic (PV) power forecasting constitutes a pivotal technology for the realization of smart grids and the Energy Internet. In this paper, a hybrid CEEMDAN-VMD-CNN-BiLSTM model, optimized using the Improved Whale Optimization Algorithm (IWOA), is proposed to enhance forecasting precision and generalization. The methodology comprises three stages: raw PV power time series is first decomposed via Complete Ensemble Empirical Mode Decomposition with Adaptive Noise (CEEMDAN), followed by K-means clustering and VMD for further signal separation. CNN extracts deep features from refined components, which are fed into BiLSTM for temporal modeling and prediction. Improved WOA (IWOA) is adopted for parameter optimization and comparison. Training inputs include historical PV output, solar irradiance, and meteorological data. Experimental results indicate that the IWOA-optimized hybrid model demonstrates superior prediction performance compared to both the WOA-optimized hybrid model and traditional models.

title: Photovoltaic power prediction based on physical data driven
authors: Decheng Zhi, Zheng Wang, Yitao Li, Bo Wang, Mao Yang
publish: 2025 IEEE 9th Conference on Energy Internet and Energy System Integration (EI2)
abstract: Aiming at the fluctuation and uncertainty of photovoltaic power generation in power system, a photovoltaic power prediction model based on Bilstm-ECL (energy conservation law) is established in this paper. Firstly, the box diagram photovoltaic working condition model is used to clear the outliers twice by using the quartile and photovoltaic theoretical power, and then the average method is used to fill in the missing values and outliers to complete the data preprocessing; Then, the law of conservation of energy is taken as a part of the loss function to form a physical consistency constraint. Finally, the effectiveness of the proposed method is verified by experiments. The results show that compared with the existing methods, the prediction accuracy of Bilstm-ECL is improved in different seasons, and it shows better stability and robustness in complex weather conditions.

title: Impacts of Unbalanced Power Mitigation on Synchronization Stability of PLL-based Photovoltaic System
authors: Minhui Qian, Jianhua Li, Wu Cao, Kaiming Luo, Ning Chen, Bo Hu
publish: 2025 IEEE 9th Conference on Energy Internet and Energy System Integration (EI2)
abstract: Voltage source converters (VSCs) synchronize to the weak electric power grid through a phase-locked loop (PLL), which might become unstable following a fault, and thus causing synchronization instability of VSC. This paper investigates the mechanism of the influence for unbalanced power mitigation on the synchronization stability of the PLL-based photovoltaic (PV) system. To this end, firstly, the transient dynamics of PV systems are analyzed while analyzing the dominate factors for causing synchronization instability. Secondly, the impact for unbalanced power mitigation on the synchronization stability is analyzed from two aspects: absorbing unbalanced power by supercapacitor and reducing the power reference for maximum power point tracking operation. Simulation results indicate that the unbalanced power mitigation could be a better option to improve the synchronization stability of the PLL-based PV system.

title: Optimal Control and Regulation Method for Distributed Self-Synchronous Voltage Sources
authors: Wei Zeng, Zheng Lan, Fei Gao, Aihua Liu
publish: 2025 IEEE 9th Conference on Energy Internet and Energy System Integration (EI2)
abstract: This paper proposes a multi-objective optimal control method for distribution networks based on Distributed Self-Synchronized Voltage Source (SSVS) inverters. Firstly, the operational characteristics of SSVS inverters and their impact on distribution network performance when connected at different locations are analyzed. A hierarchical control architecture comprising Distribution Network Central Controllers, Cluster Coordination Controllers, and Converter Local Controllers is then designed to achieve coordinated control among parallel clusters under limited communication conditions. Finally, real-time hardware-in-the-loop simulations validate the proposed control strategy across different operational modes. Results demonstrate that the method effectively mitigates PCC voltage distortion, reduces voltage source output current Total Harmonic Distortion (THD), restores PCC voltage amplitude, and improves the utilization of distributed resource capacity, thereby enhancing distribution network power quality and operational performance.

title: Resilient Robust MPC for Frequency Control of Power–Gas Coupled Systems under DoS Attacks
authors: Libo Ran, Tianlei Zang, Chuangzhi Li, Siting Li, Lan Yu, Kewei He
publish: 2025 IEEE 9th Conference on Energy Internet and Energy System Integration (EI2)
abstract: As power-to-gas (P2G) penetration in integrated energy systems increases, stronger gas–electric coupling raises challenges for frequency stability, especially under denial-of-service (DoS) attacks. This paper proposes a robust model predictive control (MPC) strategy for secondary frequency regulation that incorporates disturbance prediction, worst-case constraints, and a resilient mechanism to mitigate communication disruptions. A gas–electric system with P2G units is modeled in MATLAB and tested under load fluctuation and DoS scenarios. Results show that the proposed method effectively suppresses frequency deviations, improves regulation performance, and ensures safe P2G operation. Compared with conventional MPC and PI control, it achieves smaller frequency extrema, faster recovery, and stronger resilience against attacks.

title: ArA - Evaluating LLM Suitability as Out-of-the-Box Malware Analysts
authors: Gabriel-Cătălin Rat, Andrei Popoviciu, Radu-Marian Portase, Adrian Coleşa
publish: 2025 IEEE 21st International Conference on Intelligent Computer Communication and Processing (ICCP)
abstract: Security operations centers (SOCs) are increasingly deploying large language models (LLMs) to assist human analysts; however, the reliability of off-the-shelf LLMs for malware analysis remains uncertain. This paper investigates whether state-of-the-art LLMs can serve as “virtual malware analysts” without task-specific fine-tuning. We introduce ArA (Artificial Analyst), a novel system that combines dynamic malware sandboxing with an LLM-based reasoning engine. ArA executes programs in a controlled environment, translates the observed behaviour into natural-language reports, and prompts an LLM to classify the behaviour as malicious or benign. We evaluated 12 modern LLMs-including GPT-4, its distilled variants, open-source models (1B-27B parameters), and a security-tuned model-on a dataset comprising 712 real malware samples and 304 benign programs. GPT-4 achieved the highest accuracy at 95% (macro F1 = 0.93), correctly identifying nearly all malware samples and rarely mislabeling benign software. Smaller open-source models demonstrated mixed performance; some attained perfect malware recall but exhibited higher false-positive rates on benign data, while the security-tuned 8B model performed below general-purpose models. We analyse each model’s strengths, common errors (e.g., false-positive biases), and the usage of tentative (“suspicious”) versus definitive labels. Our findings demonstrate that advanced LLMs can accurately distinguish malware behaviours in zeroshot settings, offering immediate value for SOC triage. However, we also highlight limitations, including insufficient coverage of behaviour variations and limited explanation quality, and discuss future steps to enhance LLM-based malware analysis for practical deployment.

title: Cognitive Metaphors and Conceptual Mappings in Figurative and Conventionalized English Expressions a Linguistic and Psycholinguistic Study
authors: Shagufta Parween, Neha Jain, Neelima Srivastava, M.G. Abinaya, Hari Nair, K. Selvakumarasamy
publish: 2026 Sixth International Conference on Advances in Electrical, Computing, Communications and Sustainable Technologies (ICAECT)
abstract: The conceptual and linguistic properties of figurative and conventionalized metaphors in English, and incorporates the conceptual knowledge of cognitive linguistics, psycholinguistics and computational analysis. With the Kaggle Metaphor Detection VUA-WSD Augmented Dataset, one can classify metaphors as either figurative or conventionalized to analyze variations in conceptual mapping diversity, frequency and interpretive complexity. It has been found that figurative metaphors have more diversity in terms of source-target domain mappings, are more creative and novel, whereas conventionalized metaphors are more frequent and processed with reduced cognitive effort because of their established use. Significant differences between the two types are statistically proved by using Chi-square and t-tests, which proves the role of metaphor as a linguistic and cognitive phenomenon. The results are applicable to both language teaching and improving awareness of metaphorical patterns and to computational linguistics, by improving NLP-based metaphor detection systems. Also, it is psycholinguistics, which shows an effect of the type of metaphor on cognitive processing and gives guidance on future experimental verification and cross-linguistic research.

title: Numerical Investigation of Backpressure-Resistant Isolators for Rotating Detonation Afterburners
authors: Jiang Dong Zhang, Zhen Juan Xia, Hu Ma, Guang Chuan Xu
publish: 2025 3rd Asian Aerospace and Astronautics Conference (AAAC)
abstract: To investigate techniques for suppressing upstream pressure propagation in rotating detonation afterburners, this study employs premixed injection and conducts three-dimensional numerical simulations using FLUENT to examine the suppression effects on rotating backpressure and impacts on afterburner performance. The research focuses on oxygen-lean, low total-pressure inflow conditions (total pressure: 0.19MPa, oxygen content: 17.5%) with three backpressure resistance configurations: constant-area isolator, obstacle-equipped isolator, and Tesla valve isolator. Results demonstrate that rigid obstacle height is a critical factor influencing the backpressure resistance of obstacle-equipped isolators. Under the specified operating conditions, when the dimensionless rigid obstacle height does not exceed 0.26, the constant-area isolator exhibits superior backpressure resistance and combustion performance compared to the obstacle-equipped isolator. Further increasing the obstacle height degrades combustion performance while enhancing backpressure resistance in obstacle-equipped isolators. Elevating Tesla valve height improves its backpressure resistance, but excessive height significantly compromises combustion performance. The obstacle-equipped isolator delivers better backpressure suppression and combustion performance than the built-in Tesla valve configuration, albeit with substantial increases in chamber size and weight, offering valuable insights for future isolator selection.

title: Obesity Risk Prediction Using Explainable Stacked Machine Learning with Lime Insights
authors: Boyineni Srinivasulu, Mittapelly Snehalatha Reddy, Mekala Shirisha, Maddukuri Nitya Santhoshi, Peddigani Akhila
publish: 2026 Sixth International Conference on Advances in Electrical, Computing, Communications and Sustainable Technologies (ICAECT)
abstract: A major global health issue, obesity is associated with diabetes, heart disease, and high blood pressure. For prompt intervention and long-term problem prevention, early obesity risk prediction is essential. Conventional measures like the Body Mass Index (BMI) only offer a limited amount of information because they don't take nutritional and behavioral aspects into consideration. In order to get over these restrictions, this study suggests a machine learning framework for predicting the risk of obesity that prioritizes interpretability and accuracy. To enhance overall performance, the system employs a stacked ensemble model that combines Light GBM, Random Forest, and Logistic Regression. Logistic Regression serves as the meta- classifier. Reliable and effective training is ensured by preprocessing procedures such normalization, feature selection using RFECV, and class balance using SMOTE. The model demonstrates a high degree of prediction power across various obesity categories. This work is notable for its application of Explainable Artificial Intelligence (XAI). The method identifies the most important factors-like nutrition, exercise, and water consumption-that underlie each individual prediction by using the LIME technique. People are empowered to comprehend their health risks and trust healthcare professionals as a result of this interpretability. For early obesity risk assessment in practical healthcare applications, the suggested framework provides an accurate, transparent, and useful approach.

title: Data-driven Distributionally Robust Configuration of Integrated Hydrogen Injection Energy System Considering Source-load Correlation
authors: Yan Liang, Yao Wang, Wenxuan Zhang, Fan Li, Jia Su, Yixun Xue
publish: 2025 IEEE 9th Conference on Energy Internet and Energy System Integration (EI2)
abstract: To better implement the integrated energy system coupled with the improvement of equipment in the configuration, this paper proposes a refined modeling and configuration method for EHG-IES considering hydrogen injection. Firstly, the refined operating model of EHG-IES equipment is constructed using linearization and relaxation methods. Then, a data-driven two-stage distributionally robust optimization method based on R-Vine copula is proposed. Subsequently, A comprehensive norm that combines the 1-norm and ∞ -norm is employed as the uncertainty probability distribution information set. Lastly, a co-optimal configuration model of EHG-IES is proposed. The case study shows that, compared with the fixed energy efficiency model, the refined model of equipment can markedly reduce the annual combined cost and distributed generation power spillage rate by 1.72% and 0.21%.

title: A Method for Regulating the Diverse Flexible Resources of Virtual Power Plants Considering Cooperative Tendencies
authors: Yinli Hang, Wei Shi, Yi Yuan, Dandan Mo
publish: 2025 IEEE 9th Conference on Energy Internet and Energy System Integration (EI2)
abstract: Under the "Dual Carbon" goals and the integration of a high proportion of renewable energy into the new power system, Virtual Power Plants (VPPs), as a key technology for aggregating distributed resources, see their effectiveness determined not only by the physical characteristics of the resources but also constrained by the participation willingness and behavioral patterns of the resource owners. This paper, based on the core concept of "Cooperation Propensity," constructs a new paradigm for VPP flexibility resource aggregation that considers the active willingness of resource owners to cooperate. Firstly, detailed modeling of three key types of flexibility resources is conducted, establishing differentiated response capability evaluation models for peak shaving and frequency regulation. Then, a quantitative model for cooperation propensity including multiple indicators is established. Finally, aiming to minimize VPP operational costs, an aggregation optimization model based on the ranking of cooperation propensity is developed. Case studies verify that, while ensuring positive net revenue for the VPP, the model can achieve fast and stable tracking of power demand through dynamic optimization and combination of multiple resources, effectively identifying and prioritizing the dispatch of high-quality resources with "high willingness, high capability."

title: A compound current mismatch fault diagnosis method for PV modules based on I-V curve graph electrical characteristics
authors: Zhe Wang, Hanze Liu, Luoqi Wang, Ji Li
publish: 2025 IEEE 9th Conference on Energy Internet and Energy System Integration (EI2)
abstract: As the basic key equipment in the photovoltaic (PV) system, the reliable operation of PV modules directly determines the energy efficiency level and operating profit of the system. In the operating life cycle of PV systems, PV modules are easily affected by external factors and operating conditions to induce various defects or faults. The occurrence of current mismatch faults with high frequency will not only cause the output of PV modules to drop, but even lead to irreversible safety accidents. This paper mainly studies the current mismatch faults such as partial shading, hot spot and cracks, and focuses on the characteristics and diagnosis method of compound current mismatch faults. By investigating the PV modules with different current mismatch faults in the actual PV plants, the causes of the I-V curve of the compound current mismatch fault of PV modules are analyzed. In order to realize the detection and fault decoupling of compound current mismatch faults of PV modules, a fault diagnosis method based on I-V curve graph electrical characteristics is innovatively proposed. The mismatch and step data are identified and determined by detecting the change of the included angle between every two adjacent straight lines, which are formed by every two adjacent data points on the I-V curve. The piecewise linearization detection is performed on the step data of mismatched PV modules to further decouple the types of mismatched faults. It is verified through the I-V data of the PV modules in the field, and the results demonstrate that the method can effectively diagnose the compound current mismatch fault in PV modules.

title: Application of DSED API-Based Parallel Simulation in Grid Impedance Stability Analysis
authors: Songzhe Li, Zhuang Liu, Jinghong Zheng, Yusheng Ding, Liqiang Yuan, Bochen Shi
publish: 2025 IEEE 9th Conference on Energy Internet and Energy System Integration (EI2)
abstract: The impedance stability analysis based on frequency sweep simulation is a crucial stability assessment method for power system with high ratio of power electronic equipment, while it has problems of long simulation time and cumbersome procedure. Utilizing Discrete State Event Driven (DSED) DSIM and its automated application program interface (API), this paper develops a frequency sweeping program based on DSED API and introduces its principle and implement approach. Through parallel design, the program significantly reduces both operational complexity and simulation time. Simulation results validate its accuracy and efficiency, while a case study given demonstrates its application capabilities.

title: Fault Tolerant 3D Reconstruction using Fusion of Complementary Depth Estimation Approaches
authors: Mircea Paul Muresan, Sergiu Nedevschi
publish: 2025 IEEE 21st International Conference on Intelligent Computer Communication and Processing (ICCP)
abstract: Accurate depth estimation from cameras is a highly important task for autonomous systems. Existing monocular depth estimation methods span a wide range of approaches, from supervised techniques which leverage labelled datasets, to self-supervised methods which utilize video sequences without explicit depth annotations. Additionally, stereo vision solutions provide absolute depth measurements using the disparity maps obtained via stereo correspondence. This paper proposes a depth fusion system combining Monodepth2, MiDaS and stereo vision using semantic-aware scaling and error-aware selection. The original contributions of our work are three-fold. First, our approach proposes an original method of scaling the relative disparities from monocular depth estimation using semantic segmentation and an original depth discretization technique. Then, an original method is proposed for combining self-supervised and supervised approaches using the probability of information obtained from learning the error each type of monocular depth estimation method produces. The final contribution consists in the presentation of the fault tolerant system used to reconstruct the scene. The proposed approach has been tested on the KITTI dataset and highlights the effectiveness of combining these complementary methods showing good results even in situations where individual methods would fail.

title: Artificial Neural Network-Based Optimization and Kinetic Modeling of Magnetic Nanoparticle-Impregnated Jackfruit Seed Biomass for Pollutant Removal
authors: Anbalagan S
publish: 2026 Sixth International Conference on Advances in Electrical, Computing, Communications and Sustainable Technologies (ICAECT)
abstract: This study presents the artificial neural network (ANN)based optimization and kinetic modeling for the effective removal of Cr(VI) ions utilizing magnetic nanoparticle-impregnated jackfruit seed biomass. The novel adsorbent was synthesized through ecofriendly impregnation methods and characterized using standard physicochemical techniques. The ANN model demonstrated superior prediction accuracy over traditional adsorption models, effectively capturing complex nonlinear relationships between process variables and adsorption efficiency. The adsorption kinetics were most accurately represented by pseudo-second-order model, signifying that chemisorption is the key mechanism governing the Cr(VI) removal process. Isotherm analysis confirmed multilayer adsorption, following the Freundlich model. The magnetic properties enabled easy recovery and reuse of the adsorbent with minimal efficiency loss over multiple cycles. The integrated ANN and kinetic modeling approach not only improved process understanding but also facilitated optimal operational conditions for scalable application. This study highlights the potential of lowcost, sustainable biosorbents for efficient environmental remediation using intelligent modeling tools.

title: Optimal Operation of Distribution Networks with High PV Penetration Using Deep Hybrid Forecasting and Mobile Energy Storage Regulation
authors: Mao Yang, Wencheng Li, Yitao Li, Peng Sun, Xu Lin, Hailong Fan, Ling Wang
publish: 2025 IEEE 9th Conference on Energy Internet and Energy System Integration (EI2)
abstract: With the advancement of carbon peak and carbon neutrality goals, integrating a high proportion of distributed photovoltaic (PV) power into distribution networks is prone to causing voltage overshoot, power flow reversal, and increased network losses. To address these challenges, this paper proposes an integrated "prediction-optimization-control" framework. First, a CNN-LSTM-Attention deep learning model is constructed to achieve high-precision prediction of load and PV output. Secondly, based on the forecasting results, a multi-objective optimization model is established with the objectives of minimizing voltage vulnerability, network losses, and the capacity of the Mobile Energy Storage System (MESS). An Improved Multi-objective Particle Swarm Optimization (IMOPSO) algorithm is designed to solve it. Simulation results on the IEEE 33-node system demonstrate that the proposed method can effectively reduce voltage fluctuations and network losses.

title: Harmonic-Priority Coordinated DC-link Control for a PV-ESS-EV Integrated UPQC
authors: Xiao Chun Dong, Haiqiang Ye, Shaocong Zhou, Yonghai Xie, Guangyao Liu, Hongyu Qu
publish: 2025 IEEE 9th Conference on Energy Internet and Energy System Integration (EI2)
abstract: This paper presents a Photovoltaic-Energy Storage-Charging integrated unified power quality conditioner (UPQC) featuring a harmonic-priority coordinated DC-link control strategy. Conventional UPQC systems often experience DC-link voltage oscillations and limited current-tracking performance under fluctuating photovoltaic generation and stochastic electric-vehicle charging. In the proposed framework, the shunt converter adopts a 0.1 A fixed-band hysteresis current controller with capacitor-current compensation and one-step predictive feedforward to achieve fast DC-link regulation and ensure accurate harmonic and reactive current compensation. The series converter employs a dq-frame low-pass-filtered voltage reference combined with hysteresis-limited injection to restore the point-of-common-coupling (PCC) voltage during grid sags without overcompensation. The PV interface limits its output power when the DC-link voltage exceeds the reference, whereas the EV charger applies an under-voltage power-limiting scheme when the DC voltage drops, jointly maintaining DC-bus balance. The energy-storage system (ESS) participates in slow-timescale energy balancing to suppress long-term voltage drift. The simulation results demonstrate that the proposed method keeps the DC-link deviation within ±3 %, reduces the source-current THD from 15 % to below 3 %, and effectively balances harmonic compensation and voltage regulation. Distinguishing from existing fuzzy control and power hierarchical control, this strategy prioritizes harmonic compensation while adaptively adjusting power distribution based on converter capacity, avoiding the trade-off between voltage stability and power quality inherent in conventional methods.

title: One-Step Complementary-Magnetization-Switching Spin-Orbit Torque Memory Based on U-Shaped Bending Current for Compute-in-Memory Applications
authors: Hao Zhang, Di Wang, Long Liu, Yu Liu, Kaiping Zhang, Huai Lin, Ruifeng Tang, Yu Li, Changqing Xie
publish: IEEE Transactions on Electron Devices
abstract: The fast-paced evolution of artificial intelligence and the von Neumann bottleneck have greatly stimulated research interest in the compute-in-memory (CIM) architecture based on crossbar arrays, especially in the matrix–vector multiplication (MVM) algorithm mapping. With the additional degrees of freedom provided by decoupling the read and write channels, spin-orbit torque magnetic random access memory (SOT-MRAM) is considered to be a more suitable physical carrier for executing MVM in analog CIM, compared with the common-channel spin-transfer torque (STT)-MRAM. However, typical SOT-MRAM suffers from low read accuracy and inferior sensing margin due to the small tunneling magnetoresistance (TMR) ratio. In this work, we propose a novel complementary SOT-MRAM (CSOT-MRAM) device structure of two symmetric magnetic tunnel junction (MTJ) cells located on a shared U-shaped SOT channel. It can simultaneously function as nonvolatile memory (NVM) and implement in-memory logic operations without add-on logic circuits. First, one-step complementary magnetization switching is successfully achieved by preparing an improved U-shaped Hall-bar device, without adding extra write current. Furthermore, complementary magnetization switching dynamics at the MTJ level is studied using micromagnetic simulations. Finally, the principle of a three transistors and two MTJs (3T-2M) memory/computing unit executing <sc xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">xnor/xor</small> logic functions and its application in the MVM domain are demonstrated. The results indicate that the novel CSOT-MRAM holds promise for expanding the potential applications of traditional MRAM within the CIM field.

title: A SPICE-Compatible High-Efficiency Equivalent Mechanical Circuit Method for Electro-Thermal-Mechanical Coupling Simulation
authors: Yizhang Liu, Liang Tian, Yiqun Niu, Yinshui Xia, Wenchao Chen
publish: IEEE Transactions on Electron Devices
abstract: Accurate and efficient modeling and simulation of electro-thermal-mechanical field coupling is essential for evaluating multiphysics effects on devices/circuits’ performance and reliability, as the multiphysics coupling effects become severe in advanced integrated circuits. In our previous work, we developed the equivalent mechanical circuit (EMC) method, thereby constructing a SPICE-compatible equivalent multiphysics circuit framework to simulate electro-thermal-mechanical coupling processes in advanced integrated circuits. However, the computational efficiency of the previous EMC (pEMC) method remains limited compared with the finite element method (FEM), since the pEMC method requires multiple iterations to simulate thermal expansion, even in linear equation systems. In this article, we develop a novel EMC method by proposing voltage-controlled current sources (VCCSs) into the pEMC. Therefore, the novel EMC method can simulate thermal expansion without iteration in linear equation systems. The results demonstrate that the computational efficiency of the novel EMC method achieves a tenfold improvement compared to the pEMC method and exhibits computational efficiency comparable to the FEM under the same number of nodes.

title: An Efficient Approach Integrating Sentinel-1 and Sentinel-2 Imagery for Mapping Permanent and Seasonal Water Area_supp1-3673591.docx

publish: N/A

title: Service Innovation of Platform Ecosystem: A Case Study of Smart Campus in China
authors: Mengfei Zhou, Ping Gao
publish: IEEE Engineering Management Review
abstract: This paper presents a case study of service innovation within the smart campus of a Chinese public university. We first propose the Service-Dominant Platform Ecosystem (SDPE) model, which integrates service-dominant logic, platform ecosystem components, and a lifecycle model. We then employ the SDPE model to examine the process that SDPE components including actors, activities, resources, platform technologies, and institutions collaborate to drive smart campus service innovation by four stages - self-renewal, birth, expansion, and leadership. Our study identifies the distinct challenges associated with each SDPE component and offers actionable insights for overcoming these challenges. This study provides valuable guidance for public sector organizations seeking to leverage platform ecosystems for promoting service innovation and enhancing operational efficiency.

title: Frame-level temporal action segmentation in nonhuman primates by fusing skeleton and visual modalities
authors: Junwen Wu, Hongli Zhang, Ruijie Li, Zihao Wang, Sha Liu, Feng He, Jianzhi Wang, Lingling Xu, Jingjing Zhang
publish: IEEE Transactions on Automation Science and Engineering
abstract: Precise and objective quantification of non-human primates (NHPs) actions is vital for neuroscience research and for the phenotypic analysis of neuropsychiatric disorder models. However, existing computational ethology methods primarily focus on coarse-grained action recognition at the video-clip level and lack frame-level temporal segmentation tools for analyzing continuous action streams. This severely restricts the in-depth exploration of action dynamics, including duration, transition patterns, and rhythmicity. To address this issue, this study proposes a multi-modal, multi-stage action segmentation framework. The framework synergistically leverages the complementary strengths of different modalities by deeply fusing kinematic information from skeleton data, appearance features from RGB video, and dense motion information from optical flow, enabling fine-grained frame-wise action analysis of NHPs. To facilitate this task, MonActSeg was introduced as the first fine-grained, frame-by-frame annotated benchmark specifically designed for NHP temporal action segmentation. It encompasses a total of 1.37 million labeled frames across seven core actions. Evaluated on the MonActSeg benchmark under the video-level protocol, the proposed framework demonstrated superior performance, achieving a frame-wise accuracy (Acc) of 91.08%, an edit distance (Edit) of 85.94%, and a segmental F1@50 of 81.23%. This work provides an analytical tool and a standardized benchmark for computational ethology, enabling fine-grained quantification of NHPs behavioral phenotypes.

title: Deep Reinforcement Learning with Adaptive Enhanced Attention for CTSPs
authors: Renyi Zhang, Xiangping Xu, Xinli Shi, Jinde Cao, Jie Gui
publish: IEEE Transactions on Artificial Intelligence
abstract: The Colored Traveling Salesman Problem (CTSP) and its extended variant, the Cumulative Capacitated Colored TSP (C<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup>-CTSP), represent significant generalizations of the classical Multiple TSP (MTSP). By incorporating color constraints, capacity restrictions, and customized cost calculation mechanisms for different entities, these formulations demonstrate strong relevance to real-world applications. This paper proposes an Adaptive Enhanced Attention (AEA) model to address color-constrained traveling salesman problems based on a reinforcement learning framework. Our method introduces several novel techniques. First, we develop a dynamic color-aware decoding mechanism that systematically handles color constraints through bitwise operations and adaptive state transitions. Then, we establish a bi-objective optimization method integrating an adaptive penalty mechanism to balance cumulative cost minimization with constraint satisfaction. In addition, we design an Enhanced Attention Unit (EAU) that accelerates strategy convergence and improves solution precision through gated activation and hierarchical feature propagation. Evaluations on CTSP and C<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup>-CTSP benchmarks demonstrate that the AEA model outperforms existing neural combinatorial optimization (NCO) solvers and several heuristic baselines. Generalization experiments reveal that the EAU module effectively accelerates convergence and enhances decoding robustness, showing promising applicability to other combinatorial optimization tasks.

title: A Two-Stage Learning Guided Multi-Objective Evolutionary Algorithm for Task Offloading in Edge Computing
authors: Shijia Huang, Feng Wang, Jian Wang, Bing Li
publish: IEEE Transactions on Emerging Topics in Computational Intelligence
abstract: Task offloading in edge computing has become an effective way for reducing terminal device load and task execution time. Recently, various task offloading methods have been proposed in an effort to increase processing performance for computing tasks between edge servers and consumers. However, large-scale computing tasks need to be offloaded to edge nodes due to the terminal devices’ rapid development, which makes it difficult to obtain the optimal offloading scheme. Meanwhile, existing offloading methods usually consider a few common objectives and ignore other important factors such as economic cost. In this paper, the task offloading involving numerous computing tasks is formulated as a large-scale multi-objective optimization problem, which encompasses multiple optimization objectives, i.e., task time delay, economic cost and load balancing. Then, a two-stage learning guided multi-objective evolutionary algorithm (TSLEA) is proposed to generate task offloading schemes. Firstly, a global search based on the adaptive incremental histogram learning model is proposed to improve the exploration ability of TSLEA. Secondly, a local search based on decision space division is proposed to enhance the population convergence. Experimental results on three real-world datasets show that TSLEA achieves competitive performance compared to several representative algorithms.

title: P-type SnO thin-film transistor with ultra-thin channel and extremely high on/off current ratio
authors: Yiwen Yao, Jialong Song, Chaoran Dong, Ning Wang, Peng Dai, Zening Gao, Yiming Wang, Yuxiang Li, Jiawei Zhang, Aimin Song, Qian Xin
publish: IEEE Electron Device Letters
abstract: In this work, p-type tin monoxide (SnO) thin films and corresponding thin-film transistors (TFTs) are fabricated using the Sn precursor, bis(N,N'-di-i-propylacetamidinato)tin(II) [Sn(i-Pr-Me-AMD)<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sub>], via atomic layer deposition (ALD). The impacts of deposition temperature (200-275 °C) and channel thickness (2.7-16.2 nm) on film properties and device performance are systematically investigated. Crystalline SnO thin films with a preferred (001) orientation are prepared. At an optimized deposition temperature of 250 °C, the TFT with an ultra-thin 5.4-nm SnO channel achieves an exceptionally high on/off current ratio of 9.20×10<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">7</sup> with a hole mobility of 0.38 cm<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup> V<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">-1</sup> s<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">-1</sup>. In contrast, a thicker 16.2-nm channel exhibits a higher mobility of 2.24 cm<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup> V<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">-1</sup> s<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">-1</sup> with a reduced on/off current ratio of 5.67×10<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">3</sup>. The results highlight the strong potential of ALD-grown p-type SnO for high-performance oxide electronics.

title: Rethinking Transparent Object Grasping: Depth Completion with Monocular Depth Estimation and Instance Mask
authors: Yaofeng Cheng, Xinkai Gao, Sen Zhang, Chao Zeng, Fusheng Zha, Lining Sun, Chenguang Yang
publish: IEEE Robotics and Automation Letters
abstract: Accurate depth maps are essential for robotic grasping. However, transparent objects often cause depth cameras to produce missing or distorted depth due to reflection and refraction, making grasping them particularly challenging. Precise depth estimation for transparent objects is therefore crucial. Existing end-to-end methods aim to predict depth directly from RGB-D inputs. However, depth distortion varies significantly with environment, viewpoint, and illumination, making it difficult to predict. Models trained on limited datasets often fail to generalize effectively in real-world applications. In this letter, we propose ReMake, a novel transparent object depth completion framework that injects structured priors to reformulate the depth estimation learning objective. First, we introduce a mask prior to explicitly separate transparent regions from reliable background depth. This separation ensures consistency between training and real-world application, where masked areas correspond to transparent regions and unmasked areas to opaque ones. This improves the model's generalizability in real-world applications. Second, we further incorporate relative depth as an additional prior obtained from monocular depth estimation, which encodes the depth relationship between transparent objects and their background to guide the reconstruction of transparent regions more effectively. Extensive experiments across datasets and real-world scenes demonstrate that ReMake significantly outperforms existing methods, especially in different-view prediction experiments, evaluating its generalizabilityCode and videos are available at <uri xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">https://chengyaofeng.github.io/ReMake.github.io/</uri>.

title: Air quality mapping using PM2.5 sensors on urban buses
authors: Inés González-de-Castro, Mar Viana, José Javier Ramasco, Aina Maín-Nadal, Teresa Moreno
publish: IEEE Internet of Things Journal
abstract: Six low-cost air quality sensors were installed on the front roof area of three buses to enable real-time monitoring of air quality across the city of Valladolid (Spain) over a 7-month period, capturing variability in meteorological conditions and emission sources. Prior to deployment, the sensors were placed at a reference station in Barcelona (Spain) for validation and calibration. Measurements deviating by more than 30% from reference values were discarded, and correlation coefficients (R²) were calculated. After the monitoring campaign, the calibration procedure was repeated. The results (R<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup> = 0.85) suggest that bus-mounted sensors can effectively support real-time detection of urban air quality changes and contribute to detailed air quality mapping. Integrating data from reference air quality monitoring networks (AQMNs) with low-cost sensor (LCS) systems can strengthen evidence-based policymaking and help refine regulatory frameworks aimed at reducing urban air pollution.

title: JIT-DCK: A KAN Multi-Task Model for Just-In-Time Code Defect Prediction and Localization
authors: Jingwei Wang, Chenchen Li, Shenglin Wang, Hui Li, Furui Zhan, Qiao Ning, Shikai Guo
publish: ACM Transactions on Software Engineering and Methodology
abstract: None

title: Data Detection for Fully-Connected Pinching-Antenna-Assisted MU-MISO Uplink Systems With Tri-Hybrid Combining
authors: Jung-Chieh Chen, Tsu-Yu Chao
publish: IEEE Wireless Communications Letters
abstract: This letter investigates data detection for a pinching antenna (PA)-assisted multiuser multiple-input single-output (MU-MISO) uplink receiver employing a phase shifter (PS)-based fully-connected (FC) tri-hybrid combining architecture. To improve detection reliability under hardware constraints, we maximize the minimum post-equalization signal-to-interference-plus-noise ratio (SINR) by jointly optimizing the PA activation matrix, the analog radio-frequency (RF) combiner, and the digital baseband combiner, where the min-SINR criterion provides a tractable surrogate for BER minimization and emphasizes worst-user reliability. To solve the resulting mixed discrete-continuous problem, we develop a cross-entropy optimization (CEO)-based framework that explores discrete PA activation patterns and integrates Gram-Schmidt orthogonalization (GSO) with the linear minimum mean-square error (LMMSE) criterion for hybrid combiner design, termed the CEO-GSO algorithm. This letter presents the first joint optimization of discrete PA activation and hybrid combining for FC tri-hybrid PA-assisted MU-MISO uplinks. Simulation results show that the proposed FC architecture achieves BER comparable to sub-connected counterparts while using 33% fewer RF chains.

title: Semi-Supervised Learning-Based Permanent Scatterer Selection Method for GB-InSAR
authors: Weiming Tian, Longyue Wang, Yunkai Deng, Xin Xie, Changyu An
publish: IEEE Transactions on Geoscience and Remote Sensing
abstract: Permanent scatterer (PS) selection is a critical step in ground-based interferometric synthetic aperture radar (GB-InSAR) measurements. Conventional methods, often constrained by empirical thresholds, demonstrate limited capability in selecting PSs within low-coherence regions caused by deformations. Deep learning provides a data-driven alternative to characterize PS features. However, PSs lack an absolute truth standard. Current deep learning-based PS selection methods predominantly employ fully supervised learning approaches, where significant label noise exists in the training sets. This interferes with the feature learning process of networks and consequently reduces PS recall rates. To overcome these limitations, this paper proposes a semi-supervised learning-based PS selection method for GB-InSAR. By establishing a closed-loop optimization path of "refining the labels of training datasets and adjusting the strategy of model training", this method suppresses the negative impact of label noise and enables the network to accurately fit the PS features, thus enhancing selection performance. Firstly, initial reliable labels are generated via dual-threshold amplitude dispersion methods. Then, unlabeled pixels are processed through an optimal transport formulation solved via Sinkhorn-Knopp algorithm to improve label completeness. Finally, a Mean Teacher framework is introduced for semi-supervised training of the PS selection network, to systematically leverage implicit features of unlabeled data. A 3D-UNet serves as the base network for pseudo-label generation and semi-supervised training, which processes sequential interferograms to output PS selection results. Simulated and measured datasets demonstrate the method's superiority over fully supervised and conventional methods, which significantly increases PS density and quantity while ensuring quality, and it particularly exhibits superior performance in deformation regions.

title: Fabricated Parasitic-Interbridge-Free TreeFETs for CMOS Low Power Applications
authors: Yuzhe Yu, Jiaxin Yao, Lei Cao, Yu Wang, Yanzhao Wei, Jie Xu, Zhongrui Wang, Hang Zhang, Yangyang Hu, Qingzhu Zhang, Xiaolei Wang, Jun Luo, Huaxiang Yin
publish: IEEE Electron Device Letters
abstract: This letter demonstrates a novel Parasitic-Interbridge-Free TreeFET (PIF-TreeFET) structure with superior subthreshold electrostatic control ability through a hybrid technique of interbridge thinning and trimming (IBT<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup>) implemented on a compositionally graded SiGe superlattice, which is fully compatible with current GAA CMOS fabrication technologies. The fabricated devices, featuring a structure with the parasitic interbridge completely removed and an optimized channel profile comprising 3 nm thickness Si nanosheet (NS) and ~3 nm SiGe interbridge (IB), were realized with gate lengths (L<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">G</sub>) varying from 120 nm to 20 nm. At L<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">G</sub> = 120 nm, the devices exhibit an excellent subthreshold swing (SS) of 60/78 mV/dec (n/pFET), an ultralow drain-induced barrier lowering (DIBL) of 0.7/4.6 mV/V (n/pFET), and high I<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">on</sub>/I<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">off</sub> ratio of 3.4×10⁷/1.2×10⁷ (n/pFET). At L<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">G</sub> = 20 nm, the SS measures 67/90 mV/dec (n/pFET), the DIBL is 17.9/46.5 mV/V (n/pFET), and the I<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">on</sub>/I<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">off</sub> is 4.2×10⁶/2.5×10⁶ (n/pFET). The impact of the IB, supported by TCAD simulations, is also investigated. These results demonstrate an effective optimization approach for TreeFETs applied into future low power CMOS IC fabrications.

title: MonoRelief V2: Leveraging Real Data for High-Fidelity Monocular Relief Recovery
authors: Yu-Wei Zhang, Tongju Han, Lipeng Gao, Mingqiang Wei, Hui Liu, Changbao Li, Caiming Zhang
publish: IEEE Transactions on Visualization and Computer Graphics
abstract: This paper presents MonoRelief V2, an end-to-end model designed for directly recovering 2.5D reliefs from single images under complex material and illumination variations. In contrast to its predecessor, MonoRelief V1  [1], which was solely trained on synthetic data, MonoRelief V2 incorporates real data to achieve improved robustness, accuracy and efficiency. To overcome the challenge of acquiring large-scale real-world dataset, we generate approximately 15,000 pseudo-real images using a text-to-image generative model, and derive corresponding depth pseudo-labels through fusion of depth and normal predictions. Furthermore, we construct a small-scale real-world dataset (800 samples) via multi-view reconstruction and detail refinement. MonoRelief V2 is then progressively trained on the pseudo-real and real-world datasets. Comprehensive experiments demonstrate its state-of-the-art performance both in depth and normal predictions, highlighting its strong potential for a range of downstream applications. Code is at: <uri xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">https://github.com/glp1001/MonoreliefV2</uri>

title: An Efficient and Secure Smart Parking System with Conditional Preservation of Citizens Privacy for Smart Cities
authors: Mohammad Rasool Momeni, Abdollah Jabbari, Carol Fung
publish: IEEE Transactions on Network and Service Management
abstract: The ever-increasing world population and the number of vehicles in use have made it more difficult for drivers to find suitable parking lots in large cities. When public parking is insufficient, private parking space sharing could be a solution to alleviate the problem. In the context of private parking reservation, parking owners and drivers share their parking offers and inquiries that consist of private information, such as identity, parking spot, and desired location. Hence, cyber attacks and data leaks can reveal sensitive information about citizens. Therefore, it could be a major barrier to utilize private parking spots. To address this issue, we propose an efficient, secure, and privacy-preserving smart parking system. We use robust security methods, such as proxy re-encryption and certificateless public-key cryptography, to achieve security. We also employ cutting-edge privacy-enhancing technologies, such as (partially) blind signature and symmetric private information retrieval (SPIR), to preserve citizens’ privacy. Moreover, Shamir’s threshold secret sharing is used to provide conditional privacy. Comprehensive security and privacy analysis using the Random Oracle model and the Scyther tool demonstrates that our design is robust against relevant attacks and effectively protects citizens’ privacy. Ultimately, our performance analysis indicates that the proposed scheme is efficient, lightweight, and feasible. In particular, it achieves an average reduction of approximately 73% in communication overhead.

title: Design and Analysis of a 13.7–41 GHz Ultra-Wideband Frequency Doubler With Cross-Coupled Push-Push Structure
authors: Kai Li, Keping Wang, Fanyi Meng, Kiat Seng Yeo, Kaixue Ma
publish: IEEE Journal of Solid-State Circuits
abstract: This article presents a 13.7–41 GHz ultra-wideband frequency doubler with high efficiency and conversion gain (CG). The proposed cross-coupled push-push structure, in conjunction with the fourth-order transformer-based resonator and the series gate inductor, collaboratively shapes the input signal amplitude such that three distinct peaks emerge at different frequencies, thereby significantly improving the bandwidth. By introducing the resistor <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$R_{C}$</tex-math> </inline-formula> and inductor <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$L_{S}$</tex-math> </inline-formula> into the proposed cross-coupled structure, the in-band flatness can be finely tuned with minimal impact on the frequency of the third peak, thereby offering an additional degree of freedom for flatness optimization. To improve the second harmonic output power and efficiency, a st acked common-gate amplifier is introduced. Furthermore, a coupled-inductor-based output matching network is designed to further extend the bandwidth to low frequencies. Fabricated in a 40 nm CMOS process, the proposed frequency doubler achieves a3-dB bandwidth of 27.3 GHz (fractional bandwidth of 99.8%), a 1.5-dB bandwidth of 24.5 GHz (fractional bandwidth of 91.6%), and a peak CG of −2.7 dB at an input power of 3 dBm. The measured saturated output power is 7 dBm, with a peak drain efficiency of 14.1% and a peak total efficiency of 10.6%. Across the entire bandwidth, the fundamental harmonic rejection ratio (HRR) and the third HRR are greater than 21.3 and 35.6 dBc, respectively.

title: Cooperative Reinforced Resilient Federated Learning for Clients with Multiple Working Conditions
authors: Teng Cui, Wei Dai, Haijun Zhang
publish: IEEE Transactions on Reliability
abstract: Collaborative modeling in industrial processes with similar production procedures and complex industrial parameters is an effective approach to address issues about online soft measurement. However, it is a challenging task for collaborative modeling at the presence of non-independent and identically distributed (Non-IID) data, which is arising from variations in production materials and equipment. In this paper, we propose a cooperative reinforced resilient federated learning for clients with multiple working conditions to solve the Non-IID problem by implementing a personalized strategy centered on local data storage, which can accurately build personalized models suitable for distributed data on a single client. Inspired by the success of multi-agent reinforcement learning (MARL) in solving complex decision problems, we design a personalized aggregation mechanism guided by MARL and modeling process information. This mechanism optimizes model aggregation decisions based on data distribution and quality, thereby improving the effectiveness of the training process and enhancing the overall performance of personalized models. Meanwhile, we design an exploration and reward strategy for federated scenarios with distribution differences, which utilizes the collaborative modeling characteristics and advantages of federated learning to improve the exploration efficiency of reinforcement learning in solving personalized federated problems. Experimental results conducted on industry and benchmark datasets demonstrate that the proposed model surpasses existing methods in collaborative modeling under Non IID scenarios.

title: Heterophily-based Dynamic Hypergraph for Semi-Supervised Classification and its Application in UAV Fault Diagnosis_supp1-3672585.pdf
authors: Ying Zheng
publish: N/A
abstract: Hypergraph neural network (HGNN) excels at representing intricate relationships within non-Euclidean infinitedimensional spaces. However, traditional hypergraph frameworks are grounded in homophily assumption and encounter challenges in handling heterophily problem in semi-supervised classification tasks. To tackle this problem, this paper first analyzes the underlying reasons that limit homophily-based hypergraphs, and proposes a heterophily-based dynamic structure. Under this structure, a Heterophily-based Dynamic HGNN (HDHGNN) is then proposed for semi-supervised classification. HDHGNN decomposes the hypergraph’s triple pattern iterative process into two causally related sub-procedures, within which attention mechanisms are incorporated to enable dynamic interactions. To evaluate the effectiveness of HDHGNN, a relative entropybased heterophily degree evaluation method is first introduced, followed by comprehensive fault classification experiments on two real-world flight datasets from rotary-wing and fixed-wing unmanned aerial vehicles, with comparisons against 11 state-of-the-art methods.

title: AAAC 2025 Author Index
authors: N/A
publish: 2025 3rd Asian Aerospace and Astronautics Conference (AAAC)

title: Diffusion-Based mmWave Radar Point Cloud Enhancement Driven by Range Images
authors: Ruixin Wu, Zihan Li, Jin Wang, Xiangyu Xu, Zhi Zheng, Kaixiang Huang, Guodong Lu
publish: IEEE Robotics and Automation Letters
abstract: Millimeter-wave (mmWave) radar has attracted significant attention in robotics and autonomous driving due to its robustness in harsh environments. However, the radar point clouds are typically sparse and noisy, which limits its futher development. Traditional mmWave radar enhancement approaches often struggle to leverage the effectiveness of diffusion models in super-resolution, largely due to the unnatural range-azimuth heatmap (RAH) or bird's eye view (BEV) representation. To address this issue, we pioneer the integration of range image representations into an image diffusion framework that leverages pre-trained image diffusion priors to generate dense and accurate 3D mmWave radar point clouds with LiDAR-like quality. Extensive evaluations on both public datasets and self-constructed datasets demonstrate that our approach provides substantial improvements, establishing a new state-of-the-art performance in generating truly three-dimensional LiDAR-like point clouds via mmWave radar.

title: Visual Sculpting: Visually-Aligned Planning Representations for Long-Horizon Robot Clay Sculpting
authors: Peter Schaldenbrand, Jean Oh
publish: IEEE Robotics and Automation Letters
abstract: Clay sculpting is a nuanced, artistic task involving dexterous manipulation with long-horizon planning to achieve high-level goals. As a robotics problem, we formulate clay sculpting as a shape-to-shape matching challenge. Prior deformable object manipulation work either requires retraining a policy per goal or relies on dynamics models which represent state as sparse point clouds which do not capture important clay features, such as textures, well. We present a method for modeling the dynamics of deformable materials and planning for robotic sculpting in a representation that is visually-aligned, capturing lighting and texture features. With three different deformable materials and various end-effectors, we demonstrate that our dynamics model is comparable in performance to the state-of-the-art with the added benefit of being compatible with visual planning. Our actions are represented as parametrized pushes into clay with a single end-effector, which proved to be suitable for long-horizon (<inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$>$</tex-math></inline-formula>100 actions) clay relief sculptures. Lastly, we show the benefits of planning in a visually-aligned representation, but also provide analysis providing evidence as to why this representation is challenging to plan in compared to 3D representations.

title: A Single-Stage Bridgeless AC/DC Resonant Converter with Distributed Switch Losses and Inherent Power Factor Correction Capability
authors: Waqar Uddin, Hafiz Furqan Ahmed, Kamran Zeb, Mohammad A. Abido, Muhammad Khalid
publish: IEEE Journal of Emerging and Selected Topics in Power Electronics
abstract: This paper proposed a bridgeless AC-DC resonant converter with uniformly distributed switch losses. By replacing the bridge rectifier with a given configuration, the converter naturally achieves the uniform distribution at the primary switches in a full grid cycle, thus improving the reliability. The converter features a half-bridge structure on the primary side, with switches operating at a constant duty cycle of 50%. This layout maintains the clamp capacitor’s voltage at 2|vg|, enabling the use of a small film capacitor and obviating the need for a large one. Furthermore, it helps the converter achieve a unity power factor inherently. In addition, a semi-bridgeless configuration reduces the conduction losses of the input rectifier by half. An active resonant voltage doubler circuit with an AC switch is used on the secondary side. To achieve the desired results, controlling only the duty of an AC switch, making the control process simple and straightforward. All the switches of the proposed circuit are turned on with almost ZVS, and the AC switch is turned off with low voltage, thus further reducing the losses of the proposed converter and achieving the efficiency of 96.6%. We validate the results of the proposed AC-DC converter by developing a 444 W laboratory prototype.

title: A Remedial Course in Moodle for Undergraduate Classes of Circuit Theory
authors: Paolo Manfredi, Enrico Perano
publish: IEEE Revista Iberoamericana de Tecnologias del Aprendizaje
abstract: E-learning courses and tools have increased their importance and have been adopted by a growing number of universities, mainly due to the unfortunate occurrence of the COVID-19 pandemic. This work presents an asynchronous and online remedial course implemented in Moodle to assist students enrolled in undergraduate engineering classes of electrical circuit theory. The course is organized in weekly modules that are delivered in parallel to regular classes. Each module consists of a preliminary test to ascertain the student’s preparation on the topic and learning units whose access is restricted to students who fail the preliminary test. The approach is suitable for large classes and is aimed to avoid students’ procrastination and promote additional and constant practice throughout the course, thereby eventually improving also their performance at the final examination as well as their preparation for subsequent courses involving the same topics. Two experiments conducted in consecutive academic years, involving 407 and 379 students, are discussed. The exam performance of students who attended the remedial classes, even those with low initial performance, was significantly better compared to those who did not participate at all. Additionally, the correlation with past exam scores demonstrates that students who engage with the Moodle course show substantial improvements in their exam scores.

title: Bridging the Reality Gap in ASR for Low-Resource Dysarthric Speech: Evaluating Performance on Synthetic and Real Data_supp3-3673388.pdf
authors: Bozena Kostek
publish: N/A

title: Integrated Control of Parallel Connected Multiple SEPIC Converters in DC Microgrid Operation.
authors: Manzar Ali, Omar Zeb, Taosif Iqbal, Iftikhar Ahmad, Salman Qadir
publish: IEEE Access
abstract: This paper proposes a novel control strategy for the parallel operation of Single-Ended Primary-Inductor Converter (SEPIC) converters in DC microgrids, focusing on accurate voltage regulation, balanced current sharing, and improved disturbance rejection. The proposed Optimal LQR-Based Observer Control (OLBOC) combines Linear Quadratic Regulator (LQR) optimization with an Extended State Observer (ESO) to achieve precise state estimation and robust performance. Unlike traditional methods, this approach eliminates the need for droop control or active current-sharing mechanisms, significantly simplifying the system architecture while maintaining high efficiency and reliability. The state-space model incorporates the Equivalent Series Resistance (ESR) of SEPIC inductors, improving the accuracy of dynamic behavior analysis and ensuring robustness against component non-idealities. The DC microgrid integrates a solar-based renewable energy source, where an Artificial Neural Network (ANN)-based Maximum Power Point Tracking (MPPT) algorithm optimizes power extraction through a SEPIC converter. A battery storage system is included, with charging and discharging regulated by PI controllers to ensure stable energy management under varying power conditions. The proposed control scheme is validated through comprehensive MATLAB/Simulink simulations and Controller Hardware-in-the-Loop (CHIL) experiments. The results demonstrate superior performance, including precise voltage tracking, balanced current sharing, and robust operation under disturbances. The OLBOC strategy represents a significant advancement in DC microgrid control, offering a simplified, high-performance solution for renewable energy-integrated systems. Its ability to achieve optimal voltage regulation and current sharing without complex control mechanisms makes it a promising approach for modern power electronics applications.

title: A 4.21-kW Ultra-Thin 1.2-mm Stackable Capacitive Coupler for High-Efficiency MHz Capacitive Power Transfer
authors: Yue Wu, Haonan Song, Yuchong Peng, Yucheng Shen, Fei Lu, Hua Zhang
publish: IEEE Transactions on Power Electronics
abstract: This paper presents a 1.2-mm-thick ultra-thin stacked capacitive coupler (SCC) that provides a lightweight and compact isolated power conversion interface for space constrained MHz high-power applications. Unlike conventional four-plate and six-plate capacitive couplers in capacitive power transfer (CPT) systems that rely on horizontally distributed electrodes, the proposed SCC introduces a vertically stacked multi-layer structure with independently insulated copper-foil plates, which suppresses nonadjacent cross-coupling effectively and significantly enhances the mutual capacitance.
