Its four-capacitor equivalent model is derived, and comprehensive voltage-stress and safety analyses are performed to guide plate arrangement and design at MHz frequencies. A 1-MHz SCC prototype achieves 4.21-kW peak power, 95.29% peak dc–dc efficiency, 99.87% peak ac–ac efficiency, and a power density of 83.16 kW/m² for the active capacitive coupling area, demonstrating excellent compactness and energy transfer capability. High frequency voltage and thermal endurance tests and FEM simulations further validate the SCC's reliability. Owing to its ultra-thin profile, high-voltage endurance, simple fabrication, and high power density, the proposed SCC serves as a promising modular power supply solution for compact power modular, transportation electrification and data center power supply.

title: A True 100% Duty-Cycle Buck Converter Based on Fully Integrated Decoupled Gate Driver for Battery-Powered Applications
authors: Junying Chen, Linyun Yu, Yongyuan Li, Wei Guo, Yongbo Zhang, Yong You, Zhangming Zhu
publish: IEEE Transactions on Power Electronics
abstract: The 100% duty cycle operation extends battery runtime under extreme conditions by eliminating switching losses, while enhancing transient performance. Despite its clear advantages, achieving true 100% duty cycle operation is challenging and largely restricted by the power switches and gate driver architectures of the converters. This article presents a true 100% duty-cycle buck converter enabled by a fully integrated decoupled charge pump gate (DCPG) driver for battery-powered applications. In 100% mode, the pulsed charging of charge pump replaces the periodic charging and discharging of the gate capacitance, thereby removing switching losses. Besides, the decoupled driving scheme offers inherent dv/dt immunity by isolating the switching node LX from the driver, and a pre-charging mechanism that reduces the capacitance of bootstrap capacitor for cost-effective integration. To balance driving-speed, EMI noise and power loss, a dual-stage driving current is adopted. The design is fabricated in a 180 nm BCD process. Measurement results verify correct 100% duty cycle operation, achieving a peak efficiency of 96%. With 100% duty cycle operation, the output voltage shows a 30 mV undershoot voltage and a 7 μs settling time under a 0.1-3A load step.

title: Reactive Power and Voltage Balanced Control Strategy for Wind Farms Considering the Differences Between Grid-Following and Grid-Forming
authors: Jingbo Liu, Tao Jiang, Hui Liu, Linlin Wu, Changjiang Wang, Yuhui Wu
publish: 2025 IEEE 9th Conference on Energy Internet and Energy System Integration (EI2)
abstract: Traditional grid-following wind turbines lack active voltage support capabilities, while grid-forming units, adopting voltage source self-synchronizing control methods, possess the ability to autonomously establish voltage and frequency. Hybrid grid-following/grid-forming wind power stations can achieve both voltage stability and efficient power generation in wind farm aggregation areas, representing a key paradigm for constructing new-type power systems. However, significant differences exist between grid-following and grid-forming wind turbines in terms of response mechanisms, control modes, and support characteristics. The response coupling during dynamic regulation processes makes balanced reactive power and voltage control in hybrid wind farms particularly challenging. To address these challenges, this paper first analyzes the control structures and regulation characteristics of grid-following/grid-forming units, clarifying applicable scenarios for different device types. Subsequently, it investigates the reactive power-voltage distribution characteristics and voltage support requirements in wind farms, proposing a reactive power allocation strategy for hybrid wind farms that coordinates turbine terminal voltage control with smooth system-wide voltage regulation. This strategy achieves balanced voltage control across the wind farm. Finally, a simulation test platform for hybrid grid-following/grid-forming wind farms is established, validating the effectiveness of the proposed strategy.

title: Power System Stochastic Dynamic Safety: A Review
authors: Yudun Li, Na Li, Fangbeier Gu, Zheng Xu, Zhipeng Cong, Yiping Yu
publish: 2025 IEEE 9th Conference on Energy Internet and Energy System Integration (EI2)
abstract: With the continuous promotion and application of renewable energy sources such as wind power and photoelectric energy and new loads such as electric vehicles, the share of stochastic power sources and loads in the grid is increasing, and so is the intensity of their disturbances, posing a threat to the grid’s dynamic security. Therefore, it is essential to analyze in detail the dynamic security of power systems under stochastic excitation and evaluate their potential risks. According to the stochasticity of different sources of power system, the paper describes and summarizes the analysis methods from three aspects: source, variable type and time scale. For stochastic dynamics of power systems, perturbation model, system model and theoretical method are reviewed. Aiming at stochastic dynamics of power system, the influence of stochasticity on system dynamics is summarized and discussed by means of probability analysis from two aspects of oscillation and safety.

title: Research on the Identification of Order Parameters for the Self-Organizing Evolution of China's Energy System Based on Adiabatic Elimination Method
authors: Jing Zhou, XiaoQing Yan, Xue Ma, Fang Li, RuiJian Liu
publish: 2025 IEEE 9th Conference on Energy Internet and Energy System Integration (EI2)
abstract: In view of the crucial guiding role of order parameters in the self-organizing evolution of China's energy system, in accordance with the principles of synergetic, the adiabatic elimination method is employed to quantitatively identify the order parameters of China's energy system. Through this research, the order parameters of China's energy system are obtained, and a synergetic mechanics equation for China's energy system based on these order parameters is established.

title: Research on Next Generation O&M Platform for urban Power and Energy Facilities Based on CIM
authors: Lijuan Duan, Baoju Li, Tian Xia, Xiangdong Meng
publish: 2025 IEEE 9th Conference on Energy Internet and Energy System Integration (EI2)
abstract: The continuous healthy operation of power energy facilities is a crucial prerequisite for ensuring the safety and stability of the power system. In response to the characteristics of the new power system, such as a large number of devices, extensive distribution, and complex operating environments, traditional equipment operation and maintenance (O&M) methods face challenges like passive responses, insufficient auxiliary decision- making, and low maintenance efficiency. This article analyzes the new operational features of equipment in the new power system, elaborates on the new requirements for precise perception of equipment status, health assessment, and O&M support capabilities, and proposes and designs an O&M platform based on City Information Model technology. The article also explains the platform's architectural design and core business scenarios, and explores the application of technologies such as AI and large models within the platform. This platform aims to enhance the efficiency and reliability of equipment O&M in the new power system, reduce O&M costs, and provide new ideas and approaches for the maintenance of power energy facilities under the evolving context.

title: Comparative Analysis of Clustering Algorithms in High-Dimensional Biomedical Applications
authors: Mustafa Shareef Khanjar, Yasir Adnan Hussein, Anmar Yahya Taher, Saad Mutashar, Omar Sameer Khalis, Adnan Hussein Ali
publish: 2026 Sixth International Conference on Advances in Electrical, Computing, Communications and Sustainable Technologies (ICAECT)
abstract: High-dimensional data in biomedicine such as single-cell RNA-seq (scRNA-seq), imaging, multi-omics data, pose unique challenges for clustering analysis. In this work, we performed an extensive empirical comparison for a representative set of clustering algorithms, including k-Means, Hierarchical clustering, Gaussian Mixture Models, DBSCAN, spectral clustering, and deep clustering approaches on biomedical data. We tested each algorithm on internal and external metrics as well as stability and computational performance. Our finding suggest that Spectral Clustering, Deep Clustering methods consistently find clusters which are more biologically interpretable and less sensitive, however, all at the cost of higher computational complexity. These results contribute useful insights on when to use which algorithm in highdimensional biomedical applications, trading clustering quality for scalability and interpretability.

title: Research on the Identification Method of Background Harmonic Parameters on the AC Side of LCC-HVDC Converter Station Based on the Improved Complex Frequency Domain Robust Regression Method
authors: Zhaoliang Li, Jin Zhang, Lei Zhao, Fangyi Li, Yaqiong Li, Haijun Liu
publish: 2025 IEEE 9th Conference on Energy Internet and Energy System Integration (EI2)
abstract: Taking the parameter identification on the AC side of the converter station as the research objective, an equivalent circuit model for parameter identification was established, with the AC side of the converter station as the equivalent system side and the converter station and its downstream power grid as the user side. Among them, for the impedance parameters on the equivalent system side, the core ideas, advantages and disadvantages of common methods such as the fluctuation method, blind source separation method, probability method, and regression method were sorted out. Focusing on the problem that the harmonic voltage fluctuation on the AC side of the converter station makes it difficult for traditional identification methods to accurately calculate the impedance parameters, complex number correlation analysis is used to screen the data window of the dominant fluctuation on the system side, and an improved complex number domain robust regression algorithm based on the Huber-ESL mixed weight function is proposed. Compared with the traditional regression algorithm that calculates the real part and the imaginary part separately, The regression class method in the complex field can facilitate the search for the overall optimal solution of complex numbers. Meanwhile, compared with the traditional method based on the single weight function of Huber, namely the m-robust estimation method, the proposed Huber-ESL hybrid weight function method can improve the robustness in dealing with singular values during the regression process. Based on the research of equivalent models and calculation methods, the feasibility and effectiveness of the proposed method for identifying parameters on the AC side of the converter station are verified from two perspectives: simulation of working conditions and analysis of measured data.

title: Principal Component Analysis of Data When Processing Requests for Solving Computer Program Support Tasks
authors: Elena Makarova, Vitaly Martynov, Elena Zakieva, Elvira Gabdullina, Nataliya Khasanova, Nikita Ponomarev
publish: 2025 5th International Siberian Scientific Workshop on Data Analysis Technologies with Applications (SibDATA)
abstract: The article is devoted to solving the problem of data analysis when processing requests for solving computer program support tasks. The methods of principal component, cluster analysis and building decision trees have been applied. A procedure for data mining during request processing has been developed. Principal component analysis of the data has been performed. It is shown that it is necessary to perform three options of cluster construction in three planes defined by the principal components. Clusters have been constructed according to scattering diagrams in the plane of the first two principal components, taking into account the level of complexity of the main and additional tasks; the time spent on their solution, taking into account the qualifications of the specialist. The cluster features have been highlighted, reflecting the insufficient compliance of the specialist's qualifications with the level of complexity of the request and the presence of errors in decision-making when appointing a specialist. Decision tree has been constructed and analyzed. Clusters have been constructed according to scattering diagrams in the plane of the first and third principal components, and also in the plane of the first and fourth principal components. Comparative analysis of all three clustering options has been carried out, and an expanded summary description of the clusters has been formed, taking into account the client's level of knowledge, as well as previously obtained results. It is advisable to use the received results for the development of a fuzzy expert system

title: Analysis of Power user Payment Behavior Based on Peak Optimization K-means Clustering Algorithm
authors: Liangzhi Yin, Jifu Ren, Nuodi Wang, Xiaopeng Li, Keyang Tan, Bohan Cha
publish: 2025 IEEE 9th Conference on Energy Internet and Energy System Integration (EI2)
abstract: Aiming at improving the marketing service quality of power grid companies, optimizing the marketing service environment, insufficient service timeliness, and increasing the proportion of user-related complaints, this paper deeply studies the methods suitable for analyzing the payment behavior of power users in power grid services. Firstly, the power user behavior data of the power grid is preprocessed, and the characteristics of the power user data are extracted. Then, combined with the characteristics of the power user payment behavior, the VANMT power user payment model is constructed. Based on the above model, the peak optimization K-means clustering algorithm is proposed, and the effectiveness of the proposed modeling method is verified by simulation analysis, and the users are divided.

title: Harnessing Ensemble Learning for Dementia Detection from Speech Patterns
authors: Priyanka G, Amshakala K
publish: 2026 Sixth International Conference on Advances in Electrical, Computing, Communications and Sustainable Technologies (ICAECT)
abstract: Dementia is an progressive neurological disorder that leads to deteriorations in intellectual ability, behavior, and the capability to carry out daily activities, particularly among the elderly. According to global estimates, a substantial portion of the population is influenced by this condition. Since at present there is no known therapy for dementia, initial recognition stands both possible and critical for refining quality of life and care planning. In current years, deep learning has arisen as a significant tool in the early prediction of dementia, with frequent research studies exploring its potential. This study leverages speech transcripts of patients perceived with dementia, which were sourced from the DementiaBank repository. These transcripts are obtained from recorded interviews between dementia patients and clinical experts, providing valuable linguistic and cognitive measures. To analyze this data, we proposed a deep learning ensemble model that combines BERT, LSTM, and XLNet models-known for their progressive abilities in implementation of natural language. The utility of deep learning in diagnosing dementia from speech patterns deeply subjected to robust optimization strategies. Such strategies are significant to fine-tune model parameters, handle data variability, reduce overfitting, and progress both accuracy and interpretability. In this work, two optimization algorithms-AdamW and Adafactor-are employed to boost the learning method of the proposed model. The model's performance was analyzed by means of a 5-fold cross-validation technique, and results confirmed that the BERT-LSTM-XLNet ensemble model, when optimized with AdamW, accomplished an remarkable accuracy of 93%. This highlights the importance of coupling deep learning models with powerful optimization techniques for reliable and high- performing dementia prediction.

title: Scientometric Analysis of Data Privacy and Cloud Security: Research Trends in Access Control and Encryption Techniques
authors: R. Archana Reddy, Syed Nawaz Pasha, D Sushma Kumari
publish: 2026 Sixth International Conference on Advances in Electrical, Computing, Communications and Sustainable Technologies (ICAECT)
abstract: Data Privacy and Cloud Security are two inseparable components of contemporary digital infrastructure that allow sensitive data stored or processed in a cloud-based environment to be protected against unauthorized access, abuse, or loss. The purpose of the paper is to synthetically examine the research trends, collaboration networks, and thematic developments in the world in terms of access control and encryption methods through the method of scientometrics. Although there is a lot of literature on cryptography and secure cloud frameworks, the existing literature is still divided among various fields, with no coherent knowledge of the latest trends, interdisciplinary connections, and new security models. To fill this gap, bibliometric data from the Scopus database (20202025) were examined using VOSviewer, CiteSpace, Excel, and OriginPro. The analysis demonstrates that 694 publications are in 165 sources with an annual growth rate of 36.26, highlighting the fact that cloud security research is increasing rapidly. India and China became the most productive participants, and major topics such as blockchain-powered access control, hybrid encryption, and AI-powered privacy frameworks were introduced. The analysis of keywords and citations revealed research clusters in federated learning, post-quantum cryptography, and decentralized security models. The findings provide a thorough mapping of the intellectual organization of research on cloud security and point to further directions of explainable, energy-efficient, and regulation-consistent encryption systems for reliable cloud ecosystems.

title: Two-Stage Conditional Value-at-Risk Based Coordinated Trading Method for Steel Industrial Park IES in Electricity and Carbon Markets
authors: Rui Yuan, Haihang Sun, Shengduo Gao, Qilin Han
publish: 2025 IEEE 9th Conference on Energy Internet and Energy System Integration (EI2)
abstract: Steel industry has significant potential for energy saving and carbon emission reduction. However, these enterprises are not motivated enough to interact with the grid supply and demand during production operations, and it is difficult to balance the interests of all parties. Meanwhile, the volatility of real-time electricity prices in the power market may lead to operational costs exceeding expectations for the park. Therefore, this paper proposes a two-stage electricity-carbon market trading method for the Integrated Energy System (IES) of a steel industrial park that considers Conditional Value-at-Risk (CVaR). This method aims to provide a decision-making reference for the park's production and operation that balances reliability and economy, thereby achieving low-carbon operation. The model responds to the day-ahead market forecast electricity price and the ladder-type carbon trading mechanism in the day-ahead market for day-ahead scheduling and planning. In the real-time market, it responds to real-time electricity prices and the same carbon trading mechanism. By introducing the CVaR method, the objective function is reformulated to analyze the park's participation in the coordinated electricity-carbon market trading. This problem can be transformed into a mixed-integer linear programming (MILP) problem, which is easily solvable using existing commercial solvers. Finally, based on the production data from a steel industrial park in Yunnan Province, the proposed two-stage trading method for Steel Industrial Park IES to participate in the coordinated electricity-carbon market is validated. This approach provides a decision-making reference for the park's operation that balances reliability and economy, thereby facilitating the achievement of low-carbon operation.

title: Multiobjective Design of an Off-Grid Renewable-Based Microgrid System for UAV Power Supply
authors: Shuhao Zhao, Yaobin Zheng, Yiwen Chen, Zhichao Shi, Kaiwen Li, Rui Wang, Ze Liu
publish: 2025 IEEE 9th Conference on Energy Internet and Energy System Integration (EI2)
abstract: With the rapid development of unmanned aerial vehicle (UAV) technology, UAVs have been widely applied in communication relay, power inspection, and geographic mapping scenarios. The premise for UAVs to perform tasks efficiently lies in reliable electrical energy support, especially in remote mountainous areas and other regions far from the power grid. Aiming to address the issue of electrical energy support for UAVs undertaking communication support tasks, this study proposes an off-grid microgrid system design method based on wind-solar renewable energy sources. Specifically, by considering two objectives—the coverage area of UAV task execution and the investment cost of the microgrid system—a multi-objective planning model for the microgrid, which includes wind, solar, and energy storage units, is developed. Meanwhile, an improved NSGA-II algorithm based on Q-learning and variable neighborhood search (VNS) is designed to solve the model. Finally, experimental studies are conducted based on real wind, solar, and UAV-related data, verifying the effectiveness of the proposed model and algorithm.

title: Economic analysis of distributed energy storage system for voltage regulation in low-voltage distribution networks with rooftop Photovoltaics
authors: Zhongjie Wang, Linjue Yang, Juan Chen, Yong Peng, Shun Zhang, Jinrui Tang
publish: 2025 IEEE 9th Conference on Energy Internet and Energy System Integration (EI2)
abstract: Distributed energy storage system (ESS) is an effective device to regulate voltage in low-voltage distribution networks (LVDNs) with high-penetration rooftop photovoltaics. Traditional methods are focused on the control strategies of distributed ESSs without discussing about the economic benefits during the voltage regulation process. In this paper, economic analysis model of distributed ESSs in LVDNs for voltage regulation is constructed and used in one typical case to demonstrate the investment payback period for the ESSs under different construction costs. The benefits of distributed ESSs during the voltage regulation include the revenue generated by charging during off-peak hours and discharging during peak hours under time-of-use electricity price, and the additional electricity revenue by reducing power losses through charging and discharging of distributed ESSs. The research results show that distributed ESSs deployed for voltage regulation can recover their own costs through energy revenue alone when the unit cost of distributed ESSs falls below 0.7 yuan/Wh.

title: Modular Multi-Leg Foundations for Wind Turbines in Polar Regions: Design and Performance Analysis
authors: Wei Du, Zhen Wang, Wenrui Ying, Jingquan Wang, Bin Wang
publish: 2025 IEEE 9th Conference on Energy Internet and Energy System Integration (EI2)
abstract: In polar environments, traditional cast-in-place wind turbine foundations face long construction times, high costs, and logistical challenges. This study systematically investigated the foundation design and structural performance of a proposed wind turbine project in such an environment. Three years of measured wind speed data were analyzed, and wind speeds at 2 m and 4 m heights were converted to the reference height of 10 m using a logarithmic wind profile. A two-parameter Weibull distribution was fitted to the wind speed probability density, yielding a 20-year return wind speed of 61.08 m/s, providing a reliable design basis. Considering the turbine’s structural parameters and site conditions, three foundation schemes were proposed: wet assembled concrete, dry assembled concrete, and steel structure foundations, all employing the multi-pile anchor foundation to optimize constructability and load-bearing performance. Finite element analyses indicate that the concrete foundations exhibit superior stress distribution and stiffness, while the steel foundation maintains relatively low overall stress levels. All three schemes satisfy the strength and stiffness requirements under extreme polar wind conditions and provide a safety margin, offering a practical reference for future cross-section optimization and structural economy enhancement.

title: Deep Learning-Driven Neuro-Pedagogical Interface for Real-Time Brainwave-Adaptive Teaching in Immersive Learning Environments
authors: Abhishek Kumar Mishra, Sai Kumar Reddy Nossam, Mylavarapu Kalyan Ram, Preeti Y Shadangi, S.Suresh Kumar, B.Anitha Vijayalakshmi
publish: 2026 Sixth International Conference on Advances in Electrical, Computing, Communications and Sustainable Technologies (ICAECT)
abstract: Personalized learning systems tend to fail to learn dynamically about learners' mental states and changing abilities. Current EEG-based systems fail to be real-time adaptive and reward-optimized, hence unable to offer genuine personalized learning trajectories. Towards addressing these shortcomings, the Self-Evolving Neural Intelligence Tutor guided was introduce by Deep Q-Network (DQN) reinforcement learning, which incorporates curriculum-aware feedback loops and real-time EEG signal processing. Deployed with Python and TensorFlow, the model was trained and tested on the English Learning ITS Performance Dataset (Kaggle) with a 93.6% accuracy, surpassing previous models by 5.4 %. The DQN agent learns instructional methods based on dynamic reward feedback from learners' performance and EEG-based attention measures. Through continuous policy learning, this selfevolving framework makes the tutor responsive and intelligent between learning sessions. This approach defines a new paradigm for intelligent, explainable, and adaptive learning systems that adapt to learner behavior and enable competencybased skill acquisition.

title: Analysis and Optimization of Cross-Cutting Processes of an Educational Institution Using a Digital Double Based on Process Mining Methods
authors: Dorrer Mikhail, Masyuk M.A.
publish: 2025 5th International Siberian Scientific Workshop on Data Analysis Technologies with Applications (SibDATA)
abstract: The paper presents the experience of building a digital twin of an educational organization's business processes based on Process Mining. A comprehensive analysis of the student's life cycle processes has been implemented based on the analysis of event protocols from the CRM system. The results revealed critical deviations in the enrollment and payment processes and allowed for the classification of students' behavioral patterns. The developed solution fills the research gap between traditional Educational Process Mining and the concept of Digital Process Twin.

title: Hydrodynamic Study of Oscillating Water Column Device with Circular Front Wall in Oblique Wave Conditions
authors: Santanu Kumar Dash, Kailash Chand Swami, Santanu Koley
publish: 2026 Sixth International Conference on Advances in Electrical, Computing, Communications and Sustainable Technologies (ICAECT)
abstract: The present study explores the optimal hydrodynamic performance of a land-fixed oscillating water column (OWC) device with a quarter-circular front wall and bottom foundations under oblique wave incidence. The primary focus is on maximizing power extraction efficiency across a broad range of frequencies. By employing linear water wave theory and numerical techniques based on the boundary element method (BEM), the research investigates how key structural parameters—such as chamber width, quarter-circular radius, and incident angles—affect energy transfer and efficiency. The results indicate that configurations with moderate chamber width and submergence-to-draft ratio enhance performance by improving wave energy absorption and minimizing air losses. The influence of oblique wave angles (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\theta$</tex>) reveals that smaller angles enhance wave energy coupling, whereas larger angles broaden resonance bandwidths, stabilizing energy capture at the expense of peak efficiency. In contrast, rippled bottom profiles with moderate amplitude provide balanced performance across a wide frequency spectrum. Moreover, seabed undulations characterized by ripple amplitude (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$m$</tex>) alter resonance conditions by shifting resonance peaks to lower wave numbers and intensifying hydrodynamic force fluctuations. These findings offer valuable insights for advancing the design of OWC devices and optimizing energy capture and conversion in diverse coastal and offshore environments.

title: Voltage Security Risk Early Warning and Coordinated Topology Reconfiguration with Distributed Generation in Active Distribution Networks
authors: Zhaochu Guo, Xiaobo Dou, Xiaoyuan Wang, Shuang Ni
publish: 2025 IEEE 9th Conference on Energy Internet and Energy System Integration (EI2)
abstract: In response to the operational risks and regulatory challenges brought about by the large-scale integration of distributed photovoltaic (PV) systems into distribution networks, this paper proposes a risk assessment and hierarchical early warning mechanism for active distribution networks, based on multi-dimensional risk indicators. The mechanism quantitatively evaluates risks such as undervoltage, overvoltage, and power flow violations at both the node and system levels. To address challenges such as local power flow reversal and voltage anomalies under high levels of PV penetration, this paper proposes a collaborative optimization approach for distribution networks and distributed generation that combines topology reconfiguration with phase-shifting transformers (PSTs). Through the flexible adjustment of network structure and the regulation of voltage phase angles, the method effectively resolves power flow and voltage issues that arise under a wide range of operating conditions, including loop closing events. The results of numerical case studies demonstrate that the proposed method can significantly lower system risk levels, improve node voltage quality and the economic efficiency of distribution networks, effectively control node and line violation risks, substantially reduce impact current during loop closing, and enhance the operational safety margin of the system.

title: Recognition of Photovoltaic Power Fluctuation Patterns Based on Spatiotemporal Dynamic Graph Attention Networks and Enhanced Clustering
authors: Mao Yang, Yingjie Liu, Yitao Li
publish: 2025 IEEE 9th Conference on Energy Internet and Energy System Integration (EI2)
abstract: Fluctuations in photovoltaic (PV) weather day types significantly impact the safe and stable operation of power systems and the selection of power forecasting models. However, identifying PV power fluctuations with ideal accuracy is often challenging due to numerical forecasted errors and incomplete local meteorological information. To address this, this paper proposes a recognition method for PV power fluctuation pattern based on spatiotemporal dynamic graph attention network (STDGAN). It constructs an improved clustering distance based on sample entropy (Se) similarity to accurately establish an initial fluctuation sample set. Proposed a component extraction method based on Circuit Singular Spectral Decomposition (CISSD) for efficiently constructing fluctuation modes across different PV stations. Developed a dynamic adjacency matrix generation method based on time-varying fluctuation similarity to describe the synchrony of power fluctuation patterns between stations. The STDGAN is used to model target stations and identify their complex fluctuation patterns. Applying the proposed method to a PV cluster in Gansu Province, China, results demonstrate that the proposed fluctuation pattern recognition method achieves improvements of 10.78%, 8.97%, 40.41%, and 10.06% over traditional methods in terms of accuracy, precision, recall, and F1 score, respectively.

title: Online Identification of a Photovoltaic-Integrated Comprehensive Load Model Composition Based on Deep Neural Networks
authors: Ying Cheng, Wei Dong, Zhentao Jiang, Yi Tang, Changyou Feng
publish: 2025 IEEE 9th Conference on Energy Internet and Energy System Integration (EI2)
abstract: Real-time precise identification of load composition has a significant impact on power system simulation analysis. Current identification processes based on traditional optimization methods struggle to handle multi-dimensional time-series features and are computationally time-consuming, leading to insufficient identification accuracy and unable to meet online application requirements. Based on the feature extraction capabilities of deep neural networks, this paper proposes an online identification method suitable for active integrated load models. Firstly, an active integrated load model incorporating photovoltaic and energy storage is proposed from a mechanistic perspective. Then, a feature extraction model based on deep neural networks is constructed to capture the correlations between heterogeneous loads and effectively represent key features. Finally, an EPRI-36-bus system is built to test the identification algorithm, verifying that the proposed method exhibits higher identification accuracy and robustness compared to existing methods.

title: A Review of Power Carbon Emission Factors under the Carbon Neutrality Transition
authors: Jing Liu, Tiantian Chen, Jihong Zhang, Hao Qin
publish: 2025 IEEE 9th Conference on Energy Internet and Energy System Integration (EI2)
abstract: As a key parameter that converts electricity consumption or trading volume into CO₂ emissions, the power carbon emission factor plays a crucial role in both macro- and micro-level assessments. Accurate calculation not only supports evaluation of energy transition and carbon-neutral progress at the national level but also directly affects product carbon footprint accounting and the international competitiveness of enterprises. This paper provides a systematic review of the classification, influencing factors, and future development trends of power carbon emission factors. First, it introduces the factor categories from the perspectives of accounting boundary, temporal resolution, and spatial dimension. Second, it summarizes the key drivers affecting carbon emission factors, including generation mix, grid losses, and combined heat and power systems. Finally, the paper highlights existing challenges and outlines future development directions.

title: Multi-time Scale Optimal Scheduling Method for Thermoelectric Combined Systems based on Thermal Storage
authors: Xintong Ma, Guangyu Sun, Yifei Sun, Zhenyang Zhao, Mengyu Wang, Yikun Liu
publish: 2025 IEEE 9th Conference on Energy Internet and Energy System Integration (EI2)
abstract: To address peak-shaving pressure and wind curtailment in cold-region power grids with high renewable penetration, a grid-supporting thermal energy storage joint operation device and its digital twin system have been studied. By establishing a comprehensive energy system model that integrates thermal networks, power grids, and electrified heating coupling equipment, a unified state of energy (SOE) model is introduced to describe the characteristics of multiple energy storage systems. On this basis, an optimization scheduling model aimed at minimizing annual comprehensive costs is constructed, proposing a multi-time scale rolling optimization strategy that takes into account the dynamic characteristics of the equipment, including day-ahead optimization and intra-day rolling optimization. The results indicate that during typical winter operation days, the grid interaction energy decreases by 9.65%, daily operating costs decrease by 4.25%, and these costs only increase by 2.55% compared to ideal costs. By combining day-ahead optimization with intra-day rolling optimization strategies, the system effectively mitigates source-load fluctuations, reduces wind curtailment phenomena, and enhances the system's capacity to accommodate renewable energy.

title: Software Composition Analysis on Rust Binaries
authors: Denisa Muntean, Ciprian Oprişa
publish: 2025 IEEE 21st International Conference on Intelligent Computer Communication and Processing (ICCP)
abstract: Software Composition Analysis (SCA) is an important part of the reverse engineering process, useful for both antimalware research and vulnerabilities scanning. While traditional SCA techniques rely on Software Bill of Materials (SBOM) and strings analysis, we propose a new technique, based on binary code analysis on Rust programs. The binary code is analyzed through disassembly and OpCode n-grams extraction. The n-grams are then used to tag Rust crates using multiple methods and thresholds. The experimental evaluation shows the robustness of the proposed technique, being able to distinguish between 150 popular crates with over 87% accuracy and $48 \% F_{1}$ score (micro-averaged) on the validation data.

title: Low-Carbon Oriented Joint Generation and Storage Planning considering the Impact of Climate Change
authors: Quansheng Liao, Zhipen Lin, Xianlang Tan, Hengchao Xian, Hechang Yao, Haoyun Yuan, Jing Huang, Chenjia Gu
publish: 2025 IEEE 9th Conference on Energy Internet and Energy System Integration (EI2)
abstract: Climate change is reshaping the long-term variability and availability of renewable resources, thereby introducing new challenges for low-carbon power system planning. This paper develops a joint generation and storage planning model that explicitly incorporates climate-corrected renewable characteristics and probabilistic carbon emission limits. Future wind and solar generation profiles are constructed through a multi-stage climate–energy linkage framework combining statistical downscaling, physical conversion, representative-day aggregation, and stochastic perturbation. A chance-constrained carbon emission formulation is then proposed to balance environmental targets with operational feasibility, allowing limited emission violations under extreme renewable conditions. The resulting model is formulated as a mixed-integer linear program and solved efficiently through standard optimization solvers. Case studies on a modified IEEE 30-bus system, integrating historical and MRI-ESM2-0 projections under multiple SSP scenarios, demonstrate that climate-induced resource shifts substantially alter optimal investment portfolios and cost structures. Furthermore, introducing chance constraints yields more robust and economically efficient planning outcomes while maintaining long-term decarbonization consistency.

title: Designing a New Potato and Banana Disease Detection Using Trans-Unet++ and Classification Through Gather-Excite Network
authors: Divyakanti Rongali, Sasibhushana Rao Gottapu, Aruna Singam
publish: 2026 Sixth International Conference on Advances in Electrical, Computing, Communications and Sustainable Technologies (ICAECT)
abstract: Banana was one of the most widely cultivated fruits across the world, yet its yield were heavily impacted each year by various leaf diseases. Detecting these diseases at an early stage is essential for improving overall production. Potato farming also suffered from similar challenges, as pests reduce the quality and yield of the harvested crops. Thus, the reduction in quantity and quality led to reduced income for farmers, because of trade restrictions, limited market access, and price drops. There are various factors, which affect the degradation of agricultural products. Hence, minimizing plant diseases is essential for ensuring higher crop quality and improved farming efficiency. So, a new potato and banana disease detection technique is suggested in this research work with a deep learning technique. In the initial phase, essential images needed for the validation is collected from the benchmark resources. Next, the collected images are given to the disease detection phase using Transformer Unet++ (Trans-Unet++). Then, the disease detected images are given to the disease classification phase. Here, the potato and banana diseases are classified using Gather-Excite Network (GENet). At last, multiple experiments are carried out in the developed framework over classical mechanisms to verify its efficiency.

title: Edge-Side Intelligent Adaptive Control Method for Novel Power Systems Based on Hierarchical DQN
authors: Yangyang Ge, Shubo Hu, Ruohan Fu, Han Zhang, Bing Xie, Xintong Ma, Xinrui Liu
publish: 2025 IEEE 9th Conference on Energy Internet and Energy System Integration (EI2)
abstract: Under the "dual-carbon" goals, the high penetration of renewable energy and power electronic devices increases source–load uncertainties, while traditional centralized control struggles with latency and communication burdens. This paper proposes an edge-intelligent adaptive control method based on a Hierarchical Deep Q-Network (HDDQN). A multi-granularity task scheduling model is developed for cloud–edge–end environments to balance execution latency and resource utilization, and a cloud–edge collaborative optimization model is designed to address renewable uncertainties through an integrated objective considering energy cost, curtailment penalties, and computation cost. The two-stage multi-energy optimization is formulated as a dual-time-scale Markov Decision Process, and the HDDQN enables coordinated decision-making across time scales. Simulation results verify that the proposed method reduces latency, improves resource utilization and balance, and under high renewable penetration, lowers total system cost and mitigates curtailment, demonstrating superior performance in cloud–edge–end coordinated optimization.

title: A Data-Physics Collaborative Framework for Power System Cascading Failure Scenario Generation with Reward Guided Fine-Tuned Conditional Denoising Diffusion Probabilistic Model
authors: Yaokun Zou, Jian Xu, Xinxiong Jiang, Tong Wang, Siyang Liao, Deping Ke, Liangzhong Yao
publish: 2025 IEEE 9th Conference on Energy Internet and Energy System Integration (EI2)
abstract: The generation of high-quality and efficient cascading failure scenarios is fundamental to data-driven power system cascading failure analysis. However, due to the inherent security of power systems and the rarity of cascading failure events, conventional Monte Carlo random sampling methods struggle to efficiently produce a large number of fault samples that are both physically credible and high-risk. To address this issue, this paper proposes a novel framework of a physics-simulation-reward-guided conditional diffusion model. This framework first employs a Conditional Denoising Diffusion Probabilistic Model (DDPM) to learn the distribution characteristics of historical cascading failure samples, thereby generating diverse fault scenarios. The core innovation lies in introducing a reward signal, determined by a physical simulator, that evaluates the authenticity of generated samples. This reward is then used to fine-tune the DDPM, guiding the model to evolve towards generating more physically plausible fault scenarios. Simulation results on the New England 39-bus system demonstrate that the proposed method can efficiently generate power system fault scenarios that lead to cascading failures.

title: Research on Enhancing the Grid Support Capacity of Wind Turbines With Hybrid Energy Storage Systems
authors: Hengyu Liu, Jiazheng Sun, Zhi Zhang, Gechen Bian, Shiyun Liu, Jie Hu
publish: 2025 IEEE 9th Conference on Energy Internet and Energy System Integration (EI2)
abstract: Wind turbines are required to maintain low-voltage ride-through (LVRT) capability during grid voltage sags. However, LVRT operation can induce system frequency instability. When conventional energy storage is integrated with wind turbines to provide inertia support and participate in primary frequency regulation—using frequency as the sole input—the LVRT process may aggravate DC-link voltage rise. This paper proposes a hybrid energy storage-based strategy to enhance the grid support capability of wind turbines. A hybrid energy storage module, comprising supercapacitors and batteries, is incorporated into the DC link. This module delivers inertia support and primary frequency regulation during power fluctuations, while absorbing excess DC-link energy during voltage sags to maintain voltage stability. Simulation results validate the effectiveness of the proposed approach.

title: Detection of Technical Debt in Java Source Code
authors: Nam Le Hai, Anh M. T. Bui, Phuong T. Nguyen, Davide Di Ruscio, Rick Kazman
publish: ACM Transactions on Software Engineering and Methodology
abstract: None

title: Ringwise Codebook for Precoding in Unified Near/Far-Field Communication
authors: Haochen Wu, Liyang Lu, Yue Wang, Zhaocheng Wang, Zhu Han
publish: IEEE Transactions on Wireless Communications
abstract: Larger antenna arrays, combined with higher transmission frequencies, are prospective in fulfilling the demands of the sixth-generation (6G) communications, enabling a 10-fold increase in overall spectral efficiency. However, such configurations give rise to near-field effects, requiring spherical rather than planar wave modeling. In practical multi-user communications, it is typical that part of the user equippments (UEs) resides in the near-field region, while others are located in the far-field region, thereby leading to a unified near/far-field scenario. Conventional codebooks tailored to either regime alone thus become mismatched, resulting in notable spectral efficiency degradation. In view of this, the ringwise codebook based on the slope-intercept formulation is proposed to address the unified near/far-field communication scenario. Specifically, the slope-intercept domain is first illustrated as the foundation of our codebook design, where the correlation between near/far-field channel steering vectors is exploited by mapping the angle-distance into the slope-intercept parameters. In the slope-intercept domain, the correlation pattern of steering vectors exhibits a dual triangle structure, supported by rigorous analyses on axial symmetry and correlation width, which paves the way for the subsequent codebook development. Secondly, the ringwise codebook is proposed where all UEs coarsely estimate its own slope parameter, based on which ring-wise codebooks composed of orthogonal codewords derived from the slope-intercept domain are constructed for different UEs. The resulting codebooks are then fed back to the base station through specific slope parameters, which are leveraged in the subsequent precoding procedure. Finally, rigorous analysis demonstrates that both the computational complexity and hardware cost of the proposed ringwise codebook design are acceptable, while numerical results validate its effectiveness and feasibility, achieving gains in both spectral efficiency and complexity compared to conventional counterparts.

title: Reinforcement Learning-Based Whole-Body Motion Control for Humanoids with Position-Controlled Joints
authors: Chengju Liu, Kaiyan Xiao, Mengxian Hu, Qin Fang, Zihan Xu, Qijun Chen
publish: IEEE Transactions on Automation Science and Engineering
abstract: Reinforcement learning (RL) holds great promise for generating dynamic whole-body motions on humanoid robots, but its real-world application is hindered by the significant sim-to-real gap caused by actuator model mismatch. Most simulators assume torque-controlled or idealized PD actuators, whereas real-world platforms like the NAO robot employ position-controlled joints with hidden, proprietary control loops and nonlinear dynamics. To bridge this gap, we present a simulation-to-reality framework for whole-body motion control that integrates two key components: an equivalent torque-space PID actuator model and a neural network-based inverse kinematics method. First, we perform system identification on the physical robot by analyzing step response data and computing ground-truth joint torques via Lagrangian dynamics, from which we estimate equivalent PID parameters that replicate the real joint dynamics in simulation. Second, we design a learnable inverse kinematics module that maps human motion, represented by end-effector trajectories, into robot-executable joint commands. Using this high-fidelity simulation environment and motion retargeting pipeline, we train a whole-body control policy via RL in IsaacLab and deploy it directly on the physical NAO robot without any fine-tuning. Experimental results demonstrate successful reproduction of diverse human-like motions, including waving, squatting, and bimanual coordination, validating that accurate actuator modeling and structured motion representation are essential for reliable sim-to-real transfer in learning-based humanoid control. (Supplementary video link: https://youtu.be/zLWXgGZQAFk).

title: Anonymous Messaging Made More Flexible with PingPong_supp1-3672821.pdf
authors: Qian Wang
publish: N/A
abstract: For those seeking end-to-end private communication free from pervasive metadata tracking and censorship, the Tor network has been the de-facto choice in practice, despite its susceptibility to traffic analysis attacks. Recently, numerous metadata-private messaging proposals have emerged with the aim to surpass Tor in the messaging context by obscuring the relationships between any two messaging buddies, even against global and active attackers. However, most of these systems face an undesirable usability constraint: they require a metadata-private “dialing” phase to establish mutual agreement and timing or round coordination before initiating any regular chats among users. This phase is not only resource-intensive but also inflexible, limiting users’ ability to manage multiple concurrent conversations seamlessly. For stringent privacy requirement, the often-enforced traffic uniformity further exacerbated the limitations of this roadblock. In this paper, we introduce PingPong, a new end-to-end system for metadata-private messaging designed to overcome these limitations. Under the same traffic uniformity requirement, PingPong replaces the rigid “dial-before-converse” paradigm with a more flexible “notify-before-retrieval” workflow. This workflow incorporates a metadata-private notification subsystem, PING, and a metadata-private message store, PONG. Both PING and PONG leverage hardware-assisted secure enclaves for performance and operates through a series of customized oblivious algorithms, while meeting the uniformity requirements for metadata protection. By allowing users to switch between conversations on demand, PingPong achieves a level of usability akin to modern instant messaging systems, while also offering improved performance and bandwidth utilization for goodput We have built a prototype of PingPong with 32 8-core servers equipped with enclaves and conducted a case study on a real-world messaging metadata dataset to validate our claims.

title: Confidence-Aware Pseudo-Label Self-Correction for Weakly Supervised Visual Grounding
authors: Yang Liu, Jiahua Zhang, Yue Wu, Zijing Zhao, Qingchao Chen, Yuxin Peng
publish: IEEE Transactions on Pattern Analysis and Machine Intelligence
abstract: Weakly supervised visual grounding aims to locate a region in an image based on an input query sentence, without access to the mapping between image regions and queries during training. Current methods treat spatial grounding as an object retrieval task, relying on cross-modal similarity scores for proposal selection. However, they fail to address model overfitting caused by unreliable cross-modal similarity scores. To overcome this, we first propose the Confidence-aware Pseudo-label Learning (CPL) framework. CPL first generates diverse pseudo queries for region proposals, and then establishes reliable associations for model training based on the uni-modal similarity score. Secondly, we propose a cross-modal verification module based on the pretrained vision-language model to verify associations. However, the verification module is isolated from the grounding model, so it can only assess associations in a static manner, but not correct the suspicious ones. Finally, we introduce CPL++ to make two- fold improvements. For one thing, we upgrade the verification process based on the model's grounding loss value to identify suspicious associations dynamically and selectively leverage them in the training. For another, we propose a self-supervised association correction module to rectify suspicious associations, thereby mitigating the risk of error propagation. Experimental results on five datasets demonstrate the superiority of our approach.

title: Revocable Data Sharing Scheme Without Ciphertext Update for VANETs
authors: Li Wang, Hong Zhong, Jing Zhang, Irina Bolodurina, Jie Cui
publish: IEEE Transactions on Vehicular Technology
abstract: Revocable ciphertext policy attribute-based encryption (CP-ABE) provides an effective technology to achieve secure and dynamic data sharing for vehicular ad hoc networks (VANETs). However, existing revocable CP-ABE schemes rely on updating the ciphertext to ensure forward and backward security, where the legality of the updated ciphertext cannot be guaranteed and the cost of updating is also high. Aiming at this, a revocable data sharing scheme without ciphertext updates for VANETs is proposed. This scheme revokes attributes by introducing a chameleon hash function, and generating the update keys for users and the cloud, achieving constant ciphertext while ensuring the security of revocation. Furthermore, verification keys are developed to validate ciphertexts, guaranteeing they are generated with valid attribute encryption keys, thereby further ensuring the validity of ciphertexts. Also, outsourcing decryption and data verification are supported to efficiently optimize operations and maintain system security. The security analysis and performance evaluation results confirm the security and efficiency of the proposed scheme.

title: Proxima: Near-storage Acceleration for Graph-based Approximate Nearest Neighbor Search in 3D NAND
authors: Weihong Xu, Junwei Chen, Po-kai Hsu, Jaeyoung Kang, Minxuan Zhou, Sumukh Pinge, Shimeng Yu, Tajana Rosing
publish: IEEE Transactions on Computers
abstract: Approximate nearest neighbor search (ANNS) plays an indispensable role in a wide variety of applications, including recommendation systems, information retrieval, and semantic search. Among the cutting-edge ANNS algorithms, graph-based approaches provide superior accuracy and scalability on massive datasets. However, the best-performing graph-based ANNS solutions incur tens of hundreds of memory footprints as well as costly distance computation, thus hindering their efficient deployment at scale. The 3D NAND flash is emerging as a promising device for data-intensive applications due to its high density and nonvolatility. In this work, we present the near-storage processing (NSP)-based ANNS solution Proxima to accelerate graph-based ANNS with algorithm-hardware co-design in 3D NAND flash. Proxima significantly reduces the complexity of graph search by leveraging the distance approximation and early termination. On top of the algorithmic enhancement, we implement the Proxima search algorithm in 3D NAND flash using the heterogeneous integration technique. To maximize 3D NAND’s bandwidth utilization, we present a customized dataflow and optimized data allocation scheme. Our evaluation results show that, compared to graph ANNS on CPU and GPU, Proxima achieves a magnitude improvement in throughput or energy efficiency. Proxima yields 7× to 13× speedup over existing ASIC designs. Furthermore, Proxima achieves a good balance between accuracy, efficiency, and storage density compared to previous NSP-based accelerators.

title: Nasal Dominance and Nostril Breathing Variability: Potential Biomarkers of Acute Stress
authors: Amit Kumar, Deepak Joshi
publish: IEEE Transactions on Affective Computing
abstract: Previous research suggests that acute stress significantly impacts breathing patterns and cortical brain activity in individuals. However, the effects of varying intensities of stress on nostril-specific breathing patterns and its association with cortical activity have not been studied earlier. The present study addresses this gap by investigating the impact of acute stress on nostril breathing parameters, such as nasal dominance index (NDI), nasal laterality ratio (NLR), internostril correlation coefficient (iNCC), measures of nostril breath rate variability (nBRV), and cortical activity in frontal log alpha power (FLAP). The nasal temperature sensing (NTS) device and a 64-channel electroencephalogram (EEG) system were synchronized to capture nostril breathing patterns and brain activities simultaneously during three stress phases (pre-stress, stress, and post-stress) while varying stress intensities (SI1, SI2, and SI3) were induced. NDI showed a significant interaction [ F(4,124) = 5.536,p = 0.001, η2 = 0.152] between stress phase and stress intensity, with NDI values progressively declining as stress intensity increased from SI1 to SI2 to SI3. Additionally, NLR showed a significant main effect for both stress phase and stress intensity, with lower NLR during the stress phase than pre-stress and a subsequent increase in post-stress. There was a significant main effect of stress phase for mBR [ F(2,62) = 29.503,p = 0.001, η2 = 0.488] and mBB [ F(2,62) = 32.997,p = 0.001, η2 = 0.516], with higher mBR and lower mBB during the stress phase than both pre-stress and post-stress phases across all three stress intensities. These findings may provide a novel insight into stress biomarkers and hold significant promise in developing more reliable and feasible indicators of acute stress.

title: Gaussian Process Regression Based Condition Monitoring of DC-Link Capacitors in Three-Phase AC/DC/AC Converters
authors: Kerim Örüklü, Bo Yao, Ariya Sangwongwanich, Şener Ağalar, Frede Blaabjerg
publish: IEEE Transactions on Power Electronics
abstract: While dc-link capacitors (DLCs) are one of the core components of power electronic converters, their failure can cause unexpected downtime, costly maintenance, and even safety hazards. Therefore, monitoring their health condition is essential. This article proposes a Gaussian process regression (GPR) based condition monitoring (CM) method for DLCs in three-phase AC/DC/AC converters. The proposed method has three merits, which make it more suitable for practical applications. 1) Unlike traditional approaches, an additional hardware or a signal injection is not required. 2) It can be applied during the normal operation of the converter under different load levels and output frequencies. 3) It is possible to quantify the uncertainty associated with each prediction, which is often overlooked in similar machine learning based methods. Moreover, the experimental results on a laboratory prototype showed that the proposed method can achieve an average prediction error less than 2% and remains effective even at low sampling rates.

title: SHIELD-EB : Sustainable Hybrid Evolutionary-Boosting Framework for Carbon, Wastewater, and Cost-Aware Datacenter Management
authors: Sirui Qi, Hayden Moore, Dejan Milojicic, Cullen Bash, Sudeep Pasricha
publish: IEEE Access
abstract: For over a decade, the problem of distributed cloud workload management has been studied with the goal of co-optimizing operational costs with other metrics, such as energy efficiency, using multi-objective optimization algorithms. However, there is a lack of a multi-objective algorithm that can not only provide a diverse and high-quality Pareto-optimal solution set, but also scale to different cloud management scenarios. The heterogeneity of cloud workloads and geo-distributed datacenters introduces complex operational scenarios over time and geographic locations. The introduction of emerging sustainability objectives, such as carbon emissions and wastewater generation, further aggravates the cloud management challenge. Moreover, inter-datacenter network costs must also be considered during cloud workload management to prevent unrealistic workload migration scenarios. In this article, we propose a novel cloud resource management framework called SHIELD-EB to co-optimize operational costs, operational carbon emissions, and wastewater generation in a geo-distributed cloud datacenter platform. To generate a diverse solution set with different tradeoffs, SHIELD-EB integrates a customized evolutionary strategy (CES) with eXtreme Gradient Boosting (XGB). Experimental results show that SHIELD-EB can achieve improvements of up to 33% in Pareto hypervolume, 7.1% ($0.6 M) in operational costs, 11.6% (7.6 tons) in operational carbon, and 12.0% (102.4 tons) in water usage compared to the state-of-the-art over a duration of 120 hours.

title: Virtual Capacitor Strategy to Coordinate Stability and Power Balance in Droop-Controlled Input-Parallel-Output-Series Grid-Connected Inverter System
authors: Chengyi Liu, Tianzhi Fang, Zhiheng Lin, Qiyuan Jin, Hao Liu
publish: IEEE Journal of Emerging and Selected Topics in Power Electronics
abstract: To address the high voltage difference between the low-voltage photovoltaic (PV) array and the mid-voltage distribution grid, the input-parallel output-series (IPOS) grid-connected inverter system is employed to reduce the reliance on high-ratio step-up transformers. This article focuses on the droop control strategy for IPOS systems, where a precise mathematical model reveals the dual effects of the droop coefficient: while increasing the coefficient enhances power balancing, excessive values introduce a negative damping effect that degrades system stability. To resolve this trade-off, this article proposes a virtual capacitor-based impedance reshaping method to optimize stability margins by adjusting the positive-damping region. Theoretically, rigorous derivation establishes quantitative design criteria for both droop coefficient and virtual capacitance parameters, providing comprehensive guidance for parameter tuning. Finally, experimental results based on a two-module IPOS prototype demonstrate that the proposed strategy maintains system stability while achieving power-sharing accuracy, validating both theoretical soundness and practical feasibility.

title: Semantic Compression and Conceptual Symbolic Reasoning over Transformer Embeddings for Interpretable Crime News Classification
authors: Tayyab Rehman, Giovanni De Gasperis, Asif Saeed, Shahid Salim
publish: 2025 IEEE 21st International Conference on Intelligent Computer Communication and Processing (ICCP)
abstract: The classification of crime-related news articles poses unique challenges due to overlapping labels, subtle semantic cues, and limited annotated data. This paper proposes a hybrid semantic-symbolic classification framework that combines state-of-the-art sentence-transformer embeddings with domain-specific symbolic reasoning. We benchmark multiple transformer-based models, including MiniLM, BERT, and the multilingual E5-Large model, for embedding Italian crime news, and evaluate traditional classifiers (Support Vector Machines, Logistic Regression) as well as deep learning models (Convolutional Neural Networks). To enhance explainability and performance, we integrate Basic Formal Ontology (BFO)-compliant mappings using a symbolic post-processing layer based on predicate logic and ontological relationships. Experiments on a curated Italian crime news dataset demonstrate that our hybrid approach significantly improves classification accuracy and macro-F1 scores compared to zero-shot and purely statistical baselines. In particular, a CNN combined with E5-Large embeddings and clustering achieves the highest performance, with a macro-F1 score of $740 \%$. The results suggest that integrating structured domain knowledge into neural pipelines enhances model transparency and robustness for multi-label crime classification.

title: From Opcodes to Semantics: A Hybrid Approach for Function Name Inference in Stripped Binaries
authors: Remus Petrache, Camelia Lemnaru, Ciprian Oprişa
publish: 2025 IEEE 21st International Conference on Intelligent Computer Communication and Processing (ICCP)
abstract: The absence of debugging symbols in stripped binaries, particularly descriptive function names, hinders reverse engineering and security analysis. This paper introduces a structured pipeline for automated function name inference, combining Transformer models, calibrated confidence estimation, and context-aware refinement using large language models pretrained on code. In the first stage, a specialized Transformer model is trained on normalized assembly instructions to predict initial function names, along with statistical indicators. These indicators are then mapped to reliable confidence scores using a logistic regression calibration module. In the final stage, lowconfidence functions are reprocessed using fine-tuned, pretrained code language models, which leverage the context derived from high-confidence caller and callee relationships. The method is validated on a dataset of approximately 21.3 million functions extracted from 6,824 Windows executables compiled with various MSVC versions and architectures. The results demonstrate that our approach supports the practical recovery of meaningful function identifiers and enhances malware analysis tasks.

title: Chiral Nanocomposite of Achiral Polyoxometalates and Cellulose Nanocrystals for Enhanced Circularly Polarized Luminescence
authors: Tiehai Chen, Binqian Zhou, Junjie Hao
publish: 2025 International Conference on Energy, Materials, and Photonics (EMP)
abstract: Polyoxometalates (POMs) are anionic nanoscale transition metal oxygen clusters, which are often used as inorganic ligands to construct novel polyacid derivatives, and have a wide range in catalytic, optical, and magnetic applications. However, the structure of most polymetallic oxygen clusters is highly symmetric, making the induction of chirality on POMs a significant challenge. In this work, red-emissive chiral atomically thin films were synthesized by co-assembly strategy of cellulose nanocrystals (CNCs) and POMs containing rare earth metal ion. The composite film exhibits a tunable photonic bandgap, precise handedness control, high quantum yield and strong selective reflection of the films, the obtained glum can reach up to -0.08. Our work provides a novel approach for fabricating strong chiroptical activity composite films, significantly advancing the potential of POMs in optoelectronic devices.

title: Modified Ant Colony Beam Search Algorithm with Embedded Neural Networks for Efficient Multi-Asteroid Rendezvous Planning
authors: Zhuojin Li, V. V. Koryanov
publish: 2025 3rd Asian Aerospace and Astronautics Conference (AAAC)
abstract: This study investigates the optimization of transfer time for multi-asteroid rendezvous missions using low-thrust propulsion. The research consists of three main phases: First, an indirect method based on Pontryagin's maximum principle is employed to generate training data by randomly selecting asteroid pairs from the main belt catalog, with transfer time serving as the key metric. Next, a deep neural network (DNN) is developed to estimate the minimum transfer time between asteroid pairs, with the SMOGN library utilized for resampling to enhance prediction accuracy for fast transfers (under 150 days). Finally, an improved ant colony-based beam search algorithm is proposed based on the trained neural network. The algorithm searches all main-belt asteroids with an absolute magnitude H below 15 and, with a beam width of 20, identifies a rapid rendezvous sequence involving 19 asteroids.

title: A Resilience Enhancement Method for AC/DC Distribution Networks Based on the Coordination of Preventive and Corrective Control
authors: Zhengyang Lin, Tao Lin, Jun Li, Chen Li
publish: 2025 IEEE 9th Conference on Energy Internet and Energy System Integration (EI2)
abstract: To enhance the resilience of AC/DC distribution networks against extreme disasters, a resilience enhancement method based on the coordination of preventive and corrective control is proposed in this paper. Firstly, in the pre-disaster stage, network reconfiguration, renewable energy output and energy storage system dispatch strategies for the distribution network are optimized in advance based on predicted fault scenarios. Then, considering the uncertainty of renewable energy output during the disaster, various corrective control measures are proposed to coordinated with the preventive control to minimize control cost and load loss. Finally, the case study is conducted on a 42-bus AC/DC distribution network derived from the modification of the IEEE 33-bus system. The results indicate that the proposed method can effectively reduce control cost and load loss during disasters and enhance the resilience of AC/DC distribution network compared to the corrective control method.

title: Cooperative Luminescence Quenching by $\mathrm{A}^{+}$ in $\text{Yb}^{3+}$-Doped $\text{CaF}_{2}$
authors: Bahetiguli Asilibike, Xiaojuan Peng, Tuerxun Aidilibike
publish: 2025 International Conference on Energy, Materials, and Photonics (EMP)
abstract: The cooperative luminescence of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\text{Yb}^{3+}$</tex>-trimers and <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\text{Yb}^{3+}$</tex>-dimers was observed in <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\text{Yb}^{3+}$</tex>-doped <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\text{CaF}_{2}$</tex> under excitation by near infrared (NIR) light at room temperature. With the increase of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathrm{A}^{+}\left(\mathrm{A}^{+}=\text{Li}^{+}, \text{Na}^{+}, \mathrm{K}^{+}\right)$</tex> concentration, the cooperative luminescence from 3- <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\text{Yb}^{3+}$</tex> (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\text{Yb}^{3+}$</tex>-trimer) and <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\text{2 -} \text{Yb}^{3+}$</tex> (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\text{Yb}^{3+}$</tex>-dimer) clusters first increases, then decreases in <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\text{CaF}_{2}: \text{Yb}^{3+}, \mathrm{A}^{+}$</tex>. The doped <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$A^{+}$</tex> ions induce luminescence quenching that comes from the destruction of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\text{Yb}^{3+}$</tex>-trimers and <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\text{Yb}^{3+}$</tex>-dimers structures, resulting in structure-destroyed fluorescence quenching. This luminescence quenching belongs to the static quenching as luminescence centers were changed.

title: An Optimisation Method for the Operation of Combined Electric-Thermal Systems with Refined Modelling for Efficient Thermal Energy Utilisation
authors: Xintong Ma, Guangyu Sun, Wei Fan, Xiaotong Zhang, Yifei Sun, Nan Li
publish: 2025 IEEE 9th Conference on Energy Internet and Energy System Integration (EI2)
abstract: Against the backdrop of the global efforts to transform the energy structure and move towards the goal of carbon neutrality, hydrogen energy has attracted much attention in the energy sector due to its clean and efficient characteristics. Alkaline electrolysis of water for hydrogen production, with its relatively mature technical system and relatively low cost, has become one of the mainstream choices for large-scale hydrogen production. However, in the actual operation process, this technology has also exposed some problems that need to be solved urgently, such as low thermal energy utilization efficiency and insufficient degree of system integration and optimization. In response to these issues, this paper innovatively proposes a refined modeling method, which fully considers the efficient utilization of hydrogen energy. Based on this method, a thermal management system for hydrogen production through alkaline electrolysis of water based on the second type of absorption heat pump was constructed. Furthermore, in combination with the unique operational characteristics of the electrothermal combined system, this paper constructs a system optimization model that includes an under thermal device. To more accurately enhance the overall energy efficiency and economy of the system, an ensemble learning method integrating convolutional neural networks, bidirectional long short-term memory networks, and attention mechanisms is adopted to precisely predict the building's heat load.

title: Gaze Tracking via L2CS-Net Pipeline with Ridge Regression and Kalman Post-filtering
authors: Ioana-Maeva Cárbunescu-Stoenescu, Mircea Giurgiu
publish: 2025 IEEE 21st International Conference on Intelligent Computer Communication and Processing (ICCP)
abstract: This paper proposes a novel processing pipeline for real-time gaze tracking utilizing standard webcams in unconstrained environments. First, the study evaluates three gaze estimation estimators: the GazeTracking library, and two L2CS-Net deep learning models that have been trained on the Gaze360 and MPIIGaze datasets. Then, several improvements are proposed based on a user-specific calibration on a $5 \times 4$ grid, after which gaze data is mapped to screen coordinates using two regression approaches: linear regression is based on gaze data alone, whilst Ridge regression is dependent upon both gaze data and head pose data. A post-processing pipeline incorporating a moving median and a Kalman filter has been demonstrated to stabilize the predicted gaze point. This research demonstrates an effective, affordable pipeline for interactive systems and provides a comprehensive analysis of the trade-offs between the evaluated technical solutions.

title: Brain Tumor Detection: Comparative Performance Analysis of MobileNetV2 Classification Metrics in Healthcare
authors: Geetha R
publish: 2026 Sixth International Conference on Advances in Electrical, Computing, Communications and Sustainable Technologies (ICAECT)
abstract: For better patient outcomes, it is important to find brain cancers early and accurately using radiological imaging in healthcare. Manually interpreting hundreds of CT or MRI slices takes a lot of time and is not always consistent. The goal of this effort was to automate the process by teaching a deep-learning model to tell the difference between brain images with and without tumours. It is combined and preprocessed a balanced set of publically available MRI and CT images. The lightweight MobileNetV2 architecture model is trained for 10 epochs to do binary classification. The model did very well on a test set that was not used for training: it accurately detected 375 cancer cases and 467 non-tumor cases out of 881 test images (479 non-tumor and 402 tumour). It only misclassified 39 scans. This means that the total accuracy is about 96 %, the precision is about 97 %, and the recall (sensitivity) is 93 %. The model has a specificity (true negative rate) of 97.5 % and an F1-score of 0.95, which is a balance between accuracy and recall. These results show that automated brain tumour identification may be quite reliable, even with a tiny and efficient network. This opens the door for more improvements and comparisons with bigger architectures or more datasets.

title: Ultra-Short-Term Forecasting of Distributed Photovoltaic Power Considering Dynamic Spatial-temporal Correlations Without Meteorological Data
authors: Jing Guo, Na Zhang, Dianting Guo
publish: 2025 IEEE 9th Conference on Energy Internet and Energy System Integration (EI2)
abstract: Aiming at the issue of insufficient prediction accuracy in distributed photovoltaic systems due to the lack of meteorological measurement devices and the inability to capture dynamic Spatial-temporal correlations when relying solely on historical data, this paper proposes an adaptive graph convolution network based on a Spatial-temporal attention mechanism for ultra-short-term power forecasting without meteorological data. First, the Spatial-temporal dynamic correlation characteristics of PV power output are analyzed. An adaptive graph convolution network is employed to mine the nonlinear dynamic spatial correlations among sites that fluctuate with power output variations, while Gated Recurrent Units are utilized to capture temporal dependencies and achieve preliminary feature fusion. Second, a dual Spatial-temporal attention mechanism is introduced to enhance the capture of these dynamic correlations. Finally, the prediction results are generated through a fully-connected layer. To validate the effectiveness of the proposed method, actual power output data from distributed PV systems in Hebei Province, China, are used for comparative experiments with various forecasting models. The results confirm the accuracy and stability of the proposed method.

title: Ultra-Short-Term Wind Power Prediction with WOA-VMD and MoE-Informer
authors: Jiajing Gao, Yongsheng Wang, Hongmei Xing, Guangchen Liu, Fan Yang, Xuehui Wang
publish: 2025 IEEE 9th Conference on Energy Internet and Energy System Integration (EI2)
abstract: The power sequence of wind power is significantly affected by meteorological conditions, showing strong non-stationarity and random volatility, which poses a considerable challenge to ultra-short-term prediction. To improve prediction accuracy, this paper proposes a hybrid prediction framework based on WOA-VMD decomposition and MoE-Informer modeling. Firstly, the whale optimization algorithm is utilized to adaptively optimize the variational modal decomposition parameters to achieve multi-scale feature extraction of the wind power sequence. Secondly, an improved Informer model introducing a sparse hybrid expert mechanism is constructed to enhance the characterization ability of complex nonlinear temporal dependencies while ensuring computational efficiency. Experiments based on actual wind power data show that this method outperforms traditional models in both single-step and multi-step prediction tasks.

title: Influence of excitation controller parameters on reactive power regulation of hydropower plants with Pelton turbines
authors: Yuxiang Liu, Weijia Yang, Ruixin Shen, Zhipeng Yang, Hao Liu, Kunlong Song, Ruiji Yi
publish: 2025 IEEE 9th Conference on Energy Internet and Energy System Integration (EI2)
