abstract: With the continuous advancement of new power system construction, the large-scale integration of renewable energy has introduced challenges in stable operation. This necessitates hydropower units to possess capabilities to meet the power system's demand for reactive power regulation. However, most existing studies on the reactive power regulation characteristics of hydropower units focus primarily on Francis turbines and pump-turbines, with limited research on Pelton turbines. The hydropower plants with Pelton turbines are focused on and a hydraulic-mechanical-electrical coupling model for reactive power regulation is introduced in this paper. This model encompasses subsystems from the hydraulic and mechanical side, including penstock, six-nozzle Pelton turbine, and governor, to the electrical side, including generator and exciter. It is assumed that all six nozzles operate uniformly. Based on these, simulations of reactive power regulation operation under different control parameter combinations are conducted. The results indicate that control parameters significantly influence the reactive power regulation performance. Especially the gain K<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">a</inf> of the regulator and the time constants T<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">b</inf> and T<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">c</inf> of the lead-lag compensator. This study provides a decision-making reference for optimizing control parameters in reactive power regulation of hydropower plants with Pelton turbines, as well as for the selection and operational scenario matching of reactive power regulation equipment in new power systems.

title: Lightweight and Interpretable CNNs for Multi-Label ECG Diagnosis on the Edge
authors: Cătălin Aurelian Popa, Radu Dogaru
publish: 2025 IEEE 21st International Conference on Intelligent Computer Communication and Processing (ICCP)
abstract: Automated multi-label electrocardiogram (ECG) diagnosis promises accessible cardiovascular care, but clinical adoption is challenged by deep learning models’ size, interpretability, and poor performance on rare conditions. This paper presents an end-to-end framework addressing these issues through two novel, compact convolutional neural network (CNN) architectures—an Efficient CNN ($\sim 20 \mathrm{k}$ parameters) and a Compact MiniResNet $(\sim 35 \mathrm{k}$ parameters)-trained on the 21,837-record PTB-XL dataset. Our methodology integrates (1) a robust class weight strategy for label imbalance, (2) waveform-aligned Grad-CAM for clinically relevant visual explanations, and (3) post-training, per-class decision threshold optimization to significantly improve sensitivity on minority classes. Models are validated for edge deployment via full INT8 quantization. Our compact models achieve diagnostic performance comparable to multi-million parameter ResNet baselines (MiniResNet Macro-AUC of 0.961, Macro F1-score of 0.70). Threshold optimization boosted recall on the rarest class (Hypertrophy) from 0.17 to 0.52. Post-quantization, model size is reduced by $87 \%$ (to 65 KB) with negligible performance drop, and inference latency by $18 x$ on a CPU, confirming edge suitability. This work provides a reproducible blueprint for developing trustworthy, lightweight, and interpretable AI-powered cardiology assistants.

title: Thrust Vectoring Simulation Study of Rotating Detonation Engine
authors: Wenjie He, Zhenjuan Xia, Hu Ma
publish: 2025 3rd Asian Aerospace and Astronautics Conference (AAAC)
abstract: This study focuses on the thrust vector control principle of rotary detonation engine (RDE), aiming to explore the characteristics of the flow field and its effect on thrust vector control under non-uniform injection pressure conditions. In this study, the effect of thrust vector control of a rotary detonation engine (RDE) under different injection pressure ratios and wave head numbers is analyzed in detail through two-dimensional numerical simulations. It is found that in the single-wave mode, the peak thrust generated by the burst wave in the high-pressure region is about 15% higher than that in the low-pressure region, while the combined moment deflection angle can be up to 17.28° in the simulation time. In the two-wave mode, the sustained thrust in the high-pressure region is 10% higher than that in the low-pressure region, while the combined-moment deflection angle can be up to 23.24° during the simulation time. The combined moment deflection angle of the single-wave mode is more sensitive to the change of injection pressure ratio, which increases by 3.98° for the single-wave mode and 1.77° for the dual-wave mode, with an increment of 44.5% of the single-wave mode for a difference of 0.3 in both injection pressure ratios.

title: A review of electric vehicles’ participation in resilience enhancement of power-transportation coupled networks
authors: Lingming Kong, Zeming Lu
publish: 2025 IEEE 9th Conference on Energy Internet and Energy System Integration (EI2)
abstract: The rapid adoption of electric vehicles (EVs) is driving a profound change in transportation and power networks, forming power–transportation coupled networks (PTCNs) where energy and mobility infrastructures are increasingly interdependent. This paper provides a comprehensive literature review on the resilience enhancement of PTCNs using EVs to withstand and recover from extreme events. We first present a conceptual framework of PTCN resilience, highlighting key components of infrastructure, resilience stages, and evaluation metrics. Next, we examine the applications of EVs in resilience enhancement, including energy backup resources, emergency mobile energy supply, microgrid islanding, and interaction with distributed energy resources, along with associated problem-solving methods such as robust optimization, stochastic programming, and multilayer network analysis. We then identify critical challenges, including bidirectional charging standards, uncertainty in EV availability, battery degradation, and market design barriers. The potential research directions are also discussed, involving joint infrastructure planning, real-time coordination, user behavior modeling, equitable incentive mechanisms, and data-driven control strategies. By synthesizing recent advances and open questions, this review underscores the potential of EVs to evolve from passive energy consumers to active resilience resources, supporting the development of low-carbon, disaster-resilient urban energy and transportation systems.

title: An Optimal Capacity Configuration Method for Multi-Type Energy Storage Systems Considering Temperature Differences
authors: Baihui Yuan, Rencheng Wang, Yan Zhao, Shuguang Xu, Nana Li, Siqi Yu, Cheng Li, Cuiping Li
publish: 2025 IEEE 9th Conference on Energy Internet and Energy System Integration (EI2)
abstract: To address the difficulty of balancing economic efficiency and operational flexibility in the capacity planning of multi-type energy storage systems under different temperature conditions, this paper proposes an optimal capacity configuration method for multi-type energy storage systems considering temperature differences. A planning–operation bi-level optimization framework is established, which comprehensively accounts for the technical characteristics of pumped hydro storage (PHS), electrochemical energy storage (BESS), and flywheel energy storage (FES). The K-means clustering method is applied to extract three representative scenarios—hot, mild, and cold days—and a temperature correction model is developed to capture the impact of ambient temperature on storage efficiency and self-discharge characteristics. The multi-objective evolutionary algorithm NSGA-II is employed to minimize both the annualized system cost and the curtailed energy, achieving coordinated optimization between capacity configuration and operational scheduling. A case study based on a provincial power system in Northeast China demonstrates that the optimal configuration yields installed capacities of 3,174 MW for PHS, 1,585 MW for BESS, and 182MW for FES, corresponding to a total energy capacity of approximately 24 GWh. The results indicate that the proposed method effectively balances system economy and flexibility under varying climatic conditions, thereby enhancing the adaptability and accuracy of energy storage capacity planning.

title: Research on Dispatching Strategy of Electric Heating Load Cluster Participating in Smoothing Wind Power Fluctuations
authors: Xiaojun Bai, Dawei Yang, Xiali Liu, Shu Cao, Qiang Huang, Yunhao Hu
publish: 2025 IEEE 9th Conference on Energy Internet and Energy System Integration (EI2)
abstract: Decentralized residential electric heating loads feature wide distribution and great regulation potential, serving as high-quality adjustable resources for mitigating wind power fluctuations. First, this paper proposes a hierarchical framework based on load aggregators according to the characteristics of decentralized residential electric heating loads. Then, focusing on the scenario where electric heating loads participate in system operation for the purpose of mitigating wind power fluctuations, a dispatching model is established and its regulation effect is analyzed. By solving the model, a target-oriented optimal strategy for load combination is obtained. Combined with a case study, the value of the "relay-type" control in deeply tapping load-side resources is verified, which provides a new idea for balancing economic efficiency and the demand for mitigating wind power fluctuations, and contributes to the absorption of new energy and the stable operation of power systems. Simulation results show that when used as flexible load-side adjustment resources to participate in power grid regulation, electric heating loads can meet the power grid regulation objectives and effectively mitigate the active power fluctuations of wind power.

title: Collaborative Scheduling of Integrated Electric-gas System Based on Adaptive Robust Optimization
authors: Zhenyuan Li, Lijuan Duan, Baoju Li, Yong Sun, Haifeng Zhang, Yao Wang
publish: 2025 IEEE 9th Conference on Energy Internet and Energy System Integration (EI2)
abstract: With the increasing coupling between electric and gas systems, integrated electric-gas system dispatching has emerged as a critical research frontier. By harnessing operational flexibility across source, network, and load sectors of gas systems, additional accommodation space for wind power integration in power systems can be created. However, accurate forecasting of wind power output remains challenging due to its inherent stochasticity, and its probabilistic distribution is often unavailable a priori. Conventional robust dispatch methods tend to exhibit excessive conservatism when relying on predefined uncertainty sets. To address these limitations, this paper proposes a data-driven adaptive robust dispatching framework for integrated electric-gas systems. By synergistically integrating stochastic programming and robust optimization paradigms, the proposed method leverages historical data to construct worst-case probability distribution scenarios, achieving an optimal trade-off between economic efficiency and operational conservatism. Simulation experiments on a coupled 6-bus power network and 6-node gas network validate the efficacy of the proposed approach in enhancing wind power accommodation while maintaining system reliability.

title: Thermodynamic and Performance Analysis of an Integrated Combustor System for a Rotating Detonation Turbine Engine
authors: Bowei Meng, Gaoyang Ge, Guangchuan Xu, Zhenjuan Xia, Hu Ma, Shengbing Zhou, Changsheng Zhou
publish: 2025 3rd Asian Aerospace and Astronautics Conference (AAAC)
abstract: This study develops a thermodynamic model for an integrated kerosene/air rotating detonation turbine engine (RDTE) system, incorporating the rotating detonation combustor (RDC) and its upstream and downstream components. The model takes the inlet air conditions, fuel properties, and combustor geometry as inputs. By coupling aerodynamic analysis with Chapman–Jouguet (C–J) detonation theory, it predicts the flow-field parameter distribution within the RDC and the averaged parameters at key cross-sections. The effects of key input parameters—including isolator inlet total pressure, isolator inlet total temperature, equivalence ratio, turbine inlet total temperature, fuel temperature, and RDC divergence ratio—on system performance are investigated theoretically. The results show that, with other inputs held constant, both the RDC total pressure ratio and the post-mixing total pressure ratio first increase and then decrease as the isolator inlet total pressure rises, while they decrease monotonically with increasing isolator inlet total temperature. At an isolator inlet total pressure of 0.6 MPa and a total temperature of 450 K, the RDC total pressure ratio reaches 1.533. Regarding fuel parameters, the post-mixing total pressure ratio first decreases and then increases as the equivalence ratio varies from 0.6 to 1.6, whereas increasing the fuel temperature results in a monotonic decrease. Moreover, appropriately increasing the turbine inlet total temperature and reducing the RDC width divergence ratio can significantly improve system performance, yielding a maximum post-mixing total pressure ratio of 1.418. This work provides effective theoretical guidance for the engineering application of RDTEs.

title: Enhancing American Sign Language Detection Through Histogram of Gradient Features Using Machine Learning Techniques
authors: Nithiya Baskaran, P Aurchana, K. Manasa, B. Jogeswara Rao
publish: 2026 Sixth International Conference on Advances in Electrical, Computing, Communications and Sustainable Technologies (ICAECT)
abstract: The predominant means of communication is speech; however, there are persons whose speaking or hearing abilities are impaired. Communication presents a significant barrier for persons with such disabilities. The primary objective of this work is to develop an efficient solution with machine learning for the speech and hearing impairment community with the help of AI. This study helps in the development of future technologies in the enhancement of communication accessibility. In the proposed work, a machine learning approach for the American Sign Language alphabet using image detection and processing techniques are utilized. The experiment was conducted using an American Sign Language recognition dataset obtained from Kaggle, which provides images of hand Gestures of the alphabet. The dataset was divided into training and testing subsets in the ratio of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$80: 20$</tex>, where 80 % of the data was used in training the model and the remaining 20 % was designated for testing the model. The dataset was normalized using denoising filters and proceeded with Canny edge detection to highlight sharp edge features. From the edge detected image, Histogram of Oriented Gradient features were extracted to detect the letter and shape information. These extracted features are then fed into the classifiers: Support Vector Machine, Random Forest, and KNearest Neighbors. The study was analyzed by experimental results, which show that the Support Vector Machine classifier achieved the highest accuracy of 93.33 %, closely followed by Random Forest and K-Nearest Neighbors. The analysis confirms that applying edge-based preprocessing along with the Histogram of Oriented Gradients feature extraction enables an effective classification of American Sign Language alphabets. This Comparative study provides a solid foundation for developing low-complexity, high-accuracy sign language recognition systems.

title: Joint Decision Optimization for Strategic Load Aggregators in Coupled Electricity-Carbon-Demand Response Markets Based on MTMA-DDPG
authors: Zhiyu Guan, Liheng Li, Zile Yang, Jie Xi, Zhangtian Xie, Hongsheng Xu
publish: 2025 IEEE 9th Conference on Energy Internet and Energy System Integration (EI2)
abstract: This paper investigates the deep coupling among the electricity market, carbon market, and demand response market by establishing a bi-level optimization model for the strategic Load Aggregators (LAs). In the upper level, the LA aims to maximize its overall profit through joint optimization of electricity bidding, carbon allowance trading, and demand response incentive strategies. The lower level integrates the clearing mechanisms of the electricity-carbon-demand response markets, enabling dynamic feedback among energy dispatch, electricity price, carbon price, and load adjustment. To address the complexity and uncertainty of multi-market decision-making, a Markov Decision Process (MDP) framework is developed, and a Multi-Task Multi-Agent Deep Deterministic Policy Gradient (MTMA-DDPG) algorithm is proposed to achieve coordinated strategy learning across coupled markets. Simulation results based on an improved IEEE 9-bus system demonstrate that the proposed approach significantly enhances the learning efficiency of the LA agent and enables it to acquire the optimal joint strategy across the electricity-carbon-demand response markets.

title: Circulating Current Suppression Strategy for Modular Multilevel Converters in Offshore Wind Farms
authors: Haokang Jin, Jialu Yuan, Hanwen Yu
publish: 2025 IEEE 9th Conference on Energy Internet and Energy System Integration (EI2)
abstract: With the increasing global demand for low-carbon requirements, the modular multilevel converter (MMC) has been widely adopted in flexible HVDC transmission systems due to its excellent scalability and favorable output characteristics. However, challenges such as circulating currents and sub-module voltage imbalance must be addressed. This article analyzes the mathematical model and main components of circulating currents. Based on this analysis, it proposes a double-frequency circulating current suppression strategy using a quasi-PIR controller. Simulations are performed to verify the effectiveness of the proposed control strategy from both qualitative and quantitative perspectives.

title: High-Frequency Resonance Mechanism and Contributing Factors in DFIG Wind Farm with SVG
authors: Jikai Chen, Shuangshuang Yao, Zhuang Chu, Haibo Zhong
publish: 2025 IEEE 9th Conference on Energy Internet and Energy System Integration (EI2)
abstract: Existing research frequently attributes the frequent occurrence of resonances to three main factors: improper settings of internal control parameters in converters, the capacitive effect of collection cables, and the capacitive nature of some equipment's impedance characteristics within specific frequency bands. However, the fundamental causes and influencing factors behind high-frequency resonance in parallel systems comprising a Static Var Generator (SVG) and clustered Doubly-Fed Induction Generators (DFIG) have not been thoroughly investigated. Therefore, this study employs the harmonic linearization method to establish sequence impedance models for both the SVG and the clustered DFIGs. Using impedance analysis combined with stability criteria, the mechanism by which the energization of unload lines high-frequency resonance in the wind farm system is revealed. We specifically analyzed the impact of the number of wind turbines in operation, and variations in wind speed on the overall impedance of the wind farm system. Finally, electromagnetic transient simulations validated the correctness of the theoretical analysis concerning the resonance's nature and its triggering factors.

title: Frequency-Domain Approach for Predicting Random Vibration Fatigue Life of Spacecraft Honeycomb Sandwich Structure Considering Fatigue Property Dispersion
authors: Daiyang Gao, Guangyuan Shi, Wenhe Liao
publish: 2025 3rd Asian Aerospace and Astronautics Conference (AAAC)
abstract: A frequency-domain model for predicting random vibration fatigue life of a single structural component of honeycomb sandwich structure was proposed by p-S-N curves, incorporating the fatigue property scatter effect of material, thereby obtaining the cumulative distribution function P(T) of fatigue life. Furthermore, considering the distribution characteristics of random vibration fatigue lives of different structural components honeycomb sandwich structure, a frequency-domain method for predicting the overall random vibration fatigue life of spacecraft honeycomb sandwich structure was developed by the derivation on the Cumulative Distribution Function (CDF) for the minimum random variable based on structural reliability analysis. This method is validated by the numerical analysis on the random vibration fatigue life experiment in literature, and then applied on the fatigue failure analysis on spacecraft honeycomb sandwich structure.

title: Machine Learning Integrated Zero Trust Automation with DevOps Principles for Continuous Security Enforcement
authors: Ankur Mahida
publish: 2026 Sixth International Conference on Advances in Electrical, Computing, Communications and Sustainable Technologies (ICAECT)
abstract: Modern distributed infrastructure, ongoing delivery pipelines and hybrid cloud environments have caused the existing models of security based on perimeters to be inadequate. The study presents the ML-ZTAF (Machine Learning Integrated Zero Trust Automation Framework) as the dynamic and flexible security enforcement framework based on the principles of Zero Trust and the operation of DevOps to make sure that verification is done continuously, behavior is observed, and policies are enforced automatically. The framework uses the machine learning-based anomaly detection, contextual trust scoring, and automated identity validation to remove implicit trust and actively reduce security threats throughout the software lifecycle. ML-ZTAF is an ongoing user, device, and system behavior analysis to detect deviation and add access controls without interrupting operational processes. Comparison of experiments with the existing Zero Trust models has shown that ML-ZTAF produces better detection accuracy, reduced false positive rates, and better automation maturity and better scalability. The findings reveal that ML-ZTAF helps companies to stay resilient in security in real time, as well as facilitate fast and scalable deployments based on DevOps.

title: VFIR: Vector Fields Implicit Representation for Surface Reconstruction from Point Clouds
authors: Siyu Jin, Mingxiu Tuo, Yikuan Gu, Yuhe Zhang, Shunli Zhang
publish: IEEE Transactions on Visualization and Computer Graphics
abstract: Surface reconstruction from point clouds has attracted considerable attention in the computer vision community. Most deep learning-based methods focus on modeling signed distance functions (SDFs) and unsigned distance functions (UDFs). However, SDFs are limited to watertight models, and UDFs are non-differentiable at the surface boundary, hindering the learning of smooth representations. In this paper, we introduce a novel method, VFIR, which leverages neural implicit functions to learn vector fields (VFs) for 3D shape modeling. We enhance the network's fitting capability by displacing points along the predicted vector directions, aiming to reduce the deviation between the generated point cloud and the original input. To accelerate convergence, we introduce a progressive learning strategy that incrementally incorporates denser point clouds and corresponding normals during training. For isosurface extraction, we define truncated vector fields (TVFs) that focus computation around surfaces and present an optimized three state marching cubes (OT-MC) algorithm tailored to the vector field representation. Extensive experiments demonstrate that VFIR achieves state-of-the-art performance with high accuracy and robustness across diverse 3D models, exhibiting strong generalization capabilities and thus representing a promising solution for surface reconstruction across various applications.

title: Lightweight Dual Correlation Variance Compensation for Salient Object Detection in Optical Remote Sensing Images
authors: Lamei Di, Bin Zhang, Yiming Wang
publish: IEEE Transactions on Geoscience and Remote Sensing
abstract: Salient Object Detection in Optical Remote Sensing Images (ORSI-SOD) confronts three fundamental challenges: extreme multi-scale object heterogeneity where targets span multiple orders of magnitude within single scenes, subtle inter-class boundaries with minimal contrast between objects and backgrounds, and computational scalability demands for high-resolution imagery processing. Existing approaches suffer from a critical limitation in that they fail to exploit the discriminative information encoded in inter-layer correlation variance, which contains essential boundary and transition cues that are systematically lost during conventional feature fusion processes. This paper introduces MambaCVC, a lightweight dual correlation variance compensation network that explicitly models and compensates for correlation variance patterns to enhance feature discriminability while maintaining linear computational complexity. Our approach centers on two synergistic modules: the Global Correlation Variance Compensation Module (GC-VCM) that selectively amplifies semantically meaningful long-range dependencies through correlation variance modeling across distant features, and the Local Correlation Variance Compensation Module (LC-VCM) that preserves fine-grained boundary details via variance-driven local enhancement mechanisms. To achieve computational efficiency, we leverage the VMamba backbone with minimal architectural adaptations optimized for ORSI-SOD. Comprehensive experiments on three public ORSI-SOD datasets demonstrate that MambaCVC achieves state-of-the-art performance with remarkable efficiency, requiring only 6.25G FLOPs and 7.26M parameters.

title: Call for Papers for IEEE Engineering Management Review
authors: N/A
publish: IEEE Engineering Management Review

title: Adaptive Generative Model Inversion Attacks in Edge-Cloud Collaborative Inference Systems
authors: Haoyu Liu, Yuwen Chen, Zhen Yang
publish: IEEE Transactions on Mobile Computing
abstract: Previous model inversion attacks in collaborative inference systems have only been demonstrated on simple models. They failed to produce convincing results on large models or large images. To improve the quality of the model inversion attack, two adaptive generative model inversion attacks are proposed. First, the distributional prior of the publicly available pre-trained Generative Adversarial Nets are used to guide the reconstruction process. Second, previous works only search the latent space of the pre-trained GAN. However, the pre-trained generative model and the target model are trained on different datasets. The distribution shifts of the datasets can lead to inevitable reconstruction errors. To handle the distribution shifts, the pre-trained generative model will be fine-tuned for target instances in the proposed adaptive generative model inversion attack. As a result, the proposed attacks can better handle the distribution shifts. And there is no need to train a separate image prior for each target model. The generators can be used to attack different models trained on different datasets within the same domain. Our extensive experiments demonstrate that the proposed attack achieves up to a 25.7% improvement in PSNR and a 26.4% improvement in SSIM compared to previous methods.

title: Label-free Electrochemical Immunosensor Toward IGFBP-7 Biomarker for Early Monitoring of Acute Kidney Injury_supp1-3671274.pdf
authors: Tanawut Tantimongcolwat
publish: N/A
abstract: Insulin-like growth factor-binding protein 7 (IGFBP-7) is a potential biomarker for early diagnosis of acute kidney injury (AKI). Herein, we have developed a novel label-free electrochemical immunosensor based on the self-assembly of 6-(ferrocenyl)hexanethiol (Fc) and antibody-coupled L-cysteine on the gold nanoparticle-modified electrode (Ab/Fc-Au/SPCE) for sensitive detection of IGFBP-7 in urine. Fc was directly embedded on the electrode surface to serve as an intrinsic redox signal for the IGFBP-7 immunosensor. The binding of IGFBP-7 target to the immobilized antibody resulted in a decrease in the peak current of the differential pulse voltammetry (DPV) measurement. The Ab/Fc-Au/SPCE exhibited a good linear DPV response to IGFBP-7 concentrations ranging from 0.0001 to 100.0 pg mL<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">-1</sup>, with a limit of detection (LOD) of 0.07 pg mL<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">-1</sup>. Validation using spiked artificial urines showed recovery rates in a range of 79.8 to 109.3%. The proposed immunosensor demonstrated good specificity and long-term stability, which are crucial properties for biosensor implementation. Importantly, the electrochemical IGFBP-7 immunosensor can function without the need for external redox agents, requiring only simple steps of sample incubation and subsequent PBS buffer rinsing. Our findings demonstrate a promising potential of Ab/Fc-Au/SPCE immunosensor as a Point-of-Care Testing (POCT) device for rapid detection of IGFBP-7 in early AKI diagnosis.

title: Deep Reinforcement Learning for Resource Management in Device-to-Device (D2D)-Assisted 6G Networks: Current Solutions, Open Issues, and Future Directions
authors: Hafiz Muhammad Fahad Noman, Kaharudin Dimyati, Effariza Hanafi, Kamarul Ariffin Noordin, Azrin Md Kasim
publish: IEEE Internet of Things Journal
abstract: Sixth-generation (6G) wireless networks have the potential to offer several emerging technologies that require efficient resource allocation. However, traditional resource allocation methods have limited performance in mitigating interference, which hinders scalability and compromises joint optimization of spectral, energy, and computational efficiency. This motivates the implementation of deep reinforcement learning (DRL) techniques for resource management in device-to-device (D2D)-enabled 6G systems towards self-sustainable networks (SSNs). Hence, this paper presents a review of DRL algorithms for resource management in D2D networks. The state-of-the-art DRL algorithms, including value-based, policy-based, and hybrid methods, are reviewed. It highlights their strengths and limitations for resource allocation and power control. Moreover, DRL-based solutions are discussed for mode selection, spectrum allocation, and power allocation, as well as the joint optimization of resource management to enhance network performance in terms of both energy and spectral efficiency. It also presents advanced learning techniques, such as multi-agent DRL and federated DRL, as potential solutions to improve network scalability and preserve users’ privacy. Finally, the paper summarizes the open issues and future research directions for adaptive and explainable resource management techniques in D2D-enabled Industrial Internet of Things (IIoT) and Integrated Sensing and Communication (ISAC) systems in the upcoming 6G networks.

title: REACT3D: Recovering Articulations for Interactive Physical 3D Scenes
authors: Zhao Huang, Boyang Sun, Alexandros Delitzas, Jiaqi Chen, Marc Pollefeys
publish: IEEE Robotics and Automation Letters
abstract: Interactive 3D scenes are increasingly vital for embodied intelligence, yet existing datasets remain limited due to the labor-intensive process of annotating part segmentation, kinematic types, and motion trajectories. We present REACT3D, a scalable zero-shot framework that converts static 3D scenes into simulation-ready interactive replicas with consistent geometry, enabling direct use in diverse downstream tasks. Our contributions include: (i) openable-object detection and segmentation to extract candidate movable parts from static scenes, (ii) articulation estimation that infers joint types and motion parameters, (iii) hidden-geometry completion followed by interactive object assembly, and (iv) interactive scene integration in widely supported formats to ensure compatibility with standard simulation platforms. We achieve state-of-the-art performance on detection/segmentation and articulation metrics across diverse indoor scenes, demonstrating the effectiveness of our framework and providing a practical foundation for scalable interactive scene generation, thereby lowering the barrier to large-scale research on articulated scene understanding.

title: Distributed Lloyd-based algorithm for uncertainty-aware multi-robot under-canopy flocking
authors: Manuel Boldrer, Vít Krátký, Viktor Walter, Martin Saska
publish: IEEE Robotics and Automation Letters
abstract: In this letter, we present a distributed algorithm for flocking in complex environments that operates at constant altitude, without explicit communication, no a priori information about the environment, and by using only on-board sensing and computation capabilities. We provide sufficient conditions to guarantee collision avoidance with obstacles and other robots without exceeding a desired maximum distance from a predefined set of neighbors (flocking or proximity maintenance constraint) during the mission. The proposed approach allows to operate in crowded scenarios and to explicitly deal with tracking errors and on-board sensing errors. The algorithm was verified through simulations with varying number of UAVs and also through numerous real-world experiments in a dense forest involving up to four UAVs.

title: Lightweight Kinematic and Static Modeling of Cable-Driven Continuum Robots via Actuation-Space Energy Formulation
authors: Ke Wu, Yuhao Wang, Kevin Henry, Cesare Stefanini, Gang Zheng
publish: IEEE Robotics and Automation Letters
abstract: Continuum robots, inspired by octopus arms and elephant trunks, combine dexterity with intrinsic compliance, making them well suited for unstructured and confined environments. Yet their continuously deformable morphology poses challenges for motion planning and control, calling for accurate but lightweight models. We propose the Lightweight Actuation-Space Energy Modeling (LASEM) framework for cable-driven continuum robots, which formulates actuation potential energy directly in actuation space. LASEM yields an analytical forward model derived from geometrically nonlinear beam and rod theories via Hamilton's principle, while avoiding explicit modeling of cable-backbone contact. It accepts both force and displacement inputs, thereby unifying kinematic and static formulations. Assuming the friction is neglected, the framework generalizes to nonuniform geometries, arbitrary cable routings, distributed loading and axial extensibility, while remaining computationally efficient for real-time use. Numerical simulations validate its accuracy, and a semi-analytical iterative scheme is developed for inverse kinematics. To address discretization in practical robots, LASEM further reformulates the functional minimization as a numerical optimization, which also naturally incorporates cable potential energy without explicit contact modeling.

title: An Inductive Hollow-shaft Absolute Angular Displacement Sensor with Large size
authors: Yongshang Liu, Qifu Tang, Qingyun Tang
publish: IEEE Sensors Journal
abstract: Angular displacement sensors often face a trade-off among high measurement accuracy, low environmental sensitivity, and an ultra-large hollow structure. This paper presents an large size inductive hollow-shaft absolute angular displacement sensor capable of achieving high precision with a large hollow ratio. Its unique structural design enables critical applications in energy industry, heavy industry, and automotive manufacturing, where both high accuracy and reliability are required. The sensor’s stator and rotor have planar structure, whose coils are fabricated on PCBs (Printed Circuit Board), with an outer diameter of 400mm and an inner diameter of 340mm. The stator generates high-frequency alternated magnetic field, while the rotor receives it and generates secondary magnetic field. The pickup coils of the stator are induced by the secondary magnetic field to output signals related to the angular displacement of the rotor. The sensor was designed with a coarse sensing channel and a fine sensing channel. The coarse one and fine one co-operate to realize accurate absolute measurement. The sensor structure and working principle were verified by simulation first. Then, a sensor prototype was fabricated and tested. Experimental results show that the prototype could provide absolute measurement in different stator-rotor gaps and achieve measurement accuracy of -32.06″to 34.68″over the full range of 0° to 360°.

title: Robust Design of Beyond-Diagonal Reconfigurable Intelligent Surface Empowered RSMA-SWIPT System Under Channel Estimation Errors
authors: Muhammad Asif, Zain Ali, Asim Ihsan, Ali Ranjha, Zhu Shoujin, Manzoor Ahmed, Xingwang Li, Symeon Chatzinotas
publish: IEEE Transactions on Wireless Communications
abstract: This work explores the integration of rate-splitting multiple access (RSMA), simultaneous wireless information and power transfer (SWIPT), and beyond-diagonal reconfigurable intelligent surface (BD-RIS) to enhance the spectral-efficiency, energy efficiency, coverage, and connectivity of future sixth-generation (6G) communication networks. Specifically, with a multiuser BD-RIS-empowered RSMA-SWIPT system, we jointly optimize the transmit precoding vectors, the common rate proportion of users, the power-splitting ratios, and scattering matrix of the BD-RIS, under the assumption of imperfect channel state information (CSI). Additionally, to better capture practical hardware behavior, we incorporate a nonlinear energy harvesting model and ensure that the resulting system satisfies all energy harvesting constraints. In the considered system, we design a robust optimization framework to maximize the system sum-rate, while explicitly accounting for the worst-case impact of CSI uncertainties. To tackle the inherent non-convexity of the problem, we introduce an alternating optimization framework that partitions the problem into several blocks, which are optimized in an iterative manner. More specifically, the transmit precoding vectors are optimized by reformulating the problem as a convex semidefinite programming problem through successive-convex approximation (SCA), whereas the inherently convex power-splitting problem is solved using the MOSEK-enabled CVX toolbox. Subsequently, to optimize the scattering matrix of the BD-RIS, we first employ SCA to reformulate the problem into a convex form, and then design a manifold optimization strategy based on the conjugate-gradient method. Finally, numerical simulations are conducted to evaluate the performance of the proposed scheme, revealing significant performance improvements over existing benchmarks and demonstrating rapid convergence within a reasonable number of iterations.

title: A Tor-based Anonymous Network Covert Channel
authors: Qi Chen, Ming Yang, Zhen Ling, Zixia Liu, Lan Luo, Changwei Cao, Shan Wang, Xinwen Fu
publish: IEEE Transactions on Dependable and Secure Computing
abstract: Network Covert Channels (NCC) enhance covertness by concealing the existence of information transmission. However, traditional NCCs remain vulnerable to traffic analysis. Once NCC is detected, adversaries can breach anonymity by uncovering users' network identities and even communication relationships. While certain indirect NCCs offer limited anonymity to protect the identity of at most one party and the relationship, this level proves insufficient. This paper proposes ANCC, an innovative Anonymous Network Covert Channel that is the first to achieve comprehensive anonymity for the sender, the receiver and the communication relationship. By leveraging the Tor network's Hidden Service Directories (HSDirs) as intermediate nodes, Tor-based ANCC modulates covert information through the publication and retrieval statuses of hidden services distributed on multiple HSDirs. This mechanism allows ANCC traffic to blend seamlessly into legitimate Tor traffic, ensuring both robust covertness and high-level anonymity. Theoretical analysis demonstrates that even against a powerful adversary compromising fifty intermediate nodes, the detection probability remains below 0.25%, with the risk of identity or relationship exposure staying negligible (under 0.0021% and 0.00002% respectively). Additionally, the multiple HSDirs supporting parallel transmission enhance the channel capacity and error correction encoding strengthens the robustness. Extensive evaluation within the real-world Tor network demonstrates a transmission accuracy exceeding 99.6% and a channel capacity of around 3 Kbps, proving its effectiveness for practical applications.

title: Real-time Monocular 2D and 3D Perception of Endoluminal Scenes for Controlling Flexible Robotic Endoscopic Instruments_supp2-3672482.mp4
authors: ruofeng wei
publish: N/A
abstract: Endoluminal surgery offers a minimally invasive option for early-stage gastrointestinal and urinary tract, but is limited by basic surgical tools and a steep learning curve. Robotic systems, particularly continuum robots, provide flexible instruments that enable precise, intuitive tissue resection in confined spaces, potentially improving outcomes. This paper presents an integrated visual perception platform for a continuum robotic system in endoluminal surgery. Our objective is to leverage monocular endoscopic image-based perception algorithms to accurately identify the position and orientation of flexible instruments and measure their distances from surrounding tissues. This thorough understanding of continuum robots and surgical scenes enhances the robustness of robotic procedures. We introduce 2D and 3D learning-based perception algorithms and develop a physically-realistic simulator that models the dynamics of flexible instruments. This simulator features a pipeline for generating realistic endoluminal scenes, enabling control of flexible robots in a realistic environment and substantial data collection. Using a continuum robot prototype, we conducted extensive evaluations, including module assessments and system-level evaluation of the perception platform. Results demonstrate that our perception algorithms significantly improve control of flexible instruments, reducing manipulation time by over 70% for trajectory-following tasks and enhancing the understanding of complex surgical scenarios, leading to robust endoluminal surgeries.

title: Towards Optimal Communication Scheduling with Automatic Configuration for Distributed DNN Training
authors: Jinbin Hu, Xinming Xu, Hao Wang, Yiqing Ma, Xiaolin Chen, Yiming Zhang, Jin Wang, Kai Chen
publish: IEEE Transactions on Networking
abstract: ByteScheduler partitions and rearranges tensor transmissions to improve the communication efficiency of distributed Deep Neural Network (DNN) training. The configuration of hyper-parameters (i.e., the partition size and the credit size) is critical to the effectiveness of partitioning and rearrangement. Currently ByteScheduler adopts Bayesian Optimization (BO) to find the optimal configuration for the hyper-parameters beforehand. In practice, however, various runtime factors (such as worker node status and network conditions) change over time, making the statically-determined one-shot configuration result suboptimal for real-world DNN training. To address this problem, in this paper we present a realtime configuration method (called AutoByte) that automatically and timely searches the optimal hyper-parameters as the training systems dynamically change. AutoByte extends the ByteScheduler framework with a meta network, which takes the systems’ runtime statistics as its input, dynamically adjusts the triggering threshold based on system environment characteristics, and outputs predictions for speedups under specific configurations. Evaluation results on various DNN models show that AutoByte can dynamically tune the hyper-parameters with low resource usage, and deliver up to 33.2% higher performance than the best static configuration method on the ByteScheduler framework.

title: Predictive Analytics for Machine Failures Using IoT Sensor Data and Machine Learning
authors: Geetha R, Diana Grace Thomas
publish: 2026 Sixth International Conference on Advances in Electrical, Computing, Communications and Sustainable Technologies (ICAECT)
abstract: Predicting machine failure is essential for maintaining industrial dependability. It also reduces time and lowers operating expenses. This work observed the effects of sensor-based variables on machine health for sustainable industralization. It also includes temperature, input pressure, current consumption, rotational position, air quality index, volatile organic compounds and ultrasonic proximity data. Machine learning models for predictive maintenance were trained on a large dataset that includes both operating and failure status. Accuracy, precision, recall and F1-score were used to assess the model performance in order to determine its robustness and dependability. The results of the comparative study shows that the chosen models had a high prediction accuracy. The top-performing model had a balanced F1-score and an accuracy above 93%, demonstrating a good capacity to handle imbalanced failure data. These results shows that combining machine learning with multi-sensor data greatly enhances failure prediction, allowing for proactive maintenance plans and prolonging machine lifespan in actual industrial settings.

title: Knowledge Graph Integration in Natural Language Processing for the Smart Cooking
authors: Vădean Vlad, Camelia Lemnaru, Vlad Andrei Negru, Rodica Potolea
publish: 2025 IEEE 21st International Conference on Intelligent Computer Communication and Processing (ICCP)
abstract: In this paper, we propose a solution that leverages knowledge graphs to model and compare cooking recipes, aiming to bridge the gap between free-form user instructions and a structured recipe database. By integrating a hierarchical, domain-specific knowledge graph with inductive graph convolutional networks (for scalable embedding) and a fine-tuned multilingual transformer (for precise information extraction), our approach excels in both efficiency and matching accuracy. We achieve 97.91% accuracy on embedded recipes within the semantic network, yielding a maximum similarity of 0.54 for different versions of the same dish, and reach up to 0.32 similarity for dishes not present in the database. The motivation for this work lies in the need for end users, especially in low-resource or multilingual settings, to retrieve reliable, relevant recipes without manual browsing or query formulation. Our system addresses this by parsing user-provided recipe text in six languages, embedding both user and candidate recipes into a shared vector space, and computing similarity results to return the best match.

title: Overvoltage Prevention for Wind-thermal-bundled HVDC Transmission System during Commutation Failure
authors: Xiuting Rong, Xiaotong Wang, Chao Huo, Songhao Yang, Yubo Zhang, Xuetao Dong, Zhiguo Hao
publish: 2025 IEEE 9th Conference on Energy Internet and Energy System Integration (EI2)
abstract: In line commutated converter based high voltage direct current (LCC-HVDC) systems, commutation failure can easily induce transient overvoltage at the sending-end converter bus, threatening equipment safety and system stability, particularly in wind-thermal-bundled power transmission systems. Through mechanism analysis and simulation, this paper derives a mathematical formulation for overvoltage peak calculation and reveals that the "first high then low" dynamic characteristic of DC current during commutation failure leads to reactive power surplus, which is the primary cause of transient overvoltage. To conquer this challenge, a coordinated suppression strategy is proposed. On the rectifier and inverter sides, an enhanced VDCOL control is adopted, which employs a dual-mode switching strategy to dynamically regulate the reference current, thereby suppressing reactive power oscillations. On the wind turbine side, a fast voltage control based on converters is adopted, which dynamically regulates the reactive current commands of the Rotor Side Converter (RSC) and Grid Side Converter (GSC) to provide active voltage support. PSCAD/EMTDC simulations demonstrate the effectiveness of the proposed control strategy. Through establishing diverse fault conditions, the adaptability of the suppression strategy is verified under different short-circuit fault types and durations. This provides a validated solution for securing renewable energy integration.

title: AgriBot: An LLM-Guided IoT System for Real-Time Soil Monitoring and Irrigation Advice
authors: Saranya S. N., Madhumala R. B., S. Dishan Samuel, Shashank G. Yaplar, Shashwath V., Vincent Samuel Paul
publish: 2026 Sixth International Conference on Advances in Electrical, Computing, Communications and Sustainable Technologies (ICAECT)
abstract: AgriBot is an LLM-based decision-support system integrated with sensor-based monitoring for IoT irrigation control. The prototype, built on an ESP32 node, measures soil moisture, pH, temperature, and ambient relative humidity in real time. An LLM constrained by control rules generates human-understandable irrigation recommendations with explanations. Seven days of testing using 240 aggregated samples demonstrated water savings of approximately 37.5% (±3.2%) relative to manual scheduling while maintaining soil moisture within the target 40-70% band. The system exhibits a mean response latency of about 1.6 s and energy consumption near 122mJ per measurement cycle. The results indicate that hybrid rule-AI controllers can achieve effective resource optimization on low-cost embedded hardware.

title: A Dual-Layer Optimization Model for Off-Grid Wind-Solar-Storage PEM Electrolyzer Hydrogen Production Based on NSGA-II: Scenario Generation Combining Kernel Density Estimation and Copula
authors: Shanshan Xu, Ying Zhou, Chao Zhang, Hai Jiang, Junhong Hao
publish: 2025 IEEE 9th Conference on Energy Internet and Energy System Integration (EI2)
abstract: This paper investigates an off-grid wind-solar-storage PEM electrolysis hydrogen production system, proposing a capacity optimization configuration model for such systems. First, a combined method of kernel density estimation and Copula functions is employed to construct the joint distribution of wind and solar power outputs. Subsequently, K-means clustering is applied for dimensionality reduction, generating scenarios that balance correlation and representativeness for wind and solar resources. Second, addressing system economics and stability, a two-layer capacity optimization model for the off-grid wind-solar-storage PEM electrolysis hydrogen production system is established based on the NSGA-II algorithm. Finally, a specific case study is selected to validate the proposed two-layer optimization model.

title: Research on Carbon Emission Accounting Method and Optimization Strategy of Near-Zero Carbon Substations
authors: Ting Zeng, Mingpeng Yuan, Chuteng Ni, Xiaoming Gao, Zhangqi Lyu
publish: 2025 IEEE 9th Conference on Energy Internet and Energy System Integration (EI2)
abstract: To respond to the "double carbon" goals and accurately control the carbon emissions of substations throughout their life cycle, this paper constructs a life-cycle carbon emission accounting method and model for near-zero carbon substations, clarifies the accounting boundaries and stage division, selects authoritative carbon emission factors, and establishes quantitative formulas for each stage. Taking a 110kV intelligent substation as a case, the carbon emissions during the construction, building material production and transportation, operation, and demolition stages are calculated, and the key carbon emission links are identified. Based on the accounting results, optimization strategies are proposed from three dimensions: source emission reduction, process consumption reduction, and end carbon sink enhancement, providing technical support for the construction and operation of near-zero carbon substations.

title: Understanding EV Charging Patterns under TOU Tariffs: A Machine Learning Approach
authors: Zhifeng Guo, Kaile Zhou
publish: 2025 IEEE 9th Conference on Energy Internet and Energy System Integration (EI2)
abstract: The rapid proliferation of electric vehicles (EVs) significantly contributes to reducing greenhouse gas emissions. However, uncoordinated charging of large EV fleets presents substantial challenges to maintaining grid stability. To address this issue, guiding EV users through strategic price signals can not only alleviate grid pressure but also enhance social welfare by reducing charging costs. This study introduces a machine learning-based framework to analyze EV charging behaviors within the context of time-of-use (TOU) electricity tariffs, utilizing charging records from the stations operated by State Grid Anhui Electric Power Company. The framework identified 15 typical intraday charging profiles, comprising three main user groups that predominantly charge during peak tariff periods, seven groups that charge during flat tariff periods, and five groups that charge during valley tariff periods. These findings indicate that existing TOU tariff structures may not be sufficiently effective as price signals to promote coordinated charging behaviors. To enhance policy effectiveness, additional customer engagement initiatives should be introduced to increase awareness of pricing schemes and encourage energy-conscious charging habits. The insights gained from this study provide valuable guidance for power companies seeking to refine TOU tariff strategies and improve the overall impact of related policies.

title: Brain Tumour Detection System Using Deep CNN and GenAI
authors: Poovizhi P, Rajasekaran P, Vijaya Kumar T, Sadhana S A, Sanjana N, Aakash S
publish: 2026 Sixth International Conference on Advances in Electrical, Computing, Communications and Sustainable Technologies (ICAECT)
abstract: The identifying of brain tumor is one of the most important elements of the whole process of medical diagnosis nowadays and the result must be very accurate and explainable so that the clinical decision-making process be enabled. In general, the new method can be utilized for a simplified version of a deep convolutional neural network (CNN) which is adjusted to emphasis on the extraction and the identification of the predominant features of the medical imaging data. When all the redundant or unnecessary data are rejected, the process becomes very precise for diagnostics and at the same time, it has a very small computational load. The core of this process is a model that has been pre-trained and optimized to identify tumor patterns by feature prioritization. Light, a few features, and an effective model design were what mainly contributed to the successful carrying out of the categorization. Hence, for clinical application which makes the framework computationally and scaled, this is highly suitable. One of the things that separate this hybrid system from the others is that this is a GenAI (Generative Artificial Intelligence) module that has been implemented in order to solve the interpretability problem of conventional deep learning models. The reason for classification is the combination of the most important tumor features gained from the study together with the descriptive, human-like explanations that are the result of the generative power of this component, thus, the CNN is persuaded to make its output further. The feedback loop, in addition to the explanation, points out and reports any errors or inaccuracies in the classification) thus, there is a way for the system to be improved by the use of the reiterative approach. Moreover, it was very user-friendly and the doctors could have an intuitive interface to use the system for interpretability and categorization. The solution is a revelation of the potential of future AI-based healthcare technology development and also more brain tumor detection is the best way to the improved results in the form of diagnostic results that are more accurate and medical doctors that are empowered.

title: Analytical and Computational Study of MRDN in Caterpillar Graphs using Python with Networking Applications
authors: R. Vinodhini, T. N. M. Malini Mai
publish: 2026 Sixth International Conference on Advances in Electrical, Computing, Communications and Sustainable Technologies (ICAECT)
abstract: This study investigates the structural characterization of the Middle Roman Domination Number for any caterpillar graph; a significant subclass of trees derived from a central spine by attaching pendent vertices. The proposed theorem establishes an explicit formula for MRDN in terms of the spine length and the degree distribution of pendent vertices. By constructing a Middle Roman Dominating Function that minimizes the total weight under adjacency constraints, we analytically derive MRDN and verify its correctness through Python-based enumeration. The algorithm employs a combinatorial search strategy that evaluates all feasible MRDF assignments. Empirical validation on caterpillars of varying orders confirms the theoretical bound and demonstrates the consistent minimality of the derived the value. This approach integrates theoretical graph analysis with computational verification, reinforcing the applicability of domination concepts in tree-like network structures and hierarchical communication systems.

title: Techniques for Discrete Mitigation and Control in Four Area System With DFIG System
authors: Gaurav Jain, Ritu Tak, Bhuvnesh Rathore, Mayengbam Sashilal Singh, Aashiq Hussain
publish: 2026 Sixth International Conference on Advances in Electrical, Computing, Communications and Sustainable Technologies (ICAECT)
abstract: Distributed power (DP) generation through solar and wind energy holds immense potential in meeting future energy demands. Recent advancements in grid technology enable efficient utilization of local energy sources, minimizing transmission line losses and facilitating enhanced power distribution capabilities. This study focuses on examining the impacts of disturbances and faults within a four-area system linked to a Doubly Fed Induction Generator (DFIG) using discrete-time modeling. The primary control approach within the four-area system involving the DFIG incorporates various discrete-time Power System Stabilizers (PSSs). A comparative analysis of time-domain responses is conducted across various scenarios, including systems without controllers (no PSS), systems equipped with traditional PSSs such as Delta Omega, Multi-Band, Delta Pa and Fuzzy Logic (FL) based PSSs. A discrete-time mode PSS is specifically tailored for the silent-pole synchronous generator associated with the DFIG system. To assess the performance of the Fuzzy Logic (FL) controller in ensuring power system stability, a test DFIG model is employed. Time-domain simulations of the test DFIG model are carried out using the MATLAB/Simulink (R2015a) environment.

title: Design and Model Verification of a Containerized Energy Storage System for Harsh Antarctic Environment
authors: Kang Fu, Bin Wang, Yu Su
publish: 2025 IEEE 9th Conference on Energy Internet and Energy System Integration (EI2)
abstract: For Antarctic research stations with high-proportion clean energy systems, electrochemical energy storage serves as the core equipment for providing frequency regulation and peak shaving capabilities while supporting short-term power balancing. In the extreme Antarctic environment, conventional energy storage systems suffer from poor low-temperature performance and inadequate utilization of waste heat. To address this, this paper designs a containerized liquid-cooled lithium battery energy storage system adapted for Antarctic low-temperature environments. A collaborative heat transfer model integrating the battery, liquid cooling, and container modules was established. Based on actual field requirements in Antarctica, heat transfer simulation tests were conducted under various operational and insulation scenarios. Simulation results demonstrate the feasibility and effectiveness of the proposed containerized storage system in the extreme Antarctic environment.

title: Exploring the Dynamics of Modular Multiplicative Divisor Labeling in Interconnected Cycle Graph Gangs
authors: P. Kalarani, R. Revathi, R. Vijaykrishnaraj, V G Smilin Shali, B. Kalaiselvi, B. Roopa
publish: 2026 Sixth International Conference on Advances in Electrical, Computing, Communications and Sustainable Technologies (ICAECT)
abstract: This study investigates Modular Multiplicative Divisor (MMD) labeling in gangs of cycle graphs, a structure that has been largely unexplored in graph theory. The work aims to identify optimal labeling configurations that satisfy MMD criteria while examining the structural and labeling characteristics of interconnected cycle graphs. A systematic framework is developed for assigning vertex and edge labels, ensuring consistency through modular validation. The proposed approach addresses unresolved issues in MMD labeling and extends its theoretical foundation within combinatorial graph theory. Verified through illustrative examples, the results confirm the existence and reliability of MMD labeling in gangs of cycle graphs and provide valuable insights for further research on complex graph structures.

title: Wideband Harmonic Impedance Envelope Method for AC Systems Based on Nonlinear Programming
authors: Chenting Zhao, Haitao Zhang, Kejun Yang, Yiming Wei, Baoquan Li, Juzhen Wu, Kanghang He, Hongchuan Dong
publish: 2025 IEEE 9th Conference on Energy Internet and Energy System Integration (EI2)
abstract: In resonance stability analysis, the stable region of a system can correspond to the impedance stability intervals of the source and load subsystems, making the description of impedance intervals is important. Therefore, to efficiently characterize the impedance variation range of the system under various operating conditions, compensate for the limitations of traditional single-operating-condition analysis, and improve the comprehensiveness and engineering practicality of resonance risk assessment, this paper proposes a method for calculating and enveloping the wide-band harmonic impedance of AC systems. First, the wideband harmonic impedance model of the AC system, observed from the Point of Common Coupling (PCC) is established. Second, the acquisition of the impedance envelope is transformed into a nonlinear programming problem with the objective of "minimizing the envelope area", and the interior-point barrier method is used to solve the problem. To verify the effectiveness of this method, this paper conducts application tests with the IEEE 39-Bus system as an example. The results show that this method can be applied to AC systems with various topologies and operating conditions, providing a new reference for studying system resonance stability using the impedance analysis method.

title: Optimal long-term scheduling of integrated energy system considering carbon emissions
authors: Ce Wang, Peng Sun, Mao Yang, Yitao Li, Yi Chai
publish: 2025 IEEE 9th Conference on Energy Internet and Energy System Integration (EI2)
abstract: The uncertainty of both sources and loads and the problem that the existing emissions trading scheme ignores the timing characteristics of carbon emissions restrict its economic low-carbon operation efficiency. To this end, scheduling method that couples the long-term characteristics of carbon emissions: Firstly, a multi-energy flow coupling operation model covering power, heat, natural gas physical energy flow and carbon virtual energy flow is constructed. Secondly, aiming at the seasonal fluctuation law of system carbon emissions, a long-term differentiated carbon emission quota allocation mechanism is designed, and a carbon trading market structure of ' stepped carbon price + dynamic reward and punishment coefficient ' is innovatively introduced to drive seasonal emission reduction synergy with dynamic price signal. Finally, a three-layer collaborative optimization framework of energy network layer-carbon flow tracking layer-scheduling decision layer is established. While ensuring that the cost is within the economically affordable range. The coupling mechanism of ladder carbon price and dynamic rewards and punishments effectively enhances the marginal regulation of carbon constraints.

title: Two-Stage Planning Method for Capacity Planning and Site Selection of Grid-Forming Energy Storage Considering Voltage Support Capability
authors: Zhibin Yan, Bohui Jia, Peng Yang, Yuxuan Zhang, Liangchen Lyv, Xiang Gao, Xinpo Lin, Huihui Song
publish: 2025 IEEE 9th Conference on Energy Internet and Energy System Integration (EI2)
abstract: The inherent intermittency and uncertainty of renewable energy generation lead to a decline in system inertia and voltage support capability, posing severe challenges to the stable operation of the power grid. To accurately evaluate the system's voltage support strength, an improved multiple renewable energy stations short circuit ratio (MRSCR) calculation method that accounts for grid-forming energy storage (GFMES) is proposed in this paper, along with the establishment of a two-stage planning algorithm for GFMES capacity determination and site selection. Firstly, a capacity planning model based on multi-scenario probability is constructed for the first stage. With the minimization of total cost as the objective, this model comprehensively optimizes the GFMES capacity and its charge-discharge strategies. Secondly, a site selection optimization model is proposed for the second stage. Aiming to maximize the system's voltage support capability, this model rationally allocates the total GFMES capacity to each power grid bus. Simulation results based on the modified IEEE 33-bus system demonstrate that the proposed method significantly enhances both the system's voltage support capability and operational economy, providing technical references for GFMES planning in power grids with high penetration of renewable energy.

title: Modeling Electric Vehicle Charging Station Choices in an Extended Traffic Network with Heterogeneous User Preferences
authors: Ruiyang Liang, Xiaolong Jin, Tian Luan, Shuo Liang, Zixuan Cao, Defeng Su
publish: 2025 IEEE 9th Conference on Energy Internet and Energy System Integration (EI2)
abstract: This paper presents an extended traffic network model to analyze electric vehicle (EV) charging station choices. The model virtualizes charging stations as directed links, seamlessly integrating them into the road network topology to avoid path redundancy. It captures heterogeneous user preferences through a multi-criteria cost function that balances travel time, charging service fees, and station service quality. The user equilibrium (UE) problem, governing the flow distribution, is solved using a combined approach of an extended Dijkstra algorithm—which finds the shortest feasible paths under charging constraints—and the Frank-Wolfe method. A case study on a 9-node network demonstrates the model’s effectiveness. Results show that distinct user types (Money-Saving, Service-Oriented, and Time-Saving) exhibit fundamentally different route and charging station selection behaviors. This validates the critical impact of preference heterogeneity on charging infrastructure utilization. The proposed framework provides a practical tool for planners to simulate realistic user decisions and optimize EV charging infrastructure deployment.

title: Enhancing Mask R-CNN to Improve Camouflaged Object Detection
authors: Maria Zubaşcu, Cristian-Cosmin Vancea
publish: 2025 IEEE 21st International Conference on Intelligent Computer Communication and Processing (ICCP)
abstract: Camouflaged Object Detection (COD) involves identifying and segmenting objects that become part of the background. It is a complicated task for Computer Vision, which requires techniques able to exploit visual features beyond human eye capabilities. Traditional methods using pixel-wise local analysis in the RGB color space are usually insufficient for this task. After comprehensive testing, we noticed that Mask R-CNN models, provided with a supporting attention map using Discrete Cosine Transform (DCT) information, proved to be more accurate in detecting camouflaged objects. We developed five original models based on Mask R-CNN with different backbones, including ResNet-50, Res2Net, ResNeXt. Additionally, the models were enhanced by inserting a module based on DCT, which generates attention maps using the Self-Attention mechanism from Transformers. We used pre-trained backbones and trained our models on COD10K dataset. The models were evaluated using standard COCO IoU-based metrics. We also adopted Structure Measure to assess a superior qualitative examination specific to the segmentation task. Experiments conducted on the COD10K dataset demonstrate that using a ResNet-50 backbone combined with our DCT Self-Attention module improves the performance of standard Mask R-CNN with approximately 0.08 on $\mathrm{AP} \text{@} 0.5$ and 0.03 on Structure Measure.

title: IoT-Driven Deep Learning Logic to Identify Cardiovascular Diseases using Electrocardiogram Images
authors: J. Gokulapriya, P. Logeswari, Thiyagarajan Jayaraman, R. Rajesh, Senthil Kumar Sengottaiyan, Yuvaraja Thangavel
publish: 2026 Sixth International Conference on Advances in Electrical, Computing, Communications and Sustainable Technologies (ICAECT)
abstract: Cardiovascular diseases (CVDs) are the major cause of death in all parts of the world, and therefore, the detectability of the spread of these diseases needs a way of detecting that is accurate and scalable in the early stages. This paper suggests a new IoT-based deep-learning system that uses ECG signatures and highly effective image transformation algorithms to detect different CVDs in real-time. The methodology starts with the signal acquisition of 3-leads ECG by the set of wearable sensors that would be connected to Raspberry Pi-based IoT modules. The preprocessing of the signals includes wavelet-based denoising and detection of the QRS complex followed by transformation to informative 2D representations, e.g., Gramian Angular Fields (GAF). We use these images as input to a convolutional neural network based on InceptionNet that learns deep morphological features. A mixed taxonomic scheme is used with XGBoost over the CNN-derived features to complement the decision boundaries. A federated learning method is used to protect privacy and scalability since raw data is not constrained and device-wide model updates are combined. The overall classification accuracy quantity of the proposed model is 97.61%, and sensitivity and specificity are 0.96 and 0.96, respectively, as shown in comparative experimentation on PTB-XL and MIT-BIH datasets. The hybrid model when compared to standalone CNNs and other models, such as ResNet and EfficientNet performs consistently better in task of detecting diseases, whilst being more computationally efficient, able to run in an IoT context. Also, the framework allows low inference latency and energy requirements at the edge-devices, which enables it to be deployed in a mobile healthcare environment or a rural healthcare application. This paper presents a solid start toward real-time, privacy-preserving CVD detection and leads to future personalized cardiac monitoring systems.

title: Assessment of Handling Characteristics for the Hover Mission Task Element of TRC-based Evtol Aircraft
authors: Liqun Ma, Ziteng Liu
publish: 2025 3rd Asian Aerospace and Astronautics Conference (AAAC)
abstract: To deeply explore the low-speed handling character istics of electric vertical take-Off and landing aircraft, this paper conducts a study on the maneuverability of eVTOL aircraft based on the translational rate command. Firstly, a mathematical mode l of the eVTOL aircraft is established. Then, a nonlinear dynamic inverse control law and a translational rate command model based on a unified control framework are constructed. Subsequently, a detailed description and in-depth analysis of the operation of the hover task subject unit are carried out, and a task-oriented pilot model is established. This model selects three different pilot decision-making commands and control parameters to represent pilots with different levels of experience and handling proficiency. Finally, mathematical simulations are carried out, and the wavelet analysis method is introduced to quantify the pilot workload during the flight mission task element. The simulation results show that, under the TRC model, the three pilot models can basically meet the performance requirements of the established task subject unit. However, there are significant differences in control workload. The results of this research can provide valuable references for the evaluation of the airworthiness handling characteristics of eVTOL aircraft in low-speed mission task element.

title: A Wind Farm Power Forecasting Method Considering the Cyclic Characteristics of Wind Direction Fluctuations
authors: Peishi Li, Yongqian Liu, Shuang Han
publish: 2025 IEEE 9th Conference on Energy Internet and Energy System Integration (EI2)
abstract: Wind speed and wind direction, as two primary parameters characterizing the inflow wind conditions at wind farms, exhibit fluctuation characteristics that are crucial for the safe and economical operation of both wind farms and power systems. Existing research has predominantly focused on the patterns of wind speed fluctuations and their application in power forecasting. However, wind direction and its fluctuations are equally vital for the operational safety of wind farms and power forecasting. Addressing the lack of quantitative research on wind direction fluctuations, this paper introduces the concept of wind direction periodicity intensity to quantify its fluctuation characteristics and verifies the correlation between wind direction and power periodicity features. Based on this, a physically interpretable feature method framework is constructed: considering wind speed, the wind direction periodicity intensity metric guides the selection of multi-scale wavelet coefficients for wind direction, which are then incorporated as input features to enhance the performance of minute-level power forecasting models for wind turbines. Experimental results from a representative unit at a northwest wind farm demonstrate that incorporating wind direction wavelet features reduces the model's RMSE on the test set from 1.60 to 1.48.

title: Research on spot electricity energy and frequency modulation market trading mechanism to promote new energy consumption under new power system
authors: Zhanyi Chen, Mao Yang, Yitao Li, Peng Sun, Xiuxiang Ren
publish: 2025 IEEE 9th Conference on Energy Internet and Energy System Integration (EI2)
abstract: In order to cope with the core contradiction between the lack of coordination between spot electricity energy and frequency regulation auxiliary service market, and the difficulty in coordinating security consumption and market bidding caused by the high proportion of new energy participating in market-oriented transactions in the new power system, this paper constructs a coordinated clearing mechanism of spot-frequency regulation market integrating power generation substitution strategy. By combing the new energy trading model and frequency regulation market practice, it is pointed out that the existing mechanism has limitations in volatility adaptation, opportunity cost internalization and consumption target coordination. On this basis, a market clearing model based on the self-declared capacity and mileage quotation of the unit is proposed, the opportunity cost accounting process is simplified, and a power generation substitution mechanism based on the principle of maximum price difference matching is designed to guide the high-quoted thermal power to transfer power generation space, supporting compensation and settlement mechanism to protect the rights and interests of market players. The simulation results show that the proposed mechanism has a significant effect on improving the level of new energy consumption and optimizing the market clearing efficiency, which verifies the effectiveness and applicability of coordinating the security consumption and market competition under the background of the new power system.

title: Multi-Source Sentiment Analysis for Cryptocurrency Prediction Using Machine and Deep Learning Models
authors: Rithu S, Namrata Chirmade, Dhruv Khatri, Amrit Kochar, Mangal Singh
publish: 2026 Sixth International Conference on Advances in Electrical, Computing, Communications and Sustainable Technologies (ICAECT)
abstract: Cryptocurrency markets are highly volatile and driven not only by technical indicators but also by rapidly shifting public sentiment expressed across digital platforms. Traditional sentiment analysis approaches using a single data source—either social media or news—are often insufficient for capturing the multidimensional dynamics influencing price movements. This study proposes an enhanced multi-modal sentiment analysis framework that integrates Twitter discussions, online news headlines, and market data to provide a deeper and more reliable understanding of cryptocurrency sentiment. By employing a fusion of machine learning and deep learning techniques such as Logistic Regression, Random Forest, Support Vector Machine, and Long Short-Term Memory networks, the research examines cross-platform sentiment patterns and their predictive power on cryptocurrency price fluctuations. Advanced pre-processing techniques, including tokenization, stemming, stop-word removal, class-imbalance handling, and noise filtering, ensure data quality and improve model performance. The system further incorporates sentiment drift detection and feature importance analysis to explain sentiment variations across platforms and over time. Experimental results demonstrate that multi-source sentiment fusion significantly outperforms singlesource models, achieving improved accuracy and stronger correlation with real market movements. The findings confirm that integrating diverse sentiment streams provides traders, analysts, and investors with more actionable insights, enabling informed decision-making in highly uncertain markets. This study contributes to the evolving field of financial sentiment analysis by demonstrating the benefits of multidimensional data integration and suggesting pathways for future enhancements using transformer-based models and real-time processing.

title: Research on Artificial Intelligence Fault Diagnosis for Acoustic Signal of Wind Turbine Gear Box
authors: Xiaoyi Zhang, Yinke Dou, Cheng Cui, Yuchen Wang
publish: 2025 IEEE 9th Conference on Energy Internet and Energy System Integration (EI2)
