abstract: In view of the outstanding contribution of artificial intelligence in the field of wind power generation safety, a multimodal feature fusion fault diagnosis method for signal fault diagnosis of wind turbine gear box is proposed for the problem of poor anti-interference performance of single signal source. Firstly, the noise is filtered in the one-dimensional contraction denoising convolution neural network, which improves the signal quality and extracts the characteristics. Then the sound signal is converted into recursive image in Swin transformer network with valuable channel characteristics and taken. Finally, the multidimensional feature is aligned and the output feature is strengthened with residual structure to complete the fault identification. The validity and noise resistance of the method are verified by using the real collected fault data set of industrial wind turbine gear box. The experimental results show that the diagnostic accuracy of the real industrial environment is more than 98%. Compared with WDCNN-LSTM, Mobile NetV2 and multi-voice lines diagnostic methods have the advantages of high diagnostic accuracy, strong noise resistance and good generalization.

title: Adaptive AI-Driven Learning Pathways for Real-Time Knowledge Personalization in Multilingual Digital Education Ecosystems
authors: Namita Breja, Rippandeep Kaur, Ranjeeta Saini, Harpreet Singh, Priya Sharma, Prema S
publish: 2026 Sixth International Conference on Advances in Electrical, Computing, Communications and Sustainable Technologies (ICAECT)
abstract: This study targets the increasing demand for personalized learning in multilingual digital learning environments, where current adaptive systems fail to provide real-time personalization of knowledge and strong support for multiple languages, constraining learner engagement and performance. To address these issues, an AI-augmented adaptive learning pathway framework introduce that combines Deep Knowledge Tracing (DKT-LSTM) with Neural Machine Translation and Reinforcement Learning for dynamic, curriculum-adaptive content personalization. Enacted on the Personalized Learning & Adaptive Education Dataset with Python and TensorFlow, this model gains 7.8% accuracy (82.4% to 90.2%) over baselines. The architecture keeps track of learner knowledge states in real time and adjusts pathways across languages, improving accessibility and learner achievement. The unique combination promises scalable and effective personalized learning through multilingual adaptation, deep sequential modeling, and reinforcement-driven content optimization. The findings show strong improvements in retention, learner engagement, and performance, making the suggested model a valuable contribution to next-generation multilingual learning technologies.

title: Optimal Planning for Receiving-End Power Grid Considering Constraints of Short-Circuit Current Security and Voltage Support Capability
authors: Yenan Wang, Yiliu He, Haiwang Zhong, Yingjun Zhuo, Siyu Lu, Baorong Zhou
publish: 2025 IEEE 9th Conference on Energy Internet and Energy System Integration (EI2)
abstract: With the increasing penetration of renewable energy and the expansion of multi-DC infeed, receiving-end power grid is facing the challenges of excessive short-circuit current (SCC) and reduced voltage support capability. This paper proposes a coordinated planning model for receiving-end power grid considering constraints of SCC security and voltage support capability. By constructing a linearized model of node impedance matrix elements, the constraints of SCC and multi-infeed shortcircuit ratio (MISCR) are jointly formulated. Furthermore, an MILP model integrating multi-DC terminal configuration, DC capacity allocation, and AC grid structure optimization is constructed. The results of the case study indicate that the proposed method can effectively reduce the SCC level and enhance the voltage support capability of DC infeed nodes, achieving coordinated improvement of the operational security and DC carrying capacity of the receiving-end power grid.

title: Simulation and Experiment Research on Transient Characteristics of Grid-forming SVG
authors: Wankai Yang, Guoliang Zhao, Guangyao Qiao, Yunfei Xu
publish: 2025 IEEE 9th Conference on Energy Internet and Energy System Integration (EI2)
abstract: The grid-forming SVG (Static Var Generator) has voltage source characteristics and can provide reactive voltage support for weak grid systems, enhancing the stability of the system. By configuring super-capacitor and other energy storage components on the SVG's DC side, transient inertia support can also be achieved. This technology has been applied in energy storage, renewable energy integration systems, and weak power grid. This paper conducts an in-depth analysis of the transient control function of grid-forming SVG. The simulation calculations and RTDS simulation experiments also examined the transient active inertia support provided by SVG and the phase jump control. The simulation calculations and test results show that in the event of a grid fault, SVG can provide reactive voltage support and short-term active output, providing reactive and active support for the stable operation of the power grid.

title: Optimization Model for Inter-Provincial Medium- to Long-Term Centralized Bidding Trading with Pumped Hydro Storage Participation
authors: Yuankang He, Yan Liu, Tao Ding, Jinghui Yu
publish: 2025 IEEE 9th Conference on Energy Internet and Energy System Integration (EI2)
abstract: To promote the inter-provincial consumption of high-penetration renewable energy and enhance large-scale resource allocation efficiency in China, this paper constructs an optimization model for pumped hydro storage (PHS) participating in inter-provincial medium- to long-term centralized bidding trading (IMCBT). Aiming to maximize social welfare while satisfying power grid security constraints, the model simultaneously optimizes both the electricity quantity of inter-provincial medium- to long-term power trading contracts and the pumping/generation schedules of PHS plants. Case studies show that the proposed model can effectively exploit the arbitrage and regulation potential of pumped storage, contributing to an increase in overall social welfare and transaction quantity, thereby providing effective model support for promoting the participation of pumped storage in medium- to long-term electricity markets.

title: From Scenes to Places: Enhancing Film Exploration through the RevIS Ontology-based consultation App
authors: Silvio Alovisio, Liliana Ardissono, Angelo Geninatti Cossatin, Vincenzo Lombardo, Diego Magro, Noemi Mauro, Andrea Nasi, Livio Scarpinati
publish: Journal on Computing and Cultural Heritage
abstract: None

title: A Cooperative 3D Perception Framework via Representation Alignment and Latent State Reasoning for Spatially Variant LiDAR Observations
authors: Zhenyu He, Jianxu Mao, Yaonan Wang, Caiping Liu, He Xie, Ziming Tao, Junfei Yi, Hui Zhang
publish: IEEE Transactions on Instrumentation and Measurement
abstract: Precise 3D spatial sensing is critical for connected and autonomous vehicles (CAV) systems. The development of LiDAR sensors and communication technologies has facilitated the advancement of cooperative perception. Such systems utilize point cloud data collected by LiDAR sensors mounted on multiple vehicles as input to measure the 3D spatial parameters (position, size, orientation) and the category of dynamic objects, achieving a unified understanding of the environment. However, due to distinct sensor viewing angles, the raw point cloud data exhibit severe discrepancies in spatial distributions. Current methods primarily employ CNN and Transformer architectures, yet they struggle with issues such as limited global receptive fields and quadratic computational complexity, respectively. To tackle this issue, we propose RALSR, a cooperative 3D perception framework for spatially variant LiDAR observations via representation alignment and latent state reasoning. Specifically, we design the Multi-Agent Representation Aligner (MARA) to capture interagent dependencies using an improved linear attention mechanism and align spatially variant features through multiplicative gating. Building on this, we introduce the Unified Latent State Reasoner (ULSR), which combines a visual state space model with CNNs to capture both global and local receptive fields while maintaining linear complexity. Our approach not only resolves the challenge of spatial variance but also optimizes the deficiencies found in current methods. Extensive experiments are conducted on the real-world dataset (V2V4Real) and simulated datasets (V2X-R and V2XSet). The results show that RALSR achieves superior performance compared to existing collaborative 3D sensing methods in both accuracy and computational efficiency.

title: Suppression Method for Stochastic Line Errors in Grating Interferometers Based on Spatial Wavefront Phase Analysis Theory_supp1-3670520.pdf
authors: Wenhao Li
publish: N/A
abstract: Grating interferometers have been extensively adopted in modern precision manufacturing industries. Diffraction grat-ings are the core components of grating interferometric displace-ment measurement systems. Notably, stochastic line errors of the grating introduce additional phase errors into the phase infor-mation of diffracted beams, ultimately degrading measurement precision. To eliminate the interference from stochastic line errors, this study establishes the spatial wavefront phase analysis theory (SWPAT). The adverse effects of stochastic line errors on the phase of diffracted beams are investigated, and a method of using large-diameter beams is proposed to suppress these errors. To val-idate this method, a heterodyne Littrow grating interferometric experimental setup is built, and controlled experiments with four different beam diameters are designed using the control variable method. Experimental results demonstrate that when the meas-urement beam diameter increases from 1 to 4 mm, the maximum displacement error is reduced by 34.3%, and the 3σ value is re-duced by 45.9%, thus verifying the correctness of SWPAT. This work enriches the theoretical framework for error compensation in grating interferometric displacement measurement systems and holds significant importance for improving displacement meas-urement precision.

title: Throughput Maximization of Hybrid NOMA-FDMA-TDMA
authors: Ding Xu
publish: IEEE Transactions on Vehicular Technology
abstract: Hybrid non-orthogonal multiple access (NOMA) has been widely explored to enhance system performance by integrating NOMA with frequency-division multiple access (FDMA) or time-division multiple access (TDMA). In this paper, we propose a new hybrid NOMA-FDMA-TDMA scheme that incorporates both FDMA and TDMA into NOMA. We jointly optimize the user pairing, channel allocation, time allocation, and power allocation to maximize the total system throughput, subject to the minimum throughput and transmit power constraints for each user. The resulting problem is a non-convex and mixed-integer nonlinear programming problem. To efficiently solve it, we first determine the maximum throughput for every feasible user pair, and then develop a low-complexity algorithm for optimal user pairing and channel allocation. Simulation results demonstrate that the proposed hybrid NOMA-FDMA-TDMA scheme significantly outperforms the existing hybrid NOMA-FDMA and hybrid NOMA-TDMA schemes.

title: Flotation Fault Trace Recognition Using Dynamic Edge Weight-Based Cross-Cell Interaction Graph Transformer and Joint Task Learning
authors: Ying Fan, Zhaohui Tang, Jin Luo, Yongfang Xie, Weihua Gui
publish: IEEE Transactions on Industrial Informatics
abstract: Froth flotation is a complex industrial process involving multicell cascades. During the froth flotation process, a single-cell fault not only compromises its own functionality but may also propagate to adjacent cells, ultimately affecting the entire production line. Therefore, accurate and timely recognition of fault traces in the flotation process is critical for ensuring production stability. In this article, we propose a novel fault trace recognition framework using a dynamic edge weight-based cross-cell interaction graph Transformer and joint task learning. Initially, we propose a dynamic edge weighting method to update multicell node features, enhancing the model’s adaptability to dynamic industrial scenarios. Then, we introduce a cross-cell attention mechanism to decode the fault propagation path, explicitly capturing interaction-aware state features among multiple cells. Furthermore, we employ a two-stage joint task learning scheme, progressing from fault interval prediction to fault trace recognition, thereby significantly improving efficiency and accuracy. Finally, extensive experiments conducted on both benchmark datasets and real-world froth flotation processes demonstrate the effectiveness and robustness of the proposed method.

title: Battery-Aware Dynamic Adaptive Low-Power Device Selection for IoT-enabled FL Networks
authors: Alaa AlZailaa, Hao Ran Chi, Ayman Radwan, Rui L. Aguiar
publish: IEEE Transactions on Mobile Computing
abstract: The integration of Internet of Things (IoT) and Federated Learning (FL) marks a significant step towards pervasiveness, supported by distributed computational resources and enhanced by 5G advancements. However, the efficient and sustainable operation of IoT-enabled FL systems faces critical challenges, particularly in selecting devices that balance energy consumption and system performance. To address this, we propose the Battery-Aware Dynamic and Automated Device Selection (DADS) framework, a novel solution specifically designed for IoT-enabled FL environments. DADS introduces an innovative adaptive mechanism that dynamically adjusts optimization parameters, such as inertia weights and crossover/mutation rates, ensuring a seamless balance between exploration and exploitation. Unlike conventional optimization approaches, DADS employs uniquely developed Adaptive Particle Swarm Optimization (APSO) and Adaptive Genetic Algorithm (AGA) within a cohesive framework. This design enables DADS to respond to dynamic IoT network conditions, such as fluctuating battery levels and device capabilities, ensuring energy-efficient device selection while preserving robust performance. Through extensive performance analysis, DADS demonstrates its novelty by achieving a 20% reduction in energy consumption and a 30% improvement in FL training time compared to state-of-the-art methods. Moreover, DADS significantly enhances battery lifespan, reducing degradation by more than 50%, and optimizes communication efficiency, extending the operational sustainability of IoT devices. These results position DADS as a groundbreaking framework, setting a new benchmark for energy-aware and sustainable IoT-enabled FL systems.

title: Detecting Social Bots via Multi-Motif Attention Fusion Network
authors: Jianbo Wang, Jialong Chen, Ping Li, Zhanwei Du, Xiao-Ke Xu
publish: IEEE Transactions on Computational Social Systems
abstract: The rapid growth of social networks has enabled the widespread deployment of social bots that manipulate public opinion and disseminate misinformation, thereby posing significant cybersecurity risks. Most existing detection methods for social bots primarily focus on individual features and low-order neighbor information, while neglecting the higher-order topological semantics embedded in frequent substructures, or motifs. This oversight limits their ability to effectively identify sophisticated social bots exhibiting complex behaviors. To address this gap, we propose a novel approach called the multi-motif attention fusion network (MMAFN). Our method enhances the ability to capture complex structures by explicitly modeling higher-order topological relationships within social graphs. Specifically, we first extract typical network motifs from the original graph to generate motif networks that preserve higher-order interaction patterns. We then integrate the original graph structure with these motif networks across multiple scales, constructing a composite adjacency relationship that captures multilevel semantics. Following this integration, we design specialized graph convolution operators that perform parallel feature propagation based on the composite adjacency matrix, generating node representations that incorporate multiorder topological information. Finally, we dynamically merge features from different motif views through an attention mechanism, establishing interaction channels between motifs and ultimately predicting the anomaly probability of user accounts. Experimental results on two large-scale datasets demonstrate that our method significantly outperforms state-of-the-art methods, exhibiting superior detection performance and robust generalization capabilities.

title:
Multi-Stage Multi-Fidelity Optimal Design Method Based on Evolutionary Computation with Dynamic Population for High-Power High-Voltage High-Frequency Transformers (H
                    <sup>3</sup>
                    FTs)
authors: Yongliang Dang, Lingyu Zhu, Zhanlei Liu, Shengchang Ji
publish: IEEE Transactions on Power Electronics
abstract: Optimal design is essential for enhancing the power density and efficiency of H<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">3</sup>FTs. Traditional optimal design methods struggle to find a satisfactory trade-off between optimization efficiency and quality due to the low prediction accuracy of analytical models, the high computational cost of high-fidelity models, and the extensive evaluation requirements of evolutionary algorithms. This paper proposes a multi-stage multi-fidelity optimal design framework based on evolutionary computation with a dynamic population to address these challenges. The search process for H<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">3</sup>FT optimal design is decoupled into two stages: exploration and exploitation. In the exploration stage, low-fidelity H<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">3</sup>FT parameter models for both objectives and constraints are employed to form the low-fidelity design space. The promising H<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">3</sup>FT design candidates characterized by high power density and efficiency are globally searched and identified in the low fidelity design space. In the exploitation stage, low-fidelity H<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">3</sup>FT parameter models are dynamically replaced by high-fidelity H<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">3</sup>FT parameter models to form the high-fidelity design space for precise local refinement. The promising H<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">3</sup>FT designs from the first stage serve as the initial population for the second stage to accelerate convergence. To further reduce the computational burden of expensive high-fidelity evaluations, a dynamic population size reduction strategy is implemented in the exploitation stage. The proposed framework is validated by optimizing a 70 kW, 10 kV H<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">3</sup>FT for dual-active bridge converter. NSGA-III is employed for the evolutionary computation. The results demonstrate a 68.8% reduction in total evaluation time compared to high-fidelity-model-only approach. Furthermore, the framework maintains superior design quality, achieving a hypervolume value of 0.356, which is 18.7% higher than the results obtained from the low-fidelity-model-only optimization.

title: RadarGaussianDet3D: Gaussian Representation-Based Real-Time 3D Object Detection With 4D Automotive Radars
authors: Weiyi Xiong, Bing Zhu, Zewei Zheng
publish: IEEE Robotics and Automation Letters
abstract: 4D automotive radars have gained increasing attention for autonomous driving due to their low cost, robustness, and inherent velocity measurement capability. However, existing 4D radar-based 3D detectors rely heavily on pillar encoders for BEV feature extraction, where each point contributes to only a single BEV grid, resulting in sparse feature maps and degraded representation quality. In addition, they also optimize bounding box attributes independently, leading to sub-optimal detection accuracy. Moreover, their inference speed, while sufficient for high-end GPUs, may fail to meet the real-time requirement on vehicle-mounted embedded devices. To overcome these limitations, an efficient and effective Gaussian-based 3D detector, namely RadarGaussianDet3D is introduced, leveraging Gaussian primitives and distributions as intermediate representations for radar points and bounding boxes. In RadarGaussianDet3D, a novel Point Gaussian Encoder (PGE) is designed to transform each point into a Gaussian primitive after feature aggregation and employs the 3D Gaussian Splatting (3DGS) technique for BEV rasterization, yielding denser feature maps. PGE exhibits exceptionally low latency, owing to the optimized algorithm for point feature aggregation and fast rendering of 3DGS. In addition, a new Box Gaussian Loss (BGL) is proposed, which converts bounding boxes into 3D Gaussian distributions and measures their distance to enable more comprehensive and consistent optimization. Extensive experiments on TJ4DRadSet and View-of-Delft demonstrate that RadarGaussianDet3D achieves high detection accuracy while delivering substantially faster inference, highlighting its potential for real-time deployment in autonomous driving.

title: Future Trajectory Representation-Aided DRL for Real-Time Battery Energy Storage Dispatch in Distribution Networks
authors: Pengfei Zhao, Weihao Hu, Di Cao, Jialin Du, Zhenyuan Zhang, Qi Huang, Zhe Chen
publish: IEEE Transactions on Smart Grid
abstract: Dispatching battery energy storage systems (BESS) in distributed grids is challenging due to the sequential nature of the decision-making process, which needs to account for future uncertainties in system states and time-coupling constraints. Numerous forecasting techniques have been developed to capture such uncertainties, but most prioritize accuracy alone, and their potential for enhancing optimization performance has rarely been studied. To bridge this gap, this paper proposes a novel forecasting-aided deep reinforcement learning (DRL)-based BESS dispatch approach that incorporates future trajectory representation into the DRL state space to facilitate more proactive decisions. Specifically, an error-free representation network is first trained offline using self-supervised contrastive learning to encode upcoming electricity prices, load demand, and renewable generation trajectories. It explicitly disentangles trend and seasonal patterns using a mixture of autoregressive experts and a learnable Fourier layer. During online control, the DRL agent receives both the current system state and the learned forecast representation, which are fused using a dynamic routing attention mechanism that adaptively balances present versus future information. The trajectory contrastive learning allows the proposed method to better capture the temporal dynamics of stochastic variables and generate representations that are easier for the agent to exploit. By incorporating structured predictive representations into the control loop, the proposed method can make more forward-looking charging and discharging decisions that consider future uncertainties. Extensive simulations on the IEEE 34-node and 123-node test systems demonstrate that the proposed method yields more cost-efficient BESS control policies compared to baseline approaches.

title: Polarization-Insensitive Bidirectional W-band Millimeter-wave Fiber-Wireless Integrated System Based on Full Photonic Up/Down Conversion
authors: Qingyu Han, Boyu Dong, An Yan, Yinjun Liu, Yaxuan Li, Yuqin Yuan, Dianyuan Ping, Liangtao Chen, Xiaolong Wang, Leiyan Zhou, Chaoran Yang, Jialin He, Yiyun Cai, Jianyang Shi, Nan Chi, Junwen Zhang
publish: Journal of Lightwave Technology
abstract: With the development of the next-generation mobile communication, the demand for large-capacity and high-rate communications has emerged drastically. Millimeter-wave (MMW) and even Terahertz have been attracting attention as a promising solution owing to their higher frequency and broader bandwidth. Photonic-assisted MMW systems further take advantage of the large bandwidth, facilitating the dense deployment of active antenna units (AAUs) and achieving high-rate transmission. However, most existing bidirectional fiber-wireless systems remain polarization-sensitive, requiring polarization control during MMW signal generation and reception on the optical network side, which imposes constraints on flexible and large-scale deployment and increases operational complexity. To address this issue, we propose a bidirectional W-band MMW fiber-wireless integrated system, which innovatively realizes polarization insensitivity in the downlink (DL) and uplink (UL) by Alamouti coding and a full coherent receiver respectively, thereby decreasing the polarization controllers (PCs) usage. Additionally, specific algorithms for polarization insensitivity are designed in our work. By implementing full photonic up- and down-conversion on the fiber side with a shared optical source, the system enables seamless integration of the optical network and wireless network, reducing reliance on electronic devices and avoiding multi-stage conversion impairments. Experimental results demonstrate that the proposed system supports the line rates of 128 Gbps in the DL at a carrier frequency of 94 GHz, and 50.5 Gbps in the UL at a carrier frequency of 90 GHz over 10-km single-mode fiber and 1-m wireless link. These results confirm the system's high-rate and large-capacity transmission capability, providing a novel solution for the development of simplified, integrated fiber-wireless networks in future communication systems.

title: Low-loss SCF-to-MCF Power Splitter Based on a Biconical Splice Taper
authors: Sijing Liang, John D. Downie, Sergejs Makovejs, Periklis Petropoulos, Yongmin Jung
publish: Journal of Lightwave Technology
abstract: We present a splice-taper-based power splitter which efficiently and evenly distributes optical power from a single core fiber (SCF) into multiple cores of a multicore fiber (MCF). By carefully optimizing the design and implementing precise fabrication control, we achieve a record-low insertion loss of 0.3 dB. We further investigate the impact of MCF core inhomogeneity on power distribution uniformity of SCF-to-MCF power splitters. To relax the stringent requirement for highly homogeneous MCFs in high-performance power splitter fabrication, we propose a macrobending-based post-processing technique that equalizes the power among the cores. This method is experimentally validated, effectively reducing the power variation across MCF cores from 24% to 8% without causing additional loss. These results highlight the versatility and manufacturability of the splice taper approach for making SCF to-MCF power splitters with high power efficiency and very good distribution uniformity, even in the presence of realistic MCF imperfections.

title: Technology of Digital Transformation of Theterritorial Safety Management
authors: Valeriy Nicheporchuk, Tatiana Penkova, Svetlana Kobyzhakova
publish: 2025 5th International Siberian Scientific Workshop on Data Analysis Technologies with Applications (SibDATA)
abstract: This paper presents a universal structure of information resources to support the management of a wide range of tasks related to ensuring natural and technological safety of territories. The compactness of the entities presentation simplifies the implementation of independent services for receiving, processing, and presenting data, that operate according to a unified scheme. The framework enables the identification of uncertain factors in situations, improving the quality and efficiency of decision-making. The use of data maturity enhancement technologies, multiparameter analysis, and visualization tools for various management tasks enables the systematic implementation of measures to improve the safety of the population and territories. The application of data processing technologies for various management tasks enables the systematic implementation of measures to improve the safety of the population and territories. Algorithms for decision support processes have been implemented using an organizational management model. The digitalization of emergency response plans enables the creation of knowledge bases for typical situations and more accurate allocation of response resources.

title: FedMindPurpose: A Federated AI Framework for Purpose Discovery and Mental Health Support in Youth
authors: Thilakavathy P, Rajakumar B, Sundara Rajulu Navaneethakrishnan, Deepa R, Surendran R
publish: 2026 Sixth International Conference on Advances in Electrical, Computing, Communications and Sustainable Technologies (ICAECT)
abstract: Scalable, adaptable, and privacy-protecting digital solutions are needed to tackle rising teenage anxiety, depression, and identity theft. Data heterogeneity, non-IID distributions, and ethical concerns around sensitive data aggregation hinder typical centralized AI systems' mental health analytics capabilities. Due to these constraints, this study proposes FedMindPurpose, a Federated Learning Framework for Youth Purpose Discovery and Mental Health (FedMindPurpose), which enables decentralized institutional nodes to participate. This proposed framework handles non-uniform distributions via federated averaging and adaptive optimization. Transformer-based encoders capture semantic information from introspective writings, generative neural networks chronicle social support and interaction patterns, and temporal attention modules imitate mood changes. Federated meta-learning customises models by maintaining global consistency while correcting demographic and cultural variability. With high privacy guarantees <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$(\varepsilon=1.2$</tex> under differential privacy), a cross-institutional dataset of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\text{1 2, 4 0 0}$</tex> adolescent records demonstrated a 22.4 % decrease in early stress detection inaccuracy and a 17.6 % increase in purpose-discovery accuracy. The results show that FedMindPurpose provides comprehensive, moral, and adaptive AI-powered assistance for adolescents to develop a sense of purpose and mental resilience.

title: Pre-Transaction Fraud Risk Prediction in DeFi Using Explainable AI
authors: Rino Thomas, Savitha K.K
publish: 2026 Sixth International Conference on Advances in Electrical, Computing, Communications and Sustainable Technologies (ICAECT)
abstract: Decentralized Finance (DeFi) has changed the financial ecosystem but is extremely vulnerable to complex schemes such as rug pulls, honeypots, and flash loan attacks, which cause massive losses for platforms and users. This study responds to the critical necessity of early and actionable warning for fraud by suggesting a real-time system predicting and justifying the risk level of freshly launched DeFi tokens prior to their engagement with the users. Drawing on a hybrid methodology, integrating smart contract code analysis, on-chain behavioral data, and social metrics, the system utilizes cutting, edge machine learning models, including Graph Neural Networks (GNNs) and ensemble methods (XGBoost, FT-Transformer), to provide sophisticated risk scoring. For user and regulatory trust assurance, explainable AI methods such as SHAP and LIME are utilized to clearly identify important risk drivers, including unsafe contract functions, wallet concentration, and liquidity lock patterns. The solution provides stage-aware and cross-chain surveillance, coupling functionality like anomaly detection, federated model training, and governance analysis. With large-scale literature synthesis and empirical validation, this framework shows that proactive, pre-transaction fraud identification and open risk valuation are possible, and it achieves a scalable defense for DeFi players and infrastructure.

title: Source-Network Matching Evaluation of the Desert, Gobi and Wasteland Renewable Energy Base on AHP-Entropy Weight Method
authors: Cuiping Li, Jingwen Zhang, Xingxu Zhu, Siyu Lu, Liang Zhang, Jiayang Wang, Qinglin Sui, Junhui Li
publish: 2025 IEEE 9th Conference on Energy Internet and Energy System Integration (EI2)
abstract: Aiming at the problem of source-network mismatch in the delivery system of the desert, Gobi and wasteland renewable energy base, this paper constructs a comprehensive evaluation system based on AHP-entropy weight method. Firstly, the contradiction mechanism between the randomness and intermittence of renewable energy output and the rigid constraint of the capacity of the outgoing channel is analyzed. On this basis, five core indicators such as the utilization rate of the outgoing channel and the fluctuation of the outgoing power are selected, and the comprehensive weight of the index is determined by combining the analytic hierarchy process (AHP) and the entropy weight method to form the source-network comprehensive matching index. Taking a desert, Gobi and wasteland renewable energy base in Northwest China as an example, the calculated comprehensive matching degree is 0.74, indicating that the system has source-network mismatch.

title: Extended Planning of Nano-Microgrid Clusters Considering Environmental Assessment and Improved Multi-Objective Optimization
authors: Jun Zhou, Fei Cai, Dandan Zhou, Song Zou
publish: 2025 IEEE 9th Conference on Energy Internet and Energy System Integration (EI2)
abstract: Conventional planning methods often overlook the quantitative expression of environmental factors, and traditional multi-objective optimization algorithms suffer from an imbalanced distribution of Pareto solutions. To address these issues, this paper proposes an extended planning model for nano-microgrid clusters that incorporates environmental assessment and an improved multi-objective optimization algorithm. First, a source-load-storage optimization planning model is developed by integrating economic efficiency, environmental benefits, and system resilience, with detailed quantification of environmental benefits. Second, to enhance the uniformity of the Pareto solution set distribution, an improved multi-objective optimization algorithm is designed for problem solving. Finally, a case study is conducted to verify the effectiveness of the proposed model and method.

title: DeepNavX: A Deep Vision Framework for Robust Navigation in Weather-Degraded Driving Scenarios
authors: Shambhavi Singh, Gaurav Sharan
publish: 2026 Sixth International Conference on Advances in Electrical, Computing, Communications and Sustainable Technologies (ICAECT)
abstract: Perceptual systems in autonomous vehicles face significant challenges under adverse weather conditions such as fog, rain, and low-light night scenarios. This paper introduces DeepNavX, a robust framework designed to enhance navigational safety in these degraded environments. Our methodology employs a two-stage pipeline. First, an image enhancement module, leveraging a U-Net architecture, restores scene clarity from degraded visual inputs. A comparative analysis against other models, including ViT-UNet, confirms the U-Net's superior restoration performance based on quantitative metrics, specifically PSNR and SSIM. Second, a dedicated U-Net lane detection module processes the restored images to accurately identify road boundaries. Our experimental results demonstrate that this decoupled, enhancement-first approach yields significantly more accurate lane detection compared to baseline models operating directly on degraded images. This framework provides a reliable solution for improving perceptual robustness, a critical requirement for safe autonomous navigation.

title: Research on the Supply-Demand Boundary Generation Method for Interconnected Power Grids Considering Meteorological Characteristics
authors: Zhong Liu, Xipeng Zhang, Yanyong Liu, Xinyi Chen
publish: 2025 IEEE 9th Conference on Energy Internet and Energy System Integration (EI2)
abstract: Against the backdrop of the accelerating global energy transition and the increasing integration of high-penetration renewable energy into power grids, the output of wind and photovoltaic power generation remains highly dependent on meteorological conditions. Their inherent intermittency and volatility pose significant challenges to maintaining supply-demand balance in interconnected grids. Addressing the limitations of existing methods in systematically incorporating extreme weather scenarios and collaboratively quantifying the multiple uncertainties involving wind, solar, and load, this study proposes an integrated methodology for generating supply-demand boundaries that incorporates meteorological characteristics and industrial development projections. Using a multi-indicator weighted ranking method, three types of renewable energy Typical Meteorological Years (TMYs) were constructed: Average, High-Volatility, and Extreme. Furthermore, time-series load boundaries with probabilistic guarantees were generated based on a Random Forest model and the quantile method. Case studies demonstrate that the method successfully established highly climatically representative meteorological year sequences in the Jinping Hydropower Station area. Furthermore, it achieved a high forecasting accuracy of R<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup> = 0.973 in load tests conducted in Fujian Province, producing smooth and reliable boundaries. This methodology provides technically rigorous and practical support for the planning and dispatch of power grids with high penetration of renewable energy.

title: Digital Twin Modeling and Graph Neural Networks for Predictive Analytics in Remote Healthcare Monitoring Platforms
authors: Rubeena, Ammar Younas, Bobonazarov Abdurasul, Saptaparni Roy Chowdhury, V S Krushnasamy, R Saravanakumar
publish: 2026 Sixth International Conference on Advances in Electrical, Computing, Communications and Sustainable Technologies (ICAECT)
abstract: The Healthcare Monitors is a new predictive analytics model that has Digital Twin Modeling and Graph Neural Networks (GNNs) to assist in remote healthcare monitoring. Existing methods are usually inadequate when handling time-dependent health data and patient-equipment-symptom inter-relationship, decreasing prediction accuracy and personalization. To overcome, a new hybrid which combines Digital Twin profiles with Informer-based Transformers and Graph Attention Networks to model strong in terms of time and relational. Healthcare Iot Dataset from Kaggle (heart rate and oxygen levels) is used to train and test. The methodology in Python uses a combination of graph and time-series to predict patient degradation and risk of alertness in real-time with an accuracy of 93%. Prediction of adverse events has been very precise and this is evidence that the system is reliable. The patient, clinician and healthcare practitioner are the beneficiaries of the methodology in terms of timely interventions and improvement in decision support.

title: Optimal Control of Key Sections of Power System Against Cascading Failures Under Extreme Weather
authors: Xiaozhe Song, Shimin Zheng, Xu Jiang, Changjiang Wang, Song Gao, Gang Du, Peng Wang
publish: 2025 IEEE 9th Conference on Energy Internet and Energy System Integration (EI2)
abstract: High renewable penetration under "dual carbon" goals increases cascading failure risks from extreme weather. Existing models lack dynamic accuracy, while pattern search-based accident chain models suffer from path explosion. To overcome manual critical interface misidentification, this paper proposes an improved Fuzzy C-Means (FCM) clustering method. First, a typhoon and overload-integrated accident chain model simulates cascading processes. Second, a novel composite risk index quantifies power flow fluctuation, overload severity, upstream fault impact, and voltage violations via normalization and weighting. Finally, improved FCM clustering identifies critical interfaces per failure stage. IEEE 39-bus tests show the method’s effectiveness: generator adjustments at these interfaces reduce subsequent line risks, block failure propagation, and enhance resilience.

title: Dynamical Calibration of Tunable Illumination System by Precise Aperture Control
authors: Enqi Li, Weisheng Ke, Zhaokang Luo, Haoxiang Wang, Qihan Zhang, Sijia Kuang, Haodong Tang
publish: 2025 International Conference on Energy, Materials, and Photonics (EMP)
abstract: The characterization of optoelectronic materials requires light sources with stable and precisely tunable spectral power. This study presents a dynamically controlled, wide-spectrum (400-1800 nm) tunable light source that addresses the spectral non-uniformity and temporal drift of conventional halogen lamp systems. By integrating a monochromator, a motorized iris, and a high-precision optical power meter within a Python-based closed-loop feedback system, real-time control of the output power is achieved. Experimental validation demonstrates that the system delivers a constant optical power output with a relative error consistently maintained below 1 % across various power levels and spectral ranges. The developed system provides an automated and stable illumination tool for high-precision spectroscopic applications.

title: Hourly Power Prediction Method of Large-Scale PV Base Based on Multi-Source Data and Cloud Image Data Structuring
authors: Jinggang Wang, Pei Pei, Liang Yuan, Xiaodong Chen, Jingyang Ping, Liang Yao, Yang Cui, Shuo Wang
publish: 2025 IEEE 9th Conference on Energy Internet and Energy System Integration (EI2)
abstract: Large-scale PV base covers a wide region. The power generation of the base is susceptible to clouds, which indirectly interferes with power prediction. In this paper, a PV power prediction framework based on multi-source data and cloud image data structuring is proposed. The framework is divided into two stages. The first stage integrates advanced spatio-temporal codec and cloud image optimizer. The model can be used to generate high-precision future time cloud image. The second stage aims to solve the problem of format heterogeneity between cloud image data and other data. A cloud image data structuring method based on cloud factor generator is proposed in this paper. This method transforms unstructured cloud image data into structured cloud factors. The cloud factor can be combined with other structured data to achieve co-processing of multi-source data. The experimental results show that the accuracy of PV power prediction is significantly improved by using multi-source data and the prediction framework proposed in this paper compared with only considering benchmark data.

title: Impedance Characterization of Positive–Negative Sequence Separation on MMC-HVDC
authors: Yuming Wang, Yunfeng Li, Siyi Xia, Yizhi Wang
publish: 2025 IEEE 9th Conference on Energy Internet and Energy System Integration (EI2)
abstract: In practical engineering, the positive–negative sequence separation algorithm is the basis of positive and negative sequence current control of MMC. Therefore, the influence of sequence separation on the impedance models of MMC system needs to be further studied. This article develops sequence impedance model by harmonic state-space method and comprehensively analyzes the impact of sequence separation on impedances and of the MMC-HVDC. It is found that the positive– negative sequence separation introduces the negative damping of the system in the low- and medium-frequency regions and results in periodic fluctuations in the high-frequency regions of ac impedance.

title: State Estimation of Lithium-ion Batteries Based on BKA-ALC-CNN-BiLSTM-Attention Hybrid
authors: Xiping Pei, Changsheng Chen, Wei Li
publish: 2025 IEEE 9th Conference on Energy Internet and Energy System Integration (EI2)
abstract: For the precise estimation of lithium-ion battery State of Health (SOH), this study proposes a hybrid prediction model based on BKA-ALC-CNN-BiLSTM-Attention architecture. Utilizing NASA battery datasets (B0005, B0006) as experimental data, the model achieves intelligent hyperparameter optimization through the integration of Black Kite Algorithm (BKA) and Adaptive Learning Control (ALC). The framework first extracts local spatial features from charge/discharge curves using 1D Convolutional Neural Networks (CNN), then captures bidirectional temporal dependencies in battery aging processes via Bidirectional Long Short-Term Memory networks (BiLSTM), and finally incorporates an Attention mechanism to adaptively weight features at critical timesteps. Experimental results demonstrate stable performance under conditions of early-cycle data deficiency and dynamic operating profiles. Attention weight visualization analysis reveals that temperature fluctuations and voltage plateau characteristics significantly impact capacity degradation. This research provides a high-accuracy, robust solution for lithium-ion battery health management.

title: Calculation of Adjustable Potential of Flexible Source-Loads in Port Areas Based on Improved Grey Target-Prospect Theory
authors: Yilin Pan, Bo Zhang, Chang Guo, Xuegao Wang, Jiazheng Zhu, Sheng Xu, Lin Yan, Yiming Gao, Yuchen Zhang, Jiayu Cai
publish: 2025 IEEE 9th Conference on Energy Internet and Energy System Integration (EI2)
abstract: This article proposes an improved grey target-prospect theory method to quantify the adjustable potential of electrified flexible source-loads in ports while meeting core operational requirements. Focusing on three types of key electrified equipment, four adjustable dimensions are defined: time shift, berthing sequence adjustment, load interruption, and power regulation, each with corresponding indicators. The initial decision matrix is processed via improved grey target theory, and a risk-preferences-based value function and decision weight model are constructed using prospect theory. Optimal weights are determined by the maximum deviation method to quantify equipment adjustable potential. A case study confirms the method’s effectiveness.

title: An Application of Precision Agriculture Based on Machine Learning for Prediction of Crop Yield and Fertilizer Recommendation
authors: Utkarsha Bonde, Aryan Koshal, Sharvil Kolhe, Priya Dasarwar, Praveen Kumar Dhankar, Shreyas Holey
publish: 2026 Sixth International Conference on Advances in Electrical, Computing, Communications and Sustainable Technologies (ICAECT)
abstract: Nowadays, maximizing crop yields while reducing resource use is essential for successful and sustainable farming. Personalized crop and fertilizer suggestions are desperately needed in India's many agricultural regions, and the software helps with that. Based on a variety of environmental elements and soil conditions, our model uses machine learning techniques including logistic regression, random forests, k-nearest neighbours, linear regression, and support vector machines (SVM) to provide farmers unambiguous instructions. Crop databases, which are now accessible on many websites in the agriculture industry, are essential for recommending appropriate crops. Accordingly, farmers may be able to choose crops more effectively if a decision support system analyses the crop dataset using machine learning techniques. Using crop cloud data, global positioning system coordinates, and machine learning algorithms, the project's main objective is to provide farmers with more accurate and practical crop recommendations more rapidly. This comprehensive strategy aims to boost our proposal's legitimacy in light of the particular difficulties presented by India's climate and diversity. Crop history, soil properties, climate data, and fertilizer usage patterns are among the variables that the model takes in. In order to give farmers in various areas real advantages, we worked to develop a range of practical and adaptable goods that might be more effective and beneficial. For the project, a model that gives farmers information on crop planting and selection is required. Through the use of machine learning techniques like Random Forests and SVM, the effort promotes sustainable practices, modernizes agriculture, and increases the overall profitability of Indian agriculture.

title:
Study on Propagation Characteristics of Rotating Detonation Wave with Pre-heated CH
                    <sub>4</sub>
                    /CO/H
                    <sub>2</sub>
                    Gas Mixtures
authors: Jinping Wang, Qiaodong Bai, Jiaxiang Han, Jing Liu, Quanbiao He, Quan Zheng, Chunsheng Weng
publish: 2025 3rd Asian Aerospace and Astronautics Conference (AAAC)
abstract: To investigate the rotating detonation characteristics of the primary components in high-temperature solid fuel-rich gas, experimental studies were conducted using pre-heated CH<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">4</inf>/CO/H<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</inf> gas mixtures with variable equivalence ratios. The gas mixture temperature was maintained at 648 ± 27 K in this study, enabling successful initiation within an equivalence ratio range of 0.86-1.32 under these conditions. Lower equivalence ratios facilitated the formation of multi-wave modes, though with reduced wave velocities and lower average peak pressures. In contrast, the single-wave mode demonstrated significantly increased filling height of the combustible mixture along with higher average peak pressures.

title: Extended BIGS Algorithm to Find All Possible Metric Bases and Fault Tolerant Metric Bases in Certain Johnson Graphs for Error-Correcting Code Design
authors: Abigail M V, Simon Raj F
publish: 2026 Sixth International Conference on Advances in Electrical, Computing, Communications and Sustainable Technologies (ICAECT)
abstract: Efficient and fault-tolerant communication networks demand topologies that can uniquely identify nodes and recover from single-point failures. The metric dimension of a graph provides a mathematical foundation for such identification using carefully selected reference nodes known as metric bases. This study applies the concept to Johnson graphs, that are used to represent interconnection networks and errorcorrecting code structures. An extended BIGS (Bharati-Indra-George-Simon) algorithm is introduced to determine all possible metric bases and 1-fault-tolerant metric bases for Johnson graphs <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$J(n, 2), 4 \leq n \leq 10$</tex>. The proposed method improves the reliability of node identification by accounting for possible vertex failures. This research combines theoretical graph parameters with practical applications in data and communication networks.

title: Initial Sizing of an Electric Tiltrotor UAV for Urban Air Logistics
authors: Boxu An, Yongzhi Wang
publish: 2025 3rd Asian Aerospace and Astronautics Conference (AAAC)
abstract: Electric tiltrotor aircraft represent a transformative class of variable-flight-state vehicles that synergize the cruise efficiency of fixed-wing platforms with the hover capability of rotary-wing systems. This paper presents a comprehensive initial sizing methodology for small-scale electric tiltrotor unmanned aerial vehicles (UAVs) targeting air logistics applications. This method enables rapid preliminary design of an electric tiltrotor UAV for Urban Air Logistics during the conceptual design phase and determines weight parameters and flight performance characteristics. The approach employs a requirements-driven framework derived from practical logistics operational scenarios to establish design specifications. Design points are selected through fixed-wing performance constraints, integrating vertical takeoff and landing dynamics with conventional fixed-wing aerodynamics. Battery mass fraction (BMF) requirements for target range achievement are derived via electric aircraft range estimation formulas. Phase-specific power and energy analysis across mission profiles determines required battery capacity and mass allocation. Iterative mass reconciliation of airframe components converges total aircraft weight, while consolidated performance parameters including operational range and flight envelope characteristics are established through systematic mass property breakdown.

title: A Convex Optimization-Based Approach for Rapid Straight-Line Rendezvous of Spacecraft
authors: Hongcai Li, Xiang Zhang, Shuhui Fan, Hossam Hendy, Yanchen Pan
publish: 2025 3rd Asian Aerospace and Astronautics Conference (AAAC)
abstract: Aiming at the problems of high fuel consumption in traditional spacecraft straight-line rendezvous guidance and the difficulty of integrating non-convex obstacle avoidance constraints into the optimization framework, this paper proposes a convex optimization-based method for rapid straight-line of spacecraft. Taking the Clohessy-Wiltshire (CW) equation as the relative dynamic model, a constraint system including thrust, corridor path, obstacle avoidance and boundary conditions is constructed. The non-convex obstacle avoidance constraints are iteratively linearized through first-order Taylor expansion, and slack variables are introduced to soften the hard constraints of the corridor path. A standard convex optimization model is built with the goal of minimizing fuel consumption, and an iterative algorithm is designed to update the reference trajectory to improve constraint accuracy. Simulation results show that this method can realize the rapid rendezvous of spacecraft along the straight-line reference path and avoid obstacles, taking into account fuel economy and mission safety, and provides an engineering-feasible solution for the on-orbit emergency services of spacecraft.

title: A Stochastic Optimization Method for Distribution Network Operation and Maintenance Driven by Load Uncertainty Scenarios
authors: Yufei Gu, Jun Deng, Hongrui Lu, Yuxiong Huang, Ji Qiao, Minjie Jin
publish: 2025 IEEE 9th Conference on Energy Internet and Energy System Integration (EI2)
abstract: In order to enhance the distribution network's resilience against load fluctuations, this paper proposes a scenario-driven stochastic optimization method for distribution network operation and maintenance under load uncertainty. Initial load scenarios are generated using Latin hypercube sampling as the initial step in the process. Following the processes of scenario reduction and multi-period concatenation, the K-Means clustering algorithm extracts representative load scenarios. A stochastic optimization model is subsequently established with the objective of minimizing total lifecycle costs. This is transformed into a mixed-integer linear programming problem, which can be solved directly via the solver. The efficacy of the model in achieving operation and maintenance decisions with superior economic efficiency and reliability is demonstrated by case studies.

title: Optimization of Power Generation Efficiency in Tower Solar Heliostat Fields in High-Latitude Regions Based on an Improved Grey Wolf Algorithm
authors: Xiao Zhou, Jingxue Guo, Yinke Dou, Yucheng Wang, Boyang Huang, Zhengxiang Xu, Haoqin Wang, Yizhuo Zhang
publish: 2025 IEEE 9th Conference on Energy Internet and Energy System Integration (EI2)
abstract: Tower solar thermal power generation is a relatively ideal and technically mature approach for large-scale utilization of solar energy. In view of the problems of unreasonable heliostat field layout and energy waste, optimizing the heliostat field layout is of great significance. By combining the solar conical model prediction and the Monte Carlo ray-tracing method, this study proposes a multi-objective optimization model with the average optical efficiency per unit area and the maximum thermal power output of the heliostat field as the objectives. While optimizing the mathematical model of the heliostat field layout, the maximum thermal power output of the field is introduced as an evaluation metric, which reflects both the collection efficiency of the heliostat field and the land-use economy. Furthermore, a heliostat field layout optimization method for tower solar thermal power plants based on an improved Grey Wolf Optimizer (GWO) is proposed. The improved algorithm integrates the Grey Wolf Optimizer with memory, local search, and evolutionary operators, thereby enhancing the global search ability and accuracy of the optimization.

title: Multi-objective Optimization Autonomous Strategy for Distribution Transformer Based on Double-layer Optimization
authors: Laite Dang, Zhou Sha, Ming Ni, Xiaochuan Song, Feng Li, Moxuan Zhao
publish: 2025 IEEE 9th Conference on Energy Internet and Energy System Integration (EI2)
abstract: Under the background of the acceleration of energy transition and the construction of new power systems, distributed photovoltaic (PV) and electric vehicle (EV) charging facilities have experienced rapid growth. The "dual randomness" characteristics of their power sources and loads pose novel challenges to the safe operation of the transformer district (TD). To address issues such as power/voltage exceeding limits in distribution transformer and high-power fluctuation disturbances caused by the random generation/discharge patterns of distributed PV, EV charging/discharging resources, this paper proposes a multi-objective optimal autonomous control strategy for distribution substations based on dual-layer optimization. Firstly, based on the output characteristic models of distributed PV, electric vehicles, and energy storage resources, this paper establishes an equivalent autonomous control model for distribution substations. Second, a multi-objective optimal autonomous strategy focusing on consumption, economy, and efficiency is formulated for substation-level operations. Finally, the proposed strategy’s rationality and effectiveness are verified through dual-layer optimization algorithm implementation.

title: Missing Data Reconstruction with Spatiotemporal Robustness Using Adversarial Learning
authors: Shijie Guan, Yingying Zheng, Shaoshuai Liu, Li Liu
publish: 2025 IEEE 9th Conference on Energy Internet and Energy System Integration (EI2)
abstract: Wind power time series often exhibit non-completely random missing data, leading to bias accumulation and unstable forecasting. This study proposes a generative adversarial imputation framework that integrates physical consistency constraints with uncertainty modeling. Based on daily-scale serialization and diurnal time encoding, the method employs self-supervised masking and a prompting mechanism to stabilize adversarial training, and performs imputation only at the originally missing positions during inference. Using data from the Biliu River wind farm in Inner Mongolia as a case study, the proposed method significantly improves the accuracy and robustness of missing segment reconstruction while ensuring consistency with observed values, providing a stable input for subsequent forecasting and dispatch tasks.

title: A Generalized Baseline Estimation Method for Diverse Flexible Load Resources in Demand Response
authors: Hao Feng, Weiwei Zhang, Kun Wang, Jianghui Luo, Yongliang Yin, Li Yang, Qinyu Chen, Xuan Wei
publish: 2025 IEEE 9th Conference on Energy Internet and Energy System Integration (EI2)
abstract: Flexible load resources (e.g., electric vehicles, data centers, and so on) exhibit significant flexibility and cost-effectiveness, holding immense potential for enhancing grid resilience and reliability. Accurate and fair baseline load estimation is crucial for incentive-based demand response (DR). However, due to the inherent variability of flexible load resources, traditional baseline load estimation methods struggle to meet these requirements. To address this challenge, we propose a novel method for baseline load estimation based on neural network-driven multi-objective collaborative optimization. Firstly, we devise a framework within neural networks that collaboratively optimize accuracy and fairness. Secondly, we develop distinct estimation methods for customer baseline load (CBL) and aggregated baseline load (ABL) for flexible resources. Finally, by integrating the load characteristics of different flexible resources, we present specific baseline estimation methodologies and validate them using real-world datasets. Experimental results demonstrate that the proposed approach outperforms traditional algorithms and other state-of-the-art methods in terms of both accuracy and fairness.

title: Optimal Load Reduction Calculation Based on CBAM-CNN Under Typhoon Disasters
authors: Song Gao, Ying Wang, Hao Ding, Changjiang Wang, Xiaozhe Song, Wenqi Han
publish: 2025 IEEE 9th Conference on Energy Internet and Energy System Integration (EI2)
abstract: This paper proposes a resilience optimal load reduction calculation method for power systems under typhoon disasters by integrating Convolutional Block Attention Module (CBAM) with CNN. The hybrid CBAM-CNN model enhances feature extraction through channel-spatial attention mechanisms, while the resilience optimal load reduction calculation framework quantifies component failure rates using Batts typhoon model and rain load theory. Computational analysis of the IEEE-RTS79 system verifies the effectiveness and superiority of the proposed method.

title: Personalized Content Recommendation on Instagram: Leveraging AI to Enhance User Reach
authors: Ruchi Sharma, T. Dhivya Bharathi, Ajmeera Kiran, Saravanan R, Sajiv G, N. Dharani
publish: 2026 Sixth International Conference on Advances in Electrical, Computing, Communications and Sustainable Technologies (ICAECT)
abstract: Reel recommendations are useful in increasing user engagement by presenting content that fits their interests. Previous systems like collaborative filtering and content-based systems are unable to capture dynamic user-content interaction in complex forms and therefore give inaccurate suggestions. To overcome, a GCN-based model predicts engagement by modeling users and Reels as graph, where direct and indirect relationships are learnt. Instagram Reels Cleaned Dataset contains lemmatized hashtags, number of tags, topics and encoded topical features, so the model could combine content attributes with user behavior patterns. Users and Reels are nodes that has histories, and the links, commenting, and sharing. The GCN uses several convolution layers to aggregate information of the neighboring node to reveal latent patterns and hence make more accurate recommendations. This system ultimately provides the personalized Reel recommendations that are more aligned with user interests and enhance the overall experience of the user in a more engaging fashion.

title: Enhancing Financial Inclusion Through AI: A Machine Learning Methodology for Credit Rating and Loan Accessibility
authors: Kavitha U, Sreejith S, Manoj P, Poornima, Harshad Ali Khan
publish: 2026 Sixth International Conference on Advances in Electrical, Computing, Communications and Sustainable Technologies (ICAECT)
abstract: With the advent of big data, machine learning machine learning and prediction analytics are essential for empowering businesses to use vast amounts of user-related data to guide better decisions. The ability to precisely predict a borrower's creditworthiness has a substantial impact on financial stability and institutional growth, making credit risk assessment Among the primary uses of predictive analytics. Even though traditional methods for data mining have been used to construct a number of credit scoring models, issues like uneven accuracy and sample variability still exist. In this piece, we suggest an improved predictive modelling framework that divides the dataset used for training into more logical subgroups using an Optimized Clustering Algorithm (OCA) ensemble. Each model can discover more pertinent patterns inside each group because to this separation, which also lowers intra-group variation, we evaluate the effectiveness considering five different models for machine learning using publicly accessible German and Australian consumer credit datasets. The findings show that by producing precise, intelligible IF-THEN decision rules, our OCA-based ensemble framework continuously enhances predictive performance. It also shows notable decreases in credit risk, giving financial organizations a more accurate and comprehensible system for evaluating credit. Our method promotes better informed lending decisions, improves forecasting accuracy, and fortifies risk management techniques.

title: Enhancing Internet Attack Detection Through Advanced Feature Engineering, Deep Learning and Data Augmentation Techniques
authors: N. Raja Rajeswari, B. Sarvesan, K. Ramesh Babu, M.P. Kumar, N. Babu, M. Giri
publish: 2026 Sixth International Conference on Advances in Electrical, Computing, Communications and Sustainable Technologies (ICAECT)
abstract: Day by day the numbers of threats on cyber applications are increased rapidly, insiders of an organization modify the data by following security approaches like latest tools, grouping of networks and without trust. The main goal of attackers is to identify weakness in cyber security to force various types of attack to extract valuables. To achieve these attackers applied latest approaches to hack payload key and then achieve their goals. In this research to identify insider attacks we studied number of attempts on network connections and then detect attacker's details like IP, protocols used and signature of attackers. The dataset is collected by using IDS techniques, data samples are preprocessed, apply augmentation approach to fit data samples, features are discovered with help of feature engineering, and then applied ML and DL techniques to detect various types of attacks. Performance of various ML and DL techniques are evaluated by using various types of metrics.

title: An Event-Aware Price Spread Arbitrage Decision Framework for Electricity Retailers in Spot Markets
authors: Haozhe Lu, Xiangrui Meng, Huanxin Liao, Kaixiang Zhang
publish: 2025 IEEE 9th Conference on Energy Internet and Energy System Integration (EI2)
abstract: In power spot markets, electricity retailers (ER) arbitrage the Day-Ahead and Real-Time Price Spread (DRPS), while facing highly volatile prices and binding market rules.This paper proposes an event-aware arbitrage decision framework for electricity retailers in spot markets, integrating probabilistic price spread forecasting, large language model-based event extraction, and reinforcement learning. A two-stage Long Short-Term Memory (LSTM) predicts the direction and magnitude of day-ahead and real-time price spreads, while LLMs extract event-driven features from unstructured texts to enhance forecast accuracy. These signals feed into a deviation-aware RL module that optimizes arbitrage decisions under market constraints. Empirical results on Australian market data demonstrate improved forecast accuracy, higher cumulative profits, and effective risk control compared to benchmark strategies, validating the framework’s practical value for electricity retailers.

title: Transient Voltage Stability Assessment of Renewable Energy Power Systems Based on CBAM-CNN-LF
authors: Yanchao Gao, Chengliang Hao, Changjiang Wang, Youqing Xu, Minglei Jiang, Peng Wang
publish: 2025 IEEE 9th Conference on Energy Internet and Energy System Integration (EI2)
abstract: To address the issues of insufficient model accuracy and weak physical interpretability in transient voltage stability assessment for power systems with a high proportion of renewable energy, this paper proposes a hybrid data-model driven method that integrates deep neural networks with Lyapunov stability theory. The proposed method first utilizes a Convolutional Block Attention Module-based Convolutional Neural Network (CBAM-CNN) to extract features from the input data sequentially along two independent dimensions—channel and space—thereby accurately characterizing the spatiotemporal dynamics of the system during transients. Subsequently, a Lyapunov energy function is constructed based on the neural network, and its derivative along the system trajectory is computed using automatic differentiation. Finally, a composite loss function, which integrates classification accuracy with physical constraints, is designed to guide the collaborative optimization of the model. Simulation results on a modified IEEE 39-bus system demonstrate that the proposed method achieves significantly higher assessment accuracy than traditional approaches.

title: Transient Photoresponse Analysis of Quantum Dot Photodetectors for Ultra Fast Imaging
authors: Gan Fang, Yueyang Luo, Yihan Song, Wei Chen, Haodong Tang
publish: 2025 International Conference on Energy, Materials, and Photonics (EMP)
abstract: Transient photoresponse characteristics of quantum dot (QD) photodetectors are critical for their application in ultra-fast imaging systems. In this work, we systematically analyze the response dynamics of PbS QD photodiodes under varying optical power levels. Our measurements demonstrate a clear dependence of transient response speed on light intensity, where higher optical powers lead to slower response, manifested by prolonged rise times. This phenomenon aligns with bimolecular recombination dynamics and is attributed to increased photogenerated carrier densities that enhance recombination rates and induce space-charge effects, impeding charge separation and transport. These insights deepen the understanding of transient behaviors in QD-based photodetectors and provide guidance for mitigating recombination effects to advance high-speed optoelectronic imaging applications.

title: Distributed Voltage Optimization Control of Energy Storage–PV Coordinated Distribution Network
authors: Haoyi Zhu, Bibo Chen, Ting Yuan, Lin Zhang, Tian Cheng, Yonggang Wang
publish: 2025 IEEE 9th Conference on Energy Internet and Energy System Integration (EI2)
abstract: To address the voltage violation problem caused by high penetration of distributed photovoltaic (PV) generation in distribution networks, this paper proposes a distributed voltage coordination control strategy based on the alternating direction method of multipliers (ADMM). First, with energy storage nodes as the centers, a greedy expansion algorithm is employed to intelligently partition the distribution network by comprehensively considering electrical distance, topological continuity, and regional balance. Then, a distributed voltage optimization model is developed to achieve coordinated control between energy storage systems (ESS) and PV inverters, where the bidirectional active power regulation capability of ESS is utilized as the primary voltage control mechanism, and the reactive power support of PV inverters serves as an auxiliary measure. Finally, the ADMM algorithm based on the IEEE 33-bus test system is applied to realize parallel computation and boundary coordination among sub-regions. Simulation results demonstrate that the proposed strategy effectively eliminates voltage violations, maintaining all node voltages within the safe range of [0.95, 1.05] p.u. After control, the overall voltage quality and operational stability of the distribution network are significantly improved, verifying the effectiveness of the proposed distributed control strategy.

title: Day-ahead operational risk assessment of dual-high system considering large-scale fluctuation of inertia
authors: Xinyi Wang, Haibo Zhang
publish: 2025 IEEE 9th Conference on Energy Internet and Energy System Integration (EI2)
abstract: With the rapid development of high-renewable power systems, multiple uncertainties have intensified the fluctuation of system inertia supply and demand, making traditional deterministic assessments inadequate for identifying inertia limit risks. To address this issue, this paper proposes a two-layer hierarchical day-ahead inertia risk assessment method. Probabilistic models of renewable generation and system operating states are established to quantify multi-source uncertainties. The framework consists of two layers: the first quantifies the impact of operational uncertainties such as equipment outages, while the second applies the Conditional Value at Risk (CVaR) theory to capture extreme risks arising from renewable variability. A "baseline correction and bidirectional risk aggregation" mechanism then transforms these indices into a practical inertia security interval, providing quantitative support for day-ahead scheduling. Case studies on an equivalent power grid demonstrate that the proposed method effectively reveals the temporal evolution of inertia risks and provides scientific guidance for secure and economical system operation. Keywords: System inertia, operational risk assessment, uncertainty, inertia supply.

title: Preliminary Study on the Dynamic Security Domain of Grid-Forming Generating Units
authors: Youqing Xu, Yong Jiang, Minglei Jiang, Bowen Wang, Yuchen Qiu, Changjiang Wang
publish: 2025 IEEE 9th Conference on Energy Internet and Energy System Integration (EI2)
abstract: This paper proposes a method for constructing the feasible scheduling domain of a system incorporating GFM-VSC. First, by integrating the equal-area criterion, it analyzes the stability mechanisms under both "normal" and "current-limiting" operating modes. The study examines the influence mechanisms of GFM-VSC power contribution ratio, current overload capacity, and control parameters on transient stability performance, thereby establishing instability criteria for identifying system transient stability boundary operating points. Subsequently, numerical simulations identify critical unstable operating points, while the least-squares high-order approximation method approximates DSR boundaries to analyze GFM-VSC boundary influence patterns. Finally, analyses and validations are conducted on an improved three-machine nine-node test system using MATLAB/Simulink. Results demonstrate that the proposed method effectively characterizes DSR in power systems, providing valuable reference for power system dispatch operations.

authors: Sebastian Wittlich, Hendrik Wöhrle, Tobias Krawutschke
