title: Deep Reinforcement Learning-Assisted Automated Operator Portfolio for Constrained Multi-Objective Optimization
authors: Shuai Shao, Ye Tian, Shangshang Yang, Xingyi Zhang
publish: IEEE Transactions on Emerging Topics in Computational Intelligence
abstract: Constrained multi-objective optimization problems (CMOPs) are of great significance in the context of practical applications, ranging from scientific to engineering domains. Most existing constrained multi-objective evolutionary algorithms (CMOEAs) usually employ fixed operators all the time, which exhibit poor versatility in handling various CMOPs. Therefore, some recent studies have focused on adaptively selecting the best operators for the current population states during the search process. The evolutionary algorithms proposed in these studies learn the value of each operator and recommend the operator with the highest value for the current population, resulting in only a single operator being recommended at each generation, which can potentially lead to local optima and inefficient utilization of function evaluations. To address the dilemma in operator adaptation, this paper proposes a reinforcement learning-based automated operator portfolio approach to learn an allocation scheme of operators at each generation. This approach considers the optimization-related and constraint-related features of the current population as states, the overall improvement in population convergence and diversity as rewards, and different operator portfolios as actions. By utilizing deep neural networks to establish a mapping model between the population states and the expected cumulative rewards, the proposed approach determines the optimal operator portfolio during the evolutionary process. By embedding the proposed approach into existing CMOEAs, a deep reinforcement learning-assisted automated operator portfolio based evolutionary algorithm for solving CMOPs, abbreviated as CMOEA-AOP, is developed. Empirical studies on 33 benchmark problems demonstrate that the proposed algorithm significantly enhances the performance of CMOEAs and exhibits more stable performance across different CMOPs.

title: SPNet: Leveraging Sketch Proxies for Robust Cross-View Geo-Localization
authors: Shuaiyuan Du, Chaoyi Hong, Zhiguo Cao
publish: IEEE Transactions on Circuits and Systems for Video Technology
abstract: Cross-view geo-localization (CVGL) determines the geographic location of a query image by matching it with the most similar GPS-tagged satellite images. Existing methods have improved cross-view feature consistency through complex network architectures but still face challenges under significant illumination variations. Inspired by cognitive psychology, we observe that humans can selectively ignore color distractions and focus on structural information under certain circumstances. Therefore, we propose an SPNet, leveraging sketches to enhance structural feature consistency and achieve illumination-robust CVGL. SPNet employs Sketch Proxy Training, which introduces both RGB and sketch images during training. By leveraging the structural consistency provided by sketches under color variations, it mitigates the network’s over-reliance on color cues and achieves robustness to illumination changes. To further integrate multi-source information, we design a Recurrent Progressive Channel Attention (RPCA) module that progressively selects and reweights channel features, effectively combining the semantic cues of RGB images with the structural information of sketches. In addition, we introduce a High-Pass Cross-Layer Connection (HPCL) to transmit high-frequency information across feature layers, emphasizing edges and details to reinforce structural modeling and cross-layer feature consistency. Extensive experiments demonstrate that our SPNet achieves superior performance on several CVGL datasets, including University-1652, SUES-200, CVUSA, and CVACT, and ranks first on the public leaderboard of the University-160k-WX dataset released by UAVM 2024, further validating its robustness and generalization capability.

title: Dual-Verifiable Federated Learning with Vector Commitments Against Collusion Attacks
authors: Kaiping Cui, Xia Feng, Liangmin Wang, Zhiquan Liu
publish: IEEE Transactions on Dependable and Secure Computing
abstract: Collusion attacks, where the server and malicious clients collaborate to bypass gradient source confirmation or tamper with aggregation results, cause a fundamental damage to the training process in federated learning (FL). However, existing verifiable FL frameworks typically adopt a split-verification model-clients can independently validate the correctness of aggregation results, while the server is responsible for confirming the legitimacy of gradient sources. Thus, the collusion attack has emerged as a critical and intractable vulnerability, as it completely collapses this split-verification model, thereby invalidating such verification mechanisms. To tackle this fundamental issue, we propose an innovative dual-verifiable FL framework. Specifically, by leveraging vector commitments, our scheme first integrates both gradient source confirmation and aggregation result verification into a unified framework. Based on this unified design, our scheme implements two targeted strategies to defend against collusion attacks. To prevent collusion-enabled gradient source spoofing, our scheme introduces a semi-trusted verification cluster in place of unreliable server-side validation and embeds an anonymized identity-check strategy to collaboratively confirm gradient source legitimacy. To counter collusion-driven manipulation of gradient aggregation results, our scheme customizes auxiliary verification proofs with a computational one-wayness for client-uploaded gradients. This renders it infeasible for adversaries to tamper with the aggregation result through reverse engineering. Under experiments and security analyses, our scheme achieves reliable dual-verification and robust resistance to collusion attacks. Moreover, it reduces computation and communication overhead by at least 40.83% and 50.47%, respectively, compared to state-of-the-art verifiable FL schemes.

title: Secure Transmission in Space-Air-Ground Integrated Networks assisted by Reconfigurable Holographic Surface
authors: Yingchen Gu, Ruyan Wang, Yaping Cui, Peng He, Dapeng Wu
publish: IEEE Transactions on Vehicular Technology
abstract: This paper proposes a secure space-air-ground in tegrated network (SAGIN) transmission scheme in which an unmanned aerial vehicle (UAV) equipped with reconfigurable holographic surfaces (RHS) antennas to tackle the challenges of ultra-long distances in ground-space (G2S) links and eaves dropping risks in wireless channels. Leveraging the holographic beamforming principle, the UAV can relay confidential G2S signals while transmitting interference as artificial noise toward eavesdroppers to counter interception. The closed-form expressions of the secrecy transmission rate for SAGIN are first derived. Then, an optimization strategy is designed under onboard energy consumption constraints, which jointly optimizes the beam radiation amplitude, 3D trajectory and power allocation, aiming to maximize the average secrecy rate (ASR) while satisfying the quality of service for legitimate users. A block coordinate descent algorithm is employed to decouple the original non convex problem into four subproblems, and feasible solutions are obtained through successive convex approximation. Simulation results validate the advantages of the proposed scheme, with the ASR significantly outperforming baseline schemes, such as those without holographic beamforming, horizon-only trajectory deployment, and without power control. The variations of the ASR with RHS element size and UAV transmit power are also analyzed. This research provides new theoretical insights and technical solutions for secure communication in SAGIN.

title: Sparse-View Computed Tomography Reconstruction Using a Unified Multi-Domain Network
authors: Wenting Xu, Hyesun Yang, Dae Yup Han, Hyosung Cho
publish: IEEE Transactions on Nuclear Science
abstract: Sparse-view computed tomography (CT) reduces radiation dose by limiting the number of projection angles; however, the resulting data incompleteness introduces severe streak artifacts and texture blurring when conventional reconstruction methods are applied. To address this challenge, we propose a multi-domain unified framework that operates in two stages. First, a sinogram-domain module equipped with sparse–dense mixture self-attention (SMSA) completes missing projection data while suppressing spurious correlations. Second, a joint spatial–frequency encoder–decoder integrates SMSA with Fourier-based frequency fusion, enabling the fusion of global contextual cues and spectral information with local spatial details to generate high-quality reconstructed images. The entire network is trained end-to-end on two distinct datasets across multiple projection settings. Quantitative evaluations show that the proposed method achieves competitive or superior peak signal-to-noise ratio (PSNR) and structural similarity index (SSIM), together with consistently lower root-mean-square error (RMSE), compared with six representative sparse-view CT reconstruction methods, indicating its effectiveness in recovering fine anatomical structures while suppressing artifacts.

title: Benchmarking NC-CrossFET: Device-Level Insights and System Implications
authors: A. S. S. V. Sathvik, Radha Bayya, D. Rajesh Reddy, Satish Maheshwaram, Nitanshu Chauhan, Shuvam Pawar
publish: IEEE Transactions on Electron Devices
abstract: In this work, we introduce, for the first time, a negative-capacitance-based cross field-effect transistor (NC-CrossFET) and report a systematic quasi-static and transient evaluation of its operation. The CrossFET, formed by orthogonally arranged n- and p-channel devices, can function as an NFET, PFET, or inverter, offering footprint reduction through a shared-channel design. While the compact layout improves area efficiency, the baseline device exhibits a larger subthreshold swing (SS) and degraded static switching performance compared with a gate-all-around (GAA) FET-based counterpart of the same gate length. To overcome these limitations, a ferroelectric (FE) layer is integrated into the gate-stack to obtain performance improvement with negative-capacitance (NC) behavior. Simulation results show that NC-CrossFETs: 1) restore steep SS (<inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$\lt\!\!\!{60}{\,}\textrm {mV}/\textrm {dec}$</tex-math> </inline-formula>); 2) enhance <sc xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">on</small>- and <sc xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">off</small>-current performance; 3) increase inverter gain; and 4) improve static noise margin. We performed quasi-static simulations while varying the FE layer thickness to evaluate its effect on electrostatic gate control and device performance. Transient analysis reveals reduced propagation delay in NC-CrossFET circuits compared with baseline CrossFETs. The NC-CrossFET is compatible with CMOS process flow, enabling practical integration for compact and energy-efficient logic applications.

title: A Two-Wave Evaluation of the Web-Related Security Standards Implemented by Higher Education Institutions in Germany
authors: Jackson Barreto, Sebastian Neef, Pedro Pinto
publish: IEEE Access
abstract: Higher Education Institutions (HEIs) have a large and transient user base, hold rich and diverse data, and thus represent high-value targets for cyber threats. Given their broad attack surface, HEIs must ensure that fundamental security services and configurations, including the Web-related ones, are correctly implemented and routinely audited. This study presents a two-wave evaluation of the web security postures of 400 German HEIs. The web security posture is evaluated across three domains: Hypertext Transfer Protocol Secure (HTTPS), Hypertext Transfer Protocol (HTTP) Security Headers, and Domain Name System Security Extensions (DNSSEC), using measurement snapshots with data collected in the years 2023 and 2025. The results were aggregated by Level 2 of the Nomenclature of Territorial Units for Statistics (NUTS2) regions and further segmented by public and private sectors. The general results reveal a sound cryptographic baseline, with approximately 90% of institutions using valid certificates and high-security key lengths. However, the downside is evident in DNSSEC adoption, which reached only 13%, and in the implementation of security headers, where all observed Content-Security-Policy (CSP) deployments and 70% of HTTP Strict Transport Security (HSTS) configurations are classified as weak. The two-wave comparison between 2023 and 2025 indicates a trajectory of slow, incremental modernization, with modest gains in header adoption but near-stagnation in DNSSEC deployment. These results suggest that, while German HEIs are gradually improving their defenses, a systemic gap still exists between the availability of web-related security standards and their effective and secure implementation.

title: Wide-Range Three-Port Converter with Voltage Constraint Elimination and All-Mode High Efficiency for PV-Storage Systems
authors: Zhongxian Wang, Tao Lian, Yunfei Li, Shuli Sun
publish: IEEE Transactions on Power Electronics
abstract: Conventional non-isolated three-port converters (TPCs) suffer from port voltage constraints and multi-stage power conversion, leading to limited efficiency and operating range. This paper presents a universal TPC architecture construction that systematically integrates missing power paths into classical P1S3 and P2S2 structures to enable a unified and flexible topology derivation platform. Utilizing basic switching cells, six novel non-isolated TPCs with full-port wide-range capability are derived. Among them, three feature a P1S2 architecture, which ensures single-stage power conversion in all power flow modes, achieving all-mode high efficiency—defined as consistently high efficiency across every operational mode. For the selected optimal topology TPC–IV, a competitive control strategy between an output voltage regulator and a battery discharge regulator is developed to enable smooth power distribution and stable operation. Experimental validation using a 150W prototype shows that TPC–IV achieves independent voltage regulation across all ports over a wide voltage range, with efficiencies from 91.06% to 97.97% over varying voltages and from 92.05% to 97.83% under different load levels. The close alignment between experimental and theoretical results confirms the correctness and superior performance of the proposed approach.

title: Shape Sensing for Detecting Low Curvature Using Indoor Optical Cable
authors: Shintaro Nakamoto, Makito Kobayashi, Nobutomo Hanzawa, Takashi Matsui, Kazuhide Nakajima, Hideaki Murayama
publish: Journal of Lightwave Technology
abstract: This paper presents a novel approach for shape sensing using indoor optical cables. The distributed strain sensing (DSS) system adopts Brillouin optical time domain reflectometry (BOTDR), which makes it suitable for applications that require long-distance DSS system, such as structural health monitoring of large-scale infrastructures. To the best of our knowledge, previous studies using BOTDR have been limited to detecting the curvatures of approximately 1.0 m<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">-1</sup> or higher. This limitation is mainly due to a low signal-to-noise ratio (SNR), where the amount of bending strain is small relative to the measurement error. The use of indoor optical cables, which have relatively large core-to-core distances, are effective in improving the SNR. Moreover, the rectangular cross-section of the cable allows installation with its longer side in contact with the structure, which helps suppress fiber twists. In this study, we developed a two-dimensional shape sensing algorithm with the compensation of core-to-core distances. The algorithm also averages measurements from multiple cores to reduce the measurement errors. Experimental results show the shapes with the curvature as low as 0.098 m<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">-1</sup> were reconstructed successfully, demonstrating the potential of indoor optical cables for the detection of relatively low curvature. Indoor optical cables thus provide an effective approach to shape sensing, particularly in the applications that require the monitoring of large-scale structures.

title: Topology-based Binary Integer Linear Programming for Optimal Meter Placement in Power Systems
authors: Abdul Aziz Guevarra Mabaning
publish: IEEE Transactions on Power Systems
abstract: The Optimal Meter Placement (OMP) problem is traditionally addressed using heuristic algorithms or nonlinear programming, which often face challenges regarding solution reproducibility and the guarantee of global optimality, particularly in large-scale networks. Furthermore, existing models are typically tailored to specific sensor types, limiting their adaptability. To address these methodological limitations, this paper presents a generalized, topology-based Binary Integer Linear Programming (BILP) framework. While the proposed model is demonstrated using Phasor Measurement Units (PMUs), Line Flow Meters (LFMs), and Bus Injection Meters (BIMs), its mathematical structure is designed to be extensible to any type of meter. By translating topological observability rules into linear inequality constraints, the formulation circumvents the nonconvexity inherent in traditional nonlinear models. This linearization allows the optimization problem to be solved using determinis tic branch-and-bound algorithms, ensuring convergence to the global optimum within finite time. The framework minimizes instrumentation costs while satisfying complete numerical observability and is validated across standard IEEE test systems. The results indicate that the proposed BILP approach provides a computationally tractable, reproducible, and adaptable solution, offering a rigorous baseline for diverse metering strategies in power system planning.

title: A Low-Latency, High-Accuracy Second-Order Generalized Integrator-Based Synchrophasor Estimation Method for P-Class PMU
authors: Maidul Islam, Shamim Reza, Aynal Haque
publish: IEEE Access
abstract: Phasor measurement units (PMUs) that are protection-oriented (P-class) must achieve a balance between superb precision of measurements and minimal latency in order to promptly identify any unusual power system operation circumstances. This article provides a novel generalized integrator of second order (SOGI)-based phasor estimation method integrated with the Park transformation (PT) and cascaded delayed signal cancellation (CDSC) technique, designed to adhere to P-class PMU IEEE standard 2018. The objective is to enhance synchrophasor estimation accuracy under dynamic and distorted grid conditions while maintaining computational efficiency. The proposed method integrates SOGI for orthogonal signal generation, the Park transformation for accurate frequency demodulation, and CDSC for effective harmonic suppression, while maintaining minimal latency. This combined approach ensures robust performance across off-nominal frequencies, harmonics, and transient events. The proposed method exhibits improved accuracy and responsiveness, consistently remaining well within IEEE standard limits across all compliance test scenarios. It excels in dynamic tests with low error and quick response times and achieves exceptionally low values in steady-state tests with total vector error (TVE) of 0.0026%, frequency error (FE) of 2.38 × 10<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">−8</sup> Hz, and rate of change of frequency error (RFE) of 5.6 × 10<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">−8</sup> Hz per second. This work advances PMU technology by addressing the limitations of traditional discrete Fourier transform (DFT), phase-locked loop (PLL), and subspace-based methods, offering a high-precision, low-latency solution for modern power systems.

title: AutoML-Pipeline: A RAG-Enhanced Code Generation Framework with Pre-validation for Cloud-Native Machine Learning Workflows
authors: Wenyu Zhao, Tingjie Chen, Jie Si Yang, Lei Qiu
publish: IEEE Access
abstract: The proliferation of cloud-native machine learning platforms has significantly accelerated model development and deployment cycles. However, constructing and maintaining heterogeneous pipeline code spanning multiple languages (Python, YAML, Spark SQL) and cloud-specific configurations remains labor-intensive and error-prone. Existing LLM-based code generation tools lack awareness of runtime constraints and historical execution patterns, frequently producing code with resource misconfigurations or dependency conflicts that fail upon deployment. To address these challenges, we propose AutoML-Pipeline, a closed-loop code generation framework that integrates Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) with reinforcement learning feedback mechanisms. Our approach leverages a knowledge base constructed from successful pipeline execution logs to guide GPT-4 in generating deployment-ready code that adheres to platform-specific constraints. The key innovation lies in a novel Pre-validation Agent that employs simulated execution environments to predict resource consumption and detect dependency conflicts before actual deployment. This agent iteratively refines generated code through a feedback loop informed by predicted execution profiles and dependency graphs. We evaluate our framework on the CodeSearchNet dataset augmented with Azure ML pipeline specifications, demonstrating a 43.7% improvement in first-submission success rate and 31.2% reduction in resource over-provisioning compared to vanilla GPT-4 baselines. Ablation studies confirm that both the RAG retrieval mechanism and pre-validation agent contribute substantially to performance gains. Our work establishes a practical paradigm for integrating large language models with domain-specific runtime intelligence, with potential applications extending to other infrastructure-as-code generation tasks.

title: STA-UNet: Lightweight Spectral Segmentation for Mowing Robots
authors: Zhiwei Deng, Tao Lv, Zhan Shi, Xun Cao
publish: IEEE Robotics and Automation Letters
abstract: Achieving robust semantic segmentation for autonomous mowing robots requires discriminating between materials with subtle visual differences under challenging outdoor conditions. To address this, we present a novel framework that co-designs feature-specific spectral imaging and a lightweight deep learning network. On the sensing front, we develop a low-cost multispectral camera using a custom multi-band-pass (MBP) filter, engineered to capture distinctive spectral signatures of key classes like grass and shrubs, enabling parallax-free and compact data acquisition. For efficient inference, we propose STA-UNet, a lightweight encoder-decoder network that innovatively incorporates a Spectral Attention (SA) module to adaptively fuse the acquired spectral bands, and a Cross-Scale Texture Perception (CSTP) module to enhance spatial feature representation. Trained on Grass8K—a new 8,191-image multispectral dataset for lawn environments—STA-UNet achieves a remarkable mIoU of 88.63%, outperforming a standard U-Net by 7.11% while utilizing only one-quarter of the parameters. The system achieves real-time segmentation on an edge device with 5 TOPS of computing power, demonstrating a practical, high-performance perception solution for resource-constrained robotic applications. The dataset and code are made available at: <uri xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">https://drive.google.com/drive/folders/</uri> 1g1dzyc-Rn6iUWGGvIbnRr wRKUn2bic4?usp=drive link

title: Climb with SHERPA: Heuristic-Guided Reinforcement Learning via Segmented Experience Relay
authors: Minji Kim, Ganghun Lee, Minsu Lee, Byoung-Tak Zhang
publish: IEEE Robotics and Automation Letters
abstract: In sparse-reward, long-horizon domains, reinforcement learning (RL) often suffers from slow convergence and instability, complicating robotic manipulation. Previous heuristic-guided approaches have relied on step-level actions and imitation loss, but struggle to maintain temporal coherence or solve multi-stage tasks. We present SHERPA (Segmented Heuristic Experience Relay for Policy Assistance), which alternates control between a heuristic policy and an RL policy in contiguous segments, preserving coherent sub-trajectories and yielding more stable learning. Unlike imitation-based designs, SHERPA learns entirely through RL optimization, enabling the policy to benefit from and surpass heuristic guidance. We also incorporate phase-specific rewards derived from heuristic rules to provide targeted subgoal feedback. Across ten tasks in the Fetch and Panda suites, including four long-horizon benchmarks, SHERPA consistently outperforms RL, IL, and heuristic-guided baselines and demonstrates robustness even under degraded heuristics. Real-world experiments on a UR5 robot further confirm SHERPA's practical scalability.

title: Stability Optimization Strategy of Photovoltaic Power Farm and Flexible HVDC System Under Weak-Grid Conditions
authors: Guixing Yang, Hongshan Zhuang, Xingang Wang, Jiayu Bian, Shenhui Shao, Yi Qiao, Rongqin Hua, Peng Yi, Jiang Gu
publish: 2025 IEEE 9th Conference on Energy Internet and Energy System Integration (EI2)
abstract: With the rapid development of renewable energy, long-distance power transmission through Flexible High Voltage Direct Current (HVDC) systems has become mainstream. However, the stability issues of the interconnected system have become increasingly evident. To address this, this paper studies the stability of Photovoltaic (PV) Power Farms and flexible HVDC systems, with a focus on their operating characteristics under weak-grid conditions. First, an impedance model for the PV Power Farm and Modular Multilevel Converter (MMC)-based system is established, analyzing its electrical characteristics. The stability boundary of the system is then evaluated using the Nyquist stability criterion. Next, a passivity optimization strategy for the inverter, based on the point of common coupling (PCC) voltage feedforward, is proposed to improve the system's stability in the mid-to-high frequency range. Finally, through simulation verification, a comparison between theoretical and simulation results demonstrates the effectiveness of this strategy in enhancing the stability of the interconnected system and suppressing high-frequency oscillations.

title: A Bi-layer Optimization Planning Method of the Regional Integrated Energy System Considering High Reliability and High Exergy
authors: Yang Liu, Jinyu Li, Shuai Zang
publish: 2025 IEEE 9th Conference on Energy Internet and Energy System Integration (EI2)
abstract: In view of the fact that the existing planning models of regional integrated energy systems (RIES) rarely comprehensively consider the reliability of energy supply and the efficiency of energy use, this paper proposes a bi-layer optimization planning method for RIES. Firstly, the scenes are extracted based on the Copula function and the Markov chain-Monte Carlo simulation method, and evaluated by temporal correlation coefficient and autocorrelation coefficient, thereby obtaining the optimal scenes that can highlight the uncertainty, correlation and autocorrelation characteristics of wind and solar output. Then, a bi-layer optimization model of RIES is established, which makes optimization decisions by comprehensively considering economy, reliability and energy use efficiency. Finally, through the simulation of numerical examples, it is demonstrated that the planning method proposed in this paper can achieve a balance in three aspects, verifying its rationality and effectiveness.

title: Peak Shaving Strategy for Long-Process Steel Enterprises Participating in System Regulation Based on Adjustable Boundaries
authors: Xiang Li, Hengyu Liu, Qiang Chen, Yanze Cheng, Su Zhang, Gechen Bian, Xingxu Zhu, Gangui Yan
publish: 2025 IEEE 9th Conference on Energy Internet and Energy System Integration (EI2)
abstract: With the rapid development of renewable energy, the power grid is facing increasing challenges in maintaining the balance between generation and load, while traditional supplyside regulation is no longer sufficient to accommodate the large-scale integration of renewables. Industrial loads — particularly steel plants — possess significant flexibility potential and can contribute to peak shaving and valley filling through demand-side response.This paper proposes a state - task network (STN) model and a self-owned power plant model based on the production processes of steel enterprises to analyze their adjustable operating range when participating in grid scheduling. By optimizing the production scheduling of key processes and coordinating the generation capacity of self-owned power plants, steel plants can effectively provide flexibility support to the power grid. Furthermore, an optimal scheduling strategy is developed to minimize the overall system operating cost while enhancing the flexibility of industrial load adjustment. Case studies verify that the participation of steel enterprises in demand response can significantly reduce system costs, improve renewable energy utilization, and optimize power dispatch. The results demonstrate that flexible industrial loads in steel plants can alleviate grid load pressure and promote the green transition of the power system.

title: Faint Satellite Streak Detection Using the Raw Output of a YOLO-type Object Detector
authors: Attila Ernő Füzes, Zsombor Somai, Lara Taraş-Zinveliu, Răzvan Itu, Vlad Turcu, Radu Dănescu
publish: 2025 IEEE 21st International Conference on Intelligent Computer Communication and Processing (ICCP)
abstract: Space surveillance using optical instruments involves detecting faint streaks from long exposure images. As the space object moves, it creates a line segment trajectory in the image space, against the fixed star background. Clouds, light pollution, or the weak illumination of the object by the Sun may lead to a very small signal to noise ratio. The YOLO family of neural networks is a lightweight solution for detecting various objects from images. Starting from existing streak detection solutions, we have iteratively generated training datasets by a combination of manual and automatic annotation and augmentation. The bounding box output of the neural network detector must be further processed to detect the satellite streak’s line segment. The unfiltered output of the neural network, containing multiple overlapping boxes, can be used to indicate the position of the line, without any additional processing cost. The complete processing pipeline can detect faint streaks from any type of input image, with real time performance.

title: The Impact of Distributed Photovoltaic stations on Transient and Steady-State Zero-Sequence Fault Currents in Peterson Coil Grounded Distribution Networks
authors: Mengru Zhang, Jinrui Tang, Yushan Zhou, Zhenhua Xu, Yunyu Cao, Xianqian Li
publish: 2025 IEEE 9th Conference on Energy Internet and Energy System Integration (EI2)
abstract: To analyze the impact of distributed photovoltaic (PV) stations on the zero-sequence fault current in a distribution network grounded via a Peterson coil (arc suppression coil), this paper establishes a composite sequence network diagram for steady-state single-phase-to-ground faults and a transient equivalent circuit based on the Karrenbauer mode transformation. The theoretical mechanism of PV’s influence on both transient and steady-state zero-sequence currents is derived. The MATLAB/Simulink simulation results for a 10 kV distribution network show that after photovoltaic (PV) integration, the peak value of the fault-phase voltage increases by approximately 0.45%, the steady-state zero-sequence current increases by about 1.7%, while the amplitude of the transient zero-sequence current changes little. Furthermore, PV integration does not affect the relative amplitude and phase of the zero-sequence currents between the faulted and healthy feeders. Therefore, transient-feature-based approaches for fault line selection remain applicable in Petersen-coil grounded system with PV integration.

title: An Explainable Cognitive Bidding Agent for Electricity Markets: A Framework for Zero-Shot Generalization using Large Language Models
authors: Yuheng Cheng, Wenxuan Liu, Jie Huang, Junhua Zhao, Fushuan Wen
publish: 2025 IEEE 9th Conference on Energy Internet and Energy System Integration (EI2)
abstract: In the increasingly complex and uncertain modern electricity markets, traditional bidding models struggle to cope with unstructured information and unforeseen contingencies. Moreover, the "black-box" nature of data-driven methods, such as reinforcement learning, hinders their adoption in critical decision-making contexts that demand high reliability. This paper introduces a novel framework, the Explainable Cognitive Bidding Agent (ECBA), centered around a Large Language Model (LLM). The ECBA pioneers a "Narrative Understanding, Logical Reasoning, and Quantitative Decision" paradigm. This framework not only integrates structured market data with unstructured scenario narratives (e.g., textual descriptions of disturbance events) but also produces human-interpretable and auditable decision logic through a mechanism we term the "Chain-of-Thought Bidding Protocol." The core contributions are threefold: 1) A new paradigm that incorporates the LLM’s narrative understanding capabilities into bidding decisions, effectively addressing the limitations of traditional models in handling unstructured information. 2) A breakthrough in explainability by designing the Chain-of-Thought Bidding Protocol, which transforms the LLM’s decision process from a "black box" to a "white box," significantly enhancing the trustworthiness and suitability of AI agents in critical tasks. 3) Experimental validation of the agent’s zeroshot generalization capability, demonstrating its ability to adapt to novel, unseen disturbances without retraining intelligently. Our results show that the ECBA substantially outperforms traditional baseline strategies in terms of economic performance and loss mitigation, especially in complex disturbance scenarios. It’s transparent and logical decision-making process offers a new solution for the application of advanced AI in critical infrastructure.

title: A Novel Grid-Forming Converter with Complex Decoupling for Enhanced Transient Stability Analysis and Control
authors: Hongyi Zhang, Zaijun Wu
publish: 2025 IEEE 9th Conference on Energy Internet and Energy System Integration (EI2)
abstract: With the increasing penetration of renewable energy, grid-forming converters (GFM) play a crucial role in maintaining system stability. Among various GFM control strategies, the virtual synchronous generator (VSG) has received significant attention due to its capability of emulating the inertia and damping characteristics of synchronous machines. However, conventional VSG suffers from inherent active-reactive power coupling, which degrades dynamic performance and may trigger system oscillations. To address this issue, this paper proposes a complex-variable virtual synchronous generator (CVSG) control strategy. The proposed method introduces a power-decoupling mechanism while preserving the traditional VSG parameter design process, thus achieving effective separation of active and reactive power while maintaining tunable inertia and damping characteristics. Moreover, the CVSG captures the distinct time-scale differences between mechanical and electromagnetic processes, thereby simplifying transient stability analysis and enhancing system robustness. Simulation results on a 15 kVA GFM inverter verify that the proposed method achieves superior dynamic performance and decoupling capability compared with conventional VSG under varying line impedances and voltage disturbances. This work provides a feasible approach for improving the stability and controllability of power systems with high renewable energy penetration.

title: Research on the Parameter Design of the Main Circuit of Grid-forming SVG
authors: Wankai Yang, Guangyao Qiao, Guoliang Zhao, Xiaoming Luan, Yunfei Xu, Huidong Tian
publish: 2025 IEEE 9th Conference on Energy Internet and Energy System Integration (EI2)
abstract: Grid-forming(GFM) converters, which have voltage source characteristics, have been applied in energy storage, renewable energy access systems, and weak systems that require reactive power and voltage support as well as transient active power inertia support. This paper analyzes the electrical parameters design of the main circuit for grid-forming SVG (Static Var Generator). Based on the grid-forming SVG project configured in the Naqu and Ngari regional power grids in Xizang, the project proposed above that the main circuit of grid-forming SVG adopts a direct-hung modular multilevel full-bridge and half-bridge hybrid converter. It is the first time in China that super-capacitors are used as energy storage components. According to the design requirements of the SVG system, the current and voltage parameters of the insulated gate bipolar transistor (IGBT) were proposed. The selection of IGBT devices for the SVG and the number of modules in the modular multilevel converter (MMC) as well as the capacitance values of the modules were determined. Through simulation calculations and field tests, it is verified that the capacity of the main circuit components of the SVG meets the steady-state and transient requirements of the SVG.

title: A Data-Driven Framework for Inertia Requirement Assessment and Its Application in Unit Commitment
authors: Liushuangfei Xie, Tao Ding, Yanhe Li, Jingyu Wan, Wenqian Zhang, Dongning Zhao
publish: 2025 IEEE 9th Conference on Energy Internet and Energy System Integration (EI2)
abstract: The rapid growth of renewable energy significantly reduces system inertia, increasing the risk of frequency instability. While conventional unit commitment (UC) models address this challenge by incorporating frequency security constraints, their nonlinear nature leads to high computational complexity and limits real-time applicability. To address these limitations, this paper proposes a data-driven framework for predicting system minimum inertia requirements. Specifically, a UC model with explicit frequency security constraints is first employed to generate a dataset. From this, a machine learning–based predictor is developed to estimate inertia demand directly from renewable and load forecasts. The predicted requirement is then integrated into UC through a simplified linear constraint, effectively replacing the original nonlinear conditions. To further mitigate the risk of unsafe underestimation, an improved XGBoost model with a modified loss function is proposed. Case studies on the Qinghai power system verify that the proposed approach maintains frequency security, reduces computational time, and provides an efficient tool for secure scheduling under high renewable penetration.

title: Study on Classification and Identification of Power Quality Disturbances Based on Multi dimensional Statistical Representation
authors: Xiaojie Gao, Shouxiang Wang, Dandan Feng, Qianyu Zhao, Tongxun Wang, Luyang Guo, Qianyue Yu, Shuangyin Dai
publish: 2025 IEEE 9th Conference on Energy Internet and Energy System Integration (EI2)
abstract: With the widespread integration of high proportion new energy into the distribution network, massive node data poses challenges to power quality monitoring, and there is an urgent need to develop lightweight feature extraction and efficient classification and identification methods. This article proposes a disturbance classification framework integrating higher-order statistical characterization with attention-enhanced convolutional neural networks (ACNN). The proposed model first extracts variance, skewness, and kurtosis from segmented waveforms, constructing three two-dimensional feature pairs to reduce data volume. Subsequently, a lightweight ACNN architecture incorporating progressive convolutions, channel attention, and adaptive regularization is developed to process the compact inputs and enhance discrimination. Finally, the feasibility and effectiveness of the method are verified through simulation examples.

title: Load Prediction Based on TimesNet and visual Attention Mechanism
authors: Xiaorong Wang, Yile Li, Yan Zhao
publish: 2025 IEEE 9th Conference on Energy Internet and Energy System Integration (EI2)
abstract: Accurate prediction of power load is of great significance to optimize production process, reduce production cost and ensure stable operation of regional power grid.This paper proposes an innovative prediction method that combines the TimesNet architecture with visual attention mechanism for short-term power load prediction. This method improves prediction accuracy by enhancing modeling capabilities at the periodic level, effectively capturing load fluctuation characteristics and their influencing factors. First, a framework based on TimesNet is constructed to achieve multi-period decomposition and parallel reconstruction of time series, accurately capturing fluctuation characteristics within and between periods. Subsequently, a decoding network module based on visual attention mechanism is proposed, which can accurately mine and decode prediction features of load across multiple periodic levels. Verified through real datasets from multiple cities in China, this method demonstrates higher prediction accuracy and stronger generalization ability compared to traditional methods, providing an efficient and intelligent solution for power load prediction.

title: Multimodal Information Interaction Mobile Support Technology for Customer Safety Electricity Use Inspection
authors: Jiwei Gao, Zihao Zhang, Junxu Su, Bowen Zheng, Zishang Xu, Chang Liu
publish: 2025 IEEE 9th Conference on Energy Internet and Energy System Integration (EI2)
abstract: This paper addresses the challenges of insufficient technical support for on-site operations and inefficient hazard identification during electricity safety inspections for power customers by conducting intelligent technology research. This paper proposes a hazard diagnosis method based on multimodal information fusion. By designing an iterative fusion model, it effectively integrates multiple data sources, including equipment status, images, and electrical quantities, improving the comprehensiveness and accuracy of diagnosis. Furthermore, the introduction of augmented reality (AR) technology enables virtual and real-world overlay and remote interaction of on-site information, enhancing the intelligence level of mobile operations. At the technical implementation level, a multimodal information interaction system was developed, and software and hardware debugging and power parameter measurement verification were completed. The results demonstrate that the system's measurement error meets requirements and that the host computer's monitoring and alarm functions are reliable. The research demonstrates that this technical solution effectively improves the efficiency and intelligence level of electricity safety inspections.

title: Multi-Timescale Optimal Scheduling for Hydropower-Photovoltaic Systems Considering Temporal Dynamics of Water Release Rates
authors: Dawei Chen, Qingliang Wang, Biao Li, Lixuan Zhu, Mengshuang Liao, Tianyu Xu, Yonghong Hu
publish: 2025 IEEE 9th Conference on Energy Internet and Energy System Integration (EI2)
abstract: Against the backdrop of energy transition and the rapid development of renewable energy, hydropower stations play a pivotal role in power system operations due to their clean energy generation and regulatory capabilities. This paper proposes a multi-objective optimisation scheduling model for multi-unit cascade hydropower stations, incorporating photovoltaic output to achieve dual optimisation of load following and hydropower output stability. The objective function of the model integrates minimising residual load and minimising the peak to valley difference in hydro output, while further incorporating penalties for water curtailment and unit start-stop costs. This facilitates coordination among power balance, stability, and economic efficiency. Constraints encompass water balance, reservoir capacity limitations, unit start-stop durations, and flow-power coupling. The solution approach employs YALMIP modelling with CPLEX for obtaining optimised dispatch results. Experimental results demonstrate that this model effectively enhances coordination in hydro and solar complementary operation, reduces load fluctuations, and improves renewable energy utilisation efficiency. Compared to the baseline scenario, the optimised dispatch plan employs more rational unit start-stop strategies, reducing water curtailment rates by approximately 67%. Finally, verification across multiple timescales confirms that the optimised hydroelectric output increases by approximately 2.15% and 3.78% at the monthly and annual scales respectively.

title: Hierarchical Electromagnetic Modeling and Validation of Outdoor Dry-Type Hollow Series Reactors from Geometry to System Level
authors: Wenqi Wang, Jiafei Liu, Minglei Xie, Zhiyun Han, Liang Zou, Kejie Huang, Hanwen Ren
publish: 2025 IEEE 9th Conference on Energy Internet and Energy System Integration (EI2)
abstract: For an actual ten-layer triple-enclosed outdoor dry-type series reactor, this paper presents an electromagnetic modeling chain from geometry to system. At the layer scale, the mutual inductance matrix and AC resistance are directly constructed: Mutual inductance is centered on analytical solutions, combining representative turn sampling (equidistant segmentation and array weighting) with intra-segment strip counting. This approach significantly reduces computational load while maintaining energy consistency with the turn-by-turn reference. Symmetrization and semidefinite projection ensure passivity and stability. Resistance side follows IEC 60287, decomposing skin and proximity effects while incorporating shunt and transposition factors to reflect parallel strands and transposition impacts. For scenario-based applications, it exhaustively enumerates short-circuit boundaries layer by layer, generating RL data packages and checklists directly usable for system simulation. For engineering alignment, it provides independent post-processing to uniformly convert DC resistances across all layers to 75°C and complete layer-to-enclosure-to-whole-machine parallel equivalences. Finally, it imports the resulting mutual inductance and resistance matrices to establish a Simulink interturn short-circuit equivalent circuit model. Results demonstrate that this method accurately reconstructs hierarchical inductance and DC resistance in actual devices, supports batch evaluation and rapid simulation for any interturn short-circuit location, and lays the foundation for interturn short-circuit research on dry-type air-core reactors.

title: Transient Stability of Low-Frequency AC Transmission System Considering Phase-Locked Loop
authors: Feifan Wang, Zhe Lv, Xin Qian, Haoyue Xue, Tong Wang, Zengping Wang
publish: 2025 IEEE 9th Conference on Energy Internet and Energy System Integration (EI2)
abstract: This paper begins by analyzing the overall topology of the low-frequency renewable energy collection and transmission system, establishing a dynamic mathematical model of the M3C in the dq rotating coordinate system. A dual-dq decoupling control strategy is designed, employing V/f control on the low-frequency side and average capacitor voltage control of the stator modules on the power-frequency side. Furthermore, an extended nonlinear model incorporating the PLL is developed to analyze the transient synchronization stability of the system. The existence conditions of equilibrium points and stability criteria are derived, systematically revealing the influence of the proportional coefficient and integral coefficient on the equivalent damping and stability region of the system. Finally, a simulation model is built in PSCAD platform. Steady-state and transient simulation results validate the correctness of the theoretical analysis and the effectiveness of the proposed control strategy, demonstrating that appropriate configuration of PLL parameters can significantly enhance the transient synchronization stability of the system.

title: Lightweight and Explainable ResNet-50 Model for Real-Time Pneumonia Diagnosis on Edge Platforms
authors: Lisha Kurain, P Amudha
publish: 2026 Sixth International Conference on Advances in Electrical, Computing, Communications and Sustainable Technologies (ICAECT)
abstract: Pneumonia continues to cause significant respiratory illness, underscoring the importance of timely diagnosis through chest radiographs in clinical decision making. Automated pneumonia detection continues to benefit from recent advancements in deep learning, although high-accuracy model implementation on resource-limited environments remains a challenge due to computation inefficiency and poor model explainability. This research proposes a light explainable pneumonia classification model using a ResNet-50 model optimised for real-time inference on edge devices. The model employs transfer learning to domain-adapt radiograph feature representations and a custom augmentation and inverse class frequency augmentation to address imbalance in the dataset for better model generalisation. The Chest X-Ray dataset was split into training, validation and test sets, and performance was evaluated using precision, recall, F1-score, and AUC. Experimental results show an overall classification accuracy of 93 %, with the pneumonia class attaining a 0.97 recall value and diagnostic sensitivity. To support clinical trust and transparency, Grad-CAM was used to produce visual localisation maps, demonstrating that the model's decisions were based on relevant pathological areas of the lung. The developed framework is deployed in a Raspberry Pi 4B singleboard computer and maintains an appropriate balance between diagnostic accuracy, explainability, and computational sufficiency, allowing possible integration into telemedicine workflows and low-resource clinical environments.

title: Analysis of Low-carbon Economic Characteristics of Multiple Data Centers and Multi-time Scale Flexible Adjustment Potential Assessment
authors: Xiao Cheng, Shiwei Li, Ru Ling, Hui Zhang, Lin Dong, Junjie Hu
publish: 2025 IEEE 9th Conference on Energy Internet and Energy System Integration (EI2)
abstract: As computing power increases, data centers (DCs) consume vast amounts of electricity to meet computational demands, placing significant strain on power supply systems. This process generates substantial indirect greenhouse gas emissions, posing challenges to dual carbon goals. This paper proposes a flexible regulation characteristic evaluation model for DCs within distribution networks (DNs), comprehensively considering the electrical and carbon attributes of multiple DC. First, based on the spatiotemporal flexibility characteristics of multiple DC loads and the economic implications of server startup/shutdown, an electrical performance evaluation model for every DC was established. A carbon performance evaluation model was then developed using a dynamic carbon emission factor model. Subsequently, integrating the response characteristics of single DC equipment, a low-carbon assessment model covering both day-ahead and intraday periods was constructed to validate the potential for load adjustment. Finally, validation through IEEE 33-node network simulations demonstrates that this method effectively reduces DCs operational costs and carbon emissions while significantly enhancing the DNs capacity to accommodate renewable energy.

title: Targeted Demand Response under Stable Pricing and Relative Temperature Conditions: Evidence from Pecan Street Homes
authors: Lin Tan, Yi Kuang, Bin Duan
publish: 2025 IEEE 9th Conference on Energy Internet and Energy System Integration (EI2)
abstract: We study household net load under everyday operating conditions with long-standing, non-notified pricing and relative temperature variation. Using 15-minute data for 25 Pecan Street homes from July 19 to October 31, 2019, we develop an operator-oriented pipeline that combines Difference-in-Differences (ATE), a dynamic Event Study (with explicit pre-trend diagnostics), and a Causal Forest (CATE) to quantify average, temporal, and heterogeneous responses. Price peaks are defined by wholesale-price quantiles to avoid clock confounding, while hot days are defined by relative temperature quantiles to enhance transferability. Under quantile-defined peaks, average price effects are small and statistically unstable, consistent with pre-trend evidence and potential price endogeneity; in contrast, relative hot days yield a clear but short-lived same-day increase in grid imports. Heterogeneity is substantial and interpretable: price, temperature, and household temperature sensitivity dominate, with PV/EV households exhibiting near-zero or slightly negative CATEs and others responding positively. Turning heterogeneity into action, a CATE-targeted design outperforms uniform peak-window tweaks in evening hours, although mild end-of-window rebounds motivate ramping constraints. Overall, rather than relying solely on passive price signals, integrating temperature-driven triggers, heterogeneity-aware targeting, and operational constraints offers a more practical pathway to distribution-level demand response under stable pricing.

title: Multi-Dimensional Risk Prevention and Emergency Management for Hydropower and Pumped Storage Projects: A Framework and Review
authors: Zaiqiang Wu, Kun Hu, Yangwei Dong, Peng Guo, Fei Zhang
publish: 2025 IEEE 9th Conference on Energy Internet and Energy System Integration (EI2)
abstract: The increasing penetration of intermittent renewables forces hydropower and pumped storage units to operate under high-frequency start–stop, multi-head transitions, and superimposed transients, creating nonlinear, compound risk pathways. This paper proposes a cross-level framework that integrates mechanism-based modeling, probabilistic risk assessment, 3D flooding simulation, multiphysics fatigue life prediction, and a digital-twin-enabled emergency management hub. A unified risk–resilience–economics indicator system and an integration architecture are outlined to reconcile model and objective conflicts. Typical scenarios—underground powerhouse flooding, earthquake induced internal fire/flood, and low-cycle fatigue—are analyzed to derive actionable isolation and drainage strategies. Key gaps are identified and prioritized development directions are summarized. The pathway compresses the sense– interpret–decide–act loop, reduces low-frequency high consequence tail risk exposure, and improves functional retention and recovery efficiency for pumped storage and conventional hydropower projects.

title: Exploring Flexibility of Loads in Agricultural Park Integrated Energy System Planning
authors: Yu Su, Zhaoguang Pan, Bin Wang, Haotian Zhao, Yurong Hu, Ning Zhou
publish: 2025 IEEE 9th Conference on Energy Internet and Energy System Integration (EI2)
abstract: Agricultural parks aggregate various producers and processes related to the agriculture sector, and have diverse energy needs including electricity, gas, heat and cold. Integrated energy systems (IESs) can offer a wide range of energy services and have the benefit of higher energy efficiency and cost-effectiveness, which makes it ideal for agricultural parks. However, existing planning methods for IESs did not consider load characteristics in agricultural parks, and can lead to system overdesign and drive up costs. In this paper, an IES planning method considering load flexibility in agricultural parks is presented. First, detailed models for agricultural park loads, sources, and storage systems are introduced. Then, a mixed-integer linear programming (MILP)-based model is proposed for the optimal sizing of sources and storage in the IES.

title: Enhancing the Detection of Minority Attack Classes in Network Intrusion Detection Systems Using Ensemble Deep Learning Approaches
authors: Ch. Kodanda Ramu, T. Srinivasa Rao
publish: 2026 Sixth International Conference on Advances in Electrical, Computing, Communications and Sustainable Technologies (ICAECT)
abstract: Network intrusion detection systems (NIDS) are now integral to protecting contemporary digital infrastructure. However, many widely accepted intrusion detection datasets have substantial class imbalance, which presents real-world challenges for the effective detection of rare and minority attack classes. Traditional machine learning and deep learning models show lower sensitivity to these minority classes, which could also lead to a higher propensity for undetected network breaches due to an increase in false negative rates. This paper introduces an ensemble deep learning framework designed to improve rates of detection for the minority attack classes in network intrusion datasets. The framework implements specialized deep models and is further enhanced through advanced balancing, fusion, and feature selection techniques to maximize detection accuracy and reliability across all attack classes. Systematic experimental evaluation on benchmark datasets shows how ensemble methods improve precision, recall and <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{F 1}$</tex>-score rates for minority classes, compared to single-model baselines. This work demonstrates how ensemble deep learning, with an emphasis on strategies targeting minority classes, can provide practical, generalizable approaches to real-world intrusion detection systems, while reducing the risk of failed detection of sophisticated attacks.

title: Luminescent properties of CdSe/ZnS quantum dots LEDs
authors: Qianju Zheng, Zuyong Feng, Weiren Zhao
publish: 2025 International Conference on Energy, Materials, and Photonics (EMP)
abstract: Although quantum-dot light-emitting diodes (QLEDs) offer narrow-band, high-efficiency emission, stability degradation under continuous operation remains a key challenge. In this work, we focus on enhancing the performance of 3014 package QLEDs by synthesizing CdSe/ZnS core-shell quantum dots (QDs) with a SiO <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$_{2}$</tex> protective shell and combining with UV-curable gel in varying proportions. Through systematic analysis of the electroluminescence (EL) spectra, the optimal quantum dot to UV glue ratio was determined to be 1:6.5. Subsequently, an optimized device emitting light at a wavelength of 543 nm was fabricated and evaluated. Consequently, the optimized devices exhibit exceptional monochromaticity, stability, precise EL control, and significant luminous efficiency. This work presents a effective approach for fabricating and packaging high-performance and stable green QLEDs devices.

title: Research on Subsynchronous Oscillation of Power System Based on Oscillation Energy
authors: Yanqi Hou, Kongming Sun, Yixin Xian, Zheng Xu, Bincai Zhao, Yiping Yu
publish: 2025 IEEE 9th Conference on Energy Internet and Energy System Integration (EI2)
abstract: Subsynchronous oscillation is accompanied by the transformation and transfer of energy, reflecting the essential characteristics of oscillation. This paper conducts a research on the subsynchronous oscillation phenomena of the power system by the method of oscillation energy, and the main achievements are as follows: the oscillation energy characteristics of the subsynchronous oscillation of the wind farm are analyzed. Based on the mathematical model of the wind farm, an energy function construction method for subsynchronous oscillation analysis of wind farms is proposed. Considering the oscillation energy of the wind farm, the virtual potential energy of the wind farm is constructed. In this way, the accuracy of the energy analysis of the subsynchronous oscillation of the wind farm has been improved. The energy characteristics of the subsynchronous oscillation of the wind farm are obtained as well.

title: A Deep Residual Learning Framework with Grad-CAM Explainability for Multi-Class Dermatological Image Classification
authors: Reshma R, Shwetha M K, Raja Thejaswini N
publish: 2026 Sixth International Conference on Advances in Electrical, Computing, Communications and Sustainable Technologies (ICAECT)
abstract: This paper presents a sustainable, AI-powered web platform designed to identify six common dermatological conditions: Acne, Eczema, Melanoma, Hair Loss, Psoriasis, and Normal skin. The proposed framework employs a fine-tuned DermaNet50 convolutional neural network, trained on a dataset comprising <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{5, 5 4 7}$</tex> dermoscopic images for multi-class skin condition classification. Uploaded images are analyzed to provide disease predictions with confidence scores, while Gradient-weighted Class Activation Mapping (Grad-CAM) visualizations highlight the most influential regions contributing to each decision. Additionally, the platform automatically generates a comprehensive PDF report containing patient information, predicted class, confidence metrics, visual explanations, and preliminary care guidance. Experimental evaluation demonstrates that the model achieves an overall accuracy of 89.4%, precision and recall above 89%, and F1-scores exceeding 89% across all classes. This interpretable and accessible system offers a scalable and sustainable solution to assist dermatological assessments, particularly benefiting users in regions with limited access to specialist care. The work aligns with the United Nations Sustainable Development Goal (SDG) 3: Good Health and Well-Being, promoting equitable healthcare access through AI-driven diagnostic support.

title: Key Influencing Factors of Speed Pull-Back Control in Doubly-Fed Variable-Speed Pumped Storage Units under Power Priority Control Strategy
authors: Xiangxuan Dong, Weijia Yang, Qingbo Wang
publish: 2025 IEEE 9th Conference on Energy Internet and Energy System Integration (EI2)
abstract: Doubly-fed variable-speed pumped storage units(DFVSPUs), with their flexible power regulation capability and rapid response to grid demands, are increasingly becoming a core development focus in building new power systems. They hold significant strategic importance for promoting the green energy transition and ensuring the safe and stable operation of power grids. Addressing the potential rotational speed overlimit issue during dynamic response under a power-priority control strategy, this study employs mathematical modeling and dynamic response analysis to design a speed pulling-back control that fully considers safety requirements. By selecting critical operating conditions in which the unit's speed approaches its limits, the study compares the variations in key physical quantities, such as rotational speed and active power during the dynamic response process, both before and after incorporating the speed pulling-back control and under different parameter settings. The results show that the speed pulling-back control effectively reduces the peak rotational speed during active power regulation. Furthermore, as the dead-zone threshold decreases and the proportional coefficient (K<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">p</inf>) and derivative coefficient (K<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">d</inf>) increase, the effectiveness of control in suppressing speed overlimit gradually enhances, but this comes at the cost of increased fluctuations in the unit's active power. The findings indicate that the designed speed pulling-back control is an effective measure to ensure the safe operation of DFVSPUs, and this research can provide technical support for the secure operation of practical engineering cases in China.

title: Disturbance Classification and Hybrid Simulation Analysis for Extreme Events
authors: Yurou Jiang, Kai Jiang, Zhongliang Han, Nian Liu, Jie Huang, Zhen Wang
publish: 2025 IEEE 9th Conference on Energy Internet and Energy System Integration (EI2)
abstract: The rapid growth of renewable energy penetration presents substantial challenges in maintaining supply and demand balance in China’s power spot market, which has emerged as a key mechanism to determine system operation. To reveal this issue, this study reviews representative extreme-event cases and classifies balance disturbances from the perspective of the Cyber–Physical–Social System of Energy (CPSSE). A comprehensive spot market model for joint energy and reserve clearing is established. By incorporating both source and load side characteristics suitable for market-based environments, a set of risk assessment indicators is developed. Numerical simulations are conducted to systematically evaluate the balance risks of conventional market mechanisms under hybrid disturbance scenarios.

title: Newton-Raphson based Power Flow Calculation of Islanded Microgrids Considering the Droop Control of Distributed Generation
authors: Yulei Cao, Jinbo Meng, Cunzhi Shan, Jing Yang
publish: 2025 IEEE 9th Conference on Energy Internet and Energy System Integration (EI2)
abstract: In islanded microgrids, the droop control has been extensively employed in the distributed generators (DGs). As a proportional adjustment mechanism, droop control tends to induce frequency deviation. Besides, the power imbalance present in the system is shared by all DGs in proportion to their droop coefficients, and the slack bus is typically nonexistent in such scenarios. Consequently, conventional power flow calculation methods are not applicable. In this paper, a Newton-Raphson based power flow calculation approach for islanded microgrids is proposed. This method takes into account the droop control of the DGs, as well as the impacts of frequency deviations on the network and loads. Furthermore, there is no need to configure the slack bus in this method. Finally, case studies are conducted on a three-DG system, and simulation experiments are performed to validate the effectiveness of the proposed approach.

title: Study on Cook-off Characteristics of AP/HTPB Composite Solid Propellant
authors: Teng Zhang, Jinsheng Xu, Zongtao Guo, Shuwen Wang, Songlin Pang, Xiong Chen
publish: 2025 3rd Asian Aerospace and Astronautics Conference (AAAC)
abstract: To investigate the cook-off characteristics of AP/HTPB solid propellant under thermal stimuli, a three-dimensional cook-off model of solid propellant was established based on the Universal Cook-off Model (UCM) and the Micro-Mechanical Pressurization (MMP) model. Numerical simulations of the cook-off process were conducted under three different heating rates as thermal loading conditions to explore the cook-off characteristics of the solid propellant. The results indicate that: (1) The heating rate influences the ignition location of the solid propellant during coo k-off; (2) Pressure accelerates the self-heating reaction of the soli d propellant; (3) The internal pressure of the propellant under different heating rates affects the severity of the cook-off response.

title: Experimental study on bearing performance of partially prestressed RPC equal diameter power poles
authors: Xiangsheng Kong, Dehong Wang, Yanzhong Ju
publish: 2025 IEEE 9th Conference on Energy Internet and Energy System Integration (EI2)
abstract: The reactive powder concrete (RPC) equal diameter power pole is characterized by high strength, lightweight, and exceptional durability. It has become a prominent research topic in transmission line engineering. To investigate the effects of reinforcement ratio, partial prestressing, and wall thickness on the bending bearing capacity of RPC equal diameter power poles, a set of six full-size RPC equal diameter pole specimens was fabricated. Bending tests were conducted on these specimens, resulting in the acquisition of load-displacement curves and the ultimate bearing capacity for each specimen. The results showed that the ultimate load of RPC poles increased significantly with the reinforcement ratio, with an increase of 54.59% when the ratio rose from 5.71% to 11.43%. Additionally, the application of partial prestressing led to an increase in the load of the poles at the same displacement, resulting in a 6.01% increase in ultimate load. Furthermore, the increase in wall thickness significantly enhanced the ultimate bearing capacity of the partially prestressed RPC poles; specifically, an increase in wall thickness from 50 mm to 100 mm resulted in a 28.25% increase in ultimate load. These findings indicate that increasing the reinforcement ratio and wall thickness, as well as applying partial prestressing, are effective strategies for enhancing the ultimate bearing capacity of RPC poles. The results hold significant implications for the design and application of partially prestressed RPC equal diameter power poles.

title: Modeling Emotional Trajectories in Storytelling LLMs: Toward Emotionally Coherent AI Narratives for Learning Contexts
authors: Rohit Bharti, Manmohan Sharma
publish: 2026 Sixth International Conference on Advances in Electrical, Computing, Communications and Sustainable Technologies (ICAECT)
abstract: This paper addresses the critical gap in current Large Language Models (LLMs) to generate emotionally coherent narratives for educational applications. While LLMs achieve grammatical fluency, their stories often lack the dynamic emotional arcs vital for learner engagement. We propose a lightweight framework for modeling and embedding emotional trajectories within LLM-generated stories. Our methodology leverages pre-trained emotion classifiers (e.g., Ekman's emotions) to analyze existing narratives and subsequently prompts state-of-the-art LLMs (e.g., GPT-4o, Llama 3) to generate stories that adhere to predefined emotional arcs. We empirically evaluate emotional consistency by comparing baseline LLM generations with emotion-conditioned counterparts using quantitative metrics (e.g., cosine similarity of emotion vectors, Dynamic Time Warping for arc alignment) and an automated evaluation using an LLM-as-Judge. Our findings demonstrate that explicit emotion conditioning significantly improves the emotional coherence and intended pedagogical tone of AI-generated stories, laying the foundational work for emotionally intelligent AI tutors.

title: Resilience Assessment of Power Grids in Extreme Cold Considering Temperature-Failure-Load Coupling
authors: Siqing Sheng, Yuhan Sun, Yucui Wang, Chutong Wang
publish: 2025 IEEE 9th Conference on Energy Internet and Energy System Integration (EI2)
abstract: Extreme cold weather poses serious threats to power systems. Traditional resilience assessment methods often overlook the coupling between dynamic temperature changes and component failure rates, as well as the quantitative analysis of temperature-load interactions. This paper proposes a multi-time-scale resilience assessment framework for power systems under extreme cold conditions. Firstly, an hourly temperature-equipment failure rate correlation model is established, identifying key patterns such as reduced line transmission capacity, transformer insulation aging, and generator efficiency loss. Secondly, a bidirectional temperature-load coupling mechanism quantifies the impact of heating load growth on supply-demand balance. A resilience indicator system, including recovery rate, load loss probability, and graded load shedding, is introduced. Simulations on the IEEE 39-bus system demonstrate that the proposed method more accurately reflects system vulnerability under extreme cold compared to traditional static approaches, improving critical user power supply reliability by ~25%. The framework provides an effective decision-making tool for grid planning and emergency dispatch in extreme cold conditions.

title: Grid Impedance Identification Method Incorporating Grid-Following and Grid-Forming Renewable Energy Integration
authors: Yidian Hao, Hao Liu, Tianshu Bi
publish: 2025 IEEE 9th Conference on Energy Internet and Energy System Integration (EI2)
abstract: Currently, the proportion of renewable energy integration in power systems is increasing. Conducting wideband grid impedance measurement is significant for monitoring grid strength and ensuring the stable operation of the power system. However, existing impedance measurement methods suffer from limited injection signal bandwidth, leading to low robustness, and their measurement accuracy needs improvement. This paper proposes a grid impedance identification method incorporating grid-following and grid-forming renewable energy integration. By analyzing the control process of renewable energy systems containing grid-following and grid-forming converters, the injection point for the MLBS signal is clarified, enabling the accurate transmission of the injected MLBS signal's time-frequency characteristics to the PCC point. An impedance fitting method based on the least squares method is adopted, which improves convergence speed and achieves accurate measurement of grid impedance.

title: Multi-process collaborative optimization and regulation of steel enterprises considering equipment response characteristics
authors: Gechen Bian, Haifeng Li, Yidong Zhu, Li Yan, Jiguang Xue, Wenwen Li, Junhui Li, Cuiping Li
publish: 2025 IEEE 9th Conference on Energy Internet and Energy System Integration (EI2)
abstract: To achieve energy conservation, consumption reduction, and efficiency improvement in the production processes of steel enterprises, this paper investigates a coordinated regulation mechanism to address issues such as mismatched dynamic response characteristics among internal equipment units and poor coordination of control commands. First, the dynamic response characteristics of typical equipment in steel enterprises are analyzed, and their mathematical models are established. Based on this, a hierarchical and efficient control command transmission and collaboration mechanism is designed, aiming to solve the problems of delay and conflict in command transmission between processes. Finally, simulation analysis is conducted through specific production cases. The results show that the proposed coordinated regulation mechanism can significantly improve the system's coordination and stability, reduce the total production cost under typical working conditions, and effectively validate the practical value of this mechanism in enhancing the economic benefits of enterprises.

title: Editorial IEEE Transactions on Technology and Society Achieves Major Indexing Milestone
authors: Katina Michael, George Roussos, Jordan Richard Schoenherr
publish: IEEE Transactions on Technology and Society

title: Game-Theory-Based Optimisation Scheduling Scheme for Virtual Power Plants: Joint Trading of Electricity, Carbon Emission Rights and Green Credits
authors: Hongyang Jin, Jingyu Tang, Xiangluan Dong, Wei Wang, Yucai Li, Yuzhou Duan
publish: 2025 IEEE 9th Conference on Energy Internet and Energy System Integration (EI2)
abstract: As numerous distributed energy sources participate in electricity trading markets, carbon trading markets, and green certificate trading markets through the aggregation mechanism of virtual power plants, the coordinated optimization scheduling of multiple virtual power plants engaging in joint markets has become a pressing focus. Consequently, this study conducts an in-depth exploration of multi-market joint operation mechanisms, designing a ‘electricity-carbon-green’ integrated trading market framework. Building upon this framework, a multi-virtual power plant optimization dispatch model based on dynamic evolutionary games is proposed. A computational example is constructed to simulate and analyses the coordinated dispatch and cooperative evolutionary strategies among multiple virtual power plants, whilst comparing how their cooperative patterns evolve under different carbon quota assignments. Simulation results fully validate the model's effectiveness, demonstrating that the proposed evolutionary strategy effectively balances conflicts of interest among multiple VPPs while enhancing their willingness to participate in market transactions.

title: Numerical Study on Heat Transfer Performance of Reversed Spiral Fins
authors: Yan Zhou, Uglanov Dmitry A, Junjie Liu
publish: 2025 3rd Asian Aerospace and Astronautics Conference (AAAC)
abstract: In this study, a new type of reversed spiral fin heat exchanger (RSFHE) was numerically simulated. Through computational fluid dynamics (CFD) simulation, the changes of the Fanning friction factor f and the Kolben factor j of the RSFHE with the change of the fin spacing were studied. And its total heat transfer capacity was compared with that of the wavy fin. The results show that the RSFHE has a greater advantage over the wavy fin. When the Reynolds number is 1000, the total heat transfer factor is 48% higher than that of the wavy fin with similar structural dimensions at the same Reynolds number. As the Reynolds number increases, the difference in the total heat transfer factor of the two fins decreases, but it is still 13.3% higher at a Reynolds number of 5000. Reducing the fin spacing of the RSFHE will cause its f factor to decrease first and then increase, while the j factor will not change much. Therefore, when other geometric parameters remain unchanged, choosing a suitable fin spacing can achieve the best performance of the RSFHE.

title: Similarity-Based Evaluation of Individual Electric Heating Regulation Capability
authors: Xiali Liu, Kai Li, Kai Yun, Qiaoli Wang, Simayi Sholati, Qingqing Ren
publish: 2025 IEEE 9th Conference on Energy Internet and Energy System Integration (EI2)
abstract: To address issues such as unknown outdoor temperature and complex solution in the R-C model for evaluating the regulation capability of distributed electric heating, this paper proposes a similarity-based PSO-LSSVM (Particle Swarm Optimization-Least Squares Support Vector Machine) evaluation method. First, the method selects similar days and calculates local similarity by integrating meteorological factors, then optimizes LSSVM parameters using PSO to construct the model.
