The mobility of these devices leads to frequent changes in their topology and data traffic. This causes a challenge in detecting the critical nodes for maintaining security. These critical nodes change dynamically with the changes in topology, mobility of the devices, and also based on the type of traffic that passes through them. There have been recent developments in determining the critical nodes of dynamic networks based on security and dynamic factors by enforcing weights for various related metrics. In this paper, we optimize the execution time of the securitycentric method by modifying the centrality measures and by applying the Genetic Algorithm. Analysis of the proposed method over simulations of different networks shows that it is as efficient as the existing method in detecting the critical nodes but in less time, and is suitable for dynamic complex networks with a large number of nodes.

title: Hidden Fault Diagnosis Technology for Relay Protection Based on Multi-Device Correlation Analysis
authors: Dan Liu, Liying Yu, Lingrao Wang, Shenglin Xu, Peng Dong, Hongzhi Su, Yanfei Li, Peng Guo
publish: 2025 IEEE 9th Conference on Energy Internet and Energy System Integration (EI2)
abstract: With the increasing complexity of power systems, the timely detection of hidden faults in relay protection has become crucial for ensuring the safe and stable operation of power grids. This paper proposes a hidden fault diagnosis technology for relay protection based on multi-device correlation analysis. By extracting device correlation information and employing a point-by-point error integration method for waveforms, the operational risks in voltage and current sampling links are accurately identified. Firstly, the cubic spline interpolation algorithm is used to unify waveform data with different sampling frequencies. Secondly, potential faults are detected by extracting fault moments and calculating waveform deviations. Finally, case studies demonstrate the effectiveness of this technology in diagnosing hidden faults such as inconsistent current comparisons in transformer protection and multiple grounding points in PT circuits. Experimental results show that this method significantly improves the operational reliability of relay protection devices, providing strong support for power system safety.

title: Short-Term Power Load Forecasting Based on Hybrid Dilated Convolutional Neural Network
authors: Bingchu Jin, Zesheng Hu, Yawei Zhao, Chao Xue, Chao Zhao, Jinran Song, Jingyu Zhang, Yu Yang
publish: 2025 IEEE 9th Conference on Energy Internet and Energy System Integration (EI2)
abstract: Addressing the challenge of short-term power load forecasting, this paper proposes a Hybrid Dilated Convolution Neural Network (HDCNN)-based forecasting method. First, the power load time series is converted into a Gramian Angular Field (GAF) representation through coordinate transformation and Gramian matrix computation, this transformation extracts image-based features of the power load. Subsequently, these GAF features are input into the HDCNN-based forecasting model. Compared to traditional time-series load features, the GAF representation captures more fine-grained characteristics of the load data. The proposed HDCNN achieves a Mean Absolute Percentage Error (MAPE) of 3.26%, significantly outperforming traditional CNN and DCNN.

title: Evaluation of Photovoltaic Hosting Capacity Considering Source-Network-Load Coordination
authors: Cuiping Li, Wenwen Qin, Junhui Li, Chen Jia
publish: 2025 IEEE 9th Conference on Energy Internet and Energy System Integration (EI2)
abstract: With the high-penetration integration of distributed Photovoltaic (PV) in distribution grids, the operational characteristics of traditional power systems have undergone significant changes. The intermittent and stochastic nature of PV generation imposes greater challenges on grid security and dispatch flexibility. This paper proposes a systematic evaluation framework for assessing the PV hosting capacity of autonomous grids based on the source-grid-load coordination concept. The framework features a multi-level indicator system covering three criteria safety, reliability, and adaptability and nine key metrics that account for source-side generation capacity, grid-side power flow uniformity, and load-side supply reliability. A hybrid weighting method combining the Analytic Hierarchy Process (AHP) and the Entropy Weight Method (EWM) is employed for indicator standardization and dynamic weight determination. Case studies on the NL County autonomous grid demonstrate that the PV hosting capacity exhibits a "rise-fall" trend with increasing penetration, and the LS grid reaching its optimal value at around 40% PV penetration.

title: Optimized Attitude Maneuver Planning for Spacecraft with 2-DOF Turntables
authors: Zhe Zhu, Rui Xu, Zhaoyu Li, Shengying Zhu
publish: 2025 3rd Asian Aerospace and Astronautics Conference (AAAC)
abstract: Compared with rigid three-axis spacecraft, spacecraft with movable mechanisms is more efficient in attitude maneuver missions. This paper investigates the application of an optimization method for attitude trajectory planning of a spacecraft with dual two-degree-of-freedom turntables. A decoupled strategy is employed to decompose the desired pointing direction into the body quaternion and the turntable joint angles. Energy consumption and trajectory smoothness objective functions are designed while satisfying pointing constraints. Using the axle-fixed rotation of the quaternion sequence as a design variable, an optimization algorithm based on differential evolution is employed for attitude trajectory optimization. The optimal solutions for the body attitude and joint angles are parameterized using cubic spline functions, and the complete trajectory of the system states is solved. Simulations of attitude trajectory planning for spacecraft with dual two-degree-of-freedom turntables verify the effectiveness of proposed method.

title: The Technical Feasibility Study on the Participation of Electric Vehicle Charging Facilities in Carbon Quota Trading
authors: Xinxin Lu, Rong Li, Xiang Gao, Xiaomin Shi
publish: 2025 IEEE 9th Conference on Energy Internet and Energy System Integration (EI2)
abstract: With the continuous development of electric vehicles, the development of electric vehicles has become China 's implementation of the national energy strategy. The connection between its charging facilities and the carbon trading market is increasingly close. At the same time, carbon trading, as an effective carbon emission reduction measure, has attracted more and more attention from all over the world. The participation of electric vehicles in carbon trading and the expansion of profit channels for charging facilities operators have also received much attention. This paper mainly introduces the construction status of electric vehicle charging facilities and its economic benefits in the direction of regional development, investment subject, business model and profit model, reflecting its importance and development scale. By showing the relevant data of electric vehicle charging facilities participating in carbon market transactions, it can not only promote the participation of electric vehicles in carbon trading, but also expand the profit channels of charging facilities operators. At the same time, this paper predicts the charging infrastructure by calculating the carbon emissions in the construction stage and operation stage of the whole life cycle of electric vehicle charging facilities, and provides the necessary data support for its feasibility, which highlights the necessity of charging facilities participating in carbon trading. This paper is of great significance for enriching the carbon trading market, accelerating regional development, improving the income level of investors, improving the business model and promoting the rapid development of charging facilities, thus promoting the development of electric vehicles and achieving carbon emission reduction targets.

title: Analysis of Modulation Characteristics of Quantum Dot Light-Emitting Diodes Based on Fine Optimization of Equivalent Circuits
authors: Can Zhou, Xiangtian Xiao
publish: 2025 International Conference on Energy, Materials, and Photonics (EMP)
abstract: The equivalent circuit of quantum dot lightemitting diodes (QLEDs) can simulate their transient electroluminescent (TREL) characteristics, making it crucial for analyzing the modulation bandwidth of QLEDs. Traditional QLED equivalent circuits only consider the electrical processes of the device and lack analysis of the electro-optical conversion process of exciton recombination luminescence. As a result, bandwidth models based on traditional equivalent circuits cannot accurately predict the actual bandwidth of the device. In this study, the QLEDs equivalent circuit was optimized by modeling the electro-optical conversion process as a low-pass filter, which was then incorporated into the equivalent circuit model, achieving an electro-optical coupled modulation bandwidth model. The optimized equivalent circuit can effectively fit the TREL and frequency response of QLEDs, laying a solid foundation for analyzing the modulation bandwidth characteristics of the device.

title: An improved Grey-TOPSIS method for power quality risk assessment based on the subjective and objective combined weighting method
authors: Meng Zha, Wei Zhang, Ziyan Jin, Xiaohui Yang, Qijin Hu, Shi Yu, Bin Li
publish: 2025 IEEE 9th Conference on Energy Internet and Energy System Integration (EI2)
abstract: To address the challenges posed by the randomness and volatility of distributed power sources to the safe and stable operation of power grids, this paper comprehensively considers power quality state factors such as voltage, frequency, and waveform, as well as multi-dimensional influencing factors including power supply reliability and serviceability, to construct a comprehensive power quality evaluation index system. In terms of index weight calculation, a combined weighting method is employed, integrating the subjective and objective weights of the Apriori-ANP method and the improved CRITIC method to enhance the scientificity and accuracy of the evaluation. Meanwhile, to address the shortcomings of existing evaluation methods in power quality risk prediction, this paper establishes an improved Grey-TOPSIS evaluation model. It incorporates the fusion of grey relational grade and Euclidean distance, and integrates the power quality grade matrix into the comprehensive evaluation matrix, achieving an objective graded quantitative evaluation of power quality. Finally, through case analysis, it is verified that this method can obtain more accurate and detailed power quality graded evaluation results, providing an important basis for ensuring the quality of electricity consumption for users and guiding power quality management in power grids.

title: Mathematical Modelling and Implementation of Traffic Flow Simulation Using Cellular Automata
authors: M.Siva Mala, B Uppalaiah, Syed Omar Ballari, S. Vijay Peter, Naveen Malik, R Saravanakumar
publish: 2026 Sixth International Conference on Advances in Electrical, Computing, Communications and Sustainable Technologies (ICAECT)
abstract: In most cities, traffic jam leads to delays, high consumption of fuel and pollution to the environment. The classical traditional means of traffic modeling do not succinctly address complicated and diverse traffic scenarios. This study examines methods that can be used to simulate and test the traffic flow in various conditions. It applies Cellular Automata (CA) model. The vehicles are modeled as discrete point objects in a grid, which move based on density-related rules. The model is effective in the capturing of smooth flow, slow oscillations and stop and go waves. The results of simulation indicate that the waiting times between free flow and congestion conditions increase, which proves the reality of the model. Results indicate that the CA-based framework can be used in traffic simulation and can also be used to aid traffic management and that future innovations need real-time information and the behavioral elements.

title: A Unified Deep Learning Approach for Accurate Estimation of Power System Stabilizer Parameters in SMIB Networks
authors: Shashank Kumar, Mangal Singh
publish: 2026 Sixth International Conference on Advances in Electrical, Computing, Communications and Sustainable Technologies (ICAECT)
abstract: This paper analyzes the performance of four deep learning models -Feedforward Neural Network (FNN), Long Short-Term Memory (LSTM), Convolutional Neural Network (CNN), and Transformer to predict the Power System Stabilizer (PSS) parameters K<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">ss</inf> and T<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">a</inf> of a Single machine Infinite Bus (SMIB) system. Training of the models to capture the nonlinear dynamic aspect involved in optimal stabilizer parameter tuning is done using a dataset created by SMIB-PSS simulations. Scatter plots, residual distributions, convergence properties and quantitative measures such as Mean Squared Error (MSE), Mean Absolute Error (MAE), Root Mean Squared Error (RMSE) and coefficient of determination (R<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup>) are used to evaluate model performance. As per the results of experiments, the LSTM architecture has the greatest predictive accuracy and generalization ability with the lowest error rates and the highest correlation coefficients in comparison with all the models. The FNN and CNN models demonstrate moderate results with signs of mild overfitting, and the Transformer demonstrates an effective training convergence with lower sustainability in validation. The additional evidence of the high robustness of the LSTM model in acquiring the underlying system dynamics is supported by radar plots and cumulative distribution function (CDF) analyses. Altogether, the LSTM architecture can be recognized as the most efficient one to use to get the right estimation of PSS parameters in SMIB systems, thus to provide better controller tuning and power system stability.

title: Active Worst-Case Scenario Guided Physics-Informed Diffusion Modeling for SVSR Characterization in DC Systems
authors: Jinwen Sun, Jianhua Li, Yi Liu, Lu Cao, Haibao Zhai, Zhiguang Huang, Yanqiang Shi, Jiachen Liu, Wei Luo, Yijing Zhang, Minchang Zhang, Jianzhe Liu
publish: 2025 IEEE 9th Conference on Energy Internet and Energy System Integration (EI2)
abstract: Due to the reduced AC–DC conversion stages, DC systems are increasingly adopted in large-scale renewable integration and islanded scenarios with high security requirements. However, these systems often exhibit pronounced source–load uncertainties, resulting in low stability margins. To quantify such issues, this paper focuses on the characterization of the Static Voltage Stability Region (SVSR), defined as the set of power injection profiles where the power flow equations remain solvable. Existing analytical methods provide explicit SVSR approximations but are typically conservative, while numerical methods require extensive sampling and simulations, leading to high computational cost. To close this gap, this paper develops an active worst-case scenario guided physics-informed diffusion modeling approach for SVSR characterization in DC systems. We first propose an active worst-case scenario recommendation method to identify critical operating conditions near the stability boundary. A diffusion-based generative model is then introduced to generalize and densify the critical scenarios, forming a flexible modeling platform for SVSR boundary exploration. Additionally, physics-informed constraints are embedded into the diffusion process to ensure a theoretically guaranteed SVSR characterization. By actively generating critical worst-case scenarios near the stability boundary, the proposed approach efficiently refines the SVSR with reduced simulation effort. Simulation case studies verify the validity of the proposed work.

title: Ethical Implications and Emerging Opportunities of Machine Learning Models in Automated English Language Testing
authors: Bhanu Rekha, Banti Dutta, Shveta Tripathi, Joy Christina Sara Jayakodi, Viju M. J., Sreela B
publish: 2026 Sixth International Conference on Advances in Electrical, Computing, Communications and Sustainable Technologies (ICAECT)
abstract: Automated English language testing is becoming a prevalent trend as the educational institutions in need of scalable, objective, and effective assessment systems. Nevertheless, the models currently employed tend to lack reliability, transparency, as well as fairness, which restricts their use on high-stakes testing. The purpose of this work is to create a powerful machine learning based on the English proficiency assessment system, which combines linguistic characteristics with contextual embeddings to increase the level of accuracy and decrease bias. The novelty of the work is a combination of the BERT-based deep language representations with the ethical protection against the unfairness of fair automated scoring. The suggested framework was found to be 98% accurate, which is much better than other old methods like e-rater and AES models. Comparative analysis showed that there was an improvement in error management, similarity in varied inputs and decreased variation in prediction results. These findings underscore the capability of a developed NLP models in standard test. Finally, the work can add to the literature a scalable and ethically matched framework that will provide accuracy, fairness, and practicality in English language assessment.

title: Predictive Velocity Obstacle Method for UAVs in Low-altitude Urban Environments
authors: Yanxia Liang, Huan Wang, Xin Liu, Xiaofan Zhao, Yinghui Ye, Yawen Zheng
publish: 2025 3rd Asian Aerospace and Astronautics Conference (AAAC)
abstract: Unmanned Aerial Vehicles (UAVs), as the core nodes of Low-altitude Internet of Things (LIoT), play a pivotal role. Ensuring the UAVs’ safety and efficiency, autonomous obstacle avoidance has been a critical research focus. Velocity Obstacle(VO) method with its revised versions which are commonly used obstacle avoidance methods for UAVs overlook the non-linear motion of obstacles, leading to increased potential collision risks. To address this limitation, we propose a Predictive Velocity Obstacle method(PVO). First, the CTRV (Constant Turn Rate and Velocity) model is introduced for obstacle state prediction. Second, predictive collision avoidance mechanism is constructed by extending the velocity obstacle region. Simulation results demonstrate that in the three common obstacle avoidance situations which are overtaking, crossing, and head-on encounter, the maneuver time decreased about 48.77%. And the cumulative angle change drops nearly 47.11%. Furthermore, the PVO halves the avoidance maneuver counts in the first two scenarios.

title: Algorithmic Chain Encryption (ACE): Enhancing Cryptographic Security through Dynamic Sequential Multi-Algorithm Application
authors: Kartik Aslia, Navjeet Kaur, Rajesh Kumar
publish: 2026 Sixth International Conference on Advances in Electrical, Computing, Communications and Sustainable Technologies (ICAECT)
abstract: The rapid advancement of quantum computing technology makes protecting sensitive information an absolute necessity. Symmetric encryption algorithms remain strong, but their security decreases dramatically when a stolen key or a quantum attack occurs. Using a single encryption algorithm creates substantial security vulnerabilities because attackers can execute brute-force attacks, known-plaintext attacks, and quantum-based attacks. The loss or theft of encryption keys results in severe data exposure, demonstrating the requirement for advanced cryptographic systems to defend against such threats. This research introduces Algorithmic Chain Encryption (ACE) as a dynamic multi-layer symmetric encryption system. The encryption process of ACE uses multiple symmetric encryption algorithms in sequence through separate keys for each stage. The encryption process becomes more secure through two mechanisms, i.e., the system selects algorithms dynamically, and the system encrypts the sequence of algorithms used. The evaluation results show that ACE provides better security through additional computational requirements. The time required for encryption grows directly with the number of algorithms in the chain. The encryption time of 1 KB data requires approximately <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$35 \mu ~\mathrm{s}$</tex> when using two algorithms, but increases to <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$60 \mu ~\mathrm{s}$</tex> with three algorithms and reaches <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$85 \mu ~\mathrm{s}$</tex> with four algorithms, while a single AES128 encryption requires <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$15 \mu ~\mathrm{s}$</tex>. The decryption times follow the same pattern, which supports ACE as a viable solution for highsecurity applications.

title: Research on Discrete Phase-Shift Control for Isolated AC-DC Matrix Converters
authors: Jiajun Du, Wenlang Deng, Jiajun Ren, Lingke Gao
publish: 2025 IEEE 9th Conference on Energy Internet and Energy System Integration (EI2)
abstract: The Isolated AC-DC Matrix Converter (IAMC) features bidirectional power transmission capabilities and serves as a critical device in new energy power conversion systems. However, traditional control strategies exhibit dynamic response delays during rapid power changes and generate excessively high inductor current stresses, compromising system operational safety. This paper proposes integrating Multi-Level Discrete Extended Phase-Shifting (MD-EPS) control with enhanced Stable Vector Pulse Width Modulation (SVPWM) for IAMC regulation. The mathematical relationship between IAMC transmitted power and inductor current is derived, with control parameters designed using a four-level discrete extended phase-shifting (4LD-EPS) approach. Simulation results demonstrate that the proposed strategy effectively enhances IAMC dynamic response, reduces inductor current stress, and ensures stable output voltage.

title: Chronic Obstructive Pulmonary Disease Detection Using a Smart Stethoscope Prototype
authors: Letitia Nimshi, P. Manimegalai
publish: 2026 Sixth International Conference on Advances in Electrical, Computing, Communications and Sustainable Technologies (ICAECT)
abstract: The progressive respiratory disorder known as Chronic Obstructive Pulmonary Disease (COPD) is typified by abnormal lung sounds including crackles and wheezes as well as restricted airflow. Better patient outcomes and prompt intervention can result from early diagnosis via non-invasive auscultation. This study describes the creation of a Smart Stethoscope for COPD detection that records lung sounds by combining an electret condenser microphone with a conventional stethoscope chest piece. LabVIEW is used to view and analyse the recorded signals in real-time, enabling frequency-domain analysis and waveform visualization to differentiate between normal and abnormal breath sounds. Bandpass filtering (20-1000 Hz) is applied to signals in order to separate out pertinent auditory characteristics linked to COPD. This study uses a publicly available lung sound dataset from Kaggle to investigate the usage of machine learning techniques in addition to hardware-based signal capture for respiratory condition classification. Records from patients suffering from bronchitis, pneumonia, COPD, and other respiratory conditions are included in the dataset. To categorize these conditions using Mel Frequency Cepstral Coefficients-extracted features from audio signals, a hybrid Convolutional Neural Network-Gated Recurrent Unit model was used. The findings show that normal and abnormal breathing patterns may be successfully identified, with filtering overcoming obstacles such as low signal amplitude and background noise. For real-time, AI-driven COPD detection in clinical and remote situations, future research attempts involve incorporating the machine learning model into the Smart Stethoscope.

title: Quantitative Evaluation Process for Reliability Optimization Design and Weak points Analysis of Reusable Spacecraft Systems
authors: Wenming Zhou
publish: 2025 3rd Asian Aerospace and Astronautics Conference (AAAC)
abstract: This paper analyzes the essential distinctions and connections between reusable and disposable spacecrafts. It outlines the typical characteristics of reusable spacecraft system, elaborates the mathematical foundation and probability assessment methods for system average mission reliability. The study proposes the concepts and computational formulas for average mission reliability for products with exponential and weibull failure distribution. Given the challenges in reliability assessment of reusable spacecraft system, the research constructs a dynamic quantitative assessment and weak points identification process. The methodologies and formulas provided can be used to the reliability dynamic assessment of reusable spacecraft system.

title: CTF-PWN100: Automated Binary Exploitation of CTF Challenges Leveraging Large Language Models
authors: Tudor Cristea, Radu-Marian Portase, Adrian Coleşa
publish: 2025 IEEE 21st International Conference on Intelligent Computer Communication and Processing (ICCP)
abstract: In this paper, we introduce CTF-PWN100, a dataset and automated framework designed to evaluate the performance of large language models (LLMs) in solving Capture-The-Flag (CTF) binary exploitation challenges. We specifically assess the capabilities of DeepSeek-R1, Gemini-2.5-Pro (Preview), and Gemini-2.5-Flash (Preview) on a balanced and diverse dataset consisting of 100 binary exploitation problems covering multiple vulnerability types, including Buffer Overflow, Format String, Heap Overflow, Use-After-Free, and Miscellaneous. Using an automated evaluation framework integrating decompilation, code filtering, and dynamic feedback from Python-generated exploit scripts, the three models achieved success rates of ${5 1 \%}, {6 6 \%}$, and $46 \%$, respectively. The results indicate that reasoning-oriented LLMs demonstrate promising potential in addressing complex cybersecurity tasks. However, limitations persist regarding vulnerability identification and the development of sophisticated exploit scripts.

title: A Self-Adaptive Physics-Constrained Graph Attention Network for Customer-Transformer Topology Identification in Low-Voltage Distribution Grids
authors: Min Zhou, Wenkai Zhao, Yun Chen, Chen Wu, Zhiwen Ge, Dong Han
publish: 2025 IEEE 9th Conference on Energy Internet and Energy System Integration (EI2)
abstract: Precise customer-transformer topological relationships are critical for the safe operation and management of a low-voltage distribution grid (LVDG). This paper proposes a customer-transformer topological relationship identification method for LVDG using self-adaptive physics-constrained graph attention network (SA-PCGAT). First, considering voltage data misalignment in time series, dynamic time warping (DTW) is used for preprocessing. This aligns the raw voltage data to precisely measure fluctuation similarities. Second, the DTW is used as a similarity measure to cluster customer voltage sequences, thereby preliminarily identifying the customer-transformer topological relationships. Then, a SA-PCGAT is introduced to leverage a multi-head attention mechanism to extract nodal electrical features. Its novel adaptive coordination mechanism dynamically balances various physical constraints, achieving refined precision in the topological relationships. Finally, case studies demonstrate that the customer-transformer topological map generated by the proposed method achieves an approximate 10% performance improvement over standard DTW-based clustering methods (with an accuracy of 80.5%). This result validates the precision of the proposed method, highlighting its practical value and potential for widespread application.

title: Optimization and Application of a Thermal-Electric Collaborative Management System for Polar Icebreakers
authors: Boyang Huang, Rong Huang, Dou Yinke, Yuchen Wang, Bin Wang, Zhengxiang Xu, Haoqin Wang, Yizhuo Zhang
publish: 2025 IEEE 9th Conference on Energy Internet and Energy System Integration (EI2)
abstract: During Antarctic scientific expeditions, it is essential to ensure a stable supply of fresh water on board the polar icebreaking vessel when it docks at Zhongshan Station. The seawater desalination system and its associated batteries must operate within a temperature range of 2°C to 10°C, otherwise their performance will significantly decline. To address the issues of thermal load fluctuations and energy instability in low-temperature environments, this paper proposes a thermoelectric collaborative energy management system centered around thermal control of the desalination system. Based on meteorological data from Zhongshan Station, typical meteorological weeks are extracted using K-means clustering to establish a thermal management model for the desalination system, and a multi-source energy allocation strategy prioritizing thermal management is formulated. The system integrates wind energy, solar energy, diesel generators, and battery energy storage, achieving precise temperature control and energy efficiency optimization through dynamic scheduling. An improved particle swarm optimization algorithm is employed to perform multi-objective optimization of wind turbine, photovoltaic, and battery capacities, balancing temperature stability and fuel economy. Simulations indicate that the system can control temperature fluctuations within the desalination system within ±2°C, reduce diesel consumption by 18%-25%, and optimize energy storage configuration. This study provides a practical reference for thermal management and energy optimization of seawater desalination in polar icebreaking vessels operating in extreme environments.

title: Optimization Strategy of Voltage Stress and Input Current Distortion of Three-Phase Six-Switch Buck-Type Rectifier
authors: Jingyi Zhao, Ping’An Tan, Rui Tang, Huiling Wang
publish: 2025 IEEE 9th Conference on Energy Internet and Energy System Integration (EI2)
abstract: The Three-Phase Six-Switch Buck-Type Rectifier, despite its potential in applications like new energy vehicles and energy storage, suffers from two bottlenecks in its traditional topology: high line voltage stress on switches and input current distortion during sector switching. To address these issues, we propose a two-fold optimization: a modified circuit topology with revised SVPWM signals clamps the switch voltage stress to the phase voltage, and a reordered vector distribution method eliminates sector-transition current distortion. To verify effectiveness, a PSIM simulation model and an experimental prototype were built. The research shows the proposed methods effectively solve the traditional rectifier’s core problems, providing technical support for its low-cost, high-reliability applications.

title: Energy storage planning for the joint construction and sharing of new energy clusters considering the dynamic spatiotemporal correlation
authors: Chu Jin, Wenli Wu, Xiaoyi Duan, Tingke He, Shichang Cui, Wei Xu, Yutong Wang
publish: 2025 IEEE 9th Conference on Energy Internet and Energy System Integration (EI2)
abstract: Recent years have seen a sharp rise in the installed capacity of wind, photovoltaic and other renewable energy sources, leading to significant output fluctuations that pose unprecedented challenges to power system peak shaving and frequency regulation. This paper focuses on shared energy storage planning for new energy clusters. First, a new energy cluster output sequence model based on dynamic spatio-temporal correlation is constructed to simulate the random fluctuations of wind-solar power generation. Then, a co-construction and sharing operation mechanism for cluster energy storage is proposed: efficient resource utilization is achieved through inter-station coordination and centralized energy storage management, while the interest balance and cooperation enthusiasm of all parties are guaranteed via capacity weight allocation and Nash bargaining-based revenue distribution. With the goal of minimizing investment and operation costs, relevant constraints such as energy storage configuration and power balance are formulated to ensure model rationality. Results show that compared with single-station independent energy storage and traditional planning without considering correlations, the proposed strategy reduces the cluster’s total cost while lowering the average daily comprehensive cost and maintaining each station’s revenue. It provides theoretical and practical support for efficient planning and optimal operation of new energy cluster energy storage.

title: Effect of Composite Particles (K-BNNSs) on the Surface Charge Transport Characteristics of Aramid Paper
authors: Qikun Cheng, Cong Wang, Rongxin Sun
publish: 2025 IEEE 9th Conference on Energy Internet and Energy System Integration (EI2)
abstract: Aramid paper, owing to its excellent electrical and thermal properties, is widely employed as the primary insulation material in high-frequency transformers. However, prolonged exposure to repetitive high-frequency electrical stress results in surface charge accumulation, increased charge density, and a heightened risk of insulation failure. Nanomodification has been recognized as an effective approach to enhance interfacial charge dissipation in composite insulation systems.In this work, composite aramid papers containing 0%, 5%, 8%, 10%, and 13% K-BNNSs by mass fraction were fabricated, and their surface potential decay and trap distribution characteristics under positive and negative pulse voltages were systematically investigated. The results demonstrate that the incorporation of K-BNNSs significantly modifies the surface potential decay rate, which reaches its minimum at a 10% nanoparticle mass fraction. Furthermore, the nanoparticles introduce additional hole and electron traps, increasing both deep trap energy levels and trap densities, with peak values observed at 10% loading. This study elucidates the regulatory role of nanofillers in the charge transport process of composite aramid paper and provides theoretical insights and technical guidance for improving the insulation reliability of high-frequency transformer systems.

title: Enhancing Credit Card Fraud Detection Through Principal Component Analysis and Machine Learning Models
authors: Jyoti Singh
publish: 2026 Sixth International Conference on Advances in Electrical, Computing, Communications and Sustainable Technologies (ICAECT)
abstract: We investigate the effect of Principal Component Analysis (PCA) as a dimensionality reduction step for creditcard fraud detection on a publicly available dataset with 284,807 transactions (0.172 % fraud). We systematically compare logistic regression and a feedforward neural network trained on (a) scaled raw features and (b) PCA-transformed features. PCA (fit on the training folds only) reduced dimensionality to 17 components and retained 95.7 % of variance. On the held-out test set, PCA + Logistic Regression achieved a test precision of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{9 6 \%}$</tex>, recall of <insert>%, and an F1-score of 97% (report mean ± std over 5 runs). The neural network achieved a test accuracy of 93% and complementary precision/recall metrics reported in Table II. We also compare against tree-based baselines (Random Forest and XGBoost) and evaluate imbalance-handling strategies (class weights, SMOTE). We release configuration details and code to ensure reproducibility. Our experiments show that PCA can improve model compactness and sometimes performance, but model choice and imbalance handling remain critical in highly skewed fraud datasets.

title: Research on Robust Optimization Strategy of Microgrid with Distributed Photovoltaic Cold Chain Logistics Park Based on LHS-CCG Algorithm
authors: Gong Taorong, Wang Shuyang, Liang Chen
publish: 2025 IEEE 9th Conference on Energy Internet and Energy System Integration (EI2)
abstract: Aiming at the problem that the photovoltaic accommodation of cold chain logistics park is affected by meteorological uncertainty, a two-stage robust optimization strategy combining Latin hypercube sampling and column constraint generation algorithm is proposed in this paper. Firstly, a two-stage robust model considering photovoltaic fluctuations is constructed, and the uncertainty set of photovoltaic and load is generated by combining Latin hypercube sampling and K-means to balance conservatism and scene representativeness; Then, the model of charging refrigerated vehicle, temperature control of cold storage and energy storage is established to realize multi energy collaborative scheduling; Finally, the column and constraint generation algorithm is used to decompose the solution to minimize the cost. Numerical examples verify the effectiveness of the model, which can significantly improve the efficiency of renewable energy consumption.

title: Research on Optimal Configuration Method of Energy Storage Systems in Distribution Networks with High Proportion of Renewable Energy
authors: Haoyi Zhu, Bibo Chen, Ting Yuan, Lin Zhang, Zeguang Yu, Yonggang Wang
publish: 2025 IEEE 9th Conference on Energy Internet and Energy System Integration (EI2)
abstract: With the advancement of the "dual carbon" goals, the penetration rate of distributed generations such as wind power and photovoltaic power in distribution networks has been continuously increasing. Their intermittency and volatility pose severe challenges to the safe and stable operation of distribution networks. As an important flexible regulation resource, the reasonable configuration of energy storage systems is of great significance for improving the absorption capacity of distribution networks for renewable energy. Aiming at the operation optimization problem of distribution networks containing wind and photovoltaic power, this paper proposes a cooperative optimization method for the site selection and capacity determination of energy storage systems. A multi-objective optimization model comprehensively considering voltage deviation, line loss, peak load and investment cost is established, and an energy storage peak-shifting operation strategy is designed to achieve cooperative complementarity. The differential evolution (DE) algorithm is adopted to solve the optimization problem of the IEEE 33-bus distribution system, and the computational efficiency is improved through time sampling and candidate node screening. Simulation results show that compared with the benchmark scheme without energy storage, the proposed DE optimization scheme effectively reduces the system voltage deviation, optimizes the voltage stability, and significantly improves the network loss, peak load and peak-valley difference, thereby enhancing the operational economy and voltage quality of the distribution network.

title: Explainable Hybrid Architectures for Hematologic Malignancy Detection in Bone Marrow Smears
authors: C. Nithiya, R. Pari
publish: 2026 Sixth International Conference on Advances in Electrical, Computing, Communications and Sustainable Technologies (ICAECT)
abstract: Hematologic malignancies are challenging to diagnose because of variability in morphology seen on bone marrow smear, and the requirement of an experienced evaluator. In this study, we propose an explainable hybrid deep learning framework that combines convolutional neural networks (CNNs) with transformer-based attention mechanisms to identify hematologic malignancies in digitized bone marrow smear images. The hybrid architecture uses CNNs to extract local features and transformers to capture global contextual dependencies, thus improving accuracy. The framework also includes explainability modules which can generate visual and textual justifications for hematopathologist scores, facilitating interpretability of malignant cell identification, and conformity with hematopathology expertise. A curated clinical dataset showed improved performance ratings compared to any one model, with improved trustworthiness and transparency towards clinical integration. Our framework provides hematopathologist with reliable, interpretable, and actionable insights stemming from complex morphological data to assist in diagnostic practices.

title: Aggregated Optimization Scheduling for Virtual Power Plant and Hydrogen Fueling Station Considering Uncertainty
authors: Yachao Yang, Leiming Suo, Ligen Xie, Yubo Song, Haibo Zhang, Xiang Chen
publish: 2025 IEEE 9th Conference on Energy Internet and Energy System Integration (EI2)
abstract: Nowadays, the incorporation of a high percentage of renewable energy sources into the electrical grid has introduced unprecedented challenges to the system's reliability, safety, and economic viability. Hydrogen has the potential to serve as a long-term storage resource and contribute to the regulation of energy output. Furthermore, its compatibility with wind and solar energy, among other renewable sources, enables the generation of clean energy during periods of energy surpluses and the utilization of natural gas during periods of energy shortages, thereby establishing a more stable and adaptable energy supply structure. The incorporation of virtual power plant (VPP) and hydrogen fueling station (HFS) can assist power grids in effectively addressing the variability and uncertainty associated with renewable energy sources. This paper proposes an optimized scheduling strategy for the aggregation of VPP and HFS. The objective can be achieved by leveraging distributed renewable energy charging networks (DRCC) to refine HFS strategies and reduce the economic cost of hydrogen. The HFS consolidates the hydrogen load of the gas station, thereby ensuring the operational efficiency of the upper level VPP. This consolidation facilitates the regulation of hydrogen load, thereby reducing energy costs and enhancing hydrogen reliability.

title: Secure Key Derivation Using a Custom PRNG: Design and Application in AES
authors: Ayshwarya K, Mary Shamala L
publish: 2026 Sixth International Conference on Advances in Electrical, Computing, Communications and Sustainable Technologies (ICAECT)
abstract: In the modern digital landscape, the generation of secure and unpredictable random numbers plays a vital role in ensuring the strength and reliability of cryptographic systems. This paper focuses on designing and implementing a Secure Pseudo-Random Number Generator that leverages a hybrid approach to enhance the quality of randomness. The system integrates real-time entropy collected from the operating environment, applies grammatical evolution to generate mathematical expressions, and incorporates a mixing phase to produce highly unpredictable random sequences. These sequences are subjected to standard statistical validation tests, such as the NIST Statistical Test Suite, to assess and confirm their randomness and security potential. Beyond theoretical experimentation, the generated random numbers are directly applied in practical scenarios such as encryption key generation and secure token creation, making the system functionally relevant. This paper underscores the importance of robust random number generation in cryptographic systems, and combining multiple randomness sources can strengthen the foundation of secure communication systems in real-world applications.

title: Probability Prediction Method of Transmission Line Icing Thickness Based on Correlation Feature Extraction and Quantile Regression
authors: Peng Li, Zhendong Li, Geng Han, Feiran Wang, Long Cai, Xinyang Guo
publish: 2025 IEEE 9th Conference on Energy Internet and Energy System Integration (EI2)
abstract: Accurate prediction of transmission line icing thickness and its probability interval in cold wave weather is of great significance to the safe operation of power system. In the existing research, the differential variation law of line icing thickness in different development stages of icing events is not fully considered, and the extraction and utilization of related information are ignored. Therefore, this paper proposes a probability prediction model of transmission line icing based on correlation feature extraction and quantile regression. Firstly, the statistical characteristics of historical icing thickness data are analyzed and extracted, and it is divided into four icing modes by K-means algorithm. Secondly, convolution neural network is established to extract the correlation features between ice thickness and various meteorological factors, as well as their associations with icing modes, thereby enabling the prediction of the icing mode for future time periods. Finally, the probability prediction model of icing under different icing modes is established by quantile regression of long-term and short-term memory neural networks. Based on historical ice thickness data and corresponding meteorological conditions, the model predicts the ice thickness interval at the target time. Experimental results show that, compared with traditional methods, the coverage of prediction interval of the proposed model is improved by 2.14% at 90% confidence level, and the overall performance index is improved by 9.95%, which verifies the effectiveness of the proposed method.

title: A Complete Solution to Generalized Relative Pose Estimation from Affine Correspondences_supp1-3673525.pdf
authors: Ji Zhao
publish: N/A
abstract: In recent years, affine correspondences (ACs) have emerged as widely adopted alternative to point correspondences (PCs) in geometric problems in computer vision. An AC is composed of a PC across two different views plus an affine transformation between the small patches around this PC. Prior studies have shown that a single affine correspondence (AC) generally yields three independent constraints for estimating relative pose. This work addresses relative pose estimation in multi-perspective camera systems, a relevant problem given their prevalence in modern technologies such as autonomous vehicles and augmented reality. More specifically, we introduce the first comprehensive suite of minimal solvers for 6DoF relative pose estimation across multiple cameras using only two ACs, which is notably valuable for robust model fitting scenarios. We analyze all possible configurations of two ACs in two views, and present minimal solvers covering all identified minimal cases. We make use of the hidden variable technique to eliminate the translation parameters, and represent rotation using either Cayley parameters or quaternions. We furthermore introduce novel constraints on the generalized relative pose problem that are beneficial in deriving more compact solvers with fewer solutions. Comprehensive experiments on synthetic and real-world data show that the proposed affine correspondence-based solvers are highly effective and computationally efficient.

title: Modeling and Simulation of Vacuum Arc With the Ionization–Recombination Characteristics of Anode Jets Under the Effect of Anode Melting Pool Protrusions
authors: S. Jia, H. Sun, Q. Zhang, Y. Liu, X. Liu, X. Huang, Y. Bai
publish: IEEE Transactions on Plasma Science
abstract: During the high current interruption of vacuum circuit breakers, the anode melts due to the substantial energy flux injected, forming an anode melting pool. The repeated melting–solidification processes around the melting pool result in the accumulation of metal materials, ultimately giving rise to protrusions of varying types and sizes. These locally overheated protrusions evaporate anode neutral atom vapor into the interelectrode region. This article establishes a 2-D model to indicate the effect of the anode melting pool protrusions on the plasma transport properties of the vacuum arc and the ionization–recombination characteristics of the anode jets. The results indicate that the peak number density of neutral atoms in the anode vapor increases with the number of protrusions. Additionally, the jet shape of the anode vapor becomes more widely distributed as the protrusions get bigger. Ionization activity primarily occurs in the region where neutral atoms and electrons from the arc interact, while recombination activity mainly takes place near the protrusions. Furthermore, the intensity of ionization activity is significantly higher than that of recombination, approximately by two orders of magnitude. The ionization activity region leads to the formation of an ionization–recombination layer. Moreover, it shifts toward the cathode side as protrusions increase. This layer maintains a consistent thickness of around 3–4 mm, regardless of the size or number of protrusions.

title: D 2 FNet: Degradation-aware Diffusion Feature Guided Network against Physical-World Attack for Monocular Depth Estimation
authors: Zhongyang Wang, Shengjin Xu, Yechang Xu, Guoxia Xu, Lizhen Deng, Hu Zhu, Marius Pedersen
publish: IEEE Transactions on Consumer Electronics
abstract: In consumer healthcare and intelligent consumer electronics, robust monocular depth estimation is essential for applications such as portable endoscopic devices, AR/VR-assisted diagnosis, home-use health monitoring systems, and smart wearable imaging platforms. However, low-cost sensors and compact optical systems often introduce motion blur, uneven illumination, compression artifacts, and sensor noise. Beyond these degradations, AI-enabled physical-world attacks—such as lighting perturbations, surface reflections, or optical interference—can mislead perception systems, posing serious reliability and safety risks in endoscopic scenarios. These factors jointly degrade performance and hinder the deployment of AI-enabled perception in real-world devices. To address this challenge, we propose D2FNet, a degradation-aware diffusion feature guided network explicitly designed to enhance robustness against both imaging degradation and physical-world attacks. D2FNet integrates a Diffusion Feature Generation Module (DFGM) to synthesize degradation-invariant features, a Style-free Content Reconstruction Block (SCRB) to suppress style-related distortions, and a Frequency Refined Adaptive Fusion Block (FRAF) to adaptively fuse spatial and frequency information. Extensive experiments on multiple endoscopic datasets demonstrate that D2FNet achieves superior robustness, generalization, and attack resilience compared to existing approaches. This work provides a scalable and secure AI perception framework for consumer healthcare electronics, enabling reliable monocular depth estimation under real-world adversarial imaging conditions.

title: Optimal Planning of Offshore Wind Farm Collector System considering Intensive Sea Use
authors: Haowei Yang, Xinwei Shen, Wenhao Gao, Yunfei Du, Ming Shi, Qingxin Li, Wuqing Wei, Yingjun Chen
publish: IEEE Transactions on Sustainable Energy
abstract: Driven by the global clean energy transition, the offshore wind power has been expanding rapidly worldwide. In offshore wind farms (OWFs), the electrical collector system (ECS) already accounts for a substantial portion of the total project cost, while emerging sea-use regulations call for compact, corridor-based cable layouts instead of traditional dispersed radial schemes. This paper proposes an OWF-ECS planning framework tailored to the intensive use of sea areas. We for mulate a mixed-integer linear programming model (MILP) that incorporates the requirement of the intensive sea-use and uses the parallel-laying length of cables to represent spatial intensification. A clustering-based warm-start strategy and distance based candidate-arc reduction are further designed to improve solution efficiency and scalability. Case studies demonstrate that the proposed framework can accommodate diverse sea-use requirements and intensification levels, consistently generating economically efficient and spatially intensive ECS layouts and obtaining high-quality feasible or optimal solutions within acceptable computation time.

title: CNPE-KalmanNet: Learning-aided Kalman Filter With Complex Number Position Encoding
authors: Yucheng Long, Wei Mei, Jian Song, Yunfeng Xu, Qiang Fu, Lina Bu
publish: IEEE Signal Processing Letters
abstract: KalmanNet demonstrates potential for nonlinear state estimation, but it directly concatenates heterogeneous components with disparate semantics into a composite vector. This approach ignores capturing semantic component correlations, limiting the network's ability to utilize this information when computing the Kalman gain. To address this, we propose CNPE-KalmanNet, a novel learning-aided Kalman filter that models semantic component correlations by integrating element-wise rotary encoding with multilayer perceptrons in the complex domain. CNPE serves as a plug-and-play module compatible with two baseline KalmanNet architectures. Experiments on the Michigan NCLT dataset demonstrate that CNPE-KalmanNet significantly improves estimation accuracy. Compared to the existing KalmanNet, our method reduces the RMSE by 20%-25% in state estimation tasks and by 15%-20% in sensor fusion tasks.

title: Tiny-PMDet: A Knowledge-Distilled Lightweight Edge-AI Detector for Grapevine Powdery Mildew Detection on CMOS Sensor Based AIoT Systems
authors: Qiang Tong, Nanxin Zeng, Songtao Wu, Kuanhong Xu
publish: IEEE Transactions on AgriFood Electronics
abstract: Edge-AI-powered Artificial Intelligence of Things (AIoT) technologies enable low-power and efficient solutions for intelligent crop monitoring. This article presents Tiny-PMDet, a lightweight yet high-accuracy detector designed for on-sensor grapevine powdery mildew detection using an edge-AI-enabled CMOS (Sony IMX500) based camera. The proposed model integrates an EfficientNet-Lite0 backbone, a sBiFPN neck, and a lightweight decoupled detection head, achieving an effective balance between detection accuracy and computational efficiency. To further enhance detection precision without increasing inference cost, a loss optimization strategy combining Weighted Cross-Entropy, Efficient IoU, and Quality Cross-Entropy is adopted, together with a dual knowledge distillation scheme based on Pearson correlation and cross-head guidance. Experimental results demonstrate that Tiny-PMDet achieves 95.1% AP@0.5 and 94.4% F1-score, outperforming state-of-the-art lightweight detectors while maintaining a lightweight model size of 2.9 MB and a low computational cost of 0.9 GFLOPs. After posttraining quantization and deployment on the IMX500 sensor, Tiny-PMDet achieves real-time inference at 36 FPS with only an approximately 1% accuracy degradation. These results highlight the advantages of edge-AI CMOS sensor-based solutions for enabling low-power, on-site, and network-independent agricultural monitoring, marking a significant step toward scalable AIoT-driven smart agriculture.

title: Dynamic Reinforcement Learning Control for Fractional-Order Neural Networks with Higher-Order Interactions and Multiple Time Delays
authors: Hua Li, Min Xiao, Chengdai Huang, Qingxiang Fang, Xia Huang, Wenwu Yu, Yi Yao, Tingwen Huang, Leszek Rutkowski
publish: IEEE Transactions on Artificial Intelligence
abstract: Conventional neural networks’ reliance on pairwise connections limits their ability to model the modular higher-order interactions inherent in biological neural systems. The stability analysis and control of large-scale networks that integrate these higher-order interactions with fractional-order dynamics and multiple time delays present a significant challenge due to analytical intractability. This study establishes a theoretical and computational framework for a large-scale fractional-order neural network model featuring modular triplet clusters. Analytical criteria for stability and Hopf bifurcation are derived. To address the control challenge, a reinforcement learning framework is implemented for the adaptive tuning of higher-order coupling strength to suppress destabilizing oscillations. Numerical results quantify the profound impact of system parameters on stability while demonstrating the reinforcement learning controller’s ability to maintain stability where traditional methods fail. By adaptively tuning the coupling strength without any prior knowledge of the system model, the proposed approach effectively suppresses oscillations, showcasing a advantage over model-based controllers for such high-dimensional, analytically intractable systems.
