title: Enhancing Large Language Models (LLMs) for Telecom Using Dynamic Knowledge Graphs and Explainable Retrieval-Augmented Generation
authors: Dun Yuan, Hao Zhou, Xue Liu, Hao Chen, Yan Xin, Jianzhong Zhang
publish: IEEE Wireless Communications
abstract: Large language models (LLMs) have shown strong potential across a variety of tasks, but their application in the telecom field remains challenging due to domain complexity, evolving standards, and specialized terminology. Therefore, general-domain LLMs may struggle to provide accurate and reliable outputs in this context, leading to increased hallucinations and reduced utility in telecom operations. To address these limitations, this work introduces KG-RAG—a novel framework that integrates knowledge graphs (KGs) with retrieval-augmented generation (RAG) to enhance LLMs for telecom-specific tasks. In particular, the KG provides a structured representation of domain knowledge derived from telecom standards and technical documents, while RAG enables dynamic retrieval of relevant facts to ground the model’s outputs. Such a combination improves factual accuracy, reduces hallucination, and ensures compliance with telecom specifications. Experimental results across benchmark datasets demonstrate that KG-RAG outperforms both LLM-only and standard RAG baselines, e.g., KG-RAG achieves an average accuracy improvement of 14.3% over RAG and 21.6% over LLM-only models. These results highlight KG-RAG’s effectiveness in producing accurate, reliable, and explainable outputs in complex telecom scenarios.

title: Autonomous USV Landing of VTOL Fixed-Wing UAV Based on Raptor Vision
authors: Xiaobin Xu, Haibin Duan, Zhigang Zeng
publish: IEEE Transactions on Aerospace and Electronic Systems
abstract: An autonomous unmanned surface vehicle (USV) landing system of vertical takeoff and landing (VTOL) fixed-wing unmanned aerial vehicle (UAV) based on raptor vision is presented in this paper. The color saliency detection method based on raptor vision in the biological raptor vision color resolution space is developed to obtain the landing target and markers in the mission scene. A three-color nested landing target scheme is designed to accommodate the different height of UAV. Considering the various interferences exist in the complex environment, the target occlusion strategy and the go-around (GA) strategy are supplemented in the vision navigation system to improve the accuracy and security of the landing mission. The complete UAV/USV flight verification system is developed to validate the effectiveness of the proposed method. Results show that the autonomous landing system based on raptor vision obtain a quite satisfactory landing outcome. The horizontal errors of UAV autonomous landing are both less than 5cm. The success rate of UAV landing has been greatly improved. Therefore, the proposed autonomous landing system provides a feasible technical pathway for UAV landing on a USV.

title: GRAND: Guidance, Rebalancing, and Assignment for Networked Dispatch in Multi-Agent Path Finding
authors: Johannes Gaber, Meshal Alharbi, Daniele Gammelli, Gioele Zardini
publish: IEEE Robotics and Automation Letters
abstract: Large robot fleets are now common in warehouses and other logistics settings, where small control gains translate into large operational impacts. In this article, we address task scheduling for lifelong Multi-Agent Pickup-and-Delivery (MAPD) and propose a hybrid method that couples learning-based global guidance with lightweight optimization. A graph neural network policy trained via reinforcement learning outputs a desired distribution of free agents over an aggregated warehouse graph. This signal is converted into region-to-region rebalancing through a minimum-cost flow, and finalized by small, local assignment problems, preserving accuracy while keeping per-step latency within a 1 s compute budget. We call this approach GRAND: a hierarchical algorithm that relies on Guidance, Rebalancing, and Assignment to explicitly leverage the workspace Network structure and Dispatch agents to tasks. On congested warehouse benchmarks from the League of Robot Runners (LoRR) with up to 500 agents, our approach improves throughput by up to 10% over the 2024 winning scheduler while maintaining real-time execution. The results indicate that coupling graph-structured learned guidance with tractable solvers reduces congestion and yields a practical, scalable blueprint for high-throughput scheduling in large fleets.

title: Enabling Streaming Analytics for Digital Twin Applications in Mobile Edge Computing Networks
authors: Qiufen Xia, Peichen Liu, Zichuan Xu, Jiankang Ren, Weifa Liang, Guangyuan Xu, Wenzheng Xu, Pan Zhou, Hao Li, Zhen Feng
publish: IEEE Transactions on Parallel and Distributed Systems
abstract: Digital twin is emerging as a key technology to monitor the status of complex industry systems. Valuable insights, such as running statuses and anomalies, can be analyzed from the collected system status timely. Considering that the data updating from each system component (known as a physical object) to its digital twin is performed continuously, timely and accurate streaming analytics based on machine learning models is a key technology to analyze such data efficiently. In this paper, we focus on enabling low-delay yet highly-accurate streaming analytics for digital twin applications in mobile edge computing (MEC) networks. Specifically, we formulate a fundamental optimization problem of digital twin placements and model selections for streaming analytics, with the aim of minimizing both the analytic loss and the processing delay. To this end, we first consider the problem with a single query, for which, we propose an approximation algorithm with provable approximation ratio for a special case, and then devise an efficient algorithm for the original problem with a single query. We then study the online digital twin placement and model selection problem for streaming analytics with multiple queries under real scenarios, where resource demands of arrival queries and resource availability of MEC network are uncertain. We propose an online learning algorithm with a bounded regret to make admission policies. We finally evaluate the performance of the proposed algorithms by extensive simulations. Results show that the weighted sums of the total processing delay and the cumulative loss in the solution delivered by the proposed algorithms outperform their counterparts by 12.5% with a single query and 13.3% with multiple queries, respectively.

title: Whole-Body Path Following of an Active-Joint Active-Wheel Snake Robot Based on Head-Body Stepwise Control
authors: Fuxi Wan, Xian Guo, Huawang Liu, Wei Huang, Zhong Huang
publish: IEEE Robotics and Automation Letters
abstract: This paper presents a two-dimensional planar path-following controller for active-joint active-wheel snake-like robots. Compared to passive-wheel snake robots, active-wheel snake robots can navigate narrower spaces and generate stronger driving forces, enabling adaptation to various terrains. However, due to the highly redundant multi-link and multi-active-wheel body configuration, achieving whole-body path following for active-joint active-wheel snake robots is challenging. To address this, we propose a head-body stepwise path-following control method based on a multi-rigid-body kinematic model to solve the problem of high degrees of freedom and the coordination between joints and wheels. First, by applying the no-side-slip constraint, we construct a multi-joint and multi-wheel kinematic model for the snake body path-following control task. Then, a motion controller is designed for the fundamental motion of the active wheeled snake robot. To achieve path following, an arbitrary path following controller is designed for turning angle. Then, the designed turning angle is substituted into the motion controller, and the angular velocity of each joint is calculated based on kinematic model. Subsequently, an incremental PID controller is designed to control the angle of each joint. Finally, the whole-body path following task of active-wheel snake-like robot can be implemented. Simulation and extensive experimental results for both curvature-continuous and curvature-discontinuous paths demonstrate the superior following performance of the proposed controller compared to the PD method and passive-joint conditions. Furthermore, the robustness of the proposed method to variations in friction is validated.

title: L-Shape Bounding Box Detector for LiDAR Point-Clouds
authors: Vlad Lazăr, Robert Varga
publish: 2025 IEEE 21st International Conference on Intelligent Computer Communication and Processing (ICCP)
abstract: This paper proposes a hybrid vehicle detection method for LiDAR point clouds, combining geometric algorithms for segmentation and bounding box fitting with a lightweight machine learning classifier. Our approach is based on an L-shape fitting method but also integrates spatial filtering and adaptive clustering to enhance robustness in complex urban scenes. The pipeline consists of ground removal, density-based clustering, vehicle cluster identification, and refined L-shape fitting, which efficiently approximates vehicle footprints. A Random Forest classifier enhances robustness by filtering non-vehicle clusters. Experiments conducted on the SemanticKITTI dataset show that the proposed method achieves a mean precision of 68.16%, while maintaining a significantly lower computational cost. The obtained results are comparable to deep learning methods, while preserving real-time performance and interpretability, making the approach suitable for lightweight autonomous driving platforms.

title: Data-Driven Protection Method for Converter Bridge Arms in Flexible DC Transmission Systems
authors: Jintao Shi, Dahai Zhang, Xiaochen Hu, Yajin Wang
publish: 2025 IEEE 9th Conference on Energy Internet and Energy System Integration (EI2)
abstract: To address the challenges of complex fault characteristics and difficult analysis in converter arm protection for flexible high-voltage direct current (HVDC) transmission systems, this paper proposes a data-driven protection method based on a combined one-dimensional convolutional neural network (1D-CNN) and bidirectional long short-term memory (BiLSTM) model. This approach performs feature extraction and classification on fault data to enable accurate identification and effective protection of converter arm faults. The simulation results demonstrate that the proposed 1D-CNN-BiLSTM model achieves an overall fault identification accuracy of over 97%, significantly outperforming the standalone 1D-CNN (83.33%) and BiLSTM (86.54%) models. The method enhances diagnostic reliability and provides robust assurance for the safe operation of flexible DC transmission systems.

title: Towards Data-Scarce Scenarios: Self supervised and Transfer Learning Method for Voiceprint based Fault Detection of Power Equipment
authors: Jie Zhou, Xizhou Du, Xing Lei, Yufan Wu, Junjia Li, Zhiqiang Liu
publish: 2025 IEEE 9th Conference on Energy Internet and Energy System Integration (EI2)
abstract: In industrial fault diagnosis, voiceprint analysis offers noninvasive, real-time monitoring of equipment health. Deep learning has introduced powerful new techniques for fault detection, but its reliance on large labeled datasets poses challenges in data-scarce environments. We propose a training strategy that combines self-supervised learning and transfer learning. First, a reconstruction proxy task is used to pretrain the model without labels, enhancing its representation capacity. Then, transfer learning adapts the pretrained model from a source domain to a target domain under few-shot conditions. Extensive experiments on both domains demonstrate that our method outperforms alternatives, confirming its ability to significantly improve feature representation and cross-domain generalization in low-data scenarios.

title: Study on Response Characteristics of Grid-Forming Energy Storage in New-Type Power System
authors: Yifan Li, Mao Yang, Yitao Li, Peng Sun, Wencheng Li
publish: 2025 IEEE 9th Conference on Energy Internet and Energy System Integration (EI2)
abstract: Under the global pursuit of clean energy and the "dual carbon" goals, the development of new-type power systems has become an inevitable trend. This paper focuses on the optimal control of inertia response for grid-forming energy storage considering the voltage and frequency stability characteristics of new-type power systems. To begin with, it analyzes the key points of grid-connected testing technology for GFL and compares the differences in technical standards among the United States, Europe and China. Secondly, it sorts out the research status of inertia for GFL Domestically and internationally, introduces the four-stage evolution path of domestic standards as well as the technical focuses and shortcomings of foreign standards, and also explains the functional requirements of grid-forming standards for power grid support and other aspects, along with the formulas and principles related to inertia response optimization. Finally, it points out that the research provides references for the improvement and internationalization of testing technology standards and the stable development of new-type power systems.

title: Group Peak-Shaving Strategy Based on Regulation and Distribution Characteristics of Building Electric Heating Loads
authors: Jun Ma, Hao Sheng Li, Xiao Jun Bai, Kai Li, Xiang Yu Chen, Yang Jin
publish: 2025 IEEE 9th Conference on Energy Internet and Energy System Integration (EI2)
abstract: To address the challenges to grid security posed by the integration of electric heating loads—characterized by randomness, volatility, and spatiotemporal aggregation—in the context of advancing the "dual carbon" goals and developing new power systems, this study conducts research on group peak-s having of building electric heating loads. Based on measured data and by dividing regulatory time intervals in conjunction with power grid dispatching modes, the analysis reveals that the load group's regulation capacity is discrete and volatile, allowing o nly short-term, low-power adjustments. Accordingly, a hierarchical dispatching architecture based on load aggregators is constructed, and a dispatching model is established with the objective of "minimizing the adjustment amount of peak-shaving buildings under constraints," incorporating constraints on target quantity deviation, number of calls, and adjustment time. Case verification shows that under an indoor temperature range of [19°C, 23°C] and an average outdoor temperature of -9.51°C, the average power decreases by 7.05% during peak loads and increases by 6.23% during off-peak periods, while the actual indoor temperature range [18.85°C, 23.29°C] meets the requirements. The model can satisfy the system's peak-shaving needs.

title: EnfXAI: Enhancing Seizure Detection with Interpretable Deep Neuro-Fuzzy Architectures
authors: Flavia Costi, Emanuel Covaci, Ovidiu Roman, Darian Onchis, Codruta Istin
publish: 2025 IEEE 21st International Conference on Intelligent Computer Communication and Processing (ICCP)
abstract: Automatic detection of epileptic seizures based on EEG signals represents a major challenge in neuroscience and AI-assisted medical applications. Currently, over 50 million people worldwide live with epilepsy, and a significant proportion of them experience recurrent seizures that can occur unpredictably and severely affect their quality of life. For this reason, we propose EnfXAI, a hybrid system that integrates a deep learning model (CNN), an explainability component (SHAP), and a fuzzy logic module for risk estimation. The CNN model is trained on an EEG dataset segmented into one-second windows, achieving an accuracy of over 98% in classifying seizure and non-seizure windows. SHAP is then used to identify the EEG features with the highest positive contribution to seizure prediction. These features are subsequently processed by a fuzzy logic system that provides an interpretable seizure risk estimate—classified as low, medium or high. In this way, we contribute to the early and interpretable identification of epileptic seizures, helping affected individuals benefit from faster and more personalized interventions. As a result of the analysis, the test data was split into two categories: at-risk (693 individuals) and not at-risk (494 low-risk and 1113 medium-risk individuals). The final output is both a highly accurate and easy-to-understand prediction, with strong potential for application in real-world clinical contexts. The proposed system represents a significant step toward integrating explainable AI (XAI) into neurological diagnostics.

title: Detection Method for Incipient Cable Faults in Small-Resistance Grounded Systems Based on Multidimensional Information
authors: Fenglan Tian, Wei Bao, Yanan Yang, Wenli Wang, Chaoliang Qi, Qilong Chen
publish: 2025 IEEE 9th Conference on Energy Internet and Energy System Integration (EI2)
abstract: To address the problem of small fault currents, short fault duration, and the failure of traditional fault-location methods caused by distributed generation (DG) integration in active small-resistance grounded distribution networks, a fault section location method based on multidimensional information correlation analysis is proposed. An equivalent model of incipient cable fault is established using the Kizilcay arc model, and the fault evolution mechanism is characterized considering the impedance characteristics of water-tree and electrical-tree channels. The generalized S-transform is employed to extract three types of features from the transient zero-sequence current, namely, transient energy magnitude, phase-polarity, and waveform similarity. A comprehensive distance-similarity coefficient is then constructed as the location criterion. Finally, simulation results under different fault sections, DG access positions, and noise disturbances verify the effectiveness of the proposed method. The results show that the method can accurately locate the fault section in small-current grounded systems and has potential application value for fault self-healing in active distribution networks.

title: Typical Scenarios Division and Analysis of Rural Distribution Network
authors: Xin Yang, Fan Zhou, Ran Xu, Yalin Zhong, Fei Jiao, Jun Xu
publish: 2025 IEEE 9th Conference on Energy Internet and Energy System Integration (EI2)
abstract: Currently, rural distribution networks are in a stage of rapid development and transformation, with their network structures continuously evolving. This paper proposes a typical scenario classification method for rural power systems, and this method considers four dimensions - economy, society, geography, and electricity. First, a four-dimensional three-level indicator system is constructed to evaluate the characteristics of rural distribution networks. Meanwhile, the entropy weight method, an objective weighting approach, is adopted to assign weights to each indicator. Next, the elbow method is used to determine the optimal number of typical scenario classifications, and the K-means algorithm is applied to cluster and classify the rural power systems in Anhui Province. Finally, the quality of the clustering results is characterized with the silhouette coefficients.

title: XMedium-Voltage Flexible Interconnection Equipment based on Thyristor Multi-Level Voltage Regulation
authors: Xin Yang, Fan Zhou, Ran Xu, Yalin Zhong, Fei Jiao, Jun Xu
publish: 2025 IEEE 9th Conference on Energy Internet and Energy System Integration (EI2)
abstract: This paper proposes a thyristor-controlled hybrid transformer (TCHT) for the flexible interconnection in medium-voltage distribution networks, aiming to address the challenges of inrush current during loop-closing operations and limited power flow regulation capability under high penetration of renewable energy. By integrating parallel and series transformers with a multi-level thyristor valve control unit, the TCHT enables active regulation of voltage magnitude and phase angle, thereby achieving soft loop-closing and bidirectional power flow control. The proposed dynamic voltage tracking strategy effectively mitigates initial switching transients, while the shortest path optimization method maps power commands to discrete voltage vectors, enabling fast and accurate active/reactive power regulation. MATLAB/Simulink simulation results demonstrate that the TCHT efficiently tracks power commands. The TCHT offers an economical and reliable alternative to complex power electronics-based equipment, significantly enhancing the flexibility and reliability of distribution networks.

title: Analysis Model and Optimization Method of Power Supply Service State Based on Behavior Test Classification
authors: Jinming Cao, Zeng Li, Tingyi Pan, Luojia Yang, Jiaqi Wang, Yipin Han
publish: 2025 IEEE 9th Conference on Energy Internet and Energy System Integration (EI2)
abstract: In order to make power users feel more comfortable and better service, this paper proposes a power supply service state analysis model and its optimization method based on behavior test classification. Firstly, the data of user behavior evaluation system is obtained, and the K-means clustering algorithm is used to classify the user power consumption data and complaint data. Then, a power supply service status analysis model based on behavior test classification is established. Finally, the convolutional neural network ( CNN ) is used to solve the model and its effectiveness is verified by experiments. The experimental results show that this method can effectively improve the growth rate of electricity consumption, reduce the peak-valley difference rate, reduce the probability of users ' arrears, improve the marketing conversion rate and customer satisfaction, shorten the average response time, and promote the stable development of power enterprises under the new situation.

title: Closing Overvoltage Characteristics in Flexible Low-Frequency Power Transmission Systems
authors: Jianhua Chen, Yisheng Chen, Zhichao Zhou, Jiawei Wang, Ziling You, Xianqiang Li
publish: 2025 IEEE 9th Conference on Energy Internet and Energy System Integration (EI2)
abstract: With the continuous expansion of offshore wind power integration, flexible low-frequency AC transmission systems are being widely implemented in wind power landing project due to their superior power transmission capability. Closing overvoltage, as a critical factor affecting system insulation, requires particular attention. This study develops an electromagnetic transient model for automatic reclosing overvoltage in low-frequency submarine cable systems, analyzes the characteristics of closing overvoltage, and discusses the influence of factors including closing phase angles, closing resistors, and surge arresters. Results indicate that the maximum closing overvoltage multiplier in low-frequency submarine cables reaches 2.76 p.u., with predominant frequency components near 20 Hz, 196.6 Hz, and 589.8 Hz. Both same-potential closing and the application of a appropriate closing resistor can effectively suppress the amplitude of closing overvoltage. Furthermore, after installing surge arresters, the maximum closing overvoltage multiplier can be reduced to 2.01 p.u.. The findings can serve as a design basis for insulation coordination in flexible low-frequency transmission systems.

title: Quantum-Based Damage Prediction Model for Overhead Transmission Line During Typhoon Disaster
authors: Fei Yi, Hui Hou, Ruizeng Wei, Zhongda Li
publish: 2025 IEEE 9th Conference on Energy Internet and Energy System Integration (EI2)
abstract: Typhoon disasters occur frequently, posing a significant threat to social economy as well as human life and property safety. Traditional prediction methods, limited by the computing power of classical computers, struggle to efficiently process large-scale meteorological data and high-dimensional nonlinear relationships. This paper proposes a prediction model for distribution tower damage under typhoon disasters based on quantum-based algorithms, aiming to improve prediction accuracy and provide support for disaster prevention and mitigation. The specific process is as follows: First, by integrating data from meteorological stations and electric power departments, a dataset is constructed by matching tower parameters, geographic information, and actual damage conditions. The data is preprocessed through operations such as data division and feature preprocessing to obtain data suitable for prediction. Second, in the tower damage prediction stage, a random forest prediction model is established; its prediction results are combined with original features to serve as the input for the quantum-based model, and a quantum-based machine learning model is built for prediction. The prediction performance of the two models is then compared. Finally, taking the distribution tower damage in Jiangmen City, Guangdong Province caused by Typhoon "Mangkhut" in 2018 as an example, a prediction analysis is conducted. The results show that the quantum-based machine learning model exhibits better performance.

title: Application of an Optimized Isolation Forest Algorithm to Satellite Fault Detection
authors: Yanchen Pan, Xiang Zhang, Hao Lei, Hossam Hendy, Hongcai Li, Haojie Ji
publish: 2025 3rd Asian Aerospace and Astronautics Conference (AAAC)
abstract: In modern spacecraft systems, the increasing functional integration of satellite platforms has led to the emergence of more complex and diverse fault modes. Timely and accurate identification of potential faults within massive volumes of telemetry data is critical to ensuring the long-term stable operation of satellites in orbit. This paper proposes a satellite fault detection method based on an optimized Isolation Forest algorithm. By improving the anomaly score evaluation mechanism and introducing an adaptive thresholding strategy, the approach aims to enhance the accuracy and real-time responsiveness of fault detection in complex satellite systems. The research findings are expected to provide methodological support for the development of intelligent and autonomous on-orbit fault diagnosis systems.

title: Real-time Prediction and Control Method of Taxiing Conflicts Based on Fuzzy Timing Workflow Net
authors: Xiaoxuan Xie, Yong Tian, Jiangchen Li
publish: 2025 3rd Asian Aerospace and Astronautics Conference (AAAC)
abstract: With the rapid development of the aviation industry, the daily average number of flights at airports continues to rise, posing severe challenges to the safety and efficiency of operations during peak hours. To reduce ground delays during peak hours at airports and improve the robustness of taxiway systems, this paper proposes a real-time taxiing conflict prediction and control method based on fuzzy temporal workflow network. Firstly, this article models the aircraft taxiing process as a workflow network and uses trapezoidal fuzzy numbers to fuzzify the taxiing time of straight sections and intersections. Secondly, based on the dynamic time consistency verification mechanism, the probability of aircraft taxiing conflict and its occurrence time window are calculated in real time according to the membership function of trapezoidal fuzzy numbers. Further subdividing conflicts into potential conflicts and deterministic conflicts, a conflict control method with the goal of minimizing fuel consumption was established to provide controllers with the optimal conflict resolution strategy. Finally, this paper validates the method based on real historical operational data from a large airport in China, and the results show that the method can effectively predict chain conflicts caused by external disturbances, providing more accurate decision support for air traffic controllers.

title: A Novel Method for DC Overvoltage Suppression and Protection in DRU-MMC Power Transmission System
authors: Longze Kou, Zhiwei Mi, Minghui Su, Yang Gao
publish: 2025 IEEE 9th Conference on Energy Internet and Energy System Integration (EI2)
abstract: This paper proposes an overvoltage suppression method for medium-frequency Diode Rectifier Unit - Modular Multilevel Converter (DRU-MMC) power transmission systems. It adopts an arrester-based overvoltage suppression strategy, a DC energy-consuming device based on half-bridge sub-modules, and an active voltage reduction method for offshore AC systems. These measures effectively suppress overvoltage in the system, ensure the safe and reliable operation of the system, and reduce costs simultaneously.

title: Withdrawal time estimation per anatomical region in colonoscopy videos
authors: Florin Dumitrescu, Maria Andreea Tudosie, Mihai Trăscău
publish: 2025 IEEE 21st International Conference on Intelligent Computer Communication and Processing (ICCP)
abstract: Colonoscopy is a widely used method for mucosal inspection and lesion detection. Accurate measurement of withdrawal time during colonoscopy is critical to ensure adequate mucosa inspection and improve the rate of adenoma detection. In this paper, we present a two-stage pipeline that combines a frame filter, based on a voting system (frame sharpness, structural richness, and color context), with a deep learning model for anatomical colon region classification to accurately estimate the withdrawal time for each colon region. Specifically, we introduce an adaptive thresholding mechanism to robustly identify noninformative frames, providing reliable frame exclusion, enhancing subsequent analysis. Qualitative results indicate that our model effectively tracks the colonoscope’s position through the colon regions during the withdrawal phase with reasonable temporal alignment, while managing uncertain frames.

title: Research on Source-Grid-Load-Storage Interaction Models for Multi-Energy Storage Integration in photovoltaic Plants
authors: Qiang Zhou, Pengfei Gao, Zhenzhen Zhang, Jinping Zhang, Yongrui Zhang, Ruixiao Zhang
publish: 2025 IEEE 9th Conference on Energy Internet and Energy System Integration (EI2)
abstract: To address the challenges of high volatility, poor grid stability, and low renewable energy accommodation rate in large-scale photovoltaic power integration, this study focuses on the research of Source-Grid-Load-Storage (SGLS) interaction models for multi-energy storage (MES) integration in photovoltaic power plants. Taking the photovoltaic-rich region, a comprehensive simulation system was constructed, encompassing mathematical models of photovoltaic power generation, MES systems (lithium-ion batteries, flywheels, and hydrogen storage), multi-type loads, and power grids. Simulation results show that compared with the scenario without MES integration, the proposed model increases the photovoltaic power accommodation. This research provides a theoretical basis and technical support for improving the stability, economy, and renewable energy utilization of photovoltaic power plants, and contributes to the construction of new power systems.

title: Anomaly Detection for Battery Systems Based on a Physics-Data-Driven Fusion Model
authors: Zitong Chen, Chen Yan, Xiaoyu Ji, Wenyuan Xu
publish: 2025 IEEE 9th Conference on Energy Internet and Energy System Integration (EI2)
abstract: With the rapid development of electric vehicles and smart grids, battery systems have become critical energy units for energy transition and transportation electrification. However, within the environment of Cyber-Physical Systems (CPS), battery systems face increasingly complex cybersecurity threats. The openness of communication interfaces and the diversity of operating environments make them potential attack targets. Among various cyber threats, False Data Injection Attacks (FDIAs) pose a particularly insidious challenge, as attackers deliberately inject falsified sensor data that remains consistent with system models, thereby bypassing conventional anomaly detection mechanisms. To address this issue, this paper proposes a hybrid physical-data-driven method for detecting FDIAs behaviors in battery systems. Residuals from a physics-based model capture physical consistency, while a deep learning model predicts battery dynamics; temporal features from both are analyzed via a threshold mechanism to accurately identify false data. In the experimental study, four types of anomalous data were constructed based on standard EV driving cycle datasets. And the results show that the proposed fusion model exhibits strong performance in battery system anomaly detection tasks.

title: Face Recognition System using Fully Homomorphic Encryption
authors: Talida Dîrnu, Vlad-Cristian Miclea
publish: 2025 IEEE 21st International Conference on Intelligent Computer Communication and Processing (ICCP)
abstract: Guaranteeing privacy in facial recognition remains an open challenge due to the sensitive nature of biometric data. This paper presents the development of a biometric authentication system based on facial recognition, designed to ensure complete data confidentiality by integrating fully homomorphic encryption. Using this technique, sensitive data remains encrypted throughout the entire processing pipeline and is never exposed in plaintext on the server. The system is based on a Siamese neural network trained to compare two facial images and determine whether they belong to the same person, with all computations performed directly on encrypted data using the CKKS scheme, which enables approximate arithmetic over encrypted real numbers. The performance of the encrypted system is evaluated against the unencrypted baseline model, highlighting the trade-offs between security and computational efficiency.

title: PPG-Based Respiration Monitoring Using CycleGAN
authors: D Loganathan, Nihal B, Rachana S Kamalpur, Gowtham R
publish: 2026 Sixth International Conference on Advances in Electrical, Computing, Communications and Sustainable Technologies (ICAECT)
abstract: This paper presents a deep learning-based approach for estimating respiratory rate (RR) from Photoplethysmogram (PPG) signals, a non-invasive method that measures blood volume variations in peripheral vessels. Conventional RR measurement techniques, such as impedance pneumography and capnography, require specialized hardware, manual calibration, and patient-specific adjustments, limiting scalability and ease of deployment. A new propose a Cycle Generative Adversarial Network (CycleGAN) framework to reconstruct respiratory waveforms directly from raw PPG data, enabling automated and accurate RR estimation without the need for traditional respiratory sensors. The model employs paired generator and discriminator networks to map between PPG and respiratory domains while enforcing cycle consistency for high fidelity signal reconstruction. Pre-processing steps include normalization, down sampling, and segmentation of PPG signals into fixed-length windows for model training. Using the BIDMC PPG and Respiration Dataset, our approach achieved a Mean Absolute Error (MAE) of 0.52 and a Respiration Rate Error of 4.24, demonstrating robust performance and adaptability across datasets. This methodology offers strong potential for integration into wearable health monitoring devices, telemedicine platforms, and other real-time non-invasive diagnostic systems, with future work exploring real-time deployment and extension to other physiological signal translations.

title: Power Systems Reliability Assessment with High Penetration of Renewable Energy Considering Real-Time Reliability of Generating Units
authors: Tianfang Zhang
publish: 2025 IEEE 9th Conference on Energy Internet and Energy System Integration (EI2)
abstract: The rapid integration of wind and photovoltaic (PV) renewable energy sources (RES) is pivotal for global carbon neutrality, yet current power system reliability assessment methods, while partially addressing RES intermittency, largely overlook the real-time reliability of generating units. This reliability dynamically changes with operational conditions (voltage/frequency fluctuations, load rate), equipment aging, and latent defects, leading to inaccurate supply capability evaluations. To resolve this, this paper proposes a novel assessment method. Firstly, a dynamic forced outage rate (FOR) model is developed via multi-source data fusion (operational, environmental, life-cycle data) to quantify real-time outage risks under multifactor coupling. Then, Latin hypercube sampling (LHS) generates wind-solar scenarios to characterize RES uncertainty. Finally, sequential Monte Carlo simulation integrates both for system-level assessment. A case study with 746 generation units of 9 types and a peak load of 75 000 MW showes that the proposed method accurately captured the impact of abnormal scenarios, i.e., voltage, frequency, load rate, and health status anomalies, and equipment aging.

title: An Enhanced Coverage Algorithm Using Meta-Heuristic Method for 3D Topology in UASN (Underwater Acoustic Sensor Network)
authors: Archana Senapati, Abhishek Kumar Dewangan, Mahesh Singh
publish: 2026 Sixth International Conference on Advances in Electrical, Computing, Communications and Sustainable Technologies (ICAECT)
abstract: Due to constant enlargement in the marine sector, nations across the globe are paying much inquisitiveness towards maritime rights as well as zest. Therefore, all around the world, UASNs (Underwater Acoustic Sensor Networks) is constantly becoming a more famed research arena. The sensor's deployment schemes have a momentous impact on UASNs performance evaluation. The effective deployment of the finite sensors within a 3-Dimensional (3D) setting has become a very critical and challenging job for the enlargement of the required coverage quality in real-time. In this article, the authors proposed an enhanced coverage algorithm (indicated as ECAMM) utilizing the meta-heuristic technique for 3D topology in UASN. This suggested scheme is one of the highest energy savers and all the sensing nodes within the coverage spectrum consume very minimal power in the real-time functioning. One of the main advantages of using the ECAMM technique is that it makes use of both the sleeping and waking strategies to reduce total energy usage. ECAMM provides total network lifespan (rounds) of 1100, 1400, 2500, 3500, 4900, 7800, 9000 over 130, 221, 347, 516, 733, 1000, and 1200 nodes, correspondingly, during the investigation. The findings reveal that the proposed ECAMM (Enhanced Coverage Algorithm using Meta-heuristic Method) algorithm outperforms significantly and overall energy expenditure is very minimal as well in comparison to the existing approaches. During the last few years, multifarious investigators developed a variety of algorithms for effective coverage in the UASNs environment. However, the prevailing schemes have umpteenth limitations such as higher energy expenditure and many more. Thus, for fixing the aforementioned downsides, there is a need to investigate more pragmatic algorithms for the UASNs coverage effectively in the future.

title: Transmission Line Developing Fault Identification Based on Computer Vision Technology
authors: Kaichen Li, Zengli Yang, Xiaozhou Huang, Youhuai Wang, Chao Ma, Zhenghao Wang, Xinsheng Lv, Jingyu Wang, Dongyuan Shi
publish: 2025 IEEE 9th Conference on Energy Internet and Energy System Integration (EI2)
abstract: Transmission lines are essential infrastructure for power transmission, and their safe and reliable operation is crucial to the stability of power systems. Faults on transmission lines, arising from various causes, display distinct characteristics. Instantaneous faults, such as those induced by lightning strikes, are generally amenable to reclosing operations. In contrast, developing faults, like those resulting from ice-covered insulator flashovers, often lead to reclosing failures. Due to the limited availability of real-world fault waveform data, this paper presents a MATLAB/Simulink-based simulation model to generate transient waveforms corresponding to lightning-induced faults and ice-covered insulator flashovers according to their respective mechanisms. Subsequently, the Gramian Angular Field (GAF) technique is employed to transform time-series voltage and current signals into GAF images. Furthermore, a transfer learning approach is adopted by utilizing a pretrained ResNet-50 network for fault identification. Experimental results confirm that the proposed method can effectively distinguish between instantaneous and developing faults, thereby providing a reliable foundation for reclosing decision-making.

title: Transient Fault Information Fusion Based High-speed Pilot Protection for UHV Transmission Lines
authors: Jianwei Yan, Kuihua Liu, Jianming He, Sibei Luo
publish: 2025 IEEE 9th Conference on Energy Internet and Energy System Integration (EI2)
abstract: With the transient fault information fusion, a high-speed pilot protection scheme for UHV transmission lines is proposed. The transient based pilot protection (TBPP) makes full use of fault polarity and direction information, which is able to protect the entire line in less than 5 ms. TBPP consists of transient starting element, polarity element, directional element and faulted phase selection element. Mathematical morphology is well utilized in the TBPP elements. A simulation model of Jindongnan-Nanyang-Jingmen UHV transmission project is established in ATP/EMTP. Simulation results demonstrate the feasibility of the proposed TBPP.

title: Research on Planning Methodology for Direct Green Power Connection Projects
authors: Junhui Liu, Yihan Zhang, Wenjing Zu, Yao Lu, Minghao Cao
publish: 2025 IEEE 9th Conference on Energy Internet and Energy System Integration (EI2)
abstract: Direct Green Power Connection holds practical significance for promoting the local consumption of renewable energy and better meeting enterprises' green energy demands. To facilitate the effective implementation of national specialized policies on direct green power connections, this research conducts a study on project planning methodologies based on user load profiles and renewable energy output curves, adhering to the "load-determined source" principle to design three scenarios: "no energy storage configured for renewables," "energy storage configured to meet basic constraints only," and "energy storage configured for optimal system economics," thereby scientifically determining renewable energy source types and installed capacities while proposing the system-economically optimal solution through comparative selection with rational energy storage allocation. Empirical analysis is conducted using a sample enterprise to develop feasible solutions considering local renewable energy resources, followed by impact analysis from three perspectives: end-users, grid companies, and investors. Finally, recommendations are provided to enhance project feasibility and economic efficiency—including comprehensive evaluation of direct-connection model cost-effectiveness, utilization of spot markets to increase revenue, and proactive adjustment of electricity consumption loads—offering critical references for project investment decision-making.

title: A feature selection method for power system dynamic early-warning using binary grasshopper optimization
authors: Wei Zhang, Meng Zha, Ziyan Jin, Xiaohui Yang, Qijin Hu, Shi Yu, Bin Li
publish: 2025 IEEE 9th Conference on Energy Internet and Energy System Integration (EI2)
abstract: Driven by the "dual carbon" goals, the renewable-dominated new power system poses greater demands for real-time and accurate dynamic security early warning, due to the inherent volatility and intermittency of renewables. To overcome the limitations of existing methods, such as inefficient feature selection and local optima entrapment in high-dimensional data scenarios, this paper proposes an enhanced feature combination optimization method. Our approach first employs the Maximum Information Coefficient to quantify feature importance for guiding the search. Subsequently, the Binary Grasshopper Optimization Algorithm, known for its strong global search capability, is utilized to evolve feature subsets by optimizing a fitness function that balances early-warning accuracy and feature subset size. Validation on a multi-regional power system congestion dataset demonstrates the method’s effectiveness, achieving a 98.37% early-warning accuracy while reducing computation time by 28.29% compared to traditional methods, offering a reliable solution for precise and efficient dynamic early warning.

title: Research on the Construction of Evaluation Indicators for Capacity Market and Comprehensive Evaluation Method
authors: Simin Geng, Wenyuan Huang, Yaxian Zheng, Xinhong Shi, Yanmin Guo, Suyan Long
publish: 2025 IEEE 9th Conference on Energy Internet and Energy System Integration (EI2)
abstract: With the comprehensive acceleration of the construction of China's electricity spot market, the development of new power systems has put forward higher requirements for the reliability of the power system. The design and implementation of capacity market mechanisms have become a key task in the current electricity market reform, but a systematic and scientific evaluation index system for the capacity market has not yet been established. This study innovatively starts from the four core dimensions of capacity market construction and operation, constructs a complete evaluation index system, and employs a combined weighting method that integrates subjective and objective approaches. To further validate the practical value of the theoretical framework, this study conducted empirical research based on actual operational data from a certain province. Through case analysis, it fully demonstrated the scientificity and feasibility of the proposed evaluation index system and comprehensive evaluation method, providing important theoretical support and practical reference for promoting the construction of China's capacity market.

title: Challenges and Approaches in Machine Learning for Climate Change Prediction: A Comprehensive Review
authors: Saima Khan, Rakesh Singh Rajput
publish: 2026 Sixth International Conference on Advances in Electrical, Computing, Communications and Sustainable Technologies (ICAECT)
abstract: Machine learning (ML) techniques have emerged as promising tools for climate change (CC) prediction and offer improved accuracy, efficiency, and practical implications across various sectors. However, ML-based climate change prediction methods face several challenges, including data quality and availability, complexity of climate systems, model interpretability, temporal and spatial scale issues, uncertainty quantification, extrapolation limitations, computational resources, bias and over fitting, integration with physical models, handling of extreme events, and model validation. Support Vector Regression (SVR) has shown promising results in climate change prediction using various datasets, such as ocean-atmospheric variables and wind speed data. SVR's ability of SVR to adapt to changing relationships between predictors and predictands makes it particularly suitable for climate change modeling. However, the success of SVR-based models often depends on proper data sampling, attribute selection, and parameter optimization. tis paper have compared the validation performance of SVR and Decision Tree (DT)-based CC for the Mumbai region temperature data. ML-based climate change predictions offer practical implications for enhancing adaptation strategies, improving urban planning, and addressing region-specific ecological challenges. based on study concluded that ongoing research, innovation, and practical AI-ML applications will be crucial in harnessing these technologies to effectively combat climate change consequences. The SVR improves RMSE by 0.181 and R-squared by 0.03, while Tree reduces MAE by 0.4382 and MAPE by 0.5%

title: Green Hydrogen as an Alternative Aviation Fuel in Asia and the Pacific: A Qualitative and Quantitative Analysis
authors: Xiaolong Liu, Ziming Chen, Yixiao Li, Monan Cheng, Jiadeng Li, Jun Onn Khor, Long Gao, Fei Xiao, Lizhong Yang
publish: 2025 3rd Asian Aerospace and Astronautics Conference (AAAC)
abstract: Green hydrogen can be used as a clean aviation fuel or serve as a key feedstock in the production of sustainable aviation fuel (SAF). To evaluate its long-term potential in aviation, this study forecasts green hydrogen prices in Asia and the Pacific. First, a comparative analysis of green hydrogen, blue hydrogen, white hydrogen, conventional jet fuel, and SAF reveals that green hydrogen offers significant advantages in carbon reduction and energy density, but its cost remains high. Representative airports in Asia and the Pacific region are then selected as hydrogen-refueling hubs by benchmarking passenger throughput and flight frequencies. Subsequently, the costs of green hydrogen are broken down into production, storage, transport, and refueling, and the impacts of policy subsidies and supply-demand dynamics are explicitly incorporated. Three time-series models are employed to project green hydrogen prices from 2025 to 2050. The USA has the highest overall green hydrogen prices, while India and Thailand have the lowest, with other countries in the Asia-Pacific region falling in between. USA green hydrogen is projected to reach 3.25 USD/MJ_chem in 2027, and decline to 0.9 USD/MJ_chem by 2050. The current green hydrogen remains too costly as an aviation fuel. Significant cost reductions in the future will depend on technological breakthroughs and stronger policy support.

title: Research on Optimal Scheduling of Wind-Solar-Hydrogen Storage Multi-Energy Complementary Microgrid Based on Improved Adaptive Lyapunov Hybrid Particle Swarm Optimization
authors: Guanyuan Li, Mao Yang, Yitao Li, Peng Sun, Jianyu Geng
publish: 2025 IEEE 9th Conference on Energy Internet and Energy System Integration (EI2)
abstract: Multi-energy hybrid microgrids enhance the stability and adaptability of power systems by integrating diverse renewable sources, thereby facilitating higher penetration of intermittent renewables. However, the inherent uncertainties of wind and solar power, coupled with complex system configurations and multiple constraints, pose significant challenges to optimal scheduling. This paper proposes a novel optimal scheduling method for a wind-solar-hydrogen storage multi-energy complementary microgrid, leveraging an Improved Adaptive Lyapunov-based Hybrid Particle Swarm Optimization (IAL-HPSO)algorithm. The system comprises wind turbines, photovoltaics, diesel generators, battery storage, a hydrogen energy system (electrolyzer, fuel cell, and hydrogen storage tank), micro gas turbines, and grid interconnection. A tri-objective optimization model is established, incorporating economic cost, environmental cost, and comprehensive cost, while considering power balance and operational constraints. The proposed IAL-HPSO algorithm introduces a Lyapunov-based adaptive parameter adjustment mechanism to enhance convergence speed and solution accuracy. Simulation results based on a northern Chinese industrial park microgrid demonstrate that the proposed method effectively coordinates multiple energy sources, mitigates renewable uncertainties through hydrogen-energy synergy, and achieves superior scheduling performance under various optimization targets.

title: Bridge: Leveraging Vision Foundation Models for Efficient Cross-Domain Remote Sensing Segmentation
authors: Bin Wang, Zeyu Chen, Zhi Li, Ling Lei, Yiguang Liu
publish: IEEE Transactions on Geoscience and Remote Sensing
abstract: Adapting large-scale Vision Foundation Models (VFMs), pretrained on natural images (NI), to remote sensing (RS) downstream tasks such as Domain Adaptation Semantic Segmentation (DASS) though Parameter-Efficient Fine-Tuning (PEFT) is a promising yet challenging direction. However, due to the large domain gap between NI and remote sensing images (RSI), as well as the inherent intra-domain variations in RS DASS task, existing PEFT methods struggle to achieve satisfactory adaptation. To address these challenges, we propose Bridge, a framework with small trainable parameters specifically designed for RS DASS. Bridge introduces a set of learnable Domain- Invariant Tokens that capture transferable knowledge shared across NI and RSI domains. To complement this, a Spatial Prior Module (SPM) is proposed to extracts multi-scale spatial priors that provide auxiliary guidance for Domain-Invariant Token Adaptation (DITA), which produces Domain-Specific Tokens. Finally, Domain-Aware Injection (DAI) injects the Domain-Specific Tokens into intermediate VFM features, aligning them with RS distributions and facilitating effective DASS task adaptation. With significantly fewer trainable parameters, Bridge efficiently transfers NI-pretrained VFMs to RS DASS task. Extensive experiments demonstrate that Bridge substantially outperforms state-of-the-art PEFT approaches. Remarkably, compared with the current SOTA, Bridge achieves a significant improvement in mIoU. The code will be released at Bridge.

title: Unveiling Deepfakes: A Frequency-Aware Triple Branch Network for Deepfake Detection
authors: Qihao Shen, Jiaxing Xuan, Zhenguang Liu, Sifan Wu, Yutong Xie, Zhaoyan Ming, Yingying Jiao, kui Ren
publish: IEEE Transactions on Dependable and Secure Computing
abstract: Advanced deepfake technologies are blurring the lines between real and fake, presenting both revolutionary opportunities and alarming threats. While it unlocks novel applications in fields like entertainment and education, its malicious use has sparked urgent ethical and societal concerns ranging from identity theft to the dissemination of misinformation. To tackle these challenges, feature analysis using frequency features has emerged as a promising direction for deepfake detection. However, one aspect that has been overlooked so far is that existing methods tend to concentrate on one or a few specific frequency domains, which risks overfitting to particular artifacts and significantly undermines their robustness when facing diverse forgery patterns. Another underexplored aspect we observe is that different features often attend to the same forged region, resulting in redundant feature representations and limiting the diversity of the extracted clues. This may undermine the ability of a model to capture complementary information across different facets, thereby compromising its generalization capability to diverse manipulations. In this paper, we seek to tackle these challenges from two aspects: (1) we propose a triple-branch network that jointly captures spatial and frequency features by learning from both original image and image reconstructed by different frequency channels, and (2) we mathematically derive feature decoupling and fusion losses grounded in the mutual information theory, which enhances the model to focus on task-relevant features across the original image and the image reconstructed by different frequency channels. Extensive experiments on six large-scale benchmark datasets demonstrate that our method consistently achieves state-of-the-art performance. Our code is released at https://github.com/injooker/Unveiling_Deepfake.
