title: A Lightweight Compact Cable-Driven Hip Exoskeleton with High Torque Capacity
authors: Jianquan Ding, Liping Wang, Jianyu Chen, Weihao Yin, Zhibo Jing, Jianda Han, Juanjuan Zhang
publish: IEEE Robotics and Automation Letters
abstract: Lower limb exoskeletons have shown great promise for enhancing mobility. Existing systems are limited by either excessive weight or insufficient torque output. In this study, we present a cable-driven hip extension exoskeleton featuring lightweight, compact, and compliant end-effectors with high-torque capacity. The system can provide up to 90 Nm (static) / 65 Nm (dynamic) assistance torque while the end-effector weighs only 1.46 kg. With a model-based feedforward controller combined with iterative learning, the system achieves a closed-loop bandwidth of 11.3 Hz and a torque tracking error with an average root-mean-square error of 3.3% of the peak torque. Preliminary experiments with unoptimized, experience-driven assistance demonstrated an average 44.4% reduction in gluteus maximus muscle activation across three walking speeds. The system is well suited for controlled indoor experiments and can be translated to portable on-board implementations, supporting diverse research and application scenarios.

title: Predicting Cognitive Performance from Three-Electrode Auditory Brainstem Responses Using Convolutional Neural Networks_supp1-3671708.pdf
authors: Hung Cao
publish: N/A
abstract: Age-related cognitive decline is a growing global concern, motivating the search for non-invasive, accessible biomarkers to support early detection and monitoring. Click-evoked auditory brainstem responses (ABRs), collected in routine clinical settings, offer a promising signal source. Building on prior evidence that ABRs relate to cognitive function, this study investigates whether raw human ABR waveforms can predict cognitive performance using deep learning without manual peak measurements. We used a dataset from 118 adults spanning a broad range of cognitive abilities, pairing each click-evoked ABR with a cognitive score. A one-dimensional convolutional neural network (CNN) was trained to learn time-series patterns directly from the raw signal. Model performance was evaluated using two, five, and ten-fold cross-validation and compared against traditional wave V metrics and a randomized-input baseline. After adjusting scores for age, the CNN achieved a mean area under the receiver operating characteristic curve of 0.77 ± 0.06, outperforming all benchmarks. To interpret model decisions, Gradient-weighted Class Activation Mapping (Grad-CAM) was applied. Three key latency windows were identified: 1.8 to 2.3 ms, 3.2 to 4.0 ms, and 4.9 to 6.5 ms. These correspond to canonical ABR waves I, III, and V, supporting the physiological relevance of the learned features and highlighting Wave III as a previously underutilized marker. Although limited to click stimuli from a single recording system, this work demonstrates that a CNN can extract meaningful features from raw ABRs collected using only three electrodes and predict cognitive status more accurately than traditional methods using the same dataset.

title: Toward a Smartwatch-Based System for Automated Social Interaction Monitoring in Autism
authors: Srinivas Goud Kalagotla, Fang Wang, Kirti Datla, Bradley Ferguson, David Q. Beversdorf
publish: IEEE Access
abstract: Autism Spectrum Disorder (ASD) is a complex neurodevelopmental condition that affects social interactions and communication. Traditional assessment methods are often manual, subjective - lack ecological validity and scalability. This study presents a fully automated, machine learning–based system for continuous monitoring of social interactions in individuals with ASD using voice and physiological data collected in naturalistic environments. The system leverages speaker diarization to quantify the duration the ASD individual speaks during real-life conversations. An enhanced deep learning–based diarization method built on pyannote-audio is used to identify the target speaker, with each recording beginning with a short voice sample from the participant for identification purposes. Audio and heart rate data are simultaneously collected via a standalone smartwatch app, requiring no phone or network connectivity during data capture. Data are then processed through a cloud-based pipeline. The system was validated in both controlled and real-world settings, including testing with ASD individuals. Results show that the system can accurately and effectively capture speaking time, identify speaker turns, and track physiological signals during social interactions. This approach provides an objective, scalable framework for social behavior assessment in ASD, with strong potential for clinical application and long-term, real-world monitoring.

title: StarVid: Enhancing Semantic Alignment in Video Diffusion Models via Spatial and SynTactic Guided Attention Refocusing_supp1-3668668.pdf
authors: Qi Mao
publish: N/A
abstract: Recent advances in text-to-video (T2V) generation with diffusion models have garnered significant attention. How ever, they typically perform well in scenes with a single object and motion, struggling in compositional scenarios with multiple objects and distinct motions to accurately reflect the semantic content of text prompts. To address these challenges, we propose StarVid, a plug-and-play, training-free method that improves semantic alignment between multiple subjects, their motions, and text prompts in T2V models. StarVid first employs large language models (LLMs) to perform two-stage motion trajectory planning based on the input prompt, providing object-level spatial priors that guide the generation process. These priors are then used to impose a spatial-aware loss that refines cross-attention (CA) maps, encouraging attention to be focused on distinct regions corresponding to different subjects. Furthermore, a syntax guided contrastive constraint is introduced to explicitly enhance the association between verbs and their corresponding nouns in the CA maps, thereby improving motion-subject binding. Both qualitative and quantitative evaluations demonstrate that the proposed framework significantly outperforms baseline methods, delivering videos of higher quality with improved semantic consistency.

title: SeFA: Seed-Filter Adaptation of Robust CNN Services for IoT Devices
authors: Chuntao Ding, Longquan Zhang, Junna Zhang, Yu Yang, Shangguang Wang
publish: IEEE Transactions on Services Computing
abstract: Using low-rank adaptation to fine-tune pre-trained neural network models has attracted widespread attention due to its advantages of low resource requirements, high precision, and no additional inference delay. However, most existing methods are designed for large language models based on the Transformer structure and lack adaptation to convolutional neural networks (CNN) widely used on Internet of Things (IoT) devices. In addition, IoT devices are usually deployed outdoors and collect large amounts of data that are affected by environmental conditions. Providing a highly robust CNN model is a prerequisite for providing high-quality services. To this end, this paper proposes a highly robust seed-filter adaptation method (SeFA) for pre-trained CNNs. SeFA introduces an adaptation branch with the same structure as the backbone network. In the adaptation branch, some filters are first designated seed filters and grouped. Then, additional filters are generated from the grouped seed filters and nonlinear transformation functions (NLFs) with different hyperparameters. The seed filters' parameters are updated during model training, and the NLF hyperparameters are randomly initialized and frozen. Both grouping seed filters and configuring NLFs with non-learnable hyperparameters can improve the model's robustness. This is because grouping seed filters can generate diverse filters on demand without increasing the model's complexity, and the NLFs' rules can regularize the model. It is worth mentioning that the number of fine-tuning parameters of the pre-trained model that can adapt to downstream tasks can be flexibly controlled by specifying the number of seed filters. The key idea of this paper is to propose SeFA with flexible, controllable, learnable parameters, high robustness, and adaptability to pretrained CNN models, thereby facilitating fine-tuning on resource-constrained IoT devices and providing highly robust visual services. Experimental results on the CIFAR-10, CIFAR-10-C, CIFAR-100, CIFAR-100-C, and Icons-50 datasets demonstrate that the proposed SeFA outperforms other state-of-the-art methods. Specifically, on the ResNet-152 model and the CIFAR-10-C dataset, the accuracy of our SeFA is about 7% higher than that of the full fine-tuning method. In addition, we verify the efficiency of SeFA-based models for fine-tuning and inference on the device.

title: Vector Field Augmented Differentiable Policy Learning for Vision-Based Drone Racing
authors: Yang Su, Feng Yu, Yu Hu, Xinze Niu, Linzuo Zhang, Fangyu Sun, Danping Zou
publish: IEEE Robotics and Automation Letters
abstract: Autonomous drone racing in complex environments requires agile, high-speed flight while maintaining reliable obstacle avoidance. Differentiable-physics-based policy learning has recently demonstrated high sample efficiency and remarkable performance across various tasks, including agile drone flight and quadruped locomotion. However, applying such methods to drone racing remains difficult, as key objectives like gate traversal are inherently hard to express as smooth, differentiable losses. To address these challenges, we propose DiffRacing, a novel vector field–augmented differentiable policy learning framework. DiffRacing integrates differentiable losses and vector fields into the training process to provide continuous and stable gradient signals, balancing obstacle avoidance and high-speed gate traversal. In addition, a differentiable Delta Action Model compensates for dynamics mismatch, enabling efficient sim-to-real transfer without explicit system identification. Extensive simulation and real-world experiments demonstrate that DiffRacing achieves superior sample efficiency, faster convergence, and robust flight performance, thereby demonstrating that vector fields can augment traditional gradient-based policy training with a task-specific geometric prior.

title: Bridging the Reality Gap in ASR for Low-Resource Dysarthric Speech: Evaluating Performance on Synthetic and Real Data_supp2-3673388.pdf
authors: Bozena Kostek
publish: N/A

title: Shuffler: A Novel Read Reclaim Scheme for Superblock Management in NAND Flash
authors: Jianwei Liao, Zhibing Sha, Yuanbo Li, Jun Li, Zhigang Cai, Francois Trahay
publish: ACM Transactions on Storage
abstract: None

title: A Non-Cascade Dual-Time-Scale Sliding Mode Control for High-Performance Vienna Rectifiers
authors: Yu Jiang, Yougui Guo, Bomin Huang, Zhixian Huang, Zicheng Nie, Xijun Yang, Fei Gao
publish: 2025 IEEE 9th Conference on Energy Internet and Energy System Integration (EI2)
abstract: To enhance the dynamic performance and disturbance rejection capability of Vienna rectifiers, this paper proposes a novel non-cascade control strategy based on dual-time-scale sliding mode theory. Unlike traditional cascaded structures restricted by bandwidth separation, the proposed method overcomes these limitations by fully leveraging the distinct fast (current) and slow (voltage) dynamics inherent in the rectifier. A dual-time-scale mathematical model is first established through singular perturbation theory. Based on this model, a composite sliding mode controller is systematically designed: a slow- subsystem SMC is developed for the DC voltage loop to ensure robust stability, while a fast-subsystem SMC is tailored for the AC current loop to guarantee rapid and precise tracking. Furthermore, a tracking differentiator is integrated to generate a smooth reference trajectory for the DC voltage, enabling an overshoot-free start-up. Comparative simulation results against the classic Sliding Mode Control validate the superior performance of the proposed method. The system achieves fast DC voltage regulation without overshoot, maintains high-quality sinusoidal input currents with near-unity power factor, and demonstrates exceptional robustness with minimal voltage dip and rapid recovery under load disturbances.

title: Personalized Drug Toxicity Risk Assessment Model Using Machine Learning
authors: Pooja Dhawane, Narendra Saryam, Yash Ankush, Pratik Madan, C. Puri, Piyush Patmase, Bhargav Bhujade
publish: 2026 Sixth International Conference on Advances in Electrical, Computing, Communications and Sustainable Technologies (ICAECT)
abstract: Adverse drug reactions (ADRs) pose a serious threat to patient safety, causing needless economic expenses to the healthcare system and creating unnecessary hospitalizations. The traditional toxicity tests do not take into account the personal. history- physiology, lifestyle and drug use. To address these shortcomings, we propose a personalized risk assessment approach built on Graph Neural Networks (GNNs). This model stratifies individuals into Normal, Low, or High risk categories of drug toxicity by examining demographic, physiological, and behavioral data. Unlike GNNs, which capture elaborate feature interdependencies, such as the duration of addiction, the functioning of various organs, and the frequency of substance use in a unified graph, traditional machine learning models compartmentalize features. Our model was trained on a synthetic behavioral-health dataset and achieved high accuracy alongside enhanced interpretability. We also designed a practical web-based interface for real-world application. This work demonstrates the transformative potential of GNNs in personalized ADRs and clinical decisionmaking.

title: Research on Electric Vehicle Charging Optimization Scheduling Method for Carbon Emission Reduction Based on Population Trajectory Data
authors: Yue Ma, Xiangsong Mao, Jie Yan, Yongqian Liu
publish: 2025 IEEE 9th Conference on Energy Internet and Energy System Integration (EI2)
abstract: This paper addresses the challenge of coarse granularity in existing electric vehicle (EV) travel data by proposing an improved modeling method based on population trajectory data. By refining probability models with day and location types, high modeling accuracy (0.966–0.999 fitting accuracy) is achieved. A carbon-oriented spatiotemporal optimization framework is developed, extracting flexible loads and solving the scheduling model with Gurobi to minimize emissions. Case results demonstrate significant carbon emission reductions (21.65% temporally, 76.47% spatially) alongside user cost savings, validating the method's effectiveness in promoting renewable energy integration and carbon reduction.

title: A Parameter Tuning Method for Voltage and Current Inner Loops in Grid-Forming Control
authors: Tianxiang Wu, Feng Ji, Huaiguang Gu, Lu Gao
publish: 2025 IEEE 9th Conference on Energy Internet and Energy System Integration (EI2)
abstract: Increased integration of renewable energy sources into power grids needs effective control strategies. Voltage loops and current loops are widely used in power systems, particularly for renewable energy grid integration. This paper presents a tuning methodology for calculating the PI parameters of voltage and current loops directly from the system’s component parameters. This approach eliminates the need for complex and time-consuming manual parameter adjustment. Finally, simulation results verify the effectiveness and performance of the proposed strategy.

title: Research on Thrust Vector Control Technology for Rotating Detonation Engines Based on Zoned Fuel Supply
authors: Zhenghao Wang, Dong Wang, Hu Ma
publish: 2025 3rd Asian Aerospace and Astronautics Conference (AAAC)
abstract: To combine the characteristics of the simple structure of a rotating detonation engine with the vector thrust capability, a numerical simulation of an annular rotating detonation engine was conducted to calculate various parameters of the engine's combustion chamber. By creating a fuel equivalence ratio difference in the inlet region, the thrust of the combustion chamber was analyzed. The results show that the zoned fuel supply scheme contributes to achieving thrust vectoring, providing the engine with limited lateral force and deflection torque.

title: A New Energy Prediction Error Characterization Method based on Probability Modeling and Quantile Regression
authors: Xin Zhang, Zifen Han, Ruonan Liao, Shen Lei, Kaicheng Zhu, Yaru Cai
publish: 2025 IEEE 9th Conference on Energy Internet and Energy System Integration (EI2)
abstract: To effectively address the characterization of uncertainty in renewable energy output, this paper proposes a hybrid approach integrating probability modeling and quantile regression for error representation. The methodology first employs kernel density estimation to fit probability distribution characteristics of renewable energy prediction errors across different time periods, weather conditions, and seasonal scenarios, thereby clarifying statistical patterns under various operational contexts. Subsequently, a LightGBM-based quantile regression model is applied to precisely quantify prediction error ranges through quantile estimation, effectively capturing extreme fluctuation intervals. Building upon this foundation, a comprehensive evaluation index system for renewable energy uncertainty is constructed. Through case studies using historical operational data from representative renewable energy facilities in Gansu Province, the proposed method demonstrates its capability to fully capture the fluctuation characteristics of renewable energy output, achieving comprehensive and accurate characterization of associated uncertainties.

title: Check DTC On Message Event for Automotive Industry Using CAN Protocol
authors: Florian-Aurelian Krech
publish: 2025 IEEE 21st International Conference on Intelligent Computer Communication and Processing (ICCP)
abstract: This study presents an algorithm designed for the Controller Area Network (CAN) protocol, which is capable of receiving both standard CAN frames and CAN Flexible DataRate (CAN FD) frames to identify active Diagnostic Trouble Codes (DTCs) within a vehicle. Given the pivotal role of the automotive sector in today’s industrial landscape, the research emphasizes autonomous diagnostics, predictive maintenance, and prescriptive maintenance methodologies — approaches that are critical not only for modern vehicles but also for broader Industry 4.0 contexts. Experimental results demonstrate a minimum improvement of 15% in data validation and interpretation accuracy. This enhancement is particularly significant in industrial and real-world applications, where the timeliness and reliability of malfunction detection are essential for operational efficiency and safety.

title: Construction and Application of Evaluation Metric System for Electric-Computing Collaborative Power Supply in Data Computing Centers
authors: Di Zhang, Kaiyu Chen, Wanjin Li, Xin Shi, Feng Gao
publish: 2025 IEEE 9th Conference on Energy Internet and Energy System Integration (EI2)
abstract: As a new type of critical infrastructure, the stability, flexibility, and economy of the power supply system in Data Computing Centers are essential for supporting the development of the digital economy. However, conventional power system evaluation methods struggle to adapt to the complex and dynamic operational demands of modern Data Computing Centers. To address this, this paper proposes a novel evaluation metric system specifically designed for flexible power supply architectures in Data Computing Centers. The system is structured across four key dimensions: Reliability, Flexibility, Economy, and Resilience, encompassing a total of 12 secondary indicators. These indicators cover critical parameters such as power supply availability, computing power loss rate, power response rate, power supply energy efficiency, and duration of guaranteed supply for critical loads. The paper details the design of both the conventional AC power architecture and an AC/DC hybrid power structure based on Energy Routers for Data Computing Centers. Furthermore, it provides the calculation models and assessment methodologies for each metric, followed by an illustrative analysis utilizing typical equipment reliability data. This comprehensive framework offers theoretical support and a decision-making basis for the planning, operation, and optimization of power supply systems in next-generation Data Computing Centers.

title: Distributed Renewable Energy Forecasting Based on CEEMD Multi-model Integration
authors: Tao Zheng, Lijuan Jiang, Chao Xue, Ruoyu Wang, Xiaolong Zhang
publish: 2025 IEEE 9th Conference on Energy Internet and Energy System Integration (EI2)
abstract: To address the issues of low prediction accuracy and poor real-time performance in distributed renewable energy power forecasting under the "carbon peak and carbon neutrality" strategy, particularly in response to the increasing uncertainty associated with complex geographic and meteorological conditions as well as new load integrations in the distribution network, this paper conducts an optimization study on multi-model fusion. We propose a composite model named CEEMD-XGBoost-LSTMELM: it employs CEEMD to decompose complex power series to reduce data complexity, utilizes XGBoost to select key features while eliminating redundant information, and integrates the advantages of LSTM for capturing long-term dependencies and ELM for rapid predictions. The model is built on various sub-sequences and combines the output results. Training data includes photovoltaic and wind power loads along with local meteorological data from a regional power grid from January to December 2024, with the load from January to February 2025 as the prediction target. The model’s performance is compared with LightGBM and linear regression models. Results show that the composite model significantly outperforms the comparative models in both relative mean square error (eRMSE) and maximum error (eMAX), demonstrating improved prediction accuracy and stability. The research findings provide technical support for dispatch optimization of distribution network transformer stations, enhancement of renewable energy absorption, and safe operation of power grids, contributing to regional energy transition and the construction of new power systems.

title: Slot Allocation for a Multi-airport System Considering Traffic Uncertainty
authors: Liu Fengfan, Hu Minghua, Zhang Qingxian, Yang Lei
publish: 2025 3rd Asian Aerospace and Astronautics Conference (AAAC)
abstract: Demand-Capacity Balance is pivotal to strategic-level slot allocation. However, the traffic of the airport airside and airspace is fraught with multi-dimensional uncertainty. This paper models slot execution deviation, taxiing, and flying times as random variables, characterizes multi-dimensional traffic uncertainty via joint chance constraints, and constructs a slot allocation model for multi-airport systems. Experimental results show that moderately relaxing the constraint of sector traffic can significantly reduce the schedule adjustment cost. Additionally, the " on-demand allocation" mechanism of the joint chance constraint further enhances the model’s flexibility and capacity utilization efficiency. Furthermore, we evaluate the robustness of the model from the perspectives of expected traffic and maximum traffic. The results show that even when the violation probability is increased to 0.4, the number of over-capacity time windows is reduced by 34.9% and 47.4% compared with the original schedule and traditional model, respectively, for expected traffic; meanwhile, in terms of maximum traffic, it is reduced by 20.6% and 22.3% relative to the same two baselines, with peak traffic decreasing by 25.4% and 8.9% against them, respectively. In summary, the model we proposed significantly improves the robustness of the strategic schedule.

title: Flexibility Considered Multiple-Time-Scales Optimization Scheduling of Island Microgrids
authors: Liming Xiao, Jun Zhou, Xiwei Fu, Juanhua Wang
publish: 2025 IEEE 9th Conference on Energy Internet and Energy System Integration (EI2)
abstract: With the increasing demand for clean renewable energy resources, microgrids have emerged as an effective solution for integrating distributed energy sources and improving energy utilization efficiency. However, islanded microgrid operation faces significant challenges due to the loss of main grid support and the intermittent nature of renewable energy sources. This paper proposes a multi-time scale optimal scheduling strategy for islanded microgrids that simultaneously considers economic efficiency and flexibility supply-demand matching degree. The proposed approach employs a two-stage optimization framework: first, day-ahead scheduling is performed to optimize microgrid economic operation while ensuring sufficient flexibility for handling net load fluctuations. Second, real-time optimal scheduling based on Model Predictive Control (MPC) is implemented. The effectiveness of the proposed strategy is validated through simulation studies on an islanded microgrid system comprising photovoltaic generation(PV), energy storage system(ESS), and micro gas turbine(MT). Results demonstrate that the proposed multi-time scale optimization approach can effectively balance economic operation and system flexibility.

title: Vision Transformer-Based Early and Real-Time Detection of Cowpea Mosaic Virus for Precision Farming
authors: Parthasarathy Seethapathy, Kannan M, E Madesh, Arjun S, K S Harisankar, Santhosh S
publish: 2026 Sixth International Conference on Advances in Electrical, Computing, Communications and Sustainable Technologies (ICAECT)
abstract: Plant disease is one of the major problem in agriculture sector which reduces the yield and economic loss. Among various diseases, Cowpea Mosaic Virus (CPMV) causes a serious hazard to cowpea cultivation and significantly reduce the yield and quality of the product. Accurate detection and on-time recommendation is necessary for effective disease management. In this study the AI-based deep learning framework YOLOv8 used for early and stage-specific detection of CPMV. Real-time dataset consists of healthy and varying severity-level cowpea leaf images is collected under different lighting and climate conditions. The open-source software Label Studio is used for annotation. Python and data science libraries are used to create YOLOv8 model. After training, performance evaluation parameters like precision, recall, and mean AveragePrecision (mAP) are used to assess model efficiency. In this study the trained model achieved mAP scores approaching 0.95. This result shows the effectiveness of the model to identify healthy and infected leaves at different disease stages. This work highlights the potential of YOLOv8 in precision agriculture.

title: Feature Extraction for Anomaly Detection in Power Grids Based on Multi-Source Data Fusion Analysis
authors: Qiuquan Deng, Cuiyun Luo, Yin Wu, Guangming Li, Xiejin Ling, Zhencheng Liang, Yuan Zeng, Yuhao Xie, Junzhi Ren
publish: 2025 IEEE 9th Conference on Energy Internet and Energy System Integration (EI2)
abstract: The modern power system faces challenges including weak grid structures, equipment overloads, and difficulties in accommodating renewable energy. With the widespread integration of power electronic devices, grid dynamics have grown increasingly complex, r posing significant challenges to traditional analytical approaches. The extensive deployment of wide-area measurement systems and other data acquisition terminals has significantly enhanced the observability of grid. High-frequency data provide valuable insights into previously hidden dynamics, thereby enhancing both system security and operational efficiency. Moreover, big data technologies enable operators to anticipate and mitigate potential faults in advance. However, the surge of heterogeneous data sources also introduces significant challenges in synchronization, processing, and anomaly detection. To address these issues, this paper proposes an intelligent identification method for identifying abnormal operating states in power grids based on multi-source data fusion analysis. The objective is to overcome the limitations of single-source data, improve the accuracy and timeliness of anomaly detection, and provide intelligent recognition and prediction support for complex grid operating environments, thereby strengthening the security and stability of power system operation.

title: A Typhoon Post-Disaster Distribution Network Restoration Method with Energy Storage as the Core
authors: Sifan Xie, Lu Zhang, Ning Zhou, Ziya Xing
publish: 2025 IEEE 9th Conference on Energy Internet and Energy System Integration (EI2)
abstract: The frequent occurrence of typhoon disasters poses a severe threat to the safe operation of distribution networks. Due to issues such as weak network frameworks and insufficient emergency resources, low-voltage distribution networks face significantly increased difficulties in post-disaster recovery. This paper proposes a typhoon post-disaster distribution network restoration method with energy storage as the core, aiming to enhance the power supply resilience of distribution networks under extreme weather conditions. Firstly, by characterizing the source-load characteristics of distribution networks before and after typhoon disasters, this study analyzes the impact mechanism of typhoon disasters on distribution network lines, sorts out typical fault scenarios in the aftermath of typhoons, and interprets the demand changes of critical loads after disasters. On this basis, with the goal of maximizing the recovery volume of critical loads, a fault optimization model utilizing energy storage as the key restoration means is constructed. Finally, a typhoon post-disaster distribution network restoration method with energy storage at its core is proposed.

title: Machine Learning with Wrapper Methods Approach for Feature Selection
authors: Yadavendra Kumar Cheruvu, Hemanta Kumar Bhuyan
publish: 2026 Sixth International Conference on Advances in Electrical, Computing, Communications and Sustainable Technologies (ICAECT)
abstract: Machine learning has provided many diverse approaches to solve various problems. One such approach is feature selection, which plays a vital role in machine learning for many data analysis tasks. Although feature selection is not a new approach, it is necessary to select specific features from a pool of features based on data analysis. The primary objective of this feature selection approach is to reduce dimensionality with effective accuracy performance. Thus, the machine learning model is considered, incorporating wrapper methods that effectively evaluate the dataset and aim to achieve better performance than existing methods. The proposed model is demonstrated using a specific dataset and evaluated with various machine learning methods that incorporate wrapper approaches. The results are compared with themselves, and a select number of features are chosen from the feature sets of different datasets. According to the result analysis, the Bagging Regressor method performed better than the other methods (more than 98%).

title: Research on DG Site Selection and Capacity Optimization for Low-Voltage AC/DC Systems
authors: Zhenfeng Li, Hua Bai, Zhen Liu, Xingxin Chen
publish: 2025 IEEE 9th Conference on Energy Internet and Energy System Integration (EI2)
abstract: Aiming at the problems of voltage out of limit, three-phase imbalance and economic decline caused by high permeability DG connected to low-voltage AC/DC system, this paper proposes a DG location and capacity optimization method considering source load uncertainty. Firstly, the probability models of PV (Beta distribution), wind power (Weibull distribution) and VSC steady-state model are constructed to reveal the influence mechanism of DG output randomness on the system; Secondly, based on Latin hypercube sampling (LHS) and alternating iteration method, a stochastic power flow calculation method for AC/DC distribution network is established to accurately solve the stochastic power flow distribution; Then, taking the annual investment cost and minimum network loss as the objective, considering the constraints of branch power flow, VSC operation and DG output, a multi-objective programming model is constructed, and the solution is obtained by using ichpso to realize the collaborative optimal configuration of DG and VSC. The calculation example of 415 V low-voltage system in Australia shows that when VSC is close to the load center and DG is centrally arranged, the daily investment cost of the system is reduced to 379400 yuan, saving 2.3%~3.4% compared with other schemes, and the node voltage fluctuation is controlled in the range of 0.96~1.05 p.u., which verifies the effectiveness of the proposed model in improving economy and voltage stability, and provides theoretical support for DG planning of low-voltage AC/DC system.

title: A Scalable 4 Kelvin 24×24 LED Matrix for KIDs Focal Plane Mapping
authors: F. Cacciotti, P. de Bernardis, A. Coppolecchia, I. Di Nitto, L. Lamagna, S. Masi, A. Paiella, G. Pettinari
publish: IEEE Transactions on Applied Superconductivity
abstract: Large arrays of cryogenic detectors are essential for enhancing the sensitivity of Cosmic Microwave Background (CMB) telescopes. Lumped Element Kinetic Inductance Detectors (LEKIDs) are widely used in modern CMB experiments due to their intrinsic frequency-domain multiplexing, low noise, and fast response time. Depending on the operating wavelength, waferscale arrays can host up to several tens of thousands of LEKIDs densely packed within a readout bandwidth of a few hundred MHz, enabling the readout of up to a thousand detectors on a single feedline. However, fabrication imperfections and nonideal effects introduce small, random fluctuations in the expected resonant frequencies, sometimes causing resonance swaps and disrupting the one-to-one correspondence between each detector's resonant frequency and its position by design in the focal plane. Precise knowledge of this mapping is crucial for identifying inactive pixels, troubleshooting, optimizing detector yield, and accurately determining the sky region each detector observes. In this work, we present the development and testing of a 24 × 24 LED matrix, with a 2.5 mm pitch, operating between 4 K and 1 K with negligible dissipation, emitting at 660nm, and designed to spatially map densely packed LEKID arrays. The measurement principle relies in sequentially switching on the LEDs of the matrix and measuring the effect of ballistic phonons produced by LED light absorption in the substrate, which locally perturb the superconducting state of the detectors. We tested the system, including custom analysis pipeline, with an array of 313 LEKIDs on a 2 inches wafer and a 2.44 mm pitch in the horncoupled configuration. Possible extensions of the method based on localized infrared illumination at 1550nm are briefly outlined as a perspective for future work.

title: A 3 K Testbed to Screen Superconducting Circuits Based on a Commercial Probe Card with 140 Contacts
authors: Erin E. Maloney, Carl D. Reintsema, Johnathon D. Gard, Malcolm S. Durkin, W. Bertrand Doriese, John P. Biesecker, Richard A. Lew, Tammy J. Lucas, Nathan J. Ortiz, Daniel R. Schmidt, Michael R. Vissers, Joel N. Ullom, Douglas A. Bennett
publish: IEEE Transactions on Applied Superconductivity
abstract: The NIST Quantum Sensors Division has developed a novel cryogenic testbed to qualify the two-dimensional superconducting quantum interference device (SQUID) time division multiplexer (TDM) devices that will provide front-end readout of the transition-edge-sensor detector array in the Xray Integral Field Unit (X-IFU) imaging spectrometer onboard NewAthena. This screening system uses a custom probe card with 140 tungsten-cantilever probe tips to connect to the device under test. Probing instead of wirebonding is required to enable subsequent bump bonding of TDM chips to X-IFU carrier wafers. It also allows testing of wafers before dicing, which allows some fabrication steps to occur after testing, including potential defect repair. The system is able to accommodate up to 150 mm wafers but is currently being used to test circuits on 76.5mm (‘3 inch’) wafers. The probe card is aligned and lowered to contact the DUT while at room temperature, so the system does not need or allow cold mechanical motion or cold optical access. The system is cooled by a commercial, two-stage, Gifford-McMahon cryomechanical cooler. Cooling to 3 K is achieved in less than 12 hours and the acquisition of a full set of test vectors takes less than an hour. Including re-warming to room temperature, the full test cycle can be run on a 48-hour cadence from one device to the next. With tens of test cycles successfully completed, this system has proven to be robust, reliable, and efficient.

title: NeuroRhythm: EEG-Based Adaptive Music Therapy for Calming Autism Hyperactivity
authors: Jaeyalakshmi M, Nithish Rao P, Nishal I P
publish: 2026 Sixth International Conference on Advances in Electrical, Computing, Communications and Sustainable Technologies (ICAECT)
abstract: Children with Autism Spectrum Disorder don't always find it easy to communicate socially or manage their emotions, and many shows repetitive behaviors along with heightened anxiety. This paper proposes NeuroRhythm, which brings together realtime EEG monitoring and adaptive music generation to help these children find emotional calm. The proposed approach differs from existing methods instead of playing pre-recorded tracks, the system reads brain wave patterns and adjusts musical elements like tempo, pitch, and duration right away to match what's happening in the user's mind at that moment. This creates a personalized listening experience that changes based on actual brain activity as it happens. Traditional music therapy stays the same throughout a session, but our closed-loop system responds dynamically when the user's anxiety levels or cognitive state shifts. Initial experimental observations indicate that soundscapes informed by EEG data can effectively support emotional self-regulation in children with ASD, and this approach provides a practical, non-invasive option for therapeutic intervention. The system shows real promise as an accessible way to connect neuroscience research with actual clinical practice, and it doesn't require expensive equipment or extensive training to use.

title: An Optimization Method for Equipment Rotation and Material Balance of Agricultural Processing Loads Oriented to Demand Response
authors: Yi Zhang, Gangui Yan, Shuaishuai Lv
publish: 2025 IEEE 9th Conference on Energy Internet and Energy System Integration (EI2)
abstract: The aggravating peak-valley difference and insufficient regulation capability caused by the high penetration of renewable energy have become critical challenges for power system operation. Agricultural processing loads, characterized by tightly coupled production processes and continuous material flow, have not yet been fully explored for their demand response potential. To address the issue of load concentration during peak hours, this paper proposes an optimization method for equipment rotation and material balance of agricultural processing loads oriented to demand response. The method is based on the principle of material conservation, utilizing the buffering capacity of intermediate silos and the segmental operating characteristics of processing equipment to decouple continuous processes. A minute-level mixed-integer linear programming (MILP) model is established, incorporating material balance, process sequence, and power fluctuation constraints, and coupled with a time-of-use (TOU) electricity pricing mechanism. The objective is to achieve simultaneous peak shaving and electricity cost minimization under production continuity constraints. Case studies show that, while ensuring production continuity and output stability, the optimized strategy reduces peak-period load by approximately 40% and electricity cost by 42%, demonstrating both effectiveness and economic feasibility. The proposed method provides a practical engineering pathway for agricultural processing enterprises to participate in demand response and electricity markets as flexible loads.

title: Microstructure Evolution and Mechanical Properties of Al-Cu-Mg-Ag Alloy are Investigated Through Heat Treatment
authors: Yujie Chang, Zhi He, Zhifei Xu, Min Xu, Xiaoming Yin, Yan Wang, Wenhui Li
publish: 2025 3rd Asian Aerospace and Astronautics Conference (AAAC)
abstract: Al-Cu-Mg-Ag alloy, as a high-specific-strength and excellent heat-resistant aluminum alloy material, is widely used in the aerospace industry as a primary structural material. Wire arc additive manufacturing (WAAM) exhibits significant advantages in the fabrication of large aerospace components due to its outstanding design freedom. In this study, the heat treatment of Al-Cu-Mg-Ag alloy fabricated by WAAM was investigated to explore its high-temperature performance. The size, type, and distribution characteristics of precipitation strengthening phases under different solid solution and aging conditions were compared and analyzed, and the variation in mechanical properties under different heat treatment conditions was summarized. The results indicate that the optimal solid solution condition is 560°C for 6 h, and the optimal aging condition is 175°C for 6 h. After optimized heat treatment, the average ultimate tensile strength of the alloy at 200°C reaches 310.3 MPa, which is 79.0% of the room-temperature strength of the as-deposited alloy, demonstrating excellent high-temperature stability.

title: An Overview of Cyber Security on Virtual Power Plants: Threats and Challenges
authors: Yuchen Zeng, Min Gao, Xiang Xu, Xiaomin Lu, Kun Qu, Huijuan Tan, Kaishun Xiahou
publish: 2025 IEEE 9th Conference on Energy Internet and Energy System Integration (EI2)
abstract: With the growing proportion of new energy resource, virtual power plants (VPPs) have become an effective solution for integrating distributed energy. However, VPPs have complex structures and many access devices, making them vulnerable to attackers. This paper systematically sorts out the various attack forms and technical challenges encountered by VPPs in the current operation process. Firstly, by integrating the existing research results, this paper deeply analyzes the existing solutions to these problems and their corresponding application effects. On this basis, combined with trusted computing, this paper proposes an operating architecture of VPP and discusses the future research direction of VPP, thus providing a theoretical reference and research ideas for improving the safety, stability and intelligent development of VPPs.

title: Research on Dynamic Evaluation Method for Multi-Time-Scale Regulation Capability of Thermo-Mass Energy Storage-Load-Waste Heat Combination
authors: Xintong Ma, Guangyu Sun, Shubo Hu, Bing Xie, Mengyu Wang, Yaou Tian
publish: 2025 IEEE 9th Conference on Energy Internet and Energy System Integration (EI2)
abstract: Aiming at the problems of power fluctuation, high curtailment rate, and insufficient power grid stability faced by the new power system under high-proportion renewable energy integration, this paper proposes a multi-time-scale regulation system combining thermo-mass energy storage, hydrogen production load, and waste heat, and constructs a dynamic evaluation method for its regulation capability. Firstly, by integrating the characteristics of water electrolysis for hydrogen production technologies, a coupled model of hydrogen energy storage hydrogen production load-waste heat recovery is established. Secondly, a multi-time-scale regulation strategy covering second-level, minute-level, and hour-level is designed. Finally, a dynamic evaluation system including indicators such as response speed, renewable energy consumption rate, and waste heat utilization rate is proposed, and verified based on the modified IEEE-RTS79 case. The results show that the combined regulation system can reduce the amplitude of power grid frequency fluctuation by 60%, decrease the wind and solar curtailment rate from 18% to 7%, and achieve a waste heat utilization rate of 82%, which verifies the effectiveness of the evaluation method and provides support for the operation of the new power system.

title: A Review of Frequency Control Standards in New Power Systems
authors: Zining Liu, Wenying Wu, Suwei Zhai, Wenyun Li, Yiping Chen, Shiyang Li, Yihui She, Yuming Liu
publish: 2025 IEEE 9th Conference on Energy Internet and Energy System Integration (EI2)
abstract: The rapid growth of renewable energy in modern power systems has reduced system inertia and frequency regulation capability, posing new operational challenges and accelerating the shift to new power systems. Renewable sources such as wind and solar are variable and often interfaced through power electronics, limiting the natural inertial response provided by conventional synchronous generators. Consequently, single-range frequency standards are insufficient to ensure system stability and economic efficiency. This paper surveys international frequency control standards, focusing on frequency range, system inertia, and Rate of Change of Frequency (RoCoF). Segmented frequency standards adopted in regions such as the UK, Australia, and the EU are reviewed, covering steady-state, quasi-steady-state, and maximum instantaneous frequency bands, along with their operational requirements. Inertia limits and RoCoF thresholds are analyzed to maintain system resilience. The influence of generation mix, device capabilities, load characteristics, and electricity market mechanisms on standard setting is also discussed. The study provides insights for developing adaptive frequency control standards to ensure secure and efficient grid operation under high renewable penetration.

title: MLP-Based Intelligent Control Signal Estimation for Buck–Boost DC–DC Converters Using SHAP Analysis
authors: Vishnu Kumar Sahu, Mahesh Singh, Shruti Tiwari
publish: 2026 Sixth International Conference on Advances in Electrical, Computing, Communications and Sustainable Technologies (ICAECT)
abstract: This work introduces a complete machine-learning-driven scheme to make proper predictions and analysis of a nonlinear buck-boost DC-DC converter duty-cycle. The model of multilayer perceptron (MLP) is built based on the important electrical characteristics, such as input voltage, feedback voltage, inductor current, capacitor voltage, which allows making strong data-based predictions without using typical averaged or small-signal models. Through performance analysis, it is shown that the performance of the process is very much tracked accurately between the predicted and measured duty-cycle values with very few deviations of errors as confirmed by time domain plots, error histograms and correlation analysis. Moreover, SHAP-based explainability methods are used to measure the level of importance of features and understand the behavior inside the model, which show that the most important parameters affecting the duty cycle regulation is <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$V_{\text {in }}$</tex> and <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$i_{L}$</tex>. The findings verify that the suggested machine learning framework offers a valid, interpretable, and computationally efficient answer that can be used in the context of real-time power-electronic control, intelligent monitoring and renewable-energy scenarios.

title: Momentum Oscillators in Action: A Unified Framework for Share Market Price Prediction
authors: Sridhar Udayakumar, E. Anbalagan
publish: 2026 Sixth International Conference on Advances in Electrical, Computing, Communications and Sustainable Technologies (ICAECT)
abstract: Stock markets are dynamic and volatile, and they are often inexact and unpredictable, which is why having reliable forecasting tools is so important for traders and analysts. Momentum oscillators such as RSI, Stochastic, CCI, Williams %R, ROC and MACD are widely used by traders to calculate how fast and how strongly prices are moving, giving them an idea of when a market reversal might be on the horizon. These tools record the speed and strength of the price movements indicating potential trend reversals. We present a consolidated framework that integrates multiple oscillators into ML and DL pipelines. Historical OHLCV records, enriched with oscillator-based features, are used to train models including Random Forest, XGBoost, and LSTM. Our comparative analysis shows that integrating oscillators consistently improves forecasting accuracy, reducing directional error rates by up to 14% compared with single-indicator or raw-price baselines. The findings confirm that oscillators, when combined within an ML-DL architecture, provide not only stronger predictive performance but also practical interpretability for real-world trading. This work thus offers a bridge between traditional technical analysis and modern algorithmic trading systems.

title: MMC Fault Defense and Protection Scheme Based on a Hierarchical-Time Adaptive Framework and Heterogeneous Ensemble Learning
authors: Xiaochen Hu, Dahai Zhang, Pengfei Tang, Dan Wang
publish: 2025 IEEE 9th Conference on Energy Internet and Energy System Integration (EI2)
abstract: The fault defense and protection of modular multilevel converters (MMCs) face fundamental challenges due to the multi-level and multi-time-scale characteristics of fault types, where a single strategy often fails to balance response speed and high accuracy. To address this issue, this paper proposes a fault defense and protection scheme based on a "hierarchy–time" adaptive framework combined with heterogeneous ensemble learning. First, sensitive protection triggering is realized by monitoring the peak-to-peak value of submodule capacitor voltages and their differential features. Subsequently, a two-stage adaptive diagnosis process is employed: a lightweight shared classifier provides rapid assessment of fault severity based on early fault current data, while in the second stage, diagnostic resources and strategies are dynamically allocated according to the preliminary results. Finally, the system executes differentiated protection responses. Simulation results show that the proposed method achieves fault identification rates of over 95% and 97% at the submodule and arm levels, respectively, with a full-process response time of less than 3 ms for severe faults. This scheme significantly enhances the fault defense capability of MMCs under complex operating conditions, providing a theoretical framework and solution for fast and reliable protection of MMCs and other power electronic equipment.

title: Low wind Output Process Forecasting Based on the Integrating of Diffusion Model and Generative Adversarial Network
authors: Wuyang Xiao, Jie Yan, Han Wang, Xuanru Chen, Shihua Liu, Shuang Han, Yongqian Liu
publish: 2025 IEEE 9th Conference on Energy Internet and Energy System Integration (EI2)
abstract: Low wind output process (LWOP), which often occur under extreme weather conditions, exhibit low frequency and few-sample characteristics, making challenges of direct modeling with historical data. To address this, sample augmentation is required to obtain sufficient high-quality data for model training. This study proposes a LWOP forecasting method based on the integrating of Diffusion Model and Generative Adversarial Network (GAN). Firstly, the historical LWOP is identified based on indicators such as duration, extremal, and average value. Then, a sample generation model of LWOP integrating Diffusion Model and GAN is developed, where the generator of GAN is replaced by Diffusion Model. The discriminator evaluates the authenticity of samples generated by Diffusion Model, while generator aims to deceive the discriminator through adversarial training. Finally, a forecasting model of LWOP based on transfer learning is built, where the generation samples are used as input for model pre-training, and historical samples are used as input for model fine-tuning. Operation data of ten wind farms in a certain province of China is used for case study; BiLSTM, DNN, and TCN are employed to verify the effectiveness and robustness of the proposed method. The results show that, the proposed method can improve the forecasting accuracy of LWOP for 4.24% to 81.08% compared with traditional methods, when root mean square error is used as evaluation index.

title: Automatic Multi-Class Segmentation and Landmark-Based Osteotomy Planning for Hip Arthroplasty
authors: Spinciu Ioana - Teodora, Stefana Duă, Petean Corina - Elena, Alina Elena Sultana, Doru - Daniel Cristea, Eduard Liciu
publish: 2025 IEEE 21st International Conference on Intelligent Computer Communication and Processing (ICCP)
abstract: This paper presents an automated framework for anatomical landmark detection and multi-class segmentation of the femur from CT images, designed to support preoperative planning in total hip arthroplasty (THA). Using a manually annotated subset of the TotalSegmentator dataset, we trained and evaluated two deep learning architectures-Attention U-Net and V-Net 2D-on 11 distinct femoral substructures, including head, neck, and trochanters. The proposed pipeline integrates segmentation with automatic extraction of key anatomical landmarks and enables precise computation of the femoral neck resection plane directly from model predictions. Both networks achieved high Dice scores (above 0.96), with Attention U-Net offering faster convergence and V-Net showing greater stability on imbalanced classes. Our results demonstrate that deep learning-based segmentation combined with landmark detection can significantly enhance accuracy and reproducibility in THA planning workflows.

title: Comparative Analysis of Deep Learning Models for Human Movement Assessment in Physical Rehabilitation
authors: Camelia Lazar, Crina Grosan, Maria Iuliana Bocicor
publish: 2025 IEEE 21st International Conference on Intelligent Computer Communication and Processing (ICCP)
abstract: Automated evaluation of rehabilitation exercises is essential for supporting home therapy and long-term recovery for patients with physical impairments. This study investigates several deep learning-based approaches for the assessment of gesture correctness, comparing two original Long Short-Term Memory based architectures with three deep convolutional models previously proposed in the literature for time series classification. While most existing approaches use separate models for individual exercises, we employ a single, general model for all gestures. The experimental evaluation was performed using two different evaluation strategies: a standard split, with mixed subjects in training and testing and a more realistic leave-one-subject-out (LOSO) procedure, that performs the tests on unseen individuals. We employed two rehabilitation datasets in our experiments and the results show that LSTM-based models generally outperform convolutional architectures, particularly for the LOSO strategy.

title: Optimal Wind Farm Layout Design for Polar Regions: A Global-Local Collaborative Optimization Approach
authors: Yingqi Sun, Bin Wang, Yu Su, Hongbin Sun, Tianqing Yu
publish: 2025 IEEE 9th Conference on Energy Internet and Energy System Integration (EI2)
abstract: Polar wind resources are abundant, yet conventional planning is ineffective under extreme climate and construction constraints. We develop a polar-adapted planning framework that: (i) models wakes via the Blondel super-Gaussian with logarithmic wind shear and STF turbulence; (ii) unifies turbine number-type-position through our proposed virtual turbine method; and (iii) optimizes a mixed-integer objective targeting unit cost of electricity.A global-local strategy combines an improved genetic algorithm for exploration with SLSQP for sub-meter positional refinement. In real sites, Station 1 (13 turbines) attains 4.812% wake loss and cost of 447.441 units/GWh. Station 2 (6 turbines) attains 4.085% and 662.982 units/GWh, respectively - well below results from baseline methods.Mixed large-small layouts thus offer practical and economical advantages for polar deployment.

title: Deep Reinforcement Learning-Based Multi-Source Energy Management for Hypersonic Vehicle Mode Transition
authors: Yiqun Wang, Xingjian Jin, Lei Yu, Si Gao, Jian Lu, Jiecheng Fu
publish: 2025 3rd Asian Aerospace and Astronautics Conference (AAAC)
abstract: During the mode transition phase of hypersonic vehicles, significant changes in the propulsion system's state lead to instability in the electric energy supply, necessitating the development of a multi-source, adaptive, and high-energy-efficiency power generation solution. This paper proposes a multi-source energy management strategy based on DRL (Deep Reinforcement Learning) and the DQMR-DDPG (Double Q-learning with Multi Replay Buffer for DDPG) algorithm, aimed at minimizing fuel consumption during power generation while meeting system performance and safety constraints. The improved DQMR-DDPG algorithm is proposed to solve the optimal multi-source energy allocation strategy. By introducing innovations such as state-partitioned multi-stage experience replay, uncertainty-weighted dual Q-network fusion, and adaptive gradient soft update strategies, the algorithm effectively addresses issues such as poor strategy adaptability, unstable value estimation, and inaccurate convergence under strong nonlinear changes in system efficiency. Finally, simulations validate the effectiveness of the proposed multi-source energy management strategy.

title: Modified Virtual Synchronous Generator Control Strategy for Black-Start of Energy Storage Systems
authors: Shihao Cheng, Zhenxin Li, Naiyuan Liu, Zhifeng He, Jiaxin Wang, Xudong Ji, Yidong Yang, Zhenglong Sun
publish: 2025 IEEE 9th Conference on Energy Internet and Energy System Integration (EI2)
abstract: The increasing grid integration of converter-based generation is challenging the stability of the power system. Converter-based wind and photovoltaic (PV) power generation exhibit intermittency and instability. This has increased the risk of wide-area blackouts. Therefore, power systems require energy storage systems (ESS) with grid-forming (GFM) converters to provide black-start services for power restoration after blackouts. However, the conventional virtual synchronous generator (VSG) control strategy fails to consider the governor characteristics of synchronous generators, which significantly prolongs the frequency recovery time of black-start. This paper applies a VSG control strategy considering virtual speed governing control, enabling faster frequency recovery and enhancing the frequency support of black-start processes. Virtual speed governing control improves frequency stability by continuously tracking angular frequency changes and adjusting the angular frequency to reach the reference value. Finally, the modified control strategy is verified through simulations.

title: Optimal Scheduling of Photovoltaic Accommodation Based on the Regulation Potential of Flexible Resources
authors: Qishan Xv, Feng Zhang, Runze Yin, Huahua Jiang, Zongsheng Li, Xia Lv
publish: 2025 IEEE 9th Conference on Energy Internet and Energy System Integration (EI2)
abstract: The large-scale integration of high-penetration photovoltaic (PV) generation has exacerbated the curtailment problem in distribution networks, severely restricting the effective utilization of PV power. Unlocking the regulation potential of flexible and controllable resources has become a critical approach to enhancing system accommodation capability and reducing PV curtailment. This paper focuses on distribution networks with high PV penetration and investigates the operational characteristics and regulation potential of flexible resources, including PV inverters, static var generators (SVGs), energy storage systems (ESSs), and electric vehicle battery swapping stations (BSSs). On this basis, a coordinated optimal scheduling model is established with the dual objectives of maximizing PV accommodation and minimizing regulation cost. The proposed model is solved using a genetic algorithm. Simulation results demonstrate that the proposed scheduling strategy effectively exploits the complementary characteristics of multiple types of flexible resources, significantly alleviates PV curtailment, and achieves cost reduction while enhancing PV accommodation.

title: Low-Carbon Dispatch Optimization of Multi-Energy Systems Considering Wind-Solar Power Generation Uncertainty
authors: Cuiling Jia, Qiming Wan, Zitian Liu, Hongrui Wang, Chenxi Fu, Xiaoran Yu, Xin Shi
publish: 2025 IEEE 9th Conference on Energy Internet and Energy System Integration (EI2)
abstract: With the increasing penetration of wind and solar power and other renewable energy sources into energy systems, the intermittency and volatility of their power output can cause significantly heightened uncertainty in system energy supply. This poses substantial challenges for the operational optimization of multi-energy systems (MES) incorporating wind and solar power. To address this, this paper establishes models for the key equipment within a wind-solar integrated MES. Building upon this foundation, a low-carbon dispatch optimization model for the MES is developed, explicitly addressing the stochastic nature of wind and solar energy resources. The Benders decomposition is used to solve the model, through which achieving coordinated low-carbon optimal scheduling between energy sources and loads. Finally, the proposed approach is tested on an MES within an industrial community. The test results validate the feasibility of the approach, demonstrating its ability to promote source-load coordination and interaction. Furthermore, it ensures stable system operation while effectively enhancing both economic efficiency and environmental friendliness.

title: Specialized Information Retrieval with Improved Reliability via LLM Finetuning
authors: George Catalin Robotin, Radu Razvan Slavescu
publish: 2025 IEEE 21st International Conference on Intelligent Computer Communication and Processing (ICCP)
abstract: When implementing an automated support system, organizations tend to rely on complex, computationally intensive architectures. This paper presents the results we obtained when exploring the possibility of developing a cost-effective question answering framework for this purpose. We explored how various models, context configurations, and personas (instructions that guide the system in terms of answer purpose and/or tone) impact customer satisfaction regarding the respective answers. Three Llama-3, 7 billion parameter version variants, fine-tuned across different combinations of training parameters, extra context provision and personas were evaluated. The solution obtained was implemented as a chatbot focused on questions regarding a specific university. Its performance was assessed based on the customer satisfaction scores obtained from university students. Results demonstrate that fine-tuned models with 1000 iterations (the point at which the model starts to overfit slightly) achieved up to 85% customer satisfaction when combined with both extra context and persona features, compared to 25% for the base model alone. These findings suggest that effective specialized chatbots could be implemented without complex Retrieval Augmented Generation architectures and provide practical guidance for organizations which aim for simplicity, cost-effectiveness and replicability in their systems’ development.

title: Automated Deep Learning Framework for Multi-Label Skin Cancer Classification Using Balanced Dermatoscopic Data
authors: Bukke Deekshith Naik, Konduru Mahesh Raju, Jawalkar Balaji Rao, Mude Nagarjuna Naik, Sriramkumar R, Lakshmanan M
publish: 2026 Sixth International Conference on Advances in Electrical, Computing, Communications and Sustainable Technologies (ICAECT)
abstract: Skin cancer is the most common cancer worldwide, and early diagnosis greatly increases treatment success. Diagnosis is usually carried out using visual inspection and biopsy, which are both slow and subjective. Recently, the development of artificial intelligence has progressed, and deep learning has shown great potential in the analysis of medical images and the support of clinical diagnosis. This paper investigates the use of deep learning models in the automatic detection of skin cancer. A custom-built convolutional neural network is used, and a fine-tuned ResNet-50 architecture is also implemented. The models were trained and tested on the HAM10000 dataset, which contains 7 different classes of dermatoscopic images. The dataset was balanced and data augmented for the indexes of model performance to be calculated. The CNN model achieved 98.18% accuracy, and the ResNet-50 model achieved an accuracy of 93.25%. It was shown that the simpler CNN model achieved a more efficient classification on this task. This work also highlights the implications that deep learning could have on dermatologists with respect to allowing for quicker and more reliable diagnosis of skin cancers, especially due to the large reduction in human error that could be achieved, leading to quicker diagnosis times and improved diagnosis rates.

title: A Roadmap for Modern Code Review: Challenges and Opportunities
authors: Zezhou Yang, Cuiyun Gao, Zhaoqiang Guo, Zhenhao Li, Kui Liu, Xin Xia, Yuming Zhou
publish: ACM Transactions on Software Engineering and Methodology

title: Large-Signal Instability Mechanism and Stability Enhancement of the Submarine DC Power System
authors: Yi Lei, Yang Li, Xiangyang Wu, Feng Zhao, Zhen Mei, Zhikang Shuai
publish: IEEE Transactions on Circuits and Systems I: Regular Papers
abstract: Due to extreme operational conditions, submarine DC power systems are prone to large-signal instability. Therefore, it’s essential to investigate the underlying physical mechanisms and develop effective stabilization strategies. This paper first establishes a large-signal model of the submarine DC power system. Incorporating the physical characteristics and control dynamics in the system, the large-signal stability boundary is defined by using the mixed potential function method, and key factors influencing the stability boundary are identified. Subsequently, the instability mechanisms, along with the effect mechanisms of these key factors’ variations on large-signal stability, are further analyzed based on the model of source-side and load-side power interactions. The analysis indicates that suppressing the transient reduction in the voltage conversion ratio of the submarine converter is critical to maintaining stability. Motivated by this insight, a switching frequency compensation strategy is proposed, and it demonstrates improved disturbance-rejection performance compared with conventional approaches. Finally, simulations and hardware-in-the-loop experiments are conducted to validate the stability analysis results and the effectiveness of the stability enhancement strategy under large disturbances.

title: Model-Free Fault-Tolerant Control for Constrained Wastewater Treatment Processes
authors: Hong-Gui Han, Han-Qian Hou, Hao-Yuan Sun, Jun-Fei Qiao
publish: IEEE Transactions on Industrial Informatics
abstract: In wastewater treatment processes (WWTPs), actuator faults (such as those of blowers) or input signal saturation may cause the constrained dissolved oxygen concentration (DOC) to exceed the preset range, which not only compromises the operational efficiency of WWTPs but also increases the risk of membrane fouling. To solve these challenges, the model-free fault-tolerant control (MFFTC) strategy based on a log-type barrier nonlinear transformation function (LNTF) is proposed. First, to directly incorporate the DOC constraint bounds into the MFFTC design process and achieve more robust boundary control, an LNTF is constructed. Based on this, an extended compact form dynamic linearization model for WWTPs subject to actuator faults is developed. Then, considering that the faults are unknown, the faults are estimated by the fuzzy neural network. Second, the integrated dynamic compensator (IDC) is designed to overcome the input saturation problem by combining the fault estimation information. In addition, MFFTC is designed based on the IDC to compensate for the impact of actuator faults. Third, the bounded input bounded output stability analysis is provided to ensure that the MFFTC maintains the DOC within the constraints under actuator faults and saturation. Finally, the correctness and effectiveness of MFFTC are verified through extensive simulations in the membrane bioreactor model.

title: Complexity-Constrained D-SCFlip Decoding
authors: Anna Fominykh, Daria Ustinova, Alexey Frolov, Kirill Shabunov
publish: IEEE Open Journal of the Communications Society
abstract: Dynamic successive cancellation flip (D-SCFlip) decoding has emerged as a low-complexity alternative to successive cancellation list (SCL) decoding for polar codes, bridging the performance gap between conventional successive cancellation (SC) and SCL decoding. While D-SCFlip enhances SC decoding with adaptive bit-flip capabilities, its practical efficiency remains constrained by several factors, including the rapid growth of the search space with code dimension, lack of spatial error localization, and uniform resource allocation for correcting bit error decisions. This work presents a complexity-constrained D-SCFlip decoding algorithm that leverages segmented cyclic redundancy check (CRC) as a tool for fine-grained complexity budgeting, enabling dynamic allocation of fixed computational resources based on localized error information. First, we adapt the segmented CRC approach to the D-SCFlip context, replacing the global CRC with localized checks across optimally sized segments to constrain the flip search space to specific error regions. Second, we introduce a computational complexity budgeting mechanism that efficiently allocates decoding resources across segments based on their error susceptibility and correction potential. Our contribution also includes rigorous optimization of segment configuration specifically designed for complexity-constrained decoding. Simulation results demonstrate that our approach achieves better block error rate performance compared to conventional D-SCFlip decoding under equivalent worst-case complexity constraints, with gains of 0.05-0.14 dB across various code rates and lengths.

title: BFA++: Hierarchical Best-Feature-Aware Token Prune for Multi-View Vision Language Action Model
authors: Haosheng Li, Weixin Mao, Zihan Lan, Hongwei Xiong, Hongan Wang, Chenyang Si, Ziwei Liu, Xiaoming Deng, Hua Chen
publish: IEEE Robotics and Automation Letters
abstract: Vision-Language-Action (VLA) models have achieved significant breakthroughs by leveraging Large Vision Language Models (VLMs) to jointly interpret instructions and visual inputs. However, the substantial increase in visual tokens, particularly from multi-view inputs, poses serious challenges to real-time robotic manipulation. Existing acceleration techniques for VLMs, such as token pruning, often result in degraded performance when directly applied to VLA models, as they overlook the relationships between different views and fail to account for the dynamic and task-specific characteristics of robotic operation. To address this, we propose BFA++, a dynamic token pruning framework designed specifically for VLA models. BFA++ introduces a hierarchical pruning strategy guided by two-level importance predictors: an intra-view predictor highlights task-relevant regions within each image to suppress spatial noise, while an inter-view predictor identifies critical camera views throughout different manipulation phases to reduce cross-view redundancy. This design enables efficient token selection while preserving essential visual cues, resulting in improved computational efficiency and higher manipulation success rates. Evaluations on the RoboTwin benchmark and real-world robotic tasks demonstrate that BFA++ consistently outperforms existing methods. BFA++ improves the success rate by about 10% on both the <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$\pi _{0}$</tex-math></inline-formula> and RDT models, achieving speedup of 1.8× and 1.5×, respectively. Our results highlight that context-sensitive and task-aware token pruning serves as a more effective strategy than full visual processing, enabling faster inference and improved manipulation accuracy in real-world robotic systems.
