title: Spatial Short-Term Fourier Transform Based Single-Channel Single-Fiber 3D Shape Sensing
authors: Guochong Qiu, Danqian Cao, Yanjin Zhao, Wei Wang, Hongbin Liu
publish: IEEE Robotics and Automation Letters
abstract: Accurate three-dimensional (3D) shape sensing is vital for continuum robots in minimally invasive surgery. Conventional optical fiber methods depend on multi-fiber or multicore configurations, increasing integration complexity and associated costs. Single-fiber approaches support miniaturization, but struggle to decouple 3D bending and twist. We propose a single-channel single-fiber framework based on the spatial short-term Fourier transform (SSTFT) for real-time 3D reconstruction. A helically wrapped fiber encodes multiple deformation modes into periodic strain patterns, which localized Fourier domain analysis converts into curvature, direction, and twist parameters. These parameters feed a piecewise constant curvature and torsion model to efficiently reconstruct the backbone. Experiments on a 1.45 m sensor achieve average shape errors of 2.15 % (bending), 5.32 % (3D helix), and 7.90 % (twist). Compared to multi-fiber Frenet–Serret methods, our approach improves accuracy and robustness while reducing system complexity, demonstrating a promising low-cost, miniaturized shape sensing approach with potential applications in surgical navigation.

title: Hyper-Compression: Model Compression via Hyperfunction
authors: Feng-Lei Fan, Juntong Fan, Dayang Wang, Jingbo Zhang, Zelin Dong, Shijun Zhang, Ge Wang, Tieyong Zeng
publish: IEEE Transactions on Pattern Analysis and Machine Intelligence
abstract: The rapid growth of large models' size has far outpaced that of computing resources. To bridge this gap, encouraged by the parsimonious relationship between genotype and phenotype in the brain's growth and development, we propose the so-called hyper-compression that turns the model compression into the issue of parameter representation via a hyperfunction. Specifically, it is known that the trajectory of some low-dimensional dynamic systems can fill the high-dimensional space eventually. Thus, hyper-compression, using these dynamic systems as the hyperfunctions, represents the parameters of the target network by their corresponding composition number or trajectory length. This suggests a novel mechanism for model compression, substantially different from the existing pruning, quantization, distillation, and decomposition. Along this direction, we methodologically identify a suitable dynamic system with the irrational winding as the hyperfunction and theoretically derive its associated error bound. Next, guided by our theoretical insights, we propose several engineering twists to make the hyper-compression pragmatic and effective. Lastly, systematic and comprehensive experiments confirm that hyper-compression enjoys the following PNAS merits: 1) Preferable compression ratio; 2) No post-hoc retraining; 3) Affordable inference time; and 4) Short compression time. It compresses LLaMA2-7B in an hour and achieves close-to-int4-quantization performance, without retraining and with a performance drop of less than 1%. We have open-sourced our code in https://github.com/Juntongkuki/Hyper-Compression.git for free download and evaluation.

title: Deep Learning-Driven Segmentation of Unseen Objects in Indoor Robotic Environments
authors: Ying Zhang, Maoliang Yin, Qiao Zhang, Cui-Hua Zhang, Simon X. Yang, Zhijun Li, Changchun Hua
publish: IEEE Transactions on Multimedia
abstract: For robots operating in unstructured indoor environments, the ability to accurately perceive and interact with invisible objects is crucial because it is infeasible and impractical to assume that every object in the environment is modeled. Despite advances in this direction, recognizing unseen objects remains a challenging perceptual task. The success of deep learning (DL) in many fields has promoted the development of DL-based methods to address this challenge. This paper proposes a comprehensive review of DL-driven Unseen Object Instance Segmentation (UOIS). To the best of our knowledge, this paper is the first to present the state-of-the-art solutions for UOIS, since existing reviews focused on either known object segmentation, on learning-based network models, or on some part of these, and little effort can be found on DL-based UOIS. In addition to conducting a comprehensive evaluation of existing literature on UOIS, we also classify DL-based UOIS methods according to their technical characteristics. After that, popular synthetic and benchmark datasets for UOIS are outlined, and a performance comparison of DL-based UOIS on the datasets is given. Finally, current challenges are discussed, and potential research opportunities are suggested.

title: Thermal Design Guidelines for Multifinger GaN HEMTs
authors: Daniel C. Shoemaker, Seokjun Kim, Husam Walwil, Bill Zivasatienraj, Puneet Srivastava, Isaac Wildeson, Sukwon Choi
publish: IEEE Transactions on Electron Devices
abstract: Gallium nitride (GaN) high-electron-mobility transistors (HEMTs) are crucial for high-power applications, but their performance is often limited due to self-heating effects, particularly in multifinger layouts where thermal crosstalk exacerbates the temperature rise. To address this, a systematic study was conducted to establish thermal design rules for the layout of these devices. Aseries of multifinger GaN HEMTs on a 6H-SiC substrate with varying gate-to-gate (GG) pitch, channel length, gate width, and number of channels was fabricated and characterized to quantify the impact of each parameter on self-heating. The temperature rise of the centermost channel was measured using micro-Raman thermometry and validated using a thermal model based on measured thermal properties. By increasing the GG pitch of a <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$12\times 50~\boldsymbol {\mu } $</tex-math> </inline-formula>m device from 19 to <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$50~\boldsymbol {\mu } $</tex-math> </inline-formula>m, the thermal resistance was found to reduce by 41.9% at a dissipated power density of 12W/mm. For devices with an identical total gate periphery of <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$600~\boldsymbol {\mu } $</tex-math> </inline-formula>m, a layout with more, narrower fingers (<inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$16\times 37.5~\boldsymbol {\mu } $</tex-math> </inline-formula>m) exhibited superior thermal performance, offering a 32.6% reduction in thermal resistance compared to a <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$4\times 150~\boldsymbol {\mu } $</tex-math> </inline-formula>m configuration. The findings of this work provide guidelines for optimizing the layout of multifinger GaN HEMTs without costly cooling schemes.

title: An Electric Current Field Source Reconstruction Method for Coordinate Positioning of Pulmonary Interventional Surgical Actuator Terminal
authors: Wei Zhang, Jingang Wang, Pengcheng Zhao, Wei He, Qi Jiang, Hekai Yang, Haiting Xia, Xiaotian Wang
publish: IEEE Transactions on Biomedical Engineering
abstract: The advancement of intelligent surgery has imposed greater requirements on the precision and real-time performance of pulmonary minimally invasive surgical navigation. However, existing intraoperative navigation techniques, including optical tracking, X-ray imaging, and magnetic resonance imaging (MRI), have inherent limitations such as inadequate real-time performance, complicated workflows, strong equipment dependency, and restricted visual fields. These constraints hinder the ability of interventional surgeries to provide continuous and stable three-dimensional coordinate feedback in deep, non-line-of-sight environments. Therefore, this study proposes an electric current field source reconstruction method for determining the terminal coordinates of surgical actuators. An electric current is injected from the tip of the surgical instrument, creating an electric field within the human tissue. The potential measured by surface electrodes are then used to reconstruct the current source coordinates, enabling real-time and active sensing of the surgical probe coordinates. A mathematical model for electric current field-based coordinate positioning was developed, involving analyses of the forward and inverse problems as well as coordinate reconstruction. Random single-point positioning simulations were conducted, and a 16 + 1-electrodes experimental platform was constructed for coordinates navigation tests to evaluate positioning and navigation performance. In addition, dynamic positioning experiments of multiple physiological tissues were carried out to assess the robustness and anti-interference capability of the proposed method. Experimental results indicate that the positioning error remains within 2 mm under single-point, linear, and curved trajectory conditions, satisfying the precision requirements for intraoperative navigation. This method significantly improves the accuracy and safety of surgical positioning and navigation, thereby holding substantial engineering significance and clinical value for the advancement of intelligent surgical systems.

title: Real-time Signal Processing on Transition Edge Sensor for Confocal Spectral Microscopy
authors: Koki Shirota, Takahiro Kikuchi, Kazuki Niwa, Tetsuya Tsuruta, Nobuhiko Nomura, Daiji Fukuda
publish: IEEE Transactions on Applied Superconductivity
abstract: Broadband confocal spectral imaging in life-science microscopy often requires high excitation power to secure sufficient signal-to-noise ratio, which can induce phototoxicity and photobleaching. A transition-edge sensor (TES) offers high-efficiency, low-dark-count, single-photon energy-resolving detection; however, under continuous-wave excitation, the microsecond-scale TES pulse decay makes the readout susceptible to pulse pile-up, degrading both energy resolution and throughput. Here we developed a practical TES-based confocal spectral imaging platform by implementing a trapezoidal filtering algorithm as a linear time-invariant convolution on a field-programmable gate array (FPGA) for real-time pulse-height analysis. The FPGA synchronizes TES pulse-height information with scan metadata and streams the data to a host PC to assemble a spectral image cube. Using an 848 nm pulsed laser, we calibrated the pulse-height bin to photon energy with a third-order polynomial and converted energy histograms to wavelength via a Jacobian transformation. The proposed TES laser-scanning microscope (TES-LSM) successfully acquired spectroscopic images of fixed bovine pulmonary artery endothelial cells stained with Alexa Fluor 488 at an excitation power of 112 nW, achieving an image signal-to-noise ratio exceeding 30. In repetition-rate tests, the trapezoidal filter maintains nearly constant performance (<inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$E/\Delta E \approx 5$</tex-math></inline-formula>) up to 100 kHz and enables photon-number discrimination up to 120 kHz, outperforming an optimal filter at high count rates. These results demonstrate real-time, pile-up-tolerant TES readout for low-power confocal spectral imaging.

title: A Digital Twin Model Adaptation Method based on the Uncertainty Quantification for Power Electronics Converters
authors: Hao Bai, Ning Mao, Zhen Yao, Xinyang Li, Wentao Jiang, Rui Ma, Yang Zhou, Shuai Mao, Fei Gao
publish: IEEE Transactions on Power Electronics
abstract: To address the evolution challenge and improve the fidelity of power electronic digital twin, this paper proposes a model adaptation method based on uncertainty quantification (UQ) for power electronic converters. The UQ framework accounts for both parametric and model-form uncertainties and synthesizes their combined effects. Sensitivity analysis of uncertain variables is conducted to identify the adaptation approach. In addition, a Bayesian inference approach with an improved Markov Chain Monte Carlo (MCMC) sampling technique is introduced to update the posterior distribution of model parameters using experimental measurements. A Buck converter is employed as a case study. Experimental validation on a Buck prototype demonstrates the effectiveness of the proposed method, achieving an average uncertainty reduction of 61% after digital twin adaptation.

title: SD-GPON: A Dynamic Prioritization for Efficient Traffic Management Based on Controlling DSCP
authors: Nahlah Abdulrahman Alkhalidi, Fouad Ali Yaseen, Hamed S. Al-Raweshidy
publish: IEEE Access
abstract: Existing Gigabit Passive Optical Network (GPON) networks exhibit weaknesses in their traffic management abilities, specifically in the dynamic prioritization of data flow according to the policy constraints imposed by network administrators. In legacy GPON, the real-time priority process is achieved between the Optical Line Terminal (OLT) and Optical Network Unit (ONU) to manage different types of traffic like voice and video. The precedence process in GPON could be complicated to manage, particularly in networks with multiple traffic types. As a result, latency may adversely affect network performance. To tackle these shortcomings, we introduced a Software-Defined GPON (SD-GPON) approach by taking benefits of the SDN and virtualization techniques to manage data flow prioritization dynamically. Our proposal permits an entire control prioritization of data flow by modifying the Differentiated Service Code Points (DSCP) classes via a central vision of the Software-Defined Networking (SDN) controller to forward data based on its flow tables. In addition, we used Python programming language to virtualize the OLT to facilitate traffic management by the SDN Controller (SDNc), Mininet simulation to emulate the proposed network, and the ONOS platform controller to apply instances on the proposed network. The analysis of the proposed scheme revealed the superiority of the SD-GPON over GPON networks upstream and downstream for audio and video traffic by approximately 36%.

title: Evolutionary Game Dynamics in Tripartite Crowdsourcing with Incentive Regulation
authors: Zishuai Zhu, Shijia Hua, Linjie Liu, Xiaojie Chen
publish: IEEE Transactions on Knowledge and Data Engineering
abstract: Crowdsourcing, which decomposes tasks or projects through the internet platform and outsources them to a large number of people, has gained significant academic and industry interest due to its efficiency. Although prior theoretical studies have employed evolutionary game theory to study crowdsourcing systems, existing studies have predominantly focused on the game interactions between crowdsourcing worker and requester, often neglecting the platform as a stakeholder. Here we propose an evolutionary game model for tripartite crowdsourcing, accounting for bounded rationality and potential collusion between platform and crowdsourcing worker, with incentives designed to counteract free-riding and deceptive feedback. Our theoretical results indicate that the system can achieve a desired state where worker is certain to choose to provide high-quality work, platform is certain to implement diligent monitoring, and requester is certain to adopt an integrity strategy. Moreover, when the platform consistently adopts the monitoring strategy, the system exhibits oscillatory dynamics, with the probabilities of the worker providing high-quality work and the requester adopting an integrity strategy fluctuating within a bounded interval. Finally, we present numerical examples to validate our theoretical results.

title: Orthogonal Ray Projection: A Tangent-Space Visual Measurement Model for Robust Visual-Inertial Odometry
authors: Bing Han, Tuan Li, Yuezu Lv, Weisong Wen, Zhipeng Wang, Chuang Shi
publish: IEEE Robotics and Automation Letters
abstract: The reprojection error in Visual-Inertial Odometry (VIO) suffers from high nonlinearity due to perspective division, which degrades estimator consistency and robustness, particularly under large depth uncertainty. To address this, we propose a novel visual measurement model, the Orthogonal Ray Projection Error (ORPE), which is formulated in the tangent space of the observation ray. By minimizing the orthogonal distance between the estimated landmark and the measurement ray, ORPE decouples the measurement error from the scalar depth, rendering the residual function linear with respect to the feature position. We derive the exact analytical Jacobians and an uncertainty propagation model, integrating ORPE into both the MSCKF-based OpenVINS and the optimization-based ORB-SLAM3 frameworks. Simulations confirm that ORPE achieves geometric linearity for features, while significantly reducing the system nonlinearity with respect to camera pose. Extensive real-world experiments demonstrate that the proposed method significantly improves trajectory accuracy and estimator consistency in challenging weak-parallax scenarios, while maintaining computational efficiency comparable to standard approaches. To facilitate reproducibility and further research, we release our source code implemented in both OpenVINS and ORB-SLAM3 frameworks at our public repository.

title: Iron Loss Analysis in Amorphous Alloy High-Speed Permanent Magnet Motors Utilizing a Preisach Hysteresis Based Method
authors: Wei Li, Shiyi Liu, Tianran He, Lixun Zhu
publish: IEEE Transactions on Energy Conversion
abstract: Amorphous alloy materials are highly interested for high-speed applications because of low iron loss and high magnetic permeability, in comparison with conventional silicon steel materials. The iron loss in the stator of high-speed permanent magnet (HSPM) motors is difficult to be measured directly, and it increases with working frequency regardless of the material used. The accurate calculation of HSPM motor iron loss is significant for the design and analysis of the high-speed motor. In this paper, a calculated method of iron loss for high-speed motors is proposed by combining the ring specimen measurement, Preisach hysteresis model and finite element analysis. The magnetic properties of two ring specimens are measured, both of which are manufactured using the same processing technology as motor cores. The database of frequency-dependent Everett functions for the Preisach hysteresis model is established based on measurement data. An identification algorithm based on the symmetric minor hysteresis loops of the Preisach hysteresis model is adopted. The iron loss of an amorphous alloy and a silicon steel HSPM motors are calculated using the proposed method, from which the iron loss differences due to different materials are quantitatively analyzed. To verify the analysis results, motor experiment is executed by utilizing the high-speed airflow test platform and the non-magnetized motor experiment platform. The performance of amorphous alloy and silicon steel HSPM motors are tested at the same operating frequency, including total losses and efficiency etc. The air friction and mechanical losses in two motors are measured through non-magnetized motor experiments, and proved to be equal. The validity and accuracy of proposed method is verified with comparison results of loss separation model and motor experiments, which demonstrates the applicability of Preisach hysteresis model for calculating iron losses in high-speed motors.

title: Design and performance research of a spindle-integrated rotational piezoelectric testing system for upset force measurement during welding processes
authors: Mingyu Yuan, Shilu Mai, Jinlong Liu, Jin Wang, Xuejian Peng, Qingzeng Meng, Jun Zhang, Wei Liu, Zongjin Ren
publish: IEEE Sensors Journal
abstract: Accurate monitoring of high-frequency upset force during friction stir welding (FSW) is a critical step for optimizing welding process parameters and ensuring joint quality. However, traditional platform-type force gauges cannot measure the upset force in the bobbin tool friction stir welding (BT-FSW) process, which poses a technical challenge. To resolve this issue, a high-frequency spindle-integrated rotational piezoelectric testing system (SIR-PTS) mounted on the equipment spindle was designed. This system uses piezoelectric sensors as measuring elements and comprises a force gauge, a built-in signal conditioning circuit, and monitoring software. First, a theoretical mechanical model under rotational conditions was established to determine the dimensional parameters of the piezoelectric quartz wafers. Subsequently, ANSYS finite element simulation was utilized to analyze the static structural characteristics and dynamic response of the testing system. Meanwhile, a modular hardware circuit integrating charge conversion, filtering, and wireless transmission functions was developed. A series of verification experiments were conducted to evaluate the system performance. The experimental results demonstrate that the system exhibits a static linearity error of 0.64% and a repeatability error of 0.34%. Its main-direction natural frequency is 2082.52 Hz, which meets the requirements of high-frequency dynamic testing. These results confirm that the system can realize high-precision, real-time dynamic monitoring of upset force in BT-FSW processes.

title: Design and Systematic Assessment of A Novel Underactuated Assistive Hand Exoskeleton_supp1-3673112.mp4
authors: Long Cheng
publish: N/A
abstract: This study presents an innovative hand exoskeleton based on a linkage-transmission mechanism, aimed at supporting individuals with motor impairments in executing grasping activities essential for daily life. An underactuated mechanism leveraging redundant degrees of freedom is proposed, enabling the exoskeleton to adaptively grasp objects of varying shapes and sizes. The mechanical design ensures kinematic compatibility between the finger joints and the exoskeleton by establishing appropriate constrained kinematic chains. Its kinematics and quasi-statics are systematically analyzed to optimize the mechanical design. To evaluate the functional performance of the assistive hand exoskeleton, a systematic assessment framework is developed, incorporating three dimensions: subjective perception, kinesiology, and physiological response. Two experimental paradigms were conducted using the hand exoskeleton prototype with six healthy participants. Six assessment metrics (motion transparency, grip similarity, muscle activation, muscle fatigue, the System Usability Scale (SUS), and the Rating of Perceived Exertion (RPE) scale) were employed to assess the exoskeleton’s functionality. Experimental results demonstrate that the proposed exoskeleton effectively aids users in grasping various objects while significantly reducing physical exertion and fatigue.

title:
Finite-time Adaptive FeedForward Fractional-order RISE
                    <sup>α</sup>
                    Control of an Actuated Ankle-Foot Orthosis
authors: Oussama Bey, Hala Rifai, Ahmed Chemori, Yacine Amirat, Samer Mohammed
publish: IEEE Transactions on Automation Science and Engineering
abstract: This paper presents a new finite-time fractional-order Robust Integral of the Sign of the Error (RISE) control of an actuated ankle-foot orthosis (AAFO) to assist in plantar-flexion and dorsi-flexion movements of the ankle joint; within an assistance-as-needed paradigm. The proposed approach incorporates a nonlinear disturbance observer (NDOB) to estimate the human torque alongside other external torques acting on the AAFO-wearer system. In complement to the estimated human effort and the other external torques, the control input torque provided by the AAFO is based on two main terms, (i) a smooth adaptive finite-time fractional-order RISE term built based on fractional integral and derivative calculus to ensure the tracking performance, and (ii) an adaptive feedforward term to compensate for the system nonlinearities and reduce the effect of measurement noise on the control signal. A new augmented fractional-order filtered error is designed to modulate the convergence of the tracking ankle joint position and velocity errors and the adaptive parameters. The adaptation mechanism of the proposed fractional RISE also adopts fractional dynamics. A finite-time stability analysis of the whole proposed control approach is demonstrated. The effectiveness of the whole proposed controller is evaluated through real-time experiments on five healthy subjects. Comparison, in terms of tracking performance shows that the proposed controller outperforms several benchmark approaches and robustness tests are also conducted to validate its effectiveness.

title: Class-Imbalanced Learning via Multi-Task Multi-Objective Feature Selection
authors: Ruwang Jiao, Naoki Masuyama, Yusuke Nojima
publish: IEEE Transactions on Artificial Intelligence
abstract: Class-imbalanced and high-dimensional data pose significant challenges in machine learning, often leading to learning models that underperform on minority classes and struggle with the curse of dimensionality, respectively, and these two issues are inherently interconnected. This paper simultaneously addresses both class imbalance and high dimensionality through a multi-task multi-objective feature selection method. To be specific, the proposed method establishes two related yet distinct feature selection problem formulations for a given task, each focusing on different aspects of overall and minority classification performance. By selecting important features from the original imbalanced data set, the proposed method avoids the need to artificially equalize class distribution during training. Additionally, the cross-task genetic transfer operator enables the reuse of valuable feature subsets evolved from separate searches, thereby improving both minority class accuracy and overall model performance. Experimental results on 17 class-imbalanced data sets show that the proposed method achieves better results in terms of overall classification performance and minority class accuracy than the compared state-of-the-art methods, particularly on the high-dimensional data sets.

title: IEEE BITS the Information Theory Magazine Information for Authors
authors: N/A
publish: IEEE BITS the Information Theory Magazine

title: Multibeam High Throughput Satellite: Hardware Foundation, Resource Allocation, and Precoding
authors: Rui Chen, Wen-Xuan Long, Bingqian Wang, Yuan He, Ruijin Sun, Nan Cheng, Gan Zheng, Dusit Niyato
publish: IEEE Communications Surveys & Tutorials
abstract: With its wide coverage and uninterrupted service, satellite communication is a critical technology for next-generation 6G communications. High throughput satellite (HTS) systems, utilizing multipoint beam and frequency multiplexing techniques, enable satellite communication capacity of up to Tbps to meet the growing traffic demand. Therefore, it is imperative to review the-state-of-the-art of multibeam HTS systems and identify their associated challenges and perspectives. Firstly, we summarize the multibeam HTS hardware foundations, including ground station systems, on-board payloads, and user terminals. Subsequently, we review the flexible on-board radio resource allocation approaches of bandwidth, power, time slot, and joint allocation schemes of HTS systems to optimize resource utilization and cater to non-uniform service demand. Additionally, we survey multibeam precoding methods for the HTS system to achieve full-frequency reuse and interference cancellation, which are classified according to different deployments such as single gateway precoding, multiple gateway precoding, on-board precoding, and hybrid on-board/on-ground precoding. Finally, we discuss the challenges related to Q/V band link outage, time and frequency synchronization of gateways, the accuracy of channel state information (CSI), payload light-weight development, and the application of deep learning (DL). Research on these topics will contribute to enhancing the performance of HTS systems and finally delivering high-speed data to areas underserved by terrestrial networks.

title: Electromagnetic Pulse Induced Failure Analysis of GaAs pHEMT MMIC Low Noise Amplifier
authors: Xuanlin Wang, Wei Li, Shiyue Wu, Wei Song, Fan Bi, Jiaxi Zhang, Yufei Lu, Yue Diao, Yucheng Wang, Shaoxi Wang
publish: IEEE Transactions on Electron Devices
abstract: The low noise amplifier (LNA) is a crucial component in RF front-end circuits, responsible for amplifying weak signals. However, it is susceptible to damage under high-power microwave (HPM) exposure, which can lead to performance degradation and eventual failure of the RF circuit. This article presents a cross-scale investigation of the failure behaviors and mechanisms of a commercial GaAs MMIC LNA chip subjected to HPM injection, integrating both experimental and simulation-based analyses. Initially, we developed an HPM injection test platform and monitored critical parameters, such as output gain and operating current, in real-time during the injection process. By analyzing the gain variations, we determined the power thresholds for chip degradation and damage. At the macroscopic level, analysis of the damaged circuits indicated that the GaAs pHEMT device in the first amplification stage of the LNA is the most vulnerable and fails first under HPM exposure. At the microscopic level, failure mode analysis using focused ion beam (FIB), Transmission electron microscope (TEM), and energy dispersive X-Ray spectroscopy (EDX) techniques revealed that the failure mode is primarily thermal failure, with melting occurring in the aluminum gate and the gate–source region. Simulation-based analysis of the failure mechanisms showed that under HPM exposure, the electric field concentrates in the region near the source beneath the gate, resulting in avalanche breakdown. This initiates a thermal-electrical positive feedback loop, progressively increasing the internal temperature of the device, which leads to the melting of the gate and gate–source region, ultimately causing thermal failure. The findings of this study offer critical insights into the failure mechanisms of LNAs in high electromagnetic environments, providing a foundation for future work on enhancing the reliability of RF circuits under HPM conditions.

title: Neural Radiance Field (NeRF) Based Nozzle Reconstruction: A Novel Approach for 3D Morphology Modeling
authors: Chi Zhu, Jingjing Xiao, Bingning Jin, Ji Liu, Xin Lv, Zhiren Deng, Yu Bai, Suwen Zhang
publish: 2025 3rd Asian Aerospace and Astronautics Conference (AAAC)
abstract: The engine nozzle operates in a high-temperature, high-speed gas flow environment, resulting in deformation due to thermal expansion and contraction during operation. This paper introduces computer-based three-dimensional reconstruction technology for the 3D modeling of engine nozzles. The study focuses on a specific liquid-fueled attitude control engine nozzle, for which a 3D reconstruction setup has been established. This setup employs a single-camera arrangement, where the camera, supported by a rotating platform, moves in a circular motion around the engine nozzle to capture images. Both the traditional SFM-MVS method and the deep learning-based NeRF method are used to reconstruct the engine nozzle, and the results are compared. While the SFM-MVS method produces point clouds with some missing data, the texture details are clear. Conversely, the NeRF method yields more complete point clouds, although some details are smoothed out and lost. The 3D reconstruction of the engine nozzle lays the groundwork for the reconstruction of three-dimensional physical fields related to the nozzle and can be applied in future measurements of parameters such as the three-dimensional temperature field.

title: Real-time Optimal Operation of Low-Carbon Integrated Energy Systems Based on Hydrogen By-Product Utilisation
authors: Yingying Luo, Yubin Wang, Mingchao Xia, Qifang Chen, Qianhao Sun
publish: 2025 IEEE 9th Conference on Energy Internet and Energy System Integration (EI2)
abstract: With the advancement of the goal of energy decarbonisation, improving energy efficiency and reducing carbon emissions have become key tasks. In this paper, a real-time optimal operation strategy of low-carbon integrated energy system (IES) based on hydrogen by-product utilisation is proposed, integrating hydrogen production from electrolytic water, electricity to gas, carbon capture, utilisation and storage and energy storage systems, and coupling hydrogen energy and carbon resources to produce high value-added by-products in industrial processes. Among which, a scheduling model is constructed based on model predictive control to optimally manage the system energy. Simulation results show that the proposed circular hydrogen economy model and optimisation method can achieve low-carbon, high-efficiency and flexible operation of the IES.

title: Slot Value Prediction based on LSTM and Transformer Hybrid Model
authors: Zhang Qingxian, Wu Weiwei, Ding Chengjin, Gao Qiang
publish: 2025 3rd Asian Aerospace and Astronautics Conference (AAAC)
abstract: As a scarce resource, slot allocation has garnered significant attention from multiple stakeholders including aviation authorities and airlines. Slot value prediction involves comprehensive consideration of various complex factors, yet traditional quantitative models struggle to effectively interpret and process this issue due to intricate feature interactions and high-dimensional characteristics. Accurately predicting the value of slot and improving slot utilization efficiency are crucial for airlines, which helps allocate resources effectively and activate the secondary market for slot. First, by analyzing influential factors and constructing a multidimensional feature set, we propose a hybrid prediction model that integrates Long Short-Term Memory (LSTM) networks and Transformer architectures. Its superiority is validated through comparative experiments against six baseline models. Subsequently, we introduce the SHAP (Shapley Additive Explanations) interpretable framework to further reveal the influence and contribution of each feature. The analysis focuses on flights departing and arriving at Shanghai Pudong, Shanghai Hongqiao, and Shenzhen Bao'an airports. The LSTM-Transformer hybrid model is applied to predict slot values on 24 typical routes. The results show that in all experimental scenarios, the LSTM-Transformer hybrid model achieves a prediction accuracy exceeding 92%, surpassing the six other baseline models. The most significant contributing factors to the LSTM-Transformer model are seat capacity, discount, and load factor, with the introduction of airline and departure time features contributing 22.07% to the model's prediction accuracy.

title: Dynamic Energy Flow Calculation of Integrated Electricity-Gas Systems via a Semi-Analytical Solution
authors: Jiheng Xu, Yinqiao Liu, Xiaomeng Li, Hang Tian, Haoran Zhao, Luhao Wang, Xiandong Wang
publish: 2025 IEEE 9th Conference on Energy Internet and Energy System Integration (EI2)
abstract: Dynamic energy flow calculation (EFC) for integrated electricity-gas systems (IEGS) is challenged by the partial differential algebraic equation (PDAE) nature of gas transport and its disparate time scale relative to quasi-steady electric networks. This paper develops a semi-analytical solution (SAS) framework that (i) approximates pipeline mass-momentum dynamics with a spatio-temporal power series whose coefficients are obtained from linear recursions, and (ii) expands variables of the electric network and coupling device in time and solves the resulting fixed-size linear systems, thereby producing Newton-free, time-domain trajectories that couple the gas and electric subsystems through a time-continuous interface without interpolation. Two benchmarked studies—a single-pipeline case and a coupled 14-bus/13-node IEGS—illustrate improved fidelity and markedly reduced computational burden compared with discretization-based solvers, supporting long-horizon dynamic analysis and real-time validation.

title: An Efficient Calculation Method for the Flexible Domain of Active Distribution Networks Based on Optimal Power Flow and Second-Order Cone Relaxations
authors: Tianhong Jiang
publish: 2025 IEEE 9th Conference on Energy Internet and Energy System Integration (EI2)
abstract: To achieve friendly interaction between active distribution networks (ADNs) with high-permeability distributed generation (DG) and the upper-level power grid, it is necessary to accurately quantify their flexible adjustment capabilities. Aiming at the problem that traditional flexibility region calculation methods are difficult to balance between accuracy and efficiency, a flexibility region calculation method combining optimal power flow (OPF) and convex relaxation is proposed. This method first accurately obtains several key poles on the boundary of the flexibility region by solving the alternating current optimal power flow (AC-OPF) model multiple times, thereby defining the core range of the flexibility boundary. Furthermore, the second-order cone relaxation (SOCR) technology is used to convert non-convex power flow constraints into convex constraints, and quickly calculate a compact outer approximation convex set of the real flexibility region. This method not only ensures the accurate description of the core flexible range but also greatly improves the overall calculation efficiency through convex approximation, providing technical support for distribution system operators (DSOs) to participate in real-time market transactions and dispatching.

title: Repair Crew Dispatch Optimization in Three-Phase Distribution Systems Under Unbalanced Loading Conditions
authors: Aoyu Lei, Yongqiang Gao, Jialu Li, Yongjie Zeng, Haicheng Yao, Chen Yu
publish: 2025 IEEE 9th Conference on Energy Internet and Energy System Integration (EI2)
abstract: Effective post-disaster repair strategies and emergency resource dispatch schemes can accelerate power restoration and reduce outage losses. However, existing repair and resource scheduling strategies typically rely on the assumption of three-phase balanced distribution networks. In reality, distribution networks frequently operate under three-phase unbalanced conditions due to unbalanced loads, asymmetric line parameters, and non-full-phase connections of small-scale distributed energy resources. This paper introduces a linear three-phase power flow model to characterize the coupling relationships among the three phases. Using wind turbines as an example, the paper employs the probabilistic scenario method to address the uncertainty of renewable energy generation. A stochastic optimization model is established for the coordinated scheduling of repair crews, distribution network reconfiguration, and mobile generators. The proposed model is validated on a modified IEEE 37-bus system. The simulation results demonstrate that three-phase unbalanced constraints will impact resource scheduling and load restoration plans, and the developed model enhances system security during the repair process.

title: Veritime: Enhancing Service Industry Accountability with Smart Contracts and IoT Integration
authors: Meenal R Kale, Yogesh Mehta, Kathari Santosh, A. Annie Lotus, Archana Borde, K. Selvakumarasamy
publish: 2026 Sixth International Conference on Advances in Electrical, Computing, Communications and Sustainable Technologies (ICAECT)
abstract: In fast-moving business environments, timely and reliable service delivery is required, although the traditional methods of verification are seldom accountable and transparent. Veritime addresses these issues through an automated verification system based on blockchain, smart contracts, and IoT sensors. It enables secure delivery verification, automated payment upon successful delivery, and real-time tracking of shipment by using cryptographic passphrases from Ethereum contracts and IoT-enabled containers. The key elements in Veritime involve the sender, receiver, blockchain network, IoT sensors, and the MQTT server. Developed in Python, Veritime topped the benchmark for performance and delay in power efficiency and packet delivery compared to traditional systems. Gas cost analysis showed that functions like “Register Manufacturer” and “Assign Distributor” consume 47,335 and 56,789 transaction gas, confirming the efficiency and reliability of the system.

title: Event-Driven Fixed-Time Control for Underactuated Nonlinear Systems: Application to Quadrotor UAVs
authors: Madhumita Pal, Sanjoy Mondal, Amal Barman, Ashish Raj Verma, Satyajeet Prakash, Sagnik Chakraborty, Avishek Soe Mondal
publish: 2026 Sixth International Conference on Advances in Electrical, Computing, Communications and Sustainable Technologies (ICAECT)
abstract: This paper presents an event-driven implementation of fixed-time sliding mode control (FxT-SMC) strategy for underactuated nonlinear systems, with application to quadrotor unmanned aerial vehicles. The event-triggered (ET) mechanism is designed to reduce communication and computation burdens while preserving the fixed-time convergence properties of the continuous controller. A nonsingular fixed-time sliding manifold is employed to ensure global convergence within a predefined time and to avoid singularities near the origin. When the tracking error enters a small neighborhood of the equilibrium, the manifold transitions smoothly to a quadratic form, ensuring continuity and eliminating potential singular behavior. Lyapunovbased analysis establishes global fixed-time stability (FxTS) under the ET framework, and Zeno behavior is excluded by design. Simulation results on a quadrotor UAV demonstrate that the proposed event-triggered FxT-SMC achieves precise trajectory tracking with reduced control updates by 74.2% and improved robustness against disturbances and model uncertainties.

title: Self-Evolving Multi-Agent Reinforcement Learning Systems for Decentralized Decision-Making in Open-Ended Environments
authors: Harsh Wasnik, Yash Wasnik, Rishab Khatokar, Dhawal Gajwe
publish: 2026 Sixth International Conference on Advances in Electrical, Computing, Communications and Sustainable Technologies (ICAECT)
abstract: We propose a self-evolving multi-agent reinforcement learning (SE-MARL) system for decentralized decisionmaking in open-ended settings. Agents co-evolve with populationbased meta-learning and automatic curriculum generation, with a lightweight evolution loop that intermittently evolves architectures and hyperparameters and retains Pareto-efficient policies. To make the method more realistic, we construct a city-scale, large-scale fleet control benchmark from NYC TLC trip histories (2014-2019) and map observed demand and travel time into a partially observable, non-stationary environment. In zero-shot expansions, distribution shifts, and unseen agent types, SEMARL maintains coordination by means of on-policy distillation and local message passing without imposing any global controller. In experiments, the method outperforms decentralized training and deployment and non-evolutionary MARL baselines on task reward and regret and scales to thousands of agents. Ablations show that both self-evolution and curriculum signals are necessary for robustness, and that emergent specialization is responsible for sample efficiency. These results suggest an operational path to adaptive, robust decentralized systems from openly available, real-world data.

title: Modeling and Operational Characteristics Analysis of Distributed Electric Heating Loads
authors: Kai Li, Jun Ma, Xiali Liu, Yanjun Wang, Jiao Wan, Xubei Nie
publish: 2025 IEEE 9th Conference on Energy Internet and Energy System Integration (EI2)
abstract: The electric heating load serves as the connection point between the user's heating system and the power system. Its participation holds significant reference value for power system dispatching. The load characteristics describe the temporal and dynamic changes between the electric heating power and indoor temperature. Considering the differences in energy supply methods, equipment types, and user behavior characteristics, the electric heating load exhibits various regulation characteristics. This paper focuses on analyzing the operating characteristics of the electric heating load and the room heat transfer characteristics, constructing an R-C equivalent circuit model to describe its load characteristics, and further analyzing its operating characteristics, and the R-C model parameters were fitted using the heating data collected through electric heating load experiment. laying a theoretical foundation for simulating the electric heating load group of buildings.

title: Unified Present-Value Planning of a Provincial 500 kV Backbone for Cost Reliability and Tail Risk
authors: Yunfei Chu, Fanyu Meng, Yuejin Yang, Jiazhen Wu, Zedong Yang, Long Cheng
publish: 2025 IEEE 9th Conference on Energy Internet and Energy System Integration (EI2)
abstract: This paper develops a unified present-value formulation for planning a provincial 500 kV backbone transmission network. The objective minimizes life-cycle cost (LCC) by jointly accounting for capital expenditure, discounted energy-loss charges and carbon costs, operation and maintenance, outage cost, and decommissioning value. Reliability is enforced by embedding the expected energy not supplied (EENS) limit and the N-1 security criterion as constraints. To capture low-frequency, high-impact events, an extreme-event library is constructed and tail risk is managed in the objective via conditional value at risk (CVaR). Physical fidelity and tractability are ensured using direct-current power-flow (DCPF), while McCormick linearization handles the coupling between angle differences and binary build decisions, yielding a scalable mixed-integer linear program (MILP). Tests on a provincial 500 kV prototype show that the baseline plan yields an LCC of about 4.002 billion CNY; enforcing N-1 significantly reduces the present value of expected outage cost as well as loss and carbon components; further adding CVaR reduces tail risk with only about 0.16 billion CNY risk premium. Under the EENS limit and N-1 security, selecting higher-capacity conductors and larger reactive-power settings achieves a controllable cost-risk trade-off.

title: Research on the Strategy of Aggregated Distributed Green Power Directly Connected to Steel Plants Participating in Grid Interaction
authors: Feixiang Gong, Zesen Wang, Taorong Gong
publish: 2025 IEEE 9th Conference on Energy Internet and Energy System Integration (EI2)
abstract: Load-load interaction among steel enterprises is a crucial means to address the power grid supply-demand balance. With the implementation of the direct green power connection policy, the randomness and uncertainty of the steel industry's own load have become more pronounced. This paper proposes an innovative source-grid-load coordination optimization method. By integrating photovoltaic power generation resources and adjustable load resources of steel plants, a multi-objective optimization model is constructed and solved using the Sequential Least Squares Programming (SLSQP) algorithm. This approach addresses the optimal regulation strategy for multiple aggregated steel plants participating in grid interaction under distributed photovoltaic coupling. Case studies demonstrate that this method can effectively reduce the grid peak-to-valley difference by 24.54% and improve the load rate by 18.68%. This study offers a technical pathway for refined energy management in the industrial sector, as well as participation in grid coordination interactions, which is of significant importance for promoting green and low-carbon transformation.

title: Unification Stability Analysis of Continuously Adjustable Reactor Under Four Operation Modes
authors: Yizheng Zhang, Dayi Li, Jianjun Sun, Zhu Liu, Ke Xu, Xiaorong Wan
publish: 2025 IEEE 9th Conference on Energy Internet and Energy System Integration (EI2)
abstract: To ensure the stable operation of the continuously adjustable reactor under four distinct operating modes—current-controlled current source (CCCS), current-controlled voltage source (CCVS), voltage-controlled current source (VCCS), and voltage-controlled voltage source (VCVS)—this paper presents a unified stability analysis framework. First, the fundamental architecture of the continuously adjustable reactor is described, and the corresponding impedance expressions for each operating mode are derived as functions of the respective control coefficients. Subsequently, a unified stability analysis principle applicable to all four modes is formulated. Based on this principle, the admissible stability ranges of the control coefficients are obtained through a unified characteristic equation, and the numerical stability boundaries are further evaluated to facilitate parameter-independent coefficient selection in practical applications. Finally, the proposed analytical framework is validated through simulation studies conducted with representative parameter sets, thereby demonstrating the effectiveness of the theoretical results.

title: Research on Voltage Regulation and Harmonic Control Capacity Enhancement of Large-Capacity DC Charging Piles
authors: Zhaoliang Li, Liu Yang, Fangyi Li, Peng Zhang, Zhikai Wang, Haijun Liu
publish: 2025 IEEE 9th Conference on Energy Internet and Energy System Integration (EI2)
abstract: With the construction of China’s new power system, the urban distribution network is experiencing increasingly complex power supply and consumption conditions. Key issues include elevated harmonic levels due to high penetration of DC infeeds, changes in system impedance and reactive power distribution resulting from growing cableization rates, and the widespread integration of power electronic devices such as charging piles and rooftop photovoltaics. These factors contribute to wide-band, strongly-coupled power quality disturbances, leading to frequent harmonic over-limit and voltage violation incidents, which threaten grid security and end-user equipment. Conventional mitigation approaches—such as Static Var Generators (SVGs) and Active Power Filters (APFs)—have been widely deployed, yet the potential of DC charging piles to support grid voltage regulation and harmonic suppression remains underexplored. Given the rapid deployment of charging infrastructure, its distributed nature, and its topological similarity to traditional compensation devices, charging piles possess inherent advantages for providing reactive power compensation and harmonic mitigation. However, existing systems lack such functionality. To address this gap, this paper proposes an integrated method involving both topological and control modifications to enable DC charging piles to participate in urban distribution grid voltage regulation and harmonic suppression. The proposed framework offers a complete solution for enhancing power quality through charging piles, supporting the growing demands of electric vehicle integration and the grid’s need for high-quality power supply.

title: Deep Reinforcement Learning-based Optimization Strategy for Improving Stability of VSG
authors: Minhui Qian, Wu Cao, Jianhua Li, Kaiming Luo, Ning Chen
publish: 2025 IEEE 9th Conference on Energy Internet and Energy System Integration (EI2)
abstract: This paper proposes a method for improving the transient stability of voltage source converters based on deep reinforcement learning. First, a grid-connected converter system model based on the voltage source converter is established, and the impact of voltage source converter control parameters on transient stability is analyzed. Then, an active and reactive power joint regulation method is proposed to enhance the transient stability of voltage source converters by combining deep neural network models with the deep deterministic policy gradient algorithm in reinforcement learning. The active-reactive power loop parameter adjustment problem is modelled as a Markov decision process to dynamic optimize of active and reactive power loop parameters for the voltage source converter. Finally, using the Matlab/Simulink, a converter grid-connected system based on virtual synchronous generators is constructed, and simulation experiments are conducted. The results show that, compared to traditional control strategies, the proposed method for improving transient stability through active-reactive power joint regulation demonstrates stronger adaptability and stability across various scenarios.

title: Performance Evaluation of Grid-Connected Solar Power Plant for Utility Load Utilising Reactive Power Compensation
authors: Khushaboo, Virendra Sharma, Vikas Kumar Sharma, Harsh Shrivastava, Garima Mathur, Gaurav Jain
publish: 2026 Sixth International Conference on Advances in Electrical, Computing, Communications and Sustainable Technologies (ICAECT)
abstract: This manuscript illustrates a method to analyze a grid-connected 400 kWp solar renewable energy source as active power compensation (APC) with 400 kVAr reactive power compensation (RPC) for utility network load. This strategy to reduce the consequences of utility grid load demand and also promote the use of renewable sources of energy (RESs). APC is achieved by optimistic use of solar power plant (SPP) with the help of voltage source converter (VSC) which is controlled by using parallel DC link capacitor and synchronous reference frame theory (SRFT) techniques. A prosed test system (PTS) has mostly two buses one is taken for APC from solar PV system as DC bus and second is taken for traditional grid (G) as infinite bus as well as AC bus or load bus to integrate the utility network load for the evaluating the performance as contribution of power exchange and PQ enhancement by switching event. DC bus is used for contribution of green energy from SPP to utility network load by using DC to AC inverter topology through point of common coupling (PCC) which is used to measure the data of grid as well as load parameter for the performance analysis of GCSPVS. The utility network loads have designed as L1 300 kW; 300 kVAr and L2 1 MW; 1 MVAr connected through circuit breakers (CBs) for the event of ON/OFF switch the loads with and without capacitor bank as energy storage system (ESS), the PTS can provide power for the intended utility load and contribution of APC as well as RPC successfully implemented for all potential loads. Additionally, diagnosis the power quality (PQ) in terms of total harmonic distortions (THD's) as well as switching impact on grid parameters as voltage/current signal is evaluated to be more than 66.68 % & 28.95 % respectively when SPP integrated with and without ESS.

title: Study on the Combustion Response Mechanism of AP/HTPB Propellant Under Acoustic Oscillation
authors: Guochenyang Xing, Bingning Jin, Zhiren Deng, Jiacheng Li
publish: 2025 3rd Asian Aerospace and Astronautics Conference (AAAC)
abstract: This study investigates the nonlinear combustion response characteristics of AP/HTPB propellant under acoustic oscillations. A non-contact optical measurement system, utilizing CH radical chemiluminescence combined with high-speed photography and narrow-band filtering (427±10 nm), was developed to capture the radiation intensity distribution of CH radicals in the flame during combustion. This system captured the radiation intensity distribution of CH in the flame during propellant combustion, with high spatial and temporal resolution achieved through a photomultiplier tube (PMT). Additionally, high-speed spectroscopic synchronicity measurement and dual-wavelength temperature measurement techniques were used to capture flame shape changes and temperature data. Experiments were conducted under oscillations within the 100-500 Hz frequency range, revealing low-pass filtering characteristics in the flame response, with more pronounced effects at lower frequencies. Flame morphology analysis revealed that low-frequency excitation caused the flame near the propellant surface to sway from side to side, with the overall shape transitioning from clustered combustion to a more cohesive, group-like combustion pattern. Oscillatory effects brought the heat release zone closer to the propellant surface, enhancing thermal feedback, which increased the surface temperature and ultimately accelerated the propellant burn rate. This research enhances the understanding of the nonlinear combustion dynamics of AP/HTPB propellants under oscillatory conditions, offering both new experimental methodologies and theoretical insights for the study of solid propellant combustion instability.

title: Rootalyx: Stealthy Dynamic Analysis for Linux Malware using Kernel-Level Instrumentation and a Hybrid LSTM-Transformer Model
authors: Doru Leşe, Andrei Sântoma, Ciprian Oprişa
publish: 2025 IEEE 21st International Conference on Intelligent Computer Communication and Processing (ICCP)
abstract: This paper presents the design and implementation of a stealthy dynamic analysis environment for Linux, named Rootalyx. The system addresses the challenge of evasive malware that can detect and circumvent traditional monitoring tools. Its core component is a kernel module that uses the ftrace subsystem to instrument system calls, integrated with rootkitinspired hiding techniques to ensure a covert operational profile. The collected behavioral data, including system call arguments, is processed by a hierarchical hybrid model combining the LSTM and Transformer architectures for automated malware classification. Experimental results show that the proposed system introduces lower performance overhead compared to traditional tools for monitoring like strace and successfully evades detection by common security scanners. The machine learning model achieves a classification accuracy of 80.34% on the extracted data, validating the viability of kernel-level stealth monitoring for robust malware analysis.

title: Real-Time Sign Language Detection and Translation Using Mediapipe Hand Tracking and TensorFlow Neural Networks
authors: Dileep Reddy Bolla, Naveen K, Anirudh S Menon
publish: 2026 Sixth International Conference on Advances in Electrical, Computing, Communications and Sustainable Technologies (ICAECT)
abstract: Hand sign detection, a transformative technology utilizing TensorFlow and MediaPipe, is poised to revolutionize communication for individuals with speech impairments. This paper provides an overview of the current state of hand sign detection technology, with a specific focus on its integration potential into virtual meeting platforms like Zoom and Google Meet. By leveraging TensorFlow's machine learning capabilities and MediaPipe's sophisticated perception pipelines, this research aims to create an inclusive environment, making virtual communication accessible for those who face challenges in verbal expression. The Custom Hand Gesture Dataset (CHGD-2025), which includes more than 25, 000 annotated gesture samples from 36 sign classes (A-Z alphabets and 0-9 digits), was used to validate the system. Thirty different individuals' data was gathered in a variety of background and lighting settings. In order to improve generalisation, preprocessing included scaling for invariance, landmark normalisation with respect to wrist coordinates, and augmentation methods like rotation, mirroring, and temporal jittering. The results of the study show that the suggested system has a real-time inference latency of less than 45 milliseconds per frame and an average detection accuracy of 98.3% across test samples. The effectiveness of landmark-based features was demonstrated by a comparison with baseline CNN models trained on raw image data, which revealed a 7-10% increase in accuracy and a <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$3 \times$</tex> reduction in processing time. These findings support the system's potential to transform accessible communication by facilitating real-time, intuitive gesture- based engagement and helping people with disabilities communicate more effectively.

title: A Robust EfficientNet-B3 Framework for Smart Waste Classification with Focal Loss and Cosine Annealing
authors: Potturi Reshma, Viswaprasad Kasetti, M S Radha Mangamani, Hemanand Chittapragada, Solleti Phani Kumar, Uday Kumar Dosanapudi
publish: 2026 Sixth International Conference on Advances in Electrical, Computing, Communications and Sustainable Technologies (ICAECT)
abstract: Waste production rates have surged due to urbanization together with industrial development which requires smart automated waste management approaches to optimize segregation and recycling scales. The combination of deep learning methodologies combined with image classification particularities demonstrates substantial potential to resolve this issue. This research utilizes EfficientNet-B3 as its state-of-theart deep convolutional neural network for TrashNet dataset multi-class waste classification on 2,557 images with precise labels. The inherent class imbalance receives attention through the incorporation of Focal Loss which represents a specialized loss function optimized to direct learning toward challenging cases. The model receives better adaptability through Mixup data augmentation which both combines images and labels for increased generalization and robustness. The learning rate is governed by a cosine annealing learning rate scheduler so that training benefits from automatic dynamic rate adjustments for best results. The interpretation of models for transparency occurs through Score-CAM (Score-Weighted Class Activation Mapping) which creates distinct graphical representations to explain the network's examination procedure. The proposed framework demonstrates EfficientNet-B3's superior capabilities for waste classification while providing a robust solution that enables the development of intelligent recycling systems which support sustainable waste management and environmental protection.

title: A System for Logic Circuits Interpretation from Hand-Drawn Diagrams
authors: Alexandru Cristian Moldovan, Constantin Nandra
publish: 2025 IEEE 21st International Conference on Intelligent Computer Communication and Processing (ICCP)
abstract: This paper describes an improved solution for the problem of accurate detection and classification of hand-drawn logic circuit components. The solution builds upon previous work which has shown promising initial results in logic gate classification and experimented with connection detection. Herein, we describe an improved connection detection solution, which is based on a YOLO classifier, and evaluate its performance. Additionally, we expand upon the initial collection of classes, adding more complex logic circuit components. The result is an in-memory model that can be used to test the circuit and verify its correctness. Throughout the paper, we provide an overview of our solution and describe a series of test scenarios we devised to evaluate the proposed solution. Index Terms-logic circuit classification, neural networks, deep learning, optical character recognition

title: Performance Study of a Single-Server Queue with Backup Server, Catastrophe, and Reneging Customer
authors: M. Seenivasan, R. Vijayalakshmi, R. Ramesh, F. Patricia
publish: 2026 Sixth International Conference on Advances in Electrical, Computing, Communications and Sustainable Technologies (ICAECT)
abstract: In this study, a Markovian queueing model incorporating catastrophe, restoration, server breakdown with a backup facility, and customer reneging is analyzed. The arrival process follows a Poisson distribution, while the service times are exponentially distributed. When the primary server experiences an unexpected failure, it undergoes repair before resuming operation, during which a backup server is activated to maintain service continuity. Reneging customers, characterized by impatience, leave the system without receiving service. The matrix-geometric method is employed to obtain the steady-state solution and evaluate various performance measures of the system

title: Unsupervised Learning Techniques for Intrusion Detection in Computer Networks A Comparative Study Using the CICIDS2017 Dataset
authors: M.Beulah Viji Christiana, Shashiraj Teotia, Safeena C, S. Dhivya, K. Santhi, Subramani
publish: 2026 Sixth International Conference on Advances in Electrical, Computing, Communications and Sustainable Technologies (ICAECT)
abstract: The growing number of cyberattacks highlights the importance of Intrusion Detection Systems (IDS) for securing computer networks. This work evaluates the predictive performance of four unsupervised outlier detection algorithms-Isolation Forest, Local Outlier Factor (LOF - Novelty), One-Class Support Vector Machine (SVM), and LOF Stream-applied in both batch and stream learning scenarios. The CICIDS2017 dataset was selected, with experiments performed on Tuesday and Friday subsets. After preprocessing, the Tuesday dataset contained 445,645 instances (13,832 attacks, 431,813 normal), while the Friday dataset contained 702,718 instances (288,785 attacks, 413,933 normal), each with 74 attributes. Performance was assessed using precision, recall, and F1-score. On Tuesday, One-Class SVM achieved the best balance, with precision <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$=0.36$</tex>, recall <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$=0.52$</tex>, and <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{F 1}=0.43$</tex>, while Isolation Forest achieved the highest recall (0.79) but very low precision (0.16). On Friday, LOF - Novelty outperformed others, with precision <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$=0.85$</tex>, recall <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$=0.41$</tex>, and <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathrm{F} 1=0.55$</tex>. Across both datasets, the highest F1-score for the attack class was 0.55, reflecting difficulty in maintaining recall without increasing false positives. Results indicate that no single algorithm consistently outperforms the others, suggesting that hybrid methods and parameter optimization are necessary for robust IDS in high-dimensional, imbalanced environments.

title: Transient Reserve Capacity Optimization Method of Power System Considering Multi-energy Support Resources on the User Side
authors: Junyu Liu, Ye Zhang, Yingkai Cai, Yubo Ning, Naidi Kang, Hanchen Fu
publish: 2025 IEEE 9th Conference on Energy Internet and Energy System Integration (EI2)
abstract: Aiming at the problems of insufficient transient reserve and transient stability of power system, this paper proposes an optimization method of transient reserve capacity of power system considering multi-energy support resources on the user side. Firstly, the energy regulation characteristics of multi-energy power system transient support resources are given. Secondly, according to its regulation characteristics, a multi-energy transient support reserve capacity optimization model based on risk cost quantification is established, and the convolutional neural network is used to optimize the model. Finally, through the specific example solution and comparative analysis, it is proved that the proposed optimization method has better transient stability control effect.

title: A New Driving Framework Based on High-Resolution Micro-LED Display
authors: Lirong Zhang, Wenping Xiao, Lifang Qiu, Weijing Wu
publish: 2025 International Conference on Energy, Materials, and Photonics (EMP)
abstract: A new driving framework has been proposed for high-resolution Micro-LED display, where a line-by-line driving approach is adopted to address the limitation in current analog pulse width modulation (PWM) driving method. This driving framework decouples the relationship between resolution and emission time of Micro-LED, ensuring abundant emitting time for each pixel, realizing the effect of progressive emission (PE). Compared to the conventional driving framework, the time of scan and emission time are twice as long under the same resolution. The advantages become increasingly pronounced as the resolution improves. Therefore, this framework is suitable for high-resolution and large-sized Micro-LED displays.

title: Day-Ahead Scheduling of a Hybrid Wind-Battery-RO Desalination Plant with Detailed Modeling of Energy Recovery Devices
authors: Wente Yao, Chongqi Huang, Shibo Liu, Xia Zhao
publish: 2025 IEEE 9th Conference on Energy Internet and Energy System Integration (EI2)
abstract: Seawater desalination, being a highly energy-intensive process, has long been challenged by its high operational costs. In recent years, reverse osmosis (RO) desalination plants powered by renewable energy sources such as wind power have been increasingly developed. However, in most studies on the optimal scheduling of such plants, the role of energy recovery devices (ERD) is often neglected or oversimplified using fixed efficiency assumptions. As a result, these models fail to accurately represent the actual operational behavior of ERDs and their impact on system energy consumption. To address this gap, this paper proposes a day-ahead optimal scheduling model for a wind-battery-RO desalination plant that incorporates a detailed model of the ERD. Case study results demonstrate that the proposed model allows for a more accurate assessment of energy recovery benefits and provides useful insights for achieving energy-efficient and cost-effective operation of renewable-energy-powered desalination systems.

title: A UAV Path Planning Approach Based on Improved Artificial Lemming Algorithm in Urban Low Altitude Environments
authors: Xuejun Zhang, Shiyao Wang, Yan Li
publish: 2025 3rd Asian Aerospace and Astronautics Conference (AAAC)
abstract: With the rapid development of low-altitude economy, the complex low altitude environment requires more efficient and accurate path planning algorithms for unmanned aerial vehicles (UAVs). The artificial lemming algorithm (ALA), as an emerging bionic meta-heuristic optimization algorithm, has advantages in adaptability and search ability in path planning problems. However, it suffers from slow convergence, easy entrapment in local optima, and poor robustness in complex 3D spaces. To address these issues, this paper proposes an improved artificial lemming algorithm (IALA) for UAV path planning, which mainly incorporates three improved strategies. Firstly, the Gaussian wandering strategy is introduced to enhance the pertinence of the migration direction. Secondly, the search range is expanded through Cauchy's inverse cumulative distribution variation. Thirdly, periodic variation is considered to prevent the attenuation of search kinetic energy. The simulation results show that compared with other comparative algorithms, IALA shortens path length by 8%-30%, reduces turns by 10%-16%, and lowers risk by 31%-44%. The proposed IALA can successfully balance path efficiency, smoothness, and safety, making it more suitable for practical operation in urban low altitude environments.

title: Improving Overall Performance of Totem Pole PFC Converter by Introducing Feedforward and Sliding Mode Observation Control Scheme
authors: Hanyang Ma, Ji Xiang, Junjie Wang
publish: 2025 IEEE 9th Conference on Energy Internet and Energy System Integration (EI2)
abstract: This paper presents a control strategy to enhance both the steady-state and dynamic performance of the totem-pole PFC converter under conventional dual-loop control across a wide load range. The proposed approach incorporates input voltage feedforward and duty cycle precomputation, along with a sliding mode observer to estimate the load current. This estimated current is fed forward to the current loop to improve the dynamic response speed. Simulation and experimental results demonstrate that the proposed strategy achieves accurate load current estimation, high power factor (PF), low total harmonic distortion (THD), and improved dynamic response, without requiring additional hardware components.

title: A Hypothesis on the Mechanism of Normal Pressure Hydrocephalus Involving Brain Fluid Interactions: A Mathematical Approach
authors: Galina S. Valova, Olga B. Bogomyakova, Andrey A. Tulupov, Alexander A. Cherevko
publish: IEEE Transactions on Biomedical Engineering
abstract: Objective: Hydrocephalus is a severe disorder characterized by pathological enlargement of the brain ventricles, leading to compression and deformation of brain tissue. The pathophysiological mechanisms underlying some subtypes of hydrocephalus remain poorly understood. Normal pressure hydrocephalus (NPH) continues to be a clinically significant and unresolved issue in elderly care. This study proposes a novel approach to investigate this pathology using mathematical modeling techniques. Methods: Using stationary multicomponent poroelasticity equations with physiological boundary conditions, we examine the interactions between brain parenchyma and fluid (including arterial, capillary, venous blood, and interstitial fluid). The model describes these interactions through four specific“interaction coefficients”. Results: Analysis revealed how interaction coefficients govern ventricular wall pressure and displacement. The derived analytical approximations of these relationships provide a foundation for hypothesizing the mechanisms of NPH initiation and development. Conclusions: This hypothesis suggests that NPH results from compromised vascular autoregulation, which under normal conditions maintains stable ventricular volume. Significance: This work identifies specific interaction parameters that govern transitions between physiological stability and pathological ventricular dilation. These results may assist in refining diagnostic criteria and in developing therapeutic strategies aimed at correcting the condition and treating NPH.

title: ICCP 2025 Committees
authors: N/A
