During this process, due to the volatility and uncertainty of wind energy, there is a serious problem of wind curtailment during its grid connection. This article constructs an economic dispatch model that takes into account the characteristics of hydrogen energy storage for microgrid power systems connected to wind power. Firstly, based on the energy structure of the islanded microgrid power system with hydrogen storage, model the various costs. Secondly, with the objective function of minimizing the operating cost of the power system, an economic dispatch model for the power system is constructed that takes into account the efficiency characteristics of hydrogen production equipment and seawater desalination equipment. Multiple constraints are comprehensively considered to achieve the minimization of economic costs. On this basis, an optimized power system economic dispatch model considering the thermoelectric characteristics of hydrogen fuel cells is constructed, further achieving the economic optimization of system operation. Finally, the model is validated in an isolated microgrid case study, and the results show that it improves the hydrogen energy storage system’s ability to absorb excess wind power and optimizes its operational economy. The research results indicate that this scheduling framework provides a feasible reference for the economic and safe operation of high proportion wind power systems.

title: Event-Driven Non-Intrusive Load Identification in Three-Phase Power Supply Systems
authors: Qiyang Li, Mao Tan, Zibin Li, Jinling Chen, Wei Liu
publish: 2025 IEEE 9th Conference on Energy Internet and Energy System Integration (EI2)
abstract: In complex and diverse industrial and commercial three-phase power supply systems, load identification holds significant importance for energy consumption optimization and power management. To address the challenges of prolonged load transient processes and difficulty in accurately distinguishing event types in three-phase systems, this paper proposes an event detection method based on voting-based generalized likelihood ratio, dual-scale sliding windows, and backtracking strategy, achieving precise localization of start and end points for events in each phase. Through a three-phase event joint decision mechanism, it effectively distinguishes between single-phase and three-phase load switching behaviors, thereby accurately capturing load events and extracting effective features to support identification tasks. To further enhance identification performance, this paper introduces a Transformer-based multi-modal feature fusion method, which strengthens the ability to differentiate between different types of loads and effectively improves load identification accuracy in complex scenarios.

title: Resilience Assessment for Electro-Hydrogen Coupled Systems Under Typhoon Scenarios
authors: Yujia Wang, Dongrui Quan, Haipeng Xie
publish: 2025 IEEE 9th Conference on Energy Internet and Energy System Integration (EI2)
abstract: With global climate change, extreme weather events such as typhoons occur more frequently, challenging the secure and reliable operation of power and hydrogen energy systems. Enhancing fast response and recovery under disaster impacts is critical for comprehensively assessing the resilience of electro-hydrogen coupled systems. This study presents a resilience assessment method tailored to typhoon scenarios. Firstly, the impact of typhoons on electro-hydrogen coupled systems is analyzed, and failure models for six critical components are developed. A dynamic state evolution process is introduced. Secondly, a resilience assessment algorithm is constructed using optimal power flow model, Monte Carlo simulation, and post-disaster recovery logic. Finally, an IEEE 33-bus test system is employed to validate the method, demonstrating its capability to capture resilience characteristics of electro-hydrogen systems under typhoon-induced disruptions.

title: Grid-Froming Control Based on Converter Voltage Orientation
authors: Zhengxuan Li, Qiang Song, Yucheng Wu, Fang Cai
publish: 2025 IEEE 9th Conference on Energy Internet and Energy System Integration (EI2)
abstract: With the increasing penetration of renewable power, existing studies have revealed the inherent disability of the inertia support of the conventional grid-following (GFL) control and proposed multiple grid-forming (GFM) controls. However, the existing GFM controls commonly take the point of common coupling (PCC) as the voltage phase reference. To capture the phase at the PCC, a q-axis controller that always controls the q-axis voltage as 0 must be added. This increases the complexity of the control structure, and makes it difficult to tune the control parameters. This paper discusses about a GFM control based on converter voltage orientation. Different from the existing ones, in this paper, the d axis in the dq frame is directly assigned to the voltage at the ac port of the converter rather than the voltage at the PCC, thereby eliminating the additional phase assignment control on the q axis and facilitate the parameter tuning. Then, a simulation model is built in MATLAB/Simulink to verify the feasibility of the proposed control under a weak (inner inductance is 0.79 p.u.) and an ideal (inner inductance is 0) ac grid. The simulation also verifies the robustness to various grid strengths.

title: Resilience Improvement Method of Distribution Network Considering Energy Storage Configuration and Line Reinforcement
authors: Sifan Xie, Lu Zhang, Ning Zhou, Ziya Xing
publish: 2025 IEEE 9th Conference on Energy Internet and Energy System Integration (EI2)
abstract: Aiming at low-voltage distribution network (DN) disconnection and power loss from extreme disasters, this paper develops a strategy to boost DN resilience by integrating energy storage (ES) configuration (ESC) and line reinforcement (LR). First, it constructs a post-disaster ES-based fault recovery model for DN s. Second, it analyzes the pros and cons of the two measures, establishes a bi-level optimization model incorporating both, and derives a "LR-ES coordination" scheme balancing power supply performance and investment economy. Finally, a 21-node case verifies the scheme: it ensures critical load supply, improves non-critical load recovery rate, reduces investment significantly compared with line-only reinforcement, and effectively enhances DN resilience under extreme weather.

title: Assessing the Performance of Traditional and Deep Learning Models for Text Generation and Classification: An Empirical Study
authors: Shouryadhar Karamsetty, Danda Sanjana Reddy, Annadi Abhinaya Reddy, Annam Varsha
publish: 2026 Sixth International Conference on Advances in Electrical, Computing, Communications and Sustainable Technologies (ICAECT)
abstract: In this work, deep learning and traditional models for text generation and classification are empirically compared. Three methods are assessed: the LSTM-based RNN (sequence-focused), the Transformer (self-attention-based), and the <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{N}$</tex>-gram model (probabilistic). Accuracy, precision, recall, <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$F 1$</tex>-score, and perplexity are evaluated for the models using a publicly available dataset and a standardized preprocessing pipeline. The findings demonstrate that Transformers outperform in both classification and generation tasks but demand a large amount of computational power, LSTMs increase contextual accuracy at the expense of higher training costs, and N -gram models are straightforward but inadequate at handling lengthy contexts. In addition to providing guidance on model suitability for various NLP applications, the study highlights trade-offs between efficiency and performance.

title: Effect of Gas Generator Equivalence Ratio on Flow Field and Outlet Uniformity in an Air Turbo-Rocket Engine with Rotating Detonation Combustor
authors: Junjie Wu, Hu Ma, Shizhuo Hou, Dong Wang
publish: 2025 3rd Asian Aerospace and Astronautics Conference (AAAC)
abstract: Comprehensive mathematical models for all Air Turbo-Rocket Engine components upstream of the Rotating Detonation Combustor (RDC) were developed in this paper. Utilizing the derived combustor-inlet boundary conditions, two-dimensional numerical simulations of a RDC flow field at varying gas generator equivalence ratios were conducted. Results indicate that increasing gas generator equivalence ratio narrows the equivalence ratio range for self-sustained Rotating Detonation Wave (RDW) propagation and inducing decoupling at high gas generator equivalence ratio. Within the stable regime, higher gas generator equivalence ratio increases post-wave temperature and reduces RDW height. Peak RDW intensity occurs at gas generator equivalence ratio of 5, where combustor equivalence ratio aligns with stoichiometry. Increasing gas generator equivalence ratio reduces then recovers outlet pressure uniformity while consistently improving temperature uniformity. Minimum pressure uniformity and maximum outlet temperatures occur at gas generator equivalence ratio of 5, confirming optimal detonation wave intensity dominated by wave-induced disturbances. The condition gas generator equivalence ratio of 5 balances maximum wave intensity with operational stability, defining the optimal operability window for RDC design.

title: Federated Deep Learning Framework for Privacy-Preserving Student Performance Prediction in Multi-Institutional Educational Systems
authors: Gagandip Kaur, Ranjeeta Saini, Rippandeep Kaur, Yogesh Punia, Priya Sharma, Vimochana. M
publish: 2026 Sixth International Conference on Advances in Electrical, Computing, Communications and Sustainable Technologies (ICAECT)
abstract: As educational data generation continues to grow, conventional student performance prediction models tend to be incapable of addressing issues of privacy, interpretability and scale. Current research on shallow classifiers or privacy-agnostic deep learning models has a poor accuracy and deployment issues. The proposed solution to fill these gaps is a new hybrid deep learning model, BATNet with Federated Learning (FL) which is a combination of BiLSTM, Attention-based MLP and TabNet trained in a distributed fashion across multiple clients without exchanging raw data. The model was applied to PyTorch and tested on a recent Kaggle student performance dataset. BATNet + FL had 96.6 accuracy, which is 13.2 percent better than the traditional models such as Naive Bayes, and high precision (90.2%), recall (89.7%), and F1-score (89.9%). The suggested approach achieves the predictive power and privacy preservation, which makes it an effective solution to secure, scalable, and intelligent educational analytics.

title: Comparative Machine Learning Approaches for Imbalanced Multiclass Classification in Early-Stage Prediction of Hepatitis C Virus
authors: Sivamani Selvaraju, K. Kanagasabapathi, Vishnuprasath A., C. Thirunavukkarasu, Sivamani Selvaraju, M. Ananthalakshmi
publish: 2026 Sixth International Conference on Advances in Electrical, Computing, Communications and Sustainable Technologies (ICAECT)
abstract: Accurate prediction of hepatitis C virus (HCV) stages remains challenging due to severe class imbalance, where healthy samples dominate and early-stage cases are underrepresented. This study systematically compared multiple machine learning classifiers-including Naïve Bayes, Random Forest, XGBoost, LightGBM, CatBoost, Support Vector Machines (SVM), K-Nearest Neighbours (KNN), Multilayer Perceptron (MLP), and Improved Denoising Autoencoder (IDAE)-using a multiclass clinical dataset with five outcome classes (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$0-4$</tex>). LightGBM achieved the best overall performance (Accuracy <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$=95.9 \%, \mathrm{F} 1=0.955$</tex>), followed closely by MLP and Gaussian SVM, while simpler models such as KNN and Naïve Bayes performed less effectively. However, despite high weighted metrics, macro-averaged scores were substantially lower (Precision <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\approx 0.666$</tex>, Recall <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\approx 0.637, \text{F1} \approx 0.647$</tex>), reflecting poor sensitivity for minority classes. Class-1 was completely misclassified, classes 2 and 3 were moderately detected (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$F 1=0.667$</tex>), and class-4 performed relatively well (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$F 1 =0.909$</tex>). Most misclassifications occurred between adjacent stages (class-2 ↔ class-3), consistent with overlapping biochemical profiles. Importantly, false negatives where earlystage patients were classified as healthy raise significant clinical concerns. To improve minority-class detection, strategies such as rebalancing (SMOTE/ADASYN, class weights), class-specific threshold tuning, ordinal modelling, probability calibration, and SHAP-based feature refinement are recommended. These findings underscore that evaluating models solely by overall accuracy is misleading in imbalanced medical datasets, and that robust, class-sensitive evaluation is essential for clinically reliable prediction.

title: Real-Time English Speech Assessment Using Edge AI and Acoustic Feature
authors: Punayya Yentrapati, Sailaja Gaddam, Shagufta Parween, Kasarapu Esther Rani, S.T. Gopukumar, Sreela B
publish: 2026 Sixth International Conference on Advances in Electrical, Computing, Communications and Sustainable Technologies (ICAECT)
abstract: Real-time English performance rating is becoming critical in the contemporary language learning and communication but the established manual or cloud-based systems have the drawbacks of delays, security issues, and high operation expenses. The key issue is that there are no effective low-resource tools that allow delivering the immediate and stable feedback without always having internet. To solve this, researchers suggest a lightweight speech assessment system based on CNN-based features extraction and an Edge Pronunciation Scoring Network. The system effectively examines hinge measurements of including pitch, energy, and voice starting point in edge devices. According to experimental findings, the accuracy of pronunciation is above 98 % of correctly identified, there is a high level of correlation with human raters and the latency is small. The model provides realtime feed-back which is friendly to mobile and embedded systems. The further improvements are multilingual support, analyzing emotional tone, and explainable AI.

title: Influence of Fin Aspect Ratio on Linearity Performance of JL-FinFET Device for Low Power Analog Operation
authors: Sanu Gayen, Suchismita Tewari, Avik Chattopadhyay
publish: 2026 Sixth International Conference on Advances in Electrical, Computing, Communications and Sustainable Technologies (ICAECT)
abstract: In order to meet the growing market demand for compact, portable, multifunctional devices, which ultimately lead to System-on-Chip (SoC) and All-in-One (AiO) integrated circuits, there is an increasing need for miniaturized devices capable of operating under low power conditions without dissipating much power in the OFF-state or standby mode. At the same time, device linearity aspect has become one of the most crucial studies to ensure the reliability, stability, and predictability of the device. In this paper, the impact of the architectural geometry, in terms of the Fin aspect ratio (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathrm{H} / \mathrm{W}$</tex> ratio), of an amalgamated JL-FinFET device, is thoroughly investigated in terms of both analog and linearity performance metrics. Interestingly, while H/W <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$=30 / 10$</tex> has emerged as the superior performer in the analog domain, it does not maintain the same performance trend in the linearity and harmonic distortion aspect. This highlights an important point: excelling in the analog domain does not necessarily guarantee enhancement in the linearity aspect. Instead, <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$H / W=25 / 12$</tex>, followed by 10/30, proved to be the most optimized devices when considering performance across both the aspects.

title: Non-Contact Heart Rate Monitoring for Real World Health Surveillance
authors: Kajri K. K, Rahul Krishnan Pathinarupothi
publish: 2026 Sixth International Conference on Advances in Electrical, Computing, Communications and Sustainable Technologies (ICAECT)
abstract: Contactless heart rate (HR) monitoring by utilizing facial vedio recordings shows significant potential for long-term, non-invasive health monitoring. However, modern remote photoplethysmography (rPPG) systems have limitations such as depending on monitored conditions and sensitivity to variations in skin tone, mobility, and light. To address these drawbacks, this work examines rPPG for HR estimation under realistic experimental conditions. This research investigates rPPG for HR estimation in realistic experimental settings to address these limitations. Over a period of ten days, 87 videos were collected from 11 participants using a mobile camera in an indoor environment, with preprocessing applied to preserve natural variability. Brightness, hue, Chrominance-based (CHROM), Plane-Orthogonal-to-Skin (POS), and Independent Component Analysis (ICA) methods were among the several signal extraction techniques explored and evaluated to identify the most effective approach. The best-performing signal-based approach achieved a standard deviation of 13.79 beats per minute (BPM). To further enhance prediction accuracy, a fully connected neural network, a Multi-Layer Perceptron (MLP), was trained using the extracted signals. The optimized MLP model attained a Mean Absolute Error (MAE) of 6.54 BPM, a Root Mean Squared Error (RMSE) of 9.04 BPM, a Mean Absolute Percentage Error (MAPE) of 9.31 %, and a standard deviation of 6.24 BPM. These results emphasize the difficulties as well as the potential for reliable non-contact heart rate estimation in daily settings, laying the groundwork for scalable, practical, and real-world health monitoring systems.

title: Research on optimal allocation of new energy shared energy storage based on market mechanism
authors: Yi Chai, Yunfei Tian, Hangyu Zhou, Jing Peng, Dezhou Yang, Peng Sun
publish: 2025 IEEE 9th Conference on Energy Internet and Energy System Integration (EI2)
abstract: Aiming at the capacity configuration of SES (shared energy storage) and how to obtain stable benefits, this proposes a new energy SES optimization method based on market mechanism simulation and reliability evaluation. Firstly, the market clearing model is constructed with the goal of minimizing the total operating cost of the energy storage system operator. The indirect evaluation is carried out through the simulation operation of the electricity spot market, and the shortage load rate is used as the evaluation index to obtain the system capacity required to achieve the same reliability level. Secondly, based on the operator 's market scheduling strategy, this study constructs an optimal energy storage configuration model, aiming to minimize the average annual total cost of the SES system within the new energy base. Then, this paper proposes a novel energy storage operation mode. This mode not only provides shared capacity services but also participates in the electricity energy market as an independent entity. Finally, using the actual historical data, three modes are designed for comparative analysis. The findings demonstrate that the proposed model can significantly enhance the economic viability of energy storage projects, improve the utilization efficiency of storage resources, and effectively facilitate the integration of renewable energy. In electricity markets with substantial peak-valley price spreads, energy storage systems can achieve higher returns by flexibly engaging in charging and discharging transactions.

title: Cooperative Operation Method of Pumped Storage Clusters and Multi-type Power Sources for Multi-time Scale Power and Energy Balance
authors: Yue Han, Zhongchun Li, Haili Wang, Meng Wang, Haibin Shi, Linyu Li
publish: 2025 IEEE 9th Conference on Energy Internet and Energy System Integration (EI2)
abstract: Aiming at the problem of multi-time scale balance of power and energy in high-proportion new energy power systems, this paper explores the hydrological connections among multiple pumped storage stations, studies the cooperative mechanism of pumped storage clusters based on different hydrological coupling characteristics, and decouples the hourly power balance of 8760 hours in a year into short-term power balance and long-term energy balance. It explores the role of pumped storage clusters in peak shaving, valley filling, new energy accommodation, and reducing the output of conventional power sources across multiple time scales. The analysis of Qinghai Power Grid shows that the proposed method can improve the economic efficiency of system operation across multiple time scales, ensure the adequacy of power and energy in the new power system, and reveals the potential of pumped storage clusters to support the low-carbon transformation of the new power system through hydrological coupling.

title: Customer Impatience in M/M/1 Queuing System with Working Vacations
authors: M. Seenivasan, N. Siva Sankari, V. Anita, R. Ramesh
publish: 2026 Sixth International Conference on Advances in Electrical, Computing, Communications and Sustainable Technologies (ICAECT)
abstract: This study explores the impact of Customer Impatience in M/M/1 Queuing System with Working Vacations. Entities enter the system based on a Poisson arrival process, with service durations being exponential. The customer received service according to the order in which they arrived, following the FCFS policy. The term working vacation denotes a period when the server is not entirely inactive but continues to deliver service at a slower rate, Once the vacation is complete, it resumes to its normal service routine and also considered is the customers abandoning the queue due to increased wait times during working vacations. The system is studied through the Matrix Geometric Approach, with the stochastic process expressed as <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\{(\mathrm{J}(\mathrm{t}), \mathrm{K}(\mathrm{t}))$</tex>: <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{t} \boldsymbol{\geq} \mathbf{0}$</tex> \}. The study yields several numerical data, system performance evaluations, and graphical presentations.

title: An Aggregation Method of Distributed Energy Resources in Distribution Networks based on Zonotopes
authors: Ruoyi Xu, Ruina Zhao, Yuxuan Chen, Jing Peng, Fujun Qi, Haipeng Xie
publish: 2025 IEEE 9th Conference on Energy Internet and Energy System Integration (EI2)
abstract: As large-scale distributed energy resources (DERs) are integrated into distribution networks, fully exploiting their significant dispatch potential becomes essential. However, DERs have characteristics such as dispersion, heterogeneity, and small unit capacities, which necessitates unified management and dispatch in the form of aggregation clusters. Therefore, this paper proposes an aggregation method of DERs in distribution networks based on zonotopes. First, based on a bottom-up framework, diverse and heterogeneous DERs are classified into two categories: power-type and storage-type, and their original feasible domains are precisely characterized. Then, leveraging the algebraic structure of zonotopes, we formulate an optimization model to approximate each individual feasible domain with a zonotope. Finally, the feasible domain of the aggregation cluster is computed via the Minkowski sum. Case analysis demonstrates that the proposed method achieves average accuracy indexes above 0.7 under most scenarios, verifying the universality, accuracy and efficiency of the proposed aggregation method.

title: ADMM-Based P2P Trading Optimization Model for Distributed Energy Storage
authors: Yuehui Hu, Na Zhang, Dianting Guo, Jing Guo, Zhaoming Zhang, Qinglong Xia
publish: 2025 IEEE 9th Conference on Energy Internet and Energy System Integration (EI2)
abstract: To address privacy, computational efficiency, and resource allocation issues in distributed energy storage P2P transactions, this paper develops an ADMM-based distributed optimization model. It minimizes user costs under power and storage constraints, using a net contribution-based settlement mechanism to encourage participation. The method decomposes the problem into parallel subproblems, improving computational efficiency while protecting privacy. Case results show the model achieves full renewable consumption with minimal cost increase and lower incremental cost under inaccurate load forecasts, balancing economy and privacy in high-renewable systems.

title: Reliability Evaluation Method for Integrated Electricity Gas Energy Systems Considering Load Redistribution Attacks
authors: Siting Li, Tianlei Zang, Chuangzhi Li, Libo Ran, Kewei He, Lan Yu
publish: 2025 IEEE 9th Conference on Energy Internet and Energy System Integration (EI2)
abstract: In cyber-physical power systems, load redistribution (LR) attacks represent a typical form of false data injection attacks. With the increasing coupling between power systems and natural gas systems, such attacks, when launched against an integrated electricity natural gas system (IEGS), pose more significant and complex threats to secure system operation. This paper investigates the impact of LR attacks on the reliability of IEGS. First, semi-Markov modeling approaches are proposed for LR attacks on both the power network side and the gas network side. Furthermore, a bi-level optimization model is constructed, consisting of an upper-level attacker and a lower-level dispatch response. Finally, based on an IEGS combining a modified IEEE 14-bus power system and an NGS 10-node gas network, the impact of different attack types, resource allocations, and load falsification on system reliability is analyzed, validating the effectiveness of the proposed approach.

title: Passivity Enhancement and Harmonic Suppression Strategy for Grid-Connected Inverters Based on PCC Voltage Feedforward
authors: Hongshan Zhuang, Guixing Yang, Xingang Wang, Shenhui Shao, Tiansheng Wang, Xingang Liu, Jiang Gu
publish: 2025 IEEE 9th Conference on Energy Internet and Energy System Integration (EI2)
abstract: Distorted point of common coupling (PCC) voltage and complex grid impedance often degrade the current quality of grid-connected inverters and raise stability concerns. To overcome these challenges, this work proposes a PCC voltage feedforward-based approach that enhances admittance passivity and mitigates harmonics. First, the method introduces low-frequency resonance compensation into the feedforward path, thereby extending the low-frequency passivity of the inverter output admittance. Then, it incorporates a second-order derivative feedforward with a low-pass filter, which effectively reduces the admittance gain in the medium-to-low frequency range and strengthens the suppression of background PCC voltage harmonics. As a result, the proposed strategy extends the passivity of the inverter output admittance up to the Nyquist frequency. Finally, experimental results confirm the effectiveness of the proposed approach.

title: A Short-Term Power Prediction Model for Wind Farm Clusters with Embedded Cold-Wave Identification Module
authors: Mao Yang, Renxian Jiang, Jianfeng Che, Wei Xu, Wei He, Kang Wu
publish: 2025 IEEE 9th Conference on Energy Internet and Energy System Integration (EI2)
abstract: Amid escalating global climate change, extreme weather events are becoming more frequent, significantly impacting renewable power generation. This paper proposes a cold-wave-aware short-term power prediction framework for wind farm clusters. First, diffusion-based generative learning is employed to augment the limited cold-wave samples. Second, an embedded cold-wave identification module is integrated directly into the wind-power prediction model, establishing an end-to-end pipeline from cold-wave detection to power output estimation. Finally, a composite loss function is devised that aligns the cold-wave identification objective with the power-prediction task, yielding demonstrable gains in forecast accuracy for wind farm clusters.

title: Comprehensive Review of Low-Power Flip-Flop Architectures in 18nm CMOS Technology
authors: Tasneem Kausar, Komal Gupta, Dhandapani Vaithiyanathan, Preeti Verma, Rikmantra Basu, Manish Verma, Baljit Kaur
publish: 2026 Sixth International Conference on Advances in Electrical, Computing, Communications and Sustainable Technologies (ICAECT)
abstract: Flip-flops will be essential sequential components that determine the performance and energy efficiency of digital integrated circuits. With the ongoing CMOS reduction to the 18 nm technology down the line, the issues of power, delay, and robustness of the flip-flop design have become increasingly acute. In this paper, a review of five popular architectures is presented: the Transmission-Gate Flip-Flop (TGFF), Adaptive-Coupling Flip-Flop (ACFF), Single-Phase Contention-Free Flip-Flop (S2CFF), True Single-Phase Clocked Flip-Flop (TSPC), and Multiplexer-Based Dual Edge-Triggered Flip-Flop (MUXDETFF). The power consumption, delay, number of transistors, and operational robustness of various designs are evaluated using 18nm CMOS technology. The review highlights the trends in the evolution of design, the primary performance measures, and the optimization of low-power applications.

title: Study on the Influence of Gas Inlet Structure on the Performance of Solid Ramjet Engines
authors: Tianle Liang, Yonghui He, Mingcheng Zhang, Wenxiang Cai
publish: 2025 3rd Asian Aerospace and Astronautics Conference (AAAC)
abstract: To provide a reference for the design of solid rocket–ramjet combined engines, this study employs the k-ε model and the King boron particle ignition model to numerically simulate multiphase combustion under varying gas inlet nozzle numbers and gas inlet angles, followed by comparative analysis.The results indicate that a greater number of nozzles accelerates gas-phase diffusion, increases the gas-phase combustion rate, and enhances phase transition in the particle phase, thereby improving the overall two-phase combustion efficiency.However, a larger number of nozzles also raises the wall temperature and increases the number of condensed-phase particles near the wall, intensifying thermal ablation, oxidation, and condensed-phase erosion of the wall. Considering flow losses, the total pressure loss decreases initially and then increases as the nozzle number rises.As the gas injection angle increases, there is no significant effect on gas-phase combustion, but the impact on condensed-phase particle combustion is substantial. An excessively large angle can result in a large number of particles failing to burn completely, reducing the overall combustion efficiency in the secondary combustion chamber, although it lowers the total pressure loss of the engine.Considering both combustion efficiency and total pressure loss, the nozzle number and gas injection angle should neither be excessively large nor excessively small.

title: An Impedance-Based Comparative Analysis of Grid-Forming Inverter Controls: Virtual Oscillator and Droop Control
authors: Jinpeng Zhou, Chun Miao, Yu Shan, Ke Ma, Feng Liu, Zhen Wang
publish: 2025 IEEE 9th Conference on Energy Internet and Energy System Integration (EI2)
abstract: Virtual oscillator control (VOC) and droop control are two grid-forming(GFM) inverter control strategies exhibiting similar steady-state characteristics. This paper investigates the dynamic performance differences between VOC and droop control for GFM inverters by constructing impedance models for both systems and deriving equivalent virtual droop coefficients to establish a unified comparative basis. Time-domain simulations verify the enhanced stability margins of VOC control. However, it is also noted that due to the inherent nonlinearity of VOC, excessive reactive power output leads to excessively rapid inverter voltage drop. This rapid drop causes nonlinear distortion in waveforms, a phenomenon exacerbated by an increase in the equivalent virtual droop coefficient.

title: Dynamic Prediction of Conductor Tension under Coupled Icing–Wind Loads
authors: Yue Li, Anjiang Liu, Youzhuo Zheng, Chen Dou, Shuqing Hao, Fuguo Jiang
publish: 2025 IEEE 9th Conference on Energy Internet and Energy System Integration (EI2)
abstract: As a critical component of power system, distribution conductors are susceptible to fatigue damage under complex meteorological conditions involving combined wind and ice effects. Such damage can lead to strand breakage, thereby compromising the reliability and safety of distribution networks. To address the strong nonlinearity and temporal variability of conductor tension under the coupled influence of icing and fluctuating wind loads, this study develops a hybrid prediction framework that integrates CNN, CBAM and BiLSTM network. Research results indicate that the proposed CNN–CBAM– BiLSTM model significantly outperforms the conventional LSTM, BiLSTM, CNN–LSTM, and CNN–BiLSTM models in both prediction accuracy and stability, achieving RMSE and MAE values of 41.62 and 30.92, respectively. These results highlight the model’s superior ability in spatiotemporal feature extraction and adaptive feature fusion, effectively capturing the nonlinear and time-varying behavior of conductor tension under coupled wind– ice loading. This study provides a valuable reference for accurate conductor tension prediction and operational safety evaluation of distribution lines under the coupled influence of icing and fluctuating wind loads.

title: Multi-spacecraft Hybrid Heuristic Cooperative Task Planning for Asteroid Exploration
authors: Bang Wang, Rui Xu, Zhaoyu Li, Shengying Zhu
publish: 2025 3rd Asian Aerospace and Astronautics Conference (AAAC)
abstract: With the increasing complexity of asteroid exploration missions, the limitations of single spacecraft in terms of functionality and mission coverage have become increasingly evident. Multi-spacecraft collaborative exploration has emerged as an effective approach to enhance mission efficiency and scientific return. To address the inefficiencies in collaborative planning caused by the complexity of spacecraft subsystems and the diversity of cooperative tasks, this paper proposes a logic-time hybrid heuristic method for multi-spacecraft cooperative task planning. The method constructs a multi-agent state transition graph to comprehensively evaluate both local and cooperative activity costs. A logic-time hybrid heuristic function is designed to guide the planning search, effectively reducing the expansion of ineffective branches. The proposed approach reduces the running time required for collaborative task planning.

title: An Improved Unperturbed Unit Vector Method for Initial Orbit Determination of Hyperbolic Orbits
authors: Wang Xuliang, Guo Caifa, Chen Hongying, Kang Deyong
publish: 2025 3rd Asian Aerospace and Astronautics Conference (AAAC)
abstract: The Unit Vector Method (UVM) for satellite initial orbit determination has been widely used in maritime satellite tracking and control missions. However, the original UVM was only suitable for elliptical orbits and could not be applied to hyperbolic orbits. Based on the principles of UVM, refines the UVM mathematical model and improves the calculation method, establishing a Hyperbolic UVM (HUVM) applicable to both elliptical and hyperbolic orbits. Simulations and actual measurement calculations demonstrate that this method effectively adapts to both orbital types while maintaining high accuracy in initial orbit determination. It successfully meets the requirements for determining the initial orbit of the near-Earth segment in deep-space exploration missions using maritime measurement ships.

title: Editorial Interview: Proliferating Free-Space Laser Communications for Space-Based Networks and Scientific Exploration
authors: Bryan S. Robinson
publish: IEEE Journal of Selected Topics in Quantum Electronics

title: Neuron Patching: Semantic-based Neuron-level Language Model Repair for Code Generation
authors: Jian Gu, Aldeida Aleti, Chunyang Chen, Hongyu Zhang
publish: ACM Transactions on Software Engineering and Methodology
abstract: None

title: Fraud-RLA: A Reinforcement Learning Adversarial Attack Against Credit Card Fraud Detection
authors: Daniele Lunghi, Yannick Molinghen, Alkis Simitsis, Tom Lenaerts, Gianluca Bontempi
publish: IEEE Transactions on Dependable and Secure Computing
abstract: Adversarial attacks pose a significant threat to data-driven systems, and researchers have devoted considerable effort to studying them. Despite its economic relevance, credit card fraud detection has received comparatively little attention. To address this gap, we propose a novel threat model that highlights the limitations of existing attacks and motivates new approaches. We introduce Fraud-RLA, an adversarial attack against credit card Fraud Detection Systems that leverages Reinforcement Learning to evade detection. Fraud-RLA is designed to maximize the amount stolen by optimizing the exploration-exploitation trade-off while requiring substantially less prior knowledge than competing methods. Our experiments on a realistic Fraud Detection System show that Fraud-RLA is effective, even under the severe limitations imposed by our threat model.

title: Truthful Online Double Auction-Based Resource Allocation Mechanisms for Partial Computation Offloading in Collaborative Edge Computing
authors: Dongkuo Wu, Xingwei Wang, Xueyi Wang, Qiang He, Siyu Yan, Min Huang
publish: IEEE Transactions on Mobile Computing
abstract: As mobile applications become increasingly computation-intensive, mobile devices (MDs) face growing limitations due to their constrained computational capabilities and battery life. Collaborative Edge Computing (CEC) has emerged as a promising solution to address these challenges by enabling multiple edge service providers (ESPs) to offer computation offloading services to MDs. As such, a CEC resource trading market is essential for efficient interactions between MDs and ESPs. However, jointly determining the offloading ratios, allocating combinatorial computation and communication resources, and designing appropriate pricing strategies in a dynamic market remains a significant challenge. To this end, we propose a truthful online double auction based resource allocation mechanism for partial computation offloading (TRAPO) that explicitly accounts for the stochastic nature of both MDs and ESPs. TRAPO first leverages spatial diversity to construct a set of bids for each MD by mapping their task requirements into resource demands through considering MDs' preferences and partial offloading. Next, we match resource-demanding MDs with resource-supplying ESPs based on adaptive valid price thresholds to maximize social welfare, and calculate the payments of MDs and the rewards of ESPs. Theoretical analyses demonstrate that TRAPO satisfies truthfulness, budget balance, individual rationality, and computational tractability. Simulation experiments further verify the effectiveness and efficiency of TRAPO.

title: On the Performance of Pinching Antenna Array
authors: Tianwei Hou, Jun Wang, Wenfei Gong, Anna Li, Arumugam Nallanathan
publish: IEEE Wireless Communications Letters
abstract: In this letter, to realize a motor-based pinching antenna systems (PASS), a pinching antenna array (PAA)-assisted downlink transmission system is investigated, where the pinching antennas are clustered on a array located on the waveguide. The closed-form expression for the antenna gain of the PAA in line-of-sight (LoS) scenarios with randomly deployed users are derived. To comprehensively evaluate the performance of PAA-assisted networks, we propose a priority-oriented (PO) case, where the PAA is configured to maximize the achievable rate of prioritized users. The achievable rate of both prioritize users and non-prioritize users are derived. The simulation outcomes highlight several important findings: i) The proposed PAA system achieves substantially better performance compared to traditional PA networks by leveraging enhanced antenna gain; ii) User distribution has a pronounced impact on the achievable sum rate in the PO case.

title: A New Image Compensation and Segmentation Algorithm to Retrieve Atmospheric Boundary Layer Height for Lidar RHI 2D Scan
authors: Le Chen, Zhibin Yu, Shihai Wang, Chunhui He, Lin Mei, Jianchuan Zheng, Zhangjun Wang, Taofeng Gu, Guoliang Shentu
publish: IEEE Transactions on Geoscience and Remote Sensing
abstract: The atmospheric boundary layer controls surface-atmosphere energy and matter exchange, and plays a central role in air pollution and weather dynamics. However, conventional vertically pointing lidar observations suffer from a near-surface blind zone and cannot provide two-dimensional information on aerosol distributions, limiting their capability to resolve delicate spatial structures and capture the evolution of the boundary layer. To overcome this limitation, this study employed lidar range–height indicator (RHI) scanning to obtain two-dimensional aerosol observation data with no blind zones and high spatiotemporal resolution. Based on the characteristics of RHI image data, we propose an image compensation and segmentation algorithm for detecting the atmospheric boundary layer height (ABLH). The algorithm compensates for the RHI backscatter signal to more accurately represent aerosol concentration and incorporates Otsu's multi-threshold segmentation and connected-component analysis. It offers a new approach to accurately characterize the boundary-layer structure and evolution. The proposed algorithm can stably retrieve the ABLH without a predefined height range, and when a predefined height constraint is applied to the gradient method (GM) and STRATfinder methods, the ABLH results from the three methods are generally consistent. Two additional cases further verify the effectiveness of the proposed algorithm in identifying the ABLH during complex boundary-layer evolution. The results demonstrate that the method can accurately capture structural variations of the atmospheric boundary layer associated with turbulent mixing and convective transport within the wind field. It also clearly depicts the redistribution process of aerosols under the intermittent turbulent effects induced by near-surface temperature inversions. These cases not only demonstrate the algorithm's exceptional capability to resolve the fine structure of the atmospheric boundary layer but also provide a reliable pathway to deeper insights into the coupled dynamic-thermal mechanisms within the near-surface layer.

title: Intelligent Sky Mirrors: SAC-Driven MF-RIS Optimization for Secure NOMA in Low-Altitude Economy
authors: Sai Zhao, Fanjin Kong, Dong Tang, Tuo Wu, Shunxing Yang, Kai-Kit Wong, Kin-Fai Tong, Kwai-Man Luk
publish: IEEE Transactions on Vehicular Technology
abstract: Low-altitude economy (LAE) has become a key driving force for smart cities and economic growth. To address spectral efficiency and communication security challenges in LAE, this paper investigates secure energy efficiency (SEE) maximization using intelligent sky mirrors, UAV-mounted multifunctional reconfigurable intelligent surfaces (MF-RIS) assisting nonorthogonal multiple access (NOMA) systems. These aerial mirrors intelligently amplify legitimate signals while simultaneously generating jamming against eavesdroppers. We formulate a joint optimization problem encompassing UAV trajectory, base station power allocation, RIS phase shifts, amplification factors, and scheduling matrices. Given the fractional SEE objective and dynamic UAV scenarios, we propose a two-layer optimization scheme: SAC-driven first layer for trajectory and power management, and channel alignment-based second layer for phase optimization. Simulations demonstrate that our proposed scheme significantly outperforms benchmark approaches.

title: Generalized Fiducial Inference for Accelerated Life Tests with Failure Free Life Based on Three-Parameter Weibull Distribution
authors: Zhuqing Miao, Tianzi Tian, Yige Li, Jun Yang
publish: IEEE Transactions on Reliability
abstract: Assessing the lifetime of products or materials effectively is essential for formulating maintenance strategies and warranty policies. With advancements in engineering technology, high-reliability, long-lifetime products are becoming increasingly common. To quickly obtain lifetime data for such products, constant-stress accelerated life tests (CSALT) have been widely employed. The failure-free life (FFL) characterizes the early stage during which these products experience no failures, and the three-parameter Weibull distribution (TPWD) models FFL through its threshold parameter. However, the effect of acceleration on the threshold parameter has not been adequately investigated, particularly in terms of modeling and estimation. To solve the above problem, firstly, we introduce a modeling approach based on the linear cumulative exposure model and fatigue theory, which accounts for the acceleration of the threshold parameter in CSALT. Applied to TPWD, the resulting model is referred to as CSALT-TPWD. Then, we examine the non-regular problem in the maximum likelihood estimation of CSALT-TPWD. Next, generalized fiducial inference is employed to provide both point and interval estimates. Furthermore, to enhance the sampling efficiency of the posterior distribution, a Hadamard-based <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$\ell ^{2}$</tex-math></inline-formula> norm prior is proposed. Theoretical analysis confirms that this prior accelerates computation compared to other commonly used norms, while preserving the value of the traditional <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$\ell ^{2}$</tex-math></inline-formula> norm prior. Finally, numerical simulations and real case analysis demonstrate that the proposed method captures the characteristics of FFL effectively.

title: A Bio-Inspired Spatio-Temporal Residual Framework for Secure GNSS Localization on Resource-Constrained Consumer Devices
authors: Faan Wang, Aoqi Nie, Yanbo Lu, Shifei Jiang, Saru Kumari, Rahul Kumar, Chien-Ming Chen, Zhuoxiang Li, Jinhao Liang
publish: IEEE Transactions on Consumer Electronics
abstract: Consumer-grade Global Navigation Satellite System (GNSS) receivers are highly vulnerable to spoofing, jamming, and other forms of malicious signal manipulation. To address inaccurate positioning, unsafe navigation, and security risks caused by these threats on resource-constrained consumer devices, this paper proposes a lightweight and biologically inspired residual-compensation framework that strengthens the security of GNSS-based localization without requiring additional hardware. The system uses an Interacting Multiple Model Kalman Filter (IMMKF) to perform adaptive model switching, which mirrors the self-regulating behavior of biological sensory systems when facing uncertain external stimuli. A Residual Compensation Network further models structured errors through temporal learning mechanisms that resemble neural adaptation and memory formation. Cooperative geometric cues from nearby vehicles are incorporated to emulate swarm intelligence, allowing the system to resist manipulated or misleading GNSS measurements. The proposed method helps protect GNSS positioning against persistent bias, spoofing-induced distortions, and interference-driven drift. Experiments in simulation and real urban environments show that the framework significantly improves positioning security while preserving low computational cost. The method offers an engineering-ready solution for secure localization in next-generation consumer electronic devices.

title: Design of Extended Horseshoe-and-Spur Optical Networks Leveraging Nonlinearity-Awareness
authors: Mohammad M. Hosseini, Giuseppe Parisi, João Pedr, Pablo Torres-Ferrera, Antonio Napoli
publish: Journal of Lightwave Technology
abstract: This research introduces a novel nonlinear aware approach to design filterless horseshoe-and-spur optical networks aiming to enhance their scalability and cost-effectiveness. By operating at higher power levels, we leverage an increased power budget which helps in reducing the required number of optical amplifiers, a primary cost factor. The network design is framed as an integer linear programming (ILP) problem, optimizing amplifier placement and coupler selection to minimize costs while maintaining a high quality of transmission (QoT). A key contribution is the introduction of a fiber nonlinearity modeling tool in this context, based on the split-step Fourier method (SSFM), which accurately predicts signal distortion and enables the relaxation of conventional power constraints. Our results demonstrate that this nonlinear-aware design can reduce the number of amplifiers by up to 20% as compared to traditional linear designs. This significant improvement in design efficiency leads to substantial capital expenditure (CapEx) savings and a reduced environmental footprint, all without compromising feasibility of the design criteria. This approach proves that allowing a manageable degree of nonlinearity can be a highly effective strategy for creating more scalable and economically viable coherent metro-scale optical networks.

title: An Algorithmic Framework for Black-Box Reductions from Bayesian Mechanism Design to Algorithm Design
authors: Yang Cai, Constantinos Daskalakis, S. Matthew Weinberg

title: A Review of Surrogate-Assisted Evolutionary Multi-Objective Optimization
authors: Qing Wang, Yong Zhang, Dunwei Gong, Xianfang Song, Chunlin He, Xinfang Ji, Fei Chu
publish: IEEE Transactions on Emerging Topics in Computational Intelligence
abstract: Expensive multi-objective optimization problems (EMOPs) are widely encountered in science and engineering, but solving them often requires expensive evaluation costs. To alleviate the computational burden, surrogate-assisted multi-objective evolutionary algorithms (SAMOEAs) have emerged as an efficient paradigm by integrating evolutionary search with surrogate models. This survey provides a systematic overview of the field from multiple perspectives. First, EMOPs are classified based on their attributes, including the structure of objective space, Characteristics of decision variables, dynamics and constraints, and augmented optimization methods. Second, we analyze the core algorithmic components, including surrogate model management, infill criteria, and other acceleration mechanisms. Furthermore, we highlight the design of SAMOEAs tailored for special scenarios, such as multimodal optimization, heterogeneous objectives, and inverse modeling. In addition, applications across benchmark problems and real-world domains are also reviewed. Finally, we outline future promising research directions, including the development of more efficient and robust surrogate models, parallel and distributed frameworks, LLM-assisted SAMOEAs, privacy-preserving SAMOEAs and theoretical analysis of SAMOEAs. This survey aims to establish a systematic perspective on SAMOEAs, bridging methodological advances with practical challenges, and offering a roadmap for future developments.

title: Synthetic-to-Real Translation for Class-Agnostic Motion Prediction_supp2-3673580.pdf
authors: Guosheng Lin
publish: N/A
abstract: Motion understanding is critical for ensuring safety and robustness in autonomous driving systems, driving increasing interest in motion prediction. A key challenge in this domain is the high cost associated with acquiring real-world motion labels. It is therefore ideal if we could transfer motion knowledge from synthetic data to real data. In this context, we explore the potential of synthetic-to-real translation for motion prediction (SRMP). However, the most used naive motion regression methods are notably sensitive to the synthetic-to-real domain shift, resulting in unreliable knowledge translation. To address this, we propose a novel approach integrating a motion knowledge translation framework with two key components: (1) objectness-aware motion prediction, which explicitly models the joint distribution of motion patterns and objectness priors to improve domain-invariant feature learning, and (2) objectnessaided motion enhancement, a motion label refinement mechanism that leverages learned objectness priors to filter motion noise. Furthermore, we present a practical pipeline for generating Motion4D, the first synthetic 4D LiDAR dataset tailored for SRMP research, addressing the lack of synthetic motion benchmarks. Experimental results demonstrate that our approach effectively bridges the domain gaps and yields superior performance on real scenes. Code and dataset will be made publicly available.

title: Multi-teacher Self-training and Semantic-augmented Alignment for Multi-Target Domain Adaptive Semantic Segmentation
authors: Qinghua Ren, Yibo Wu, Congyu Zhang, Qirong Mao, Ming Dong
publish: IEEE Transactions on Multimedia
abstract: Multi-target domain adaptive semantic segmentation (MTDA) trains a single model on labeled data from one source domain to generalize across multiple unlabeled target domains. Existing MTDA methods typically train multiple domain-specific models and then distill their knowledge into a unified model. In this paper, we propose a novel multi-teacher self-training framework for MTDA by introducing a mean teacher (MT) model, which consists of a shared feature extractor and domain-specific classifiers. For each target domain, the teacher classifier, constructed by Exponential Moving Average of the historical versions of the student, distills its knowledge to the current student classifier. Moreover, we design a domain-agnostic classifier that learns jointly from all domain-specific teacher classifiers. Leveraging the MT architecture, our design enhances the consistency of self-training across multiple target domains. Additionally, to mitigate the class-mismatch issue, we propose semantic-augmented feature alignment, which aligns target features with a dynamically updated source feature dictionary during training. Extensive experiments on multiple benchmarks demonstrate that our method achieves state-of-the-art performance in MTDA tasks. The code is available at <uri xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">https://github.com/qh-ujs/SAFA</uri>.

title: Personalized Control Strategy for Electrified Vehicles with Decoupled Brake System Considering Drivers’ Maneuvering Preferences
authors: Qixiang Zhang, Junjie Gong, Fengwei Dong, Jinxiang Wang, Dawei Pi, Guodong Yin
publish: IEEE Transactions on Transportation Electrification
abstract: Brake-by-wire systems can shorten braking distance, improve regenerative efficiency, and enhance safety and comfort. Braking comfort reflects perceived braking intensity and smoothness and varies considerably across drivers. However, the fixed brake servo characteristics (BSC) cannot adapt to diverse driver preferences, potentially degrading safety and comfort. To address these issues, this paper proposes a driver behavior-aware personalized control framework for electrified vehicles with decoupled brake systems (DBS). First, the driver-in-the-loop platform is built to collect data from 149 drivers under typical car-following braking conditions, with driving styles identified by the unsupervised clustering algorithm. Then, based on the feature samples obtained from real-vehicle experiments, Vacuum Booster’s BSC is modeled by the radial basis function neural network, and driver-type adaptive BSCs of DBS at different braking aggressiveness are designed. To ensure robust pressure tracking under personalized BSCs, the nonlinear cascade controller is designed, leveraging equivalent simplified models of DBS to enhance both response speed and control accuracy. Experimental results demonstrate that the proposed strategy accommodates three driver types with distinct BSC preferences, achieving mean satisfaction scores above 7.91 and satisfactory dynamic control performance, with 43% higher accuracy and over 32% lower response delay than baseline methods.

title: Identifying Vital Nodes in Hypergraphs Under Time-Decaying Contagion Dynamics
authors: Ming Xie, Yanggang Cheng, Shibo He
publish: IEEE Transactions on Computational Social Systems
abstract: Identifying vital nodes in hypergraphs is a fundamental problem with wide-ranging applications, including epidemic mitigation, rumor suppression, and public awareness promotion. Since node influence is shaped by the underlying spreading dynamics, it is crucial to consider temporal attenuation, where user interest in a topic on social platforms typically declines over time. Motivated by this observation, we study a novel problem of identifying vital nodes in hypergraphs under time-decaying (TD) spreading dynamics. To this end, we first introduce a TD spreading model on hypergraphs and analyze its dynamical behaviors using a mean-field approximation (MFA). The analysis reveals unique features such as delayed phase transitions and low steady-state infection densities under decay. Building on these insights, we propose a decay-aware structural-dynamical hybrid algorithm that integrates spectral characteristics with temporal decay information to tackle the problem. We validate the performance of proposed method on six real-world hypergraphs from diverse domains, including online social platforms, offline contact networks, and legislative co-bill systems. Experimental results demonstrate that our approach consistently outperforms eight state-of-the-art baselines, achieving up to a 10.04% improvement in influential node ranking. Further analysis reveals that the performance gains arise from the effective balance of topological structure and decaying temporal dynamics. In addition, the superior performance of our method leveraging paths of increasing order, over existing path-based ranking benchmarks, highlights the significance of incorporating dynamical information for identifying crucial nodes under decaying dynamics in higher order networks.
