title: Advanced Real-time State of Charge Estimation in a Hybrid Aircraft Using Dual Filter Interacting Multiple Model
authors: Reza Hosseininejad, Steven Recoskie, Patrick Zdunich, Peyman Setoodeh, Saied Habibi
publish: IEEE Access
abstract: Hybrid and electric aircraft offer a viable solution to reducing the environmental impact of conventional aviation, which aligns with the industry’s intention to reduce its carbon footprint while maintaining safety and operational standards. However, current limitations in battery production technology and the insufficient robustness and accuracy of existing algorithms in Battery Management Systems (BMS) for monitoring and estimating battery states, including State of Charge (SOC) and State of Health (SOH), pose significant challenges in the adoption of electrifying technologies in aviation applications. This paper proposes an approach for SOC estimation by combining advanced model-based concepts into the aviation environment that have not been implemented before. This method is applied to an aircraft’s Energy Storage System (ESS) by integrating merged sets of third-order Equivalent Circuit Models (ECM) representing battery dynamics at the module level. A novel dual-model filtering structure is introduced, in which a Dual Filter (DF) architecture enables concurrent estimation of internal resistance and SOC bias at the module level. The DF structure is deployed based on the Smooth Variable Structure Filter (SVSF), in which one filter provides the estimated internal resistance vector, while the other filter estimates the SOC bias. These filters are embedded within the Interacting Multiple Model (IMM) framework, which lets the algorithm utilize a mixture of several model-filter structures at each time step with respect to the discharge rate. Instead of using off-line and simulated data, the developed methods are implemented and tested in real-time in the laboratory and on a hybrid electric aircraft. This work enables safer and more efficient hybrid-electric aircraft operations by reducing SOC estimation error to below 1%.

title: Research on Cost-based Reliability Assurance Technology for Aerospace Product
authors: Liang Ao, Zexuan Jia
publish: 2025 3rd Asian Aerospace and Astronautics Conference (AAAC)
abstract: In response to the urgent demand for low-cost and highly reliable aerospace product driven by the rapid development of the aerospace industry, this paper constructs a cost-based reliability assurance process based on the principles of systems engineering. The main tasks for validating and verifying reliability requirements under cost considerations are presented. On this basis, this paper focuses on the connotation and key technologies of cost-based system/equipment reliability requirement determination, cost-based system/equipment reliability work planning and cost-based system/equipment reliability allocation method, so as to provide technical support for the low-cost development of aerospace products.

title: Identification of Dominant Control Parameters of Virtual Synchronous Generator and Analysis of Transient Characteristics
authors: Changjiang Wang, Jiahao Song, Yening Lai, Jing Tan, Peng Wang
publish: 2025 IEEE 9th Conference on Energy Internet and Energy System Integration (EI2)
abstract: With the expanding integration of renewable energy sources, Virtual Synchronous Generator (VSG) technology has emerged to address stability challenges in power electronic- dominated systems. This paper systematically investigates the impact of VSG parameter variations on transient response characteristics. Through sensitivity analysis, key control parameters governing the transient support capability are identified. The influence of these parameters on system dynamics is thoroughly examined, with comprehensive simulations verifying the proposed analytical approach.

title: Analysis of Flow and Heat Transfer in an Inlet for Wide-Range Pre-Cooled Aircraft
authors: Bing Zhou, Zhongjin Zheng, Han Yin, Bo Sun
publish: 2025 3rd Asian Aerospace and Astronautics Conference (AAAC)
abstract: The SABRE pre-cooled engine offers a promising power solution for wide-speed-range flight, but its underlying mechanisms require further investigation. In this study, a biconical intake was designed. A virtual pre-cooling zone method was employed, which utilizes the Zukauskas correlation to simulate flow resistance and convective heat transfer within the pre-cooler. An integrated aerodynamic model of the internal intake passage and the pre-cooling zone was developed, and numerical simulations were performed. The results demonstrate that the flow resistance effect and cooling effect of the precooler gradually intensify with increasing Mach number of the incoming flow.

title: Comparative Analysis of Explainable AI Techniques for MRI-Based Neurodegenerative Disease Detection Using LIME, SHAP, and Grad CAM
authors: M. Raja Nandini, Peddi Meenakshi, Muda Keerthi, Kurva Akhila
publish: 2026 Sixth International Conference on Advances in Electrical, Computing, Communications and Sustainable Technologies (ICAECT)
abstract: The neurological disorders such as Alzheimer's and Parkinson's disease continue to pose significant challenges for early diagnosis and patient care. While machine learning models have shown promise in disease detection, the lack of transparency in model decisions limits their clinical adoption. In this research, we propose a dual-modality framework that combines imagebased classification of Alzheimer's disease with tabular biomedical signal analysis for Parkinson's prediction, enhanced with explainable AI techniques to support interpretability and trust. The Alzheimer's module employs a convolutional neural network (CNN) trained on publicly available brain MRI datasets, achieving accurate multi-class classification across disease stages.

title: Optimization of DNNs Using a Hardware Aware Two Stage NAS for Automotive Embedded Targets
authors: Andrei Viman, Mihai Negru, Martin Rapp, Taha Soliman
publish: 2025 IEEE 21st International Conference on Intelligent Computer Communication and Processing (ICCP)
abstract: Deep learning models are widely used in the context of automated driving. Achieving the goal of performance in Automotive Embedded Targets presents numerous challenges due to the trade-off between accuracy and run-time. This paper addresses the task of finding the best candidate that fulfills System-on-Chip (SoCs) constraints using two-stage neural architecture search. We employed this approach to find a balance between accuracy and run-time. Our method can be applied in the context of optimizing deep learning models. Starting from a search space and a neural network, we can automatically create a super-model (by replacing all layers specified in the search space) that can be trained in the first stage of NAS. In the second stage, we use the already trained model to subsample architectures to find the best trade-off between accuracy KPIs and runtime KPIs. These two metrics are not arbitrary; they define the main constraints of SoCs and were used in the construction of the Pareto front from which the users can choose a configuration that satisfies their expectations. We conducted multiple experiments, but the most important ones are: optimizing a YOLOv8s for Texas Instruments (TI) SoC with 8 TOPS per Neural Network Accelerator and a multitask network for RENESAS SoC that has 3.4 TOPS. We ran YOLOv8s experiments on a public COCO dataset and managed to find a neural network that runs approximately 16% faster on TI, with a drop of less than 1.1% in mAP. For the Multitask Network, we conducted experiments on a custom dataset and found a neural network that runs 13% slower on RENESAS, but with an increase of 0.4% in overall evaluation metrics.

title: How Passivity of Bus Dynamics Influences Power System Stability: A Sensitivity Analysis
authors: Zhongze Li, Xi Ru, Xiaoyu Peng, Beisi Tan, Pengfei Gao, Feng Liu
publish: 2025 IEEE 9th Conference on Energy Internet and Energy System Integration (EI2)
abstract: In recent years, passivity-based stability analytics have garnered significant attention due to the challenges posed by the ever-increasing integration of massive power electronic devices. However, the impact of the passivity of individual components or ports, which is characterized by the passivity index, on the stability of power systems with heterogeneous dynamic devices remains incompletely understood. This paper leverages sensitivity theory to capture the marginal contribution of the component-wise (or port-wise) passivity to system-wide small-signal stability. Noticing that the passivity index uses a scalar to depict the passivity shortage/excess of coupled phase-angle and voltage dynamics, which is usually conservative, we further extend the conventional scalar passivity index to a vector. This extension enables us to explore the distinct sensitivities of passivity indexes related to phase-angle dynamics and voltage dynamics. In this way, the most influential components for stability improvement can be identified. Simulations carried out on a rudimentary 3-bus system verify our results. This finding sheds new light on the understanding and enhancement of stability in power systems with high shares of heterogeneous power electronic devices.

title: Application of Intelligent DCS in Combined Cycle Power Generation Units
authors: Liu Ye, Wang Peng Kun, Wu Qian
publish: 2025 IEEE 9th Conference on Energy Internet and Energy System Integration (EI2)
abstract: From an application perspective, this paper briefly describes the application effects of the 9H single-shaft combined cycle unit, which is the first of its kind to be put into operation in China and employs intelligent control technology in Production Area I. By demonstrating the architecture of the intelligent DCS, the functions of the intelligent monitoring section (intelligent analysis, intelligent early warning, and intelligent operation), and the application results, it illustrates that the intelligent DCS in Production Area I effectively plays an auxiliary role in operation, providing early warnings and optimizing operations.

title: Research on Grid Impact Characteristics of Current Rise Rate in Accelerator Magnet Power Supplies
authors: Zhu Mingxi, Si Weibin, Pu Lu, Li Zhenxiao
publish: 2025 IEEE 9th Conference on Energy Internet and Energy System Integration (EI2)
abstract: This paper focuses on the impact of DC-side current rise rates in accelerator magnet power supplies—a special type of impact load—on the grid. By establishing a grid–power supply coupling model, simulations were conducted to analyze key indicators such as grid voltage fluctuations and reactive power impact under three typical rise rates: fast, medium, and slow. The results demonstrate a positive correlation between the current rise rate and the degree of grid voltage sag, with the severity of transient voltage sag primarily influenced by the rate of change of apparent power. Furthermore, the study evaluates the grid impact under the extremely slow current ramp-up scenarios required for superconducting magnets, concluding that such impacts are negligible. This research provides a valuable reference for grid integration and power quality assessment of large experimental facilities.

title: Linear Active Disturbance Rejection Control of the DC Bus Voltage in Railway Power Conditioner
authors: Pei Luo, Ting Lei, Qian Ma, Zhipeng Ou, Xinyu Zhu, Teng Xie
publish: 2025 IEEE 9th Conference on Energy Internet and Energy System Integration (EI2)
abstract: With the gradual integration of energy storage and photovoltaic power into the railway power conditioner, the complementary characteristics of multiple energy sources can be fully utilized, thereby achieving the comprehensive advantages of green and low-carbon development and promoting the evolution of the system toward source–grid–load–storage integration. However, due to the inherent characteristics of the railway traction power supply system, such as large load fluctuations and strong randomness, together with variations in line parameters, environmental disturbances, and the integration of renewable energy, the DC bus voltage of the railway power conditioner is prone to fluctuations, which may further affect the stable operation of the system. To address this issue, this paper proposes a DC bus voltage active disturbance rejection control strategy based on a reduced-order state observer. By applying a variable substitution method, the requirement of the observer to input the derivative of the DC voltage is avoided, which reduces the implementation difficulty in practical systems. This approach improves the steady-state and control performance of the railway power conditioner, and enhances its stability and robustness under complex operating conditions. Finally, a simulation model is established in Simulink to verify the effectiveness of the proposed control strategy.

title: Modeling Discharge Behavior of a Na–Li Dual-Cation Liquid Metal Battery with Displacement Reaction at the Anode–Electrolyte Interface
authors: Yunqi Tang, Cheng Xu, Xianbo Zhou, Yewei Guo, Kai Jiang, Kangli Wang
publish: 2025 IEEE 9th Conference on Energy Internet and Energy System Integration (EI2)
abstract: Liquid metal batteries (LMBs) offer advantages of low cost and long lifespan, making them a promising candidate for grid-level energy storage. The Na–Li dual-cation LMB is a sodium-based LMBs with superior performance; however, its discharge mechanism is more complex than that of LMBs with pure metal anodes and is difficult to observe. This paper establishes a model for a Na–Li dual-cation LMB and incorporates the displacement reaction and its equilibrium shift at the anode–electrolyte interface. Numerical simulation results indicate that the model accurately predicts the discharge behavior of the battery and reveals the association between voltage curve characteristics and the multistage discharge mechanism. This study provides an effective theoretical tool for understanding and optimizing dual-cation LMBs.

title: Study and Performance Analysis of Single-Precision Floating-Point Multiplier for Multimedia Applications
authors: Vardan Kumar, Dhandapani Vaithiyanathan, Nitin Singh Singha, Baljit Kaur, Preeti Verma, Manish Verma
publish: 2026 Sixth International Conference on Advances in Electrical, Computing, Communications and Sustainable Technologies (ICAECT)
abstract: One of the most important arithmetic operations in contemporary digital systems, such as scientific processors, machine learning accelerators, and embedded computing platforms, is floating point multiplication. Numerical behavior is governed by the IEEE 754 standard, but hardware designers are free to choose the underlying multiplier algorithm. Four multiplier architectures are compared in this paper: an Iterative Pseudo Booth multiplier, a CORDIC based multiplier, the Karatsuba multiplier, and a generic baseline multiplier. Every multiplier is synthesized on an FPGA platform with consistent flow and constraints after being built in Verilog. Hardware usage, power consumption, number of nets used, and I/O count are the main areas of comparison. The results show clear architectural tradeoffs: the Iterative Pseudo-Booth approach offers the lowest power and net count, the Generic and Karatsuba designs minimize cell utilization, while the iterative structure of CORDIC results in much greater logic resources. These results provide guidance for designers choosing a suitable architecture for low-power or resource constrained floating point processing devices.

title: Research on Active-Reactance Coordinated Regulation Strategy Based on Multi-objective Particle Swarm Optimization Algorithm
authors: Feiming Wang, Fucheng Lang, Xiaoli Liu, Zhenyi Xu, Wenlong Yi
publish: 2025 IEEE 9th Conference on Energy Internet and Energy System Integration (EI2)
abstract: With the large-scale integration of distributed photovoltaic power, the current distribution network has encountered the problem of voltage exceeding limits where there are both daytime overvoltage and nighttime under-voltage simultaneously. This further affects the power quality and economic issues. Adding energy storage can effectively improve the voltage stability problem of the distribution network. However, energy storage devices have capacity limitations, and relying solely on energy storage devices is insufficient to control the voltage within a safe range. Therefore, a strategy of energy storage devices collaborating with multiple reactive power compensation devices for active-reactive voltage control is proposed. Firstly, aiming at minimizing voltage deviation and achieving the optimal economic performance, the operation constraints of photovoltaic and reactive power regulation equipment are comprehensively considered to establish a voltage control model. Secondly, the traditional independent reactive power regulation model and the energy storage active power support model are organically coupled. Finally, the simulation results show that the proposed strategy effectively improves power quality and enables the distribution system to operate safely and economically.

title: Optimization Methodology for Third Defense Line Strategies Addressing New Energy Uncertainty
authors: Liang Lei, Wang Changjiang, Liu Xianchao, Lv Yazhou, Sun Ning
publish: 2025 IEEE 9th Conference on Energy Internet and Energy System Integration (EI2)
abstract: To address the challenges posed by the integration of high-proportion new energy into the power grid on grid frequency stability, this paper focuses on the issues of insufficient coordination and imprecise control quantities in the traditional Third Defense Line, and proposes an optimized configuration scheme based on the "centralized coordination and decentralized control" architecture. Building upon the existing architecture of Second Defense Line, the scheme leverages the monitoring and communication capabilities of its multi-resource cooperative control master stations, sub-stations, and execution stations to establish a hierarchical and collaborative defense system. By incorporating new energy generation into high-frequency generation tripping and low-frequency load shedding control strategies, and enabling the upload of controllable capacity and station status information, the approach achieves synergy between global optimized decision-making and local rapid execution, effectively avoiding the risks of over-tripping and under-tripping. The feasibility of the proposed method is validated through simulations based on actual power grid scenarios.

title: To Flash or Not To Flash: A Case Study on Vine Foliar Disease Detection
authors: Robert-Anton Konievic, Levente Tamás, Keresztes Barna
publish: 2025 IEEE 21st International Conference on Intelligent Computer Communication and Processing (ICCP)
abstract: This paper investigates the detection of black rot disease in grapevines using both flash and natural outdoor imagery, emphasising real-world field conditions over controlled laboratory environments. Leveraging a dataset captured from an Unmanned Ground Vehicle (UGV), the study evaluates the robustness of deep learning models under few-shot learning scenarios using flash and natural light. A hybrid architecture combining a Feature Pyramid Network is employed, with models initially trained on annotated source data and adapted to unlabeled target domains through transfer learning. The results demonstrate that few-shot learning performance in visual disease detection is significantly enhanced when adaptation is guided by a prior model trained on broader symptomatology, underscoring the importance of generalised representations in few-shot conditions.

title: Data Centric Framework Measuring Social Climate in Study-Abroad Destinations Using Targeted Sentiment Analysis
authors: Ahmed Adam, Mohd. Zaid Merchant, Veer Shah, Prashasti Kanikar
publish: 2026 Sixth International Conference on Advances in Electrical, Computing, Communications and Sustainable Technologies (ICAECT)
abstract: The decision-making process for pursuing higher education abroad involves evaluating various factors, including university reputation, affordability, chance of visa approval, and quality of life. However, an often-overlooked dimension is the social climate of host countries. The social climate can be shaped by immigration policies, societal inclusiveness, and public sentiment towards international students. This paper proposes a data-centric approach that streamlines unstructured social climate clues derived from sentiment analysis of immigrationrelated news. The supporting literature survey reviews key research across sentiment analysis which highlights contributions, limitations, and gaps.

title: Research and Application of Non-Destructive Testing Technology for Reliability of HVDC Control and Protection Components
authors: Jie Chen, Jun Li, Xing Lei, Quanquan Yan
publish: 2025 IEEE 9th Conference on Energy Internet and Energy System Integration (EI2)
abstract: With the rapid development of HVDC transmission systems, both the number and the defect rate of long-term service DC control and protection circuit boards increase rapidly. Under conditions of limited maintenance resources including the labour, budget, and project term, the renovation model for over-service devices could not be complete handled in time by the equipment management departments, which has become a prominent hidden threat to the safe operation of the power grid. In response to the rapid increase in the proportion of over-serviced HVDC control and protection devices, this paper conducts application research on the reliability testing technology in HVDC control and protection components based on non-destructive testing on line or off line. The reliability testing for the quality improvement could supports the optimization of operation and maintenance strategies and ensures the safe and efficient operation of the power grid. This paper could also provide theoretical support and practical basis for the reliability testing procedure of the components level for HVDC control and protection devices.

title: Improved Active Disturbance Rejection Control Strategy For Three-phase Interleaved Parallel DC-DC Converters
authors: Hongbin Pan, Hanqin Zhang, Pengyu Cao, Jiarui Yao
publish: 2025 IEEE 9th Conference on Energy Internet and Energy System Integration (EI2)
abstract: To address the challenge of dynamic performance degradation caused by uncertain disturbances in three-phase interleaved parallel buck converters and to improve high-level integration of renewable energy sources, this study proposes an enhanced double-loop active disturbance rejection control (ADRC) strategy. This approach incorporates a cascade extended state observer in the outer loop to boost estimation accuracy. Through Bode diagram analysis, its benefits in wide-band disturbance suppression, phase compensation, and robustness become evident. An inner-loop active disturbance rejection control based on observation value compensation is designed, relying on the Lyapunov function to determine the system’s stable convergence conditions. This method enhances observer accuracy through observation value compensation, strengthens disturbance suppression of input voltages, optimizes the tracking performance of the outer loop output command, and improves dynamic response. To verify the effectiveness of the proposed strategy, simulation models were developed for dual-loop PI control, traditional dual-loop ADRC, and the proposed improved ADRC. The performance of all three methods was compared and tested on a remote, wide-range, semi-physical simulation platform. Results show that, compared to PI and conventional dual-loop ADRC, the improved ADRC achieves response speeds that are 55% and 30% faster, tracking performance that is 69% and 64% better, and disturbance rejection capabilities that are 65% and 58% more robust, respectively.

title: Design and Implementation of a Data Acquisition System for Flexible Pressure Thin-Film Sensors: Extensibility and Adaptability to Large Areas
authors: Liu Fengquan, Hu Zhengfa, Niu Xinrui, Dong Hao
publish: 2025 International Conference on Energy, Materials, and Photonics (EMP)
abstract: The advanced flexibility and malleability of pressure-strain material enable flexible thin-film sensors applying to flatness and pressure detection. Integration into large-area arrays of flexible thin-film sensors has significant implications for data acquisition and greater health. However,data scanning needs to be done in a way that real-time collection and analysis is still difficult. In order to solve this problem, an expandable large-area array fast data acquisition system based on <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$16 \times 16$</tex> arrayed flexible films was investigated. Implementation of the function of fast scanning, reading and feedback of pressure data.

title: Characterization of New Energy Power Characteristics and Extraction of Typical Scenarios Based on a Multi-dimensional and Multi-level Indicator System
authors: Bo Yang, Wei Wang, Yawei Xu, Yijie He, Mingdong Zhang, Zhihua Zhu, Aoqian Wang, Liming Li
publish: 2025 IEEE 9th Conference on Energy Internet and Energy System Integration (EI2)
abstract: With the large-scale integration of renewable energy, its inherent randomness and volatility are exerting a growing impact on power system analysis. However, existing research lacks a comprehensive multi-dimensional characterization of renewable energy output features and a systematic indicator system to support the extraction of typical scenarios and subsequent applications. To address this gap, this paper proposes a multi-dimensional and hierarchical quantitative evaluation indicator system. First, based on the essential characteristics of renewable energy output, an indicator system encompassing energy, fluctuation, and complementarity characteristics is constructed to comprehensively depict its energy supply, dynamic variations, and synergistic potential. Second, a two-level clustering approach is adopted to extract typical operational scenarios based on this indicator system, providing a foundational scenario set for power system analysis. Third, the representativeness of the typical scenarios generated by the proposed indicator system is validated, followed by a further demonstration of its technical and economic advantages using a power grid dispatch model. Results indicate that the proposed indicator system performs well in terms of comprehensiveness and accuracy, effectively supporting the extraction of typical renewable energy output scenarios and related power system analysis, while achieving a 5.9% reduction in grid dispatch costs. It thus provides a robust tool for system analysis and decision-making under high-penetration renewable energy integration.

title: Analytical Evaluation of Post-Fault Temporary Overvoltage in Multi-Infeed Renewable Power Systems under Asymmetrical Faults
authors: Xiuting Rong, Yunlong Chu, Ning Guo, Chao Huo, Zhipeng Bao, Xinyu Liu
publish: 2025 IEEE 9th Conference on Energy Internet and Energy System Integration (EI2)
abstract: The increasing penetration of renewable energy resources has introduced challenges such as post-fault temporary overvoltage (TOV) due to plant-level control lagging, which has been widely investigated in existing studies under symmetrical faults. However, TOV analysis under asymmetrical grid faults has often been ignored in existing studies. In particular, negative-sequence fault ride-through (FRT) control, involving additional negative-sequence current injection, results in unclear TOV mechanisms and the lack of corresponding TOV evaluation methods in multi-infeed renewable power systems (MIRPS). To address this gap, this paper investigates the TOV mechanism under asymmetrical faults considering negative-sequence FRT control and establishes an analytical evaluation method applicable to the MIRPS. The effectiveness of the proposed method is verified through EMT simulations in PSCAD/EMTDC.

title: Intelligent Sleep Disorder Predictor and Personalized Wellness Guide Using Enhanced Machine Learning
authors: G. Prabhakar, Arshia Mehtaab Katika, Attili Manisha, Devarla Keerthana
publish: 2026 Sixth International Conference on Advances in Electrical, Computing, Communications and Sustainable Technologies (ICAECT)
abstract: Sleep disorders are increasingly becoming a known risk factor to global health and overall quality of life. In the paper, an intelligent system is described that utilizes a Random Forest Classifier trained on 15 demographic, physiological, lifestyle, and behavioral attributes to predict Sleep Apnea and Insomnia. Integrated within a Flask web application, the system has two functions: It provides an immediate risk prediction based on input health parameters from the user, and it has a chatbot that provides individual lifestyle health recommendations, treatments, and specialist referrals. The proposed system has the potential to offer a costeffective, early sleep disorder screening assessment, and can be used both as a stand-a-lone system as well as starting point before meeting with a sleep specialist. It highlights the potential of integrating machine learning with actionable health recommendations.

title: Attention-Driven Spatio-Temporal Graph Neural Networks for Fraud Monitoring
authors: Krishna Chaitanya Yarlagadda
publish: 2026 Sixth International Conference on Advances in Electrical, Computing, Communications and Sustainable Technologies (ICAECT)
abstract: Detection of fraud is a key challenge for institutions, as financial transactions and digital interactions generate large amounts of complex data. Conventional fraud detection systems often have difficulty detecting dynamic patterns in the temporal and spatial dimensions of transaction networks. Such approaches may ignore the nuanced interlinkages between actors and may be slow to adapt to changing fraudulent activities, which could result in delays in detection and loss of money. This work introduces a spatiotemporal graph neural network (ST-GNN) attention-oriented model to enhance fraud detection in dynamic networks. The method captures complex relationships between different time periods and interaction patterns by combining ST modelling with attentional mechanisms, which improves the detection of aberrant behaviour. Real-world transaction data sets are used to evaluate the proposed framework. Precision, recall, F1 score, and the area under receiver operating characteristic (AUC) are important performance metrics that show significant improvements over the current GNN and conventional machine learning techniques. According to the experimental results, STGNN with attention to detail consistently detects fraud more quickly and accurately, while reducing the number of missed detections and false positives.

title: Predictive Analysis of Internet Use and Risk Among Children and Adolescents via Machine Learning
authors: Mansi Jaiswal, Udit Ramalingegowda Manchattahalli, V. Seedha Devi, Maheswari S., Minu Susan Jacob, Jose Anand A.
publish: 2026 Sixth International Conference on Advances in Electrical, Computing, Communications and Sustainable Technologies (ICAECT)
abstract: With the rapid expansion of internet use and the increasing activity of children and young people on social media platforms, new social risks and vulnerabilities have emerged. This study explores the impact of digital communications on young users, with a particular focus on the potential for embarrassing or harmful online experiences. Research conducted in Monte Carmelo schools showed that teachers and parents experienced a decline in learning outcomes and an increase in the amount of time they spent with students, highlighting the urgency of this issue. Using machine learning (ML) techniques, this study shows that avoidance behavior can be detected in such online behavior. These findings provide a basis for future research and the development of early detection systems to strengthen digital safety measures for children.

title: Joblinker: Automated Recruitment Through Verified Intelligence
authors: Uddipan Ghosh, Apalok Roy, Indrashish Bhattacharya, Suvobrata Sarkar, Soumabha Ghosh, Shatakshi Shandilya
publish: 2026 Sixth International Conference on Advances in Electrical, Computing, Communications and Sustainable Technologies (ICAECT)
abstract: In the modern world, when it comes to the online recruitment ecosystem, there's a demand for accurate and authentic solutions. Keeping these points in mind, we have developed JobLinker, an AI-powered recruitment framework which is designed to automate candidate-job matching with minimal bias and human effort. The model consists of natural language processing, ML, and layered verification to extract, process, and compare both resume and job data. Recruiter and applicant profiles are encoded as weighted feature vectors, and a similarity based computation determines a fit score to suggest the most suitable pairings. A feedback-learning module simultaneously refines recommendation precision, while verification layers safeguard data integrity and reliability. Experimental evaluation demonstrates that JobLinker achieves 91.7% and 95.3% accuracy, reducing manual effort by up to 90%. These outcomes position JobLinker as a transparent, adaptive, and costefficient solution that reshapes digital hiring for employers and applicants alike.

title: Quantitative Evaluation of Git-Blockchain Synchronization Models for Trustworthy Software Provenance in Internet of Vehicles
authors: Iwinosa Aideyan, Mert D. Pesé, Richard. R. Brooks
publish: Distributed Ledger Technologies: Research and Practice
abstract: None

title: SOR-BDNet: Semantic-Optical Representation for Boundary-Aware Video Anomaly Detection with GPT-4o
authors: Yi Sun, Bryan W. Scotney, Shuai Zhang, Xiushan Nie, Xingbo Liu, Lanting Qiu
publish: ACM Transactions on Multimedia Computing, Communications, and Applications
abstract: None

title: A Novel Likelihood Gradient-Based Incipient Fault Detection Approach for Avionics Systems
authors: Wenxin Sun, Zhongmei Li, Bing Wang, Jialiang Wang, Hongtian Chen, Bin Jiang
publish: IEEE Transactions on Automation Science and Engineering
abstract: This paper presents a gradient-based fault detection method for pitch control systems in avionics. On the basis of the dynamic model of the airplane, the proposed method detects both operator and sensor faults by monitoring the online data. By integrating fault-related behaviors over an extended time window, the method effectively amplifies small changes caused by incipient faults, improving detectability. Theoretical analysis reveals that under normal flight conditions, the gradient has a zero expected value and a finite, analytically tractable variance. These characteristics make the method compatible with traditional fault detection approaches. Sufficient tests on real flight data verify its ability to detect hard-to-identify faults.

title: Performance Analysis of Multi-UAV-Aided NB-IoT Communication System
authors: Siva Rama Krishna M., Naveen Mysore Balasubramanya
publish: IEEE Transactions on Mobile Computing
abstract: Non-terrestrial networks (NTNs) for Internet of things (IoT) are one of the prominent scenarios envisioned in the fifth generation (5G) of wireless communication and beyond. The third generation partnership project (3GPP) has identified the narrowband IoT (NB-IoT) standard as a candidate technology for NTN-based IoT. In this work, an unmanned aerial vehicle (UAV)-based NTN using NB-IoT for communication is considered, where multiple UAVs act as relays between user equipment (UEs) and the terrestrial base station (BS). The communication procedure for the proposed system is designed by building upon the uplink and downlink communication procedures of a single UAV-aided system. To facilitate efficient data transmission between UAVs and the BS, scheduling mechanisms using orthogonal multiple access (OMA) and non-orthogonal multiple access (NOMA) are explored. For the NOMA scheduling mechanism, two grouping strategies are proposed for UAVs - static grouping and dynamic grouping, where the latter leverages the longest transmission time first (LTTF) algorithm for optimal grouping. Closed-form expressions for average transmission time are derived under both strategies, accounting for UE distribution modeled by discrete uniform, Poisson, and Beta distributions. Finally, we perform end-to-end physical layer simulations using 3GPP-compliant channel models and evaluate the latency and energy consumption of NB-IoT UEs. It is observed from simulations that NOMA, along with dynamic grouping, offers lower latency and reduced energy consumption when compared to OMA and static grouping, validating its suitability for multi-UAV-based NTN applications.

title: Trusted Data Spaces for Vehicle-Road-Cloud Collaboration: Architecture, Challenges, and Future Directions
authors: Juhao Hu, Jiazheng Zhang, Jing Wu, Yuhan Yang, Chengnian Long, Bo Li
publish: IEEE Network
abstract: Vehicle-Road-Cloud Collaborative Systems (VRCCS) promise enhanced intelligent transportation by integrating vehicles, road infrastructure, and cloud computing. However, data sharing among these components raises concerns of trust, security, and privacy. This paper proposes a Trusted Data Space (TDS) architecture for VRCCS to enable secure and efficient data sharing among multiple stakeholders. We first analyze the heterogeneity and security attributes of VRCCS data and propose a blockchain-powered architecture that integrates edge-cloud federated systems, lifecycle-aware data governance, and trust mechanisms. We then identify three critical challenges: (1) realtime fusion of multi-source sensor data across dynamic networks, (2) scalable and efficient management of spatio-temporal dynamic V2X data, and (3) regulatory compliance with evolving standards (e.g., GAIA-X/IDS interoperability). Our goal is to provide insights and recommendations to guide future deployments of trusted data spaces in vehicular networks.

title: Continuous Feature Representation of Power Grid Topology for Generalizable Data-Driven Dynamic Security Assessment
authors: Hang Qi, Changgang Li, Yutian Liu, Jiongcheng Yan, Hua Ye
publish: IEEE Transactions on Power Systems
abstract: The power grid topology is a critical factor influencing the dynamic security of power systems. Due to regular line maintenance, the topology undergoes frequent changes. To adapt to different topologies, the input feature set for data-driven dynamic security assessment (DSA) needs to incorporate topological information. In this paper, a continuous feature representation method of power grid topology is proposed, aiming to achieve the data-driven DSA with high topological generalization performance. Firstly, the power grid topology is continuously represented by the electrical coordinates of representative nodes, and an efficient calculation method of electrical coordinates after topology changes is proposed. Then, representative nodes are selected based on canonical correlation analysis, and the injective characteristic of topology features is considered in the selection process. Finally, the features of the pre-fault operating condition, fault location, and topology are merged via multimodal feature fusion, and a topology-adaptive DSA model is constructed. The generalization performance of the DSA model on unknown topologies is verified on the New England 39-bus system and a provincial system in China.

title: LLM-Based Emulation of the Radio Resource Control Layer: Towards AI-Native RAN Protocols
authors: Ziming Liu, Bryan Liu, Alvaro Valcarce, Xiaoli Chu
publish: IEEE Journal on Selected Areas in Communications
abstract: Integrating Large AI Models (LAMs) into 6G mobile networks is a key enabler of the AI-Native Air Interface (AI-AI), where protocol intelligence must scale beyond handcrafted logic. This paper presents, to our knowledge, the first standards-compliant emulation of the Radio Resource Control (RRC) layer using a decoder-only LAM (LLAMA-class) fine-tuned with Low-Rank Adaptation (LoRA) on a multi-vendor corpus of real-world traces spanning both 5G and 4G systems. We treat RRC as a domain-specific language and construct a segmentation-safe Question-and-Answer (QA) dataset that preserves Abstract Syntax Notation (ASN.1) structure through linearization prior to Byte Pair Encoding (BPE) tokenization. The proposed approach combines parameter-efficient adaptation with schema-bounded prompting to ensure syntactic and procedural fidelity. Evaluation introduces a standards-aware triad—ASN.1 conformance, field-level coverage analysis, and uplink-to-downlink state-machine checks—alongside semantic similarity and latency profiling across 120 configurations. On 30k 5G request–response pairs plus an additional 4.8k QA turns from 4G sessions, our 8B model achieves a median cosine similarity of 0.97, a 61% relative gain over a zero-shot baseline, while sustaining high conformance rates. These results demonstrate that LAMs, when augmented with protocol-aware reasoning, can directly orchestrate control-plane procedures, laying the foundation for the future Artificial Intelligence (AI)-native Radio Access Network (RAN).

title: Distilling Missed Samples for Remote Sensing Oriented Object Detectors
authors: Longfei He, Peng Chen, Bin Dong, Yidan Zhang, Jingen Ni, Zicong Zhu
publish: IEEE Journal of Selected Topics in Applied Earth Observations and Remote Sensing
abstract: Knowledge distillation is one of the most effective methods for improving the performance of lightweight detectors, which is crucial for the development of remote sensing (RS) edge intelligence. However, lightweight detectors exhibit significant missed detection when detecting RS targets with arbitrary orientations and extreme scales. This is due to insufficient discriminative features learned by the lightweight student network under standard training, as well as the lack of a targeted, coordinated sample-optimization design. To address these problems, this paper proposes a knowledge Distillation method based on Missed-sample Reinforcement, named DMR. Specifically, a Cross-scale Missed-sample Enhancement module is designed to transfer the teacher's classification knowledge, thereby bridging the core differences in classification. Based on differences between teacher and student predictions, it dynamically selects missed-detection samples, guiding the student model to focus on them. A Dynamic Local Feature Alignment module is also proposed to distill the local key-feature distribution from the teacher model. It efficiently acquires contextual information from local features, guiding the student model to extract discriminative target features. Furthermore, we introduce a generative adversarial process to constrain the student's angle predictions, enabling it to fully utilize the angular information predicted by the teacher model. Extensive experiments on three public RS datasets achieve state-of-the-art (SOTA) performance, demonstrating the effectiveness of the proposed method.

title: A Simple SEU-Rate Equation Derived From an Exponential Cross-Section Curve Approximation
authors: Daisuke Kobayashi, Ayumu Ikuta
publish: IEEE Transactions on Nuclear Science
abstract: Predicting the single-event upset (SEU) rates of semiconductor devices is essential for their deployment in space and terrestrial cosmic-ray environments. Although various SEU-rate equations were derived for this purpose from different approximations of heavy-ion SEU cross-section curves, no previous studies used an exponential function approximation. This study aims to derive an SEU-rate equation from this approximation and evaluate its validity. The derived equation has a simple closed-form comprising only three physically-intuitive parameters. It shows the potential to predict the SEU rates of a device for galactic cosmic-rays (heavy-ion radiation) on the geosynchronous orbit and atmospheric cosmic-rays (neutron radiation) on the ground in a unified manner, simply by replacing a coefficient. Testing the equation with experimental data of devices fabricated in 250 to 7nm technology nodes shows that our equation can predict their SEU rates within a factor of 2 for the galactic cosmic-rays and 3 for the atmospheric cosmic-rays as accurate as the famous Petersen’s figure-of-merit formula. A further exploration of this similarity between our equation and Petersen’s formula indicates that they are not merely similar but rather identical. The current Petersen’s formula relies on heuristic calibrations to enhance its prediction accuracy. These calibrations can be explained through the exponential-function approximation.

title: Dishonest Majority Passive-to-Active Compiler over Rings for MPC with Constant Online Communication
authors: Jiandong Zhang, Han Jiang, Chenkai Zeng, Qi Feng, Debiao He, Chuan Zhao, Yunxue Yan, Qiuliang Xu
publish: IEEE Transactions on Information Forensics and Security
abstract: Secure multiparty computation (MPC) over Z2k is more efficient than computations over fields, and studying MPC protocols under malicious security has practical application value. Malicious security with a dishonest majority over rings remains challenging. The most popular approach is SPDZ2k , however, this is a specific protocol that does not support the transformation of any existing semi-honest MPC protocols into malicious security protocols. The zero knowledge proof (ZKP)-based compiler satisfies this requirement. Existing state-of-the-art protocols have logarithmic online communication overhead in terms of the circuit size |C|, and their direct application to rings is nontrivial as they were originally designed for finite fields. In this work, we investigate the communication overhead to develop malicious security protocols. We bridge the gap between malicious security with abort and semi-honest security, by constructing a “GMW-style” verification protocol to achieve malicious security in a dishonest majority setting. This approach incurs a constant online communication overhead by enhancing the machinery of zero-knowledge fully linear interactive oracle proof (zk-FLIOP). Additionally, we extend the zk-FLIOP to work over any ring by invoking reverse multiplication friendly embeddings (RMFEs). Our results show that the online communication complexity of the verification process depends on only the security parameter, the number of parties, and the ring size. Furthermore, for small-scale circuits over Z2, we designed a distributed lookup table argument where both the total communication complexity and the computational cost are independent of the circuit size but of the input wires.

title:
Rare Earth-Activated Y
                    <sub>2</sub>
                    O
                    <sub>3</sub>
                    Tio
                    <sub>2</sub>
                    Anode Materials for Uv and Nir Harvesting in Dye-Sensitized Solar Cells
authors: Bingxin Zhao, Ying Su, Junjie Hao, Xiao Wei Sun
publish: 2025 International Conference on Energy, Materials, and Photonics (EMP)
abstract: This study introduces a novel photoluminescent anode material for dye-sensitized solar cells (DSSCs), utilizing <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathrm{Y}_{2} \mathrm{O}_{3}-\text{TiO}_{2}$</tex> activated with <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\text{Eu}^{3+}$</tex> and <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\text{Yb}^{3+} / \text{Er}^{3+}$</tex> to enhance sunlight harvesting in the ultraviolet 220-300 nm and near-infrared 900-1020 nm regions. The integration of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathrm{Y}_{2} \mathrm{O}_{3}$</tex> onto <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\text{TiO}_{2}$</tex> crystallites enables up-conversion with <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\text{Yb}^{3+} / \text{Er}^{3+}$</tex> and down-conversion with <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\text{Eu}^{3+}$</tex> within the anode, converting non-absorbable photons into visible light 500-700 nm aligned with the N719 dye's absorption spectrum. Photovoltaic performance data demonstrate modest efficiency gains, with <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\text{Yb}^{3+} / \text{Er}^{3+}: \mathrm{Y}_{2} \mathrm{O}_{3}-\text{TiO}_{2}$</tex> achieving a power conversion efficiency of 7.87 % and JSC of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$16.44 ~\text{mA} / \text{cm}^{2}$</tex>, and <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\text{Eu}^{3+}: \mathrm{Y}_{2} \mathrm{O}_{3-} \text{TiO}_{2}$</tex> reaching 7.84% with JSC of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$16.90 ~\text{mA} / \text{cm}^{2}$</tex>, compared to the undoped reference at 7.83 % and <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$16.08 ~\text{mA} / \text{cm}^{2}$</tex>. These improvements, driven by increased JSC, align with calculated maximum efficiencies, <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\Delta \eta_{\max }=0.0101 \%$</tex> for <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\text{Yb}^{3+} / \text{Er}^{3+}$</tex>, 0.0020 % for <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\text{Eu}^{3+}$</tex> using corrected absorbance, reflecting contributions from up-converted NIR and down-converted visible photons. The <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\text{Eu}^{3+}$</tex> system's limited efficiency is attributed to negligible solar flux at 220-300 nm and weak <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$f-f$</tex> transitions at 300-470 nm.

title: Grid-Forming Direct Power Control to Improve Power Tracking Performance
authors: Zhaohua Hu, Zhen Gong, Xu Li, Yufei Teng, Pengyu Pan, Xiaorong Xie
publish: 2025 IEEE 9th Conference on Energy Internet and Energy System Integration (EI2)
abstract: To address the limitations of existing grid-forming (GFM) controls, e.g., Virtual Synchronous Generator (VSG) regarding slow response characteristics of tracking power references and real & reactive power couplings, this paper presents an innovative Grid-Forming Direct Power Control (GFM-DPC) strategy, aiming to enhance rapid power tracking control and keep grid forming performance. First, the development of a control structure for GFM-DPC is presented; Second, the closed-loop transfer function model that captures the power dynamics of the GFM-DPC system is investigated; Third, a comparative analysis of the dynamic performance between the proposed method and conventional VSG controllers is conducted. Finally, theoretical derivations and simulation experiments validate that the GFM-DPC significantly improves the converter's power reference tracking accuracy and power decoupling performance in weak grids, while also ensuring grid forming performance.

title: Laser-Assisted Grinding and Polishing of Titanium Alloys
authors: Sifan Huo, Yunzhou Ma, Zhiquan Guo, Yan Wang, Zhongpeng Zheng, Jingnan Zhao
publish: 2025 3rd Asian Aerospace and Astronautics Conference (AAAC)
abstract: Laser-assisted grinding and polishing (LAG) technology combines the advantages of laser heating and mechanical grinding and polishing, significantly improving the processing efficiency of titanium alloys and enhancing surface quality. This paper reviews the research progress of LAG in titanium alloy processing, focusing on the mechanism of laser-grinding composite field coupling and the response characteristics after laser-assisted grinding. It analyzes the impact of this technology on material removal behavior, tool wear, and subsurface damage, and summarizes its application potential and future development directions in aerospace manufacturing.

title: A Methodology for Assessing the Demand Response Potential of Air-Conditioning Loads Based on Electric-Thermal Equivalent Parameter Model
authors: Xu Chen, Xinming He, Mingjie Ma, Yutong Wu, Guohui Wang, Lufang Guo, Huijuan Zhou
publish: 2025 IEEE 9th Conference on Energy Internet and Energy System Integration (EI2)
abstract: Demand-side management serves as a critical mechanism for enabling load-side resources to actively respond to grid demand. Air conditioning (AC) loads had exhibited substantial potential for demand response (DR), as one of the most widely distributed demand-side resources. However, the diversity of AC load behaviors and their inherent uncertainties pose significant challenges to efficiently and reliably harnessing their DR potential. To address these challenges, this paper proposes an equivalent parameter model-based methodology for assessing the DR potential of AC loads, with a focus on analyzing the uncertainty factors influencing their responsiveness. First, an electric-thermal parameter model for aggregated AC loads is developed to investigate the operational mechanisms of AC loads participating in DR. Subsequently, a comprehensive DR potential evaluation framework is established, incorporating standby capacity, response time, duration time, and ramp rate as key metrics. By introducing ambient temperature as an uncertainty factor, the uncertainty range of AC load DR potential is comparatively analyzed. Experimental results demonstrate that ambient temperature exerts lower uncertainty on the flexible participation of AC load clusters in peak shaving, with enhanced controllability during peak load periods.

title: Stepwise Offering of Wind-Storage Plants in Hourly-Ahead Energy Markets
authors: Ziqi Shen, Wei Wei, Xuemin Zhang, Shengwei Mei
publish: 2025 IEEE 9th Conference on Energy Internet and Energy System Integration (EI2)
abstract: Equipped with energy storage, wind farms can benefit from short-term market participation through flexible scheduling and bidding. The volatility of wind generation is the primary challenge affecting the profitability of wind-storage plants in energy markets. This paper presents a stepwise offering strategy for wind-storage plants in the hourly-ahead energy market. In each hour, the plant must submit a piecewise linear supply-price curve without knowing the exact market-clearing price or wind output. After the clearing price is revealed, the portion of the submitted supply curve below this price is accepted. In real-time operation, the plant is required to deliver the accepted volume, and any shortfall incurs a penalty. The proposed method leverages ultra-short-term forecast information for the next four hours using predictive trajectories. By treating the offering volumes at fixed price levels as decision variables, the optimal bidding problem is cast as a two-stage stochastic program and solved with a mixed-integer linear programming solver. Numerical results demonstrate that the proposed stepwise offering strategy earns a 3% higher profit than the point-offering strategy.

title: Research on Low-Carbon Economic Optimization Game Scheduling for Integrated Energy Systems Based on CCUS
authors: Tengfei Wei, Jichang Yang, Cong Wang, Qikun Cheng
publish: 2025 IEEE 9th Conference on Energy Internet and Energy System Integration (EI2)
abstract: To achieve equitable distribution of benefits among multiple entities within Integrated Energy Systems (IES), address environmental pollution, and promote low-carbon energy transition, in this paper, we propose an optimization dispatch model for IES incorporating carbon capture, utilization, and storage (CCUS) within a master-slave game framework. This model effectively reduces carbon emissions from cogeneration units, lowers system operating costs, and mitigates environmental pollution. The paper constructs a joint operation model integrating CCUS, Power-to-Gas (P2G), and Combined Heat and Power unit (CHP). Considering comprehensive demand response, it establishes models for each entity within the integrated energy system and constructs a Stackelberg game model featuring one master and multiple slaves. Using a northern region as a case study and validated with an adaptive differential evolution algorithm, the analysis demonstrates that the scheduling strategy for the CCUS-based integrated energy system effectively reduces operational costs and carbon emissions compared to traditional energy systems while enhancing the integration capacity of renewable energy.

title: Research on Fault Line Selection Method for Neutral Point Flexible Grounding System Based on Zero-Sequence Current Phase Difference Characteristics
authors: Feng Gao, Hongbo Guo, Yuebo Jia, Zhendong Xu, Huanbin Liang
publish: 2025 IEEE 9th Conference on Energy Internet and Energy System Integration (EI2)
abstract: In the small-current grounding system of distribution networks, the zero-sequence current signal of the line is extremely weak during a single-phase grounding fault, making accurate fault line selection particularly challenging, especially in high-impedance grounding faults. This paper proposes a novel fault line selection scheme for distribution networks based on a new neutral point flexible grounding approach. The proposed method introduces a small resistance grounding branch at the neutral point via a high-speed power electronic switch during a single-phase fault, combining the high reliability of small-current grounding systems with the prominent signal characteristics of large-current grounding systems. By utilizing the zero-sequence current amplitude and the phase difference changes of the zero-sequence current before and after the introduction of the small resistance as criteria, the method effectively addresses the issue of unreliable fault line selection under high-impedance grounding conditions, achieving accurate fault line selection in distribution networks. Simulations of single-phase grounding faults in a distribution network were conducted using the PSCAD/EMTDC simulation environment, and the results demonstrate the high reliability and effectiveness of the proposed scheme under various fault conditions.

title: Economic Evaluation Model for Peak-Shaving Arbitrage of Energy Storage on the Side of Large Industrial Users Considering Load Optimization Strategies
authors: Bing Gu, Sheng Qiao
publish: 2025 IEEE 9th Conference on Energy Internet and Energy System Integration (EI2)
abstract: In the context of China's deepening energy transformation in the power industry, the expansion of renewable energy installed capacity and the optimization of the energy structure have made energy storage technology crucial for addressing grid integration challenges of renewable energy (context and background). However, there is a need to assess the economic feasibility of energy storage projects for large industrial users to encourage wider adoption (motivation). We hypothesize that by developing a peak-shaving arbitrage economic evaluation model, we can effectively assess the economic benefits of energy storage systems for these users, maximizing net present value (hypothesis). This study investigates the 24-hour time-of-use electricity price curve and load curve for large industrial users in the Jibei region and analyzes the impact of energy storage charging and discharging strategies on load curve optimization (methods). Our results indicate that the proposed economic evaluation model demonstrates high economic viability for large industrial users installing energy storage systems for peak-shaving arbitrage, with a positive net present value suggesting significant economic benefits over a 10-year period (results). We conclude that the model provides a valuable tool for evaluating the economic feasibility of energy storage projects on the large industrial user side, promoting the development of energy storage technologies and the optimization of renewable energy integration (conclusions).

title: Adaptive Self-Healing Video Encryption Framework for Robust Multimedia Security
authors: P Padmaprasad Shenoy, Hemalatha S, Prashanth Barla
publish: 2026 Sixth International Conference on Advances in Electrical, Computing, Communications and Sustainable Technologies (ICAECT)
abstract: With the rapid growth of digital video transmission, ensuring multimedia confidentiality and resilience has become increasingly critical. This study presents a novel self-healing video encryption framework that builds on the Advanced Encryption Standard (AES) by integrating adaptive key derivation, chunk-level processing, and automated recovery mechanisms. The system intelligently detects and reconstructs corrupted video segments during decryption, preventing complete data loss and preserving playback continuity. All encryption and decryption procedures are carried out entirely on the client side to ensure that no unprotected video data leaves the user's system. The client sends only the encrypted key file and encrypted video to the server for storage or sharing. Through configurable security levels and optional pre-encryption compression, the framework strikes a balance between speed, storage efficiency, and data protection.

title: Fault Ride-Through and Transient Reactive Power Support Strategy for Grid-Forming Converter Based on Inner Potential Amplitude-Phase Correction
authors: Xijun Ren, Zhiwei Li, Yufeng Liu, Chencan Zheng, Huizhou Liu, Hao Liu, Ziran Jin, Donghai Zhu
publish: 2025 IEEE 9th Conference on Energy Internet and Energy System Integration (EI2)
abstract: Grid-forming (GFM) converters are susceptible to overcurrent during grid faults. The typical current limiting strategy is switching the converter to grid-following (GFL) control mode, thereby sacrificing the converter's grid-forming capabilities. Conversely, if the GFM control is maintained, the power outer loop exhibits slow dynamic response, which hinders the converter's ability to fulfill the rapid reactive current support requirements of grid codes. To address this issue, this paper first analyzes the requirements for converter's internal potential in terms of fault current limiting and reactive power support. Building on this analysis, the amplitude and phase correction term are designed for both fault occurrence and recovery stage. Finally, the analysis and proposed control strategy are verified by the simulations.

title: Multi-Objective Scheduling Method for Integrated Operation of Thermal Energy Storage-Load-Residual Heat Systems
authors: Guangyu Sun, Xintong Ma, Yangyang Ge, Luyu Yang, Kaiwen Shi, Yao Liang
publish: 2025 IEEE 9th Conference on Energy Internet and Energy System Integration (EI2)
abstract: Addressing the persistent conflict between intermittent renewable generation and inflexible heating demand in integrated energy systems (IES), this study proposes a multi-objective optimization scheduling method that leverages coordinated operation of thermal energy storage, load management, and waste heat recovery. An integrated electricity-heating system model is developed, incorporating heat network transmission dynamics, complementary coordination between electrical and thermal storage, and constraints from multiple flexible resources. The resulting model is formulated as a mixed-integer nonlinear programming (MINLP) problem aimed at minimizing operating costs, curtailment penalties, peak-valley differentials, and power fluctuations. Nonlinear constraints are handled via thermal storage linearization and the big-M method, and the model is solved using the SBB solver in GAMS. Simulation studies based on a coupled system demonstrate that the proposed method significantly enhances the system’s capacity to absorb curtailed renewable energy and strengthens its peak shaving performance during high-demand periods through coordinated electricity and heat storage scheduling. The approach effectively achieves multi-objective optimization across economic, low-carbon, and operational stability goals.

title: Whole Process of Frequency Response Embedded Two-stage Robust Optimal Operation of Transmission and Distribution Network
authors: Guangming Liu, Yanjing Chen, Peng Gao
publish: 2025 IEEE 9th Conference on Energy Internet and Energy System Integration (EI2)
abstract: It is difficult for independent transmission and distribution systems (T&DSs) to achieve optimal economic scheduling and frequency security due to low inertia characteristic and uncertainty of renewable energy. For this reason, this paper proposes a two-stage robust optimal operation model considering the whole process of frequency response which is based on the cooperation of TDS. The column and constraint generation (C&CG) algorithm and strong duality theory are used to transform the above model into a mixed-integer second-order cone programming (MISOCP) model and solve it and improve the frequency security and operation economy of the system at the same time.

title: Fine-Tuned BERT-Based Framework for Accurate Grammar Error Detection in English Academic Writing
authors: G Immanuel, B Sridevi, Mary Surya Kala, Shagufta Parween, M.G. Abinaya, R Saravanakumar
publish: 2026 Sixth International Conference on Advances in Electrical, Computing, Communications and Sustainable Technologies (ICAECT)
abstract: Grammatical Error Detection is vital to natural language processing as it detects spelling, punctuation, wording, and grammar errors in written text. This task forms a core component of automated writing tools and learning applications. Owing to their inability to grasp the deep contextual relationships within sentences, conventional rulebased and machine-learning methods have exhibited only modest performance. Contemporary transformer-based architectures, exemplified by BERT, have made major strides by extracting bidirectional contextual information and semantic connections. However, models that employ BERT with conventional SoftMax outputs frequently exhibit erratic predictions in sequence-tagging tasks. To mitigate this shortcoming, this research presents a novel framework named CrBERT, which merges BERT, fine-tuned for grammatical awareness, with a Conditional Random Field layer to enhance grammatical error detection. The CrBERT model leverages BERT for generating deep contextual embeddings, while the CRF component ensures well-structured and consistent output by modeling the dependencies among predicted labels. Training and assessment performed on the public GED dataset deliver an outstanding 98 % accuracy, markedly surpassing baseline approaches. Experimental results indicate that CrBERT exhibits robust ability in identifying grammatical errors in context-sensitive settings. Future work could involve enhancing the system's capabilities by accommodating multilingual data, integrating syntactic and semantic parsing modules, and investigating semi-supervised learning to lessen reliance on labeled data. This study supplies a solid groundwork for the development of grammar checking systems that are both more intelligent and more adaptable.

title: Analysis of Common-Mode Interference Characteristics and Influence Mechanisms in Cascaded H-Bridge Converter-Based Battery Energy Storage Systems
authors: Gaoxian Du, Hao Liu, Xiong Zhan, Ke Li, Yating Liu, Bohao Huang, Zhanzhan Qu
publish: 2025 IEEE 9th Conference on Energy Internet and Energy System Integration (EI2)
abstract: Common-mode interference poses significant challenges to the safety and stable operation of cascaded H-bridge converter-based battery energy storage systems (CHB-BESS). To address the inadequate understanding of the conduction mechanisms and influencing factors of common-mode interference in such systems, this paper first conducts experimental tests on a self-built prototype of a storage submodule (SSM). The parasitic capacitances from the SSM's positive and negative busbars to ground are accurately extracted. Using these extracted parameters, a detailed simulation model of a 35kV CHB-BESS is constructed to analyze the electrical characteristics of common-mode interference. The results show that the dv/dt of the SSM can reach 1.4 MV/s, and the common-mode current amplitude can be as high as 143.2 mA. Furthermore, parasitic capacitance and switching frequency are identified as key factors influencing the amplitude and frequency distribution of common-mode interference. These findings provide a technical basis for the reliable operation of high-voltage battery energy storage systems.

title: Design and Analysis of a PET-Based Conformal Ultra-Broadband Monopole Antenna
authors: Kanwar Preet Kaur, Simardeep Kaur, Shimpy Ralhan
publish: 2026 Sixth International Conference on Advances in Electrical, Computing, Communications and Sustainable Technologies (ICAECT)
abstract: In this paper, a planar monopole antenna with ultra-broadband and conformal characteristics made of flexible Polyethylene Terephthalate (PET) substrate is designed and analyzed. The conductive element, a ten-point star (TPS), is engraved on a partially grounded PET substrate. The proposed antenna exhibits an ultra-broad response from 2.9 GHz to 14.46 GHz, which is sufficiently broad to be utilized in diverse applications. The desired ultra-broad bandwidth is achieved with a substrate thickness of 0.8 mm by stacking four layers of PET, 0.2 mm each. The measured relative permittivity and the loss tangent of the PET are 1.7 and 0.075, respectively. The proposed antenna has a small profile of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$1.158 \lambda_{0} \times 0.868 \lambda_{0} \times 0.023 \lambda_{0}$</tex> at the center frequency of 8.68 GHz with physical dimensions of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$40 \times 30 \times 0.8 \text{mm}^{3}$</tex>. Numerical data shows that this antenna has an ultra-broad -10 dB impedance bandwidth of 11.56 GHz and a fractional bandwidth of 133.18%. The antenna has an average gain of 3.3 dBi and a radiation efficiency of above 60%. Due to its ultra-broadband nature, mechanical flexibility, and small geometry profile, the designed antenna is a good candidate for wearable, portable, and high-frequency wireless communication systems.

title: Dynamic State Estimation for Distributed Photovoltaic Systems Using Cubature Kalman Filter
authors: Yuan Li, Linli Zhang, Yi Zhang, Min Huang, Jianan Shan
publish: 2025 IEEE 9th Conference on Energy Internet and Energy System Integration (EI2)
abstract: The growing integration of renewable energy sources, especially photovoltaic (PV) systems, plays a vital role in enhancing energy efficiency and promoting sustainability. However, due to their high dependence on weather conditions, PV systems often exhibit significant intermittency and unpredictability, which complicates power system monitoring, control, and overall stability. To address these issues and strengthen situational awareness as well as operational reliability in PV-integrated grids, accurate and real-time Dynamic State Estimation (DSE) has become increasingly critical. In this paper, we propose the application of the Cubature Kalman Filter (CKF) for DSE in PV systems. The CKF offers a powerful nonlinear filtering framework, which is capable of accurately estimating the internal dynamic states of the PV systems. The simulation results demonstrate that the proposed CKF-based DSE approach effectively tracks the dynamic states of the PV system, thereby contributing to the improved accuracy of state estimation of the PV system.

title: Privacy Preservation Techniques with Federated Learning in IoT Context
authors: Codruta Maria Serban, Gheorghe Sebestyen, Anca Hangan
publish: 2025 IEEE 21st International Conference on Intelligent Computer Communication and Processing (ICCP)
abstract: Centralized processing of data from IoT devices can generate privacy issues, as sensitive information, such as medical information, would be transmitted to a less secure cloud system. In classification problems, one solution would be to use federated learning (FL) to generate a global model from locally trained classifiers without transferring raw data through the network. There is always a trade-off between privacy and performance that should be reviewed when designing an IoT system. In this paper, we conducted experiments in two use cases (myocardial infarction detection and smoke detection) to demonstrate the applicability of FL in various domains. Using multiple federated learning techniques, we show that the small negative impact on accuracy is reasonable considering the privacy benefit of FL. The experiments show that with an appropriate aggregation scheme, FL obtains accuracy similar to that of the centralized approach and outperforms the non-FL distributed computation.

title: GenX: AI-Powered Genomic Hybridization Analysis and Prediction
authors: Mayukh Neogi, Saphalya Das, Anasuya Sengupta
publish: 2026 Sixth International Conference on Advances in Electrical, Computing, Communications and Sustainable Technologies (ICAECT)
abstract: GenX is a computational framework designed to simulate and analyze genomic hybridization between different species by creating hybrid DNA sequence using an advanced window-based merger method that retains essential genetic elements such as start codes with configurable prices. The system uses a comprehensive functional extraction pipeline to analyze genomic properties including GC content, sequence length, CG dinucleotide frequency, sequence complexity, and repeat density, while utilizing a random forest classifier trained on extensive simulated datasets (N = 1000) to predict hybrid viability with strong accuracy. Framework includes an interactive visualization suit that provides detailed representation of genomic disruption, inheritance patterns, crossover points, mutation events and confidence intervals, as well as a genetic contribution to the original species of extensive heritage analysis. GenX acts as a valuable tool for genetic engineering science, protective genetics and evolutionary biology research by offering a computational approach to studying the potential consequences of genomic hybridization events before experimental verification, making researchers effective to identify the hybridization landscapes.

title: A Digital Lisheng Platform-Based Substation Equipment Status Prediction Method Integrating Transformer and Spatiotemporal Attention Mechanism
authors: Chunmei Zhang, Lijuan Duan, Xuan Guo, Xingque Xu, Jianyi Li
publish: 2025 IEEE 9th Conference on Energy Internet and Energy System Integration (EI2)
abstract: In the current context of smart grid development, it is difficult to effectively analyze the complex spatiotemporal coupling features and long-range dependencies in equipment monitoring data. This study discusses a prediction model that combines Transformer and spatiotemporal attention mechanisms. First, the multi-head self-attention mechanism in the Transformer encoder is leveraged to globally capture the complex long-term dependencies and dynamic patterns in power equipment state sequences. A parallelized spatiotemporal attention module is then designed. Finally, a gated fusion mechanism is used to adaptively integrate spatiotemporal features. Experimental results show that the proposed FTST (Fusion Transformer Spatio-Temporal) model achieves an RMSE of 1.93 and a MAPE of 47.29%. Model ablation experiments verify the critical role of the spatiotemporal attention mechanism in model performance. Visual analysis of the spatiotemporal attention mechanism reveals the spatiotemporal coupling relationships between devices. These results demonstrate that the model integrating the Transformer with the spatiotemporal attention mechanism achieves high accuracy and interpretability in substation equipment state prediction, providing a more accurate and reliable solution for smart grids.

title: Deloading Status Based PFR Parameters Allocation Method for Wind Turbines
authors: Qingwei Jiao, Qizhen Yang, Runtian Qiu, Xiaobo Sun, Jingwen Xin, Lei Chen
publish: 2025 IEEE 9th Conference on Energy Internet and Energy System Integration (EI2)
abstract: The integration of a high proportion of wind turbines into the grid poses severe challenges to the frequency security of power systems. Deloading-based primary frequency regulation (PFR) serves as a crucial technical scheme for wind farms to participate in system frequency support, which aims to reserve spare capacity through deloading control and increase the active power output during frequency events to provide active power support. This paper proposes a PFR parameter allocation method for wind turbines considering their deloading status. First, deloading allocation strategies for wind farms are introduced, including over-speed deloading, pitch-angle deloading, and coordinated over-speed and pitch-angle deloading. Then, an improved PFR parameter allocation method is proposed based on the rotor speed and pitch angle of individual turbines within the wind farm after deloading. Finally, a system model incorporating thermal power unit, wind farm, and loads is developed to validate the superior performance of the proposed method.

title: A Dual-Optimized VMD-LSTM Photovoltaic Power Output Forecasting Model Based on SSA*
authors: Guoliang Lv, Guomin Luo, Miao Chen, Haozhe Qin, Chengwei Tian, Luying Wang, Xiudan Yao
publish: 2025 IEEE 9th Conference on Energy Internet and Energy System Integration (EI2)
abstract: The generation of distributed power sources exhibits randomness and uncertainty, which significantly impacts the operation and maintenance of existing distribution networks. To enhance the performance of distribution network protection and mitigate the impact of distributed generation on the grid, this paper proposes a photovoltaic (PV) output forecasting model based on a dual-optimized VMD-LSTM neural network using the Sparrow Search Algorithm (SSA). The paper first reviews the current state of domestic and international research on PV output forecasting, then establishes a VMD-LSTM neural network, and finally employs SSA for dual-layer parameter optimization. Simulation experiments validate the effectiveness of the proposed PV output forecasting model and demonstrate its excellent predictive capability.

title: Semantic Reconstruction and Infrared Anomaly Detection in ADAS Perception Systems
authors: Timotei-Cristian Morar, Vlad-Cristian Miclea
publish: 2025 IEEE 21st International Conference on Intelligent Computer Communication and Processing (ICCP)
abstract: Advanced Driver Assistance Systems (ADAS) are vulnerable to physical adversarial attacks in the infrared (IR) spectrum, which remain invisible to human drivers yet are captured by vehicle cameras.
