This paper presents a two-stage architecture for detecting and correcting IR adversarial noise when applied on traffic signs<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">1</sup>. The first stage employs classical image processing techniques to extract the region of interest (ROI) and detect anomalous patches using color segmentation in the HSV space. The second stage leverages a Context Encoder, trained adversarially, to semantically inpaint the masked area based on surrounding visual context. A convolutional neural network (CNN) classifier is then used to accurately recognize the reconstructed sign. The proposed system was tested on a custom-augmented GTSRB dataset including synthetically corrupted IR patches. Results show that our method achieves a PSNR of 26.64 dB and SSIM of 0.81 for reconstruction, and classification accuracy of $95.85 \%$ for the full system, significantly improving robustness compared to baselines. This approach enables real-time, software-based mitigation of IR adversarial attacks without requiring hardware changes.

title: Letter from the new Editors-in-Chief
authors: Melissa Densmore, Waylon Brunette, Sunandan Chakraborty
publish: ACM Journal on Computing and Sustainable Societies
abstract: None

title: Quench Characteristics and Protection of the CEPC Detector Magnet
authors: Menglin Wang, Ling Zhao, Rui Kang, Zhilong Hou, Zhongxiu Liu, Qingyuan Li, Qingjin Xu, Feipeng Ning
publish: IEEE Transactions on Applied Superconductivity
abstract: The Circular Electron Positron Collider (CEPC) detector magnet is a large-scale superconducting magnet with stringent quench protection requirements. To enhance the mechanical stability of the coils, the aluminum-stabilized NbTi/Cu cable adopts a box-type structure, in which part of the outer high-purity aluminum is replaced by high-strength aluminum alloy. This modification leads to a degradation in the quench characteristics and thermal stability of the coils. This paper comprehensively evaluates a protection strategy combining a 50 mΩ dump resistor with the quench-back effect provided by the support cylinder. Rapid assessment using the MIITS (integral of I2 over time) method confirmed that this configuration maintains the terminal voltage below 1 kV and limits the maximum coil temperature to 59.22 K. A detailed three-dimensional finite element analysis further quantified the quench behavior: the peak hotspot temperature reached 56.7 K, and the current decay time constant is 140 s, underscoring the contribution of the quench-back cylinder. The minimum quench energy is calculated to range from 16.4 J to 20 J, depending on the quench initiation location. The magnet's intrinsic self-protecting capability is demonstrated under various fault conditions; peak temperatures remained within acceptable limits even with protection delays up to 10 s, and reached a maximum of 97.42 K only in the event of a complete protection system failure. These results validate the reliability of the quench protection design for the CEPC detector magnet.

title: A Method for Active Distribution Network Power Flow Simulation Integrating Variational Model Decomposition with CNN-BiLSTM
authors: Xuebin Jin, Dongli Jia, Xueshun Ye, Zhen Zhang
publish: 2025 IEEE 9th Conference on Energy Internet and Energy System Integration (EI2)
abstract: Building a new power system is a key measure for achieving China's dual carbon goals. As a core component of situational awareness in new distribution systems, distribution network power flow scenario simulation aims to integrate multi-source data with physical mechanisms. This enables real-time perception, dynamic assessment, and future trend prediction of distribution network operational states, thereby providing decision support for system operation and dispatch. Addressing the challenges of heightened non-stationarity in distribution network data and insufficient accuracy in power flow scenario simulation caused by large-scale distributed generation integration, this paper proposes an active distribution network power flow scenario simulation method integrating Variational Model Decomposition (VMD) with CNN-BiLSTM. First, VMD decomposes raw PV output sequences into intrinsic Model functions to mitigate non-stationarity. Second, Principal Component Analysis (PCA) is applied to reduce the dimensionality of high-dimensional meteorological features and load data, extracting key principal component factors to eliminate redundant information. Subsequently, a CNN-BiLSTM hybrid model is constructed to achieve high-precision prediction of load characteristics. The prediction results are then fed into the power flow calculation model to complete dynamic power flow simulation for all nodes. Finally, case studies based on measured data from public datasets validate the effectiveness of the proposed method.

title: A Hierarchical Dispatch Model of Vanadium Flow Batteries for Integrated Energy Systems with Demand Response
authors: Haixiao Zhu, Jiahe Xu, Yixun Xue, Xinyue Chang, Xingtao Tian, Xiaotian Wang, Hongbin Sun
publish: 2025 IEEE 9th Conference on Energy Internet and Energy System Integration (EI2)
abstract: This paper presents an optimized scheduling model for Vanadium Redox Flow Batteries (VRFB) in integrated energy systems, addressing the challenges posed by extreme weather and demand response (DR) requirements. The hierarchical model (L0–L2) integrates energy balance, power-flow constraints, pump power consumption, and flow–temperature coupling, while maintaining solvability under Mixed-Integer Linear Programming (MILP). Case studies demonstrate that VRFB outperforms lithium-ion batteries, offering superior thermal stability, longer support duration, and stable operational costs under normal and high-temperature conditions. The model’s flexibility ensures its applicability for large-scale energy systems, making VRFB a promising solution for long-duration, low-carbon energy storage.

title: Principal Component Analysis of Data on Demographic Processes in Russian Regions
authors: Elena Makarova, Vitaly Martynov, Elvira Gabdullina, Nataliya Khasanova, Nikolay Rovneyko, Artur Kartashev
publish: 2025 5th International Siberian Scientific Workshop on Data Analysis Technologies with Applications (SibDATA)
abstract: The article is devoted to solving the problem of data analysis on demographic and migration processes at the regional level. The procedure for data analysis on demographic processes in the regions has been developed. It includes the stages: preliminary principal component analysis and formation of the final sample; construction of principal components for the final sample of data on regions demographic processes; formation the axe name of the new coordinate space; construction of a cluster structure of regions in the field of demography; formation of clustering rules; identification the characteristics of the constructed cluster; determination of critical cluster zones and their analysis. The proposed procedure can be used to analyze data of the other regional social processes, including data on labor aspects. The structure of an intelligent management system of the regional demographic processes has been developed, which includes two levels of management using the principles of feedback management and situational management. There are given the results of the data analysis on demographic processes in the regions of the Russian Federation, taking into account data on migration processes in the regions. The cluster structure was constructed using principal component analysis to characterize the level of intraregional, interregional, and interstate migration. The cluster structure is shown as polarized, critical zones are identified and their characteristics are formulated. The obtained analyses results should be expanded by the analysis results of the labor aspects of regional population life and used to build management decision support systems for social and demographic policy

title: A Physical Model Experimental Platform for Flexible Hydropower Systems: Preliminary Progress
authors: Hongwu Zhang, Weijia Yang, Qingbo Wang, Zhigao Zhao, Zhiyuan Wang, Jiandong Yang, Yongguang Cheng
publish: 2025 IEEE 9th Conference on Energy Internet and Energy System Integration (EI2)
abstract: Physical model experiments of hydropower systems are indispensable core paths for the development of the hydropower field. A physical model experimental platform for flexible hydropower systems is introduced in this work. The experimental platform aims to provide application-oriented basic experimental equipment and a platform for large-scale hydropower stations to improve regulatory flexibility and reduce losses. Firstly, this work systematically introduces the overall architecture and core functions of this dynamic experimental platform. It consists of seven subsystems, namely the waterway system, hydropower unit, central control system, load system, measurement system, circulating water system, and power distribution system. Specifically, the platform's waterway system can simulate different water inertia time constants (Tw) through structural design, and adapt to the experimental needs for the dynamic characteristics of hydropower stations with different installed capacity scales and operating conditions. In order to verify whether the experimental prototype and the model have consistent dynamic characteristics, a hydropower system simulation model is established. Through simulation calculation, this work conducted comparative analysis on the variation laws of key hydraulic parameters for a single unit in the scheme under the typical operating condition. These key hydraulic parameters include spiral case pressure, draft tube pressure and rotational speed. The platform scheme can provide technical reference for the flexibility transformation of existing experimental platforms for the dynamic characteristics of hydropower stations.

title: Photovoltaic Power Forecasting Based on Parameter-Optimized Variational Mode Decomposition and WOA-LSTM
authors: Qian Ma, Teng Xie, Pei Luo, Ting Lei, Zhipeng Ou, Xinyu Zhu
publish: 2025 IEEE 9th Conference on Energy Internet and Energy System Integration (EI2)
abstract: With the support of national policies, solar power generation has become an indispensable component within the core framework of the national energy security strategy. However, photovoltaic (PV) power generation is characterized by instability and intermittency. These issues are primarily caused by factors such as equipment performance and weather conditions, making it challenging to directly use raw data for prediction, which hinders the full utilization of solar energy and leads to unnecessary energy waste. To improve the accuracy of PV power generation forecasting, enhance the efficiency of solar energy utilization, and promote an environmentally friendly green and low-carbon lifestyle, this paper proposes a PV power forecasting method based on the integration of Grey Wolf Optimizer (GWO)-Variational Mode Decomposition (VMD) and Whale Optimization Algorithm (WOA)-Long Short-Term Memory (LSTM) networks. The variational mode decomposition technique is employed to decompose the original PV power curve into multiple Intrinsic Mode Functions (IMFs) for reconstruction, while the GWO algorithm is applied to optimize the reconstructed curve using Envelope Entropy as an indicator. The resulting reconstructed curve is then fed into the Long Short-Term Memory network for prediction. The GWO-optimized VMD uses a mode number k=7 and a penalty factor α=258, while the WOA is utilized to optimize the hyperparameter configuration of the LSTM, ultimately achieving accurate forecasting of PV power data. Case study results demonstrate that, compared to the LSTM prediction model and the GWO-VMD-LSTM prediction model, the GWO-VMDWOA-LSTM model achieves higher prediction accuracy and exhibits superior reliability.

title: Optimal Allocation for Enhancing Transient Voltage Active Support Capability in Wind-Integrated Power Systems
authors: Changjiang Wang, Jialin Tang, Jingbo Liu, Linlin Wu, Yan Li, Peng Wang
publish: 2025 IEEE 9th Conference on Energy Internet and Energy System Integration (EI2)
abstract: Under the "dual carbon" goals, the insufficient voltage support capability in high-penetration wind power integration systems exacerbates the risk of transient voltage instability and cascading failures. Existing configuration methods predominantly rely on single-type devices and lack consideration of transient processes. To enhance the system's transient voltage active support capability, a coordinated optimization configuration method for multiple grid-forming devices is proposed. Firstly, the dynamic characteristic differences between synchronous generators and wind turbines under faults are analyzed, and a mechanistic model for transient voltage issues is established. Secondly, a coordinated optimization configuration model incorporating grid-forming energy storage and grid-forming wind power is constructed, aiming to minimize network losses and maximize reactive power reserve. Finally, a hybrid strategy combining an improved Particle Swarm Optimization algorithm and the interior-point method is employed for solution. Simulations based on the IEEE 39-node system demonstrate that the proposed method reduces network losses by 29.89% and increases reactive power reserve by 34.84%, effectively enhancing the system's transient voltage stability and support capability.

title: A High-Speed, Multimodal Architecture for Real-Time Contextual Voice Assistance
authors: S Pranava Saye, Abishek Poovazhagan, Gangiesh Subburaj, Vinotheni M S
publish: 2026 Sixth International Conference on Advances in Electrical, Computing, Communications and Sustainable Technologies (ICAECT)
abstract: The proliferation of voice assistants has marked a significant shift in human-computer interaction, yet their efficacy remains constrained by their inability to perceive the user's immediate digital and physical context. This paper introduces a novel architecture for a voice assistant that overcomes these limitations by dynamically integrating multimodal inputs from screen captures, webcam feeds, and clipboard content. The system utilizes a modular pipeline featuring an LLM-based intent router, a dedicated vision-language model, and a high-performance core LLM, all optimized for speed via the Groq API. This paper presents not only the system's architecture but also a quantitative analysis of its performance. We report on the system's efficiency through API token consumption tracking and validate its responsiveness with end-to-end latency benchmarks. Our results demonstrate that this architecture achieves response times conducive to natural conversation while providing a framework for managing the computational costs of multimodal interactions, representing a significant step towards the creation of practical, context-aware AI assistants.

title: System Optimization Scheduling of Multi-resource aggregator Based on Hybrid Game Theory
authors: Qin Yabin, Cao Yuanyuan, Zou Peng, Xie Yi, Zhang Chao, Li Yumin
publish: 2025 IEEE 9th Conference on Energy Internet and Energy System Integration (EI2)
abstract: The growing penetration of renewable energy sources into power systems has intensified the randomness and volatility of system operations, particularly under extreme weather conditions, which poses significant challenges to maintaining supply–demand balance. Resource aggregator, serving as an essential tool for integrating distributed resources, can improve both flexibility and economic performance through coordinated optimization. This study focuses on the cooperative operation of multi- resource aggregator systems by developing an optimized scheduling model grounded in hybrid game theory. The model formulates profit and cost functions for both operators and resource aggregators, streamlines their energy exchange procedures, and addresses scheduling issues arising from internal supply–demand mismatches and external disturbances. Simulations conducted across three typical operational scenarios reveal that the introduction of an intermediate operator combined with a Stackelberg game framework leads to a notable reduction in the overall system operating cost. These results confirm the proposed model’s efficacy and progress in enhancing both economic performance and operational efficiency.

title: An Optimized Modeling Method for Small-Signal Stability of Grid-Forming and Grid-following Based Renewable Energy Systems
authors: Yifei Meng, Shanshan Wang, Rui Yin, Jianhua Li, Jianyu Lu, Yuanyuan Sun
publish: 2025 IEEE 9th Conference on Energy Internet and Energy System Integration (EI2)
abstract: The integration of grid-following (GFL) and grid-forming (GFM) controls leverages their strengths to enhance stability in renewable power systems. However, a critical limitation in current small-signal stability analyses is the common oversight of dynamic variations of the PCC voltage phase angle. The distinct responses of GFL and GFM converters during small-signal events are not adequately captured, which can lead to errors in stability assessment. This paper proposes an optimized small-signal model that explicitly includes the dynamic behavior of the PCC voltage phase angle. The accuracy and validity of the proposed model are verified through comparative simulations using a detailed MATLAB/Simulink electromagnetic transient (EMT) model.

title: Coordinated Optimal Operation of PV–Storage Clusters in Weak Grids Considering Static and Transient Voltage Security Constraints
authors: Tianchi Zhuang, Tingxiang Liu, Lijuan Duan, Zhengxi Li, Bin Wang
publish: 2025 IEEE 9th Conference on Energy Internet and Energy System Integration (EI2)
abstract: In weak grids with a high penetration of renewable energy, the voltage stability is significantly influenced by the control characteristics of photovoltaic (PV) and energy storage inverters. To address this issue, this paper proposes an optimal operation model for PV–storage clusters that simultaneously considers static and transient voltage security constraints. The static voltage constraint is formulated based on an improved Multiple Renewable Energy Station Short Circuit Ratio (MRSCR), which quantifies grid strength and ensures steady-state voltage security. The transient voltage constraint is established through a sensitivity-based modeling approach, enabling the optimization of fault ride-through (FRT) control parameters to suppress transient overvoltage and undervoltage phenomena. By jointly optimizing the active power outputs of PV and energy storage units, the proposed model enhances both static and transient voltage stability while maximizing renewable energy utilization. Case studies on a regional weak grid verify that the method effectively improves voltage stability, increases PV accommodation capability, and reduces renewable power curtailment.

title: Landing Operation Evaluation Method Based on Curve Segmentation
authors: Gao Shangfeng, Guo Shiguang, Zhou Xiang, Wang Hanyu
publish: 2025 3rd Asian Aerospace and Astronautics Conference (AAAC)
abstract: In order to objectively measure the level of stability of the aircraft during the pilot's landing operation. This study introduces methods involving curve segmentation and volatility. Firstly, the key flight parameters of the flight landing phase are selected, and the standard curve of key parameters is obtained by screening the flight QAR data. Secondly, for the elevation angle curve, the point with large curvature is selected as the segmentation point, and the similarity distance between the curve of each segment and the standard curve is calculated, and then the total similarity distance is obtained by the cumulative summation; for the roll angle curve, the volatility index is used for description. Similar distance and volatility weights are combined into a consistency metric to assess the level of flight stability during the landing process. The example verification shows that the method has applicability. The smaller the consistency measure in the evaluation results, the better the landing stability of the aircraft. The evaluation method is simple in operation and the result is relatively intuitive and accurate, and can be applied to the prevention of flight operation quality, the prevention of flight landing or shock events, and the performance evaluation of flight operations.

title: A Ganomaly Based Machine Learning Approach for Unmasking Deepfake Voices
authors: MD. Naseera, Parkala Akshara, Mohammad Maahirah, Patlolla Shalini
publish: 2026 Sixth International Conference on Advances in Electrical, Computing, Communications and Sustainable Technologies (ICAECT)
abstract: Deepfake audio has emerged as a significant threat to security, privacy, and trust, enabling malicious activities such as fraud, impersonation, and the spread of disinformation. Conventional supervised detection methods are often ineffective against novel synthesis techniques due to their reliance on labeled fake datasets. To address this limitation, this study proposes an unsupervised anomaly detection framework based on GANbased architectures, specifically f-AnoGAN and GANomaly, trained exclusively on real human speech. Audio inputs are transformed into Mel-Spectrograms and MFCCs to capture essential speech characteristics. This approach offers a flexible, scalable, and generalizable solution for deepfake audio detection and enhances the reliability of voice-based security applications in real-world environments.

title: Research on Source-Load Aggregation and Adaptive Cooperative Regulation in Energy-Intensive Parks Under Carbon Constraints
authors: Wang Yongli, Meng Dexin, Lyu Dongyan, Wang Weixi, Bai Xiaolei, Lu Yingwei, Wang Quan, Mu Chen Yu
publish: 2025 IEEE 9th Conference on Energy Internet and Energy System Integration (EI2)
abstract: This study presents an integrated methodology for optimizing energy system operations in high-consumption industrial parks under carbon emission constraints, focusing on source-load integration and adaptive cooperative control. The approach targets the existing research shortfall concerning inadequate coordination between sustainable transport systems and multi-energy networks. Firstly, an energy topology prior model is established for the park, characterizing the profiles of industrial and transportation loads and quantifying nodal energy interactions. A balance representation mechanism is subsequently formulated using Euclidean distance and adjacency matrices. Secondly, a regional balance probability modeling technique incorporating label-aware Gaussian mixture models with Potts priors is introduced to uncover synergy opportunities between low-carbon energy sources and demand loads. Thirdly, a planning algorithm for energy infrastructure embedding higher-order topological constraints is developed to optimize the allocation of assets including electric vehicle charging stations. Case study analyses demonstrate the proposed method's efficacy in lowering operating expenses, reducing peak-valley load differentials, increasing renewable energy utilization, and cutting carbon emissions. These findings offer a viable strategy for achieving low-carbon transition in energy-intensive industrial parks.

title: Calculation of Investment Capacity for Hybrid Energy Storage in Energy and Reserve Markets: From A Grid Perspective
authors: Mufan Wang, Jiajia Qian, Qian Fang, Ning Chen, Zao Tang, Changrui Tan
publish: 2025 IEEE 9th Conference on Energy Internet and Energy System Integration (EI2)
abstract: Currently, Chinese power grid regulations prohibit grid companies from directly investing in and constructing energy storage facilities. However, the deployment of energy storage by other entities significantly impacts grid operations. Therefore, it is necessary to conduct research on hybrid energy storage system (HESS) capacity investment planning from the grid-side perspective to facilitate effective resource management. To address this need, this paper proposes a bi-level optimization model for hybrid energy storage investment capacity decision-making, designed from a grid-side perspective. The upper-level model aims to minimize the required investment capacity, providing a reference for grid management decisions while incorporating constraints such as upstream transformer capacity limits and mitigating peak-valley inversion phenomena. The lower-level model seeks to maximize the investment return for end-users, simultaneously adhering to the operational constraints of the hybrid energy storage system. The Karush-Kuhn-Tucker (KKT) conditions are then applied to transform the bi-level model into an equivalent single-level problem for solution. Finally, the feasibility of the proposed method is validated using an IEEE 30-bus test system.

title: CRGCN: A Collaborative Graph Recurrent Convolution Network with Evolving Adjacency Matrices for Residential Short-Term Load Forecasting
authors: Dingyan Hua, Luliang Zhang, Tianyao Ji, Tong Qian, Shuai Zhou
publish: 2025 IEEE 9th Conference on Energy Internet and Energy System Integration (EI2)
abstract: Accurate short-term load forecasting (STLF) requires capturing complex spatio-temporal dependencies, a task where standard Graph Neural Networks (GNNs) struggle due to static graph structures. We propose CRGCN, a novel GNN framework that addresses this by introducing evolving adjacency matrices. Our method first initializes an adaptive adjacency matrix via a dual-graph fusion module. Then, a collaborative graph convolution architecture allows this matrix to dynamically evolve across layers, capturing hierarchical spatial features. A cross-layer attention mechanism enhances feature aggregation before a Gated Recurrent Unit (GRU) generates the final forecast. Experiments show CRGCN achieves superior accuracy over state-of-the-art methods and effectively mitigates over-smoothing, enabling deeper graph model deployment.

title: Dynamic Frequency Security Constraint Modeling Method Considering Spatial Correlation of Frequency Response
authors: Lei Yu, Sheng Cai, Yunyun Xie, ChenGuang Qiu, Xiaobo Wang, Shengjun Wu
publish: 2025 IEEE 9th Conference on Energy Internet and Energy System Integration (EI2)
abstract: With the increasing proportion of new energy power generation capacity, synchronous generator sets are gradually replaced, resulting in the decrease of system inertia and significant difference in spatial distribution. The inertia level is related to the dynamic response of frequency, which leads to the obvious spatial variation of system frequency and brings challenges to frequency regulation. In this page, a dynamic frequency security constraint modeling method accounting for the frequency spatial variations is proposed. First, a nodal frequency response model is developed, which captures the impact of both inertia distribution and network topology on node frequency dynamics. Then, a set of linear dynamic frequency constraints is derived for direct integration into the power system scheduling optimization. Case studies on the modified IEEE 9-node system verify the effectiveness of the proposed model.

title: Wind power prediction method based on multi-step decomposition weighted feature construction
authors: Mao Yang, Saiyale Abudulijiang, Yitao Li, Aliya Arkin, Yuxi Jiang
publish: 2025 IEEE 9th Conference on Energy Internet and Energy System Integration (EI2)
abstract: As global fossil energy reserves are exhausted and electricity demand keeps increasing, wind power generation has achieved significant growth. However, wind energy is inherently random, intermittent, and uncertain because it relies on the natural variability of wind conditions, which brings challenges to the safe and stable operation of power systems. As the primary meteorological factor influencing wind power output, wind speed plays a critical role in both power fluctuation analysis and forecasting accuracy. To address these problems, this research proposes a cluster-oriented wind speed reconstruction method that utilizes the correlations between sub-components obtained by decomposing single-site Numerical Weather Prediction (NWP) wind speed data and aggregated cluster power. A weighted feature construction technique incorporating an attention mechanism is employed to correct forecasted wind speeds. Subsequently, scenario-specific forecasting models are developed and applied to a wind farm of China. Experimental findings show that after correction, the RMSE of wind speed forecasts dropped by an average of 4.82%, while the MAE decreased by an average of 1.19%. These improvements contribute significantly to enhancing wind energy utilization and supporting the stable operation of power systems.

title: Multimodal Emotion Recognition in English Conversations Using Fusion of NLP and Computer Vision Techniques
authors: A. Venu Gopal Reddy, Rashmi P B, Dilfuza Gulyamova, G. S. Bansode, Pavan Kumar Nowbattula, Anuradha. S
publish: 2026 Sixth International Conference on Advances in Electrical, Computing, Communications and Sustainable Technologies (ICAECT)
abstract: Efficient emotion reading is vital in suitably human-computer interaction. This study will offer a multimodal solution through the latest NLP and computer vision in order to identify emotions in English conversation. RoBERTa is used to understand linguistic meaning and context whereas any CNN trained on AffectNet data is used to detect facial expressions. Coherent preprocessing makes the data format and conversation frame CRM. Late-fusion strategy, which has weighted streams and meta-classifier, is a highly effective way of enhancing the reliability of prediction. Multimodal MELD system is 98.7 % but is more accurate compared to the text only and vision only models. These findings indicate the great potential of multimodal learning to emotion-sensitive virtual assistants, affective computing, and intelligent interaction systems.

title: Ensemble Machine Learning Approaches for Reliable Palm Oil Adulteration Detection
authors: Himanshu Pant, Garima Joshi, Bhupesh Rawat, Janmejay Pant, Devendra Singh
publish: 2026 Sixth International Conference on Advances in Electrical, Computing, Communications and Sustainable Technologies (ICAECT)
abstract: Palm oil adulteration poses a major challenge to food quality, safety, and public health. Conventional analytical techniques, including chemical and spectroscopic methods, though reliable, are often resource-intensive and time-consuming. This work investigates the application of machine learning algorithms for predicting adulteration in palm oil using its physicochemical characteristics. K-Nearest Neighbors (KNN), Random Forest, Gradient Boosting, AdaBoost, Bagging, XGBoost, Support Vector Machine (SVM), Decision Tree, Naïve Bayes, and Logistic Regression were evaluated for the purpose of detecting palm oil adulteration. Their performances were evaluated using accuracy, precision, recall, and F1-score. Results indicate that ensemble-based methods, particularly Random Forest, Gradient Boosting, and AdaBoost, achieved superior outcomes with a maximum accuracy of 94.6% and consistently strong precision and recall values. These findings demonstrate that machine learning can provide a rapid, reliable, and cost-efficient alternative for detecting adulteration in palm oil.

title: Modeling and Control of Characteristic Harmonics in Medium Voltage Modular Multilevel Converter
authors: Wubin Wang, Zhi Li, Guobing Shi, Tianren Li, Jinshi Wang, Bojin Tang, Peipeng Zhou, Yong Chang
publish: 2025 IEEE 9th Conference on Energy Internet and Energy System Integration (EI2)
abstract: The power semiconductor switching frequency of modular multilevel medium voltage converters is low, which has advantages and application prospects in back-to-back DC transmission and DC collection networks of new energy generation . In scenarios where the number of converter arm modules is limited, passive filters need to be installed on the AC side for harmonic suppression. Based on the equivalence of fundamental power transmission, this article proposes a graphical modeling method to obtain the characteristic harmonics of modular multilevel medium voltage converters. The article provides a calculation of the harmonic suppression effect of an AC passive filter under a certain range of reactive power capacity, considering the characteristics of the inverter harmonics and the requirements of system harmonic control. And after the actual operation of the demonstration project, the AC passive filter can meet the harmonic control requirements of the power grid. The characteristic harmonic modeling method proposed in this article can simplify the parameter design process of AC passive filters and improve the efficiency of system simulation and debugging.

title: Optimization strategy for frequency modulation power of energy storage cluster considering instruction tracking error
authors: Qiang Li, Yue Sun, Fan Li, Jiawang Luo, Xingxu Zhu, Zehui Wang, Jia Sun, Junhui Li
publish: 2025 IEEE 9th Conference on Energy Internet and Energy System Integration (EI2)
abstract: In the process of energy storage clusters participating in automatic generation control (AGC) of power systems, reasonable command power allocation is the key to effective execution of frequency regulation commands. Currently, the traditional AGC command tracking energy storage control strategy is to allocate according to the proportion of energy storage rated power. Due to the difference in power configuration of energy storage capacity in energy storage clusters, this will cause the state of charge (SOC) of small capacity energy storage power stations to reach the boundary first, resulting in limited output and command tracking errors, affecting the frequency regulation effect. This article proposes an energy storage cluster frequency modulation power optimization strategy that takes into account command tracking errors to address the above issues. Firstly, considering the basic process of automatic power generation control, a collaborative control architecture was designed for energy storage clusters to participate in AGC regulation; Then, a multi type energy storage regulation target considering the state of charge of energy storage was constructed. The model construction took into account the constraints of instruction power allocation and energy storage operation. Finally, the Lagrange multiplier method was used to solve the problem and obtain the output allocation of energy storage. The proposed strategy has been verified through simulation analysis to ensure the effective execution of AGC instructions and reduce the instruction tracking error of energy storage clusters participating in AGC regulation.

title: Machine Learning Based Plant Illness Identification System Bolstering Agricultural Productivity Using Texture Analysis
authors: Bipin Bihari Jayasingh, Y. Rama Devi, K. Mary Sudha Rani
publish: 2026 Sixth International Conference on Advances in Electrical, Computing, Communications and Sustainable Technologies (ICAECT)
abstract: Plant diseases pose serious problems for the agriculture industry, resulting in lower crop yields and financial losses. The creation of such an application is extremely important for agriculture since it provides a preemptive response to one of the industry's biggest problems, which is plant diseases. The capacity to quickly and reliably identify diseases can make a big impact in agriculture, where crop health directly affects productivity and economic stability. As a result, we suggest a method that uses an image of a plant to find whether it is well or ill. The goal of the paper is to use leaf image data to categorize and forecast the type of disease. Our proposed solution uses methods like texture analysis, which groups leaf images with a specific disease or those that are healthy by using the Gray Level Co-occurrence Matrix (GLCM). We specifically extract contrast, correlation, homogeneity, and energy while grouping and classifying the user-provided image. Using unsupervised techniques like K-Means Clustering, we were able to classify the leaf images from the data into four groups such as Alternaria alternata, Anthracnose, Bacterial Blight, and Healthy. Finally, we use supervised techniques such as Support Vector Machine (SVM) and K-Nearest Neighbors to classify the new image into these groups. We achieved 90.04% accuracy, that is the result of our implementation.

title: A Review: Mechanisms and Technical Evolution of Shot Peening for Aerospace
authors: Yueming Huo, Zhongpeng Zheng, Min Xu, Yan Wang, Wenhui Li, Xiaoming Yin
publish: 2025 3rd Asian Aerospace and Astronautics Conference (AAAC)
abstract: With rising demands for fatigue, wear, and corrosion resistance in aerospace and automotive industries, shot peening has attracted widespread attention as an efficient cold-working surface modification technique. This paper reviews the fundamental principles of conventional shot peening, the features and applications of advanced methods, and the mechanisms through which peening enhances mechanical properties. Emphasis is placed on the influence of process parameters and the multi-factor regulation of strengthening effects. The application boundaries and optimization strategies across different material systems are also discussed. This work provides theoretical support for advancing shot peening toward precision and intelligent applications in high-end manufacturing.

title: Autopilot Design For Fixed Wing Aircraft Under Colored Noise
authors: Ismet Husrev Akay, Hakan Temeltas
publish: 2025 3rd Asian Aerospace and Astronautics Conference (AAAC)
abstract: This study presents a state estimation filter capable of operating reliably under colored noise conditions, along with an integrated autopilot system for the longitudinal motion of a fixed-wing aircraft. In the proposed approach, noise is modeled with the assumption that it may be not only white but also colored, and the filter design is carried out accordingly by augmenting the state vector to include noise dynamics. The main objective of the study is to address the performance degradation of the Kalman filter under colored noise, despite its effectiveness in white noise conditions, and to demonstrate that the newly designed filter performs accurately even in such challenging environments. To achieve this, classical control methods were applied to the longitudinal dynamics of the aircraft, and both LQR and LQI control strategies were tested. The controllers and reference signals were developed in MATLAB Simulink, and the system was successfully simulated.

title: Multi-UAV roundup algorithm based on hierarchical MASAC with incremental learning
authors: Sun Lei, LI Lingqin, Liu Da
publish: 2025 3rd Asian Aerospace and Astronautics Conference (AAAC)
abstract: For the complex scenario of multi-UAV cooperative pursuit of dynamic targets, this paper proposes a distributed decision-making method based on hierarchical multi-agent reinforcement learning. By establishing a zero-sum game model incorporating velocity, acceleration, collision avoidance, and spatiotemporal constraints for pursuit, the multi-UAV cooperative pursuit problem is transformed into a dynamic optimization problem. A multi-agent maximum entropy policy optimization algorithm (Multi-Agent Soft Actor-Critic, MASAC) based on deep reinforcement learning is designed, combined with a group trajectory prediction network and an incremental learning mechanism, to achieve efficient collaborative decision-making in adversarial environments and provide theoretical support for intelligent swarm behavior in complex adversarial scenarios.

title: Whole Slide Images as Predictive Biomarkers for Drug Response Using Transcriptomically-Transferred Labels
authors: Fatemeh Dashtiahangar, Jiann-Shiun Yuan
publish: IEEE Access
abstract: Predicting tumor-specific drug response is a central challenge in personalized oncology. While genomic biomarkers are effective, they are not universally accessible. Histopathology slides are a ubiquitous, low-cost data source, but they lack associated chemoresponse data. In this study, we bridge this gap with a novel multimodal label transfer framework. First, "pseudo-labels" were created for 1,097 tumors from The Cancer Genome Atlas Breast Invasive Carcinoma (TCGA-BRCA) by rigorously harmonizing their RNA sequencing (RNA-seq) profiles with 457 cancer cell lines from Genomics of Drug Sensitivity in Cancer (GDSC) over a common 8,022-gene space. This alignment, which featured a critical tumor microenvironment (TME) deconvolution step, allowed us to transfer known GDSC drug responses—natural log-transformed half-maximal inhibitory concentration (LN_IC50) values—for 295 therapeutics using a squared-Pearson correlation weighted-average strategy.We then trained a specialized PerDrugEnsemble model to predict these 295 pseudo-drug responses directly from 1024-dimensional Transcriptomics-guided Slide Representation Learning (TANGLE) whole-slide image (WSI) embeddings. The model achieved a mean Spearman correlation of ρ = 0.237 ± 0.017 across 5-fold cross-validation, with 56 drugs (19%) predicted with ρ > 0.30. This work provides a strong proof-of-concept that standard histopathology slides contain a decodable morphological signal for chemoresponse, paving the way for a new class of scalable predictive biomarkers.

title: Hybrid Passive Magnetic–Sliding Bearing with Tunable Axial Equilibrium: Design Guidelines and Experimental Wear Assessment
authors: Naser Tanabi, José Roberto Cardoso, Marcos Sales Guerra Tsuzuki, Newton Maruyama, Renan Augusto Pavani, Oswaldo Horikawa
publish: IEEE Access
abstract: Hybrid bearings that combine passive magnetic suspension with minimal mechanical contact are an attractive alternative to purely mechanical or actively controlled magnetic bearings, especially in compact, low-power applications. This work presents the concept and design methodology of a Magnetic-Passive and Sliding (MPS) bearing, in which two stages of permanent magnets operating in attraction mode provide radial stiffness, while a pivot-type thrust bearing maintains axial positioning. Using axisymmetric finite element analyses, we investigate how magnet material, diameter combination, and height influence radial stiffness, and we identify simple design rules that suppress dead-zone regions and maximize stiffness for a given magnet volume. Complementary tribological considerations are used to compare flat, conical, and spherical pivots, leading to the choice of a spherical tip as a compromise between wear and friction torque. A practical air-gap tuning strategy is then proposed to balance the axial magnetic forces and any static load, keeping the resultant axial force on the pivots below sub-newton levels. The design guidelines are validated on a prototype MPS bearing operating at 9,000 rpm in a deliberately severe accelerated wear test: dry contact between a steel spherical pivot and a PVC antagonist over 120 hours. Despite the unfavorable material pairing, only modest pivot wear and very low friction torque were observed, confirming the feasibility of the MPS concept as a simple, energy-independent bearing solution for applications with dominant radial loads and limited maintenance access.

title: A One-Parameter Statistical Model for Quantum Noise–Induced Perturbations in Amplitude-Encoded Images
authors: Ola Al-Ta’ani, Mohammed M. Gharaibeh
publish: IEEE Access
abstract: This paper presents a statistical framework for characterizing quantum noise effects in amplitude-encoded images through the analysis of noise-induced amplitude perturbations. Classical grayscale images are encoded as normalized quantum amplitude vectors and subjected to stochastic amplitude damping, phase damping, and depolarizing channels. The resulting perturbations are quantified and modeled using a bounded-domain distribution obtained via a nonlinear transformation of the Ola distribution. A single shape parameter is estimated via maximum-likelihood methods, providing a compact descriptor of perturbation concentration across images, noise channels, and noise levels. Experimental results reveal a consistent decreasing relationship between the estimated parameter and noise level, along with clear channel-dependent statistical signatures. Phase damping, despite producing negligible visual changes in reconstructed images, yields measurable perturbations that are accurately captured by the proposed model. Goodness-of-fit analysis based on Kullback–Leibler divergence, mean absolute error, and likelihood-based criteria demonstrates stable model performance across noise regimes, supported by bootstrap uncertainty analysis. The proposed approach provides a unified and statistically robust representation of quantum noise-induced perturbations in amplitude-encoded images, enabling systematic comparison of noise effects beyond visual inspection. This characterization supports noise-aware quantum image processing by offering a compact, channel-dependent statistical description that can inform algorithm design and evaluation under realistic noise conditions.

title: A Cross-Scale Approach for Estimating AGB of Caragana jubata Shrubs in Subalpine Meadows
authors: Huimin Lei, Zhitao Wu, Hanxue Liang, Ziqiang Du, Xinlei He, Tianjie Lei, Bin Sun
publish: IEEE Journal of Selected Topics in Applied Earth Observations and Remote Sensing
abstract: Shrub encroachment significantly affects carbon cycling in grassland ecosystems. In recent years, the expansion of Caragana jubata in subalpine meadows has become a pressing ecological issue. Rapid and accurate estimation of its aboveground biomass (AGB) is crucial for understanding invasion mechanisms and guiding ecological management. However, scale effects and model complexity still constrain precise, rapid estimation of shrub AGB. To address this challenge, the study focused on subalpine meadows and proposed a UAV–satellite integrated cross-scale AGB estimation framework driven by the optimal single indicator. The results showed that: (1) At the UAV scale, two variants of the Vegetation Index-based Weighted Canopy Volume Model (CVMVI)—RGB_CVMVI and MS_CVMVI—outperformed individual spectral or structural metrics, with MS_CVMSAVI performing the best and achieving an accuracy of R² = 0.878 and RMSE = 355.47 g; (2) From the UAV scale to satellite imagery at different spatial resolutions, as the image resolution decreases, the kernel density curves of AGB distribution gradually became smoother and the peak progressively shifted leftward, indicating clear scale effects; (3) At the satellite scale, the unified evaluation based on generalized additive models (GAMs) showed that vegetation indices (VIs) integrating Red-Edge (RE), Near-Infrared (NIR), and Red bands consistently outperformed SAR-derived indices, with the optimal index varying according to the spatial resolution of the imagery. The proposed cross-scale AGB estimation framework enables rapid and accurate estimation from UAV to satellite scales in subalpine meadows and provides a scalable approach for large-scale shrub encroachment monitoring.

title: AI-Powered Communication Over Fiber-Optic Quasi-Distributed Acoustic Sensing Networks
authors: Haoyu Tong, Chun Hong Kang, Islam Ashry, Boon S. Ooi
publish: Journal of Lightwave Technology
abstract: Conventional distributed fiber-optic sensing (DFOS) systems are primarily limited to vibration, temperature, and strain monitoring, leaving many critical parameters reliant on discrete point sensors. In harsh environments, such as downhole, underwater, and underground, these point sensors face significant challenges due to the limited bandwidth, range, and robust ness of traditional communication methods. This work presents an AI-assisted communication framework that employs fiber optic quasi-distributed acoustic sensing interrogation to enable real-time data transmission from spatially distributed sensor nodes. The system employs a compact transmitter design and a direct-detection phase-sensitive optical time domain reflectometer (φ-OTDR) architecture, supporting both tightly coupled (piezoelectric-transducer-wrapped fiber) and spatially separated (speaker-offset) configurations. Two demodulation strategies are developed: a math-based baseline method and a lightweight convolutional neural network (CNN)-based approach. Experimental results demonstrate error-free data transmission at 4 kbps per sensing node using frequency-division multiplexing (FDM), with the ability to integrate multiple nodes simultaneously demodulated along a 1-km fiber. The CNN-based models achieve 100% classification accuracy across all FDM channels and spatial configurations. In a multi-node setup using single-carrier transmission, the CNN framework achieves 100% accuracy for three distributed nodes, outperforming the math-based baseline method, which yielded bit error rates (BERs) of 8.33 × 10<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">−5</sup>, 5.83×10<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">−3</sup>, and 8.33×10<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">−5</sup>. Even under challenging conditions with physical decoupling, the CNN model maintains 99.8% accuracy, reducing the BER from 5.80 × 10<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">−2</sup>. These results highlight the system's robustness, scalability, and suitability for next-generation distributed sensor networks—without requiring complex signal processing or hardware modifications.

title: Enhancing PV Observability in Distribution Network Via Dictionary Learning
authors: Zhao Yang, Yunwei Ryan Li, Yize Chen
publish: IEEE Transactions on Sustainable Energy
abstract: The increasing presence of residential photovoltaic (PV) systems highlights the critical need for utilities to identify their location and configuration, such as capacity, azimuth and tilt. Yet utilities often lack reliable distributed PV information arising from incomplete or inconsistently updated records. To enhance the observability of smart inverter equipped PV systems located behind smart meters for inferring both their capacity and generation characteristics, this paper proposes a generalizable and interpretable framework using limited and distributed monitoring data. Motivated by the underlying shared features of distributed PV outputs, the method jointly captures common generation patterns and infers household-specific installation heterogeneity. We design a dictionary learning-based approach to efficiently derive standard PV profiles. We also propose an impact alignment matrix which integrates meteorological factors particularly global horizontal irradiance (GHI). Proposed learning approach and matrix design greatly enhance both the interpretability and forecasting performance of day-ahead PV generation. A numerically stable orthogonal dictionary structure further enables the disaggregation of community PV capacity by azimuth specific installation styles. Simulation results demonstrate that the proposed framework achieves accurate and adaptive estimation with low computational complexity, which provides practical benefits for PV management.

title: Predictive Maintenance and Reliability in Intelligent 5G Networks based on Graph Neural Networks
authors: Isaac Ampratwum, Mourad Elhadef, Amiya Nayak
publish: IEEE Access
abstract: Weather-driven degradations can cause 5G radio link failures (RLFs), disrupting service and operator QoS objectives. Existing approaches such as threshold alarms, classical supervised models e.g., Logistic Regression (LR) and Support Vector Machines (SVM), and temporal models such as Long Short-Term Memory (LSTM) on Key Performance Indicators (KPIs) histories often struggle under extreme class imbalance and do not explicitly capture spatial/environmental dependencies across neighboring links (shared weather exposure).We propose a Graph Attention Network (GAT)-based predictor that models radio sites as nodes with KPI/site/weather features and connects sites using a weather-footprint graph derived from local weather-station context, enabling neighborhood-aware failure prediction. Using a real operator dataset from Turkcell (Turkey), our model achieves an F1-score of 0.717 and outperforms LR, SVM, and LSTM baselines. We further provide a deployment analysis with carrier-grade Service Level Objectives (sub-5-minute ingest-to-alert and≥99.9% availability) and threshold tuning to control alert budgets. Ablations show that removing class balancing reduces F1-score to 0.21, highlighting the necessity of principled imbalance mitigation for field use.

title: A Multi-Band Spectral Information and Multi-Scale Center-Guided Spatial Information Learning Framework for HSI Few-Shot Classification_supp1-3673195.pdf
authors: Yong Wang
publish: N/A
abstract: Deep learning has been widely applied for hyperspectral image (HSI) few-shot classification tasks. However, 1) existing methods directly process whole spectral bands, ignoring the diversity among bands and thus weakening the spectral representation capability; and 2) they often fail to adequately focus on the central pixel information that represents the target, leading to poor classification performance. To alleviate these two issues, we propose a multi-band spectral information and multi-scale center-guided spatial information learning framework (MBS-MSCS), which includes two parts: multi-band spectral information learning (MBSIL) module and multi-scale center-guided spatial information learning (MSCSIL) module. Specifically, MBSIL employs both intra-band and inter-band attentions to assign adaptive weights to different spectral bands, thereby extracting multi-band spectral features and enhancing the representation ability for different spectral bands. Afterward, MSCSIL makes use of multi-scale cropping and central filling on the multi-band spectral features to fully leverage the central pixel information and output multi-scale center-filling patches. Theses patches are subsequently processed via feature extraction, fusion, and decoding to obtain discriminative spectral-spatial features for classification. Extensive experiments on three datasets demonstrate that MBS-MSCS outperforms state-of-the-art methods.

title: Front Cover
authors: N/A
publish: IEEE BITS the Information Theory Magazine

title: A Novel Visible-Infrared Image Compression Framework for High-value Target Protection_supp1-3673623.pdf
authors: Xin Deng
publish: N/A
abstract: The joint compression of visible-infrared images is crucial for military and surveillance applications. The challenge lies in the protection of high-value targets (HVT) while maintaining high compression efficiency. This paper proposes a novel dual-stream compression framework that effectively addresses this challenge. In our framework, the sensitive HVT infrared signatures are concealed within the visible image stream, while residual infrared image is encoded separately. This dual-stream compression framework introduces three key innovations. 1) HVT protection: The HVT information is physically isolated and hidden within public visible images through a dedicated concealment stream; 2) Key-conditioned reconstruction: A novel decoding mechanism enables active camouflage by replacing HVTs with plausible background content when unauthorized access is detected; 3) Unified optimization: The framework integrates compression efficiency and HVT protection within an endto- end trainable network. Extensive experiments demonstrate that our approach achieves state-of-the-art compression performance while providing superior HVT protection, significantly outperforming traditional encrypt-then-compress methods. The code and weights are open-source at <uri xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">https://github.com/eecoder-dyf/rgbir-compress</uri>.

title: SIC Ordering for Impaired NOMA-ISAC Systems: A Universal Theoretical Framework
authors: Meng Liu, Jie Fang, Yuanwei Liu, Dusit Niyato, Christos Masouros, Fang Wang
publish: IEEE Transactions on Wireless Communications
abstract: As a foundational technology for the sixth generation networks that integrates communication, sensing, computing, and intelligence, integrated sensing and communication (ISAC) surpasses conventional isolated system designs across multiple performance dimensions. Given its superior interference management capability, non-orthogonal multiple access (NOMA) presents significant potential for integration into ISAC systems. To this end, this paper establishes a unified NOMA-ISAC framework that concurrently incorporates residual hardware impairments, channel estimation errors, and imperfect successive interference cancellation (SIC). Within this framework, we propose two distinct SIC designs tailored for different operational priorities: a communication-centric design (CCD) and a sensing-centric design (SCD), thereby introducing SIC ordering as a new dimension for managing the sensing-communication trade-off. For both proposed designs, we derive analytical expressions encompassing exact and asymptotic lower bounds of outage probabilities, ergodic communication rates for users, as well as probability of detection (PoD), probability of false alarm, and sensing sum rate for the base station. These analytical results provide a theoretical foundation for optimizing critical system parameters, such as power allocation and beamforming design. Our analysis reveals the synergistic effect of these impairments, leading to a simultaneous error floor in both communication and sensing performance. Notably, the proposed SIC ordering enables substantial performance gains in the prioritized domain: the CCD scheme improves ECRs by over 40%, whereas the SCD scheme enhances target PoD by more than 25%, under practical impairment conditions.

title: Cellular, Cell-less, and Everything in Between: A Unified Framework for Utility Region Analysis in Wireless Networks
authors: R. L. G. Cavalcante, T. Piotrowski, S. Stanczak
publish: IEEE Transactions on Signal Processing
abstract: We introduce a unified framework for analyzing utility regions of wireless networks, with a focus on signal-to-interference- plus-noise-ratio (SINR) and achievable rate regions. The framework provides valuable insights into interference patterns of modern network architectures, including extremely large MIMO and cell-less networks. A central contribution is a simple characterization of feasible utility regions using the concept of spectral radius of nonlinear mappings. This characterization provides a powerful mathematical tool for wireless system design and analysis. For example, it allows us to generalize existing characterizations of the weak Pareto boundary using compact notation. It also allows us to derive tractable sufficient conditions for the identification of convex utility regions. This property is particularly important because, on the weak Pareto boundary, it guarantees that time sharing (or user grouping) cannot simultaneously improve the utilities of all users. Beyond geometrical insights, these sufficient conditions have two key implications. First, they identify a family of (weighted) sum-rate maximization problems that are inherently convex, thus paving the way for the development of efficient, provably optimal solvers for this family. Second, they provide justification for formulating sum-rate maximization problems directly in terms of achievable rates, rather than SINR levels. Our theoretical insights also motivate an alternative to the concept of favorable propagation in the massive MIMO literature – one that explicitly accounts for self-interference and the beamforming strategy.

title: A Distributed Storage Routing Model and Algorithm for Resource Scarce and Abundant Network Slicing_supp1-3673297.pdf
authors: YUPING WANG
publish: N/A
abstract: In both resource-scarce and resource-abundant scenarios (i.e., in the scenarios where service demands exceed/do not exceed available resources), it is a critical challenge to design efficient task allocation model and algorithm in network slicing in 5G/Beyond 5G (B5G)/6G networks, especially in resource-scarce scenario. This challenge faces the following three difficult tasks: 1) fully utilize network resources, 2) satisfy all user requests, and 3) make the best trade-offs among service latency, service failure rate, and Mobile Network Operator (MNO) cost. To address this challenge, we propose a three objective optimization model, the Versatile Storage Routing (VSR) model. It facilitates collaborative multi-device sharing for a single Virtual Network Function (VNF) and parallel multi-path data transmission, thus enabling full utilization of the resources. Also, it can provide efficient resource allocation schemes in both resource-abundant and resource-scarce scenarios to satisfy all user requests. Furthermore, we design a novel evolutionary algorithm for VSR model called VSR-EA based on Non-dominated Sorting Genetic Algorithm II (NSGA II) framework. It features customized encoding/decoding and evolutionary operators to handle multi-device storage allocation, multi-path routing selection and distributed transmission resource allocation. As a result, the best trade-offs among service latency, service failure rate, and MNO cost can be obtained. Simulation results demonstrate that VSR-EA exhibits broader applicability and yields significantly superior solutions compared to benchmark algorithms.

title: High-Sensitive Vector Curvature Sensor with Tri-Core Fiber-based Hybrid Interferometer
authors: Fei Pan, Jie Cao, Yunhe Zhao, Mengjiao Ding, Ya-nan Zhang, Siyu Chen, Yunqi Liu, Zuyuan He
publish: IEEE Sensors Journal
abstract: This paper proposes and demonstrates a hybrid interferometer based on tri-core fiber (TCF) and capillary fiber (CF) that adopts a CF-TCF-CF (CTC) sandwich structure for simultaneous measurement of curvature magnitude and direction. The fabricated CTC structure with short length of 5.6 mm exhibits two pronounced resonant dips (dip1 and dip2) together with a resonant peak in the transmission spectrum Within the curvature range of 0.098-0.98 m⁻¹, the sensor exhibits significant differences in sensitivity in different directions, with highly linear response characteristics. The maximum curvature sensitivity could be up to –30.84 nm/m⁻¹. Benefiting from the interaction between cladding modes and surrounding medium, the sensor exhibits high sensitivity exceeding 1.5 × 10⁴ nm/RIU in high refractive index range of 1.4526–1.4552 RIU. Within the temperature range of 30-80 °C, the sensor exhibits negligible wavelength shift with a low sensitivity of –0.002 nm/°C. Notably, the curvature sensitivity remains nearly invariant with temperature, resulting in an ultralow temperature cross-sensitivity of ~ 0.0019 m⁻¹/°C. The proposed sensor supports simultaneous measurement of curvature and temperature through dual-parameter demodulation, achieving curvature and temperature resolutions of 9.85 × 10⁻⁴ m⁻¹ and 0.145 °C, respectively, illustrating that it effectively suppresses temperature cross-sensitivity during curvature measurement, guaranteeing high accuracy and long-term reliability for curvature-sensing applications. This high-performance hybrid interferometer, as a versatile, temperature-robust and high-resolution sensing platform, exhibits great application potential in structural health and environmental monitoring.

title: 3D Cal: An Open-Source Software Library for Depth Reconstruction on Vision-Based Tactile Sensors
authors: Rohan Kota, Kaival Shah, J. Edward Colgate, Gregory Reardon
publish: IEEE Robotics and Automation Letters
abstract: Tactile sensing plays a key role in enabling dexterous and reliable robotic manipulation, but realizing this capability requires substantial calibration to convert raw sensor readings into physically meaningful quantities. Despite its near-universal necessity, the calibration process remains ad hoc and labor-intensive. Here, we introduce 3D Cal, an open-source library that transforms a low-cost 3D printer into an automated probing device capable of generating large volumes of labeled training data for calibrating vision-based tactile sensors. 3D Cal also provides an end-to-end, user-friendly pipeline for training custom convolutional networks to produce high-quality depth reconstructions. Using 3D Cal, we systematically explore the relationship between training data volume and spatial reconstruction performance on two commercially available sensors, DIGIT and GelSight Mini, and derive practical, empirically-grounded guidelines for calibrating these sensors. Finally, we demonstrate depth reconstruction performance on the DIGIT and GelSight Mini comparable to state-of-the-art methods, achieving average reconstruction errors of 156 μm and 205 μm on unseen objects, respectively. By automating tactile sensor calibration, 3D Cal can accelerate tactile sensing research, simplify sensor deployment, and facilitate the integration of tactile sensing in robotic platforms.

title: Alleviating Congestion Attacks on Traffic Signal Systems
authors: Tsung-Lin Tsai, Shao-Hua Wang, Chun-Ting Wu, Ting-Yu Lin, Chia-Heng Tu, Meng-Hsun Tsai, Wei-Hsun Lee, Da-Wei Chang
publish: ACM Transactions on Cyber-Physical Systems
abstract: None

title: IIoT Data Sharing: CP-A $^{2}$ BE With Outsourced Decryption and Verifiable Revocation
authors: Wenjie Yang, Zhiyi Chen, Futai Zhang, Shengmin Xu, Zhiquan Liu
publish: IEEE Transactions on Dependable and Secure Computing
abstract: With the rapid development of Industrial Internet of Things (IIoT), data sharing as a cornerstone function of IIoT, has attracted considerable attention. Ciphertext-policy attribute-based encryption is extensively used to ensure confidentiality and fine-grained access control in such scenarios. Nevertheless, most existing schemes face critical challenges, including high decryption overhead, inadequate attribute privacy protection, and the absence of verifiable revocation mechanisms, which significantly impede their applications in industrial manufacturing systems. To address these challenges, this paper introduces a ciphertext-policy anonymous attribute-based encryption (CP-A <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$^{2}$</tex-math></inline-formula> BE) scheme with outsourced decryption and verifiable revocation. It encompasses three key innovations: Firstly, a distributed edge computing architecture is established, leveraging pre-deployed edge nodes within factories to offer outsourced decryption services. Secondly, while safeguarding the privacy of industrial data, the attribute privacy of devices and personnel is also taken into consideration. Thirdly, a verifiable revocation mechanism employing commitment-based techniques is presented to enable real-time access control updates while ensuring the data integrity of revocation operations performed by cloud servers. Experimental evaluation shows that our proposed scheme is practical for data sharing in resource-limited IIoT, especially as the decryption time consistently remains at 26 milliseconds, regardless of the number of attributes involved.

title: Real-time Monocular 2D and 3D Perception of Endoluminal Scenes for Controlling Flexible Robotic Endoscopic Instruments_supp3-3672482.mp4
authors: ruofeng wei
publish: N/A
abstract: Endoluminal surgery offers a minimally invasive option for early-stage gastrointestinal and urinary tract, but is limited by basic surgical tools and a steep learning curve. Robotic systems, particularly continuum robots, provide flexible instruments that enable precise, intuitive tissue resection in confined spaces, potentially improving outcomes. This paper presents an integrated visual perception platform for a continuum robotic system in endoluminal surgery. Our objective is to leverage monocular endoscopic image-based perception algorithms to accurately identify the position and orientation of flexible instruments and measure their distances from surrounding tissues. This thorough understanding of continuum robots and surgical scenes enhances the robustness of robotic procedures. We introduce 2D and 3D learning-based perception algorithms and develop a physically-realistic simulator that models the dynamics of flexible instruments. This simulator features a pipeline for generating realistic endoluminal scenes, enabling control of flexible robots in a realistic environment and substantial data collection. Using a continuum robot prototype, we conducted extensive evaluations, including module assessments and system-level evaluation of the perception platform. Results demonstrate that our perception algorithms significantly improve control of flexible instruments, reducing manipulation time by over 70% for trajectory-following tasks and enhancing the understanding of complex surgical scenarios, leading to robust endoluminal surgeries.

title: JDPNet: A Network Based on Joint Degradation Processing for Underwater Image Enhancement_supp1-3641833.pdf
authors: Tao Ye
publish: N/A
abstract: Given the complexity of underwater environments and the variability of water as a medium, underwater images are inevitably subject to various types of degradation. The degradations present nonlinear coupling rather than simple superposition, which renders the effective processing of such coupled degradations particularly challenging. Most existing methods focus on designing specific branches, modules, or strategies for specific degradations, with little attention paid to the potential information embedded in their coupling. Consequently, they struggle to effectively capture and process the nonlinear interactions of multiple degradations from a bottom-up perspective. To address this issue, we propose JDPNet, a joint degradation processing network, that mines and unifies the potential information inherent in coupled degradations within a unified framework. Specifically, we introduce a joint feature-mining module, along with a probabilistic bootstrap distribution strategy, to facilitate effective mining and unified adjustment of coupled degradation features. Furthermore, to balance color, clarity, and contrast, we design a novel AquaBalanceLoss to guide the network in learning from multiple coupled degradation losses. Experiments on six publicly available underwater datasets, as well as two new datasets constructed in this study, show that JDPNet exhibits state-of-the-art performance while offering a better tradeoff between performance, parameter size, and computational cost.

title: Dynamic Adaptive Correction Strategy for Position Sensorless BLDCM Commutation Error Based on Sign Detection
authors: Hao Jin, Wenyu Shen, Liandong Yu, Jieming Ke, Ying Wang, Shiqiang Zheng, Yanlong Zhao
publish: IEEE Transactions on Industrial Electronics
abstract: Correcting commutation error plays a critical role in enhancing the position sensorless brushless DC motor (BLDCM) efficiency. The existing commutation error sign detection (CESD)-based correction method gets rid of the reliance on analog-to-digital converter, reducing the demand on BLDCM system configuration. However, due to the complex structure and excessive power consumption of the existing CESD circuit, it is not conducive to long-term stable system operation. Besides, conventional algorithm with decreasing iterative increment exhibits poor anti-interference. Therefore, it is necessary to further improve the conventional correction method. First, a simplified CESD circuit is proposed by equivalently replacing components in the conventional design, increasing detection reliability and decreasing power consumption. Moreover, the compensation angle is calculated using a dynamically varying correction algorithm based on the commutation error sign. By dynamically adjusting the gain factor according to the correction condition, the proposed algorithm switches between searching and locating modes to improve adaptability to disturbances. Under this correction strategy, the compensation angle inevitably converges to the required value within a tiny accuracy range, which is theoretically proven as well. Finally, experiments on a magnetically suspended control moment gyro prototype validate the effectiveness of the proposed method.

title: Evaluation of Deep Learning-Based Event Detection for Parameter Estimation During Complex Walking in Parkinson's Disease_supp1-3673610.docx
authors: Brittney Muir
publish: N/A
abstract: Objective: Despite recent advances in wearable technology and its use in quantifying movement, there is still a need for reliable methods of quantifying complex walking tasks beyond steady-state gait (SSG). The purpose of this study is to evaluate an inertial sensor-based processing pipeline that uses a deep learning method for event detection during stride segmentation and established methods for trajectory reconstruction and gait parameter calculation of simple and complex walking tasks. Methods: We propose a method that utilizes a Temporal Convolutional Network (TCN) during stride segmentation and pre-established methods for trajectory reconstruction and parameter extraction to accurately quantify spatiotemporal parameters of steady state gait (SSG), turn, gait initiation (GI), and termination (GT) strides. The results from this pipeline were evaluated against a pressure walkway as the reference system. Results: Overall, our method was able to derive temporal and spatial parameters with small mean errors (≤ 1 ms and ≤ 2.3 cm, respectively) and strong correlation (r ≥ 0.96) with the pressure walkway for SSG strides. Turn, GI, and GT strides temporal and spatial parameters had similar performance (≤ 7 ms and ≤ 2.9 cm, respectively) and strong correlation (r ≥ 0.95) with the walkway. Conclusion: This study demonstrated that IMU derived gait metrics using TCN model event detection for stride segmentation and Gaitmap functions for stride reconstruction and parameter calculation can be used to quantify gait during both simple and complex walking tasks. Significance: The proposed method provides a reliable way to quantify complex walking tasks, allowing for a more complete understanding of mobility in home and community environments.

title: Disentangling Global Orientation with Test-Time Rectification for Unsupervised Non-rigid Point Cloud Correspondence
authors: Jiaming Guo, Haozhe Chen, Zhengbao Wang, Rui Li, Jiaqi Yang
publish: IEEE Transactions on Multimedia
abstract: Unsupervised non-rigid point cloud correspondence aims to identify dense matches between deformable point cloud pairs without ground truth annotations. While existing methods effectively handle shape deformations, they often struggle under complex global orientation changes involving large rotation angles and arbitrary rotation axes. In this paper, we propose a robust test-time point cloud correspondence method that disentangles complex orientation changes from non-rigid shape deformation. We achieve this by modeling global orientation changes as rigid transformations via deformation-invariant priors from pre-trained models to disentangle complex orientation changes while remaining insensitive to shape deformation. Moreover, to resolve symmetry ambiguities induced by complex orientation changes, we augment point clouds with rotational symmetry transformations along learned axes to capture subtle disambiguating cues. To evaluate the impact of arbitrary and complex orientation changes, we construct corrupted versions of existing datasets encompassing varying rotation scales and axes. Extensive experiments reveal the effectiveness of our proposed method.

title: Gait-Parameterized Reinforcement Learning for a Hydraulic Quadruped Robot
authors: Sungho Lee, Jungyeong Kim, Sangshin Park, Jin Tak Kim, Yonghwan Jeong, Hyun Jun Cho, Hyouk Ryeol Choi, Jungsan Cho
publish: IEEE Robotics and Automation Letters
abstract: Designing reward functions for reinforcement learning (RL)-based quadruped locomotion often requires extensive trial-and-error, limiting efficiency and interpretability. Lack of interpretability is particularly critical for large-scale hydraulic quadrupeds, where undetected unstable behaviors during deployment can cause significant mechanical damage. This paper presents a training framework that integrates biologically inspired gait parameters into RL policies, allowing robots to learn locomotion that is both system-aware and human-interpretable. The actor outputs gait parameters—such as gait period, phase offset, stride length, foot clearance, duty factor, and base height—which are coupled with gait-shaping rewards to encourage intent–execution alignment and physically plausible gait patterns. The framework improves training efficiency and provides interpretable signals for monitoring policy behavior. In simulation, we show that locomotion follows the self-generated gait intents as soft constraints, and ablation results demonstrate faster convergence than a pure-RL baseline. We further present reward-term ablation and coefficient sensitivity analyses, indicating that performance is not driven by a single shaping term and is robust to moderate coefficient changes. We validate the approach on BeTheX-Q (over 1.8 m, 350 kg), demonstrating robust real-world locomotion across 2 and 4 km/h walking, stepping-stones, and external disturbances. Finally, gait-parameter analysis reveals interpretable trends that reflect locomotion intent and adaptation across varying conditions.

title: ICCP 2025 Committees
authors: N/A
publish: 2025 IEEE 21st International Conference on Intelligent Computer Communication and Processing (ICCP)

title: A Unified Graph Data Model and Storage Method for Temporal Variations in Power Grids
authors: Zhuang Tang, Siyan Liu, Ji Qiao, Chunhui Ren
publish: 2025 IEEE 9th Conference on Energy Internet and Energy System Integration (EI2)
abstract: With the continuous development of the new power system, the scale of power system topology nodes is constantly expanding, and local state changes are becoming more frequent. Based on relational database and static graph data modeling methods, there are significant efficiency bottlenecks in the fusion expression, integrated storage, and analysis and calculation of grid data across multiple time scales. This paper analyzes the time characteristics of the grid and proposes a method for expressing incremental data of multi-time-scale grid sections based on dynamic graphs. This method constructs a unified graph data model of grid time changes, innovatively adds time attributes to nodes and time series nodes, and realizes the expression and incremental maintenance management of multi-time-scale information such as grid topology structure, life cycle, and operating status. It has been verified that this model has certain advantages in the storage and management of large-scale grid time series topology data and dynamic grid graph calculations such as cross-time period topology analysis, and can provide efficient data support for power system analysis and calculation and business integration.

title: Restoration of Islanded Distribution Networks with Peer to Peer trading mechanism
