authors: Yufeng Zhou, Zhengcheng Dong, Meng Tian, Xinyi Liu, Fangqi Shi, Zihao Zhang
publish: 2025 IEEE 9th Conference on Energy Internet and Energy System Integration (EI2)
abstract: Increasingly frequent extreme weather events pose a severe threat to the security of distribution networks, making the rapid restoration of islanded distribution networks post-disaster a critical issue that urgently needs to be addressed. Existing research often separates technical dispatch from economic incentives, failing to fully leverage the collaborative potential of distributed resources. To address this, this paper constructs a multi-agent collaborative framework involving prosumers and energy storage systems, establishes a peer-to-peer (P2P) trading mechanism, and employs time-of-use pricing incentives to optimize total social welfare. The results demonstrate that, compared to a retail-only market, P2P collaboration maintains the energy storage state of charge at a safe level, thereby avoiding blackout risks and significantly enhancing system resilience. Furthermore, the total system cost is reduced by 15.2%, achieving the dual objectives of resilience enhancement and economic optimization.

title: Fine-Tuning HateBERT for Hateful Speech Classification
authors: Victor-Andrei Căbulea, Marian Flămînzanu-Mateiuc, Ştefan-Daniel Achirei
publish: 2025 IEEE 21st International Conference on Intelligent Computer Communication and Processing (ICCP)
abstract: This paper investigates the benefits of further fine-tuning the HateBERT model for hate speech classification, addressing the challenges posed by class imbalance and linguistic subtleties inherent in hateful content. The fine-tuning process employs a gradual unfreezing schedule combined with a custom Focal Loss function to effectively handle imbalanced datasets and mitigate overfitting. Training is optimized using the AdamW optimizer with linear learning rate scheduling and gradient accumulation to maintain stable convergence. Evaluation on representative hate speech examples shows that the fine-tuned HateBERT achieves higher classification confidence compared to the original pre-trained model, improving the detection of subtle and implicit hateful expressions. Additionally, an LLM (Large Language Model)-based post-evaluation using Mistral-7B provides interpretable justifications and expert-style validation of the model’s predictions, enhancing transparency and trustworthiness. These results underscore the importance of task-specific adaptation and multi-model interpretability approaches to improve performance on complex and nuanced NLP tasks such as hate speech detection.

title: Transferability of software update requirements from the automotive industry to fire alarm systems
authors: Ulrich Rabe, Jörg Wollert
publish: 2025 IEEE 21st International Conference on Intelligent Computer Communication and Processing (ICCP)
abstract: The increasing digitalization and networking of safety-critical infrastructures requires robust software management and secure update mechanisms. This case study investigates the transferability of established software update and cybersecurity standards from the automotive industrynamely ISO 24089, ISO/SAE 21434, and ISO/IEC 27001-to the domain of fire alarm systems. While vehicles and fire alarm systems differ fundamentally in terms of mobility and user interaction, they share comparable risk profiles and regulatory demands, particularly regarding system integrity, lifecycle management, and cybersecurity. The study employs a structured risk analysis across four dimensions-technical, operational, legal, and user-related-to assess the applicability of automotive standards to stationary fire alarm systems. Findings reveal significant overlaps in key risk areas such as failed updates, compatibility issues, cyberattacks, and liability. Consequently, the layered approach of ISO 24089, which distinguishes between organizational and technical update processes, offers a suitable framework for fire alarm technology. Similarly, the lifecycle-oriented risk management approach of ISO/SAE 21434 can be adapted to enhance cybersecurity resilience in building-integrated systems. Despite these promising parallels, domain-specific constraints-such as stricter certification requirements and differing network infrastructures-must be taken into account. The qualitative methodology used provides a valuable conceptual basis for standard transferability but lacks empirical validation. Future research should therefore include practical trials and extend the approach to related domains such as industrial automation (e.g., ISO/IEC 62443). Overall, the study demonstrates that cross-domain knowledge transfer can strengthen the security, maintainability, and regulatory robustness of networked fire alarm systems, thereby contributing to the broader discourse on resilient digital infrastructures.

title: Improving Integrated Circuit Manufacturing Flow Through Machine Learning: A Focus on Pre-Silicon Verification
authors: Alecsandra Rusu, Marius Neag, Marina Topa
publish: 2025 IEEE 21st International Conference on Intelligent Computer Communication and Processing (ICCP)
abstract: Machine learning (ML) has become an increasingly important component in integrated circuit (IC) development, particularly in pre-silicon verification. Traditional methods often face challenges such as slow processing, high resource demands, and difficulties in achieving comprehensive design space coverage. In contrast, adaptive ML techniques concentrate on critical regions of the design space while simultaneously ensuring extensive coverage, enabling faster convergence and lower simulation costs. By employing stopping criteria based on the convergence of a cost function, these methods enhance both accuracy and computational efficiency. As IC complexity continues to rise, the integration of ML-based approaches into the manufacturing flow offers a practical and scalable path toward robust, cost-effective development workflows.

title: Energy Management and Benefit Distribution in Multi-Virtual Power Plants Based on Stackelberg Game with Genetic Optimization Algorithm
authors: Lijuan Li, Yabin Fu, Guoqing Luo, Fengzhao Tian
publish: 2025 IEEE 9th Conference on Energy Internet and Energy System Integration (EI2)
abstract: With the continuous influx of social capital into the electricity market, virtual power plants (VPPs) will increasingly belong to diverse stakeholders, forming a cooperative yet competitive multi-agent decision-making landscape. To achieve benefit coordination between the distribution system operator (DSO) and multiple VPPs, this paper constructs a one-leader-multiple-followers Stackelberg game model, where the DSO acts as the leader and the VPPs serve as followers. The study investigates the DSO’s dynamic pricing strategy and the VPPs’ energy management mechanisms. Furthermore, a genetic algorithm-based method is proposed to solve the equilibrium of this master-slave game. By approximating the internal energy management model of the VPPs, the algorithm efficiently derives optimal electricity prices and power output schedules during the iterative process. For comparative analysis, particle swarm optimization (PSO) is also introduced. Case study results demonstrate that the proposed algorithm outperforms PSO in terms of average profit, operational stability, and Performance in terms of accuracy, offering an efficient solution for multi-agent coordination in electricity markets.

title: Hybrid Expert Model with EEMD and Adaptive TCN-GRU-Attention for Ultra-Short-Term Wind Power Forecasting
authors: Fan Yang, Yongsheng Wang, GuangChen Liu, JiaJing Gao, Xuehui Wang, Delong Zhang
publish: 2025 IEEE 9th Conference on Energy Internet and Energy System Integration (EI2)
abstract: To overcome the limitations of traditional ultrashort-term wind power forecasting methods—such as insufficient extraction of multi-scale features and limited prediction accuracy—this study proposes a hybrid expert model that integrates EEMD with an adaptive TCN-GRU attention mechanism. A sparse-gated hybrid expert mechanism adaptively selects convolutional kernels, thereby enhancing multi-scale feature extraction. The GRU module is then employed for model construction. Using measured data from a wind farm in Inner Mongolia, experiments show that the proposed model outperforms six benchmark models, achieving the best performance across all error metrics.

title: Anti-misoperation Locking Logical Diagnosis Method Based on Deep Learning
authors: Junhui LI, Shuiyao Chen, Hui Zou, Haidong Zhang, Yudong Fang, Haihua Sheng, Xingxu Zhu, Xiaoqing Hou
publish: 2025 IEEE 9th Conference on Energy Internet and Energy System Integration (EI2)
abstract: As the power system develops towards the direction of "large power grid, high voltage, and new energy grid connection", the complexity of the anti-misoperation locking system has increased exponentially. Traditional manual configuration and verification methods suffer from problems such as low efficiency, high susceptibility to omissions, and errors, making it difficult to meet the increasingly complex operation requirements of substations.To address this issue, this paper proposes a deep learning-based logical diagnosis method for substation anti-misoperation locking. Deep learning technology possesses advantages including "automatic feature extraction", "complex nonlinear mapping", and "time-series data modeling", which provides a new approach for the logical diagnosis of anti-misoperation locking.The diagnosis method proposed in this paper can realize real-time detection, accurate classification, and root cause localization of locking logic faults, shortening the fault handling time to the minute level while reducing reliance on expert experience. It holds significant engineering value for improving the safe operation level of the power system.

title: Short-term Wind Power Cluster Prediction Method Based on Multi-Spatial-Scale Features
authors: Mao Yang, Bozhi Dai, Zhiyuan Ma, Yitao Li, Jingsi Chen, Jun Yin
publish: 2025 IEEE 9th Conference on Energy Internet and Energy System Integration (EI2)
abstract: To address the high modeling complexity in wind power cluster prediction caused by wide-area high-dimensional meteorological features, this study builds feature extraction modules at global and wind farm scales. Specifically, it uses Global Block-wise Attention Transformer for global feature processing and Dynamic Hybrid Sparse Graph Attention for wind farm-level feature extraction to reduce model complexity and improve computational efficiency. Applied to a Jilin wind power cluster, results show the method effectively enhances model computational efficiency and wind power output prediction accuracy.

title: Weak Points Identification of the Integrated Electricity-Natural Gas System Based on Static Sensitivity Analysis
authors: Jiaying Mi, Jia Su, Bohan Shi, Juan Xing, Yixun Xue, Hongbin Sun
publish: 2025 IEEE 9th Conference on Energy Internet and Energy System Integration (EI2)
abstract: To accurately identify critical weak links in power– gas coupled systems, this paper proposes a static-sensitivity-based rapid assessment method. The method first constructs a unified flow model describing the interactions between the electricity and natural gas networks; combined with the continuation power flow method, it then defines a sensitivity index centered on the change of system load margin under N−1 faults. By efficiently scanning and ranking the N−1 fault set, the proposed approach can rapidly locate weak links that pose serious threats to system security and stability. Tests on 14-bus electrical network coupled with 7-node natural gas system and 300-bus electrical network coupled with 150-node natural gas system demonstrate that the method efficiently identifies and ranks weak links, and that failures of natural gas system components generally degrade the system stability margin more severely than single power-line faults. This study provides an efficient analytical tool and decision support for vulnerability identification, resilience assessment, and risk prevention and control in integrated energy systems.

title: One-Shot Price Forecasting with Covariate-Guided Experts under Privacy Constraints
authors: Ren He, Yinliang Xu, Jinfeng Wang, Jeremy Watson, Jian Song
publish: 2025 IEEE 9th Conference on Energy Internet and Energy System Integration (EI2)
abstract: Forecasting in power systems often involves multivariate time series with complex dependencies and strict privacy constraints across regions. Traditional forecasting methods require significant expert knowledge and struggle to generalize across diverse deployment scenarios. Recent advancements in pre-trained time series models offer new opportunities, but their zero-shot performance on domain-specific tasks remains limited. To address these challenges, we propose a novel MoE-Encoder module that augments pre-trained forecasting models by injecting a sparse mixture-of-experts layer between tokenization and encoding. This design enables two key capabilities: (1) transforming multivariate forecasting into an expert-guided univariate task, allowing the model to effectively capture inter-variable relations, and (2) supporting localized training and lightweight parameter sharing in federated settings where raw data cannot be exchanged. Extensive experiments on public multivariate datasets demonstrate that MoE-Encoder significantly improves forecasting accuracy compared to strong baselines. We further simulate federated environments and show that transferring only MoE-Encoder parameters allows efficient adaptation to new regions, with minimal performance degradation. Our findings suggest that MoE-Encoder provides a scalable and privacy-aware extension to foundation time series models.

title: Research on The Disaster-Bearing Limitation Quantitative Model of Power Grid Considering Multi-Source Data Fusion
authors: Chen Hao, Zhu Naixuan, Chen Bingqian, Sun Nuoling
publish: 2025 IEEE 9th Conference on Energy Internet and Energy System Integration (EI2)
abstract: As global climate change intensifies, extreme weather disasters are becoming increasingly frequent, posing severe challenges to the ecological environment and unprecedented threats to human infrastructure, particularly power systems. At the same time, with the continuous improvement in economic and social development, the demand for reliable power supply is also rising, making it urgent to strengthen the disaster prevention and mitigation capabilities of power grids. Scientific disaster forecasting and quantifying disaster-bearing limitation can help power grid companies prepare for emergencies by predeploying maintenance teams and repair resources to minimize potential losses. In this context, this paper proposes a disaster-bearing limitation quantitative model of power grid based on the concept of multi-source data fusion, integrating various types of data, including meteorological, geographical, and power grid operating status information. The model employs convolutional neural networks to extract features from these diverse and heterogeneous data sources, analyze their interrelationships, and build a disaster-bearing limitation quantitative model of power grid. Finally, the model is validated using Typhoon Doksuri, which made landfall in Fujian in 2023 and caused damage to the power grid. The results demonstrate that the proposed method and model can accurately assess the disaster-bearing limitation of power grid, thereby enhancing the power grid’s disaster prevention and mitigation capabilities and minimizing potential losses.

title: Automatic Scoliosis Assessment via Cobb Angle Estimation using I-MedSAM-Based Instance Segmentation
authors: Daria-Eugenia Mihăilă, Raluca Didona Brehar
publish: 2025 IEEE 21st International Conference on Intelligent Computer Communication and Processing (ICCP)
abstract: The diagnosis of musculoskeletal disorders such as scoliosis is highly dependent on a correct measurement of the Cobb angle. We present a novel adaptation of the I-MedSAM model for approaching this task, by tailoring it for instance segmentation of vertebrae in spinal X-rays. Our approach introduces a detection head for generating bounding box prompts for I-MedSAM’s mask decoder and modifies the original loss function to better capture small anatomical structures such as vertebrae. On a limited clinically annotated dataset, our method achieved a mean absolute error (MAE) of 3.382, and a symmetric mean absolute percentage error (SMAPE) of 11.863, approaching the precision of manual measurements. These results are attained through training the segmentation model with regard to computational limitations and highlight the potential of our model as a low-cost AI-assisted diagnostic tool in clinical settings. Future work will focus on improving segmentation quality and dataset diversity through pseudo-labeling.

title: Hyperfused Multi-Modal Deep Learning for Automated Acute Lymphoblastic Leukemia Detection and Subtype Classification
authors: Elakkiya A, G. Manimala
publish: 2026 Sixth International Conference on Advances in Electrical, Computing, Communications and Sustainable Technologies (ICAECT)
abstract: Acute Lymphoblastic Leukemia (ALL) is a violent hematological malignancy that involves an uncontrolled multiplication of immature lymphoid cells in the bone marrow and peripheral blood which in most cases results in severe cases of immunodeficiency and organ failure. A novel HyperFused Multi-Mode Deep Learning (HFMDL) framework to be used to diagnose ALL and other subtypes is suggested in order to detect and categorize the disease and its subtypes as quickly and optimally as possible. The diagnostic protocol incorporates three complementary modalities, i.e. microscopic blood smear images, hematological parameters and genomic profile into a single diagnostic pipe. Convolutional Neural Networks (CNNs) learn morphological details of images; a Multi-Layer Perceptron (MLP) learns hematological statistics and a Transformer network learns dependencies between genes and genes in transcriptomic data. They are heterogeneous features that are fused adaptively with a HyperFusion Attention Mechanism (HFAM), an autonomous method that dynamic modality-specific importance weights are assigned, enhancing the interpretation and clinical significance. The tests on ALL-IDB, C-NMC 2019, and TCGA-ALL data sets prove to be more effective and yield 97.8% accuracy, 97.2% precision, 97.5% recall, and an AUC of 0.986. Explainability is also added through Grad-CAM visualizations and attention-weight analysis, which allows the recognition of important biomarkers. HFMDL framework offers a powerful, interpretable and clinically pertinent AI-based decision support system of precision hematopathology.

title: Uncertainty Quantification for the Impact of Clustered EVs on Microgrid Scheduling Using Latin Hypercube Sampling
authors: Gang Wang, Tianyang Su
publish: 2025 IEEE 9th Conference on Energy Internet and Energy System Integration (EI2)
abstract: As highly efficient and clean transportation vehicles, electric vehicles hold great promise for widespread adoption in the future. However, their large-scale integration into distribution grids will pose challenges such as grid overload, voltage sags, and severe load fluctuations, imposing new demands on the safe and stable operation of power systems. Therefore, effectively managing electric vehicle charging loads to mitigate their adverse effects on distribution grids has become a critical research topic. This paper takes microgrids as the fundamental unit and investigates the impact of random charging and discharging behaviors of clustered electric vehicles on microgrid scheduling under the context of clustered EV integration. First, EV charging behaviors are classified and analyzed, and probability density functions for charging durations of different vehicle models are derived. Subsequently, the boundary conditions for grid disconnection and reconnection of clustered EVs are modified and quantified. Finally, Latin hypercube sampling is employed to simulate the random charging behavior of EVs, establishing a random scheduling model for microgrids with clusters of EVs that accounts for uncertainty. The model is simulated using MATLAB, and its validity and accuracy are analyzed and verified.

title: Deep Learning-Based Rapid and Accurate Prediction Method for Internal Ballistics of Solid Rocket Motors
authors: Yixiong Wang, Xinyi Yuan, Weitao Wu, Hongbin Yan
publish: 2025 3rd Asian Aerospace and Astronautics Conference (AAAC)
abstract: Accurate prediction of solid rocket motor (SRM) internal ballistics is of great significance for flight control and safety. In practice, simplified internal ballistics programs are commonly used to predict the internal ballistics performance of SRM, but these methods often suffer from insufficient prediction accuracy. To address this issue, many researchers have proposed using system identification methods to identify engine parameters and improve prediction accuracy. However, system identification methods are computationally complex. To overcome the challenges of low prediction accuracy in SRM internal ballistics and the computational complexity of traditional parameter identification methods, this paper proposes a precise internal ballistics prediction method based on a fully connected neural network (FCNN). This method generates pressure-thrust curves under different combinations of deviation parameters (burn rate coefficient, propellant density and throat erosion rate) using zero-dimensional internal ballistics equations as training data for the FCNN model, followed by model training. Finally, a domestic long-passage solid rocket motor is analyzed as a case study. The results show that this method only requires a short period of combustion chamber pressure data after motor ignition to quickly predict the subsequent thrust curve, with high consistency between the predicted and measured data. This method can provide effective support for SRM design optimization and precise flight trajectory control.

title: Risk-Aware Reconfiguration Strategy for Power Distribution Networks during Typhoon Events
authors: Mingxuan Li, Wei Wei, Yin Xu, Chengeng Zhang, Shanshan Shi, Xinchi Wei
publish: 2025 IEEE 9th Conference on Energy Internet and Energy System Integration (EI2)
abstract: Extreme typhoons can cause line outages that compromise the integrity of power grids, leading to significant load shedding. To mitigate these impacts, network reconfiguration during disasters can be applied to promptly adjust grid topology and the restore supply. Existing studies on reconfiguration primarily focus on minimizing load loss. However, they often overlook the number of switching operations involved, which can increase the risk of operational errors during emergency conditions. To address this gap, this paper proposes a rollout algorithm for reconfiguration during disasters that jointly considers load loss and switching actions. The method evaluates the full time-horizon impact of candidate actions and selects the decision that achieves the best cumulative performance, aiming to minimize load loss with as few operations as possible. Case studies demonstrate that, compared with the rule-based strategy, the proposed approach reduces the number of switching actions and the associated risk of operational errors, while maintaining minimal load loss during extreme weather events.

title: Online Monitoring of Insulation Conditions for High-Voltage DC Cables Based on MMC Low-Frequency Injection
authors: Zhoufei Yao, Xing Lei, Xizhou Du, Ke Sun
publish: 2025 IEEE 9th Conference on Energy Internet and Energy System Integration (EI2)
abstract: Online monitoring of the insulation conditions of HVDC cables is crucial for the safe and reliable operation of HVDC transmission systems. However, current online monitoring methods suffer from insufficient insulation assessment accuracy and the need for additional hardware. To address these issues, this paper proposes an HVDC cable insulation monitoring method based on modular multilevel converter (MMC) low - frequency injection. By injecting AC signals with specific amplitude and frequency through MMC, this method eliminates the need for extra hardware. Additionally, it leverages the high ground capacitance impedance of cables at low frequencies to enhance monitoring accuracy.

title: Nonlinear Passive Control of Modular Multilevel Matrix Converter for Flexible Low-Frequency AC Transmission System
authors: Yumeng Jiang, Siyuan Wu, Ruanming Huang, Wei Xie, Chen Qian, Yongqing Meng
publish: 2025 IEEE 9th Conference on Energy Internet and Energy System Integration (EI2)
abstract: Offshore wind power is developing towards largescale and far-reaching sea areas, and efficient and reliable grid-connection technology has become a crucial issue that needs to be addressed urgently. Compared with traditional transmission methods, fractional frequency transmission systems have increasingly evident technical and economic advantages in long-distance and large-capacity power transmission, attracting widespread attention. The modular multilevel matrix converter is the core converter equipment of fractional frequency transmission systems. However, due to the complex operating characteristics of modular multilevel matrix converter, issues such as its circulating current, power transmission, and frequency leakage affect system stability and power quality. This paper designs a passivity-based control strategy that comprehensively considers the operating characteristics of modular multilevel matrix converter. Through MATLAB/Simulink simulations, the results show that the system under the proposed passivity-based control strategy has excellent steady-state performance, dynamic performance, and strong robustness.

title: Operational Strategy Optimization for a Standalone Energy Storage Station Considering Multi-Market Coordination
authors: Jia Tang, Meng Niu, Jing Yu, Mingyang Liu, Xin Wen, Jing Bian
publish: 2025 IEEE 9th Conference on Energy Internet and Energy System Integration (EI2)
abstract: This paper proposes a two-stage stochastic optimization model to enable standalone energy storage stations to participate in multi-service markets while considering their capacity value. The work: (1) develops a scenario generation model integrating energy, frequency regulation, and capacity leasing markets via K-means clustering and equivalent regulation signals; (2) formulates a two-stage stochastic program that maximizes lifecycle revenue with a capacity leasing cost allocation mechanism; and (3) introduces a hybrid solution strategy combining genetic algorithm and mixed-integer linear programming to optimize power-capacity configuration and multi-time-scale operation. The approach improves economic viability and market participation under high investment costs and long payback periods, offering decision support for integrating storage into multi-level electricity markets.

title: Decentralized Control of Grid-Forming Energy Storage Converters with Adaptive Virtual Impedance and Voltage Support
authors: Fengshun Jiao, Guoxing Wu, Jie Zhang, Yunyan Yang, Qinkai Zhu, Yujie Shi
publish: 2025 IEEE 9th Conference on Energy Internet and Energy System Integration (EI2)
abstract: Due to the differences in the AC output voltages of grid-forming power storage converters and the variations in line impedances, circulating currents are likely to occur among multiple parallel converters. The presence of such currents negatively impacts converter efficiency and grid stability. In this paper, an adaptive virtual impedance and voltage compensation algorithm is proposed based on the conventional droop control of grid-forming power storage converters. The proposed control strategy compensates for the difference in line impedances of each converter, thereby achieving output power decoupling and circulating current suppression. Moreover, it can compensate the voltage drop caused by the virtual impedance, ensuring voltage stability of the AC common bus. A small-signal model of the grid-forming power storage converter is then established. The stability of the proposed control algorithm is analyzed and validated in a simulation model. Simulation results demonstrate that the proposed control strategy effectively suppresses circulating currents and improves power sharing accuracy under decentralized control with low-bandwidth communication.

title: Identification of Vulnerable Transmission Lines in Urban Power Grids Considering Nonlinear Effects of Typhoon Disasters
authors: Aiqiang Pan, Yufan Zhang, Yiran Chen, Chengeng Zhang, Yin Xu
publish: 2025 IEEE 9th Conference on Energy Internet and Energy System Integration (EI2)
abstract: Accurate identification of critical vulnerable components is a prerequisite for enhancing the resilience of power grids. However, the N-m random failures triggered by extreme typhoons exhibit pronounced nonlinearity, which limits the effectiveness of conventional identification approaches. To address this gap, this paper proposes an identification method for vulnerable transmission lines in urban power grids that accounts for the nonlinear effects of typhoon disasters. First, we develop an identification framework that is grounded in two dimensions, failure probability and consequence, to characterize component-level vulnerability. Second, we construct a failure scenario modeling approach that incorporates the spatiotemporal characteristics of typhoon disasters. Third, we formulate a vulnerable component identification scheme that captures the nonlinear compounding of multi-factor impacts. Finally, case studies are conducted to validate the effectiveness of the proposed method.

title: Transient Overvoltage at the Sending-End Busbar During Commutation Failure: Analysis and Mitigation Strategy
authors: He Wang, Han Zhang
publish: 2025 IEEE 9th Conference on Energy Internet and Energy System Integration (EI2)
abstract: A quantitative analysis and mitigation strategy for transient voltage at the sending-end busbar, triggered by commutation failure in an UHVDC transmission system, is presented in this paper. First, by analyzing the reactive power balance during commutation failure recovery, a quantitative expression relating the transient voltage at the sending-end AC busbar to the DC current is derived. Based on this relationship, and with the constraint of preventing high-voltage tripping of renewable energy units, the critical DC current command value to avoid overvoltage is calculated. Subsequently, an improved rectifier-side constant current control strategy is proposed. This strategy incorporates a voltage criterion and a critical current calculation module. When an overvoltage risk is detected, it dynamically adjusts the DC current command to increase the reactive power consumed by the rectifier, thereby suppressing overvoltage at the sending-end busbar. Finally, simulations based on the CIGRE standard test model on the PSCAD platform verify that the proposed strategy effectively mitigates transient overvoltage and enhances the transient voltage stability of the UHVDC transmission system.

title: An AR-Integrated Learning Management Platform for Immersive Medical Training Using Multimodal Learning Techniques
authors: Reshma R, Saravana Sairam C, Kishan GS, Monish M
publish: 2026 Sixth International Conference on Advances in Electrical, Computing, Communications and Sustainable Technologies (ICAECT)
abstract: We introduce a new medical education system that combines Augmented Reality (AR) and Machine Learning (ML) to provide a fully immersive, interactive learning platform for medical students. The platform combines web-based AR for live 3D visualization of the anatomy with an Artificial Intelligence (AI)-based medical image classification system that gives instant, explainable feedback on diagnostic practice exercises. The frontend of the platform employs React, WebXR, and Three.js, while Python and FastAPI are employed at the backend to host deep learning models for medical image analysis. Explainable AI methods provide transparent model outputs and improve learning outcomes. Results of evaluation indicate significant improvements in student engagement, spatial understanding, and diagnostic accuracy in comparison to conventional methods achieving 95.99% accuracy with 95.98% F1-score. This study highlights the ability to combine AR and ML systems to respond to critical issues in medical education, which enables students to acquire necessary skills and field experience for clinical achievement.

title: Enhancing Energy Efficiency of Supplemental Lighting Through Spatial Redistribution in Dragon Fruit Cultivation
authors: Qiannan Jiang, Haiyun Chen, Qiaoyang Zhang, Hua Xiao
publish: 2025 International Conference on Energy, Materials, and Photonics (EMP)
abstract: To address the issues of low light energy utilization efficiency (UE) and nonuniform distribution in supplementary lighting systems for dragon fruit cultivation, this study proposes a light distribution optimization algorithm to enhance UE. The Monte Carlo ray tracing and simulated annealing algorithms are employed to investigate the effects of lighting position, angle, and height on light energy receiving efficiency (RE) and UE. UE of 1.19 % is achieved at the height of 1.6 m with a 45° lighting angle, which is 5.7 times higher than the conventional method of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\text{0. 2 1 \%}$</tex>. This approach significantly enhances energy efficiency and enables intelligent agricultural practices by ensuring optimal photosynthetic activity for dragon fruit plants while substantially reducing energy consumption.

title: A Pso Tuned Pid Vector Control Technique for the Improvement in Speed of Induction Motor
authors: Arpit Yadav, Ranjay Das, Ganesh Roy
publish: 2026 Sixth International Conference on Advances in Electrical, Computing, Communications and Sustainable Technologies (ICAECT)
abstract: In this paper, an optimized proportional integral derivative (PID) based vector control technique for high-performance speed control of induction motors (IMs) using particle swarm optimization (PSO) is proposed. Induction motors have observed an immense increase in usage in various domestic, industrial, and automation applications owing to their ruggedness, economical nature, and low maintenance requirements. Yet, accurate speed control during dynamic loading situations remains a serious challenge. Conventional speed control using PID controllers suffers from a constant error during loading situations. To overcome such drawbacks in a standard PID control strategy, an optimized PID control strategy based on PSO for gain parameter optimization is established. The control algorithm utilizes a rotator flux-oriented model to control both flux and torque independently. Hence, better dynamic performance, stability, and reduced harmonic content can be achieved. The optimized system enables unity power factor control with increased efficiency without increasing the complexity level of the motor drive system. Simulation studies prove that the optimized vector control technique outperformed conventional control systems in terms of fast torque response, better load handling capability, and lower losses.

title: Machine Learning Based Pigment Epithelial Detachment Detection in OCT Images
authors: M. Anand, T. M. Sheeba, S.Albert Antony Raj, J. Anita Smiles, A. S. Anshy Princella
publish: 2026 Sixth International Conference on Advances in Electrical, Computing, Communications and Sustainable Technologies (ICAECT)
abstract: An automated technique to segment Retinal Pigment Epithelium Layer (RPEL), detect Pigment Epithelial Detachment (PED) in Optical Coherence Tomography (OCT) images. This model utilizes a five-stage algorithm such as preprocessing, RPEL segmentation, feature extraction, model development and PED detection. Initially, color OCT images are converted to grayscale, and a Wiener filter is applied to reduce speckle noise. The RPEL is segmented using a thresholding approach, and 4 key features are extracted. Machine learning models, including Random Forest (RF), K-Nearest Neighbor (KNN), and Support Vector Machine (SVM), are trained using the extracted unique-features to detect PED. The models are evaluated based on accuracy, sensitivity, specificity, precision, and F1 score. Experimental results show that the Random Forest model outperforms others with an accuracy of 98 %, sensitivity of 100 %, specificity of 96 %, and an F1 score of 98 %, making it the most effective method for PED detection in OCT images.

title: Advanced Fuzzy Neural Network Modeling of Ni(II) Ion Adsorption on Modified Biochar: Synergizing Experimental and Computational Analysis
authors: Anbalagan S
publish: 2026 Sixth International Conference on Advances in Electrical, Computing, Communications and Sustainable Technologies (ICAECT)
abstract: Due to the necessary functioning of society, the contamination of water bodies by heavy metals, in particular, by nickel (Ni(II)), is a serious threat to the environmental and human health. In this work, we explore the capability of adsorbing Ni(II) ion by surface modified biochar through an extensive experimental and computational approach. Agro Waste was processed to prepare biochar and chemically modified for increase in surface functionality and adsorption performance. Key parameters on batch adsorption experiment, including pH, time, ion concentration and biochar dosage were evaluated. A deep fuzzy neural network (DFNN) model was developed and validated using experimental data to predict Ni(II) adsorption behavior under diverse conditions, demonstrating excellent predictive accuracy. The conventional modeling techniques could not capture non-linear interactions of adsorption variables, whereas DFNN model was successful to determine non-linear interactions among variables of adsorption including temperature, pressure and water concentration. Comparative analysis confirmed the model's validity, showing a close correlation between experimental outcomes and predicted values. The result of this integrated framework shows that the surface engineered biochar can be integrated with the advanced artificial intelligence tools to optimize heavy metal remediation processes. Combining experimental and computational insight fosters understanding about metal adsorption mechanism as well as implementation of scalable and sustainable water treatment strategies for Ni(II) as well as other toxic metal contaminants.

title: Hyper-Parameter Sensitivity Analysis for Deep Learning-Based Multi-Hop Channel Estimation in Vehicular Networks
authors: M.Sushanth Babu, G.A.E.Satish Kumar, D. Nagadevi, K. Vasanth, S.Siva Priyanka
publish: 2026 Sixth International Conference on Advances in Electrical, Computing, Communications and Sustainable Technologies (ICAECT)
abstract: Deep learning (DL)-based channel estimation has emerged as a key enabler for reliable multi-hop vehicular-to-everything (V2X) communications under high-mobility conditions. However, the performance of such models is highly sensitive to hyper-parameter configurations, which directly influence convergence stability, estimation accuracy, and robustness to error propagation. This paper presents a comprehensive sensitivity analysis of critical hyper-parameters, including learning rate, sequence length, pilot density, hop depth, model capacity, and batch size, within a DL-assisted multi-hop channel estimation framework. A system model is developed to capture the dynamics of vehicular multi-hop fading channels, and the Normalized Mean Square Error (NMSE) is used as the primary evaluation metric. Experimental results reveal that pilot density and sequence length exert the strongest influence on estimation accuracy, while hop depth primarily governs error propagation. Learning rate, model capacity, and batch size are shown to regulate convergence behavior and computational feasibility. Practical tuning guidelines are provided, highlighting optimal ranges for each parameter to achieve a trade-off between accuracy, overhead, and complexity. The findings establish a reproducible design space for deploying DL-based multi-hop channel estimation in next-generation vehicular networks.

title: Distributed Resource Aggregation and Cloud-Edge Collaborative Operation Optimization Method
authors: Hai He, Qian Wang, Bin Li, Jipeng Li
publish: 2025 IEEE 9th Conference on Energy Internet and Energy System Integration (EI2)
abstract: Aggregates of distributed resources form large-scale market entities to interact with the power grid by integrating massive distributed resources. However, the energy management of large-scale distributed resources is confronted with a conflict between global optimization and local demands. Centralized optimization and regulation struggle to balance users’ electricity costs and comfort, while local user energy management lacks overall coordination with the operational status of the aggregates of distributed resources. Therefore, a lightweight user energy management strategy for aggregates of distributed resources based on cloud-edge collaboration is proposed. First, a hierarchical decision-making framework for the energy management of aggregates of distributed resources is constructed, enabling the transformation of the energy management of aggregates of distributed resources from one-way control to two-way interaction. Second, considering the limited computing resources of low-cost edge devices, a local decision-making algorithm based on sparse reinforcement learning is proposed, which utilizes adaptive topology evolution to reduce the number of trainable parameters in neural networks. Finally, case studies conducted on real-world datasets verify the effectiveness and efficiency of the proposed lightweight strategy.

title: Short-Circuit Current Calculation Method for Grid-Forming Converters with Virtual Synchronous Control
authors: Wenjia Xu, Shengyi Zhu, Jiawei Zhu, Yanan Yu, Changqing Liu, Lin Zhang, Tao Liu
publish: 2025 IEEE 9th Conference on Energy Internet and Energy System Integration (EI2)
abstract: With the increasing penetration of renewable energy in the power system, its operational characteristics are undergoing profound changes. Currently, the mainstream grid-following converters rely on grid voltage synchronization and have weak support capabilities. In contrast, grid-forming control technology, which emulates the characteristics of synchronous generators, can actively support grid voltage and frequency, making it a key technology for building new power systems. However, the short-circuit current characteristics of grid-forming converters differ significantly from traditional grid-following devices. Existing international and national standards have not yet covered their calculation methods, posing challenges for power system security analysis and protection setting. This paper establishes a short-circuit current calculation model for grid-forming converters employing Virtual Synchronous Control (VSG). By analyzing the dynamic response of the active-frequency and reactive-voltage control loops during faults, a full time-domain analytical expression for the short-circuit current in the dq coordinate frame is derived. A simplified steady-state model that equivalates the grid-forming converter to a controlled current source is proposed. Building upon this, an iterative short-circuit calculation process suitable for power systems containing multiple grid-forming converters is proposed. Simulation results verify the correctness and effectiveness of the proposed calculation method, providing a theoretical basis and a practical method for solving the system short-circuit current calculation problem after the integration of grid-forming devices.

title: Supervised Model for Brain Stroke Using Feature Selection Approaches
authors: Chithirala Bala Subramanyam, Hemanta Kumar Bhuyan
publish: 2026 Sixth International Conference on Advances in Electrical, Computing, Communications and Sustainable Technologies (ICAECT)
abstract: Feature selection has been considered as a vital role in machine learning (ML) for various data analysis on classification or the importance of features for classification. Generally, ML employs multiple diverse approaches to solve different problems as needed by the experimental demand. Since feature selection is part of ML, many complex problems can be solved using feature selection techniques. Feature selection can solve the problem by selecting features from the dataset's own pool. The main aim of this feature selection approach is to select the practical features from the dataset to reduce dimensionality with effective accuracy performance. Thus, supervised model-based feature selection approaches are considered for analyzing healthcare datasets to predict diseases. Several methods for supervised models are considered to select features from the brain stroke dataset for predicting disease, based on various experiments. The proposed strategies are demonstrated using machine learning methods for various experimental performances.

title: Virtual Square Wave Signal Injection-Based Loss Minimization Control for Dual Three-Phase Permanent Magnet Synchronous Generator
authors: Ze Li, Wencong Huo, Jinhui Xia, Xiaonan Gao, Chunhai Li, Penghui Sun, Weiliang Liu, Jing Hu, Zhaoyan Zhang
publish: 2025 IEEE 9th Conference on Energy Internet and Energy System Integration (EI2)
abstract: Dual three-phase permanent magnet synchronous generators (DTP-PMSGs) are favored in wind power generation systems due to their higher power density and higher reliability. However, the conventional id=0 control scheme (equivalent to maximum torque per ampere (MTPA) control for surface DTP-PMSG) only minimizes the copper loss (CS), resulting in suboptimal efficiency. To enhance the efficiency of the DTP-PMSG, this paper proposes a virtual square wave signal injection-based loss minimum control (VSWSI-LMC) strategy for DTP-PMSG. Therein, a loss model for the DTP-PMSG incorporating both CS and iron loss (IS) is established. A VSWS is injected into the d-axis current to obtain the derivative of the total loss with respect to the d-axis current. This enables the decoupling of the iron-loss current component from the stator current, thereby minimizing the total loss while maintaining dynamic performance. Extensive comparative results verify the capability of loss reduction for the proposed method.

title: A Mixed DRL Framework for Computing Offloading and Resource Allocation in Digital Twin Assisted Wireless Powered MEC Network
authors: Shubin Zhang, Senlei Bao, Kaikai Chi, Zhiguo Shi
publish: IEEE Transactions on Mobile Computing
abstract: The digital twin (DT) assisted mobile edge computing (MEC), which adopts DT to bridge cyber and physical systems by generating digital replicas of real entities, is proposed as an effective solution to manage the proliferating Internet of Things (IoT) networks. However, it is challenging to continuously maintain the digital representation of physical system because of the limited battery and computation capabilities of IoT devices. In this paper, we present a new paradigm of DT assisted wireless powered MEC (WP-MEC) network, where IoT devices can harvest energy from hybrid access points (HAPs) and upload sensing data to maintain DT. The DT, in turn, is responsible for efficiently making offloading decisions and resource allocations to maximize the computing data volume of the WP-MEC. To maximize the computation rate and maintain the DT assisted WP-MEC network, we formulate a mixed-integer nonlinear programming (MINLP) problem that addresses task offloading, wireless power transfer (WPT) duration, energy allocation, DT uploading duration, and data processing duration. Initially, we simplify the original problem through mathematical derivation. Subsequently, we design a mixed deep reinforcement learning (mixed-DRL) framework comprising two deep neural networks (DNNs) that output discrete offloading decisions and continuous resource allocations to achieve a higher volume of processed task data. Extensive experiments demonstrate that the proposed mixed-DRL framework with the self-designed DRL algorithms can improve the processed data volume by 30% compared to the advanced algorithm in the literature.

title: A Domain Generalization Network with Inverse Gaussian Weighted Contrastive Learning for Gas Identification under Sensor Drift
authors: Yang Liu, Qiaohua Feng, Yunbo Shi
publish: IEEE Sensors Journal
abstract: With the increasing demand for industrial gas detection, methods based on deep learning are becoming increasingly important for gas recognition. However, sensor drift is inevitable, making recognition models trained on the source domain difficult to adapt to the target domain, thereby reducing gas recognition performance. Therefore, this paper proposes a domain generalization network with inverse Gaussian weighted contrastive learning (WCDG) to achieve gas recognition under sensor drift. The network decouples multi-domain features to separate recognition-related and domain-specific features, employs saliency-guided feature fusion to extract domain-invariant features, and applies weighted contrastive constraint to reduce intra-class divergence and enlarge inter-class margins, thereby enabling effective transfer of source domain knowledge derived solely from multiple batches of early stage data to a target domain that contains mid stage and late stage data. Specifically, a multi-scale feature extraction module (MSFE) is first proposed to capture the latent discriminative features of the input samples. Second, adversarial training is incorporated to achieve decoupling between recognition-related and domain-specific features. Subsequently, a saliency-guided feature fusion module (SGFF) is proposed to further extract domain-invariant features without drift. Finally, an inverse Gaussian weighted contrastive learning strategy (IGWC) is proposed to optimize the feature space and enhance discriminability. Experimental results demonstrate that the proposed method achieves average recognition accuracies of 91.80% and 94.00% on a public dataset and a laboratory-collected dataset, respectively, significantly outperforming the compared methods, thereby confirming its superiority under sensor drift and further indicating strong potential for practical applications.

title: EPRU: Efficient and Privacy-aware Reputation Update Scheme with a Dual-threshold Mechanism for Vehicular Platoons
authors: Hongyuan Cheng, Xiaosong Guan, Yining Liu, Jiuru Wang, Zhiquan Liu
publish: IEEE Transactions on Dependable and Secure Computing
abstract: Vehicular platooning demands secure and trustworthy inter-vehicle communications to maintain platoon stability, traffic efficiency, and driving safety. Existing privacy-preserving authentication schemes protect message integrity over open channels but often overlook the credibility of message content, allowing authenticated yet misleading data. Reputation management frameworks address this by evaluating vehicle behavior, yet most employ periodic updates, delaying detection of sudden misbehavior. This paper proposes a reputation-based authentication mechanism (EPRU) that establishes trust between vehicles by evaluating vehicle behavior and historical records, thereby ensuring the reliability of message transmission between vehicles. Our EPRU incorporates a dynamic aggregation trigger mechanism that updates vehicle credit scores based on verified feedback collected in real time, thereby accurately reflecting behavioral changes. Furthermore, a combination of ElGamal encryption and homomorphic encryption is employed to safeguard vehicle identities and feedback data during transmission and processing. Formal security proofs and extensive analysis demonstrate resistance to forgery, replay, and modification attacks, ensuring the confidentiality, integrity, and authenticity of both messages and feedback data. Experimental results demonstrate that our EPRU reduces computation overhead by at least 3.05% and communicational cost by 37.5% compared with recent state-of-the-art schemes, highlighting its practicality and efficiency for real-time, privacy-aware vehicular platoon systems.

title: SonoLF-ASPPNet: A Low-Frequency Focused Multi-Scale Network for Speckle Denoising in Forward-Looking Sonar Images
authors: Wenkai Wang, Shuaizheng Yan, Zhaoju Zhu, Bingwei He
publish: IEEE Geoscience and Remote Sensing Letters
abstract: Two-dimensional forward-looking sonar images are often severely degraded by speckle noise, impairing human interpretation and computer vision tasks. Existing denoising methods, including traditional multiplicative noise models and deep learning approaches, suffer from over-suppression of high-frequency information, leading to residual artifacts, incomplete noise removal, and blurred edges. To address these challenges, this letter proposes SonoLF-ASPPNet, a novel sonar image denoising network, along with a new simulated dataset construction method. The network incorporates three key contributions: the low-frequency structure-edge focusing module combines the traditional SRAD with a Laplacian kernel to enable simultaneous low-frequency denoising and high-frequency edge preservation; the low-frequency attention module incorporates an atrous spatial pyramid pooling module to enhance contextual awareness and suppress high-frequency interference; The training utilizes a composite loss function to enhance denoising in smooth areas, suppress color spots, and homogenize backgrounds. The experimental results indicate that on the simulated dataset, SonoLF-ASPPNet achieved PSNR and SSIM values of 27.76dB and 0.817, respectively, along with excellent visual performance. On the real forward-looking sonar dataset, the model outperformed comparative algorithms in terms of visual outcomes, demonstrating superior noise suppression and texture preservation. Moreover, it attained competitive quantitative scores in ENL, EPI, and M-score.

title: Dual-Component Sparse Representation Framework for Enhanced Micro- and Macro-Damage Imaging With Ultrasonic Lamb Waves
authors: Haiming Xu, Lishuai Liu, Hetang Wang, Yibang Zhou, Yanxun Xiang
publish: IEEE Transactions on Industrial Informatics
abstract: Imaging microdamage, represented by the fatigue cracks, presents a significant challenge, yet is crucial for ensuring structural integrity in industrial applications. The development of nonlinear ultrasonic Lamb wave techniques provides a practical avenue for fatigue crack imaging, but the inherently weak nonlinear features hinder the practical application. Sparse representation method paves a promising way for reconstructing the weak second harmonic component, enabling the fatigue crack imaging. However, existing sparse representation method used in the nonlinear ultrasonic research only focuses on the second harmonic component reconstruction, neglecting the fundamental component, which accounts for most of the wave energy. This oversight leads to an underestimation of the second harmonic component and the potential generation of artifact images. To address this limitation, a framework for simultaneously reconstructing the second harmonic and fundamental components from original waves, enabling micro- and macro-damage imaging, combining with the nonlinear ultrasonic Lamb wave technique is proposed in this work. The acoustic linearity- and nonlinearity-aware dictionaries, based on the wave propagation model, are designed to effectively separate the fundamental and second harmonic components. The split augmented Lagrangian shrinkage algorithm is employed to reconstruct both components, enabling imaging of macro-damage using the fundamental component and enhanced fatigue crack imaging based on the second harmonic component. Numerical simulation and experimental validation highlight the framework’s performance, achieving both fatigue crack imaging and artifact suppression.

title: Impulsive Intralayer and Interlayer Quasi-Synchronization Control in Multiplex Networks Under Deception Attacks
authors: Xin Jin, Xiaojie Chen, Zhengxin Wang
publish: IEEE Transactions on Cybernetics
abstract: In this article, we study the quasi-synchronization problems in multiplex networks under deception attacks. First, we propose a new model of multiplex networks with interlayer couplings under deception attacks. We assume that the attackers inject false data into the communication channels. Since interlayer couplings are taken into account, we consider not only the case where attacks occur in the intralayer channels, but also the case where attacks occur in the interlayer communication channels. Furthermore, we set two binary variables obeying a random Bernoulli distribution to characterize whether the attacks occur or not. We then design an impulsive controller to enable the nodal states to achieve the desired states. By means of the Lyapunov function method and the average impulsive interval method, we obtain the sufficient conditions under which the nodal states can achieve interlayer quasi-synchronization and intralayer quasi-synchronization, respectively. Naturally, we obtain the sufficient conditions under which the nodal states can achieve complete quasi-synchronization. Furthermore, we introduce a leader and design a different impulsive controller. Using the same theoretical approach, we derive the sufficient conditions under which the nodal states can achieve complete quasi-synchronization. Finally, we provide three numerical examples to confirm the theoretical results.

title: IEEE Sensors Journal Publication Information
authors: N/A
publish: IEEE Sensors Journal

title: Robust Fault-Tolerant Control of an Aerial Manipulator System with Varying Center of Mass
authors: Lei Zhang, Le Wang, Lili Gong, Zhou Wang
publish: IEEE Transactions on Aerospace and Electronic Systems
abstract: Most existing control methods for aerial manipulator systems (AMS) are developed under the assumption that the system's center of mass (CoM) is known and fixed. In this study, a dynamic model of the AMS that accounts for time-varying CoM is developed, and based on this model, a robust fault-tolerant control strategy is designed. First, by using Lagrangian modeling method, the centroid theorem and the parallel-axis theorem are applied to construct a dynamic model of the system that considers the time varying CoM. Second, a single-neuron linear active disturbance rejection controller (SN-LADRC) is proposed, where the fixed parameters of the traditional linear state error feedback (LSEF) are replaced by online-adjustable gains, thereby enhancing the con troller's adaptability to load variations and external disturbances. Furthermore, a fault-tolerant controller based on adaptive non singular fast integral terminal sliding mode control (ANFITSMC) is proposed, which can adaptively estimate faults and suppress steady-state errors simultaneously. Finally, MATLAB simulations validate the accuracy of the proposed modeling approach and demonstrate the effectiveness of the designed control strategy in terms of response speed, robustness, and fault-tolerant capability.

title: Improving Retrieval of Canopy Chlorophyll Content by Integrating Leaf Spectral Measurements into SAIL Model
authors: Lieshen Yan, Xinjie Liu, Shanshan Du, Junjie Chen, Liangyun Liu
publish: IEEE Transactions on Geoscience and Remote Sensing
abstract: Canopy chlorophyll content (CCC) is a critical biochemical indicator of vegetation growth status and photosynthetic capacity, with broad applications in global climate change monitoring, agricultural productivity assessment, and ecosystem management. The PROSAIL model has been extensively applied for remote sensing retrieval of CCC; however, it shows notable limitations in accurately simulating canopy reflectance within the red-edge spectral region (680–780 nm), which in turn reduces the reliability of CCC estimation. To overcome this limitation, this study proposes a novel canopy spectral simulation approach, termed the measured-leaf-driven SAIL (mSAIL) model, which incorporates measured leaf spectra into the SAIL model to improve the spectral simulation accuracy of the red-edge region. Firstly, measured leaf spectra from the ANGERS dataset (a widely used laboratory-measured leaf spectral dataset) were incorporated into the SAIL model to simulate mSAIL canopy reflectance under a range of leaf and canopy parameter conditions. Subsequently, the mSAIL-simulated spectra were validated using field measurements and compared with the corresponding PROSAIL simulations. Finally, empirical statistical models were constructed using the mSAIL and PROSAIL simulated spectra to retrieve CCC. Validation using field measurements collected in 2002, 2004, and 2006 indicates that mSAIL performs substantially better than PROSAIL in simulating red-edge reflectance, with corresponding improvements in the accuracy of red-edge vegetation indices (VIs). Furthermore, in empirical CCC retrieval applications, mSAIL consistently outperformed PROSAIL across all validation years. The three-year combined validation results show that mSAIL achieved RMSE reductions of 43.53%, 29.17%, and 15.18% for MTCI, CIRE, and ND705, respectively. In summary, the mSAIL approach establishes a practical hybrid framework that effectively improves the accuracy of vegetation canopy spectral simulations, particularly in the red-edge region, thereby providing strong support for precise retrieval of CCC.

title: A Nanoscale-Resolution and Low-Coupling Two-DOF Piezoelectric Microgripper for Micromanipulation_supp1-3670241.mp4
authors: Yingxiang Liu
publish: N/A
abstract: Micromanipulation technology has played a pivotal role in advancing fields such as biomedicine, micro/nano manufacturing, and materials science, while the continuous progress in these domains imposes increasingly stringent requirements on the precision and multifunctionality of micromanipulation systems. Most existing microgrippers are limited to single degree-of-freedom (DOF) operation and commonly suffer from coupling errors, which restrict their applicability. This work presents a two-DOF piezoelectric (PZT) microgripper, in which PZT ceramic plates are bonded to the compliant mechanisms for actuation, and two-stage lever mechanisms are employed for displacement amplification, with one side generating gripping displacement and the other producing rubbing displacement. To address the coupling issue, three types of flexure hinges and their geometric parameters are theoretically analyzed and compared. The active compensation modules are incorporated into the structure, where compensation PZT plates combined with closed-loop control dynamically adjust the coupled displacement, thereby effectively suppressing coupling errors. Experimental results confirm that the proposed microgripper achieves two-DOF operation at nanoscale resolution, with coupling ratio minimized to only 0.09%. Moreover, its performance is further validated through experimental demonstrations, highlighting its effectiveness in complex micromanipulation operations.

title: Penetration-free Solid-Fluid Interaction on Shells and Rods
authors: Yuchen Sun, Jinyuan Liu, Yin Yang, Chenfanfu Jiang, Minchen Li, Bo Zhu
publish: IEEE Transactions on Visualization and Computer Graphics
abstract: We introduce a novel approach to simulate the interaction between fluids and thin elastic solids without any penetration. Our approach is centered around an optimization system augmented with barriers, which aims to find a configuration that ensures the absence of penetration while enforcing incompressibility for the fluids and minimizing elastic potentials for the solids. Unlike previous methods that primarily focus on velocity coherence at the fluid-solid interfaces, we demonstrate the effectiveness and flexibility of explicitly resolving positional constraints, including both explicit representation of solid positions and the implicit representation of fluid level-set interface. To preserve the volume of the fluid, we propose a simple yet efficient approach that adjusts the associated level-set values. Additionally, we develop a distance metric capable of measuring the separation between an implicitly represented surface and a Lagrangian object of arbitrary codimension. By integrating the inertia, solid elastic potential, damping, barrier potential, and fluid incompressibility within a unified system, we are able to robustly simulate a wide range of processes involving fluid interactions with lower-dimensional objects such as shells and rods. These processes include topology changes, bouncing, splashing, sliding, rolling, floating, and more.

title: Enhancing EV User Experience with Intelligent Charging Management Using White Shark-Wombat Optimization
authors: Evangelin Jeba J, R. Hariharan
publish: 2026 Sixth International Conference on Advances in Electrical, Computing, Communications and Sustainable Technologies (ICAECT)
abstract: Electric vehicles (EVs) are becoming more and more integrated into contemporary mobility systems as a result of the growing trend towards sustainable transportation. Modern power systems face both opportunities and challenges as EV adoption increases. The rising use of EVs raises worries about grid load management and user-related issues like waiting times and charging fees, even while they help with energy efficiency and environmental sustainability. Using Vehicle-to-Grid (V2G) technology, this study suggests a hybrid metaheuristic algorithm called White Shark with Wombat Optimization (WSWO) to improve EV charging and discharging schedules. Reducing daily expenses for users and easing the strain of energy demand on smart grids are the main goals. The results of the simulation show that the WSWO-optimized approach performs better than conventional techniques. WSWO lowers peak load from 200 kW to 140 kW, average charging wait time by 16 minutes, and overall charging cost by 25.7% (from $1050 to $780) when compared to First-Come-First-Served (FCFS). It also ensures greater consumer satisfaction by improving State of Charge (SoC) compliance to 98%. These results validate the WSWO algorithm's potential to improve grid stability and EV scheduling performance.

title: Comparative Efficacy of Focal and Binary Cross-Entropy Loss in Handling Class Imbalance for Stroke CT Classification
authors: Selorm Adablanu, Utpal Barman, Dulumani Das
publish: 2026 Sixth International Conference on Advances in Electrical, Computing, Communications and Sustainable Technologies (ICAECT)
abstract: Stroke remains one of the leading causes of death and long-term disability worldwide, demanding rapid and accurate diagnosis through brain CT imaging. Deep learning can assist in stroke detection; however, class imbalance between normal and stroke-positive images presents a major challenge. This study investigates how different loss functions handle this imbalance by comparing Focal Loss and Binary Cross-Entropy (BCE) in training an Xception convolutional neural network on the TEKNOFEST-2021 brain stroke CT dataset comprising 6,653 images. The model trained with Focal Loss achieved 99.08% accuracy, 100% sensitivity, and AUC <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$=0.9996$</tex>, outperforming the BCE model, which reached 98.40% accuracy and AUC = 0.9994. An additional experiment using test-time augmentation (TTA) unexpectedly reduced accuracy to about 86%, indicating that augmentations applied during testing must align with those used in training. The findings demonstrate that Focal Loss effectively improves sensitivity in imbalanced datasets by reducing missed detections without compromising precision. This approach can strengthen the reliability of AI-based stroke screening tools. Future work will focus on adaptive focal parameters and advanced data-balancing techniques to improve generalization across diverse clinical datasets.

title: Quantum Steganography with QKD for Secure Communication
authors: Karthiaeni T, Kiruthika S, Lavanya V, Mariammal K, Vinotheni M S
publish: 2026 Sixth International Conference on Advances in Electrical, Computing, Communications and Sustainable Technologies (ICAECT)
abstract: In response to rising cyber threats and the limitations of traditional security, this project proposes a hybrid framework combining quantum cryptography and text-based steganography for secure and concealed communication. Using the BB84 Quantum Key Distribution (QKD) protocol, a secure quantum key is established and extended with SHA-256 to encrypt a secret message via XOR operations. The encrypted data is embedded into a cover text using three steganographic techniques: case-based, zero-width, and color-based methods, the latter including double-color and double-color overlay variants. The techniques are evaluated on embedding efficiency, payload capacity, entropy, and deviation percentage. Among them, double-layer color-based steganography demonstrates superior performance, achieving up to 6 bits/symbol and 95% efficiency, with minimal error and high integrity. The system is implemented using Qiskit and HTML rendering, validating its practical feasibility. This dual-layer security model ensures confidentiality and invisibility, offering a robust, quantum-enhanced approach to secure communication in sensitive applications.

title: A Theoretical and Empirical Perspective on Customer Satisfaction and Loyalty in Mobile Payment Platforms
authors: M. Karthik Ram, S. Selvabaskar, K. Nigama, R. Guhan
publish: 2026 Sixth International Conference on Advances in Electrical, Computing, Communications and Sustainable Technologies (ICAECT)
abstract: India's dramatic shift to digital payments has altered the country's financial environment by moving people from cash to mobile payments. This study uses a mixedmethod methodology (i.e., data collection and analysis using SPSS and SmartPLS) to examine factors influencing customer satisfaction, loyalty, and switching intentions with regard to digital payment users in India. With a 95.3 % active user base, mostly female (66 %) and primarily between the ages of 18 and <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{2 5}(\mathbf{7 6 \%})$</tex>, the research findings show that security, satisfaction, and functional value are important factors that contribute to loyalty, while emotional, epistemic, and monetary value have no discernible effect. Based on Cronbach's alpha testing at 0.917, there is good evidence of the measurement model, while Structural Equation Modelling results indicate that users believed efficiency, performance and platform trust meant more than a perceived loyalty in spending as indicated by promotion benefit and emotional attachment. Despite high levels of adoption, the findings suggest there is very low frequency of transaction <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$(84.1 \% \leq 10$</tex> transactions/month), indicating that although there is a high level of adoption the assumption of active transactional use may be overestimating actual engagement. Improving functionality, speed, transparency and clarity of security communications will improve user loyalty. In the future, studies may focus on using AI powered payments with blockchain based processes to increase user security, explicit desires of users to earn rewards associated with their switching behavior, and exploring user data across geographic regions to further examine digital payment users' willingness to transition to automated digital payments.

title: Stochastic Planning Method for Multiple Bases Flexible DC Transmission Considering the Uncertainty of Renewable Energy
authors: Chu Jin, Yutong Wang, Yonghang Liu, Zuohong Li, Shichang Cui, Xinmiao Liu
publish: 2025 IEEE 9th Conference on Energy Internet and Energy System Integration (EI2)
abstract: Developing reasonable flexible DC power delivery curves for renewable bases is of great significance for the optimal allocation of flexible resources in receiving grids. To this end, this paper proposes an economic planning method for multiple bases flexible DC power delivery that considers the uncertainty of new energy output. Firstly, a joint scenario generation method for multiple renewable bases is proposed, using the improved K- means clustering method based on the historical output data of each renewable bases. Next, the power transmission operation model of the flexible DC channel is established, including three parts: the renewable bases, the flexible DC channel and the receiving power grid. The model takes the minimum daily power generation cost as the objective function, and the constraints include the constraints of flexible DC transmission channels, thermal power units, wind power photovoltaic units, pumped storage units, and energy storage units. Finally, a case study is conducted on an improved IEEE 6-node system, with node 6 being improved to three small nodes, representing the three renewable bases at the sending end. The results show that the power delivery curve derived from this method can meet the flexible DC delivery requirements under various new energy output scenarios, thereby validating the effectiveness and economy of the proposed method.

title: Region-Aware Token Aggregation for Fine-Grained Change Detection
authors: Bianca-Cerasela-Zelia Blaga
publish: 2025 IEEE 21st International Conference on Intelligent Computer Communication and Processing (ICCP)
abstract: Fine-grained change detection in structured remote sensing environments, such as agricultural fields, requires models that can reason over semantically coherent regions while preserving spatial precision. Existing methods often rely on convolutional or transformer-based architectures with uniform tokenization, limiting their ability to capture changes aligned with realworld semantic structures. In this paper, we propose SFCNet, a region-aware change detection framework that leverages explicit field-level segmentation masks to guide both token aggregation and spatial decoding. Our method introduces a Field-Aware Region Encoder (FARE) that aggregates features within each semantic region, producing interpretable region tokens. These are processed through a slot attention mechanism to model temporal differences across structured units. A re-projection decoder then restores field-wise predictions to pixel-space using the original geometry. We evaluate SFCNet on the CLCD dataset, where it outperforms prior state-of-the-art models by $\mathbf{+ 3. 3 1 \%}$.

title: Scheduling Modelling of Airport Surface Guidance by Unmanned Follow-Me Vehicle
authors: Tianping Sun, Xinping Zhu, Kai Wang, Ke Tang, Dezhou Yuan, Junming Yang
publish: 2025 3rd Asian Aerospace and Astronautics Conference (AAAC)
abstract: Aircraft surface movement during taxiing, a critical flight phase, faces triple challenges: operational safety, efficiency bottlenecks, and environmental requirements. Traditional taxiing modes relying on pilot-controller coordination suffer from issues like response delays and low fuel efficiency. unmanned follow-me vehicle technology offers a new pathway to enhance the intelligence level of the taxiing process. This paper conducts research on scheduling modeling for aircraft surface taxiing processes implemented with unmanned guidance. Firstly, the scenario and core problems of unmanned-guided aircraft surface taxiing are defined. Secondly, an integrated static model of the runway-taxiway-apron system is constructed; node and segment data are extracted, and the airport topology is abstracted to lay the foundation for scheduling. Subsequently, functional, behavioral, and constraint models of the unmanned-guided unit are developed. Finally, the core focus is on modeling the scheduling for unmanned-guided surface taxiing. With the objective of minimizing aircraft delay time, and under the integration of static environmental constraints and dynamic behavioral constraints of the guidance unit, an integrated scheduling optimization model is constructed.

title: Applications and Future of Eye-Tracking Technology in Pilot Behavior Assessment: A Systematic Review
authors: Shen Jingjing, Shang Zhihua, Li Shining, Zhang Haijun, Zhang Xuyan, Xue Shaoxiong
publish: 2025 3rd Asian Aerospace and Astronautics Conference (AAAC)
abstract: In the field of aviation, analyzing the behavior of pilots holds significant importance. To clarify the areas of pilot attention and workload, researchers often utilize eye-tracking technology to analyze pilot behavior. However, domestic research in this area is still in the exploratory phase. Therefore, this paper reviews the application of eye-tracking technology in the assessment of pilot behavior to provide ideas for subsequent research. The paper primarily focuses on two major areas: flight safety and flight training. It lists multiple research cases from both domestic and international sources and summarizes the commonly used indicators in pilot behavior assessment. Additionally, the paper points out the significance of eye-tracking data visualization in the aviation field and presents related research content. Finally, the paper highlights the challenges and development directions faced by eye-tracking technology in practical applications within the aviation field.

title: Chiral Optical Response and Tunability of Type-II ZnSe�CdS Dot-in-Rods
authors: Yijing Sun, Yupu Zhaowang, Chen Xing, Jiaxin Ling, Zifeng Zhang, Yuxuan Lin, Binqian Zhou, Xiuqin Ao, Jiaji Cheng, Junjie Hao
publish: 2025 International Conference on Energy, Materials, and Photonics (EMP)
abstract: Type-II quantum rods are a promising class of nanomaterials with spatial charge separation, tunable optical properties, and have potential for applications in spintronics and chiral optoelectronics. In this work, we report the successful synthesis of ZnSe-CdS type-II dot-in-rod (DR) nanostructures with tunable aspect ratios (ARs). By precisely controlling the rod growth, we achieved ARs ranging from 2.3 to 5.4. The resulting nanorods exhibit type-II band alignment, as evidenced by redshifted photoluminescence (PL) spectra and prolonged lifetimes. Through a simple ligand-induced chiral process, we successfully synthesized cysteine-modified chiral type-II DRs, which displayed a chiral circular dichroism (CD) g-factor as high as <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$1.86 \times 10^{-4}$</tex>. This work presents a promising approach to tune the chiroptical properties of type-II DRs. Moreover, it not only demonstrates the controlled fabrication of chiral ZnSe-CdS DRs but also provides a valuable framework for stabilizing and tuning their chiral properties for next-generation chiral nanophotonics.

title: Automatic Segmentation of Orbital Walls
authors: Ioana Tofan, Fabian-Andrei Stoica, Ştefana Duţă, Alina Elena Sultana, Mihail-Theodor Dragomir, Eduard Liciu
publish: 2025 IEEE 21st International Conference on Intelligent Computer Communication and Processing (ICCP)
