abstract: This study presents a deep learning framework for automatic segmentation of orbital walls in cranial CT scans using U-Net-based architectures under multiple training regimes. Three experiments were conducted: (i) a supervised baseline trained on nine expert-annotated volumes from the public CranealCT dataset; (ii) a semi-supervised model with 50 pseudo-labeled volumes; and (iii) a final model trained on 39 heterogeneous volumes, combining public and proprietary data with expert annotations. Preprocessing included intensity normalization, CLAHE, Gaussian smoothing, and window standardization. Training used Dice and Binary Cross-Entropy loss with strong augmentation. The semi-supervised model outperformed the baseline (Dice 0.968 vs. 0.937). On the proprietary test set, the final model achieved Dice 0.9193, HD95 2.53 mm, ASD 0.87 mm, accuracy 0.9985, precision 0.8978, and recall 0.9763, demonstrating robustness to imaging variability and suitability for clinical use in orbital trauma reconstruction.

title: Research on the Control Method of Multiple Small Spacecraft Based on Simplified Reinforcement Learning and Multi-Objective Optimization
authors: Li Xinhang, Vsevolod V. Koryanov
publish: 2025 3rd Asian Aerospace and Astronautics Conference (AAAC)
abstract: In response to the need for collaborative control of multiple small spacecraft in modern space missions, this paper proposes a path planning method that integrates Q-learning reinforcement learning and the multi-objective optimization NSGA-II.This method combines deep Q-network learning with the non-dominated sorting genetic algorithm (NSGA-II) to autonomously learn efficient decision strategies in the spacecraft state space and balances metrics such as path length, coverage, collision risk, and load balancing within a multi-objective optimization framework.Simulation results show that, compared to traditional methods such as the ant colony algorithm, this method significantly improves task area coverage and the balance of multi-spacecraft task load distribution while maintaining shorter paths and avoiding collisions, thereby validating the effectiveness and superiority of the proposed method for multi-spacecraft collaborative path planning.

title: An Explainable AI-Based Context-Aware Forecasting Framework for Accurate and Understandable Demand Predictions
authors: S. Subha, M. Jasmine, M. Gomathi, R.S.V Keerthana
publish: 2026 Sixth International Conference on Advances in Electrical, Computing, Communications and Sustainable Technologies (ICAECT)
abstract: Accurate and clear demand forecasting is crucial for making good decisions in supply chain, retail, and energy management. While deep learning models like Long ShortTerm Memory (LSTM) networks and Convolutional Neural Networks (CNN) provide high predictive accuracy, their lack of transparency makes it hard to use them in practice. Current research usually focuses on either forecasting accuracy or interpretability separately and seldom offers a clear, practical framework. This paper introduces a new hybrid demand forecasting system that combines CNN-LSTM deep learning with two explainability methods, SHAP and LIME, in a context-aware forecasting pipeline. The system takes into account external factors like holidays, promotions, and weather conditions to improve reliability. In addition, we created an interactive dashboard to display forecasts, investigate feature attributions, and support “what-if” scenario analysis for practical use by stakeholders. The proposed framework pushes the boundaries by integrating accuracy, interpret-ability, and deployment into one system. To our knowledge, no previous work combines CNN-LSTM forecasting, SHAP and LIME explanations, and context-aware interactivity into a usable platform. Experimental results show that this approach enhances both forecasting accuracy and explainability, leading to more transparent and informed decision-making.

title: Dynamic Optimization Strategy for Grid-Forming Resonance Damper Utilizing an Inductance Parameter Observer
authors: Xiaoxiao Qi, Yuan Zhang, Baoyu Zhai, Bing Sun
publish: 2025 IEEE 9th Conference on Energy Internet and Energy System Integration (EI2)
abstract: The active damper can suppress resonance by constructing a resistive impedance that alters the overall impedance characteristics of multiple converters at the point of common coupling. However, the implementation of accuracy resistive impedance of the active damper requires high gain over the wide band, which is difficult to achieve. Deviations in filter inductance values caused by magnetic saturation can further degrade the resonance suppression capability of active dampers. To address this issue, this paper implements the active damper function on a grid-forming converter, emulating its principle. As a result, the resonance damper strategy for grid-forming converters based on impedance reshaping is proposed. Furthermore, an inductance parameter observer is designed to dynamically identify inductance parameters, which enhances the anti-interference capability of the resonance damper function. Experimental results validate the effectiveness of the proposed strategy.

title: A Multi-Agent Deep Reinforcement Learning Framework for Dynamic Path Planning in Autonomous Vehicles
authors: Javed. N, R. Yogesh Rajkumar
publish: 2026 Sixth International Conference on Advances in Electrical, Computing, Communications and Sustainable Technologies (ICAECT)
abstract: For self-driving cars to navigate through congested city streets with a variety of vehicles, they require intelligent, dependable, and adaptable navigation systems. This study presents DeepReinforceNav, a novel method that employs cutting-edge artificial intelligence to swiftly and dynamically plan routes for several self-driving cars at once. Each car is viewed by the system as a team member that competes with other cars while also working together. This enables every vehicle to learn how to drive safely and efficiently while reaching its destination as soon as feasible. The system employs advanced methods to comprehend the environment surrounding each vehicle, accounting for the locations of other cars, obstructions, and changes over time. This enables the vehicles to foresee issues and make informed judgements in response to their surroundings. Simulations conducted in authentic urban traffic scenarios demonstrate that DeepReinforceNav outperforms more basic AI techniques and outdated navigation systems. It responds to changing situations more quickly, is safer, and is more efficient. Additionally, the system is capable of managing intricate road networks with a variety of vehicle kinds, which bodes well for practical applications. The framework also uses real-time IoT data collected from the cloud streaming to improve situational awareness and help all self-driving cars make decisions faster and more accurately.

title: Current Stabilization Control of Electrolytic Power Supply Based on Fuzzy Fractional-Order PI
authors: Jikai Chen, Junjie Han, Shuangshuang Yao, Jiayang Zhang, Jiansheng Ming, Haibo Zhong
publish: 2025 IEEE 9th Conference on Energy Internet and Energy System Integration (EI2)
abstract: To address issues such as low conversion efficiency and low DC current stabilization accuracy in electrolytic rectification, a rectification scheme featuring coordinated control of the rectifier transformer and thyristors is proposed. The front stage adopts 18-step coarse adjustment via the rectifier transformer, while the rear stage employs fine adjustment using thyristors, thereby improving the system's power factor. First, the operational characteristics of the main circuit topology are analyzed. The analysis reveals that thyristor rectification constitutes a large time-delay system, which suffers from problems such as poor dynamic response and weak anti-interference capability. To tackle this, fractional-order theory is introduced and combined with conventional PI control, enabling more flexible control of the controlled object. Subsequently, to enhance the system's adaptability, a strategy integrating the frequency-domain graphical parameter tuning method with fuzzy adaptive fractional-order PI control is proposed. Simulation and experimental results demonstrate that the proposed strategy exhibits excellent dynamic and static performance as well as robustness.

title: Frequency Support Coordinated Control Strategy for PV-Storage Integrated Systems
authors: Shouqi Jiang, Yaxin Zhang, Lixin Wang, Mengke Liao, Qian Cao
publish: 2025 IEEE 9th Conference on Energy Internet and Energy System Integration (EI2)
abstract: To address the challenges of low inertia and weak damping resulting from the growing integration of renewable energy in power systems and to maintain grid frequency stability, a coordinated control approach for active frequency support in PV-storage integrated systems is proposed. This strategy is designed to fulfill the frequency regulation requirements of photovoltaic systems with energy storage across various operating conditions. By examining the power regulation capacity of synchronous machines and system imbalances, the frequency control needs for different scenarios are determined. A self-synchronizing control scheme for grid-connected inverters is introduced to provide inertia support while conveying dynamic frequency information. Additionally, a multi-time-scale frequency regulation method that incorporates a frequency dead zone is proposed, allowing for adaptive switching between inertia support and primary frequency control in response to different disturbances. This method effectively leverages the adjustment capabilities of synchronous machines and ensures that the energy storage system returns to its initial state, thus improving the system’s frequency stability under varying conditions.

title: An Active Support Method for Distributed Wind Power and Energy Storage Systems Aimed at Suppressing Local Power Grid Oscillations
authors: Lichun Chen, Zhiyuan Sun, Kun Zheng, Jinzhao Li, Chang Sun, Xubin Liu
publish: 2025 IEEE 9th Conference on Energy Internet and Energy System Integration (EI2)
abstract: The integration of distributed wind power and energy storage systems (DWPESS) reduces local grid inertia and damping, triggering wideband oscillations in voltage and frequency while impairing the transient stability of synchronous generators. To address this issue, this paper proposes an active support control method for DWPESS aimed at suppressing broadband oscillations in local grid voltage and frequency. The main contributions are as follows: (1) Through small-signal modeling of a wind-storage integrated system and eigenvalue analysis, the causes of wide-band oscillations under active support control are investigated; (2) A method for suppressing broadband oscillations in local grid frequency and voltage is proposed, which mitigates the impact of DWPESS integration and enhances the transient stability of the local grid. Simulation tests validate the effectiveness of the proposed method.

title: Research on Intelligent Diagnosis and Data Interchange Technology for Data Problems in Power Communication Business
authors: Shengchao Yan, Lei Zhi, Yang Li, Wei Zhao, Li Li, Qilong Wu
publish: 2025 IEEE 9th Conference on Energy Internet and Energy System Integration (EI2)
abstract: In order to achieve intelligent classification and diagnosis of communication business security and data problems between backbone transmission networks and data networks in layered, cross domain, and multi vendor device hybrid networking modes, based on data verification results, automatic classification of data problems and automatic extraction and generation of rules were achieved through diagnostic training environment modeling, realizing comprehensive governance of existing business data. Finally, the diagnostic results of business data quality will be transmitted back and shared with the information management system in reverse, achieving interconnectivity of verification data and optimizing and improving the quality of each other's business data; Provide the verified multi domain business data to various intelligent analysis functions to provide accurate and reliable business operation data.

title: ConvStem-ViT: A Vision Transformer Framework for Season-Aware Tea Leaf Disease Prediction
authors: Rajat Amat, Mourina Ghosh
publish: 2026 Sixth International Conference on Advances in Electrical, Computing, Communications and Sustainable Technologies (ICAECT)
abstract: Identification of tea leaf disease is essential for crop quality, early pest control, and long-term agricultural output. This research offers ConvStem-ViT, a unique Vision Transformer framework that combines a convolutional stem for fine-grained texture extraction with transformer encoders for capturing long-range spatial and seasonal correlations, enabling season-aware tea leaf disease prediction. The model achieved 98.7 % accuracy, 98.5 % sensitivity, 98.8 % specificity, 98.6 % precision, and an F1 score of 98.6 % after its performance was thoroughly assessed using a variety of criteria. The AUC-ROC comparison curve indicates improved discriminative capacity of ConvStem-ViT over typical CNN architectures, while the class-wise ROC curves confirm strong per-class classification reliability across all illness categories. Additionally, comparative analysis reveals that ConvStem-ViT consistently outperforms conventional CNN models in feature representation and robustness under varying seasonal conditions. The class-level UMAP projection further validates distinct clustering and effective feature separability.

title: Explainable Mobilenetv2 Framework with Knowledge Graphs for Classifying Hand-Foot-Mouth and Chickenpox Diseases
authors: N. Susmitha, T. Anuradha
publish: 2026 Sixth International Conference on Advances in Electrical, Computing, Communications and Sustainable Technologies (ICAECT)
abstract: Hand-Foot-and-Mouth Disease (HFMD) and Chickenpox are clinically significant viral infections that manifest with overlapping dermatological presentations, often complicating accurate differential diagnosis. Timely and precise identification is crucial for effective therapeutic management and outbreak prevention. Leveraging advances in deep learning, this study investigates the efficacy of MobileNetV2, a lightweight yet high-performing convolutional neural network, for binary classification of HFMD and Chickenpox using high-resolution skin lesion images. The finetuned model achieved 98 % training accuracy and 97 % test accuracy, demonstrating strong generalization across unseen data. To foster transparency and enhance clinical trust, Gradient-weighted Class Activation Mapping (Grad-CAM) was employed to highlight discriminative lesion regions guiding the classification process, while knowledge graph integration provided semantic contextualization of predictions, bridging the gap between raw outputs and clinical interpretability. The experimental findings underscore the potential of MobileNetV2 as a computationally efficient and interpretable diagnostic tool, advancing the paradigm of AI-driven medical image analysis in dermatology.

title: An Energy Management Strategy for Distribution Network-Microgrid Collaboration Based on an Energy Router
authors: Chenming Liu, Xi Zhao, Wentao Wu, Haiyu Zhang, Yaru Sun, Yuting Song, Xiang Gao, Huihui Song
publish: 2025 IEEE 9th Conference on Energy Internet and Energy System Integration (EI2)
abstract: The integration of high-penetration distributed generation (DG) into active distribution networks (ADNs) presents significant challenges for power quality and operational stability. The energy router (ER), a core component of the energy internet, offers a promising solution with its capabilities for flexible power flow regulation and energy management. This paper focuses on the energy management strategy for an ADN where multiple microgrids (MGs) are integrated via an ER. We propose a hierarchical optimization architecture coordinating the distribution network layer, the ER layer, and the MG layer. To address the uncertainties inherent in renewable DG outputs within the MGs, a two-stage distributionally robust optimization (DRO) model is developed. This model minimizes the overall system cost while accounting for the worst-case probability distribution of DG scenarios, constructed using a data-driven approach. The column-and-constraint generation (C&CG) algorithm is employed to solve this model efficiently. Case studies on a modified IEEE 33-node distribution network with three microgrids demonstrate the effectiveness of the proposed strategy. The results indicate that the proposed strategy significantly enhances distribution network power quality and DG integration, achieving superior performance over conventional uncertainty-based optimization models.

title: Simulation and Vulnerability Analysis of Cascading Failure in Power System Based on Node Dynamic Risk Outage Probability
authors: Lijuan Li, Guoqing Luo, Fengzhao Tian, Yabin Fu
publish: 2025 IEEE 9th Conference on Energy Internet and Energy System Integration (EI2)
abstract: The probability of node outages has a significant impact on the triggering and evolution of cascading failure. This paper proposes a modeling and vulnerability analysis framework based on the dynamic risk-based outage probability of nodes. First, a piecewise differential equation model is constructed to quantify the nonlinear cumulative effects of voltage amplitude deviation and abnormal duration on the probability of node outages. Second, a probability-driven cascading fault propagation mechanism is established, with node outage probability as the continuous state variable, coupling local risk dynamic terms with neighborhood fault transmission terms to reveal the cascading diffusion patterns of fault risks in spatiotemporal dimensions. Furthermore, a multidimensional comprehensive vulnerability index is designed, integrating the expected value of node outage probability, fault propagation influence, and dynamic risk exposure time, to identify critical weak links in the system. Case studies based on the IEEE-39 system validate that the proposed model effectively characterizes the dynamic risk accumulation characteristics of new energy units; critical vulnerable nodes are primarily distributed along the main transmission corridors, with their fault propagation influence significantly exceeding the system average; risk assessment results integrating the expected probability of node outages and load losses reveal the heterogeneous characteristics of system risk distribution. This validates the effectiveness of the methods proposed in this paper.

title: Transformer capacity expansion planning model with optimal network reconfiguration under N-1 contingency conditions
authors: Yaqi Sun, Haotian Zhao, Yixun Xue, Yujie Sheng
publish: 2025 IEEE 9th Conference on Energy Internet and Energy System Integration (EI2)
abstract: <sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">1</sup> With the continuous growth of distribution network loads driven by socio-economic development, transformer capacity expansion has become increasingly necessary. In the planning process, it is essential to account for N-1 contingency-induced transformer overloads. This paper proposes a transformer expansion planning method that incorporates optimal network reconfiguration following contingencies. After an N-1 failure, fault isolation and load transfer are achieved through network reconfiguration, thereby ensuring load supply and balancing transformer loading rates. The planning model expands transformer capacity based on optimal reconfiguration strategies, ensuring the secure and stable operation of the distribution network. Numerical experiments demonstrate that the proposed model can effectively enhance the carrying capability of the distribution network under fault conditions.

title: Research on optimal configuration of energy storage in new power system based on deep reinforcement learning
authors: Hangyu Zhou, Peng Sun, Mao Yang, Yitao Li, Yi Chai
publish: 2025 IEEE 9th Conference on Energy Internet and Energy System Integration (EI2)
abstract: An optimal configuration method of new energy storage system based on deep reinforcement learning is proposed to solve the uncertain challenges brought by new energy access to power system. Firstly, the optimal configuration problem of new energy storage and the deep reinforcement learning agent are modeled respectively, and the configuration problem is transformed into a sequence decision process. Secondly, a training framework is designed to enable agents to learn to make optimal energy storage capacity and power configuration decisions under complex spatio-temporal uncertainties. Finally, a multi-dimensional visualization standard is proposed to intuitively evaluate and compare the performance of different configuration schemes, providing a clear basis for decision makers.

title: Hierarchical Attention Network with GNN Embeddings for Credit Risk Prediction
authors: Krishna Chaitanya Yarlagadda
publish: 2026 Sixth International Conference on Advances in Electrical, Computing, Communications and Sustainable Technologies (ICAECT)
abstract: Credit risk forecasting is essential for financial decision-making, as stable lending activities are supported by accurate assessments of the creditworthiness of borrowers. Complex dependencies across hierarchical customer data, such as transaction patterns, demographic characteristics, and network interactions, are difficult to capture, as traditional machine learning models focus on fixed features and linear relationships. In addition, the processing of relational structures and sequence data at the same time poses problems for shallow architectures. These problems often result in misclassification and higher default rates. To bridge this gap, a hierarchical attention network (HAN) with graph neural network (GNN) embeddings is presented. This hybrid approach supports deep representation learning, feature interaction modelling and generalisation to unseen cases. While graph representations reveal hidden patterns in customer networks, layers of focus focus on the most relevant financial indicators. The proposed approach is optimised by backpropagating after training with historical credit data sets. Performance measurement metrics such as accuracy, precision, recall, F1-score and receiver operating characteristic curve area (AUC-ROC) are used. Compared to conventional neural nets and tree-based models, experimental results show that combining hierarchical attention and GNN embedding results in more accurate risk scores and probabilities.

title: Optimal Operation of the Integrated Electricity-Heat-Transportation Energy System for Resilience Enhancement
authors: Yuan Cheng, Jia Su, Bohan Shi, Xinyue Chang, Yixun Xue, Hongbin Sun
publish: 2025 IEEE 9th Conference on Energy Internet and Energy System Integration (EI2)
abstract: Currently, the coupling between electricity, heating, and transport systems within integrated energy systems is becoming increasingly close. When extreme disasters strike, enhancing the resilience of integrated energy systems remains a significant challenge. To address the challenges, the integrated energy system is modelled by incorporating heat network thermal load inertia. Secondly, construct a multi-source collaborative recovery model to optimize post-disaster system scheduling. This model minimizes load curtailment while maximizing recovery efficiency, accounting for dynamic resource scheduling and time-dependent output characteristics. Finally, case study simulations demonstrate significantly reducing load losses, enhancing System Resilience.

title: Research on New Topology and Control of AC/AC Converter Based on Waveform Transformation
authors: Bao Liu, Gaoshan Hou, Yunfei Xu, Xiaoyu Zhang, Guoliang Zhao, Chen Jia
publish: 2025 IEEE 9th Conference on Energy Internet and Energy System Integration (EI2)
abstract: As a key device for direct or indirect conversion of alternating current energy, AC/AC converters are widely used in fields such as power transmission and transformation, industrial drives, and new energy grid integration. As the current mainstream topology, modular multilevel matrix converters have advantages including flexible control, easy scalability, and excellent output performance. However, converter arms bear two voltage peaks, and they suffer from a large number of modules and poor economy. Based on the idea of waveform transformation, this paper proposes a new type of AC/AC converter topology, which reduces the number of modules by 55%. For the proposed converter topology, a mathematical model is established, the energy balance constraints of the converter are clarified, and an energy balance strategy for this new converter topology is put forward. Finally, through PSCAD simulation examples, the feasibility and effectiveness of the proposed topology and control strategy are verified.

title: Low-Carbon Optimal of VPP Considering Carbon Capture Power and Waste Incineration Power Plants
authors: Yan Cui
publish: 2025 IEEE 9th Conference on Energy Internet and Energy System Integration (EI2)
abstract: To reduce wind and solar curtailment and promote low-carbon operation of the system, this paper proposes an optimization model for virtual power plants (VPP) that coordinates carbon capture power plant (CCPP) with waste incineration power plant (WIPP). Firstly, the coordinated operation of gas unit, CCPP and power to gas (P2G) is studied. The carbon capture system (CCS) transports CO<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</inf> to P2G to generate natural gas, which is used as input to supply the gas unit. Secondly, to stabilize the volatility of renewable energy, the flue gas treatment process of WIPP is studied and analyzed. Finally, a cost-optimal VPP scheduling model is constructed, and the model is solved by using the cplex toolbox. The example simulation verifies the proposed model improves the economy of the system and reduces carbon emissions.

title: Research on Microscale Wind Speed Forecasting Method Based on Vision Transformer
authors: Ziyang Hua, Jie Yan, Kaibo Wang, Yongqian Liu
publish: 2025 IEEE 9th Conference on Energy Internet and Energy System Integration (EI2)
abstract: With the continuous growth of global wind power installed capacity, microscale wind speed forecasting plays a crucial role in improving the accuracy of wind farm power predictions. Traditional Numerical Weather Prediction (NWP) suffers from low resolution and high computational cost, failing to meet the demand for fine-grained forecasting at individual wind turbine locations.This study utilizes a Vision Transformer (ViT) to construct a downscaling model, achieving wind speed forecasts at the turbine level by leveraging data from the Global Forecast System (GFS). Compared to commercial NWP wind speed forecasts, the error is reduced by approximately 20%.A high-resolution dataset was constructed based on reanalysis data, GFS data, and wind farm measured data. Leveraging ViT's capability to capture global dependencies, a downscaling model was built that maps large-scale meteorological elements to wind speeds at turbine locations. The downscaled results showed that the average Root Mean Square Error (RMSE) decreased by 0.76 m/s spatially and was reduced to 1.5 m/s temporally.By modifying the input data to the downscaling model, microscale wind speed forecasts at the turbine level were achieved. The RMSE of the microscale forecast results relative to commercial NWP was reduced from 3.47 m/s to 2.36 m/s (a reduction of approximately 32%), validating the feasibility of the Vision Transformer-based microscale wind speed forecasting method. Furthermore, adjusting model parameters by changing the patch size revealed that increasing the patch size can further reduce prediction error, offering new perspectives for model optimization.

title: A Deep Learning Centric Framework for Real Time Voltage Stability Assessment
authors: P. Manikandan, M.A Lekshmidevi, S. Anu Priya, A.V. Suganya, Gurumoorthy. G, S. Balaji
publish: 2026 Sixth International Conference on Advances in Electrical, Computing, Communications and Sustainable Technologies (ICAECT)
abstract: The incessant advancement of integrating renewable energy, power electronics converters, and dynamic demand into power systems has made traditional analytical techniques inadequate for supplying voltage stability guarantees for contemporary power systems. Data-driven solutions based on soft computing, specifically deep learning (DL), present data-centric methods that can distinguish nonlinear dynamics in real time. This paper submits a system based on DL that utilizes convolutional neural networks (CNNs) and long short-term memory (LSTM) units for real-time voltage stability assessment (VSA) in scenarios with high dimensionality and temporal variance. Thorough case studies of the highly developed IEEE 39 bus system, with an extra feature of 30% renewable energy integration, show that the submitted solution results in a classification accuracy of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{9 8. 7 \%}$</tex> with a mean absolute error of 0.014 per unit while predicting the voltage collapse margin, thus outperforming traditional support vector machine (SVM) and fuzzy logic-based methods by margins of 12.4% and 8.9%, individually. These findings highlight the potential of soft computing-based deep learning as a key player in the resilience operations of power systems in the future.

title: Co-exploration of Hardware Acceleration and Tensor Programs via Unified Compilation Interface
authors: Yang Bai, MINGJUN LI, Yuhao JI, Bei Yu
publish: ACM Transactions on Design Automation of Electronic Systems
abstract: None

title: Real-time Multi-modal Social Event Detection: a New Dataset and A Key Instance-Driven, Quality-Aware Graph Neural Network
authors: Yifei Han, Congbo Ma, Shan Xue, Jia Wu, Jian Yang
publish: IEEE Transactions on Big Data
abstract: Social event detection (SED) involves identifying and analyzing significant real-world events using data generated on social media platforms. With the rapid growth of platforms like Weibo and Twitter, users are sharing not just text but also images. However, most existing SED methods remain text-focused, limiting their ability to fully capture the complexity of real-world social dynamics. Moreover, the lack of multi-modal datasets specifically designed for SED has blocked the development of models that can effectively exploit these rich content types. To address these limitations, we introduced WEIBO2022, an extensive multi-modal SED dataset that includes both text and image data. The dataset is available in two versions: WEIBO2022-Medium, containing 25,435 entries and WEIBO2022-Large, containing 79,825 entries. In addition, we presented a novel network called the Key Instance-driven, Quality-aware Graph Neural Network (KQGNN), which features a key instance-driven library, a quality-aware learning process, and a multi-modal fusion module, enhancing its ability to detect events accurately in both offline and real-time settings. Extensive experiments showcase the exceptional performance and superiority of the proposed model, showing improvements in detection accuracy and effective prevention of catastrophic forgetting during continuous training.

title: Improved Non-Iterative Autofocus Back Projection Algorithm for Multirotor UAV-borne SAR Imaging
authors: Yingpei Chen, Daoxiang An
publish: IEEE Sensors Journal
abstract: Synthetic aperture radar (SAR) is a radar remote sensing system that can provide high-resolution images unaffected by weather or lighting conditions. With the advantages of lightweight design, miniaturization, portability, and operational convenience, multirotor unmanned aerial vehicle (UAV)-borne SAR systems enable rapid reconnaissance of key areas. Trajectory deviations from multirotor UAVs induce serious envelope and phase errors that degrade the quality of SAR images. Thus, autofocus algorithms are indispensable for error compensation. When estimating phase errors, traditional autofocus back-projection (ABP) algorithms often exhibit susceptibility to interference from uncompensated echoes and long-aperture acquisition, which compromise their reliability. This paper proposes an improved non-iterative ABP algorithm for the UAV-borne SAR imaging structure into two processing stages. The first stage is dedicated to local phase error estimation via sub-image autofocusing, including overlapping sub-aperture segmentation, phase estimation by an improved coordinate-descent method, and the full-aperture phase error reconstruction. The second stage centers on trajectory deviation estimation, which includes quality evaluation of the phase error estimation for each sub-image to identify the valid estimates and combines these valid local phase error to retrieve residual trajectory deviation using the weighted total least squares (WTLS) method. Notably, the proposed algorithm achieves high-precision phase error estimation and accurate trajectory deviation estimation while maintaining high efficiency through non-iterative processing in both the phase error estimation stage and the trajectory deviation estimation stage. Its performance is verified by experimental data acquired by a self-developed multirotor UAV Ku-band SAR system.

title: PROTEAN: An Explainable AI Pipeline for Protein Classification in Support of Precision Medicine
authors: Luigi Di Biasi, Fabiola De Marco, Alessia Auriemma Citarella, Genoveffa Tortora, Rita Francese
publish: IEEE Journal of Biomedical and Health Informatics
abstract: Soluble N-ethyl-maleimide-sensitive factor Attachment Protein Receptors (SNARE) proteins play critical roles in intracellular trafficking and disease mechanisms, making them relevant targets for precision medicine approaches. However, distinguishing SNARE from NONSNARE proteins remains challenging due to shared sequence motifs and structural similarities. In addition, traditional AI models often lack transparency, which limits their utility in sensitive biomedical contexts. We propose PROTEAN (PROTeins classification with Explainable Artificial iNtelligence), a comprehensive methodology that combines machine learning and explainable AI (XAI) techniques for accurate and interpretable protein classification. The pipeline involves three phases: (1) data preprocessing, conducted on a balanced dataset (D128) of 128 SNARE and NONSNARE protein sequences, (2) model training and evaluation of multiple classifiers, such as Support Vector Machines (SVMs) and Neural Networks (NNs), and (3) interpretation of model decisions via SHAP and LIME XAI models to identify the most influential protein descriptors. The Medium Gaussian SVM outperformed all other models, achieving 92.1% accuracy, 94.8% sensitivity, and 89.5% specificity on a balanced test set of 458 proteins. SHAP and LIME provided consistent and biologically meaningful explanations, highlighting features related to amino acid composition and sequence order as critical for classification. PROTEAN demonstrates that integrating interpretable AI models with well-balanced datasets enhances the performance and transparency of protein classification. These insights are vital for the development of trustworthy AI systems in biomedical applications, offering new tools for disease mechanism analysis, biomarker discovery, and personalized therapeutic strategies.

title: Enabling Coherent Multipath Estimation in RIS-assisted Systems via A Synthesized Virtual Array Design
authors: Fengkai Chen, Yulin Su, Jide Yuan, Yuli Yang
publish: IEEE Communications Letters
abstract: Reconfigurable intelligent surfaces (RISs) have emerged as a promising technology for enabling high-precision wireless sensing. However, existing angular estimation methods for RIS-assisted systems face challenges in coherent multipath environments due to the rank deficiency of signal covariance matrices. To address this, this paper proposes a time-domain synthesized virtual array framework through RIS configuration design. Specifically, we design a sliding-window reflection protocol that reorganizes temporal signal samples into a virtual uniform linear array structure, effectively embedding spatial information into the time domain. We theoretically prove that the virtual structure enables the application of forward-backward spatial smoothing (FBSS), guaranteeing full-rank covariance restoration for robust angular estimation. Simulation results validate the resolution of coherent multipath and reveal that prioritizing virtual aperture over snapshot diversity maximizes precision under constrained resources.

title: TMTA: a Truthful Multi-Task Allocation Scheme for Enhancing Service Quality in Sparse Mobile Crowdsensing
authors: Xiangwan Fu, Qingyong Deng, Anfeng Liu, Haolin Liu, Zhetao Li
publish: IEEE Transactions on Services Computing
abstract: In sparse mobile crowdsensing, the platform con-structs services based on low-cost data collection through data inference schemes, where the quality of the inferred data directly affects the service quality. Existing data inference schemes assume that workers report trustworthy data, which is not practical in SMCS. It is urgent to establish a high-quality data collection scheme for data inference that can tolerate false data to enhance inferred data quality. To address this challenge, we propose a Truthful Multi-Task Allocation (TMTA) scheme. First, we estimate the spatiotemporal correlation between areas for iden-tifying areas with high importance to the data inference process. Second, a trust-based multi-task allocation algorithm is proposed to ensure that the sensing data from high-importance areas have high trust levels. Third, a multi-armed bandit based trustworthy worker identification strategy is proposed to prioritize Multi-Task allocation for workers who can be effectively identified as trustworthy. Finally, a truthful discrete heuristic algorithm is proposed to optimize the multi-task allocation using the proposed hybrid neighbor-mode strategy, which reduces the difficulty of searching for high-utility multi-task allocations. Extensive exper-iments on two real-world air-quality datasets demonstrate that TMTA consistently outperforms six baseline methods, achieving average reductions of 24.70% in RMSE and 17.79% in sensing cost across five experimental scenarios.

title: Mitigating Delivery Fraud and Path Manipulation in UAV-Based E-Commerce: A Fair Exchange Protocol
authors: Xiaohan Hao, Tianrui Song, Chao Lin, Xinyi Huang
publish: IEEE Transactions on Information Forensics and Security
abstract: UAV-based e-commerce, which employs unmanned aerial vehicles (UAV) to deliver commodities from sellers to buyers, plays a central role in the low-altitude economy. In the interests of both buyers and sellers, UAV-based e-commerce usually relies on a fair exchange protocol between them. Despite extensive research on fair exchange protocol design, UAV-based e-commerce has two features posing extra challenges to achieving mutual fairness: delivery fraud and path manipulation. On one hand, the UAVs usually deliver real-world commodities rather than virtual assets (e.g., digital books), making it hard to verify the physical delivery status. Particularly, this verification can be easily done for virtual assets by checking their hash values, but inapplicable to real-world commodities, as they cannot be naturally hashed. On the other hand, since UAV costs may vary in different air areas, the seller may manipulate delivery bills by claiming unnecessarily expensive paths. These challenges are newly emerged in UAV-based e-commerce and have not been considered in traditional protocols. The primary goal of this work is to propose the first fair exchange protocol that achieving mutual fairness in UAV-based e-commerce. To verify commodity delivery, we develop a tracing mechanism that transforms raw UAV-collected footage into immutable delivery evidence, which can be integrated into existing virtuality-oriented protocols for further verification. As for the path manipulation problem, we introduce an auditing mechanism that enables buyers to verify that the chosen delivery path was generated by a trustworthy algorithm (e.g., a well-trained artificial general intelligence model). By establishing these two mechanisms on cryptographic tools, we theoretically prove the fairness of our proposed protocol. We also implement a prototype and observe that the whole protocol has only minute-magnitude cost across different settings, which validates its practicality.

title: Distribution Network State Inference and Perception Warning Method Based on Deep Learning and Multivariate Statistical Control Integration
authors: Qianyue Yu, Shouxiang Wang, Tongxun Wang, Qianyu Zhao, Dandan Feng, Luyang Guo, Xiaojie Gao, Shuangyin Dai
publish: 2025 IEEE 9th Conference on Energy Internet and Energy System Integration (EI2)
abstract: The large-scale distributed integration of high-proportion renewable energy sources has exacerbated power quality problems in power systems. Addressing this predicament, this investigation, leveraging deep learning paradigms and multivariate statistical process control charting methodologies, has developed sophisticated approaches for power quality state inference and anomalous disturbance alerting. Regarding state inference, the methodology employs fully-connected neural networks augmented with residual connections to establish mapping paradigms from active and reactive power to voltage deviations, facilitating the prognostication of cascading power quality deterioration phenomena precipitated by multi-nodal source-load configurations. Concerning detection and alerting, a three-layer early warning framework encompassing four hierarchical classification levels has been established: the primary stratum implements rigid threshold determinations based on national standards for limit transgression classification; the secondary stratum utilizes Hotelling T<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup> multivariate control chart methodologies to achieve synergistic multi-parameter surveillance; the tertiary stratum establishes optimal voltage deviation parameters predicated upon 70% of national standard limitations, demarcating four hierarchical classifications: above tier one, intermediate between tiers one and two, intermediate between tiers two and three, and below tier three. This methodology facilitates state inference from source-load to power quality intelligence, capturing perturbation characteristics throughout the deterioration trajectory from optimal parameters to alarm thresholds, thereby achieving comprehensive prognostication and perception of spatiotemporal power quality anomalies across multiple nodes within regional networks. The efficacy of this approach has been substantiated through practical implementation within a 40-node distribution network in a designated region.

title: Physics Informed Neural Networks for Distribution Power Flow Estimation
authors: Yijiang Yao, Zhaoran Gao, Yanpei Liao, Ruisheng Diao
publish: 2025 IEEE 9th Conference on Energy Internet and Energy System Integration (EI2)
abstract: High penetration of distributed energy resources (DER) is reshaping distribution networks while amplifying data sparsity and model mismatch, which undermines the accuracy and scalability of traditional power–flow solvers. This paper presents a physics–informed neural network (PINN) for distribution network power–flow analysis. The model uses a shared multilayer perceptron with dual output heads for voltage magnitude and phase angle, trained with a composite objective that blends data fitting with power–balance residuals computed from the network admittance, together with operational limit penalties and a slack reference. A scheduled weighting strategy gradually increases the influence of physics terms to stabilize training and improve physical consistency. A high-fidelity dataset is generated in OpenDSS for the IEEE 123-node and IEEE 8500-node feeders by sampling diverse operating conditions, including stochastic variations of demand and photovoltaic output, transformer tap changes, and switching actions. Partial measurements are emulated to reflect field sparsity. On the IEEE 123-node feeder, the proposed PINN attains mean absolute errors of about 1.03% in voltage magnitude and 2.6<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">◦</sup> in phase angle. On the IEEE 8500-node feeder, the proposed method also gains good performance. It delivers over an order-of-magnitude speedup versus iterative Newton–Raphson while preserving physical feasibility.

title: Wind Speed Estimation for Wind Turbines Based on Expanded Kalman Filter
authors: Wenxuan Cui, Xinyan Feng, Chao Yang, Sixiao Xin, Jiyuan Han, Liang Zou, Zhiyun Han, Rongzhao Jia
publish: 2025 IEEE 9th Conference on Energy Internet and Energy System Integration (EI2)
abstract: Accurate wind speed estimation is crucial for enhancing the energy capture efficiency of modern wind turbines and ensuring their structural safety. Conventional anemometer-based measurements are often limited by single-point errors and sensitivity to turbulence, resulting in unreliable feedback information for turbine control systems. To overcome these limitations, this paper proposes an effective wind speed (EWS) estimation method based on the extended Kalman filter (EKF). First, a simplified dynamic model of the wind turbine drivetrain is derived, establishing a nonlinear state-space representation between rotor speed and aerodynamic torque. Next, the nonlinear dynamics are linearized using a first-order taylor expansion, and the EKF algorithm is developed to achieve real-time recursive estimation under stochastic noise conditions. Finally, the proposed method is implemented and validated in the MATLAB/Simulink environment, using the NREL 5 MW wind turbine as a case study. The results demonstrate that, compared with the conventional Kalman filter (KF) and nacelle-based measurement approaches, the EKF-based effective wind speed estimation method exhibits higher estimation accuracy, faster convergence, and greater robustness against system nonlinearities.

title: Impact of Demand Response on Reliability of Distribution Networks with Distributed Generation
authors: Yihan Zhang, Yuan Qiao, Siqi Wang
publish: 2025 IEEE 9th Conference on Energy Internet and Energy System Integration (EI2)
abstract: With the growth of social electricity consumption and the expansion of power system scale, users' demands for power supply reliability are increasing. In recent years, the rapid development of distributed generation and demand response has presented opportunities to enhance the reliability of distribution networks. This paper focuses on investigating the impact of demand response on distribution network reliability. First, a load optimization model incorporating transferable loads is established. This model uses the objective function of minimizing peak-to-valley load differences, subject to constraints such as user pricing behavior, time-of-use tariff sequence relationships, and energy storage battery operation. Subsequently, to reflect demand response's load curve smoothing effect in reliability assessment, the reliability metrics of load points at each load level are weighted by duration to calculate the distribution network reliability metric considering the load curve. Finally, case studies validate demand response's role in enhancing the reliability of distribution networks incorporating distributed generation.

title: Improving the Performance of QD Photodetectors Through PEAI-Based Interface Passivation
authors: Yihong Tang, Zhuoying Huang, Jingyi Tian, Fan Fang, Haodong Tang, Wei Chen
publish: 2025 International Conference on Energy, Materials, and Photonics (EMP)
abstract: Interfacial engineering using phenethyl-ammonium iodide (PEAI) is employed to improve inverted PbS quantum dot (QD) short-wave infrared photodetectors. Placement of a PEAI layer between NiOx and the QD active layer passivates defects, suppresses recombination, and reduces low-frequency noise. Optimal treatment lowers the 1 Hz noise current density from <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$3.06 \times 10^{-11}$</tex> to <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$3.04 \times 10^{-12} ~\mathrm{A} / \sqrt{ } \text{Hz}$</tex>, yielding a specific detectivity enhancement from <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\text{2. 8 5} \times \text{1 0}^{\mathrm{9}}$</tex> to <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\text{1. 4 2} \times \text{1 0}^{\text{1 0}}$</tex> Jones at <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\text{1 1 5 0 ~ n m}$</tex>. Bandwidth and temporal response are improved while maintaining operational stability, offering a CMOS-compatible pathway toward high-performance SWIR imaging.

title: Consensus Line Formations in Non-Holonomic Multi-Robot Systems Enabled by Decentralized Approximate MST
authors: Dan M. Novischi, Adina M. Florea
publish: 2025 IEEE 21st International Conference on Intelligent Computer Communication and Processing (ICCP)
abstract: We introduce a fully decentralized strategy enabling teams of non-holonomic mobile robots to autonomously form and maintain stable, configurable line-shaped formations during navigation. Central to our approach is a lightweight communication backbone implemented as an approximate minimum spanning tree (MST), constructed efficiently in two stages. First, robots use local geometric criteria to rapidly form initial spanning fragments, which are then incrementally merged through a decentralized, multi-scale Boruvka-inspired process. Leveraging this structure, we extend a potential field-based formation controller to incorporate local alignment behaviors, allowing each robot to dynamically adjust its orientation and inter-robot spacing according to user-defined configurations. This framework ensures robust and scalable formation control without relying on global knowledge or predefined network topologies. Extensive simulations confirm the strategy’s effectiveness, demonstrating rapid convergence, adaptability to evolving formation configurations, and guaranteed asymptotic stability.

title: A Fast and Lightweight Microgrid Load Forecasting Method Based on Linear Modeling and STL Decomposition
authors: Wenjing Li, Dashun Ma, Rukang Chong, Xiaorui Wu, Zhenyan Gao, Feng Zhang, Hongwei Dai
publish: 2025 IEEE 9th Conference on Energy Internet and Energy System Integration (EI2)
abstract: Microgrids serve as flexible and autonomous energy units that enhance distributed renewable utilization and regional energy autonomy. Accurate short-term load forecasting is essential for stable and economical operation but remains challenging due to strong periodicity and complex energy coupling among variables such as load, generation, and electricity price. This paper proposes a fast and lightweight forecasting framework based on linear layers and seasonal-trend decomposition using Loess (STL). Inter-variable dependencies are captured through variable-wise linear layers, while STL separates seasonal, trend, and residual components, each modeled by multilayer perceptrons (MLPs). Experiments on real-world district microgrid data demonstrate that the proposed model achieves comparable or superior accuracy to state-of-the-art methods, including TCN, LSTM, and Transformer, with far fewer parameters and much faster inference. Ablation studies further confirm the effectiveness of both the linear modeling and STL decomposition.

title: Optimization Dispatching of Agricultural Integrated Energy Systems Considering Irrigation Load Characteristics and Demand Response
authors: Yanlong Liu, Yilin Xie, Jiancheng Ma, Xin Chen, Xiaoguang Chen, Xueting Lei
publish: 2025 IEEE 9th Conference on Energy Internet and Energy System Integration (EI2)
abstract: With the growing demand for sustainable agriculture and clean energy utilization, integrated energy systems (IES) have emerged as a promising solution to optimize energy supply and improve resource efficiency in agricultural regions. This paper proposes an environmental-economic co-optimization method for agricultural IES that incorporates irrigation load modeling and demand response mechanisms. Firstly, the correlation between irrigation load and crop yield is quantitatively described by coupling crop water requirements with irrigation electricity consumption. Secondly, a demand response model tailored for agricultural scenarios is developed, encompassing curtailable, shiftable, and substitutable load types. Finally, an optimization scheduling model is established with the objectives of minimizing economic cost and carbon emissions. The effectiveness of the proposed method is validated through a case study based on typical meteorological and load data from northern China.

title: Enhancing Cyber Threat Prediction Through Quantum-Enabled Machine Learning Models
authors: S. Annapurna Shobitha, Gousia Thahniyath, Fenita F, K Radhika, Damodharan D
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Intelligent Systems for Cybersecurity (ISCS)
abstract: The development of cyber attacks is exerting unnecessary strain on the traditional defence measures, which in most cases struggle to cope with the speed, magnitude, and sophistication of the current attacks. Besides increasing the detection of anomalies and predictive analytics, machinelearning processes are limited by the calculational bottlenecks and the exponentially growing security streams. The adjacent one, though, is quantum computing, which has the benefits of computation that can be used to empower predictive methods in the sphere of cybersecurity. This paper presents a critical assessment of how quantum can be used to improve machine learning to improve cyber-threat detection and prognostication. The researchers assume that encoding quantum algorithms to serve quantum support vector machines and variational quantum circuits on high-dimensional security samples will prove the accuracy in the detection, false-positive suppression and efficient improvement of deciphering the nature of complex patterns which proves inaccessible in classical models. The results indicate that quantum-enhanced models have a great potential in redefining the nature in cybersecurity planning because it offers an evolutionary model of dealing with even more sophisticated and unpredictable cyber-attacks[1].

title: Earthquake Alert Prediction Using Machine Learning Models for Resilient Infrastructure
authors: Diana Grace Thomas
publish: 2026 Sixth International Conference on Advances in Electrical, Computing, Communications and Sustainable Technologies (ICAECT)
abstract: Earthquake early warning systems are necessary to reduce deaths and damage to buildings by sending out alerts before earthquakes happen. This study investigates the application of machine learning algorithms, specifically Neural Networks, AdaBoost, Naive Bayes, k-Nearest Neighbours (kNN), and Gradient Boosting, for predicting earthquake alerts based on parameters such as magnitude, depth, Community Disaster Index, Modified Mercalli Intensity (MMI), and seismic significance (sig). Normalisation and class balancing were used to preprocess the dataset and make the model more accurate. The Orange data mining environment used the following measures to quantify performance: Classification Accuracy, Precision, Recall, F1-score, ROC-AUC, and Matthews Correlation Coefficient. The tests demonstrated that ensemble models worked far better than standard classifiers. AdaBoost was the best at making predictions, with 100 % accuracy and recall. Gradient Boosting came in second, with CA and ROCAUC values of 0.97. This means that it is good at finding earthquake circumstances that are very dangerous. The findings indicate that ensemble-based machine learning techniques may enhance the responsiveness and reliability of seismic early warning systems. This would assist places that are likely to be affected by disasters make decisions faster and be better prepared for a resilient infrastructure.

title: Fault event sequence prediction model based on LSTM Attention
authors: Junming Lu, Shaowei Huang, Yongkang Wang
publish: 2025 IEEE 9th Conference on Energy Internet and Energy System Integration (EI2)
abstract: With the rapid development of modern power systems, more complex fault modes have emerged. This study seeks to rectify the shortcomings in the extant literature on fault event sequence prediction, namely the inadequate characterization of fault dynamics pertaining to DC systems and new energy sources, alongside the poor predictive performance of prevailing models.To this end, an attention mechanism is introduced, and a fault sequence prediction model based on LSTM Attention is constructed. Finally, the accuracy of the proposed prediction model is tested on a modified IEEE 39-bus AC/DC hybrid system, validating the effectiveness of the proposed method.

title: Sound-from-Video via Multiscale Spatial-Gradient Filtering of Intra-Frame Motion
authors: Vathiyedath Sulaiman Suniya, Anil Anisia, Susmitha Rajan, Vaninirappuputhenpurayil Gopalan Reju
publish: 2026 Sixth International Conference on Advances in Electrical, Computing, Communications and Sustainable Technologies (ICAECT)
abstract: This paper proposes a vision-based approach for speech acquisition, where speech is inferred by extracting motion from video recordings of sound-induced object vibrations. The method is based on a spatial gradient approach that combines steerable pyramid decomposition and smoothened gradient filtering to yield a single vibration value per frame. The approach relies on the fact that the spatial-gradient magnitude varies with the amount of motion occurring during the exposure and enables the recovery of speech-induced vibrations without segmentation or phase unwrapping. Experimental results show that the proposed method can recover intelligible speech and works reliably on different object surfaces and lighting conditions, maintaining low computational cost. These findings position intra-motion as a useful cue for visual microphones and acoustic sensing devices.

title: Research on voltage compensation control of railway power conditioner under high dual-frequency ripple conditions on the DC side
authors: Pei Luo, Xingyu Zhu, Qian Ma, Zhipeng Ou, Ting Lei, Teng Xie
publish: 2025 IEEE 9th Conference on Energy Internet and Energy System Integration (EI2)
abstract: In order to alleviate the pressure on traditional traction power supply grids, a green and intelligent railway transportation energy system will be constructed. By integrating renewable energy sources such as photovoltaics and energy storage, a diversified and coordinated energy supply structure will be established to achieve efficient consumption and optimal allocation of renewable energy, thereby further improving the power quality indicators on the grid side. However, the flexible traction power supply system of electrified railways is subject to power coupling, and the double-frequency voltage fluctuations on the DC busbar are even more severe, greatly affecting the power quality of the system. In response to the high double-frequency ripple on the DC side of railway power conditioner (RPC), a new topology structure for the DC side of RPC is proposed. A double power decoupling circuit based on a full-bridge structure is selected so that the sum of the power of the decoupling inductor and capacitor is equal to the double-frequency power that causes voltage fluctuations, ultimately achieving the goal of suppressing voltage fluctuations and reducing the total harmonic distortion rate. The simulation model is constructed in Matlab/Simulink. The simulation results show that the proposed method has a good compensation function for the double-line-frequency power pulsation.

title: An Optimization Method for Park Energy Management Integrating Low-Carbon Charge-Discharge Strategies of Electric Vehicles
authors: Zhang Yun, Li Runpei, Yang Jingjing, Kang Qi, Wang Chao, Ding Danlei
publish: 2025 IEEE 9th Conference on Energy Internet and Energy System Integration (EI2)
abstract: Against the backdrop of carbon peaking and carbon neutrality goals, managing electric vehicles (EVs) in park environments necessitates accounting for carbon emissions associated with EV charge-discharge processes to support the park’s low-carbon energy management objectives. This paper proposes an optimization method for park energy management that leverages the low-carbon attributes of EVs to achieve emission mitigation. First, the concept of EV Carbon Ratio per Unit Electricity (CRUE) is introduced, derived from the Carbon Emission Flow (CEF) theory to develop EV-oriented CEF models. Second, a low-carbon EV charge-discharge response model is formulated, incorporating a personalized pricing mechanism for EV charge-discharge operations. Furthermore, an EV charge-discharge decision-making model is presented, driven by both personalized pricing and dynamic carbon emission factors. Comparative case studies demonstrate that the proposed method not only enhances the park’s economic benefits but also reduces carbon emissions by 11%.

title: CoT4DERs: Leveraging Chain-of-Thought for Enhanced Time Series Forecasting in Distributed Energy Resources
authors: Yuxuan Chen, Ruina Zhao, Ruoyi Xu, Jing Peng, Fujun Qi
publish: 2025 IEEE 9th Conference on Energy Internet and Energy System Integration (EI2)
abstract: Power forecasting is essential for the safe operation of modern power systems. However, massive distributed energy resources (DERs) data exhibits highly diverse and often sparse characteristics, posing significant challenges. As a result, power forecasting methods must balance strong generalization capabilities with effective few-shot learning (FSL) performance. Traditional deep learning forecasting methods are mostly designed for single entities, resulting in limited model generalization and difficulties in handling FSL scenarios. To address these challenges, this paper presents CoT4DERs, a novel forecasting framework built on Chain-of-Thought (CoT) reasoning. By leveraging the extensive prior knowledge embedded in pre-trained large language models (LLMs), CoT4DERs achieves accurate and interpretable predictions for the complex power forecasting challenges associated with massive DERs. First, we build domain-specific prompt templates for CoT reasoning, which effectively capture the unique characteristics of prediction tasks across various entities. Secondly, a Bagging ensemble learning framework is developed, where the outputs from multiple LLMs using different prompts are aggregated and averaged. This ensemble approach significantly reduces the impact of LLM hallucinations, thereby enhancing overall model robustness. Experimental results on publicly available datasets demonstrate that CoT4DERs achieves competitive performance compared to state-of-the-art (SOTA) deep learning-based architectures, and further showcases substantial gains when applied to few-shot learning (FSL) scenarios.

title: Key Technologies and Applications for Analyzing Electrical Safety Hazards of High-Voltage Sensitive Users
authors: Bowen Zheng, Chang Liu, Liangze Meng, Jing Zhang, Mingming Pan, Jie Tong
publish: 2025 IEEE 9th Conference on Energy Internet and Energy System Integration (EI2)
abstract: Driven by the wave of "dual carbon" goals and the development of new power systems, large-scale connections have been made between new grid-related entities, such as distributed photovoltaics, customer-side energy storage, and charging piles, and the power grid. This change has brought enormous challenges to the power safety of high-voltage sensitive users in strategic emerging industry clusters. Aiming to address the issues of hidden generation processes, strong temporal and spatial randomness, and the rapid evolution and diffusion of power hazards among high-voltage sensitive users, this paper provides a deep and systematic exploration of the causes and evolution mechanisms of power hazards in the context of access to new grid-related entities. By constructing a multidimensional mapping relationship model of multiple disaster-causing factors, the temporal and spatial diffusion laws of hidden danger sources and the correlation mechanisms between multiple hidden dangers are accurately revealed, and an innovative multi-scenario and multi-level joint disaster-causing state assessment method is proposed. Integrating advanced technologies such as T-S dynamic fault tree, cascading failure theory, and fuzzy comprehensive evaluation method, it provides a solid theoretical basis for accurately positioning and dynamically warning high-voltage sensitive users of power safety hazards. It effectively enhances the user-side network-related safety and risk management capabilities of new power systems.

title: Subpixel Canny Edge Detection for Ceramic Composites: Algorithm Design and Analysis
authors: Yan Wang, Shibo Wang, Guijiu Xie, Xiaoming Yin, Zhongpeng Zheng, Runjie Hou
publish: 2025 3rd Asian Aerospace and Astronautics Conference (AAAC)
abstract: To meet the demands of non-destructive testing and precision measurement in aerospace applications, this paper proposes a modified sub-pixel Canny edge detection method for ceramic matrix composite A500H11. Specifically: Direction-constrained bilateral filtering is employed to suppress pseudo-edges arising from fiber textures; The Scharr operator enhances gradient calculation accuracy; Sub-pixel non-maximum suppression elevates measurement precision to the sub-pixel level; The 2D-OTSU method significantly improves detection accuracy on reflective material surfaces. Experimental results demonstrate that the enhanced Canny operator achieves 9.29% improvement in precision compared to the traditional Canny method. Furthermore, recall and F1-score are increased by 170.9% and 77.8% respectively. This method effectively enables accurate identification of microscopic defects on the material surface.

title: Self-Learning Evolutionary Control Networks for Adaptive Energy Optimization in Smart IoT Cyber-Physical Systems
authors: Asfar H Siddiqui, P. Kavitha, B. Swathi, Nurilla Mahamatov, Y Rama Lakshmanna, B.Anitha Vijayalakshmi
publish: 2026 Sixth International Conference on Advances in Electrical, Computing, Communications and Sustainable Technologies (ICAECT)
abstract: The explosive growth of IoT devices in cyber-physical systems (CPS) has caused tremendous energy consumption issues, requiring intelligent, scalable optimization techniques. Rule-based and static machine learning models do not learn adaptability in dynamic environments and are incapable of generalizing in diverse IoT deployments. The present study introduces a Self-Learning Evolutionary Control Network (SLECN) to obtain adaptive, real-time energy optimization in smart IoT-CPS environments. The architecture combines evolutionary algorithms for policy evolution with reinforcement learning for self-adaptation. It consists of preprocessing, feature engineering, model training, and deployment. A private IoT Energy Consumption dataset gathered from smart meters, sensors, and appliances in residential and commercial environments. The hybrid strategy ensures scalability, real-time adaptability, and less energy wastage without compromising system performance. The model attained 56.7 % energy efficiency, 145 ms response time, and <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\text{9 5. 4 \%}$</tex> resource utilization. Python was employed for full-stack implementation, which involved preprocessing, model training, and deployment.

title: Thermal Control Design and Simulation for Orbital Biological Payload Using Hierarchical Heating Strategy
authors: Haojie Ji, Xiang Zhang, Hossam Hendy, Ao Chen, Jieyuyang Fu, Xuanyi Li
publish: 2025 3rd Asian Aerospace and Astronautics Conference (AAAC)
abstract: Plant Cultivation Payload operates in the high-low temperature alternating space environment with multiple power modes during operation, resulting in complex thermal conditions. To meet the temperature requirements for plant growth, targeted thermal design is essential. Based on the payload's characteristics, the thermal control employs passive methods (radiative cooling, heat pipes, thermal wrapping) as the primary approach, supplemented by active measures (active heating, thermoelectric cooling). Integrating the payload's thermal management with the satellite's overall system, a hierarchical active heating strategy combining PID and Bang-Bang control is proposed. A U-Shaped Heat Pipe is designed to enhance heat transfer between non-adjacent satellite structures. Simulation results confirm that the design meets the required temperature range of 25°C ±1°C for plant experiments. The two-stage heating strategy improves temperature control accuracy and reduces energy consumption compared to single-stage heating.

title: AI for Feminist Media Studies: Exploring Menstruation Stigma on Indian Youtube Using NVivo Analysis
authors: Samiksha Jain, Priya Sachdeva, Vinay Vikram Singh, Prachi Malhotra
publish: 2026 Sixth International Conference on Advances in Electrical, Computing, Communications and Sustainable Technologies (ICAECT)
abstract: This paper presents a qualitative analysis of Indian YouTube videos focused on challenging menstrual stereotypes, with an emphasis on the application of artificial intelligence to feminist media studies. Using NVivo 12, the study systematically examines how content creators employ humor, storytelling, and informational tactics to reduce stigma, encourage open discussions, and promote better menstrual hygiene. The analysis highlights recurring themes such as period poverty, restricted access to sanitary products, and the strategic use of visuals and language to confront biased perceptions. The research not only deepens understanding of user-generated digital content but also supports the development of AI models for automated feminist discourse analysis. These findings underscore the role of digital media in facilitating social change and shaping more inclusive narratives about menstruation in India.

title: A Transformer Based Warm-start Accelerating Optimization Methodology for Local Market Clearing
authors: Renjie Wei, Yishen Wang, Sai Dai, Xinying Wang, Shuya Lei, Shijie Ji
publish: 2025 IEEE 9th Conference on Energy Internet and Energy System Integration (EI2)
abstract: Unit Commitment (UC), a core power market clearing problem, is non-deterministic polynomial hard (NP-hard), leading to the "dimensionality curse" in large-scale power grids; traditional numerical methods fail to adapt to the growing number of market participants, while existing artificial intelligence (AI)-based approaches lack interpretability. To address these issues, this paper proposes a distributed optimization method with Transformer-based warm-start for large-scale local market clearing: it uses the Alternating Direction Method of Multipliers (ADMM) to decompose the problem into subproblems for handling multi-participant constraints, and designs an encoder-decoder Transformer to extract UC temporal features via self-attention, generating feasible initial solutions from historical data to reduce optimizer iterations. Validated on an IEEE 33-bus system (with 3 dispatchable distributed generations (DGs), 1 PV farm, and 1 wind farm), results show the traditional linear programming solver takes 120 seconds, while the proposed method reduces calculation time to 48 seconds, with optimization error rising slightly from 0.1% to 0.5% (practically acceptable), effectively balancing efficiency and accuracy to meet Distributed System Operators (DSOs)’ real-time needs.

title: Study on the Relationship Between Three-Phase Unbalance Degree and Additional Loss Growth Rate of Distribution Transformers and Its Experimental Verification
authors: Yi Wang, Qionglin Li, Ningbo Chen
publish: 2025 IEEE 9th Conference on Energy Internet and Energy System Integration (EI2)
abstract: The operation of transformer in low-voltage distribution network is affected by three-phase unbalance. The method of measuring three-phase unbalance degree by the effective value of sequence current can not accurately reflect the relationship between the growth rate of additional loss caused by three-phase unbalance and three-phase unbalance degree. Based on the analysis of distribution transformer models with different connection modes, the quantitative relationship between the increase rate of additional loss caused by three-phase un-balance and the degree of unbalance of each phase is studied, and a method to calculate the DC resistance of distribution transformer winding by using the measured value of voltage and electric flow is proposed. By comparing the results and errors of different balance measurement methods, it is demonstrated that the additional loss growth rate can not only reflect the additional loss growth caused by the three-phase imbalance, but also reflect the fluctuation of the imbalance, which provides a new guiding basis for measuring the three-phase imbalance.

title: AI and the Investment Efficiency of Electric Power Enterprises: Based on Random Forest and Panel Regression Models
authors: Yantao Chen, Lin Liu, Siyu Yang
publish: 2025 IEEE 9th Conference on Energy Internet and Energy System Integration (EI2)
abstract: This study empirically examines the impact of artificial intelligence (AI) on investment efficiency using a combined framework of random forest and panel regression models, focusing on Chinese A-share listed electric power enterprises. By systematically analyzing heterogeneous effects across different levels of research report attention, analyst attention, and enterprise scales, the research reveals that AI adoption significantly enhances investment efficiency, with pronounced effects observed in firms characterized by higher external scrutiny (i.e., greater research report and analyst attention) and smaller scales. The underlying mechanism suggests that increased research report and analyst coverage intensifies external regulatory pressure, which in turn strengthens the facilitative role of AI in improving investment efficiency. For small-scale enterprises, the relative agility in AI implementation allows for more direct and impactful integration of AI-driven decision-making into investment processes, leading to more pronounced efficiency gains. Methodologically, the study employs random forest modeling for control variable selection, a technique that effectively mitigates model specification errors and enhances analytical precision. These findings contribute to both theoretical understandings of AI's organizational impact and practical insights for optimizing AI-driven investment strategies in the electric power sector.

title: Regulation Capability Requirement Analysis and Evaluation Method for Power Systems with High-Penetration Photovoltaics
authors: Liang Zhang, Junhui Li, Cuiping Li, Chen Jia
publish: 2025 IEEE 9th Conference on Energy Internet and Energy System Integration (EI2)
abstract: As the penetration of photovoltaic (PV) systems increases, their inherent volatility and intermittency pose significant challenges to power system stability. To address this issue, this paper proposes a load-side perspective for defining and evaluating regulation capability requirements in high-PV systems. A quantitative assessment framework incorporating key indicators such as Capacity Matching Rate and Peak Coincidence Rate is developed. Using actual data from Northeast China, the study reveals a nonlinear relationship between PV penetration levels and regulation demands. Results indicate that when PV penetration reaches approximately 50%, system regulation requirements undergo qualitative changes, with the lower regulation limit demand increasing sharply at 70% penetration, imposing substantial operational burdens. These findings provide valuable insights for energy storage planning and generation mix optimization in renewable-dominated power systems.

title: Study on the Optimization of Charging Pile Location Selection——Cost-Aware Algorithm Integrating Greedy Algorithm and Genetic Algorithm
authors: Jinmao Chen, Xudong Wang, Wanli Xu, Youjie Zhou, Pan Li, Man Ruan, Xing Su, Liqian Yuan, Long Huang
publish: 2025 IEEE 9th Conference on Energy Internet and Energy System Integration (EI2)
abstract: To address issues such as high construction costs, complex operation and maintenance, and unbalanced resource allocation in the charging pile location selection process, a cost-aware charging pile layout optimization method integrating the greedy algorithm and genetic algorithm is proposed. Simulation results show that the method can reduce total construction costs by 15% while improving resource utilization efficiency. With the goals of minimizing the comprehensive construction and operation costs, improving coverage rate, and enhancing service efficiency, this method constructs an optimization model for charging pile location selection. First, the greedy algorithm is used to quickly screen out initial solutions with high cost-effectiveness from candidate deployment points, so as to ensure the cost sensitivity and feasibility of the overall layout. Subsequently, a cost-aware fitness function is introduced in the iteration process of the genetic algorithm, and crossover and mutation operations are performed on candidate solutions to achieve global optimization. By combining the advantages of the two types of algorithms, this method balances the solution efficiency and the global optimality of solutions. Experimental results show that while ensuring coverage rate and service capacity, the algorithm can effectively reduce the construction and operation costs of charging piles, improve the robustness and adaptability of deployment schemes, and provide feasible technical support for the planning of charging infrastructure.

title: Optimal Configuration of Shared Energy Storage for New Energy Clusters Considering Uncertainties
authors: Wei Ma, Bin Xu, Kecheng Li, Jun Wang, Wei Tang, Bingyu Sang, Jinzhong Li
publish: 2025 IEEE 9th Conference on Energy Internet and Energy System Integration (EI2)
abstract: To address the challenges of renewable energy output uncertainty and multi-agent interest coordination in "dual-high" power systems, this research proposes a collaborative optimization method that integrates forecasting and game theory. On one hand, an improved Copula model is adopted to overcome difficulties in capturing spatiotemporal correlations and complementary characteristics. On the other hand, a three-level Stackelberg game framework is designed and solved using the Karush-Kuhn-Tucker (KKT) conditions.
